title: A la Désilière (Bread Soup with Sweetened Milk)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- butter
- milk
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut your bread as for the previous soup; you will pass your crumb in butter, as is explained in the crouton soup; you will put the number of croutons so that your soup is not too thick; at the moment of serving pour your sweetened milk over your croutons; try that your milk is neither too much nor too little sweetened; put five or six grains of salt in it.

title: A la Semoule au Lait (Semolina Milk Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- semolina
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. When your milk boils, you sow your semolina in it, and you stir your soup from time to time, so that it does not stick; be careful that it is not too thick, and that it be of good sugar, with very little salt.

title: Abbatis de Dindon en Fricassée au Blanc ou au Roux (Turkey Giblets Fricassee in White or Brown Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two turkey giblets (wings, legs, neck, liver, gizzard)
- a piece of butter
- a bouquet of parsley, chives, garlic clove
- two cloves
- thyme, bay leaf, basil
- mushrooms
- a good pinch of flour
- water or broth
- salt, coarse pepper
- three egg yolks
- cream
- vinegar or verjuice
- broth and jus (for the brown variation)
- small peas (optional)
- a good piece of butter (for the peas variation)

## Directions

1. Blanch the turkey giblets and clean them thoroughly
2. Place them in a saucepan with a piece of butter, a bouquet of parsley, chives, garlic clove, two cloves, thyme, bay leaf, basil, and mushrooms
3. Pass everything over the fire and add a good pinch of flour
4. Moisten with water or broth and season with salt and coarse pepper
5. Cook and reduce to a short sauce
6. When ready to serve, remove the bouquet
7. Add a liaison of three egg yolks with cream and thicken without boiling
8. Serve with a dash of vinegar or verjuice
9. For the brown sauce version: after flouring, moisten with equal parts broth and jus, let reduce to a short sauce
10. For the version with small peas: add them to the saucepan to cook together with a good piece of butter, flour, and moisten with equal parts broth and jus, then cook and reduce to a short sauce

title: Abricots à l'Eau-de-vie (Apricots in Brandy)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots
- Sirup (made as for plum preserves)
- One pint of eau-de-vie per hundred apricots

## Directions

1. Preserve the apricots in the same manner as explained for plum preserves
2. Place them on the fire with the sirup
3. When they boil, throw in one pint of eau-de-vie to make a broth and remove them, placing them in pots
4. Observe that only one pint per hundred is needed
5. When adding the eau-de-vie to the apricots, pull the pan off the fire because fire could ignite it; if this happens, have a white cloth ready, wet it and cover your pan, and the fire will extinguish, but try to prevent this from happening

title: Abricots à l'eau-de-vie (Apricots in Brandy)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots not quite ripe
- Water
- Three pounds of sugar per twelve pounds of fruit

## Directions

1. Choose apricots that are not fully ripe
2. Place them in a pot with cold water
3. Place on the fire
4. When the water trembles and the apricots rise to the top, carefully remove them with a skimmer
5. Plunge them into cold water and let it come to a boil once more
6. Cool them in the water
7. Drain them on a sieve
8. For twelve pounds of fruit, clarify three pounds of sugar
9. When it reaches the pearl stage, add the apricots
10. Let them reach five or six boils
11. Remove them from the syrup and remove from the heat
12. Let the apricots drain

title: Abricots à l'Eau-de-vie (Apricots in Brandy)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Apricots, not fully ripe
- Cold water
- Three pounds of sugar for every twelve pounds of fruit
- Five or six boils in the syrup
- Nine pints of brandy at 18 degrees

## Directions

1. Choose apricots that are not quite ripe and place them in a skillet with cold water
2. Put the skillet on the fire; as soon as the water begins to simmer and the apricots rise to the surface, carefully remove them with a skimmer
3. Throw them into cold water and let them boil once more; let them cool
4. Drain them on a sieve
5. Clarify three pounds of sugar for every twelve pounds of fruit; when it reaches the pearl stage, add the apricots
6. Let them boil five or six times; remove them from the syrup
7. Remove the apricots from the fire and let them drain; when they are cold, put them carefully into a jar without damaging them
8. If your sugar is not sufficiently reduced, let it boil a few more times; when you see the syrup will pearl, remove it from the fire and skim off the foam
9. Pour nine pints of good brandy at 18 degrees into the syrup; when the syrup is well combined with the brandy, pour it over your apricots
10. Put a cork stopper on the jar and a wet parchment to cover it; tie it with string

title: Agneau (Lamb)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb
- Lamb legs for filling the flanks
- Fine lard
- Atelets
- Twine
- Clear stock

## Directions

1. Remove the rib and shoulder bones, leaving only the backbone and thigh bones
2. Fill the flanks with lamb legs
3. When well trussed, lard it finely from the neck to the end of the thighs
4. Make a small separation from the beginning of the thighs to the loins
5. Spit it from the thighs to the neck
6. Secure it with atelets and twine
7. It can also be roasted in the oven
8. If not larded, it must be barded along the entire length
9. If serving as a relevé, place clear stock underneath

title: Aiguillettes d'Oie (Goose Filet Strips)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- three geese
- espagnole sauce
- orange or lemon zest
- orange or lemon juice
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Put three geese on the spit
2. When they are cooked, and at service time, cut your filets into long equal pieces
3. Take the juice thrown by your geese
4. Reduce espagnole until it is very thick
5. Pour the juice of your geese into it
6. Add a little zest of orange or lemon, the juice of one or the other, a little coarse pepper
7. Heat your sauce without boiling it
8. Pour it over your aiguillettes

title: Ailerons au Soleil (Turkey Wings au Soleil)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve turkey wings
- butter
- velouté
- bouillon
- bay leaf
- coarse pepper
- clove
- parsley and chives bouquet
- egg yolks
- bread crumbs
- eggs

## Directions

1. Sauté your twelve wings in butter as for chipolata
2. Add eight spoonfuls of Velouté, half a pot spoonful of bouillon, a bay leaf, coarse pepper, a clove, a bouquet of parsley and chives
3. Bring your wings to a boil; skim them
4. Three-quarters cooked, degrease them
5. When they are cooked, reduce your sauce so that it is well thickened
6. Remove the bouquet, leaf and clove
7. Add a liaison of three egg yolks into your ragout, with butter the size of half an egg
8. When it is thickened, place your wings to cool on a cake pan or platter, and their sauce on top
9. After this, coat them well with their sauce, and put them in bread crumbs
10. Then place them in an omelet of five raw eggs
11. Bread them a second time equally so that the sauce does not run into the frying oil
12. Give your wings a beautiful form
13. At the moment of serving

title: Ailerons de Dindons aux Petits Oignons et au Fromage (Turkey Wings with Small Onions and Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- six or eight turkey wings
- a bouquet of parsley, chives
- two cloves
- half a bay leaf
- a little basil
- one glass of white wine
- an equal amount of broth
- at least a dozen small onions
- salt, coarse pepper
- beurre manié with flour
- Gruyère or Parmesan cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Blanch the turkey wings, clean them, and place them in a saucepan with a bouquet of parsley, chives, two cloves, half a bay leaf, and a little basil
2. Moisten with one glass of white wine and an equal amount of broth
3. Cook over low heat
4. After half an hour, add at least a dozen small onions blanched for a quarter-hour in boiling water and well cleaned
5. Season lightly with salt and coarse pepper
6. Finish cooking and remove the wings and onions from the pan to drain them
7. Strain the sauce through a sieve and reduce it if too thin
8. Add a piece of butter worked with a pinch of flour and thicken over the fire
9. Put a little sauce in the bottom of the serving plat and cover with a handful of grated Gruyère or Parmesan
10. Arrange the wings and small onions on top, pour the remaining sauce over everything, and cover with more grated cheese
11. Place the plat on a small stove to simmer over low heat until almost no sauce remains
12. Brown the top with a hot shovel or a tourière cover with good heat and serve hot

title: Ailerons de Dindons en Haricot (Turkey Wings with Haricot Beans)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- ten wings from young turkeys
- lard strips
- small turnips
- parsley and chives bouquet
- bouillon
- butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Scald the turkey wings, singe them, pare them, and debone them
2. Place them in a saucepan with lard strips on top and below
3. Place a skillet on top and simmer for an hour and a half, or longer if the wings are old
4. At the moment of service, drain them and arrange them in crowns
5. Make a roux; sauté your wings; baste them with bouillon with great moisture to cook them at high heat
6. Add a bouquet of parsley and chives
7. Take care to skim and degrease your ragout
8. When your wings are three-quarters cooked, add your turnips that you have cut into small sticks of the same thickness
9. Have them finish cooking in butter at the moment of serving
10. Check that your ragout is properly seasoned

title: Ailerons en Chipolata (Turkey Wings en Chipolata)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey wings
- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- bay leaf
- coarse pepper
- salt pork
- mushrooms
- parsley and chives bouquet
- twenty-four small onions
- chestnuts
- sausages
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Scald, singe and debone your wings; pare them
2. Place a piece of butter in a saucepan with your wings; sauté them over high heat
3. When they are browned in your butter, add a tablespoon of flour, two pot spoonfuls of bouillon, a bay leaf, coarse pepper
4. Cook your wings at high heat; take care to skim
5. Blanch salt pork that you cut into flat squares to add to your wings
6. Also add the whitest possible mushrooms, a bouquet of parsley and chives
7. When your ragout is three-quarters cooked, add twenty-four small onions, well peeled and of the same size, chestnuts that you have blanched, long sausages that you tie in the middle, and that you blanch; degrease your ragout, and add these ingredients to it
8. When it is cooked and at the moment of serving, arrange your wings on your platter; remove the bouquet and bay leaf from your ragout
9. Check that it is properly seasoned, and add a liaison of two egg yolks; pour it over your wings

title: Ailerons en Haricot Vierge (Turkey Wings with Virgin Haricot)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey wings
- small turnips
- butter
- velouté or flour
- bouillon
- coarse pepper
- bay leaf
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Prepare your wings as previously described
2. Cook them in a skillet as instructed
3. Turn turnips into small sticks, olives or otherwise, but all of the same size and thickness
4. Sauté them in white butter, that is to say do not let them take on color
5. After a quarter hour, pour in eight spoonfuls of velouté; if you do not have any, add a tablespoon of flour that you will mix with your butter and turnips
6. Add a pot spoonful of bouillon, some coarse pepper, a bay leaf
7. Cook them at high heat; skim and degrease them
8. When your turnips are cooked, drain your wings; arrange them in crowns
9. Add a liaison of two egg yolks into your turnips, and place them in the middle of your wings

title: Ailerons à la Chicorée (Turkey Wings with Chicory)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey wings
- lard strips
- veal slices
- two carrots
- two onions
- clove
- bay leaf
- butter paper
- consommé or bouillon
- chicory

## Directions

1. Scald your wings, singe them; debone them
2. Lard them finely
3. Place lard strips in the bottom of a saucepan, some slices of veal, two carrots cut into slices, two onions, a clove, a bay leaf
4. Place your wings on top of this seasoning, a round of buttered paper, a pot spoonful of Consommé or bouillon
5. Simmer them for an hour with fire above and below
6. Take care that your wings do not take on too much color
7. At the moment of serving, drain them; glaze them, and place chicory on the platter, your wings on top, or arrange them in crowns, and your chicory in the middle

title: Allumettes de Palais de Bœuf (Beef Palate Matchsticks)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two beef palates cooked in water
- lemon
- salt
- parsley
- chives
- flour
- fine oil
- beer

## Directions

1. Peel the cooked palates and cut them like matchsticks
2. Marinate with lemon, a little salt, parsley sprigs, and whole chives
3. When they have taken on flavor, drain them
4. Make a batter: put two good handfuls of flour in a saucepan with a spoonful of fine oil and a little fine salt
5. Gradually add beer until the batter has the consistency of double cream
6. Dip the beef palate pieces in the batter and fry them to a beautiful color
7. Serve as hot as possible

title: Alose grillée (Grilled Shad)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one shad
- salt
- pepper
- parsley branches
- chives
- one glass of oil
- butter sauce
- capers or sorrel purée

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the shad and remove the scales
2. Dry it well and let it drain between linens
3. Place it on a platter with salt, pepper, parsley branches, chives, and one glass of oil
4. Turn it in this seasoning one hour before serving, more or less depending on its size
5. Place it on the grill at a gentle fire
6. At serving time, arrange it on the platter and coat it with butter sauce
7. Sprinkle capers over it or place it on a sorrel purée

title: Alouettes en Ragoût (Larks in Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- dozen larks
- butter
- bouquet garni
- mushrooms
- calf sweetbread
- flour
- white wine
- bouillon
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Pluck, singe and clean a dozen larks
2. Truss the feet to pass through the beak as for roasting
3. Pass them in a saucepan over the fire with a piece of butter, a bouquet garni, mushrooms and a calf sweetbread
4. Add a good pinch of flour
5. Moisten with a glass of white wine, bouillon and juice as needed to give color
6. Boil and reduce to the point of a bound sauce
7. Degrease and season with salt and coarse pepper

title: Alouettes en Salmis à la Bourgeoise (Larks in Salmis Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked larks
- toasted bread slices
- rocambole
- verjuice
- fried croutons for garnish

## Directions

1. Use larks that have been served from the table and were cooked on the spit
2. Remove the heads and what they have in the body
3. Discard the gizzards and use the rest with the toasted bread slices
4. Pound everything in a mortar
5. Dilute what you have pounded with a little crushed rocambole and a dash of verjuice
6. Heat the larks in this sauce without letting it boil
7. Serve garnished with fried croutons

title: Alouettes en Tourte (Larks in Pie)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- larks
- lard
- butter
- parsley
- scallions
- mushrooms
- garlic

## Directions

1. Clean the larks and remove the gizzard
2. Put the rest with grated lard in the bottom of the pie
3. Place the larks on top after removing their feet and head and having passed them in a saucepan over the fire with a little good butter, parsley, scallions, mushrooms and a point of garlic, all chopped
4. Let cool
5. Finish the pie as will be explained in the general article on pies

title: Amidon de Pommes de Terre (Potato Starch)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes
- Water

## Directions

1. Have a fine hair sieve
2. Wash the potatoes well
3. Grate them on your sieve placed over a terrine
4. Pour a large quantity of water over your grated potatoes
5. Let the water rest: one hour later, pour it out, and you will find the starch at the bottom of the vessel
6. Dry it if you wish

title: Anchovy Toasts (Rôties d'Anchois)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Slices of bread
- Oil
- Fine oil
- Vinegar
- Coarse pepper
- Parsley
- Chives (ciboule)
- Shallot
- Anchovy fillets

## Directions

1. Cut bread slices into neat pieces the width and length of a finger
2. Fry in oil
3. Arrange in an entremets dish
4. Make a sauce with fine oil, vinegar, coarse pepper, parsley, chives, and shallot, all chopped
5. Put the sauce over and cover halfway your toasts with anchovy fillets

title: Andouilles (Chitterlings)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- The meatiest intestines of pork
- Salt
- Pepper
- Pounded aromatic herbs
- Spices
- Intestines for stuffing
- Broth
- Vegetable roots
- Parsley and chives
- Thyme
- Bay leaf

## Directions

1. Have the meatiest intestines of pork cleaned and washed; when they are very clean, let them disgorge for twelve hours; drain them; dry them well; place them in a terrine
2. Season them with salt, pepper, pounded aromatic herbs, spices; leave them with this seasoning for twelve hours
3. Stuff them into intestines that you tie at the end, and place them in the bottom of the salt cellar
4. When you wish to eat them, cook them in broth with vegetable roots, a bouquet of parsley and chives, a little thyme, bay leaf; let them cool in their cooking liquid
5. Slice them and place them on the grill; serve them as an appetizer

title: Andouilles de Cochon (Pork Chitterling Sausages)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pork fatty casings
- vinegar
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- pork fat
- pork pieces
- salt
- fine spices
- anise
- milk
- lard

## Directions

1. Clean the pork casings well and cut them to the desired length for andouilles
2. Soak them in water with one-quarter vinegar, thyme, bay leaf, and basil to remove their charcuterie flavor
3. Cut some of these casings into thin strips, pork fat into thin strips, and pork pieces into thin strips
4. Season everything together with salt and fine spices, mix with a little anise
5. Fill your casings two-thirds full, for fear they burst while cooking if too full
6. Tie them at both ends
7. Cook them with equal parts water and milk, salt, thyme, bay leaf, basil, and a little fat to nourish them
8. When cooked, let them cool in their cooking liquid
9. When ready to serve, grill them and serve as an appetizer

title: Anguille aux Moutons de Laitue Romaine (Eel with Romaine Lettuce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- butter
- bouquet
- romaine lettuce hearts
- salt
- three egg yolks
- cream
- verjuice or wine

## Directions

1. Cut the eel into pieces and cook it as if making a chicken fricassee
2. When it is almost cooked, have romaine lettuce hearts that are well cleaned and cooked in white water with a little salt and butter
3. Drain the lettuce and let it take on flavor with the eel
4. Add a liaison of three egg yolks mixed with cream
5. Make it bind over the fire, and when serving add a dash of verjuice if you did not put wine in your eel fricassee

title: Anguille de Mer (Sea Eel)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sea eel
- Water
- Salt
- Parsley root or parsley
- Bay leaves, three or four

## Directions

1. Cook your eel in water with salt, parsley root or parsley, and three or four bay leaves.
2. Mask it with a cream sauce or a brown sauce in which you put butter the size of half an egg mixed with anchovy butter, or with a tomato sauce. (Choose the sauce you prefer.)

title: Anguille à la Broche (Eel on the Spit)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- butter
- carrots
- onions
- parsley
- bay leaf
- thyme
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- oiled paper

## Directions

1. Place the eel on a well-heated spit; let it grill superficially, and with a cloth draw the grilled skin from head to tail by pulling
2. Once the eel is stripped and its oil removed, cut off the head; empty it and roll it like a hoop; secure it with small skewers or spits and string
3. Place it on a baking dish
4. In a saucepan, put a piece of butter, carrots cut into slices, onions cut the same way, parsley, bay leaf, and thyme; brown this seasoning well
5. Moisten it with white wine, salt, and pepper; let it boil for half an hour
6. Pass it through a silk sieve over the eel and put it in the oven
7. After three-quarters of an hour, remove it with a large lid; take it off the baking dish and put it on your plate
8. Add an Italian sauce to it; pass skewers through your eel and secure it on the spit; wrap it in oiled paper

title: Anguille à la Broche (Eel on the Spit)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- Butter
- Carrots
- Onions
- Parsley
- Bay leaf

## Directions

1. Place the eel over a very hot stove
2. Let it grill superficially and with a cloth draw the grilled skin from head to tail
3. Once the eel is stripped and its oil removed, cut off the head
4. Clean it and roll it like a hoop
5. Secure it with small skewers or small spits and string
6. Place it on a baking sheet
7. Put a piece of butter in a casserole with carrots cut in slices, onions cut the same way, parsley and bay leaf

title: Anguille à la Poulette (Eel Poulette Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- parsley
- chives
- white wine
- butter
- small onions
- flour
- mushrooms
- egg yolks
- crayfish

## Directions

1. After stripping your eel, cut it into sections of three inches
2. Put them in a saucepan with salt, coarse pepper, two bay leaves, sprigs of parsley, chives, and a bottle of white wine
3. Put your eel on the fire; when it is cooked, remove the pieces and put them in another saucepan
4. Pass its cooking liquid through a silk sieve
5. Put a piece of butter in a saucepan and pass twenty small onions in white; add a spoonful of flour that you mix with your butter
6. Add your cooking liquid with your onions; add mushrooms, a bouquet of parsley and chives
7. When your onions are cooked, remove them with a slotted spoon and put them on your eel
8. Make sure the sauce tastes good; degrease it and reduce it
9. If it is too thin, add a liaison of three egg yolks; be careful that it does not boil, and pass it through cheesecloth over your eel
10. At the moment of serving, put croutons fried in butter at the bottom of the dish; arrange your eel on top; cover it with your garnish and garnish it with crayfish

title: Anguille à la Tartare (Eel Tartare Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- flour
- breadcrumbs
- eggs
- tartare sauce

## Directions

1. Strip your eel as for the eel on the spit; cut it into sections of five or six inches
2. Prepare a marinade as described for the previous eel; before moistening it, add a little flour
3. When your sauce is cooked, pass it through cheesecloth over the pieces of eel and let them cook
4. When they are cold, put them in breadcrumbs, then dip them in seasoned and beaten eggs
5. Bread them a second time
6. A good quarter hour before serving, put your sections on the grill over a gentle fire; place a well-heated camp oven on top
7. Add a tartare sauce on your plate and put the sections on it

title: Another Compote of Peaches
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight nearly ripe peaches
- boiling water
- one quarter pound of sugar
- one glass of water

## Directions

1. Take seven or eight nearly ripe peaches, split them in half; after removing the pit, put them for a moment in boiling water and remove them as soon as you can remove the skin
2. Boil a quarter pound of sugar with a glass of water, be careful to skim, and then put the peaches in it to cook
3. Reduce the syrup if it is too thin before arranging it over the peaches

title: Artichauts (Artichokes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- three artichokes of the same size
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Take three artichokes of the same size and remove what is hard found at the heart
2. Trim the leaves by removing the tips
3. Wash them well and place them in a pot with boiling salted water
4. Ensure they are submerged in water
5. After one hour, ensure they are cooked
6. Remove them from the boiling water and place them in cold water
7. Remove the choke
8. At the moment of serving, place them in boiling water
9. Drain them and place them on a plate with white or brown sauce inside or in a sauceboat

title: Artichauts au Velouté (Artichokes with Velouté Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- pepper
- five spoonfuls of velouté sauce
- six spoonfuls of consommé
- butter, the size of an egg

## Directions

1. Trim the heart of the artichokes; if they are large, cut them into eight pieces
2. Trim the leaves and remove the choke
3. Place the pieces in water, drain them, and arrange them in a pot
4. Add pepper, five spoonfuls of velouté sauce, six spoonfuls of consommé, and butter the size of an egg
5. Half an hour before serving, place them on the fire to boil vigorously
6. Place fire on the pot cover
7. Arrange the artichokes on the plate when cooked
8. If you have no sauce, make a light roux, moisten it with broth, and pour it over the artichokes

title: Artichauts aux Fines Herbes (Artichokes with Fine Herbs)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- light roux
- broth
- juice
- finely chopped herbs
- pepper
- nutmeg, grated

## Directions

1. Cut and prepare the artichokes into pieces as previously described
2. Make a light roux and moisten it with broth and a little juice
3. Put many finely chopped herbs on the artichokes, pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
4. Pour the sauce over them
5. Cook them as the artichokes with velouté

title: Artichauts Frits (Fried Artichokes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 artichokes
- salt
- pepper
- 2 tablespoons oil
- 1 whole egg
- 2 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons vinegar
- 8 tablespoons flour
- 1/2 glass beer

## Directions

1. Peel the heart of two artichokes and cut each into twelve pieces
2. Place them in water to wash; let them drain
3. Place them in a terrine or pot with salt, pepper, 2 tablespoons of oil, one whole egg, two egg yolks, three tablespoons of vinegar, eight tablespoons of flour, and half a glass of beer
4. Mix everything together until the flour is well dissolved
5. You may prepare the batter separately, then place the artichokes in it
6. Stir them so they are coated with batter all over

title: Artichauts à l'Espagnole (Artichokes Spanish Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the artichokes as previously described
2. Instead of velouté, add Spanish sauce
3. Cook them in the same manner
4. At the moment of serving, arrange the artichokes on a plate and pour the sauce underneath

title: Artichauts à la Barigoule (Artichokes Barigoule)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 artichokes
- 1/4 pound lard, minced
- butter and oil in equal amounts
- mushrooms
- 1 tablespoon shallots, finely chopped
- 1 tablespoon parsley
- spices
- salt
- coarse pepper
- thyme
- bay leaf
- 1 glass bouillon
- veal slices
- lard strips
- espagnole sauce or roux

## Directions

1. Cut off the tips of the leaves; place three artichokes in water
2. When well washed, place them in a pot of boiling water and blanch for twenty minutes
3. Refresh them and remove the choke; let them drain
4. Take hot fat and place the artichokes in it with the leaf side down; when the leaves are well fried, drain them
5. Add a quarter pound of minced lard to a pot, along with equal amounts of butter and oil
6. Chop mushrooms very fine and pass them through the pot
7. Add a tablespoon of finely chopped shallots, the same of parsley, a little spice, salt, and coarse pepper
8. Pass the herbs well and let them cool, then place them inside the artichokes
9. Line the bottom of a pot with lard strips and a few veal slices
10. Truss your artichokes and place them in the pot with thyme, bay leaf, and a glass of bouillon
11. Cover with lard strips and a round of buttered paper
12. When boiling, place on low heat and put hot coals on the lid
13. After braising for three-quarters of an hour, remove the twine and serve on a platter
14. Pour a little espagnole sauce inside; if you have no sauce, make a light roux that you moisten with the braising liquid from the artichokes and pass through a silk sieve

title: Artichauts à la Lyonnaise (Artichokes Lyonnaise Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- three large artichokes
- butter, half a pound
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. If the three artichokes are large, cut them into eight pieces
2. Remove the hard part found at the heart, the choke, and the leaves until only three or four remain
3. Reduce their width
4. Place them in water as you trim them
5. Wash them well and drain them
6. Put half a pound of butter in the bottom of your pot, spread it well, and place the artichoke heart on top
7. Sprinkle salt and pepper on the artichokes
8. Half an hour before serving, place them on a moderately hot fire and place fire on the pot cover as well
9. Take care that they do not burn
10. When cooked, arrange them on the plate and pour the butter over them
11. You may also sauté them in butter on a hot fire; when they are browned, they are ready

title: Artichauts à la Provençale (Artichokes Provençal)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 artichokes
- 12 onions
- 1/2 pound oil
- 3 anchovies
- butter
- espagnole sauce or roux
- lard strips

## Directions

1. Prepare three artichokes as for artichokes barigoule
2. Cut twelve onions into four pieces, about one line thick, each piece forming a quarter circle
3. Put half a pound of oil in a pot and place it on the fire with the onions inside
4. When the onions are well browned, pound three anchovies and mix them with butter and the onions
5. Put the mixture inside the artichokes
6. Cook them in a pot as described for artichokes barigoule
7. When serving, pour espagnole sauce inside, or make a light roux that you moisten with the braising liquid from the artichokes
8. You may also cook them simply on a lard strip with heat above and below

title: Artichaux (Artichokes)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- water
- salt
- bouquet of fine herbs
- coulis
- butter
- vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Commonly they are eaten after cutting the bottom and cutting in half the leaves on top
2. Cook them in water with a little salt and a bouquet of fine herbs
3. When cooked, put them to drain and remove the choke
4. If in the fat way, you will render good coulis and put a piece of butter in it, a small dash of vinegar, salt, coarse pepper; make the sauce thicken over the fire and put it in the artichokes
5. If in the lean way, put in its place a white sauce
6. These same artichokes when cooked in water and cooled are eaten in oil with salt, pepper, vinegar

title: Artichaux au Verjus de Grain (Artichokes with Verjuice)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four artichokes
- a piece of butter
- a pinch of flour
- two egg yolks
- verjuice
- salt
- coarse pepper
- verjuice in grain

## Directions

1. Remove the green from underneath and cut the leaves in half
2. Cook them in a small bed of coals, season lightly
3. Let them drain and remove the choke
4. In a saucepan, put a piece of butter, a pinch of flour, two egg yolks and verjuice, salt, coarse pepper
5. Bind the sauce over the fire
6. Add verjuice in grain that you have boiled briefly in water
7. Serve the artichokes with this sauce

title: Artichaux Confit pour l'Hiver (Pickled Artichokes for Winter)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- water

## Directions

1. Remove all the leaves and leave only what is good to eat on the heart
2. Throw them in water until you have prepared them all
3. Cook them afterward in water until you can easily remove the choke
4. Put them back in fresh water
5. When they are well cleaned, put them to drain
6. If it is to dry them, put them on hurdles in an oven that should not be too hot; if you can hold your hand in it without it burning you, that is enough
7. When they are dry, you will serve them to put in stews after having made them revive in lukewarm water
8. If you wish to pickle them, they will be better; put a brine as is explained for pickled green beans and follow the same method

title: Artichaux en Fricassée (Artichokes in Fricassee)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- water
- chicken

## Directions

1. Cut them the same way and cook them in water a quarter hour
2. Put them back in fresh water
3. Prepare them afterward in a fricassee of chicken
4. When they are cooked, put in a liaison and serve them as a side dish

title: Artichaux Frits (Fried Artichokes)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- flour
- eggs
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. If you wish to fry them, cut them into pieces and remove the choke
2. Wash them and drain them
3. When you are ready to fry them, marinate them in a pan with a small handful of flour, two egg whites and yolks, a dash of vinegar, salt, pepper
4. Fry them until they are golden, serve them with fried parsley

title: Artichaux Tournés (Turned Artichokes)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes of good size and regular shape
- water
- salt
- butter
- bouillon
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Choose artichokes of good size and regular shape, remove the largest leaves
2. After having removed the green by cutting it uniformly all around with a knife, and turning the artichoke so that it is cut equally on all sides
3. Cut off the stalk and place on white cloth, plunge them in fresh water and remove them
4. Retire them to fresh water to remove the black
5. Cook them in blanc made with water, butter, salt, flour, the juice of half a lemon, and grain of salt
6. When all are cooked, serve them with sauce au blanc made with water, salt, and verjuice

title: Artichaux à la Barigoule (Artichokes Barigoule)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- bouillon or water
- oil
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- roots
- bouquet garni
- vinegar
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Take three or four artichokes according to their size or the size of your side dish plate
2. Cut the green from below and half the leaves
3. Put them in a pan with bouillon or water, two spoonfuls of good oil, a little salt and pepper, an onion, two roots, a bouquet garni
4. Cook them and reduce the sauce entirely
5. When they are cooked and there is no more sauce, let them fry a moment in the oil to brown
6. Put them after on a pie dish with the oil that remains in the pan
7. Empty them of their choke and put on top a hot pie dish cover
8. Put fire on the cover to make the leaves grill
9. If you have a hot oven, they will only be more beautiful
10. When they are grilled a beautiful color, serve them with a sauce of oil, vinegar, salt and coarse pepper

title: Artichokes Preserved for Winter
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Artichokes
- Water
- Salt
- Brine
- Oil

## Directions

1. After washing artichokes well, let them drain
2. Trim the base by removing the stem and cutting off the outer surface
3. Have a large pot three-quarters full of water with two handfuls of salt
4. When the water boils, add about a dozen artichokes
5. Let them boil; after a quarter hour, remove them and place in cold water
6. Let them drain for five or six hours
7. Arrange them in a large earthenware pot or butter pot
8. Pour brine over them so they are covered; also add one or two pounds of oil depending on the width of your dish
9. To cook, place in cold water on the fire and boil until cooked, then cool and remove the fuzzy center
10. Serve with sauce or in another manner

title: Asperges (Asparagus)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Salt
- Oil

## Directions

1. The asparagus of Rony is preferred for taste and thickness.
2. Remove the leaves down to the bouquet with the knife blade near the tip.
3. Scrape away the outer layer from the bouquet to the end of the white part.
4. Make sure they are all the same length.
5. Tie them in bundles.
6. Twenty minutes before serving, put them in boiling salted water.
7. Test them by bending; if they are tender, refresh them so they do not cook too much and do not lose their green color.
8. If you want them with oil, serve them cold in a single bundle; they must be regularly arranged on the plate.

title: Asperges en Petits Pois (Asparagus as Peas)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut them to the size of peas and wash well
2. Cook them a moment in water
3. Let them drain and prepare them as small peas à la demi-bourgeoise, removing only the lettuces

title: Asperges en petits Pois (Asparagus Tips)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Small asparagus
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Velouté or flour
- Bouillon or water
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Sugar
- Egg yolks
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Cut small asparagus into pieces the size of peas
2. Blanch asparagus tips in boiling salted water
3. After twenty boils, place in a strainer and then in cold water
4. Half an hour before serving, drain completely
5. Melt a piece of butter in a saucepan with the asparagus, salt, and coarse pepper
6. Brown the asparagus in the butter
7. Add two spoonfuls of velouté or two teaspoons of flour mixed with bouillon or water, salt, coarse pepper, a little grated nutmeg, and one teaspoon of powdered sugar
8. Bring the asparagus to a boil until the sauce is reduced
9. Add a liaison of three egg yolks
10. Arrange on a plate with croutons around it

title: Aspic de Blanc de Poulet (Aspic of Chicken Breast)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight chicken breasts
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Four degrease-spoonfuls of velouté
- Four spoonfuls of jelly
- One egg yolk
- Parsley
- Butter
- Aspic

## Directions

1. Sauté eight chicken breasts.
2. Season them with salt and coarse pepper.
3. Let them cool on a platter.
4. Pour into a casserole four degrease-spoonfuls of velouté and four spoonfuls of jelly.
5. Reduce everything to half its volume.
6. Add a liaison of one egg yolk, a little chopped and washed parsley, and an amount of butter the size of half an egg, added after your sauce is bound.
7. Blend it well with your sauce.
8. Pour it over your fillets.
9. Let it cool thoroughly until well congealed.
10. Arrange aspic in your mold to a thickness of about half an inch.
11. Put it in cold storage.
12. When well congealed, decorate it.
13. Arrange your fillets symmetrically with correct form and smooth top due to the sauce that masked them.
14. On top of your fillets arrange a small cold ragout of crests and kidneys with the fillets mignons placed in a similar sauce.
15. Then pour melted aspic to fill your mold and add it when cold.
16. Let your aspic congeal well.
17. At serving time, invert it and detach it as directed for aspic.

title: Ateaux de Palais de Bœuf (Beef Palate Ateaux)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate cooked in blanc
- atelet sauce
- veal breast

## Directions

1. When your beef palates are cooked in blanc, cut them into squares measuring ten lines in all directions
2. Put them in an atelet sauce
3. Toss them in it and let them cool
4. Have a veal breast that you will have cooked in the pot and which will be cold
5. Cut small thin squares from it the same size as your palates

title: Ateaux de Palais de Bœuf (Beef Palate Skewers)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate, cooked in blanc
- veal teat, cooked in marmite and cold
- sauce à atelet
- breadcrumbs
- anglaise for breading
- clear jus or clear espagnole or italienne sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked beef palate into squares of ten lines on all sides
2. Put them in sauce à atelet, sauté them in it, and let them cool
3. Cut the cold cooked veal teat into small thin squares the same size as the palate pieces
4. Alternate pieces of palate and teat on a skewer so that the atelet is well-formed
5. If it is not regular, fill gaps with the same sauce
6. First coat the atelets in breadcrumbs, then finish with anglaise breading
7. Ensure the atelets have their four square sides well united
8. Place them on the grill with gentle heat; you may grill only three sides and color the fourth with a field oven or glazing peel
9. Serve the atelets with clear jus, clear espagnole, italienne, or without sauce

title: Aubergines (Eggplants)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Four eggplants
- Lard
- Butter
- Oil
- Mushrooms
- Shallots
- Parsley
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Spices
- Quenelle forcemeat
- Anchovies
- Egg
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut four eggplants in half lengthwise without hollowing out too much so they retain some flesh
2. Grate lard twice the size of an egg and the same amount of butter
3. Heat four large spoonfuls of oil in a saucepan
4. Add two large spoonfuls of finely chopped mushrooms, a small handful of shallots and one of parsley
5. Cook these fine herbs with the butter, lard and oil
6. When well browned, add salt, coarse pepper, a little spice and let cool
7. Add as much cooked quenelle forcemeat as you have fine herbs
8. Chop three anchovies and mix with the forcemeat and herbs
9. Fill the eight eggplant halves with this mixture
10. Smooth the top of the forcemeat with egg and bread
11. Half an hour before serving, place on a baking sheet in the oven or over moderate heat with a camp oven on top

title: Autre Canard aux Navets (Another Duck with Turnips)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one duck, cleaned, singed and trussed
- butter for making a roux
- two spoonfuls of bouillon
- salt
- pepper
- one bay leaf
- a bouquet of parsley and chives
- turnips of uniform size
- butter for sautéing
- a small piece of sugar

## Directions

1. When your duck is cleaned, singed and trussed, make a roux
2. When it is well blonde, put your duck in it and let it brown
3. When the meat is firm everywhere, pour in two spoonfuls of bouillon; if you lack bouillon, add water, salt, pepper, and a bay leaf
4. Turn your duck with its liquid until it boils
5. Add a bouquet of parsley and chives and let it cook on high heat
6. When your duck is three-quarters cooked, add turnips that you have shaped to uniform size
7. Sauté them in butter; when they are well blonde, let them drain
8. Put them with your duck and cook on low heat
9. Degrease the duck and add a small piece of sugar
10. At service time, unbind it and pour your ragout of turnips over it. Check that it is properly seasoned

title: Autre Espagnole Travaillée (Another Worked Spanish Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Spanish sauce
- three-quarters of a bottle of white wine or more, depending on the quantity of sauce

## Directions

1. Work this sauce as in the preceding recipe
2. Add the white wine
3. Ensure the sauce is well seasoned

title: Autre Manière de Bisque (Crayfish Bisque, Another Way)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish, fifty
- butter, a quarter pound and a half
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- bread crumb, tender, three times as large as an egg
- bread crusts

## Directions

1. Have fifty crayfish; when they are well washed, you must pound them raw; when they are well ground, have a quarter pound and a half of butter that you will put in a saucepan with your crayfish; use salt, coarse pepper, a little nutmeg, a piece of tender bread three times as large as an egg: put your crayfish on a fairly hot fire so that they can cook for half an hour; then you will pass your crayfish in purée through a fine sieve; when your purée is passed, you will put it in a saucepan on gentle heat; try that it does not boil, and that it is not too thick: you then prepare your crusts as for the previous soup. This soup is made with fat as well as lean. (See Lean Broth.)

title: Autre Matelote (Another Fish Stew)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Onion slices
- Parsley sprigs
- Cloves
- White wine
- Small onions
- Butter
- Mushrooms
- Roux

## Directions

1. Prepare the fish as in the previous recipe
2. Place in a casserole with salt, coarse pepper, two bay leaves, thyme, onion slices, parsley sprigs, two cloves and enough white wine to submerge the fish
3. Place over high heat
4. Once the fish is cooked, pass the broth through a silk sieve
5. Transfer the fish to another casserole and trim it well
6. Have thirty or forty small onions, peeled and of the same size
7. Sauté them in butter until golden brown
8. Drain them on a sieve
9. Make a roux proportional to the stew
10. Dilute it with the wine in which the fish cooked
11. Add mushrooms and reduce the sauce by a third
12. Add the small onions and decrease the sauce
13. Check the taste
14. When the onions are cooked, remove the garnish with a slotted spoon
15. Place it on the fish and strain the sauce over it
16. Keep the stew hot
17. At the moment of serving, arrange on a plat with bread crusts sautéed in butter and garnish with crayfish

title: Autre Ratafia de Fleurs d'Oranges (Another Orange Blossom Liqueur)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 quarterons of orange blossoms
- 3 chopines of water
- 2 pints of eau-de-vie
- 1 pound and a half of sugar

## Directions

1. Put the orange blossoms, water, eau-de-vie, and sugar in a well-corked jug
2. Put this jug in a pot full of water and bring to a boil over the fire for ten hours
3. Remove from the fire and let it cool in the jug before passing it clear

title: Autre Sauce Piquante (Another Piquant Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a setier of white wine
- As much broth
- One shallot
- A little garlic
- A pinch of salad greens, finely chopped
- Butter about the size of a walnut
- A small pinch of flour
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Boil half a setier of white wine with as much broth
2. When reduced by half, add one shallot, a little garlic, a pinch of finely chopped salad greens
3. Boil for a moment and then add butter about the size of a walnut mixed with a small pinch of flour, salt and coarse pepper
4. Bind on the fire

title: Autres Rôties au Lard (Bacon Toast, Another Method)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound soft bread, long and stale
- Small bacon, cut into lardons

## Directions

1. Take a one-pound soft bread, long and stale
2. Cut off both ends
3. Lard the entire middle with small bacon cut into lardons
4. Cut the bread into slices the thickness of two coins
5. Dip these slices in beaten egg
6. Fry in oil that is not too hot at low heat until golden brown
7. Serve with clear sauce, a dash of vinegar, and some coarse pepper

title: Aux Grenouilles (Frog Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- frogs, about fifty
- butter, a good piece
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- bread crumb, tender, weighing a quarter pound
- milk or broth

## Directions

1. You cut the head of the frog; you pull the body from its skin, observing to remove the entrails; the thighs and the neck, which you will make disgorge, must be very clean; you need about fifty; you put them in a saucepan with a good piece of butter, salt, coarse pepper, a little nutmeg; you sauté your frogs on the fire; when they have been on a good fire for ten minutes, you then put them on a gentler heat for half an hour so that they finish cooking; you must drain them and put them cooked in a mortar; you add a piece of tender bread weighing a quarter pound; you soak it in milk or broth; you pound it all together; when you have pounded it for a quarter hour, and your frogs are well in paste, you put them in a saucepan, and you thin them with the juice they have rendered; you pass it all through a fine sieve; if this were not liquid enough, you will take broth that you intend for your soup; pass your purée to make it liquid, and put it on gentle heat; try that it does not boil: you will have bread crusts that you prepare as for bread soup; you will boil a little broth, which you will pour over your crusts a quarter hour before serving; and at the moment of serving your soup you will pour your frog purée over your bread; try that your soup is neither too thick nor too clear. This soup is made with fat as well as lean. (See Lean Broth.)

title: Barbue (Brill)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one brill
- salt water or court-bouillon
- butter sauce or plain water

## Directions

1. Remove the gills and entrails from the brill
2. Wash it
3. Cook it in salted water or court-bouillon for an entrée
4. Coat it with a butter sauce or serve it in plain water

title: Bass (Le Bar)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Bass
- White wine
- Butter
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onions
- Roots
- Parsley
- Chives (ciboule)
- Oil

## Directions

1. Gut and wash the bass
2. Cook in court-bouillon with white wine, butter, water, salt, pepper, onions, roots, parsley, and chives
3. When cooked and well drained, serve on a napkin garnished with fresh parsley

title: Bass as an Entrée (Le Bar en Entrée)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Bass
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Marinate for half an hour with a little oil, salt, and pepper
2. Cook on the grill
3. Baste from time to time with the oil remaining in the dish
4. When cooked, serve with a sauce of your choice

title: Beef Tongue Hochepot Style (Langue de Bœuf en hochepot)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- carrots
- espagnole sauce
- consommé
- sugar
- small glazed onions
- butter
- flour
- coarse pepper
- cooking liquid from the tongue

## Directions

1. Prepare the tongue and cook it as for the gherkin version
2. Cut it into slices and arrange in miroton style
3. Around the plate have carrots turned into small batons, which you will blanch
4. Refresh and drain them and put them in a pan
5. Add three spoonfuls of espagnole and five of consommé and a small piece of sugar
6. Cook them; at the time of serving, arrange the tongue and glaze it, and put the small roots in the middle around the plate
7. Make a border of small glazed onions; or if you have neither sauce nor consommé, after blanching the small roots, sauté them in a pan with a small piece of butter
8. Dust them with half a spoonful of flour
9. Pass the cooking liquid from the tongue through a silk sieve and moisten the small roots with it
10. The sauce should be thin so that you may reduce it
11. When cooking the small roots, take care to degrease them well and add a little coarse pepper

title: Beef Tongue Matelote Style (Langue de Bœuf en matelote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- lard
- carrots
- onions
- thyme
- bay leaf
- three cloves
- water
- salt
- bottle of white wine
- small onions
- butter
- flour
- coarse pepper
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Prepare the tongue as for the gherkin version, except do not lard it
2. Cook it in the same seasoning with a bottle of white wine
3. When the tongue is cooked, pass the cooking liquid through a silk sieve
4. Have small onions that you sauté in butter until they are brown
5. Dust them with a spoonful of flour and moisten with the degreased cooking liquid
6. Add a little coarse pepper and mushrooms
7. When the onions are cooked, remove them
8. If your sauce is too thin, reduce it; cut the tongue into pieces
9. Once the sauce is reduced, place it on top with the onions and mushrooms, and simmer the ragout for a quarter hour
10. Take care to degrease it and ensure it is well-salted

title: Beef Tongue with Chopped Herb Sauce (Langue de Bœuf sauce hachée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- chopped sauce or small roux
- shallots or chives
- parsley
- cooking liquid from the tongue
- vinegar
- coarse pepper
- capers
- pickled gherkins
- anchovy butter

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue as in the first recipe and prepare it the same way
2. Mask it with chopped sauce, or if you do not have any, make a small roux
3. Put your shallots or chives and chopped parsley into the roux
4. Moisten the roux with the cooking liquid from the tongue and a splash of vinegar
5. Add a little coarse pepper and ensure the sauce is somewhat thin so it may be reduced
6. Add chopped capers to the sauce
7. When ready to pour over the tongue, add two or three pickled gherkins cut small, about the size of a quarter of an egg's worth of anchovy butter, which you will let melt in the sauce
8. Mask the tongue with this sauce and ensure it is well-salted

title: Beef Tongue with Mushroom Purée (Langue à la purée de champignons)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- mushroom purée

## Directions

1. Prepare the tongue as for the gherkin version
2. Cut it into slices to arrange in miroton style
3. Put a mushroom purée in the middle of your border of tongue
4. Take care to glaze the tongue, whether whole or in pieces

title: Beef Tongue with Mushrooms (Langue de Bœuf aux champignons)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- mushrooms
- butter
- lemon juice
- espagnole sauce
- consommé
- cooking liquid from the tongue

## Directions

1. Prepare the tongue as for the gherkin version
2. Turn the mushrooms into a decorative shape
3. Sauté them in butter and lemon juice
4. Add four spoons of espagnole and three or four spoonfuls of consommé
5. Reduce the sauce in which the mushrooms are cooking
6. Cut the tongue in half and place it on your plate
7. Pour the mushroom ragout on top; or moisten the mushrooms with the cooking liquid from the tongue
8. Pass it through a silk sieve and take care to degrease it
9. If the sauce is thin, reduce it
10. If it were too salty, add a splash of lemon juice at the time of serving

title: Beef Tongue with Pickled Gherkin Sauce (Langue de Bœuf aux cornichons)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- lard
- carrots
- onions
- thyme
- bay leaf
- three cloves
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- coarse pepper
- pickled gherkins

## Directions

1. Prepare the tongue by larding it
2. Cook with lard, carrots, onions, thyme, bay leaf, and three cloves, moistening with water
3. Season the cooking liquid with salt, but not too much so it may be used for the sauce
4. Make a small brown roux and moisten it with the cooking liquid from the tongue
5. Add a splash of vinegar and coarse pepper to the sauce
6. Make the sauce somewhat thin so it may be reduced and take on good flavor
7. At the time of serving, place the tongue on the plate and mask it with the sauce in which you have put pickled gherkins
8. Ensure the sauce is well-salted and flavorful

title: Beef Tongue with Spinach (Langue de Bœuf aux épinards)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- spinach
- butter
- espagnole sauce or consommé
- coarse pepper
- flour
- cooking liquid from the tongue

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue as for the gherkin version
2. Cut it into slices so that you may arrange it in miroton style and glaze it
3. When the tongue is cut, make a border around the plate
4. Have spinach that you have sautéed in butter and moistened with espagnole or consommé
5. Pour the spinach into the middle of your plate or arrange the pieces of tongue on top of the spinach
6. You may also put the whole tongue; take care to glaze it
7. If lacking sauce, sauté the spinach in butter, add a little coarse pepper, and when well browned, sprinkle a good pinch of flour and mix with the spinach
8. Moisten the spinach with the cooking liquid from the tongue, passed through a silk sieve and well degreased
9. Moisten enough so that the spinach is somewhat liquid, so that you may reduce it and it becomes somewhat thick and takes on flavor
10. Do not add salt to the spinach, because the cooking liquid is already seasoned

title: Beignets d'Orange (Orange Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five Portuguese oranges
- sugar
- white wine
- flour
- good oil
- salt

## Directions

1. Take four or five Portuguese oranges
2. Remove the outer rind by turning them with a small knife to cut the rind the thickness of a small coin as you go
3. Cut the oranges into quarters to remove the pips
4. Cook them with a little sugar
5. Make a paste with white wine, flour, a spoonful of good oil, and a little salt
6. Mix this paste so it is neither too thin nor too thick, so it flows when poured with a spoon
7. Dip your orange quarters in it
8. Cook them in hot fat until it is a beautiful color
9. Serve them

title: Beignets de Blanc-mangé (Blanc-mange Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of rice
- two eggs
- one chopine of milk
- two ounces of sugar
- a pinch of grated green lemon
- candied orange flower, pralined and chopped
- a little salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan a quarter pound of rice that you dilute with two eggs and one chopine of milk, two ounces of sugar
2. Cook over the fire like a porridge, stirring constantly for two hours
3. When it is very thick, remove it from the fire, and add to it a pinch of grated green lemon and candied orange flower, pralined and chopped, a little salt
4. When everything is mixed, spread the cream on a floured plate, sprinkle flour over it
5. When it is cold, cut it into small pieces to roll in your hands, the size of a medium ball
6. Fry them in very hot fat, and when they are black remove them promptly to roll them in fine sugar

title: Beignets de Brioche (Brioche Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- small brioches of two liards
- cooked cream or preserves
- flour
- a little oil
- salt
- white wine
- sugar
- red-hot shovel or tongs

## Directions

1. Take small brioches of two liards and cut them in half
2. Remove the crumb and put in its place cooked cream or preserves
3. Rejoin the two halves together so they appear whole
4. Dip them in a paste made with flour, a little oil, salt, and mixed with white wine
5. Fry them to a beautiful color and glaze them with sugar using a red-hot shovel or tongs

title: Beignets de Feuilles de Vigne (Grape Leaf Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- grape leaves
- brandy
- frangipane cream
- flour
- white wine
- oil
- salt
- sugar
- red food coloring

## Directions

1. Soak grape leaves for one hour in brandy
2. Drain them, put a little frangipane cream inside and wrap them well
3. Dip them in a batter like the previous fritters and finish them the same way

title: Beignets de Fraise de Veau (Calf Sweetbread Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf sweetbread
- water
- salt
- parsley
- chives
- two cloves of garlic
- one bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- three cloves
- butter
- two spoonfuls of vinegar
- shallot
- coarse pepper
- egg
- bread crumb

## Directions

1. Cook a calf sweetbread with water, salt, a bouquet of parsley, chives, two cloves of garlic, one bay leaf, thyme, basil, and three cloves
2. When cooked, let it drain and degrease it
3. Cut into small bouquets and put it to marinate for one hour with a little butter, two spoonfuls of vinegar, chopped parsley, chives, shallot, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Warm the marinade
5. Remove all the small pieces of sweetbread and roll them as you go, making sure to keep the fine herbs attached
6. When they are cold, dip them in beaten egg, coat with bread crumb, and fry to a beautiful golden color

title: Beignets de Pain (Bread Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a septier of milk
- a little sugar
- a pinch of salt
- half a spoonful of orange flower water
- a pinch of chopped green lemon
- bread crumbs
- flour
- sugar
- red food coloring

## Directions

1. Boil half a septier of milk and reduce it by half with a little sugar, a pinch of salt, a half-spoonful of orange flower water, a pinch of chopped green lemon
2. Have bread crumbs cut the size of a small coin and much thicker
3. Put them in the milk to soak for a moment
4. When they are all soaked, drain them and flour them
5. Fry them, glaze with sugar and red food coloring

title: Beignets de Pain à Chanter (Singing Bread Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- twenty-four large singing breads
- cream of frangipane or preserves
- water
- flour
- white wine
- one spoonful of oil
- salt
- sugar
- red food coloring

## Directions

1. Take two dozen large singing breads; take one where you will put on it a little frangipane cream or preserves
2. Moisten the edge with water, and apply another singing bread on top
3. Pinch them all around to glue them together
4. When they are all made, dip them in a batter made with flour, white wine, a spoonful of oil and salt
5. Fry them and glaze with sugar and red food coloring

title: Beignets de Pomme et de Pêche (Apple and Peach Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- reinette apples
- peaches
- brandy
- sugar
- green lemon rind
- orange flower water
- flour

## Directions

1. Take reinette apples that you cut into four quarters, remove the skin and pips, pare them cleanly
2. Let them marinate for two or three hours with brandy, sugar, green lemon rind, and orange flower water
3. When they have absorbed the flavor well, put them to drain
4. Then put them in a white cloth with flour and shake them well inside so they take on flour
5. Fry them to a beautiful color and glaze them with sugar using a red-hot shovel or tongs
6. Peach fritters are made the same way
7. You can also make apple fritters in batter: hollow out the apple in the middle without breaking it to remove the pit
8. Peel and cut it into slices the thickness of a coin
9. Let them marinate as the previous ones
10. Dip them in batter and fry them as for brioche fritters
11. Serve them glazed with sugar

title: Beignets de Pommes (Apple Fritters)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Several apples
- Powdered sugar
- Brandy (eau-de-vie)
- Cinnamon powder
- Batter for frying (pâte à frire)
- Oil or fat for frying

## Directions

1. Remove the peel and core from several apples with an apple corer
2. Cut them into slices of equal size and thickness
3. Put them in a terrine with powdered sugar, brandy, and cinnamon powder
4. At the moment of serving, drain them
5. Put them in a frying batter
6. Place them in hot oil that is not too hot
7. When they have a nice color, if they were not cooked enough, remove the frying pan from the fire: it will finish cooking them
8. Drain them and put powdered sugar on top
9. With a hot shovel, glaze them and then arrange them on a plate

title: Beignets de Pâte (Pastry Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a litron of flour
- butter the size of an egg
- a good pinch of salt
- about half a glass of water
- frangipane cream
- sugar
- red food coloring

## Directions

1. Put on a table half a litron of flour, butter the size of an egg, a good pinch of salt, about half a glass of water
2. Knead the dough, then beat it very thin and cut it with a small pastry cutter
3. Put a little frangipane cream on each piece, cover with a top like the bottom, moisten the edges and glue them together by pinching all around
4. Fry until golden color, glaze the top with sugar and red food coloring

title: Beignets Mignons (Delicate Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two good spoonfuls of flour
- four eggs, whites and yolks
- a little salt
- two ounces of sugar
- one green lemon, grated
- half a spoonful of orange flower water
- half a septier of milk and half a septier of cream
- two spoonfuls of flour for the batter
- one spoonful of brandy
- a pinch of salt
- two eggs for the batter
- sugar
- red food coloring

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan two good spoonfuls of flour that you dilute with four eggs, whites and yolks, a little salt, two ounces of sugar, one grated green lemon, a half-spoonful of orange flower water, a half-septier of milk and half-septier of cream
2. Cook over a small fire stirring constantly
3. When it is cooked and very thick, spread it on a floured plate, dust the top with flour
4. When it is cold, cut it into pieces with a small pastry cutter
5. Dip each piece in a batter made as follows: put in a saucepan two spoonfuls of flour, one spoonful of brandy, a pinch of salt, dilute with two eggs
6. Fry and serve glazed with sugar and red food coloring

title: Beignets Soufflés, ou Pets et Petits Choux (Puffed Fritters, or Small Choux)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- butter the size of an egg
- two grated green lemons
- orange flower water, one full teaspoonful
- a quarteron of sugar
- a little salt
- a large half-setier of water
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan butter the size of an egg, two grated green lemons, one full teaspoonful of orange flower water, a quarteron of sugar, a little salt, and a large half-setier of water
2. Boil everything together for a moment
3. Add enough flour to make a well-bound and very thick paste
4. Stir it well on the fire with a wooden spoon until it sticks to the saucepan
5. Transfer it promptly to another saucepan and mix in two eggs at a time, stirring well with your wooden spoon
6. Continue adding two eggs at a time until your paste becomes soft without being runny
7. Spread it on a plate to the thickness of a finger with a knife
8. Heat some fat, not too hot
9. Dip the handle of a ragout spoon in the fat and take paste with it the size of a walnut
10. Drop it in the pan by striking the edge
11. Fry at a gentle fire, stirring constantly
12. When they are well-puffed and a beautiful color, serve them hot after dusting with fine sugar
13. Alternatively, make small mounds of paste the size of a walnut close together on white paper
14. Turn them into fat more than half-hot
15. When your fritters detach, remove the paper and fry them the same way
16. With this same paste, you can make small choux, with only the changes of putting more butter in the paste and cooking them in the oven

title: Beignets à la Crème (Cream Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a septier of cream
- half a septier of milk
- a little salt
- a pinch of finely chopped green lemon
- three large spoonfuls of flour
- cream
- sugar
- red food coloring

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan half a septier of cream, half a septier of milk, a little salt, a pinch of finely chopped green lemon
2. Boil and reduce by half
3. Then add three large spoonfuls of flour that you dilute on the fire with cream, and stir until it is very thick
4. Remove from the fire to put on the table
5. Beat it with the roller until it is thin like a small coin
6. Cut it into diamonds
7. Fry and glaze with sugar and red food coloring

title: Beignets à la Crème (Cream Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful of flour
- three whole eggs
- six egg yolks
- four crushed macarons
- candied orange flower
- roasted and ground praline
- a little diced candied lemon
- a half-setier of cream
- a half-setier of milk
- a good piece of sugar

## Directions

1. Take a handful of flour and mix it with three whole eggs and six egg yolks
2. Add four crushed macarons, candied orange flower, roasted and ground praline, a little diced candied lemon, a half-setier of cream, a half-setier of milk, and a good piece of sugar
3. Cook everything on the fire for a quarter hour until your cream becomes very thick
4. Put it to cool on a floured board and dust more flour on top after spreading it to the thickness of an inch
5. When it is cold, cut it into small pieces and round them in your hands with flour
6. Fry them in hot fat and dust with fine sugar when serving

title: Betteraves en Fricassée (Fricasseed Beets)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Beets
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Garlic
- Flour
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the beets in water or in the oven.
2. Put them in a casserole with butter, chopped parsley and chives, a little garlic, a pinch of flour, sufficient vinegar, salt and pepper.
3. Let them boil for a quarter of an hour.

title: Bifteck de Filet de Bœuf (Beef Filet Steak)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- filet (beef filet)
- sel (salt)
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)
- beurre tiède (warm butter)

## Directions

1. Cut your filet across its length so that your piece, thick with six lines, forms a round
2. Beat it and remove the edges, leaving no skin
3. Ensure your steak is somewhat fatty
4. When your piece is trimmed, season it with salt and coarse pepper
5. Dip it in warm butter
6. Grill it at the moment of serving

title: Bifteck de Filet de Bœuf (Beef Tenderloin Steak)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef filet
- salt
- coarse pepper
- warm butter
- maître-d'hôtel sauce or piquant sauce or reduced espagnole sauce or anchovy butter sauce or tomato sauce or clear jus
- cornichons or potatoes
- butter for sautéing potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut your filet on the full, that is to say your piece, thick of six lines, forms the round.
2. Beat it and remove the outer edges, and do not leave any skin.
3. Ensure your steak is somewhat fatty.
4. Once your piece is prepared, season it with salt and coarse pepper.
5. Dip it in warm butter.
6. Grill it at the moment of serving.
7. Place underneath a maître-d'hôtel, a piquant sauce, a reduced espagnole, an anchovy butter sauce, a tomato sauce, or a clear jus, according to taste.
8. Serve with these sauces either cornichons or potatoes that you peel and sauté in butter until they are golden.
9. Powder them with salt and arrange them around your steak.
10. Ensure your steak goes over a great fire; it must be cooked rare, that is to say it should be bleeding.

title: Biscuits d'Amandes (Almond Biscuits)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One quarteron of sweet almonds
- Fine sugar
- One ounce of flour
- Three egg yolks
- Four ounces of fine sugar
- Four egg whites
- Fine sugar mixed with half flour for topping

## Directions

1. Shell and pound the sweet almonds very finely in a mortar
2. To prevent them from turning to oil, add a pinch of fine sugar from time to time
3. Beat them for a quarter hour with one ounce of flour, three egg yolks, and four ounces of fine sugar
4. Whip four egg whites and mix with the rest of the mixture
5. You have paper molds made like boxes, square, two fingers in size
6. Lightly butter them inside and dress your biscuits there
7. Sprinkle over them fine sugar mixed with half flour
8. Bake in a very gentle oven
9. When they are a beautiful color, remove them from the paper while hot

title: Biscuits d'Amandes Amères (Bitter Almond Biscuits)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two ounces of bitter almonds
- One ounce of sweet almonds
- Fine sugar
- Flour
- Egg yolks and whites (proportional amounts)

## Directions

1. Make the same way as sweet almond biscuits, but for two ounces of bitter almonds, use one ounce of sweet almonds
2. Regulate the dose to make the quantity you wish, putting everything else in proportion as stated for sweet almonds

title: Biscuits de Chocolat (Chocolate Biscuits)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Six fresh eggs
- One and a half ounces of very finely crushed chocolate
- Six ounces of fine sugar
- Six ounces of flour

## Directions

1. Break four of the six eggs and put the yolks in one bowl and whites in another
2. Put with the yolks one and a half ounces of very finely crushed chocolate and six ounces of fine sugar
3. Beat everything together for more than a quarter hour
4. Add the whipped egg whites and mix well
5. Gradually add six ounces of flour, stirring constantly
6. Dress your biscuits the same way as those à la cuiller on white paper, or if you wish in small buttered paper molds
7. Sprinkle a little fine sugar over them to glaze and bake like the previous ones

title: Biscuits de Confitures (Jam Biscuits)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Candied lemon zest
- Orange blossoms, pralined
- Two spoonfuls of apricot marmalade
- Three ounces of fine sugar
- Four fresh egg yolks
- Four egg whites

## Directions

1. Pound in a mortar candied lemon zest with a good pinch of pralined orange blossoms
2. Add two spoonfuls of apricot marmalade, three ounces of fine sugar, and four fresh egg yolks, keeping the whites aside
3. Mix everything together and pass through a sieve, pressing with a spoon until nothing remains in the sieve
4. Add the four whipped egg whites and mix with the rest
5. Dress the biscuits lengthwise on white paper like those à la cuiller
6. Sprinkle a little fine sugar over them to glaze and bake in a gentle oven

title: Biscuits Légers (Light Biscuits)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Ten eggs
- Three quarterons of fine sugar
- Orange blossoms, pralined
- Green lemon zest, finely chopped
- Six ounces of flour

## Directions

1. Put the yolks of five eggs in a bowl with a little pralined orange blossoms and finely chopped green lemon zest
2. Add three quarterons of very fine sugar and beat everything together until the sugar is well mixed with the yolks and slightly liquid
3. Whip the whites of ten eggs until stiff peaks form and mix with the sugar
4. Add six ounces of flour gradually and gently, stirring as you go with a whisk
5. Dress them in buttered molds, sprinkle with fine sugar, and bake in a gentle oven

title: Biscuits Ordinaires (Plain Biscuits)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- Sugar equal in weight to the 8 eggs
- Flour equal in weight to 4 eggs
- A little finely chopped green lemon zest
- Butter for greasing the molds
- Fine sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Weigh 8 eggs on a balance and put equal weight of sugar on the other side
2. Weigh the flour using the weight of 4 eggs and set it aside on a plate
3. Crack the 8 eggs, separating the whites into one bowl and the yolks into another bowl with the sugar and a little finely chopped green lemon zest
4. Beat the yolks well with the sugar for half an hour
5. Whip the whites until well risen
6. Mix the whites with the yolks and sugar, then gradually and lightly mix in the flour, stirring your biscuit mixture constantly
7. Have tin or paper molds ready and well buttered inside with refined butter
8. Fill them slightly more than halfway
9. Sprinkle fine sugar on top
10. Bake in a gentle oven for half an hour
11. When they are a beautiful golden color, remove them from the molds when they are half-cold

title: Biscuits à la Cuiller (Ladyfinger Biscuits)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Four eggs (weight as measure)
- Sugar (weight equal to eggs)
- Flour (weight equal to eggs)
- Fine sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Make the batter the same way as the previous recipe, with the weight of four eggs being sufficient
2. Do not put them in molds
3. When the batter is made, take one spoonful for each biscuit and lay them lengthwise on sheets of white paper
4. Sprinkle fine sugar over them
5. Bake in a slightly cooler oven than the previous biscuits
6. When cooked, remove them immediately from the paper with a knife

title: Biscuits à la Liqueur (Liqueur Biscuits)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Five eggs
- Fine sugar (weight equal to five eggs)
- Flour (weight equal to sugar)
- Green lemon zest, finely chopped
- Pralined and chopped orange blossoms
- Fine sugar for glazing

## Directions

1. Place five eggs in a balance and weigh equal amount of fine sugar on the other side
2. Remove sugar and weigh equal amount of flour
3. Put sugar in a bowl with green lemon zest and pralined chopped orange blossoms
4. Add the five egg yolks and beat everything together until sugar is well mixed with yolks
5. Add flour and beat again together
6. Whip the egg whites that you have set apart until they form stiff peaks and mix with sugar and flour
7. Prepare two sheets of white paper folded lengthwise like a channel, the width and height of a finger
8. Put two spoonfuls of biscuit mixture in each channel
9. Sprinkle fine sugar over them
10. Bake in a gentle oven
11. When they are a beautiful color, remove them from paper and place on a sieve
12. Keep them in a dry place as long as you wish

title: Biscuits à la Saint Cloud (Saint Cloud Biscuits)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two ounces of rice flour sifted fine
- Half pound of fine sugar
- Four egg yolks
- Green lemon zest, finely chopped
- Eight egg whites

## Directions

1. Put two ounces of rice flour sifted fine in a bowl with half pound of fine sugar, four egg yolks, and a pinch of finely chopped green lemon zest
2. Beat everything together for a quarter hour
3. Add eight whipped egg whites
4. Dress your biscuits in small buttered paper molds
5. Bake in a gentle oven
6. When they are a beautiful golden color, remove them from the molds while hot
7. Cover the entire top with a glaze made as follows: put on an earthenware or faience plate (recipe continues on next page)

title: Bisque (Crayfish Bisque)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish, fifty
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- butter, a quarter pound
- rice
- broth or water
- bread crusts

## Directions

1. You will take fifty crayfish that you will wash in eight or ten waters; you will put them in a saucepan, adding salt, coarse pepper, a little grated nutmeg and a quarter pound of butter; you will put them on a fairly hot fire; you will sauté or stir them with a spoon for a quarter hour. When your crayfish are cooked, you will let them drain, and you will remove the meat that you will pound. You must make rice burst for a quarter hour in broth or water; you will drain it and put it in the mortar with your crayfish meat; when it is all well pounded, you will put it in a saucepan, and thin it with a little soup broth that you will pass through a fine sieve; when your purée is made, you will thin it with broth so that it is neither too thick nor too clear, then you will pound the crayfish shells; when they are well pounded, you will put juice in them, or the butter in which your crayfish were cooked, and you will pass this purée through a fine sieve. It should then have a red color. You will put it in a saucepan on gentle heat; try that neither one nor the other purées boil, but that they are very hot; you will have crusts from the top of the bread, that you will put in your soup tureen, and you will pour a little extremely hot broth over your bread before serving; then, at the moment of service, you will pour your first purée over your bread, and as for the one of shells, you will pour it over your soup with a large spoon, so that it has a beautiful color. This soup is made with fat as well as lean. (See Lean Broth.)

title: Blanc (Blanc Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen ounces of beef fat, cut into dice
- one pound of grated lard
- half a pound of butter
- carrots
- onions
- thyme
- three or four bay leaves
- two or three lemons, with the peel, seeds, and white removed
- water
- clarified salt water

## Directions

1. Cut the beef fat into dice, or larger depending on the size of your blanc
2. Cut the carrots, onions, and sliced lemons, and put them with your fat
3. Fill a large cooking spoon with water
4. Bring to a boil until the contents of your pot froth a little and your fat is melted
5. Stir your blanc with a wooden spoon
6. Do not let it take color
7. Moisten it with water
8. Add clarified salt water to season it
9. Let your blanc boil, skim it, and use it for the purposes indicated

title: Blanc (Blanc Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of grated lard
- One pound of fat
- Half a pound of butter
- Two lemons, cut in slices without the white
- Two bay leaves
- Two cloves
- Four carrots, cut in dice
- Four onions
- One pot spoonful of water

## Directions

1. Put one pound of grated lard, one pound of fat, half a pound of butter, two lemons cut in slices with the white removed, two bay leaves, two cloves, four carrots cut in dice, four onions, and one pot spoonful of water together
2. Boil until reduced
