3. When it is a nice color, add small onions cut into quarters and cook them halfway in the same roux, adding a little more butter.
4. Moisten with equal parts red wine and lean broth.
5. Pour the onions and their sauce into the casserole with the prepared fish and season with salt, pepper, and a bouquet garni of fine herbs.
6. Cook the matelote over high heat for half an hour.
7. When ready to serve, add some bread croutons to the sauce and serve with the matelote.

title: Carpe en Matelotte (Carp in Matelotte)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- other fish such as pike, eel, crayfish, barbel, or river fish
- butter
- flour
- small onions

## Directions

1. Scale the carp and remove the gills, cut it into pieces
2. Put it in a saucepan with other fish such as pike, eel, crayfish, barbel, or whatever river fish you have available
3. In another saucepan make a small roux with butter and one spoonful of flour
4. When it is a nice color, add small onions cut into quarters and cook them halfway in this same roux, adding a little more butter

title: Carpe Frite (Fried Carp)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- flour
- oil for frying

## Directions

1. Scale and gut a carp; split it down the back so that the head is also separated; your carp should only hold together at the belly
2. Give several knife blows to the large bone so that your carp has a firm, flat appearance
3. Dust it with flour, as well as its milt or eggs, and put it in well-heated oil for frying

title: Carpe Grillée Sauce aux Câpres (Grilled Carp with Caper Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- Parsley
- Scallion
- Salt
- Pepper
- Oil
- Caper sauce

## Directions

1. After cleaning and thoroughly scaling your carp, score it and put it on a plate with parsley, scallion, salt, pepper, and oil
2. Three-quarters of an hour before serving, place it on the grill over moderately hot fire
3. When grilled, mask it with caper sauce

title: Carpe Grillée Sauce aux Câpres (Grilled Carp with Caper Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- Parsley
- Chives
- Salt
- Pepper
- Oil
- Caper sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and scale the carp well
2. Cut it into pieces and place on a plat with parsley, chives, salt, pepper and oil
3. Three-quarters of an hour before serving, place it on the grill over a fairly hot fire
4. When grilled, coat it with caper sauce

title: Carpe à l'Allemande (Carp German-Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Quatre épices
- Onion slices
- Beer

## Directions

1. Cut the carp into pieces after washing it, without cleaning the insides or removing the gills
2. Remove only the large intestine
3. Place the pieces in a casserole or cooking pot with salt, coarse pepper, quatre épices, onion slices and one or two bottles of beer
4. The fish must be submerged in the sauce
5. Place the casserole over high heat
6. Reduce the sauce until only about one glass remains
7. Serve the carp with its broth without thickening

title: Carpe à la Bourgeoise (Carp Bourgeoise Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- butter
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- white wine

## Directions

1. Cut the carp into sections and place in a casserole with butter, parsley, chives, and mushrooms, all chopped, and one chopine of white wine.
2. When cooked, serve with good taste and short sauce.

title: Carpe à la Chambord (Carp Chambord-Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp
- Fish roe (laitance)
- Lard
- White wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Onion slices
- Cloves
- Lard strips
- Carp quenelles or pigeons à la Gautier
- Glazed veal sweetbreads
- Glazed veal knuckles
- Crayfish
- Foie gras
- Truffles
- Rooster crests
- Rooster kidneys
- Espagnole sauce

## Directions

1. Clean the carp carefully, avoiding damaging it
2. Fill its body with fish roe
3. Remove the scales and skin superficially on one side
4. Lard that side from the gill to the tail
5. Place it in a fish kettle
6. Moisten with white wine and add salt, pepper, parsley leaves, six bay leaves, thyme, onion slices and cloves
7. Cover the head with lard strips and place in the oven, or cover the fish kettle with parchment paper, place fire on top and simmer for a good hour
8. At the moment of serving, drain it and place on a plat
9. Arrange around it carp quenelles or pigeons à la Gautier, glazed veal sweetbreads, glazed veal knuckles, crayfish, poultry foie gras, truffles, rooster crests and kidneys
10. If the cooking liquid is not too seasoned, pass it through silk sieve
11. In a saucepan put two spoonfuls of espagnole and two spoonfuls of the carp cooking liquid
12. Reduce the sauce by half
13. Add the small garnishes and pour the sauce around the carp after glazing it

title: Carré de Mouton (Rack of Mutton)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- rack of mutton
- fresh butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut into cutlets and grill
2. Dip the cutlets in fresh melted butter, salt, pepper, finely chopped parsley, chives, and mushrooms
3. Bread the cutlets with bread crumbs
4. Cook on the grill
5. While cooking, baste with a little butter so they do not become too dry
6. When cooked, serve dry

title: Carré de Mouton au Persil (Lamb Square with Parsley)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb square
- parsley branches
- green herbs
- lard fat
- jus
- shallots
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cut a lamb square neatly, removing the skins found on the filets.
2. Lard the entire square with parsley branches and green herbs.
3. Cook it on the spit.
4. When the parsley is well dried, have hot lard fat and baste it with this fat.
5. Continue to baste it from time to time until the square is cooked.
6. Put a little jus in a saucepan with some chopped shallots, salt, and coarse pepper.
7. Heat and serve under the lamb square.

title: Carré de Mouton en Crépine (Lamb Square in Caul)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- ten or fifteen onions
- butter
- lamb square
- bouillon
- salt
- coarse pepper
- caul
- egg yolks
- egg
- bread crumbs
- olive oil or fat
- white wine
- jus

## Directions

1. Cut into slices about ten or fifteen onions, depending on their size.
2. Pass them on the fire with a piece of butter, stirring often with a spoon, until they are completely cooked.
3. Cut a lamb square into cutlets and cook them on low heat with bouillon, a little salt, and coarse pepper.
4. When they are cooked, reduce the sauce until it clings entirely to the cutlets and remove them on a plate.
5. Drain the fat in the saucepan and put half a glass of bouillon in the same saucepan to detach what sticks to it.
6. Pass this small sauce through a sieve to mix with the onions and three egg yolks.
7. Bind them on the fire without boiling, for fear they turn.
8. Take caul that you have soaked in water; press it well to divide it into as many pieces as you have cutlets.
9. Put on each piece of caul one cutlet with the onion all around the cutlet; wrap everything with caul and seal all the edges with beaten egg.
10. Then dip them everywhere in beaten egg to coat them with bread crumbs.
11. Arrange them on a plate that can go in the fire.
12. Baste all the top with good fat or olive oil.
13. Put them in the oven or under a pastry lid cover until they have taken on a beautiful golden color and remove them on a cloth to drain them of their fat.
14. Serve with a sauce made as follows: Put in a saucepan half a glass of white wine, as much bouillon, a little jus, salt, and coarse pepper.
15. Boil and reduce by half; serve under the cutlets.

title: Carré de Mouton en Terrine à l'Anglaise aux Lentilles (Lamb Square in Terrine English Style with Lentils)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb square
- good bouillon
- salt
- bouquet garni
- one liter of lentils
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the lamb square into cutlets and cook them with good bouillon, very little salt, and a bouquet garni.
2. Cook one liter of lentils à la reine with bouillon.
3. When the lentils are cooked, pass them through a sieve to make a purée.
4. Mix the lentil purée with the cooked lamb cutlets and their seasonings.
5. If the sauce is too thin, reduce it on the fire.
6. Take a clean terrine suitable for serving at table and capable of withstanding fire.
7. Put the cutlets inside with half of the sauce and cover with bread crumbs toasted on one side.
8. Put the terrine in the oven and let it bubble for one hour.
9. When ready to serve, add the remaining sauce inside.

title: Carré de Mouton en Terrine à l'Anglaise, aux Lentilles (Rack of Mutton in Terrine English Style, with Lentils)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- rack of mutton
- good bouillon
- salt
- bouquet garni
- lentils

## Directions

1. Cut the rack into cutlets
2. Cook them with good bouillon, very little salt, and a bouquet garni
3. Also cook a liter of lentils à la reine with bouillon
4. When the lentils are cooked, pass them through as a purée
5. Mix this lentil purée with the cooked mutton cutlets and their seasonings
6. If the coulis is too thin, reduce it over fire

title: Carré de Mouton à la Conti (Lamb Square Conti Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb square
- quarter pound of well-larded bacon
- two anchovies
- coarse pepper
- two shallots
- parsley
- chives
- half bay leaf
- three or four basil leaves
- three or four tarragon leaves
- one glass of white wine
- bouillon
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Prepare a lamb square by removing the skins found on the filet.
2. Take a quarter pound of well-larded bacon and two washed anchovies; cut them into larding strips and mix with a little coarse pepper, two shallots, parsley, and chives, all chopped, half a bay leaf, three or four basil leaves chopped like powder, and three or four tarragon leaves also chopped.
3. Lard the entire filet with the bacon and anchovies.
4. Put the square with all these fine herbs in a saucepan.
5. Moisten with one glass of white wine and an equal amount of bouillon.
6. Cook on low heat.
7. When it is cooked, degrease the sauce and add a knob of butter the size of a walnut mixed with a pinch of flour.
8. Bind the sauce on the fire and serve it over the lamb square.

title: Carré de Veau (Veal Rack)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal rack
- Small skewers

## Directions

1. Trim your rack so there are no bones along your filet
2. Pass small skewers from the end of the rib to the thick part of the filet
3. Spit it with a large skewer and lay it on the spit so your filet does not have a spit hole

title: Carré de Veau Piqué Glacé aux Concombres (Larded Glazed Veal Square with Cucumbers)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Whole square of veal from the rib near the kidney to the second rib near the collar
- Fine lard for larding
- Lard strips
- Slices of veal
- Carrots
- Onions
- Laurel leaves
- Parsley
- Chives
- Cloves
- Consommé or bouillon
- Salt
- Paper
- Butter

## Directions

1. Take a whole square of veal from the rib near the kidney to the second rib near the collar.
2. Trim and cut it as squarely as possible.
3. Remove the bones that are under the fillet to the end of the rib of the chapelet.
4. Uncover the fillet, that is to say lift the skins that cover it.
5. Remove the nerves that are on it by introducing your knife between the nerve and the flesh, so that your fillet is well uncovered and no skin remains on it.
6. Then lard it with fine lard.
7. Fasten the skins covering the ribs with a trussing needle and string.
8. When your square is larded and trussed, place strips of lard in a small braising pan, slices of veal, two or three carrots, four onions, two laurel leaves, a bouquet of parsley and chives, two cloves.
9. Place your square over this seasoning so that your larded fillet can receive the heat of the fire on top of your braising pan.
10. Cover the skins covering the ribs with strips of lard.
11. Have a sheet of paper folded in double and buttered, which you place on your square.
12. Add two spoonfuls of consommé or bouillon.
13. Add a little salt.
14. Cook on low heat for three hours.
15. Be careful to put fire on top of the lid to glaze your fillet.
16. At the moment of serving, drain your square and untie it, that is to say remove the string.

title: Carré de Veau à la Bourgeoise (Veal Rack in the Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of lard, cut into lardons
- parsley
- chives
- a small pinch of garlic
- one bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- salt
- coarse pepper
- a veal rack fillet

## Directions

1. Cut half a pound of lard into lardons and mix them with parsley, chives, a small pinch of garlic, all chopped, one bay leaf, thyme, basil chopped like powder, salt, and coarse pepper.
2. Lard the fillet of a veal rack with all of this after having cut the bones that are at the base.

title: Carré de Veau à la Bourgeoise (Veal Square Prepared Bourgeoise Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of lard
- parsley
- ciboule (chives)
- a small clove of garlic
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- salt
- coarse pepper
- veal square fillet
- lard bard for the bottom
- onion slices
- carrot and parsnip zests
- one glass of bouillon
- three spoonfuls of brandy
- water

## Directions

1. Cut the half pound of lard into lardons and mix them with parsley, ciboule, a small clove of garlic, all chopped, a bay leaf, thyme, and basil chopped to a powder, salt, and coarse pepper
2. Lard with this mixture the fillet of a veal square, after having cut the bones at the bottom of the fillet
3. Place it in a terrine or small pot with a bard of lard in the bottom and a few slices of onion; zests of carrots and parsnips
4. Make it sweat for half an hour over a small fire
5. Then moisten it with one glass of bouillon and three spoonfuls of brandy; cook it over a small fire
6. When the cooking is done and the sauce is short, degrease it to serve over the square; if you want this square cold, in the manner of beef à la mode, arrange it on the plate with the sauce on top without degrassing it, and let it cool

title: Carré de Veau à la Broche aux Fines Herbes (Veal Square Roasted on the Spit with Fine Herbs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- veal square fillet
- parsley
- ciboule (chives)
- a bit of fennel
- mushrooms
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- two shallots
- salt
- coarse pepper
- grated nutmeg
- a bit of oil
- white paper
- butter
- a bit of juice
- two spoonfuls of verjuice
- butter kneaded with a pinch of flour
- egg yolk
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Lard the entire fillet of a veal square after having trimmed it properly
2. Place it in a terrine to marinate for three hours with parsley, ciboule, a bit of fennel, mushrooms, a bay leaf, thyme, basil, two shallots, all chopped very fine, salt, coarse pepper, grated nutmeg, and a bit of oil
3. When it has taken on flavor, spit the square, put all its seasoning on top, and wrap it in two sheets of white paper well buttered
4. Tie it so that the small herbs cannot escape, and cook it over a small fire
5. When cooking is done, remove the paper and scrape off with a knife all the small herbs that cling to the paper and the meat, to put them in a saucepan with a bit of juice, two spoonfuls of verjuice, butter kneaded with a pinch of flour, a bit of salt, and coarse pepper
6. Make the sauce bind over the fire to serve under the square
7. Before binding the sauce, melt a bit of butter and mix in an egg yolk to rub on top of the square and bread it with breadcrumbs, and brown it nicely; you can also serve it without being breaded if you are indifferent about appearance

title: Carré de Veau à la Crème (Veal Square with Cream)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One veal square
- Salt
- Nutmeg, grated
- False Béchamel

## Directions

1. Remove the bones and nerves that are under the fillet
2. Soak it in milk for twenty-four hours
3. At the moment of putting on the spit, remove it from the milk and wipe it dry
4. Dust it with fine salt in which you have mixed a little grated nutmeg
5. Spit it with a large lardoon and place it on the spit for two and a half hours
6. Before serving, place it near the fire
7. Instead of basting it with the fat from the dripping pan, have a false Béchamel ready and baste it with this
8. At the moment of serving, remove it from the spit
9. Reserve some false Béchamel in which you put a piece of glace about the size of a walnut, a little coarse pepper, a little nutmeg
10. Pour this sauce underneath; it should not be too thick

title: Carré et Gigot de Mouton aux Concombres (Lamb Square and Leg with Cucumbers)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- mortified lamb square
- fine lard
- two or three cucumbers
- vinegar
- salt
- butter
- ham slice
- flour
- jus
- bouillon
- coulis

## Directions

1. Have a mortified lamb square that you trim neatly, that is to say, remove the skin and nerves found on the filet, and cut the bones at the base of the ribs.
2. Lard the top of the filet with fine lard; you may also put it on the spit without being larded, but this method is more common.
3. Take two or three cucumbers that you peel, hollow out, and cut into dice.
4. Let them marinate for two hours with a small spoonful of vinegar and a little salt.
5. Then press them with your clean hands to remove all the water and put them progressively in a saucepan with a piece of butter and a slice of ham.
6. Pass them on the fire, turning them often with a spoon, until they begin to color, then add a pinch of flour and moisten them with half jus and half bouillon.
7. If you have no jus, blanch the cucumbers before passing them.
8. Cook on low heat and degrease them when they are cooked.
9. Add a little coulis to bind them, and if you have no coulis, add a little more flour before moistening them.
10. When your ragout is finished, serve it under the lamb square.
11. If you wish to serve a ragout of lamb dishes with cucumbers, cut the cucumbers into very thin slices and let them marinate and cook in the same fashion as above.
12. When the ragout is finished and tastes good, take a lamb leg cooked on the spit that has been removed from the table, cut it into very thin filets, and warm it in the ragout without letting it boil.
13. Do the same with the leftovers of a lamb square and a lamb shoulder, and indeed all sorts of meats that have been cooked on the spit.

title: Casserole (Rice Casserole)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of rice
- bouillon
- melted bacon
- any cooked meats of your choice

## Directions

1. Cook the rice three-quarters done in a small pot with bouillon and melted bacon
2. When it is nearly cooked, thick, and very fatty, spread a layer about two coins thick in the bottom of your serving dish, which should be silver or ovenproof earthenware
3. Place your chosen cooked meat or meats on top of the rice
4. Cover completely with rice so the meat is not visible
5. Smooth with a knife
6. Place your dish on hot ashes, cover with a pie dish cover and a good fire on top
7. Leave until the rice is a beautiful golden color
8. When serving, drain off any fat and serve dry, or if you wish, add a little sauce in the bottom

title: Cerises à l'Eau-de-vie (Cherries in Brandy)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful, healthy cherries, not too ripe
- A quarter pound of sugar for every pound of cherries
- One pint of brandy
- A few cloves
- A little cinnamon wood
- Brandy at 22 degrees

## Directions

1. Take beautiful, healthy cherries that are not too ripe and cut the stems halfway off
2. Put them in a jar with a few cloves and a little cinnamon wood
3. Clarify a quarter pound of sugar for every pound of cherries and one pint of brandy
4. When the sugar reaches the hard-crack stage, pour brandy at 22 degrees into it
5. Mix the syrup with the brandy; when it is cold, pour it over the cherries
6. Seal with a cork disk and wet parchment that you tie well

title: Cervelas (Seasoned Sausage)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork meat, the tenderest and most interlarded, or veal, hare, or rabbit well supplied with lard according to desired quantity
- Parsley, chopped
- Ciboules (chives), chopped
- Salt
- Mixed spices
- Intestines (boyaux) of appropriate thickness

## Directions

1. Chop the meat and mix with parsley, chopped chives, salt, and mixed spices
2. Take intestines of whatever thickness you judge appropriate, fill them with meat, and tie them at both ends
3. Put them to smoke in the chimney for two days
4. Cook them for two or three hours, according to their thickness, in broth without salt

title: Cervelas à l'Oignon (Onion Sausage)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork meat, the tenderest and most interlarded, or veal, hare, or rabbit well supplied with lard
- Onions according to the quantity of meat
- Melted lard or rendered pork belly fat (sain-doux)
- Parsley, chopped
- Ciboules (chives), chopped
- Salt
- Mixed spices
- Intestines (boyaux)

## Directions

1. Chop the meat and mix with parsley, chopped chives, salt, and mixed spices
2. Take onions according to the quantity of meat you have, chop them and cook with melted lard or rendered pork belly fat
3. When cooked three-quarters, mix them with the meat and finish your sausages as described above
4. Take intestines of appropriate thickness, fill them with the mixture, and tie them at both ends
5. Put them to smoke in the chimney for two days
6. Cook them for two or three hours according to their thickness in broth without salt

title: Cervelats (Smoked Sausages)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- casings of desired thickness
- meat
- onions (optional)
- melted lard or lard fat (for onion version)
- truffles (optional)

## Directions

1. Fill casings of desired thickness with meat and tie them at both ends
2. Smoke them in a smokehouse for two days
3. Cook them for two to three hours, depending on their thickness, in unsalted broth
4. For cervelats with onion: chop onions according to the quantity of meat you have, cook them with melted lard or lard fat, when they are three-quarters cooked, add them to the meat and finish the cervelats as described above
5. For cervelats with truffles: chop the meat and add chopped truffles without cooking them, finish the cervelats the same way

title: Cervelle de Bœuf de Plusieurs Façons (Beef Brains in Several Ways)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brains
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- bouquet garni
- small onions
- root vegetables
- vinegar
- butter
- flour
- garlic
- parsley
- chives
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil

## Directions

1. Braise the brains with white wine, salt, pepper, and a bouquet garni
2. When cooked, remove from the braise and serve with an appetizing small sauce from chapter XV, or with a ragout of small onions and root vegetables
3. Alternatively, marinate with salt, pepper, vinegar, a piece of butter mixed with flour, garlic, parsley, chives, thyme, bay leaf, and basil
4. Drain and flour the brains, then fry them
5. Serve garnished with fried parsley

title: Cervelle de Veau au Soleil (Fried Calf's Brain)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two calf's brains
- bouillon
- two or three tablespoons of white vinegar
- bouquet of parsley
- chives
- one garlic clove
- three cloves
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- two handfuls of flour
- one tablespoon of oil
- half a septier of white wine
- fine salt
- lard

## Directions

1. Soak two calf's brains in warm water to disgorge
2. Cook them with a little bouillon, two or three tablespoons of white vinegar, a bouquet of parsley, chives, one garlic clove, three cloves, thyme, bay leaf and basil
3. When cooked, cut each piece of brain in two and dip them in a batter made with two handfuls of flour diluted with one tablespoon of oil, half a septier of white wine and fine salt
4. Fry them in lard until they are a beautiful golden color and the batter is crispy
5. Serve hot

title: Cervelle de Veau en Matelotte (Calf's Brain in Wine Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two calf's brains
- white wine
- bouillon
- salt
- pepper
- bouquet garni
- small onions
- carrots
- coulis
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take two calf's brains and soak them in water to disgorge
2. Cook them with white wine, bouillon, salt, pepper and a bouquet garni
3. Make a ragout of small onions and carrots which you cook with bouillon and a bouquet garni, seasoned well and bound with coulis
4. Serve the ragout around the brains
5. Alternatively serve them in the same way with different ragouts as an entrée, or serve them as a cold side dish when marinated
6. Fry them and serve with fried parsley

title: Cervelles de Bœuf au Beurre Noir (Beef Brains with Black Butter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brains
- lard
- bay leaves
- onion slices
- carrots
- parsley and chives bouquet
- white wine
- bouillon
- black butter
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the brains by removing coagulated blood, thin skin, and fibers
2. Soak in lukewarm water for two hours
3. Cook between lard, two bay leaves, onion slices, carrots, parsley and chives bouquet, white wine, and bouillon
4. Simmer for half an hour over the fire
5. Drain the brains
6. Put black butter underneath and fried parsley in the middle
7. Alternatively, the brains may be cooked with carrots, onions, thyme, bay leaf, parsley, chives, a dash of vinegar, salt, and water

title: Cervelles de Bœuf en Marinade (Beef Brains in Marinade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brains
- marinade
- frying batter
- fried parsley
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare the brains by cleaning and blanching them
2. Cook them in a marinade
3. Make a frying batter
4. At serving time, fry them
5. Ensure your frying oil is not too hot
6. Put fried parsley on top
7. If the brains are fully cooked, cut them into pieces
8. Season with salt, pepper, and enough vinegar so they are submerged in it
9. Drain them
10. Put them in your batter and fry them

title: Cervelles de Bœuf en Matelote (Beef Brains in Matelote Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brains
- red or white wine
- small onions
- butter
- flour
- mushrooms
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the brains as in the previous recipe
2. Instead of water and vinegar, moisten them with red or white wine
3. When cooked, strain the cooking liquid through silk sieve
4. Clean and peel small onions, sauté them in butter until golden
5. Dust with flour, about one spoonful
6. Moisten with the wine in which the brains cooked
7. Add some mushrooms
8. When the ragout is cooked, drain the brains and arrange on plate
9. Pour the ragout over them; ensure good seasoning

title: Cervelles à la Sauce Piquante (Beef Brains with Piquant Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brains
- piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the brains as for black butter
2. Drain them and place on plate
3. Pour piquant sauce over them

title: Champignons à la Crème (Mushrooms in Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Flour
- Hot water
- Salt
- Sugar
- Egg yolks
- Cream
- Bread crust

## Directions

1. Put the mushrooms in a casserole with a piece of butter, a bouquet of parsley and chives.
2. When they have heated through, add a pinch of flour and moisten with hot water, a little salt and a little sugar.
3. When they are cooked and there is no more sauce left, add a liaison of egg yolks and cream.
4. Fry a bread crust in butter and place it in the bottom of the dish you will serve.
5. Place your ragout on top of the bread crust.

title: Chapon au Riz (Capon with Rice)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One capon
- Bouillon
- Two carrots
- Two onions
- Two cloves
- Half a pound of rice, washed five or six times
- Coarse pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. After emptying and cleaning the capon, tuck the legs inside, then lard it lightly
2. Truss it with a large needle as if quilting, securing the thighs and legs so they do not spread while cooking
3. Put your capon in a casserole
4. Fill it with bouillon
5. Skim your poultry, then add two carrots, two onions, two cloves, and half a pound of washed rice
6. Boil everything on low heat for two hours
7. Untruss your poultry and put it in your tureen
8. Pour your rice over it; do not include the carrots or onions
9. Season your soup well with salt and add a little coarse pepper

title: Chapon poêlé (Braised Capon)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Capon
- Lard
- Lemon slices
- Carrots
- Onions
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Parsley
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. When your capon is plucked and lightly singed, pluck it further and empty it
2. Lay the legs on the thighs and truss it with needle and string, passing the string across to join the other thigh, securing the legs and breast
3. Place lard strips in a saucepan with the capon on top
4. Cover it with very thin slices of lemon and cover with lard strips and a braising lid on top
5. A short hour is sufficient to cook it
6. If you do not have a braising lid, add some carrots, onions, thyme, bay leaf, and a bouquet of parsley, and moisten with bouillon
7. Serve under your capon with consommé, clear jus, hot aspic, crayfish butter, tomato sauce, suprême, bound velouté, sautéed mushrooms, or a ragout of crests and kidneys, as you prefer

title: Charbonnée de Bœuf en Papillote (Beef Rib in Paper)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A charbonnée or beef rib
- Bouillon or water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Oil or butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Shallots
- Mushrooms
- Basil powder
- White paper

## Directions

1. Take a beef rib, cut it neatly and cook it on a low fire with bouillon or water, a little salt and pepper
2. When cooked, reduce the sauce so it sticks entirely to the rib
3. Then marinate it with oil or butter, parsley, chives, shallots, and mushrooms, all chopped very finely, with a little basil powder
4. Put the rib in a sheet of white paper with all its marinade
5. Fold the paper like a papillote and grease it on the outside
6. Put it on a grill with a greased sheet of paper underneath
7. Grill on a low fire on both sides
8. Serve with the paper

title: Charlotte de Pommes (Apple Charlotte)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Several apples
- Sugar
- Cinnamon powder
- Breadcrumbs (mie de pain)
- Butter
- Apricot marmalade

## Directions

1. Remove the peel and core from several apples
2. Cut them into small pieces
3. Add sugar in proportion and a little cinnamon powder
4. When they become marmalade, reduce them well so no water remains
5. Let it cool
6. Cut breadcrumbs into very thin and equal thickness slices
7. Dip them in lukewarm butter and arrange them symmetrically in a mold
8. Add the apple marmalade to it
9. Leave a hole in the middle to put apricot marmalade
10. Cover the charlotte with breadcrumbs dipped in butter
11. Place it in a hot oven or in red embers with a pot lid and fire on top
12. Alternatively, mince the apples and put them in a saucepan with powdered sugar and cinnamon powder
13. Let them sit on the fire for a quarter hour
14. When they are slightly melted, let them drain on a sieve until slightly cold
15. Put a layer of apples, then a layer of apricot marmalade, and continue until the mold is full
16. Cook the charlotte the same way and unmold it onto the plate at the moment of serving

title: Chartreuse (Vegetable and Lettuce Mold)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- roots for vegetables
- lettuce
- cooking liquid from roots
- root bouillon
- demi-glace
- meat glaze

## Directions

1. Prepare roots and cook them as described for entrées.
2. Dress them the same way, or you can change the design.
3. Instead of putting meat inside, use lettuces that you have carefully drained and pressed to extract all liquid.
4. When your mold is full, keep it hot in a bain-marie.
5. Take the cooking liquid in which your roots have cooked.
6. Add root bouillon to it.
7. When it boils, pass it through a fine cloth so it is clear.
8. Reduce it to a demi-glace and pour it over the chartreuse that you have placed on your dish.
9. If your demi-glace is not sufficiently thick, add a little well-blonde meat glaze to it.

title: Chartreuse de Pommes (Apple Chartreuse)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Several apples
- Water
- Three lemon juices
- Three quarterons of clarified sugar
- One lemon juice
- Apricot marmalade
- Candied fruits (optional)
- Saffron (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the peel from several apples
2. Put water in a terrine with three lemon juices
3. Use a small tin mold to cut your apples into small sticks
4. Put them in the water, and the rest of the apples as well, so they do not blacken
5. Clarify three quarterons of sugar in which you press the juice of one lemon
6. Put the small sticks in it
7. Let them boil once or twice, they should only be softened
8. Remove them from the sugar and drain them on a sieve
9. Cut the apple scraps into small slices and put them in the sugar
10. When softened, remove them or let them reduce to marmalade
11. Arrange symmetrically the small apple sticks around a lightly buttered mold
12. Place some in the bottom
13. Put apple marmalade on top, apricot marmalade inside
14. The mold must be full so it can be unmolded and your chartreuse holds together without the decoration falling apart
15. You can add candied cherries, verjuice, basil, citron peel, etc. for decoration
16. If you do not use a mold, put a mass of marmalade on a plate, give it a round or square shape, arrange the apple sticks and candied fruits with symmetry
17. You can reduce the syrup, put it on a plate, when it is set, you can use it to mask your chartreuse

title: Chervis Frits (Fried Skirret)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Skirret
- Water
- Salt
- Flour
- White wine
- Oil

## Directions

1. Cook the skirret with water and salt (quarter hour cooking time is sufficient)
2. Drain and let cool
3. Make a batter with flour, white wine, a spoonful of oil and salt, not too thin (it should fall in streams when held in the air on a spoon)
4. Dip the skirret in this batter
5. Fry the skirret after dipping in the batter
6. Serve as an entremetс

title: Chicory Preserved for Winter
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Salt
- Chicory
- Brine
- Clarified butter
- Parchment or paper

## Directions

1. Fill a large pot with boiling water and add two large handfuls of salt
2. When the water boils, add well-washed and drained chicory, pushing it down into the boiling water so it does not blacken
3. After boiling for ten minutes, remove the chicory and place it in cold water
4. When well cooled, remove and press it in your hands to extract the water
5. Arrange it in jars or pots
6. Make a brine and when it is cold and clear, pour it over the chicory
7. Cover with clarified butter, and when frozen, seal the pot with parchment or paper
8. Alternatively, when chicorries are trimmed, washed and well-drained, make a layer in a pot or tub, sprinkle salt on top, and repeat until full, then cover with a wooden round that fits inside the tub and place a stone on top
9. To use, soak in fountain water for two hours, then place in a pot with cold water and cook, testing from time to time until it yields under the fingers

title: Chicorée au Velouté (Chicory in Velvet Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- chicory
- salt
- butter
- pepper
- velouté sauce
- consommé
- croutons

## Directions

1. Remove the outer part of the chicory so that only the white remains
2. Cut the head and place the chicory split in two in water to wash
3. Have a pot of boiling water with a handful of salt; place the chicory in it
4. Push it down in the water from time to time so it does not blacken
5. When you see it mixes with the water, it is sufficiently blanched
6. Test with your fingers; if it is soft, drain it in a colander and place it in fresh water
7. When well cooled, drain it again and squeeze it in your hands to extract the water
8. Then chop it
9. Put a good piece of butter in a pot with the chicory, a little salt, and coarse pepper
10. Stir it often and pour five tablespoons of velouté sauce and the same of consommé over it
11. Let it reduce until somewhat thick
12. Then arrange it on the platter with croutons around it

title: Chicorée Blanche Ordinaire (Ordinary White Chicory)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- white chicory
- butter
- bouillon
- coulis
- flour for roux
- salt
- pepper
- shallot

## Directions

1. Peel and wash the chicory
2. Boil it for half an hour in water
3. Remove it and place in fresh water to press well
4. Put it to cook with a little butter, bouillon and coulis, or if you don't have coulis, make a small roux of flour to bind the sauce
5. When cooked, season with good salt and remove the fat, add a little shallot for those who like it, and serve under roasted mutton, either shoulder, loin or leg
6. If you wish to serve it white in the lean way, in place of a flour roux, put a liaison of egg yolks and cream, serve it under soft-boiled eggs

title: Chicorée Braisée (Braised Chicory)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicory
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Velouté sauce
- Consommé
- Croutons

## Directions

1. If the chicory yields when pressed, drain it in a colander and place it in fresh water; when well chilled, drain it again and press it in your hands to extract the water.
2. Chop the chicory.
3. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan with the chicory, a little salt, and coarse pepper; stir it much and pour over it about five tablespoons of velouté and as much consommé; reduce it until it becomes somewhat thick.
4. Dress it on the plate with croutons around it.
5. If you wish to make chicory with cream or bouillon, add about two teaspoons of flour and moisten the chicory with cream or bouillon; if you have added too much liquid, reduce it.

title: Chicorée Conservée pour l'Hiver (Preserved Chicory for Winter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One hundred heads of chicory
- Salt
- Water
- Clarified butter

## Directions

1. Toward the end of September, take one hundred heads of chicory or more
2. Remove the hard green ribs that envelop the white part, keeping only the good parts
3. Rinse the head without cutting too close
4. Have a large cauldron three-quarters full of boiling water
5. Put two large handfuls of salt in the water
6. When the water boils, add your chicory, well-washed and drained
7. Be careful to submerge it in the boiling water so it does not blacken
8. After it has boiled for ten minutes, remove it and put it in cold water
9. When well refreshed, remove it and press it in your hands to extract the water
10. Arrange it in jars or pots
11. Make a brine
12. When it is cold and clear, pour it over your chicory
13. Cover it with clarified butter
14. When it is congealed, close your container

title: Chipoulade de Plusieurs Façons (Fricassee of Poultry Several Ways)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Legs of poulardes or turkeys, or turkey or poularde wings
- Six small sausages the length of a finger
- Small bacon cut into slices
- Small white onions, blanched
- Bouillon
- Bacon bards
- Two slices of lemon
- Bouquet of fine herbs
- Coulis

## Directions

1. Take the legs of poulardes or turkeys, or preferably wings of turkeys or poulardes depending on the season
2. Have six small sausages the length of a finger
3. Cut small bacon into slices and blanch small white onions
4. Cook everything together in a casserole with bouillon, wrapped with bacon bards above and below
5. Add two slices of lemon and a bouquet of fine herbs
6. When everything is cooked, remove it quickly to drain it
7. Arrange it in a serving dish
8. Take the sauce remaining in the casserole, deglaze it and pass it through a sieve
9. Add one spoonful of coulis to give it a little consistency
10. Taste to ensure the sauce is well flavored
11. Serve the sauce over the dish
12. Poularde wings are prepared the same way as turkey wings; you can also prepare a whole chicken the same way

title: Chou en Surprise (Cabbage Surprise)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole cabbage
- water
- chestnuts
- sausages
- bouillon
- salt
- pepper
- root vegetables
- onions
- bouquet garni
- butter

## Directions

1. Take a good whole cabbage and cook it for a quarter hour in boiling water, then remove it to fresh water
2. Press it well in your hands without breaking the leaves
3. Place the leaves on a table and separate all of them to remove the core
4. In place of the core, put chestnuts and sausages
5. Put all the leaves back as they were, so that it does not appear that there is anything inside
6. Tie up the cabbage well and cook it in a light bed made with bouillon, a little salt, pepper, root vegetables, onions, and a bouquet garni
7. When it is cooked, put it to drain and serve with a good sauce containing butter

title: Chou en Surprise (Surprise Cabbage)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One good whole cabbage
- Water

## Directions

1. Cook the cabbage for a quarter hour in boiling water
2. Remove it to fresh water and press it well in your hands without breaking the leaves
3. Place the leaves on a table and spread them all out to remove the stem

title: Chou à l'Entrée (Cabbage as an Entrée)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage, cut into quarters
- A piece of small bacon with rind
- Meat (tendons of veal, beef breast, beef rump, pork andouille, boned and rounded lamb shoulder, or capon)
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Chives
- Cloves
- A little nutmeg
- Two or three roots
- Good coulis sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage into quarters and wash them
2. Boil them for a quarter hour in water with a piece of small bacon cut into pieces with rind attached
3. Remove them into fresh water, press them well and tie them up
4. Boil the meat for two minutes in water to remove its scum
5. Cook the meat and cabbage together in a braise made of bouillon, salt, pepper, a bouquet of parsley, chives, cloves, a little nutmeg, and two or three roots
6. When the meat and cabbage are cooked, remove them and wipe them well of their fat
7. Arrange them on the serving plate with the bacon on top
8. Cover with a sauce made of good coulis and well seasoned

title: Chou à la Bourgeoise (Cabbage Bourgeoise)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One whole cabbage
- Farce (forcemeat)
- Water
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Good coulis

## Directions

1. Wash the cabbage and boil it for a quarter hour in water
2. Remove it to fresh water and let it cool
3. Press it firmly without breaking the leaves
4. Remove the leaves one by one and put a little farce on each
5. Reassemble the leaves as if the cabbage were whole and tie it up all around
6. Cook it in a braise made as for beef tongue, well seasoned
7. When cooked, gently press it in a white cloth to remove the fat
8. Cut it in half and arrange it on the serving plate
9. Cover with good coulis

title: Chou à la Flamande (Cabbage Flemish Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One cabbage
- Butter
- Good bouillon
- Seven or eight onions
- Bouquet garni
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sausages
- Bread crouton

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage into quarters and blanch it in boiling water for a quarter hour
2. Remove it to fresh water and press it to remove the water
3. Remove the stem and tie it up
4. Cook it with butter, good bouillon, seven or eight onions, a bouquet garni, salt and pepper
5. When almost cooked, add some cooked sausages
6. Fry a bread crouton larger than the hollow of your hand with butter
7. When the ragout is cooked, place the crouton in the bottom of the serving plate
8. Arrange the cabbage, sausages, and onions around it, all well wiped of fat
9. Degrease the cabbage sauce, add a little coulis if you have it, make the sauce short and well seasoned
10. Serve over the cabbage

title: Choux Farcis (Stuffed Cabbage)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two medium cabbages
- Water
- Salt
- Half pound veal, minced
- One pound pork fat
- Coarse pepper
- Four-spice
- Aromatics
- Seven or eight egg yolks
- Lard strips
- Veal slices
- Ham
- Three carrots
- Four onions
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Two cloves
- Bouillon
- Spanish sauce or light roux

## Directions

1. Blanch two medium cabbages in water and salt; when they have been twenty minutes in boiling water, refresh them and let them drain; remove the hearts.
2. Chop half a pound of veal; add one pound of pork fat, salt, coarse pepper, a little four-spice, and a little crushed aromatics; when all is well chopped, add seven or eight egg yolks that you will mix well with your stuffing.
3. Put the stuffing inside each cabbage; tie them well.
4. Arrange lard strips in the bottom of a saucepan, some veal slices, a little ham, three carrots, four onions, a little thyme, a bay leaf, two cloves; put the cabbages on top and cover them with lard.
5. Moisten them with bouillon from the bottom of the pot; add a little salt and pepper; let them simmer for an hour and a half.
6. When they are cooked, drain them on white cloth; extract the juice by pressing them a little; then untie them and give them an agreeable shape; you can dress them on the plate; glaze them and put Spanish sauce underneath; if you have no sauce, make a light roux, moisten it with the cooking liquid, and pass it through silk strainer.

title: Choux à la Crème (Cabbage in Cream)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Salt
- Butter
- Black pepper
- Nutmeg, grated
- Flour
- Cream
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Wash the cabbage and slice it finely.
2. Blanch it in salted water.
3. When the cabbage wilts under your fingers, refresh it and press it like chicory.
4. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan.
5. After cutting the cabbage with a knife several times, combine it with the butter.
6. Add salt, black pepper, and a little grated nutmeg; mix well.
7. Stir in a spoonful of flour and mix it with the cabbage.
8. Moisten with cream; if it is too liquid, reduce it.
9. Prepare it in the same manner and sprinkle with bouillon using the same seasoning.

title: Choux à la Crème (Cabbage with Cream)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. After washing the cabbage, slice it thinly and blanch it.
2. Put a handful of salt in water; when the cabbage yields under the fingers, refresh it and press it as you would chicory.
3. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan.

title: Choux-fleurs (Cauliflower)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Water
- Flour
- Butter
- Salt
- Sauce or coulis with butter (for meat days) or white sauce (for lean days)

## Directions

1. Peel and wash the cauliflower
2. Cook it briefly in water
3. Remove it and finish cooking in another white water made with a spoonful of flour diluted with water, a little butter, and salt
4. When cooked, arrange it on the serving plate and cover with sauce or coulis with butter (for meat days) or white sauce (for lean days)

title: Choux-Fleurs au Fromage (Cauliflower with Cheese)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked cauliflower
- Gruyère or parmesan cheese, grated
- White sauce, somewhat thickened
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter

## Directions

1. When the cauliflowers are cooked, drain them and place them on a plate; sprinkle them with grated Gruyère or parmesan cheese.
2. Then dress them on the serving plate; make a white sauce that is somewhat thickened, in which you will put grated cheese; mask your cauliflowers with it and cover them with your sauce as much as possible.
3. Sprinkle grated cheese on the cauliflowers.
4. Then put breadcrumbs on top with a brush dipped in lukewarm butter; baste the breadcrumbs with butter; you will use half breadcrumbs and half grated cheese, and bread your cauliflowers a second time so that it forms a crust.
5. Place them on a gentle fire twenty minutes before serving; put a field oven over the top; let them take on a nice color.
6. At the moment of serving, sponge away the butter that is on your plate with a piece of soft bread; clean the edges of the plate.

title: Choux-fleurs en Pain (Cauliflower Loaf)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful cauliflower
- Lard bacon strips
- Veal round
- Beef fat
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- Salt
- Pepper
- Three whole eggs
- Good bouillon
- Good coulis with butter

## Directions

1. Peel the cauliflower and cook it halfway in water
2. Remove it to fresh water and drain it in a colander
3. Take a small pan the size of the bottom of the serving plate
4. Line the bottom with bacon strips and arrange the cauliflower on top with tails pointing up
5. Make a good farce with veal round, beef fat, parsley, chives, and mushrooms, all chopped and seasoned with salt and pepper, mixed with three whole eggs
6. Fill all the cavities of the cauliflower with this farce, pressing it in with your fingers
7. Cook it with good bouillon, well seasoned
8. When cooked and the sauce is reduced, gently turn it out onto the serving plate
9. Remove the bacon strips and cover with good coulis with butter
10. Serve as an entrée

title: Choux-Fleurs Frits (Fried Cauliflower)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- White sauce, thickened
- Frying batter
- Oil for frying
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the cauliflowers three-quarters done.
2. Make a white sauce that is somewhat thickened; sauté the cauliflowers in it and let them cool.
3. At the moment of serving, dip the cauliflowers in frying batter and put them in moderately hot oil.
4. When golden, remove them and let them drain on white cloth.
5. Dress them on your plate.
6. Alternatively, when they are cooked and well drained, place them in a terrine with salt, pepper, and vinegar; sauté them in this seasoning; put them in the frying batter and fry them.

title: Choux-Fleurs à la Damezac (Cauliflower Damezac)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- White sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the cauliflowers until somewhat firm.
2. Drain them.
3. Have ready a white sauce that is somewhat thickened.
4. Put the cauliflowers in it and sauté them in the sauce.
5. Dress them on the plate (see White Sauce).

title: Choux-Fleurs à la Sauce Blanche (Cauliflower with White Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Cauliflower
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- White sauce

## Directions

1. Peel the cauliflowers.
2. Put a saucepan on the fire with water, a little salt, and a piece of butter the size of half an egg.
3. When the water boils, put in the cauliflowers.
4. After about a quarter hour, remove them if cooked; otherwise leave them longer; they should be removed from the fire when somewhat firm.
5. At the moment of serving, drain them and dress them on a plate or in a saucepan; turn them onto a plate and mask them with a white or brown butter sauce (see White Sauce).

title: Clarified Egg White for Clarifying
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. Break three eggs into a bowl
2. Beat them well
3. Gradually wet them while continuing to beat
4. Add three pints of water and use this water to clarify sugar

title: Clarified Sugar for Making All Sorts of Preserves
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg white
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the egg white by hand with water, the amount depending on how much sugar you want to use
2. Bring the mixture to a boil, adding cold water from time to time until the sugar is clear and well skimmed
3. Remove from heat and pass through a white cloth or a sieve
4. Use as desired

title: Cochon de Lait (Suckling Pig)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig
- Very good oil
- Sage

## Directions

1. When your pig is scalded and trussed, spit it through the rear
2. Make the spit come out through the mouth
3. Baste it with very good oil so the skin takes on the desired crispness
4. It must be very well cooked
5. Place a bouquet of sage in its belly

title: Cochon de Lait en Blanquette (Suckling Pig in White Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- scraps of roasted suckling pig
- butter
- mushrooms
- parsley
- chives
- garlic clove
- shallots
- cloves
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- flour
- white wine
- broth
- salt
- coarse pepper
- egg yolks
- verjuice

## Directions

1. Take the scraps of a roasted suckling pig and cut them into thin strips
2. Put in a saucepan a piece of good butter the size of half an egg with mushrooms cut into thin strips, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a garlic clove, two shallots, two cloves, half a bay leaf, thyme, and basil
3. Put them on the fire, add a pinch of flour, moisten with a glass of white wine and as much broth, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Boil on low heat and reduce by half
5. Remove the bouquet and add the meat strips, heat without boiling
6. Add a liaison of three egg yolks diluted with two spoonfuls of verjuice and as much broth, thicken on the fire without boiling
7. Serve hot

title: Cochon de Lait en Galantine (Suckling Pig Galantine)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- forcemeat
- ham lardons
- lard lardons
- truffle lardons
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- lard slices

## Directions

1. Once well scalded, bone it completely, spread it on a white cloth, and put a good seasoned forcemeat on top, spread to the thickness of a large coin
2. Put on this forcemeat a row of ham lardons, one of lard, one of truffles, one of hard-boiled egg yolks
3. Cover all these lardons with a little forcemeat
4. Roll up the suckling pig, being careful not to disturb the lardons
5. Wrap it in slices of lard

title: Cochon de Lait en Galentine (Suckling Pig in Galantine)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig, scalded and boned
- Good meat farce seasoned to taste
- Ham lardons
- Lard lardons
- Truffle lardons
- Hard egg yolks
- Lard barding
- Cheesecloth
- Half bouillon and half white wine
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Turnips
- Onions
- Large bouquet of parsley
- Chives
- Shallots
- Garlic
- Cloves
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Basil

## Directions

1. Once well scalded, bone the suckling pig completely and extend it on a white cloth
2. Spread a good meat farce seasoned to taste over it, approximately the thickness of a large coin
3. On this farce place a row of ham lardons, then a row of lard, then a row of truffles, then a row of hard egg yolks
4. Cover all these lardons with a bit more farce
5. Roll up the suckling pig, being careful not to disturb the lardons
6. Wrap it in lard barding and cheesecloth
7. Tie it tightly with string
8. Cook for three hours with half bouillon and half white wine, salt, coarse pepper, turnips, onions, a large bouquet of parsley, chives, shallots, garlic, cloves, thyme, bay leaf, and basil
9. Allow it to cool in its cooking liquid
10. Serve cold as an entremets

title: Cochon de Lait Farci (Stuffed Suckling Pig)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- veal liver
- lard
- sage
- four spices
- aromatics
- salt
- pepper
- large lardons
- white cloth
- lemon
- bay leaves
- good white wine
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Scald your pig; singe it and debone it up to the head which you leave whole
2. Have a nice light veal liver which you will chop
3. Put one pound of lard for one pound of liver
4. Mix in a little ground sage, a little of the four spices, a little ground aromatics, salt, coarse pepper
5. When everything is well chopped, put this forcemeat in the body of your pig
6. Have large lardons which you will put surrounded by forcemeat in the limbs and along the spine of the back of your pig
7. Fill it well with this forcemeat; sew the skin of the belly so that it has a firm appearance
8. Wrap it in a white cloth, tie it up, and rub it with lemon
9. Put in your pot a little sage, 4 bay leaves
10. Cover the back with lard, and wrap everything in a white cloth, of which you tie both ends
11. Mark a pan which you moisten with half good white wine, half bouillon, which you pour over the pig that you will have put in a braising pan
12. Let it simmer for three and a half hours on a low fire, barely boiling
13. When it is cooked, remove it and leave it one hour in its cooking liquid
14. Take it out and press it gently; try to preserve its shape
15. Let it cool completely
16. Unwrap it; tidy it up; put a napkin on a plate, and arrange your suckling pig on top

title: Cochon de Lait par Quartier au Père Douillet (Suckling Pig Quarters Père Douillet Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- beef brisket
- veal knuckle and two feet
- parsley
- chives
- garlic cloves
- cloves
- nutmeg
- onions
- roots
- suckling pig
- four large crayfish
- white wine
- salt
- coarse pepper
- lemon
- six egg whites

## Directions

1. Make a good broth with beef brisket, veal knuckle and two feet, a bouquet of parsley, chives, two garlic cloves, three cloves, half a nutmeg, onions, and roots
2. Once the meat is cooked, strain the broth through a sieve
3. Put the suckling pig in a vessel proportioned to its size, with four large crayfish and the strained broth
4. Add one chopine of white wine, salt, and coarse pepper
5. Cook for one hour and a half
6. Strain the cooking liquid through a sieve and degrease it, then put it on a stove to clarify it like a jelly by adding half a lemon with skin removed and six egg whites beaten with the shells
7. When clear and has enough body, strain through a cloth
8. Put the suckling pig in a vessel just its size, with the four crayfish underneath and branches of parsley
9. Pour the jelly over the pig to chill it
10. When the jelly is set, dip the bottom of the vessel in hot water and quickly invert it onto the platter for serving, with a cloth underneath

title: Cochon de Lait à la Lyonnaise (Suckling Pig Lyonnaise Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Suckling pig, scalded and eviscerated
- One pint of good milk
- Approximately half a pound of breadcrumbs
- Approximately one pound of veal round
- Beef fat, equal to the veal
- Breadcrumbs
- Parsley
- Chives
- Two shallots
- Mushrooms
- Salt
- Pepper
- Four eggs
- Suckling pig liver
- Raw ham
- Lard barding
- Napkin

## Directions

1. After well scalding and cleaning, bone the suckling pig completely, except for the head and feet
2. Prepare the farce: heat one pint of good milk in a saucepan; when it boils, add approximately half a pound of breadcrumbs and boil until it absorbs all the milk and becomes very thick, stirring often near the end to prevent sticking
3. Allow to cool
4. Chop together approximately one pound of veal round and an equal amount of beef fat
5. Mix in the cooled breadcrumb mixture
6. Finely chop and add parsley, chives, two shallots, and mushrooms
7. Add salt and pepper
8. Incorporate four eggs, white and yolk
9. Cut the suckling pig liver into large dice and add approximately the same amount of raw ham, mixing into the farce
10. Fill the body of the suckling pig with this mixture and sew it closed
11. Truss as if for roasting
12. Cover with lard barding
13. Wrap in a napkin and tie with string
14. Cook as the previous recipe
15. Serve cold on a napkin as a cold entremets

title: Compote Blanche de Pommes (White Apple Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large reinette apples
- 1 large glass of water
- juice of half a lemon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the apples in half, remove the skin and seeds, and immediately place them in fresh water
2. Cook with a large glass of water, the juice of half a lemon, and a piece of sugar
3. When the apples are cooked, arrange them in a serving dish
4. Reduce the syrup until it is sticky to the touch
5. Pour the syrup over the apples

title: Compote blanche de Pommes (White Apple Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- six large Reinette apples
- fresh water
- juice of half a lemon
- a piece of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut six large Reinette apples in half
2. Remove the skin and seeds, throwing them into fresh water as you work
3. Cook them with a large glass of water, the juice of half a lemon, and a piece of sugar
4. When the apples are cooked, arrange them in a compote dish
5. Reduce the syrup until it sticks to your fingers
6. Dress the syrup over the apples

title: Compote d'Abricots (Apricot Compote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots
- water
- clarified sugar

## Directions

1. In their first freshness they are employed without peeling them; but afterwards, you cut them and you remove the pit; pass them through water on the fire, as for those that one wants to preserve: when they rise above and are soft, you draw them and make them cool; afterwards you let them drain, then you put them in the little clarified sugar, and you let them throw three or four boils: take care to skim them well, and if the syrup is not cooked enough, you will give them apart still four or five boils, and you will pour it over the fruit; when the apricots are cold, you will arrange them in a compote dish to serve them

title: Compote d'Abricots à la Portugaise (Compote of Apricots Portuguese Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots that are almost ripe
- sugar
- half a glass of water

## Directions

1. Take the quantity of apricots you wish
2. Split them in half and remove the pits
3. Put sugar in the bottom of a dish with half a glass of water
4. Arrange the apricots on top
5. Place them on a moderate fire to boil until they are almost cooked underneath and there is almost no syrup remaining
6. Remove them from the fire and sprinkle fine sugar over them
7. Cover them with a tourtière lid and put fire on top until they are cooked and of a beautiful glazed color
8. Dress them in the compote dish while they are hot

title: Compote d'Oranges Crues (Raw Orange Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Portuguese oranges
- Fine sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the top from six Portuguese oranges in such a way that you can replace it as if they were whole
2. Pierce the flesh in several places with a small knife
3. Work fine sugar into the cuts
4. Replace the lids and serve
5. Alternatively, serve them after peeling, cut them in slices and arrange in a compote dish with fine sugar above and below

title: Compote de Bon-Chrétien (Bon-Chrétien Pears Compote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Five fine bon-chrétien pears
- Water
- Lemon juice
- Sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Cut the pears in half
2. Remove the cores
3. Plunge them in cold water
4. Blanch them
5. Peel them and place in water and lemon juice to keep them white
6. Cook them in the syrup
7. Add lemon juice
8. Arrange them on a serving dish
9. Reduce the syrup if it is too thin

title: Compote de Catillards (Catillard Pears Compote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Five catillard pears
- One tablespoon of syrup
- Four tablespoons of water

## Directions

1. Cut the pears in half
2. Remove the core and peel
3. Place them in a small recently tinned copper pot
4. Add one tablespoon of syrup and four tablespoons of water
5. Simmer for two hours or longer if not cooked
6. When cooked to the proper degree, arrange on a serving dish
7. Reduce the syrup if too thin and pour over the pears

title: Compote de Cerises (Cherry Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- half a glass of water
- a quarter pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the end of the cherry stems
2. Place them in a pan with half a glass of water and a quarter pound of sugar
3. Put them on the fire and let them come to a boil two or three times covered
4. Arrange them in a compote dish and neatly pour your syrup over them
5. Serve them cold

title: Compote de Cerises (Cherry Compote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of fine cherries
- half pound of sugar, or thereabouts
- water
- fresh water

## Directions

1. Take one pound of fine cherries; cut half the stem away, and pass them through fresh water
2. Put half a pound of sugar or thereabouts in a pan, with water to dissolve it; make it boil until it is almost a syrup, and throw your cherries into it, after having made them drain on a sieve
3. Make them take a high heat for about a dozen boils or so; then remove them from the fire; skim them, and with a spoon or with paper you will remove the foam from them; you will let them cool, and you will serve them in a compote dish

title: Compote de Coings (Quince Compote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Six quinces
- Water
- Sugar syrup
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut the quinces in half
2. Remove the cores
3. Blanch them like bon-chrétien pears
4. Remove the peel
5. Place clear syrup in a pot with lemon juice
6. Allow the quinces to finish cooking
7. Arrange them in the serving dish and pour the syrup somewhat thick underneath

title: Compote de Coins (Quince Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large quinces (or more if small)
- 1 quarter pound sugar
- 1 half-glass water

## Directions

1. Put quinces in boiling water to cook until tender under the fingers
2. Put them after in cold water
3. Cut them in four
4. Remove the cores and peel them cleanly
5. Put a quarter pound of sugar in a pan with a half-glass of water
6. Bring to boil and skim
7. Add the quinces to finish cooking them
8. Serve hot with light syrup

title: Compote de Fraise (Strawberry Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter pound of sugar
- a glass of water
- beautiful strawberries that are not too ripe

## Directions

1. Cook a quarter pound of sugar with a glass of water until the syrup is well strong
2. Be sure to skim it well
3. Use beautiful strawberries, not too ripe, cleaned, washed and well drained
4. Put them in the syrup and remove from the fire to let them rest a moment in the syrup
5. Let them come to a boil and remove them promptly so they remain whole

title: Compote de Framboises (Raspberry Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Make this compote in the same way as strawberry compote, with the difference that you do not wash the raspberries

title: Compote de Groseilles (Currant Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a pound of beautiful currants
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Make a strong syrup as in the previous recipe
2. Have a pound of beautiful currants washed and drained; you may leave them on the bunch if you wish
3. Put them in the syrup to give them three big boils covered
4. Remove them from the fire and skim them before dressing them in the compote dish

title: Compote de Groseilles Vertes (Green Gooseberry Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound green gooseberries
- 1 glass cold water
- Vinegar (a little)
- Salt (a little)
- 1 half-pound sugar
- 1 glass water

## Directions

1. Split the green gooseberries on the sides with a toothpick to make the seeds come out
2. Put them in hot water on the fire and leave them until they rise to the surface
3. Remove from heat and add a glass of cold water, a little vinegar and a little salt
4. Leave them in this water until they are cold so they have time to become green again
5. Remove them to put in fresh water
6. While they are cooling, put a half-pound of sugar in a pan with a glass of water
7. Bring to boil and skim until it is clear
8. Add the gooseberries well drained
9. Bring to a gentle boil without letting them do more than simmer
10. Remove them with a skimmer and put them in the compote dish
11. Finish cooking the sugar until it has the consistency of syrup
12. Put it over the gooseberries

title: Compote de Marrons (Chestnut Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Chestnuts
- 1 quarter pound sugar
- 1 half-glass water
- Lemon juice
- Fine sugar

## Directions

1. Cook chestnuts in ash the same way as if you wanted to serve them in a napkin
2. When they are peeled, put them in a pan with a quarter pound of sugar and a half-glass of water
3. Let them simmer on a small fire about half a quarter-hour
4. Before serving, squeeze a little lemon juice
5. When serving, lightly add fine sugar on top

title: Compote de Pigeons (Pigeon Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- small pigeons
- truffles
- mushrooms
- poultry livers
- calf's sweetbread, blanched
- bouquet of parsley, chives
- garlic clove
- cloves
- basil
- good butter
- flour
- meat juice and bouillon
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice or white vinegar

## Directions

1. Blanch the pigeons with legs trussed inside the body, remove the neck and wings, pluck them, and place them in a casserole with two or three truffles if you have them, mushrooms, some poultry livers, a blanched calf's sweetbread cut into four pieces, a bouquet of parsley and chives, a garlic clove, two cloves, basil, and a piece of good butter.
2. Pass them over the fire, add a good pinch of flour, moisten with half juice and half bouillon, a glass of white wine, salt, and coarse pepper.
3. Let cook and reduce to short sauce, taking care to degrease.
4. When serving, add a lemon juice or a dash of white vinegar; ensure everything is cooked properly and well salted.

title: Compote de Poires de Bon Chrétien, de Doyenné, de Vigoureuse, de Saint-Germain et autres (Compote of Bon Chrétien, Doyenné, Vigoureuse, Saint-Germain and Other Pears)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- whole pears with skin
- sugar
- half a septier of water
- a slice of lemon

## Directions

1. Blanch your whole pears with their skin in boiling water
2. When they are one-third cooked, remove them to fresh water
3. Peel them whole or in halves, placing them in fresh water as you work
4. Boil your sugar in a pan with half a septier of water
5. Put your pears in with a slice of lemon so they remain white
6. When they are cooked and have a good syrup, serve them hot or cold according to the master's taste

title: Compote de Poires de Martin sec ou de Messire Jean entier (Compote of Whole Martin Dry Pears or Messire Jean Pears)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- whole pears
- a small piece of tin
- water
- a quarter pound of sugar or more if the pot is large and there are many pears
- a small piece of cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel the pears if you wish (though they are more often served unpeeled)
2. Remove the bottoms and trim the sides of the stems
3. Place them in a small earthenware pot
4. Add a small piece of tin to make them red
5. Add water, a quarter pound of sugar or more if the pot is large, and a small piece of cinnamon
6. Cook them in front of the fire
7. When cooked and the syrup is not too thin, serve them hot

title: Compote de Poires grillées (Compote of Grilled Pears)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Previously cooked pears from an earlier compote
- Five tablespoons of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Drain the cooked pears well on white linen
2. Place five tablespoons of powdered sugar in the bottom of a pan or skillet
3. Arrange the pears on top
4. Place on the fire
5. When the bottom of the pan takes on a somewhat dark color, remove from heat
6. Place the pears on a serving dish with syrup underneath

title: Compote de Poires à la Bonne Femme (Compote of Pears Good Wife Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pears for cooking
- a glass of water
- a small piece of cinnamon
- two cloves
- half a quarter pound of sugar
- a glass of red wine

## Directions

1. Place whole pears in a pot with a glass of water, a small piece of cinnamon, two cloves, and half a quarter pound of sugar
2. Cook them well covered over some hot ashes
3. At half the cooking time, add a glass of red wine
4. When well cooked, reduce the syrup as very little is needed
5. Serve hot over the pears

title: Compote de Pommes (Apple Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- apples that are not Reinette
- a glass of water
- a little more than a quarter pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the apples in half
2. Remove the seeds
3. Prick the top of the skin in several places
4. Cook them with a glass of water and a little more than a quarter pound of sugar
5. When they begin to turn to marmalade, arrange them in the compote dish
6. Reduce the syrup and pour it over them

title: Compote de Pommes Blanches (White Apple Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- about a quarter pound of sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Cut apples in half and remove the cores
2. Arrange them in a pan with the skin side up
3. Add approximately a quarter pound of sugar and enough water for them to cook
4. When cooked on one side, turn them over
5. When fully cooked and the syrup is sufficiently reduced, arrange them in a serving dish with the syrup on top
6. Serve hot or cold as you prefer

title: Compote de Pommes entières (Compote of Whole Apples)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Seven fine reinette apples
- Water
- Lemon juice
- One half-pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Using an apple corer, remove the core and peel from the apples
2. Place them in water and lemon juice
3. Clarify one half-pound of sugar and make a syrup that is somewhat thin
4. Add the apples to the syrup
5. Cook them at a gentle boil
6. Stir them often
7. When a fork easily pierces them, remove them and place on a serving dish
8. Reduce the syrup and pour it under the apples

title: Compote de Pommes Entières (Whole Apple Compote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- seven fine reinette apples
- water
- lemon juice
- half pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Take seven fine reinette apples; with a corer, you will remove the heart and skin from them; you put them in water and lemon juice
2. Clarify half a pound of sugar; leave your syrup a little thin; put your apples in it; make them cook on small boils; taste them often; as soon as the fork teeth enter into them, remove them and place them on your compote dish
3. Make the syrup reduce, and pour it under the apples

title: Compote de Pommes à la Portugaise (Portuguese-Style Apple Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- reinette apples as needed to fill the serving dish
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the cores from the apples using an iron tube or knife
2. Arrange them in a baking dish or on a silver plate
3. Place a small piece of sugar or powdered sugar in each apple, and some sugar at the bottom of the dish
4. Cook in the oven or under a camp oven, with heat above and below
5. Serve hot with a little powdered sugar on top

title: Compote de Prunes de Reine-Claude (Greengage Plum Compote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Reine-Claude plums
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. You prick the plums with several pricks of a pin, and you throw them as you go into water; afterwards you make them blanch on the fire in other water; when they have risen above, you draw them and put them promptly to cool
2. You will make them return to green by putting them on a small fire and covering them: take care that they do not boil, because they would become marmalade
3. As soon as they are well green, you will cool them again, you will drain them, and you will put them in the little sugar, which you will heat and throw two boils only; you will leave them thus until the next day, or until evening if you have need of them
4. Then you will remove them from a pot, you will let them boil until they have well taken the syrup; you see then that they do not skim anymore and that they are complete
5. If you make only one batch, and you have too much syrup, you will give it apart still some boils to make it diminish; then you will strain it over the plums: you can prepare some more, that you will keep in this manner for some time

title: Compote de Pêches (Peach Compote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Six peaches
- Water
- Sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Fill a pot three-quarters full with boiling water
2. Place the peaches in it
3. Give them five or six boils
4. Test if the skin comes off easily
5. Refresh them in cold water and peel
6. Split them in two and remove the pit
7. Make syrup in the pot
8. Place the peach halves in the syrup
9. Let them reach a boil
10. Remove them and place in a serving dish
11. Reduce the syrup if too clear and pour under the peaches

title: Compote de Raisins (Grape Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quarter pound sugar
- 1 half-glass water
- 1 pound muscat grapes

## Directions

1. Put a quarter pound of sugar with a half-glass of water in a pan
2. Bring to boil, skim and reduce to a strong syrup
3. Add 1 pound of muscat grapes, seeded and with pips removed
4. Let boil two or three times and arrange in the compote dish
5. If there is foam, remove it with white paper

title: Compote de Reinettes Blanches (White Pippin Apple Compote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- white pippin apples
- water
- lemon juice
- clarified sugar

## Directions

1. You peel some apples and you cut them in half, after having removed the skin
2. You put them in an earthenware vessel where there is water and a lemon juice, so that they keep their whiteness
3. You clarify a half pound of sugar; as soon as the syrup is well clarified, you throw the apple halves in the sugar; take care to turn the apples; when you feel the fork sink in them, you will remove them from the syrup
4. Pass the syrup through a silk sieve, and make it reduce; you will boil it again; then you will pour it on the apples; serve them cold or hot
5. If you want to decorate them, take the skin of a red calville apple, and make a design on the halves

title: Compote de Rousselets (Rousselet Pears Compote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fifteen rousselet pears
- Water
- Sugar syrup

## Directions

1. Blanch the pears using the same procedure as for preceding pears
2. Peel them and trim the stem slightly
3. Place them in a somewhat thin syrup
4. When cooked, arrange them on a serving dish
5. Reduce the syrup if necessary

title: Compote de Verjus (Verjuice Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- verjuice
- a quarter pound of sugar
- a glass of water

## Directions

1. Remove the seeds from your verjuice
2. Put it in a pan with a quarter pound of sugar and a glass of water
3. Boil it over low heat
4. When it is well green and the syrup is reduced, dress it in the compote dish and serve cold

title: Compote de Verjus (Verjuice Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound verjuice, not fully ripe
- 1 glass water
- 6 ounces sugar

## Directions

1. Split each grain with the tip of a small knife to remove the seeds
2. Once all seeds are removed, throw them into water ready to boil
3. When the verjuice begins to pale, remove from heat and add a half-glass of cold water
4. Leave it in the same water until it is cold so it has time to turn green again
5. Put a fish of water with six ounces of sugar in a pan to boil
6. Add all the verjuice at once and let it boil two or three times
7. Take care to skim
8. Arrange your verjuice in the compote dish before putting the syrup on the verjuice
9. Reduce the syrup if it is too clear

title: Compote grillée de Poires (Grilled Pear Compote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pears for cooking that are not very ripe
- a small piece of cinnamon
- a quarter pound of sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Place pears in a well-lit good oven until the entire skin is well burnt, turning them carefully so they grill equally
2. Continue until the skin comes off by rubbing them in water
3. Remove the skin this way, cut them in half and remove the seeds
4. Rinse them again in several waters
5. Put them in a pot to cook with a chopine of water, a small piece of cinnamon, and a quarter pound of sugar
6. Cover the pot and cook them until they soften greatly under the fingers
7. Reduce the syrup and serve them hot if you wish

title: Compote of All Kinds of Grilled Fruits
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- fruit
- syrup

## Directions

1. Let your syrup reduce until your fruit begins to stick to the pan, then you must not leave it, you must move your compote around in it until you see it has a beautiful color
2. Then put a plate on your compote that you hold with your left hand, and turn it over by sliding it properly into your compote dish
