3. Serve them hot or cold, they are better hot
4. You can make compotes with those that have already been used to make a change

title: Compote of Green Apricots and Green Almonds (Bourgeoise)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- two handfuls of soda
- green apricots or green almonds
- fresh water
- boiling water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Boil water in a pan with two handfuls of soda; when it has boiled twice, throw your apricots or almonds into the pan
2. When they have boiled once, remove them with a skimming spoon and rub them well in your hands to remove the down, and as you do so throw them into fresh water
3. Have fresh boiling water ready in another pan to cook your apricots
4. You will see when they are cooked enough by pricking them with a pin; if it enters easily and the apricot falls by itself, it is a sign they are cooked enough
5. Remove them in fresh water, then boil sugar in your pan and put your apricots or almonds in it
6. Let them boil gently on a small fire until they are well green, and serve
7. Alternatively, some do not make lye for their apricots or almonds, they merely rub them with salt to remove the down, and then blanch and cook as described above

title: Compote of Grilled Peaches
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- eight or ten nearly ripe peaches
- one quarter pound of sugar
- one glass of water
- fresh water

## Directions

1. Take eight or ten nearly ripe peaches that you leave whole, put them on a well-lit stove, burn all the skin evenly by turning them as you go, throw them in fresh water
2. When you have removed all the skin and washed in several waters, put them to cook with a quarter pound of sugar in a glass of water until they give way under your fingers
3. Arrange them in the compote dish and the syrup over them

title: Compote of Lemon, Oranges, Bergamots and Chinese Limes
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lemon, oranges, bergamots, or Chinese limes
- water
- one glass of water
- one quarter pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut them into small pieces and cook them well in water until they are very soft under your fingers, remove them with a skimming spoon and put them in fresh water
2. Make a small syrup with a glass of water and a quarter pound of sugar, put your pieces in it to let them simmer very gently on a small fire for half an hour, and serve cold

title: Compote of Peaches à la Portugaise
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- seven or eight peaches
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Put seven or eight peaches on a dish with fine sugar on top and underneath, cover them with a pie-dish lid and cook them on a small fire above and below
2. When they are cooked and well glazed, serve them hot

title: Compote of Plums à la Bonne Femme
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of sugar
- one glass of water
- one pound of nearly ripe plums

## Directions

1. Boil for a quarter hour a quarter pound of sugar with a glass of water, be careful to skim; when it becomes syrup, put one pound of nearly ripe plums in it
2. Make a few boils until the plums are cooked, remove the scum and arrange them in the compote dish
3. If the syrup is too thin, reduce it before pouring it over the plums

title: Compote of Reine Claude, Mirabelle, Perdrigon and Other Plums
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- water
- clarified sugar or sugar

## Directions

1. Boil water and throw your plums in it to blanch them; when they are very soft under your fingers, remove them with a skimming spoon and put them in fresh water
2. Put them then in a pan with a little clarified sugar, or not, if you have none
3. Put them on a small fire so they can shimmer and become well green, and serve cold

title: Compote of Ripe Apricots, Whole or Halved
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots
- boiling water
- fresh water
- one quarter pound of sugar
- one glass of water

## Directions

1. Blanch your apricots in boiling water; when they are very soft, remove them with a skimming spoon and put them in fresh water
2. Boil a quarter pound of sugar with a glass of water in a pan, put your apricots in it
3. Make two or three boils, skim them well, and remove them to arrange in a compote dish
4. Put your syrup over them to serve cold or hot, as you wish

title: Compote of Sliced Peaches
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six well-ripe peaches
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Take five or six beautiful ripe peaches, peel them properly, remove the pits and cut them into slices, to arrange in the compote dish
2. Serve by putting fine sugar underneath and over the peaches

title: Concombres Farcis (Stuffed Cucumbers)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cucumbers
- cooked farce
- lard strips
- veal slices
- carrots
- onions
- bay leaf
- thyme
- bouillon
- espagnole sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the skin of three cucumbers and detach the interior with the handle of a serving spoon
2. When well emptied, fill them with a cooked farce
3. Line a pot with lard strips and place a few veal slices, some carrots and onions, a bay leaf, and a little thyme
4. Cover the cucumbers with lard strips and sprinkle with bouillon
5. Braise them for half an hour
6. Check with your finger to see if they soften; then remove from the fire
7. At the moment of serving, let them drain and arrange them on your platter
8. Top with reduced espagnole sauce

title: Concombres à l'Espagnole (Cucumbers Spanish Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- espagnole sauce
- consommé

## Directions

1. Cut cucumbers in four lengthwise
2. Peel them and give them an attractive shape
3. Blanch them and let them drain on a cloth
4. Arrange them in a pot
5. Pour in five tablespoons of worked espagnole sauce and two tablespoons of consommé
6. Place the cucumbers on the fire half an hour before serving and let them simmer
7. Then arrange them; if the sauce is too thin, reduce it and pour it over

title: Concombres à la Crème (Cucumbers in Cream Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- water
- salt
- cream sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the cucumbers into small squares after peeling them
2. Round off the corners to give them a round or oval shape, making sure the pieces are uniform in size and thickness
3. Put water and salt in a pot; when it boils, throw in the cucumbers
4. As soon as they soften under the finger, remove them from boiling water and plunge them in cold water; let them drain on a cloth
5. Make a cream sauce that is somewhat thickened
6. Place the cucumbers in the sauce and serve on a platter or in an entrée dish

title: Confiture au Cidre (Cider Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Pear cider made without water
- Fruit (pears, apples, or quinces)

## Directions

1. Take pear cider made without water for this preserve; apple cider is not sweet enough.
2. Reduce the cider to two-thirds before adding your fruit.
3. Finish your preserves the same way as those made with sweet wine.

title: Confiture au Cidre (Cider Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Pear cider made without water
- Fruit

## Directions

1. Take pear cider made without water for this preserve; apple cider is not sweet enough
2. Reduce the cider to two-thirds before adding your fruit
3. Finish your preserves in the same manner as those made with sweet wine

title: Confiture de Campagne (Country Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet wine called mou
- Fruit (pears, apples, or quinces)

## Directions

1. Take sweet wine called mou, you cannot make it too sweet, take as much as you need for the amount of fruit you want to make preserve with, put it in a copper pot or pan and boil it over a clear fire until the fruit is ready to be set aside.
2. Take the fruit you want to preserve (pears, apples, or quinces), cook it in water until softened, then peel it and put it in your sweet wine syrup, and let it boil until it is cooked.
3. Be sure to skim during cooking. You will know when it is done by putting some of the syrup on a plate; if you see it remain ruby-colored and does not run when you tilt the plate, that is proof you must remove your preserve.
4. Put it in pots and cover when cold. It matters not whether the sweet wine is white or red.

title: Confiture de Cerises (Cherry Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherries, as much as desired
- Sugar, same weight as fruit

## Directions

1. Remove the stems and pits from the cherries
2. Prepare them in the same manner as apricots, both for cooking and for the quantity of sugar

title: Confiture de Gelée de Groseilles (Currant Jelly Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sugar, clarified
- Currants, one pound of fruit per pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Clarify your sugar as explained previously on page 340
2. Cook your sugar to the crack stage, which you will know by dipping a wet finger into a goblet full of water that you will hold in your left hand, dip your fingers into your sugar, then immediately into the goblet, and if your sugar dries in the water and breaks when pressed between your fingers, it is time to put the fruit into your pan
3. Make it boil twice covered
4. Remove your preserve from the fire, pass it through a sieve and put it immediately into pots
5. When it is cold, cover your pots, dip your first paper in brandy so the preserve keeps better
6. Never cover the pots except when the preserves are cold

title: Confiture de Gelée de Pommes (Apple Jelly Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Extract the juice from the apples by boiling them in a little water
2. Pass them afterwards through a cloth and press them a little
3. Use this juice to mix with your sugar
4. Cook in the same manner as currant jelly
5. You will know when it is cooked by putting your skimming spoon into your pan and withdrawing it, and if when you lift it and hold it slightly tilted, your jelly falls in drops, this shows it is sufficiently cooked
6. Put it immediately into pots

title: Confiture de Marmelade d'Abricots (Apricot Jam Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots
- 3 quarterons of sugar per pound of fruit

## Directions

1. Peel the apricots if you wish and remove the pits
2. Clarify the sugar as explained previously
3. Cook the sugar to the large ball stage, which you will know by putting your skimming spoon in the sugar and removing it, then blowing on it - you will see the sugar fly, which marks that it is at the right cooking point
4. Add the apricots and boil, stirring constantly with a wooden spatula, until the marmalade is sticky to your fingers
5. This is a sign that you can put it immediately into jars

title: Confiture de Prunes (Plum Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Plums (such as Perdigon, Reine Claude, Mirabelle, or other varieties)
- Five pounds of sugar per hundred plums

## Directions

1. Blanch the plums
2. When they are soft to the touch, remove them with a skimmer and place them in fresh water
3. Clarify five pounds of sugar for every hundred plums
4. Place the plums one by one in a clean vessel so they do not crush, and add the sugar slightly warmer than lukewarm
5. For four or five days, morning and evening, drain the plums on a sieve and boil the sugar, skimming it each time, then return the plums to the vessel with the sugar poured over, always slightly warmer than lukewarm
6. The Reine Claude plums should remain green, and other plums should keep their color
7. At the end, if the sugar is not syrupy enough at the final cooking, cook it further by adding two glasses of water to degrease it, then pour it boiling hot over the plums

title: Confitures d'Abricots Verds et Amandes Vertes (Green Apricot and Green Almond Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Green apricots or green almonds
- Water
- Sugar, one pound per pound of fruit

## Directions

1. After you have removed the down from the apricots or almonds as marked previously for green apricot compotes on page 481, make them cook in water in the same manner until by pricking them with a pin it enters easily and the apricot falls by itself
2. Then clarify sugar and put one pound of sugar for one pound of fruit
3. Boil your sugar for four or five consecutive days, morning and evening, without your fruit, which you put to drain on a sieve
4. Put it back in a terrine and pour your sugar over it, which must be only slightly more than lukewarm
5. When your apricots and almonds are well green, your preserve will be made

title: Confitures de Cerises (Cherry Jam)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherries
- One pound of sugar for every pound of cherries

## Directions

1. Remove the stems and pits from your cherries
2. Mix one pound of sugar for every pound of cherries
3. When the sugar reaches its cooking degree, be careful to skim the jam well
4. Let it cool slightly in your basin before putting it in pots

title: Confitures de Cerises (Cherry Preserves)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the stems and pits from the cherries
2. Prepare them the same way as the apricots, both for the cooking and the dose of sugar

title: Confitures de Gelée de Groseilles (Red Currant Jelly Preserves)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- red currants
- 1 pound of sugar per pound of fruit

## Directions

1. Clarify your sugar as explained previously
2. Put one pound of sugar for one pound of fruit
3. Cook your sugar to the coffee stage, which you will know by dipping your wet finger in a goblet full of water that you hold in your left hand, then dipping your fingers in your sugar and immediately putting them back in the goblet

title: Confitures de Gelée de Muscat et de Verjus (Muscat and Verjuice Jelly Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Muscat grapes
- Verjuice

## Directions

1. Make it in the same manner as currant jelly bourgeois style, which precedes this, you have only to follow that method

title: Confitures de Groseilles (Currant Preserves)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants
- Water
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Separate the currants from the stems so the wood of the stems does not give a bitter taste
2. Place them in a pot with a glass of water
3. When they have boiled a few times and burst, place them in a horsehair sieve
4. Pass the juice a second time over the pulp so it is very clear
5. Squeeze it to extract all the liquid
6. For thirty pounds of juice, clarify thirty pounds of sugar and bring it to the crack stage
7. Pour the juice over the sugar and boil for a quarter hour
8. Skim well and pour into pots
9. Or put the currant juice on the fire and add sugar in pieces
10. Let the preserves boil well and skim
11. Place a few drops on a plate and tilt it to see if the preserve sets
12. You can use only three-quarters of a pound of sugar per pound of fruit, but the preserves must cook longer
13. If using cassonade, it must be clarified

title: Confitures de Groseilles à la Bourgeoise (Currant Preserve Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Currants
- Sugar, clarified, equal measure to the juice

## Directions

1. Clarify your sugar as explained previously on page 540
2. String the currants and put them in the pan
3. Make them melt on the fire by letting them boil once or twice
4. Put them afterwards to drain on a sieve
5. Measure your currant juice and put as many pints of clarified sugar in another clean pan that you will cook to the crack stage
6. You will know its cooking stage by means of a goblet full of water that you will hold in your left hand, wet the upper fingers of your right hand that you will quickly dip into your sugar, and then into the goblet, and if the sugar that stuck to your fingers dries and is brittle, you will put your currant juice into it
7. Make two boils covered and skim well
8. Put your jelly into your pots

title: Confitures de Verjus Entiers (Whole Verjuice Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Verjuice, one pound
- Sugar in powder, one pound

## Directions

1. Put everything into a pan and cook on a good fire
2. Make it boil three or four times covered
3. If your verjuice is not well green, you must let it cook at a gentle boil until it is well green
4. Put it immediately into pots

title: Confitures Verjus (Verjuice Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Verjuice, one pound
- Sugar, one pound

## Directions

1. Remove all the seeds from your verjuice
2. Take one part of verjuice that you will put in the pan and one part of sugar on top, which must be in powder form
3. Put your verjuice and sugar layer by layer until finished
4. Then put your verjuice on a small fire to make it boil gently until it becomes well green, which is how you will know it is at perfect cooking stage
5. Put immediately into pots

title: Conserve d'Abricots (Apricot Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 15 to 18 apricots
- water

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pound of sugar in the same way as the violet preserve
2. Make apricot marmalade: take 15 to 18 apricots not quite ripe, remove the pits and skins, cut them into pieces, and cook them with a little water until they become a well-dried and thick marmalade
3. When the sugar is at its point, add a quarter pound of apricot marmalade
4. Put the marmalade in the sugar and finish the preserve as with the redcurrant preserve

title: Conserve d'Oranges (Orange Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound or three quarterons of sugar
- ½ glass of water
- Zest of 1 sweet orange, grated very finely

## Directions

1. Put the sugar in a pan with half a glass of water
2. Boil it without skimming until, when dipping the skimming spoon in the sugar and blowing through the holes, large sugar sparks come out
3. Remove from the fire
4. When it is half cold, add the finely grated orange zest that you have ready
5. Stir it with the sugar until it begins to thicken
6. Pour the preserve into the mold
7. Lemon and bigarade preserves are made the same way

title: Conserve de Café et de Chocolat (Coffee and Chocolate Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of sugar
- 1 glass of water
- 1 ounce of ground coffee
- ½ ounce of finely grated chocolate (for chocolate version)

## Directions

1. Put 1 pound of sugar in a pan with a glass of water
2. Boil and skim
3. Continue boiling until, when dipping the skimming spoon in the sugar and blowing through the holes, small sugar sparks come out
4. Remove from the fire and let it cool a little
5. Add 1 ounce of ground coffee and stir together
6. When well mixed, pour the preserve into the mold
7. For chocolate preserve, use the same method but with only ½ ounce of finely grated chocolate per pound of sugar

title: Conserve de Cerises (Cherry Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 1 glass water
- 1 pound fresh cherries

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pound of sugar in the same way as for redcurrant preserve
2. Take 1 pound of fine cherries, remove the stems and pits, put them on the fire to release their liquid
3. Pass them through a sieve, pressing firmly so only the skins remain in the sieve
4. Put on the fire everything you have passed through to dry it
5. Finish your preserve as with the redcurrant preserve

title: Conserve de Fleurs d'Oranges (Orange Blossom Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 1 large glass water
- 4 ounces orange blossom petals
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Put 1 pound of sugar in a pan with 1 large glass of water, bring to boil and skim
2. Continue boiling until dipping a skimmer in the sugar and shaking it with a backhand motion, sparks fly that hold together
3. Remove from heat and add orange blossoms prepared as follows: take 4 ounces of white orange blossom petals, cut them with a few knife strokes, and moisten them with the juice of half a lemon
4. Put them in the sugar and stir without being on the fire until the sugar becomes white around the edges of the pan
5. Pour immediately into a paper mold as with the previous preserves

title: Conserve de Framboise (Raspberry Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 1 glass water
- 1 pound raspberries
- 2 ounces redcurrants

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pound of sugar in the same way as for redcurrant preserve
2. Crush and pass through a sieve 1 pound of raspberries with 2 ounces of redcurrants, all cleaned
3. Put what has passed through the sieve into a pan on the fire to dry it
4. Put it into the sugar and finish your preserve as with the redcurrant preserve

title: Conserve de Groseilles (Redcurrant Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound redcurrants
- 1 glass water
- 1 pound sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the currants from their stems and put them on the fire with 1 glass of water
2. Cook them until they have released their liquid
3. Pass them through a sieve, pressing firmly so that only the skins remain in the sieve
4. Put everything that has passed through onto the fire and reduce it until it forms a thick marmalade
5. Put 1 pound of sugar in a pan with 1 glass of water, bring to boil and skim
6. Continue boiling until dipping your fingers in water, then in the sugar, and stirring them in water, the sugar remaining on your fingers breaks cleanly
7. Remove from heat and add your currant marmalade
8. Stir together until you see a small glaze forming on top
9. Pour into a paper mold as with the violet preserve

title: Conserve de Guimauve (Marshmallow Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound marshmallow
- 1 glass water
- 1 pound sugar

## Directions

1. Cut into very small pieces about 1 pound of marshmallow after scraping and washing it
2. Cook it in a pot with 1 glass of water until it becomes a marmalade
3. Pass it through a sieve, pressing firmly
4. Put back on the fire what you have passed through, stirring constantly until it is very thick
5. Cook 1 pound of sugar in the same way as the redcurrant preserve
6. Add the marmalade to it and stir until the sugar begins to whiten on the edges of the pan
7. Pour into a mold as with the previous preserves

title: Conserve de Raisin (Grape Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ pounds of grapes (preferably muscat)
- 1 pound of sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Remove the grapes from their stems
2. Place them on the fire to make them burst
3. Pass them forcefully through a sieve until only the skins and seeds remain in the sieve
4. Put everything that passed through the sieve on the fire and dry it until the marmalade is very thick
5. Cook 1 pound of sugar the same way as for currant preserve
6. When the sugar reaches the proper cooking point, add the marmalade to it and finish in the same manner

title: Conserve de Verjus (Verjuice Preserve)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 1 pound ripe verjuice

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pound of sugar in the same way as the violet preserve
2. When it reaches its cooking point, remove from heat and stir for about 2 minutes
3. Make a verjuice marmalade: take 1 pound of ripe verjuice, remove the stems, put it on the fire to cook until it becomes a marmalade, and put it in a sieve to press firmly until only skins and seeds remain in the sieve
4. Put the marmalade back on the fire to dry it until it is very thick
5. Put it in the sugar and stir them well together until the sugar begins to whiten on the edges of the pan
6. Pour immediately into a mold as with the violet preserve

title: Conserves de Violettes (Violet Preserves)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one sheet of white paper
- one pound of sugar
- one glass of water
- violets, cleaned

## Directions

1. Take one sheet of white paper that you leave doubled and fold all around to make a border the height of a good inch, as if you wanted to make a case
2. Have one pound of sugar that you put in a pan with a glass of water
3. Make it boil and skim
4. Continue boiling until, dipping the skimmer in it and shaking it with a backward motion, long sparks rise in the air that hold together
5. Remove from the fire and when it is half-cold, add cleaned violets that you pound very fine in a small mortar
6. Mix them well with the sugar, stirring quickly with the skimmer

title: Consommé (Consommé)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight or ten pounds of sliced beef
- Eight old hens
- Two calf necks
- Four calf knuckles
- Great broth
- Carrots
- Turnips
- Onions
- Three cloves

## Directions

1. Take eight or ten pounds of sliced beef, eight old hens, two calf necks, four calf knuckles
2. Put your meat in your pot
3. Fill it with great broth
4. Make it skim
5. Be careful to refresh your broth three or four times to make the foam rise well
6. After make your consommé boil gently
7. Garnish your pot with carrots, turnips, onions, three cloves
8. When your meats are cooked, pass your consommé through fine cloth or a silk sieve so that your consommé is very clear
9. Do not add salt if you moisten it with great broth

title: Cornichon de Blé de Turquie (Pickled Corn)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Corn still in the kernel and green
- Water
- Vinegar (one third the amount of water)
- Cloves
- Salt

## Directions

1. Take corn that is still in the kernel and completely green.
2. Cook it halfway in water.
3. Remove it and refresh it in fresh water.
4. Then put it to pickle: boil water with a third vinegar, some cloves and salt.
5. Pour the boiling brine over the corn and place it on hot ashes.
6. The next day, make the same brine again and pour it over the corn.
7. Continue this way until it is very green.
8. Then cover your pots and store them to use the same way as gherkins.

title: Coulis Bourgeois (Bourgeois Stock)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Small pieces of lard
- Veal round
- Onions
- Root vegetables
- Bouillon
- Butter
- Flour
- One tablespoon of flour and butter per half-septier

## Directions

1. Put small pieces of lard and veal round in the bottom of a casserole according to the quantity of coulis desired; for good coulis, use one pound per half-septier
2. Add two or three onions and as many root vegetables
3. Cover the casserole well and cook over low heat so the meat has time to release its juices
4. Then increase the heat until the meat is ready to brown
5. Lower to low heat so the meat browns gently to the casserole, creating a good gratin
6. Remove the meat and vegetables to a plate
7. Put a piece of butter and flour in the casserole according to the quantity of coulis desired, about one tablespoon per half-septier
8. Cook over heat until the roux is beautiful
9. Add hot bouillon
10. Return the meat to make it cook for another two hours over very low heat; degrease the coulis often
11. When finished, pass it through fine cloth or a sieve for use

title: Coulis de Lentilles (Lentil Coulis)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Lentils
- Good bouillon, fat or lean as needed

## Directions

1. Wash and pick through the lentils
2. Cook them with good bouillon, either fat or lean depending on your intended use
3. When cooked, pass through a sieve, moistening with their cooking bouillon
4. Season with good taste
5. Serve as you judge appropriate, either as soup or in a terrine

title: Coulis de Lentilles (Lentil Coulis)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half-liter of lentils, more or less according to the size of your soup
- good bouillon

## Directions

1. Choose lentils, peel and wash them
2. Cook them with good bouillon
3. When they are cooked, strain them through a fine sieve
4. Season your coulis with good taste
5. Note that lentils à la reine are the best for all sorts of coulis

title: Cous de Mouton aux Petites Racines (Lamb Neck with Root Vegetables)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb neck
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- carrots
- small root vegetables
- lard
- bouillon or water
- flour for roux

## Directions

1. Season the lamb necks with salt, pepper, and onions
2. Cover them with lard strips
3. Braise them with bouillon or water
4. Add salt and bring to a boil
5. Cook on low heat for four hours
6. At serving time, drain them and arrange on a platter
7. Pour the small root vegetables over the top
8. If you do not have sauce for the root vegetables, make a light roux that you moisten with the braising liquid in which your lamb necks cooked
9. Prepare the small root vegetables as indicated

title: Cous de Mouton à la Purée de Lentilles (Lamb Neck with Lentil Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb neck
- lentil purée

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your lamb necks as in the previous recipe
2. At serving time, drain them and remove the string
3. Arrange them on the platter
4. Cover them with a lentil purée
5. You may also serve them with pea purée, turnip purée, bean purée, etc.

title: Croquantes (Brittle Cookies)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a litron of flour
- a quarteron of fine sugar
- one egg white
- half a spoonful of orange flower water
- butter the size of half an egg
- half a glass of water
- a small pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Put on a table half a litron of flour with a quarteron of fine sugar, one egg white, half a spoonful of orange flower water, butter the size of half an egg, half a glass of water, and a small pinch of salt
2. Knead everything together

title: Croquantes (Crispy Wafers)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a litre of flour
- a quarter pound of sugar
- one egg white
- half a teaspoon of orange blossom water
- butter the size of half an egg
- half a glass of water
- a small pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Place flour and sugar on a table
2. Add egg white, orange blossom water, butter, water, and salt
3. Knead everything together to make a well-bound and firm dough
4. Roll out very thin
5. Cut small bases and place them on small pastry molds
6. Bake for a quarter hour in a very gentle oven
7. When cold, lightly spread currant jelly or other preserves on top
8. For cut-out croquantes, use the same dough with more egg whites and no water

title: Croquettes d'Agneau (Lamb Croquettes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- a lamb leg or other lamb meat
- lamb fat
- veal udder cooked
- mushrooms cooked
- nutmeg grated
- coarse pepper
- six spoonfuls of velvet sauce
- four spoonfuls of aspic
- three whole eggs and two yolks
- fine salt
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook a lamb leg on the spit or other lamb meat that has been roasted
2. Cool and remove the meat
3. Remove skin and nerves; cut the meat into small dice
4. Take some lamb fat, cut into small dice, and mix with the meat
5. If no fat available, use cooked veal udder
6. Add cooked mushrooms cut into small dice
7. Add a little grated nutmeg and coarse pepper
8. Take six spoonfuls of velvet sauce and four spoonfuls of aspic, mix together
9. Reduce the sauce by more than half; it must be somewhat thick
10. When reduced, add a liaison of three egg yolks
11. Stir the sauce on fire without leaving it so the liaison does not break
12. When the sauce is bound, add butter the size of an egg and melt while stirring well
13. Check seasoning and pass through a sieve over the meat
14. Stir well when the sauce is added
15. Cool the ragout
16. Stir it and place spoonfuls on a tile, successively until finished, making eighteen or twenty portions
17. If the ragout is not cold enough, let it cool further to make it workable
18. Take the portions and place breadcrumbs on a table
19. Shape as desired: pear-shaped, ball-shaped, oval or elongated
20. Roll well in breadcrumbs
21. Beat three whole eggs and two yolks with a little fine salt and coarse pepper like an omelet
22. Dip the croquettes in the egg so it covers all sides
23. Place in breadcrumbs and ensure they are well breaded so they do not dissolve in frying
24. Leave on a tile
25. Just before serving, put frying oil on the fire
26. When very hot, place the croquettes in it
27. When colored, remove and drain on white linen
28. Dress in a pyramid and fry a little parsley

title: Croquettes de Lapereau (Young Rabbit Croquettes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three young rabbits
- Béchamelle, one small spoonful
- Butter, the size of an egg
- Coarse pepper
- Salt
- Nutmeg, grated
- Breadcrumbs
- Eggs, beaten with salt and coarse pepper
- Parsley for frying

## Directions

1. Roast three young rabbits on the spit
2. Remove the fillets and the largest part of the thighs
3. Cut the meat into small dice, removing nerves and skin
4. Place the meat in a casserole with one small spoonful of béchamelle
5. Reduce the béchamelle to almost half
6. Add butter the size of an egg to the sauce without placing it on the fire, allowing it to melt
7. Pass the sauce through a sieve over the rabbit meat
8. Add a little coarse pepper, a small amount of salt, and a little grated nutmeg
9. Mix the meat well with the sauce; it should be slightly thick
10. When cold, form small portions with a spoon, slightly smaller than an egg
11. Shape the croquettes as desired: egg-shaped, pear-shaped, or rounded sticks
12. Roll them in breadcrumbs
13. Dip them in beaten eggs with a little salt and coarse pepper
14. Ensure the eggs coat all the croquettes
15. Roll them again in breadcrumbs, ensuring they are well coated all over to prevent them from bursting
16. At the moment of serving, put them in fairly hot fat
17. When they have a nice color, remove them and drain on white linen
18. Fry a handful of parsley and place it on top of and around the croquettes
19. If you do not have béchamelle, reduce velouté into which you put a liaison of three eggs

title: Croquettes de Morue (Cod Croquettes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked cod
- Butter, size of two eggs
- Flour, one small tablespoon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg, grated, a little
- Cream, half a glass
- Eggs, four or five
- Breadcrumbs for coating

## Directions

1. When your cod is cooked, cut it into dice and put it in a saucepan.
2. Add butter the size of two eggs, one small tablespoon of flour, a little salt, pepper, a little grated nutmeg, and half a glass of cream.
3. Place your sauce on the fire and stir until it comes to a boil.
4. Pour it over your cod, mix it well with the sauce, and let it cool.
5. Divide it into fifteen or twenty portions and shape your croquettes into an agreeable and uniform form.
6. Coat them with breadcrumbs.
7. Break four or five eggs, season them, beat them, and dip your croquettes in them; coat a second time with breadcrumbs.
8. An instant before serving, place your frying oil on the fire; when it is very hot, put your croquettes in it, and as soon as they have a nice golden color, remove them.
9. Place them on a white napkin, arrange them in pyramids, and put fried parsley on top.

title: Croquettes de Palais de Bœuf (Beef Palate Croquettes)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three beef palates cooked in water
- bouillon
- garlic clove
- two cloves
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- meat forcemeat
- flour
- white wine
- fine oil

## Directions

1. Peel the cooked palates and cut them in two crosswise through their entire length
2. Let them take on flavor for half an hour by simmering over low heat with bouillon, a garlic clove, two cloves, thyme, bay leaf, basil, salt, and pepper
3. Drain and cool them
4. Place on each piece some seasoned meat forcemeat about the thickness of a small coin
5. Roll the beef palate pieces and dip them in a batter made with flour diluted with a spoonful of oil and half a setier of white wine, and fine salt
6. The batter should be thin enough to pour from a spoon without being too thin
7. Fry them and serve garnished with fried parsley

title: Croquettes de palais de Bœuf (Beef Palate Croquettes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate cooked in blanc
- velouté
- egg yolks
- butter
- bread crumbs
- salt
- coarse pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Your palates cooked in blanc, drain and cut them into small dice
2. Have a velouté much more reduced than for a sauce, which you bind with two or three egg yolks
3. Add a small piece of butter to give it softness
4. Throw your diced palates into your sauce and stir everything together
5. Make sure there is not too much sauce
6. Take some with a spoon and make thirty small portions on a board
7. Let them cool so they are more manageable
8. Then with these small portions, shape them with your hands into whatever form you want, whether pear-shaped, round, oval, etc.
9. First put them in bread crumbs
10. Then pass them à l'œuf, that is to say after the first breading, put them in beaten eggs seasoned with a little salt and coarse pepper
11. When your croquettes are soaked, put them in bread crumbs
12. At the moment of serving fry them
13. Your frying oil must be very hot
14. Put a little fried parsley on top

title: Croquettes de Quenelles de Lapin (Rabbit Quenelle Croquettes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbit quenelles
- Velouté sauce
- Game fumé
- Egg yolks
- Eggs
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Breadcrumbs
- Oil or fat for frying
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Make your quenelles and poach them as directed in the rabbit quenelles article
2. Place them on a dish and let them drain
3. Reduce velouté with game fumé
4. When well reduced, add a liaison of three egg yolks
5. Pour your sauce over your quenelles
6. Let them cool and coat well with sauce
7. Place in breadcrumbs
8. Break four eggs in which you put a little salt and coarse pepper and beat as for an omelet
9. Dip your quenelles in the beaten egg ensuring it covers everywhere
10. Place them in breadcrumbs and give them an attractive shape
11. Half an hour before serving, put your frying oil on the fire
12. When well hot, place your croquettes in it
13. When they have a good color, remove them
14. Drain them on white linen
15. Fry parsley leaves and place on top of your croquettes

title: Croquettes de Saumon (Salmon Croquettes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- Cream sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the salmon in good water or a court-bouillon
2. When cold, prepare it as described for cod croquettes
3. Make a cream sauce the same way as for cod croquettes

title: Croûtes au Pot (Pot Bread Crusts)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- crusts of bread
- broth
- pot fat
- three entries of bread
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Take crusts of bread that are not too thick
2. Mince them
3. Put them in a shallow dish
4. Pour broth and pot fat over them
5. Put on the fire until they are gratinéed
6. Take three entries of bread, remove the crumb
7. Soak them in pot fat broth
8. Season with a little salt and coarse pepper
9. Place them upright on your gratin
10. At the moment of serving, drain the fat so the soup is dry
11. Put broth in a vessel so each person can pour as much as they wish on their plate with bread and gratin

title: Croûtons de Purée de Bécasses (Croutons with Woodcock Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four woodcocks
- Cooked lard the size of one and a half eggs
- Aromatic herbs
- White wine
- Parsley
- Bay leaf
- Clove
- Bouillon
- Velouté sauce
- Bread for croutons

## Directions

1. Roast three or four woodcocks on the spit; when cooked, let them cool
2. Remove the flesh and interior of your woodcocks and place them in the mortar with cooked lard the size of one and a half eggs and a little pounded aromatic herbs
3. Put your trimmings in a casserole with a glass of white wine, a little parsley leaves, a bay leaf, a clove, two glasses of bouillon, and about six spoonfuls of velouté sauce
4. Reduce this sauce by half and strain it through cloth, pressing it lightly
5. When it is cold, pound the flesh of your woodcocks and add this sauce to it
6. If your purée is difficult to pass, moisten it with a little consommé
7. Place it in a casserole and keep it warm without boiling, or use a bain-marie
8. Make oval-pointed croutons, thick about an inch and a half; before browning them

title: Crème au Café (Coffee Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three half-setiers of water
- Two ounces of coffee
- One chopine of milk
- A piece of sugar
- Five egg yolks
- A pinch of flour

## Directions

1. Put three half-setiers of water in a coffee pot.
2. When it boils, add two ounces of coffee, stir with a spoon and return it to the fire to boil until it rises four or five times.
3. Let it settle to draw it off clearly and put it in a saucepan with one chopine of milk and a piece of sugar.
4. Make it boil until only the amount you need for the size of your dish remains.
5. Mix five egg yolks with a pinch of flour, then add the cream.
6. Pass it through a tamis to pour into the serving dish, which should be on a saucepan with water ready to boil.
7. Cover with a saucepan lid with a little fire on top.
8. Let it boil until the cream is set.
9. Serve hot.

title: Crème au Caramel (Caramel Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one chopine of milk
- one half-setier of cream
- a small piece of cinnamon
- a good pinch of coriander
- the zest of green lemon
- one quarteron of sugar
- one half-glass of water
- five egg yolks
- a pinch of flour

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan one chopine of milk, one half-setier of cream, with a small piece of cinnamon, a good pinch of coriander, the zest of green lemon, and bring to boil for a quarter-hour
2. Remove from the fire, and put in a small office pan one quarteron of sugar with one half-glass of water
3. Make it boil on a stove until it reaches caramel, that is to say, the color of dark cinnamon
4. Remove from the fire and add the cream to it; return to the fire until the sugar is dissolved with the cream
5. Then dilute five egg yolks with a pinch of flour, add the cream to it, pass it through a sieve to cook it in a bain-marie as the previous ones

title: Crème au Chocolat (Chocolate Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two tablets of chocolate
- One half-quarter pound of sugar
- One chopine of milk
- One half-setier of cream
- Five egg yolks

## Directions

1. Grate two tablets of chocolate and put them in a saucepan with one half-quarter pound of sugar, one chopine of milk, and one half-setier of cream.
2. Make it boil until it is reduced by one third.
3. When it is half-cold, blend in five egg yolks.
4. Pass it through a tamis and set it in a bain-marie like the previous one.

title: Crème au Petit Pain (Small Bread Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three small coffee rolls
- milk
- sugar
- two small spoonfuls of flour
- seven egg yolks
- three macarons, crushed
- green lemon, chopped
- a piece of sugar
- a chopine of cream

## Directions

1. Take three small coffee rolls, remove the bottom crust so you can remove the crumb without damaging the top crust, soak the top and bottom crusts with milk and sugar
2. In a saucepan, put two small spoonfuls of flour which you will dilute with seven egg yolks (set the whites aside in a basin), put with the yolks three crushed macarons, chopped green lemon, a piece of sugar, a chopine of cream; cook everything on the fire for a quarter-hour and remove it
3. Remove the small rolls from the milk to drain them
4. Put the dish you must serve on, put this cream in the bottom, the bottom of the rolls on top, take the tops of the rolls; in place of the crumb you removed, put cream in it, and put them in the dish as if they were whole, cover them on top with the same cream, finish them on top so that each roll keeps its shape
5. Cook them in the oven or under a covered pie dish for half an hour
6. Take egg whites which you whip into snow and put sugar into it
7. Remove the small rolls from the oven to cover them with these whipped egg whites while always preserving their shape, put fine sugar on top, and put them back in the oven until they are a beautiful yellow color, and serve

title: Crème Bachique (Bacchic Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three half-setiers of white wine
- two green lemon rinds
- a pinch of coriander
- a small piece of cinnamon
- three ounces of sugar
- a half-teaspoonful of flour
- six egg yolks

## Directions

1. Bring in a saucepan three half-setiers of white wine with two green lemon rinds, a pinch of coriander, a small piece of cinnamon, and three ounces of sugar
2. Boil at a gentle fire for a good quarter hour
3. In another saucepan, mix a half-teaspoonful of flour with six egg yolks
4. Gradually add the boiled wine mixture to the flour and eggs
5. When it is half-cooled, pass everything through a sieve
6. Cook your cream in a bain-marie
7. When it is set, remove it from the bain-marie and put it to cool until you serve it

title: Crème Blanche au Naturel (Natural White Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of milk
- one chopine of cream
- one piece of sugar
- a little rennet
- water

## Directions

1. Take a pint of milk, a chopine of cream, and a piece of sugar which you boil together and reduce to a third
2. Let it cool until you can put your finger in it without burning yourself
3. Take a little rennet which you dilute with water in a tablespoon, mix it well into the cream, and pass the whole thing through a sieve
4. Take the dish you must serve from and put it on hot ash, pour your cream into it and cover it with a lid on which you also put hot ash
5. Leave it until the cream is set, then carry it to a cool place to serve it cold

title: Crème Bourgeoise (Bourgeois Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one chopine of good cream
- half a teaspoon of orange blossom water
- a good half-quarteron of sugar
- two eggs, yolks and whites

## Directions

1. Whip together the cream, orange blossom water, sugar, and eggs until the cream is thick
2. Put everything in a silver compotier and set the cream on hot ashes, covered with a pie-dish lid with a little fire on top
3. When it is set, put it to cool on ice or in a cool place until serving

title: Crème Brûlée (Burnt Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two large spoonfuls of flour
- four eggs, whites and yolks
- one half-spoonful of orange blossom water
- finely chopped green lemon zest
- one half-setier of cream
- one half-setier of milk
- salt the size of a pea
- two ounces of sugar
- one piece of sugar
- one half-glass of water

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan two large spoonfuls of flour that you dilute little by little with four eggs, whites and yolks, one half-spoonful of orange blossom water and a pinch of finely chopped green lemon zest
2. Moisten with one half-setier of cream and one half-setier of milk, add salt the size of a pea, and two ounces of sugar
3. Cook on a small fire for half an hour, always stirring
4. Then put a piece of sugar with one half-glass of water in the dish you must serve
5. Make them boil on a good stove until it is a beautiful cinnamon color, and quickly pour the cream into it
6. Have a large knife ready to spread the sugar that is on the edge of the dish by bringing it onto the cream

title: Crème de Fraises (Strawberry Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a bucket of strawberries, cleaned, washed and drained
- three half-setiers of cream
- half a bucket of milk
- sugar
- a grain of rennet the size of a coffee bean

## Directions

1. Pound the strawberries in a mortar
2. Boil three half-setiers of cream with half a bucket of milk and sugar, let them boil and reduce by half
3. Let cool slightly and add the strawberries, mix together
4. Dissolve a grain of rennet the size of a coffee bean in the cream when it is no longer warm
5. Pass it immediately through a sieve
6. Put the mixture into a compotier that can be placed on hot ashes, cover with a lid and put some hot ashes on top
7. When it is set, put it in a cool place or on ice until serving

title: Crème de Framboises (Raspberry Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- cream
- sugar
- two fresh egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make it the same way as strawberry cream, with the difference that when removing the cream from the fire after it is sufficiently reduced, add two fresh egg yolks that you will have previously diluted with two spoonfuls of cream
2. Put back on the fire briefly while stirring the cream only to cook the eggs without boiling, for fear it will turn
3. Finish as with the previous recipe

title: Crème de Riz pour les Convalescents (Rice Cream for Convalescents)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarteron of cleaned and washed rice
- good fatty broth

## Directions

1. Have one quarteron of rice cleaned and washed in three or four lukewarm waters
2. Put it to cook with good fatty broth; when it is cooked and thick, crush it with a spoon and then put it in a strainer to pass it, pressing hard with a wooden spoon
3. Add from time to time a little hot broth to help it pass through
4. Serve it the thickness of a double cream

title: Crème de Thé, de Céleri, d'Estragon, de Persil (Tea, Celery, Tarragon, and Parsley Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one half-setier of cream
- one chopine of milk
- nearly one quarteron of sugar
- tea or tarragon or celery or parsley as desired
- five egg yolks
- a pinch of flour
- rennet or chicken gizzard skin

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan one half-setier of cream and one chopine of milk, nearly one quarteron of sugar
2. Bring to boil and reduce by a third
3. If it is a tea cream, add enough tea to make five cups; let it boil a moment and pass through a sieve
4. If you wish to make a tarragon cream, take two branches of tarragon that you will boil for half a quarter-hour in water, and then put it to boil in the cream; leave it only long enough to give the taste, and remove it promptly, for fear the taste does not dominate
5. Do the same for celery and parsley cream
6. The cream being passed through a sieve, dilute five egg yolks with a pinch of flour, and cook it in a bain-marie as the coffee one
7. If you wish to serve them cold, put no eggs or flour in them; after they are passed through a sieve and the cream is lukewarm, dilute rennet or chopped chicken gizzard skins in it, pass it through a sieve and put it in the dish you must serve, which you put on some hot ashes; cover with a lid in which you also put some hot ashes, leave until the cream is set, and put it in the cold until you serve

title: Crème en Neige (Snow Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one chopine of milk
- one chopine of cream
- one quarteron of sugar
- rennet or gizzard
- one chopine of double cream

## Directions

1. Bring to boil one chopine of milk and one chopine of cream with one quarteron of sugar and reduce by half
2. When it is lukewarm, set it with rennet or gizzard, as the previous ones
3. When it is cold, take one chopine of double cream that you will whip with a whisk
4. As it foams, remove the foam with a skimming spoon to put it on a sieve and a plate below
5. Continue to whip the cream until you have enough to cover in a dome the velvety cream
6. Serve immediately

title: Crème Fouettée (Whipped Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of good cream
- a little candied orange blossom, finely chopped
- half a quarteron of fine sugar
- the size of a hazelnut of powdered gum tragacanth

## Directions

1. Put the cream in a bowl with the candied orange blossom, finely chopped, half a quarteron of fine sugar, and gum tragacanth the size of a hazelnut, powdered
2. Whip the cream and as it foams, remove it with a skimmer and place it on a sieve with a dish underneath to catch what drips
3. Continue whipping the cream until none remains in the bowl
4. If you do not have enough, take what dripped from the sieve and whip it again
5. Dress the cream in a compotier
6. It can be garnished with candied citron cut into strips, or served plain
7. Those who like lemon can add a little finely chopped green lemon to the cream before whipping
8. Dress it in the compotier in the form of a dome, or for variety, in the form of several small rocks

title: Crème Fouettée de Fraises et Framboises (Whipped Cream of Strawberries and Raspberries)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three half-setiers of double cream
- two handfuls of strawberries or raspberries, well cleaned
- two ounces of fine sugar

## Directions

1. Whip three half-setiers of double cream, and as it foams, remove it with a skimmer and place it in a sieve with a dish underneath to catch what drips
2. When you have whipped it all, take two handfuls of strawberries or raspberries that are well cleaned and pass them through a sieve by pressing them hard
3. Mix in two ounces of fine sugar with the strawberries or raspberries
4. Mix everything together by whipping it with the cream a moment before serving, then dress it in the compotier

title: Crème Frite (Fried Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- About three spoonfuls of flour
- Six eggs, whites and yolks
- A bit of grated lemon peel
- Pralined orange flower, chopped
- One chopine of milk
- A piece of sugar
- A small pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Put about three spoonfuls of flour in a saucepan and dilute it little by little with six eggs, whites and yolks, a bit of grated lemon peel, pralined and chopped orange flower, one chopine of milk, a piece of sugar, and a small pinch of salt.
2. Cook it on a low fire for half an hour, stirring constantly.
3. When it is very thick, spread it to the thickness of half a finger on a floured dish and sprinkle flour over it.
4. When it is cold, cut it as you wish to fry it in very hot oil.
5. Then glaze it with sugar and a hot poker.

title: Crème Glacée (Iced Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a small handful of flour
- green lemon, chopped very finely
- a pinch of candied orange flower, pounded
- a piece of sugar
- eight egg yolks
- a chopine of cream
- a half-setier of milk

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, put a small handful of flour, finely chopped green lemon, a pinch of pounded pralined orange flower, and a piece of sugar
2. Dilute everything with eight egg yolks (set the whites aside in a clean basin) and dilute the yolks with a chopine of cream and a half-setier of milk
3. Cook this cream on the fire for half an hour
4. When it is thick, remove it from the fire and whip the whites with a whisk
5. When they are well whipped, mix them into the cream and put this cream in the dish you must serve
6. Put sugar on top so the cream is well covered
7. Cook it in the oven, which should not be too hot, or under a covered pie dish: when it is well risen and glazed, serve

title: Crème Gratinée (Gratinéed Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs (two whole and four yolks)
- a small handful of flour
- a chopine of cream
- three macarons, crushed
- a little green lemon, chopped
- a piece of sugar

## Directions

1. Dilute two whole eggs and four yolks with a small handful of flour, a chopine of cream, three crushed macarons, a little chopped green lemon, and a piece of sugar
2. Put the dish you must serve on a stove with gentle fire, put this cream in the dish after mixing it well, cook this cream for one hour, and pass the hot shovel over it to give it color
3. If you cook this cream at too high a heat, the gratin will be burned and taste bad

title: Crème Italienne (Italian Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three half-setiers of milk
- A bit of green lemon peel
- A pinch of coriander
- A small piece of cinnamon
- A bit more than half a quarter pound of sugar
- Two grains of salt
- A pinch of flour
- Six egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put three half-setiers of milk in a saucepan and bring it to boil.
2. Add a bit of green lemon peel, a pinch of coriander, a small piece of cinnamon, a bit more than half a quarter pound of sugar, and two grains of salt.
3. Make it boil and reduce to half.
4. Let it cool a little.
5. In another saucepan, have a pinch of flour mixed with six egg yolks.
6. Pour your cream into it little by little, stirring as you go.
7. Pass it through a tamis and pour it into the serving dish.
8. Set it in a bain-marie, and before serving, pass a hot poker over it to color it.

title: Crème Légère (Light Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three half-setiers of milk
- sugar
- green lemon rind
- orange flower water
- a full teaspoon of flour
- six egg yolks

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, put three half-setiers of milk with sugar, green lemon rind, and orange flower water, boil everything together and reduce by half, remove from the fire and let it cool
2. In a saucepan, dilute a full teaspoon of flour with six egg yolks (set the whites aside in a clean basin), then put your cream with the egg yolks, diluting little by little, pass your cream through a sieve and cook it in a bain-marie
3. When it is cooked and set as it should be, remove it from the bain-marie, whip the egg whites you set aside until they are well risen, add fine sugar to them, cover your cream with the egg whites in the shape of a dome, put fine sugar on top

title: Crème à la Duchesse (Duchess Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one chopine of milk
- one half-setier of cream
- a small piece of cinnamon
- the zest of green lemon
- one half-quarteron of sugar
- six egg yolks
- a pinch of flour
- a few bitter almond biscuits
- one half-tablet of chocolate
- a little pralined orange blossom

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan one chopine of milk with one half-setier of cream, a small piece of cinnamon, the zest of green lemon, one half-quarteron of sugar
2. Bring to boil for half an hour, and reduce by a third
3. Pass it through a sieve and then dilute it with six egg yolks and a pinch of flour
4. Add a few bitter almond biscuits, one half-tablet of chocolate, a little pralined orange blossom, all chopped very fine
5. Cook it in a bain-marie as the coffee one

title: Crème à la Frangipane (Frangipane Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoonfuls of flour
- grated green lemon zest
- toasted orange blossom, chopped
- a small pinch of salt
- five eggs, whites and yolks
- one chopine of good milk
- a piece of butter
- sugar for glazing

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan two spoonfuls of flour with grated green lemon zest, toasted orange blossom, chopped, a small pinch of salt
2. Dilute with five eggs, whites and yolks, one chopine of good milk, and a piece of butter
3. Cook it, always stirring, on the fire for half an hour
4. When it is cold, use it to make a frangipane tart or small tarts; you need only put it on puff pastry
5. When it is cooked, glaze it with sugar

title: Crème à la Magdelaine (Magdelaine Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs, whites and yolks
- a pinch of flour
- grated green lemon zest
- a very small piece of pounded cinnamon
- a few crushed bitter almond biscuits
- one half-spoonful of orange blossom water
- one chopine of cream
- one half-quarteron of sugar
- salt the size of a pea

## Directions

1. Dilute four eggs, whites and yolks, with a pinch of flour, a little grated green lemon zest, a very small piece of pounded cinnamon, a few crushed bitter almond biscuits, one half-spoonful of orange blossom water, one chopine of cream, and one half-quarteron of sugar, salt the size of a pea
2. Put your pan on a medium fire, pour the cream into it
3. When it is cooked, throw a little sugar on it to glaze it with the red spatula

title: Crème à la Moèlle (Marrow Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- eight egg yolks
- two small spoonfuls of flour
- a little green lemon, chopped very finely
- a little orange flower water
- two half-setiers of cream
- a piece of sugar
- a quarter-pound of marrow

## Directions

1. Dilute eight egg yolks with two small spoonfuls of flour, a little finely chopped green lemon, a little orange flower water, two half-setiers of cream, and a piece of sugar
2. Take a quarter-pound of marrow which you melt on the fire and pass through a sieve, and put this marrow into the cream
3. Cook this cream on the fire for half an hour, then remove it
4. Whip the eight egg whites that you set aside and mix them well into the cream, and arrange it in the dish you must serve
5. Cook it in the oven or under a covered pie dish as the previous one; when it is cooked, take a glazer or some clean feather which you dip in good hot butter, and pass it lightly over the cream, put nonpareille immediately on top (these are small dragées of all colors), and serve

title: Crème à la Reine (Cream à la Reine)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of cream
- two ounces of sugar
- half a teaspoon of orange blossom water
- two beaten egg whites

## Directions

1. Put the cream on the fire with two ounces of sugar and boil until it is reduced by half
2. Remove from the fire and add half a teaspoon of orange blossom water and two beaten egg whites
3. Stir it a moment on the fire with the whisk
4. As soon as the egg whites are cooked, dress it in the compotier and let it cool before serving

title: Crème à la Vanille Renversée (Inverted Vanilla Cream)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cream to fill a mold
- sugar
- vanilla
- 12 egg yolks
- 3 whole eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Fill a mold or small molds with cream
2. Bring the cream to a boil
3. Add sugar and vanilla
4. Allow to boil several times and then cool
5. If the mold holds a pint, use 12 egg yolks and 3 whole eggs mixed with the cream
6. Pass five times through cheesecloth
7. Lightly butter the mold and pour the cream into it
8. Place in a bain-marie to set, ensuring the water does not boil vigorously
9. Place fire on the mold cover but not too intense
10. Test if set by placing a finger in the center of the cream
11. When ready to serve, invert the mold onto a plate
12. Heat the remaining cream and turn it like a white sauce
13. When the cream coats a wooden spoon, remove from heat
14. Turn it briefly and pour over the inverted cream

title: Cuisse de Sanglier (Wild Boar Haunch)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild boar haunch
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Fine pepper
- Peppercorns
- Juniper
- Thyme
- Bay laurel
- Basil
- Onions
- Parsley
- Chives
- Lard lardoons
- Aromatic herbs
- Sage
- Quatre épices (four spices)
- White linen cloth
- Buttered braising pan
- Brine from marinade
- White wine
- Water
- Carrots
- Cloves
- Bouquet of parsley and chives
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Burn away the bristles on your haunch well
2. Clean it as best as possible
3. Bone it up to the joint of the shank
4. Lard it with thick lardoons seasoned with ground aromatics, a little sage, quatre épices, salt, and coarse pepper
5. When well larded, place ground aromatics inside
6. Place it in a terrine or tub with plenty of salt, fine pepper, peppercorns, juniper, thyme, bay laurel, basil, onions cut into slices, parsley in branches, and whole chives
7. Leave to marinate four or five days if you have time
8. When you wish to cook it, remove the aromatics from inside your haunch
9. Wrap it in white linen
10. Truss it like a piece of beef
11. Place it in a braising pan with the brine in which it has marinated, six bottles of white wine, as much water, six carrots, six onions, four cloves, a strong bouquet of parsley and chives, and salt
12. If you believe the brine is not sufficient to give it good seasoning, let it simmer for six hours
13. Test it with a skewer to ensure it is cooked; if not, cook it another hour
14. After that, let it rest half an hour in its cooking liquid and remove it
15. Leave it in its skin, or if desired, cover it with breadcrumbs, or if your haunch is fatty, remove the skin and leave it white
16. Glaze it and ensure it has a beautiful shape

title: Cuisses d'Oie à la Purée (Goose Thighs with Puree)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- six fat goose thighs
- salt
- coarse pepper
- lard, finely chopped
- lard for barding
- three carrots
- four onions
- two bay leaves
- thyme
- two cloves
- one spoonful of bouillon

## Directions

1. Lift six fat goose thighs
2. Debone them to the interior joint
3. Season with salt and coarse pepper
4. Place some chopped lard where the bone was
5. Reassemble the meat and sew them, giving them a nice shape
6. Place lard rinds in a casserole
7. Place the thighs there and cover them with lard rinds
8. Add three carrots, four onions, two bay leaves, a little thyme, two cloves, and one spoonful of bouillon
9. Braise for two hours
10. At service time, drain them and remove the thread
11. Dress them on your dish and mask them with your puree; if you wish, you can put a puree of lentils, or of green peas, of onions, and Robert sauce, tomato sauce

title: Cuisses de Dindon sauce Robert (Turkey Legs with Robert Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey legs from roasted turkey
- Salt
- Pepper
- Robert sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the legs from a turkey cooked on the spit
2. Score them with your knife
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Place them on the grill over a gentle fire
5. When they are grilled, place Robert sauce underneath

title: Cuisses de Dindons Accompagnées (Turkey Legs with Accompaniment)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's sweetbreads
- many mushrooms
- lard, parsley, chives, basil, shallots
- salt, coarse pepper
- two egg yolks
- two raw turkey legs
- one glass of white wine
- good broth
- a bouquet of parsley, chives
- lard strips
- coulis or beurre manié with flour
- fresh parsley
- lemon juice or verjuice

## Directions

1. Blanch the calf's sweetbreads in boiling water and cut them into large cubes with many mushrooms also cut into cubes
2. Combine them with grated lard, chopped parsley, chives, basil, shallots, salt, coarse pepper, and two egg yolks
3. Take two raw, well-cleaned turkey legs and bone them completely except for the end bone attached to the leg
4. Fill the legs with the sweetbread mixture and sew them shut so nothing escapes
5. Cook them in a braise made with one glass of white wine and an equal amount of good broth, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and a little salt
6. Cover with lard strips and cook over low heat
7. When cooked and little sauce remains, degrease it, remove the lard strips and bouquet
8. Add two spoonfuls of coulis to bind the sauce, or if you have none, add a piece of butter worked with a pinch of flour and a little blanched chopped parsley
9. Thicken over the fire and serve on the legs with lemon juice or verjuice

title: Cuisses de Dindons à la Crème (Turkey Thighs in Cream Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey thighs
- lard
- butter, size of an egg
- half a spoonful of flour
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- garlic clove
- two shallots
- three cloves
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- two pinches of coriander
- half a septier of milk
- bread crumbs
- half a glass of juice
- two spoonfuls of verjuice

## Directions

1. If using raw turkey thighs, lard them crosswise with thick lard
2. In a casserole, combine butter mixed with flour, salt, pepper, parsley, chives, garlic, shallots, cloves, bay leaf, thyme, basil, and coriander
3. Add half a septier of milk and bring to a boil
4. Place the turkey thighs in the mixture and boil on very low heat
5. When the thighs become tender under the fingers, remove and drain them
6. Take the fat from the cooking liquid and dip the thighs in it
7. Bread them immediately and grill on low heat, lightly basting with the remaining fat
8. In a casserole, heat half a glass of juice with two spoonfuls of verjuice, salt, and pepper
9. Serve the sauce under the thighs

title: Cuisses de Lapereau au Soleil (Young Rabbit Legs au Soleil)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Young rabbit legs
- Medium lardons, seasoned with salt, pepper and pounded aromatic herbs
- Butter
- 1 spoon of flour
- 1 pot spoon of bouillon
- 2 spoonfuls of velouté to degrease
- 1 bay leaf
- Mushrooms
- 1/2 bottle of good white wine
- Parsley and chives
- 5 egg yolks
- Fine butter
- Breadcrumbs
- 5 whole eggs
- Fine salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. After boning your legs, lard them closely with medium lardons seasoned with salt, pepper and pounded aromatic herbs.
2. Put butter in a casserole; heat it a little, and put your legs in it.
3. Place them on a hot stove; sauté them for ten minutes.
4. Add about one spoon of flour and one pot spoon of bouillon, two spoonfuls of velouté to degrease, a bay leaf, some mushrooms, half a bottle of good white wine, and a bouquet of parsley and chives.
5. Cook over high heat to make the liquid reduce.
6. Be careful to degrease your ragout.
7. When it is cooked and reduced, add a liaison of five egg yolks and a small piece of fine butter.
8. Place your twelve legs on a platter to cool; drizzle them with their sauce.
9. When they are cold, remove the bone of the foreleg, soak them well in their sauce, and put them in breadcrumbs one after the other.
10. Place them on a platter.
11. Break five whole eggs; add a little fine salt and a little coarse pepper.
12. Beat your eggs as you would for an omelet.
13. Dip your legs in it; make sure they are coated all over.
14. Roll them in breadcrumbs; give them a pleasing shape.
15. Put them on a platter; at the moment of serving, you fry them.

title: Cuisses de Lapereau en Chipolata (Young Rabbit Legs in Chipolata Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Young rabbit legs
- 1/4 pound of butter
- 1 and 1/2 spoonfuls of flour
- 2 small pot spoons of bouillon, strained through a silk tamis
- Mushrooms, sautéed in water and lemon juice
- 1 bay leaf
- Parsley and chives
- Small lard
- 20 small onions
- 15 chestnuts (not from Lyon)
- 6 sausages
- String
- 4 egg yolks for liaison
- Salt

## Directions

1. Bone your legs as in the previous recipe.
2. Throw them into boiling water for two minutes; then remove them and trim them.
3. Put 1/4 pound of butter in a casserole; place your legs in it.
4. Sauté them for ten minutes.
5. Then pour 1 and 1/2 spoonfuls of flour, which you blend with your butter.
6. Add about 2 small pot spoons of bouillon strained through a silk tamis.
7. Turn your ragout until it boils.
8. Add mushrooms sautéed in water and lemon juice to prevent them from darkening, a bay leaf, and a bouquet of parsley and chives.
9. Cook your ragout over high heat so it reduces.
10. Add small lard that you have blanched.
11. When your ragout is three-quarters cooked, add twenty small onions that you have blanched.
12. You can also add fifteen chestnuts not from Lyon.
13. Join six sausages, which you will tie in the middle with string so they are not too long.
14. Put them in boiling water for five minutes; after refreshing them, put them in your ragout.
15. Be careful to degrease it well.
16. When it is cooked, add a liaison of four egg yolks.
17. Arrange your legs on your plate and your ingredients on top. Your ragout should be properly salted. You can serve this ragout in a tureen to serve as a flan.

title: Cuisses de Lapereau en Papillotes (Young Rabbit Thighs in Parchment)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Young rabbit thighs
- Medium lardons seasoned with pounded aromatics, salt, and pepper
- A quarteron of butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Fine herbs prepared for papillotes
- Paper for papillotes
- Thin bards of lard

## Directions

1. Bone the thighs down to the joint of the foreleg
2. Lard them with medium lardons seasoned with pounded aromatics, salt, and pepper
3. Put ten thighs in a casserole with a quarteron of butter
4. Leave them on the fire for a good half-hour
5. Add salt, coarse pepper, and fine herbs prepared for papillotes
6. Simmer everything together for ten minutes
7. Place the thighs on a plate with their seasoning on top
8. Let them cool
9. Prepare paper for your papillotes
10. Put a thin bard of lard above and below your thigh with its seasoning
11. Fold the paper so that the butter does not escape
12. Tie the end with string
13. Half an hour before serving, put them on the grill over a very gentle fire
14. Turn them when one side has color
15. At the moment of serving, arrange them in crowns on your plate with a clear jus underneath, or without sauce

title: Cuisses de Lapereau à la Chicorée (Young Rabbit Thighs with Chicory)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Young rabbit thighs
- Fine lard for larding
- Small piece of lard to replace the bone
- Bards of lard
- A few slices of veal
- Two carrots
- Three onions
- Two bay leaves
- A little thyme
- Buttered round of paper
- Bouillon
- Chicory
- Cucumbers à la crème, sautéed mushrooms, tomato sauce, game stock, or glaze sauce for serving

## Directions

1. Bone the thighs down to the joint as with the previous ones
2. Lard them with fine lard
3. Season them on the inside
4. Put a piece of small lard in place of the bone
5. With a needle and thread, bring the flesh together and secure it
6. Put bards of lard in a casserole with a few slices of veal, two carrots, three onions, two bay leaves, and a little thyme
7. Place your thighs on top with seasoning
8. Cover them with a round of buttered paper
9. Pour in a full spoonful of bouillon
10. Simmer for an hour and a half, or more depending on the quality of your rabbit
11. Be careful to put fire on your lid
12. At the moment of serving, drain your thighs
13. Remove the trussing strings and glaze them
14. Place your chicory on your plate and your thighs on top
15. You may serve underneath cucumbers à la crème, a sauté of mushrooms, tomato sauce, game stock, or a glaze sauce

title: Cuisses de Lapin à la Purée de Lentilles (Rabbit Legs with Lentil Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 rabbit legs
- Medium lardons, seasoned with aromatic herbs, salt and pepper
- Lard slices for lining
- A few slices of veal
- 2 carrots
- 2 onions
- 1 bay leaf
- Thyme
- Lard strips for covering
- Butter
- 1 small pot spoon of bouillon
- Lentil purée

## Directions

1. Take rabbit legs; eight are sufficient for an entrée.
2. Bone them, that is, remove the bone up to the joint of the foreleg; try not to cut the top.
3. Lard the inside of your legs with medium lardons seasoned with aromatic herbs, salt and pepper. Since the leg is generally very dry, put many lardons in it.
4. When they are well larded, throw them into boiling water for two minutes.
5. Remove them, trim them, and put lard strips in a casserole with a few slices of veal, two carrots, two onions, a bay leaf, and a little thyme.
6. Place the legs on this seasoning. Cover them with lard strips and a round of buttered paper.
7. Pour about one small pot spoon of bouillon with a little drippings from the cooking pot.
8. Let them simmer for two hours, more or less, depending on how tender your rabbit is.
9. At the moment of serving, drain them. Arrange them on your plate and mask them with a lentil purée.

title: Cuisses de Poularde au sauté de champignons (Chicken Thighs with Mushroom Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- ten chicken thighs
- salt
- pepper
- cooked farce the size of fresh eggs
- mushroom purée
- lard
- mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. Remove and bone ten chicken thighs down to the inner joint
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Take cooked forcemeat the size of fresh eggs with an equal amount of mushroom purée
4. Fill the inside of your thighs with this mixture and sew them so the farce does not come out
5. Cut the thick end at the paw side
6. Place lard strips in the bottom of a casserole with your thighs on top
7. Cover them with lard strips and add a lid to cook them
8. Put them over the fire for one hour, keeping them always simmering
9. At the moment of serving, drain them, remove the lard, dry them around the edge of the dish, and put the mushroom sauce in the middle

title: Cuisses de Poulets au Soleil (Chicken Thighs in the Sun)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve chicken thighs from good chickens
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- One bay leaf
- One onion studded with a clove
- Parsley and scallion bouquet
- Flour
- Hot bouillon
- Mushrooms
- Three egg yolks
- Bread crumbs
- Beaten and seasoned eggs
- Oil for frying
- Fried parsley

## Directions

1. Break the bone of each thigh up to the inner joint and cut off the large end at the leg.
2. Put a good piece of butter in a casserole and heat it gently.
3. Arrange the thighs in it so they take on a beautiful shape.
4. Add salt, coarse pepper, a bay leaf, an onion studded with a clove, and a bouquet of parsley and scallions.
5. Place them over high heat and sauté until well browned.
6. Add a spoonful of flour and a pot-spoon of hot bouillon along with mushrooms.
7. Boil for three-quarters of an hour.
8. Degrease the ragout before the time expires.
9. When removing the thighs, the sauce should be reduced to three-quarters of its volume.
10. Remove the bouquet, onion, and bay leaf.
11. Add a liaison of three egg yolks.
12. Arrange the thighs on a platter with sauce on top.
13. When they are cold, coat them well with their sauce.
14. Roll them in breadcrumbs, then dip in beaten and seasoned eggs.
15. Coat them again with breadcrumbs, making sure they are well covered everywhere.
16. At serving time, place them in hot oil.
17. When they have a beautiful color, drain them on white cloth.
18. Arrange them in a crown shape.
19. Fry a good handful of parsley and place it in the center.

title: Cuisses de Poulets à la Périgueux (Chicken Thighs Périgueux)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve chicken thighs from fine fat chickens
- Eight truffles
- Half a pound of lard
- Three spoonfuls of good oil
- Four spices
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Lard for barding
- Truffle scraps

## Directions

1. Prepare twelve chicken thighs as you would for a bigarrure.
2. Chop eight peeled truffles.
3. Grate half a pound of lard and put it in a casserole with three spoonfuls of good oil.
4. Add your chopped truffles, a little four spices, a little salt and coarse pepper.
5. Pass them over heat briefly, then let them cool.
6. Stuff your chicken thighs with this mixture.
7. Sew them with needle and thread so the truffles do not escape.
8. To give them a pleasant shape, insert a leg bone through the thin end of the thigh.
9. Put lards of bacon in a casserole, arrange your thighs on top, and cover them with lard.
10. Add your truffle scraps and cook covered.
11. Three-quarters of an hour before serving, place your thighs over heat.
12. At that moment, drain them.
13. Remove the thread and arrange them in a crown shape.
14. Place a sauté of truffles in the center.

title: Culotte de Bœuf au Jour (Beef Rump Baked in Pastry Crust)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef rump of desired size
- coarse lard
- salt
- fine spices
- one chopine white wine
- pastry dough

## Directions

1. Take a beef rump of the size you judge appropriate
2. Bone it if you wish and lard it with coarse lard seasoned with salt and fine spices
3. Put it in a vessel just fitting its size with one chopine of white wine
4. Cover with a lid and seal the edges with pastry dough
5. Bake in the oven for five or six hours depending on its size
6. Serve it with its sauce well defatted

title: Culotte de Bœuf à l'Anglaise (Beef Rump English Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- beef cut of desired size
- two or three carrots
- one parsnip
- three or four onions
- bouquet of parsley and chives
- one clove garlic
- three cloves
- one bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- bouillon or water
- salt
- pepper
- one large cabbage
- farce of godiveau or other meat farce
- coulis

## Directions

1. Take a cut of beef of the size you judge appropriate and tie it
2. Put it in a pot proportioned to its size with two or three carrots, one parsnip, three or four onions, a bouquet of parsley and chives, one clove garlic, three cloves, one bay leaf, thyme, and basil
3. Moisten with bouillon or water, season with salt and pepper
4. Cook at low fire in court bouillon
5. At half the cooking time, prepare small cabbage heads: take a large cabbage, remove the greenest leaves, and blanch it whole in boiling water for half an hour
6. Remove it to fresh water and press it firmly without breaking the leaves
