7. Remove the leaves one by one and put a little farce of godiveau or other meat farce inside
8. Stack three or four leaves together as you fill them, one on top of the other, to form small cabbage heads slightly bigger than an egg
9. Tie them and cook them with the beef
10. When cooking is done, drain everything and wipe it of its fat
11. Arrange the beef in the serving dish, cut each cabbage head in half, and arrange them around the beef with the cut side out
12. For the sauce, take some of the cooking liquid, pass it through a sieve, degrease it, add a little coulis to bind it, reduce it over the fire to sauce consistency
13. Serve on the meat and on the cabbage heads

title: Culotte de Bœuf à la Cardinale (Beef Rump Cardinal Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef rump of ten to twelve pounds
- one pound lard cut into lardoons
- fine spices
- fine salt
- one-quarter pound powdered saltpeter
- one ounce juniper berries, somewhat crushed
- three bay leaves
- thyme
- basil
- one pound salt
- three chopines red wine
- one pint water
- five or six onions
- two cloves garlic
- four or five carrots
- two parsnips
- four or five cloves
- one-quarter nutmeg
- parsley
- chives

## Directions

1. Bone the beef rump as needed, cut one pound of lard into lardoons, season them with fine spices and fine salt
2. Lard the piece of beef all over without touching the top which is covered
3. Take one-quarter pound powdered saltpeter and rub the meat with it to make it red
4. Put the rump in a terrine with one ounce juniper berries somewhat crushed, three bay leaves, a little thyme and basil, and one pound salt
5. Cover the terrine and leave the meat in it for eight days
6. When it has taken on the salt, wash it with hot water
7. Put some lard on top of the rump on the side that is covered and defatted
8. Wrap it in a white cloth and tie it
9. Cook it at low fire for five hours with three chopines of red wine, one pint water, five or six onions, two cloves garlic, four or five carrots, two parsnips, one bay leaf, basil, thyme, four or five cloves, one-quarter nutmeg, parsley, and chives
10. When it is cooked, remove it from the fire and let it cool in its cooking liquid until completely cold

title: Culotte à la Braise aux Oignons d'Hollande (Braised Beef Rump with Dutch Onions)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef rump
- one pint good white wine
- good beef bouillon
- veal slice
- lard strip
- large bouquet garni
- salt
- pepper
- approximately thirty Dutch onions, or large red onions if unavailable

## Directions

1. Take a fine rump and bone it, tie it with string
2. Cook it in a good braise made with one pint of good white wine, beef bouillon, veal slice, strip of lard, large bouquet garni, salt, and pepper
3. When it is half-cooked, add approximately thirty Dutch onions, or large red onions if Dutch onions are unavailable
4. When the beef is cooked, remove it to drain it well of its fat
5. Arrange it in the serving dish with the onions around it
6. Serve with a good sauce of fine color on top

title: Cutlets with Atelet Sauce
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb cutlets
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Warm butter
- Atelet sauce
- Breadcrumbs
- Warm butter for coating
- Italienne garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare your lamb cutlets as the previous ones
2. Season them with salt and coarse pepper
3. Place them in your sautoir with warm butter poured over them
4. Place them on ordinary heat
5. When your cutlets are stiffened on both sides, drain them and let them cool
6. Trim them again
7. Coat them with atelet sauce
8. Put it in breadcrumbs, then in warm butter
9. Bread them once more
10. Place on a table to take on an agreeable form by rounding the end and smoothing it
11. Place on a plafond
12. A quarter hour before serving, place them on a grill over very low heat
13. When they have color on one side, turn them to the other
14. At the moment of serving, arrange them in a crown and add an italienne garnish

title: Cutlets with Cucumbers
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep cutlets
- Medium lardons
- Salt
- Pepper
- Spices
- Butter
- Lard strips
- Veal slices
- Carrots
- Onions
- Cloves
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Cut your cutlets somewhat thick so they are not too thin after beating them
2. Stud them with medium lardons seasoned with salt, pepper, and a little spices
3. Put a piece of butter to melt in a saucepan
4. Put your cutlets to stiffen in it
5. Finish trimming them
6. Put strips of lard in a saucepan with several slices of veal, two carrots cut in slices, three onions, two cloves, a bay leaf, and a little thyme
7. Place the cutlets on this seasoning, cover them with lard, pour a good spoonful of bouillon over them

title: Céleri au Velouté (Celery in Velouté Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- butter
- salt
- coarse pepper
- grated nutmeg
- velouté
- bouillon
- flour
- croutons

## Directions

1. Peel, wash, and cut the celery into small sprigs, leaving the tender leaves
2. Blanch it in plenty of water
3. When you see it bend under the finger, refresh it
4. Drain it well as you would for chicory
5. Chop it as you would chicory
6. Put a piece of butter in a casserole with the celery, a little salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
7. Pour 3 spoonfuls of velouté and as much bouillon over it
8. Reduce it until it is thick enough to serve
9. Place croutons around it
10. If you do not use velouté, when the celery is drained, add a small spoonful of flour mixed with the celery, moisten with bouillon as you would for chicory

title: Céleri Entier à l'Espagnole (Whole Celery in Spanish Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- celery hearts
- water
- salt
- coarse pepper
- butter, the size of an egg
- Spanish sauce
- consommé
- flour
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Remove the outer ribs and trim the head
2. Cut 7-inch pieces, all of the same size and thickness
3. Blanch them in plenty of salted water
4. When they have boiled 20 minutes, refresh them and drain
5. Trim again and place in a casserole with a little coarse pepper, an egg-sized piece of butter, 4 spoonfuls of Spanish sauce, and 6 spoonfuls of consommé
6. Cook on a fairly hot fire
7. Let it boil for half an hour
8. Arrange on a plate with the sauce underneath
9. Alternatively, make a light roux, moisten with bouillon, turn the sauce until it boils, then pour it over the celery and finish as described above

title: Côte de Bœuf au Vin de Malaga (Braised Beef Rib with Malaga Wine)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib
- Larding fat
- Malaga wine
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Prepare your beef rib as for braising; season your larding fat a little more; add a half-bottle of Malaga wine and the equivalent of a half-bottle of bouillon
2. Cook your beef rib
3. After that, pass the liquid through fine cloth; take care that there is no fat
4. Reduce all your liquid so that only a glassful remains to place under your beef rib
5. Take care not to salt your cooking much, so that your reduced sauce is not bitter

title: Côte de Bœuf aux Choux (Braised Beef Rib with Cabbage)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib
- Lard
- Carrots
- Onions
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Cloves
- Cabbage
- Salt
- Black pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare your beef rib and cook it as you would a braised one; drain it, glaze it, and arrange cabbage (see Cabbage and Spanish sauce) with small lard around it; sauce it with a reduced Spanish sauce
2. Otherwise, trim your beef rib with thick lard, tie it, put it in a casserole with four carrots, four onions, one of which is studded with three cloves, two bay leaves, a little thyme
3. Blanch one or two cabbages by quarters, refresh them, tie them, and cook them with your beef rib
4. Blanch your small lard for a quarter hour and put it in the bottom with your beef rib and cabbage on top
5. If you moisten with water, add salt and pepper
6. After that, simmer everything for two hours; it must boil constantly
7. Arrange the cabbage and lard around the beef rib on the plate
8. If you do not have sauce, take a little of the cooking liquid and pour it over your beef rib; take care not to oversalt because of the lard, and that the cabbage is not bitter

title: Côte de Bœuf aux Laitues (Braised Beef Rib with Lettuce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib
- Lettuce
- Carrots
- Onions
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Cloves
- Parsley bouquet
- Chives
- Bouillon or water
- Salt
- Black pepper

## Directions

1. Trim and cook your beef rib as you would a braised one; drain it, glaze it, and place it on the plate with glazed lettuces around it; sauce it with a reduced Spanish sauce
2. If you do not have the means to mark a braise, cook your beef rib with carrots, onions, thyme, bay leaf, cloves, bouquet of parsley and chives, a little bouillon
3. If you use only water, add salt and black pepper
4. Take the stock from your beef rib to cook your lettuces that you will have prepared; blanch them and refresh them, and press them to remove the water
5. Then put them in a casserole with the liquid in which your beef rib has cooked; an hour is enough to cook your lettuces

title: Côte de Bœuf aux Oignons Glacés (Braised Beef Rib with Glazed Onions)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib
- Glazed onions
- Espagnole sauce
- Cooking liquid

## Directions

1. Trim and braise your beef rib
2. When it is cooked, remove the string, drain it, and arrange it on your platter
3. Make sure it looks good and is completely whole
4. Put glazed onions around it, and a clear espagnole sauce that you have prepared with a little cooking liquid from your rib
5. Make sure your sauce is well degreased and passed through cheesecloth

title: Côte de Bœuf aux Oignons Glacés (Braised Beef Rib with Glazed Onions)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib
- Glazed onions
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Trim and braise your beef rib
2. When it is cooked, remove the string, drain it and arrange it on your dish; ensure it looks good and is completely intact
3. Arrange glazed onions around it and a clear Spanish sauce that you will have worked with a little of the marrow fat from your beef rib
4. Ensure your sauce is well degreased and passed through fine cloth

title: Côte de Bœuf aux Petites Racines (Braised Beef Rib with Small Root Vegetables)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib
- Small root vegetables
- Carrots
- Roux
- Stock from the beef rib
- Sugar
- Black pepper

## Directions

1. Trim and braise your beef rib
2. Drain it, glaze it and place it on your dish
3. Arrange small root vegetables around it; you can blanch carrots cut into small sticks
4. Make a light roux that you will moisten with the stock in which your beef rib has cooked
5. When your sauce has boiled, add your small vegetables
6. Add a small piece of sugar and a little black pepper
7. Make your sauce somewhat thin so you can reduce it to develop flavor
8. Degrease your small vegetables and serve them around the meat

title: Côte de Bœuf Braisée (Braised Beef Rib)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib
- Lard strips
- Veal slices
- Beef slices
- Carrots, fourteen
- Onions, five large
- Cloves, three
- Bay leaves, two
- Thyme
- Parsley
- Chives
- Bouillon, two spoonfuls
- Salt
- Pepper
- Four spices

## Directions

1. Trim your rib well
2. Clear the end of the bone
3. Lard it with large pieces of lard seasoned with four spices, salt, and pepper
4. Put strips of lard in the bottom of your casserole
5. Over your lard strips, put slices of veal and beef, fourteen carrots, five large onions, one of which is studded with three cloves, two bay leaves, a little thyme, a bouquet of parsley and chives
6. Tie with string, arrange your rib in this braise
7. Cover it with lard and put your vegetable garnishes on top
8. Add two spoonfuls of bouillon
9. Bring your braised rib to a boil, then put it on a low fire so it simmers gently for three hours; less if your rib is tender
10. When it is cooked, drain it and remove the string
11. Pass a little of the cooking liquid through a silk sieve
12. Degrease it and reduce it
13. Glaze your rib and pour your reduced liquid underneath

title: Côte de Bœuf Sauce Tomate Indienne (Braised Beef Rib with Indian Tomato Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib
- Indian tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Trim and cook your rib in a braise
2. Drain it, arrange it on the platter, glaze it, and put Indian tomato sauce underneath (see Tomato Sauce)

title: Côte de Bœuf à la Bonne Femme (Braised Beef Rib, Country Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib
- Butter
- Salt
- Black pepper

## Directions

1. Trim your beef rib; lard it with spiced thick lardons
2. Put a piece of butter the size of two eggs in a casserole and melt it
3. Put your beef rib seasoned with salt and black pepper in the casserole
4. Place the casserole on a fairly hot fire; turn your beef rib two or three times
5. When it is hot, place it on a gentle fire and also place fire on the cover of your casserole
6. When it has been cooking for an hour and a half it will be done
7. Use the cooking liquid for sauce

title: Côte de Bœuf à la Provençale (Beef Rib with Provençal Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef rib
- oil
- twenty large onions
- one and a half quarteron of oil
- vinegar
- bouillon
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Trim and cook your rib as you would cook it for a good wife preparation, but use oil instead of butter for cooking
2. When it is cooked, place it on your serving dish
3. Take twenty large onions and cut them in half from the top of the head to the tail
4. Cut your onion halves into slices so that they form semicircles
5. Put one and a half quarteron of oil in your pot and heat it well
6. Place your onions in to fry; when they are well browned, pour in a glass of vinegar, a little bouillon, salt, and pepper
7. Cover your rib with your onions; do not skim the fat

title: Côte de Bœuf à la Provençale (Braised Beef Rib, Provençal Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib
- Oil
- Onions

## Directions

1. Trim and cook your beef rib as you would a country-style one; in place of butter use oil for cooking it
2. When it is cooked, place it on your plate
3. Take twenty large onions that you cut in half from the head to the root
4. Then cut your onion halves in slices so that they form semicircles
5. Put a quarter pound and a half of oil in your casserole, that you heat well
6. Add the onions

title: Côte de Bœuf à la Purée d'Oignons (Beef Rib with Onion Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef rib
- brown onion purée

## Directions

1. Trim your rib
2. Cook it as you would cook it braised
3. When it is cooked, drain it
4. Dress it on the serving dish and cover it with a brown onion purée

title: Côte de Bœuf à la Rocambole (Braised Beef Rib with Rocambole)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef rib
- Garlic
- White wine
- Rocamboles
- Light roux and stock
- Black pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare your beef rib as for braising; add a little garlic; add a half-bottle of white wine to the liquid
2. When it is cooked, drain it, glaze it, and place it on your rocamboles
3. If you do not have sauce, make a light roux and moisten it with the stock from your beef rib that you have passed through fine cloth; add plenty of liquid with your roux so it can reduce and develop flavor; it must be very thick
4. Peel a glassful of rocamboles, blanch them until they crush when pressed under your fingers; they must remain somewhat firm
5. Put them in your sauce that you will keep warm in a bain-marie; add a little black pepper

title: Côtelettes aux Concombres (Cutlets with Cucumbers)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- larding bacon seasoned with salt, pepper, and spices
- butter
- lard strips
- veal slices
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- bay leaf
- thyme
- bouillon
- cucumbers à la crème

## Directions

1. Cut your cutlets somewhat thick so they are not too thin after beating them
2. Lard them with medium lardons seasoned with salt, pepper, and a little spices
3. Put a piece of melted butter in a saucepan
4. Place your cutlets in it to stiffen
5. Finish trimming them
6. Put lard strips in a saucepan with a few veal slices, two carrots cut into slices, three onions, two cloves, a bay leaf, a little thyme
7. Place the cutlets on this seasoning
8. Cover them with lard strips
9. Pour in just under a spoonful of bouillon
10. Simmer your cutlets for two hours
11. At serving time, drain them, glaze them, and arrange them in crowns on the platter
12. Put cucumbers à la crème in the middle, or you can place them plain in the saucepan where you had them stiffen
13. Coat them with a little aspic
14. Preserve the vegetables from the cooking liquid such as carrots, onions, etc.
15. Moisten them with bouillon

title: Côtelettes aux Laitues (Cutlets with Lettuces)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- glazed lettuces
- reduced espagnole sauce

## Directions

1. Cut your cutlets, trim them, and cook them as the cutlets with cucumbers
2. At serving time, drain them, glaze them, and arrange them in crowns, one lettuce between each, that is to say one cutlet, one glazed lettuce, and so on, until your crown is formed
3. Put reduced espagnole sauce

title: Côtelettes aux Navets (Cutlets with Turnips)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- turnip slices
- lard strips

## Directions

1. Cut and trim your cutlets
2. Cook them as the cutlets with cucumbers
3. Cover them with turnip slices and lard strips
4. Simmer them for two hours
5. At serving time, drain them, glaze them, and arrange them in crowns on the platter
6. Place your turnips in the middle

title: Côtelettes aux Petites Racines (Cutlets with Small Roots)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- carrot slices
- small carrots
- roux
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut your cutlets
2. Trim them
3. Cook them the same way as the cutlets with turnips
4. Cover them with carrot slices
5. At serving time, drain them, glaze them, and arrange them in crowns
6. Put small carrots in the middle
7. If you do not have sauce for your small roots, make a light roux that you will moisten with the liquid in which your cutlets cooked
8. Put your roots in it with a small piece of sugar
9. Reduce them

title: Côtelettes de Chevreuil Sautées (Sautéed Roe Deer Chops)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Roe deer chops
- Salt
- Fine pepper
- Butter
- Piquant sauce
- Vinegar
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Bay leaf

## Directions

1. Cut and trim your chops as you would mutton chops
2. Season them with salt and fine pepper
3. Place them in your sauté pan
4. Pour warm butter over them
5. At the moment of serving, place them on a hot fire
6. When they are stiffened on one side, turn them over
7. Leave them a moment on the fire; when they resist under the finger, remove them
8. Have a piquant sauce ready
9. At the moment of serving, drain your chops, dip them in your sauce, and dress them in a crown
10. Pour your sauce underneath
11. If you have no sauce, remove a little butter from your sauté pan, pour four spoonfuls of vinegar into your pan, reduce it; when it is completely reduced, add one spoonful of flour and mix it with your butter
12. Add a small spooonful of bouillon, a little salt, fine pepper, a dash of vinegar, and a bay leaf
13. Reduce your sauce a little, pass it through cheesecloth, and serve it for your chops

title: Côtelettes de Cochon (Pork Chops)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork chops
- Sauce Robert, tomato sauce, or cornichon sauce

## Directions

1. Cut and trim your pork chops as if they were veal chops.
2. Leave half an inch of fat on top.
3. Flatten them to give them a nice shape.
4. Grill them.
5. Serve underneath with a sauce Robert, a tomato sauce, or a cornichon sauce.

title: Côtelettes de Mauviettes (Lark Cutlets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Larks
- Lark breast meat
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Butter
- Espagnole sauce
- Meat glaze

## Directions

1. Remove the breast meat from your larks; pass a piece of leg through it, cutting off a large piece as if it were a cutlet; take care to flatten each piece of breast so it takes on more surface
2. Arrange your cutlets on a silver platter; season them with salt and coarse pepper; warm a piece of butter and pour it over them
3. At the moment of service, place them on a hot fire; as soon as they have received a little heat, turn them; after they have stayed a moment on the fire, remove them
4. Tilt your platter and place your cutlets on the edge so that your butter separates from your cutlets; arrange them in a crown; sauce them with a little espagnole in which you put about half a walnut-sized piece of meat glaze

title: Côtelettes de Mouton au Basilic (Lamb Chops with Basil)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops from a rack of lamb
- bouillon
- salt
- bouquet garni
- veal round
- beef fat
- three eggs
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- basil
- less cream
- breadcrumbs
- fried parsley
- slice of crumb bread

## Directions

1. Cut the lamb rack by ribs
2. Cook them the same way as lamb chops in dressing gown
3. Finish them the same way
4. Prepare the forcemeat the same but add finely chopped basil, one more egg, and less cream
5. When well wrapped in forcemeat and breadcrumbed, fry them to a nice color
6. Serve garnished with fried parsley, arranged around a slice of crumb bread placed in the dish

title: Côtelettes de Mouton au Gratin (Lamb Chops au Gratin)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb cut into chops
- Lard or butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Shallots
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Coulis
- Bread crumbs
- Egg yolk
- Butter

## Directions

1. Put lamb chops in a saucepan with lard or butter, parsley, chives, and two shallots, all chopped
2. Pass them over the fire with bouillon seasoned with salt and coarse pepper
3. Cook them until done
4. Degrease the liquid and add a little coulis to bind it
5. Raise the heat and add breadcrumbs mixed with egg yolk, parsley and chives chopped very fine
6. Place on a bed of ashes so that the gratin is well colored
7. Drain off the butter and serve your chops on top of the gratin

title: Côtelettes de Mouton en Robe de Chambre (Lamb Chops in Dressing Gown)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb chops
- bouillon
- salt
- bouquet garni
- veal round
- beef fat
- two eggs
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- cream
- breadcrumbs
- clear sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the chops with bouillon, very little salt, and a bouquet garni
2. When cooked, degrease the bouillon and pass it through a sieve
3. Reduce it to a glaze and dip the chops in it to glaze them
4. Remove the chops after glazing and let them cool
5. Make a forcemeat with veal round, beef fat, two eggs, salt, pepper, parsley, chives, mushrooms (all chopped), and moisten with cream
6. Wrap each chop with this forcemeat
7. Place them on a baking dish and coat with breadcrumbs
8. Cook in the oven; when they are lightly colored, drain them of their fat
9. Serve under a good clear sauce

title: Côtelettes de Mouton en Robe de Chambre (Lamb Chops in Their Broth)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb chops
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Bouquet garni (herbs)

## Directions

1. Cook the chops with bouillon, very little salt, and a bouquet garni
2. When cooked, degrease the bouillon and pass it through a tamis

title: Côtelettes de Mouton à la Marinière (Lamb Chops Sailor Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb rack cut into chops
- Butter
- White wine
- Bouillon
- Small white onions
- Lard
- Carrot
- Parsnip
- Savory
- Parsley
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut a lamb rack into chops that are short and thick
2. Put them in a saucepan with about half the size of an egg of butter
3. Pass them over the fire until slightly browned and moisten with a glass of white wine and as much bouillon
4. Add a dozen small white onions and simmer over a small fire
5. After half an hour, add a quarter pound of small lard with a carrot and parsnip, all cut into fillets, a small branch of savory and chopped parsley, little salt, coarse pepper, and a dash of vinegar
6. When the chops are done, remove them and place in the serving dish
7. Arrange the roots and lard fillets around the chops

title: Côtelettes de Mouton à la Pioche Verte (Lamb Chops with Green Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb chops
- Butter
- Bouquet of parsley and chives
- Garlic cloves
- Shallots
- Estragon leaves
- Cloves
- Laurel leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Flour
- White wine
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter mixed with flour
- Parsley blanched and chopped
- Verjuice

## Directions

1. Prepare lamb chops as in previous recipes
2. Put them in a saucepan with a little butter, a bouquet of parsley and chives, garlic cloves, two shallots, estragon leaves, two cloves, half a laurel leaf, thyme and basil
3. Pass over the fire and add a small pinch of flour
4. Moisten with a glass of white wine and a little bouillon, season with salt and pepper
5. Simmer until the sauce is reduced and the chops are cooked
6. Dress the chops on the serving plate
7. Pass the sauce through a tamis and degrease it
8. Return to the fire with butter kneaded with a pinch of flour
9. Add a good pinch of parsley blanched in boiling water, well pressed and chopped very fine, with half a spoonful of verjuice
10. Turn until it is bound like a white sauce and serve over the chops

title: Côtelettes de Mouton à la Purée de Navets (Lamb Chops with Turnip Purée)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight to twelve turnips
- Butter
- Flour
- Salt
- Pepper
- Shallots
- Bouillon
- Lamb rack cut into chops
- Fat or oil for marinating

## Directions

1. Have eight to twelve turnips, tender if possible
2. After scraping and washing them, cut them into small pieces
3. Blanch them for half a quarter hour in boiling water
4. When drained, put them in a saucepan with a piece of butter and pass over the fire, stirring often, until they are colored
5. Add a good pinch of flour, salt, pepper, and two chopped shallots
6. Moisten with bouillon and simmer until the turnips are thick like jam
7. Pass them through a sieve
8. While they cook, cut a lamb rack into chops and marinate them with salt, pepper, and a little fat or oil
9. Grill them, basting with the remaining marinade
10. Serve on the turnip purée

title: Côtelettes de Mouton à la Ravigote (Lamb Chops with Ravigote Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A rack of lamb or lamb chops
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Egg yolks
- Ravigote herbs (salad greens, chervil, tarragon, burnet, watercress)
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Keep your rack whole or cut into chops
2. Cook in a casserole with a little butter, salt and coarse pepper over a small fire
3. When degreased, prepare the same sauce you put on a plate, thin it with three egg yolks and ravigote herbs
4. Put this binding in the saucepan where the rack or chops are
5. Bind over the fire without boiling
6. Dress your chops on the plate you will serve
7. Ravigote greens should contain all salad ingredients like chervil, tarragon, burnet, watercress, using about a handful of each herb
8. Blanch them for half a quarter hour in boiling water, then drain, press them well in your hands, and pound them very fine before adding to the binding

title: Côtelettes de Pigeons sautées (Sautéed Pigeon Cutlets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- six pigeons
- salt
- coarse pepper
- aromatic spices
- butter
- clear espagnole sauce
- glace (meat glaze) about the size of half a walnut

## Directions

1. Remove the meat from the breast of your six pigeons and trim them
2. Pass a bone through them which you take from the tip of the wing fillet
3. Arrange them in your sauté pan
4. Season with salt and coarse pepper
5. Sprinkle lightly with ground aromatic spices
6. Warm a good piece of butter and pour it over your cutlets
7. At the moment of serving, place them on high heat
8. As soon as they are stiffened on one side, turn them to the other
9. Leave them only an instant
10. Drain them and arrange in a crown shape with a glazed crouton between each one
11. Serve with a clear espagnole sauce into which you put glace about the size of half a walnut

title: Côtelettes de Sanglier Sautées (Sautéed Wild Boar Chops)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar chops
- salt
- coarse pepper
- butter
- espagnole sauce
- white wine
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut and trim your wild boar chops as you would veal chops
2. Put them in your sauté pan or pie dish
3. Season them with salt and coarse pepper
4. Warm some butter and pour it over them
5. Place them on a brisk fire
6. When they are stiffened on one side, turn them on the other
7. When they are firm, arrange them in a crown on your plate
8. Put in a casserole four spoonfuls of espagnole sauce, a glass of white wine which you will pour into your sauté pan to detach the glaze produced by your chops
9. Put this wine in your sauce which you will reduce by half
10. Pass your sauce through a sieve and pour it over your chops; if you do not have this, put a spoonful of flour which you will mix in your sauce

title: Côtelettes de Veau au Petit Lard (Veal Cutlets with Bacon)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of larded bacon
- a piece of butter the size of half an egg
- veal cutlets
- two shallots
- a pinch of parsley
- salt
- coarse pepper
- half a glass of white wine
- bouillon or water
- three egg yolks
- two spoonfuls of bouillon
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into slices and place in a casserole with butter
2. Brown the bacon lightly, then add the veal cutlets
3. Cook over low heat, turning frequently until the cutlets are cooked
4. Remove the cutlets and bacon to a plate
5. Remove half the fat from the casserole and add two shallots, a pinch of chopped parsley, salt, and coarse pepper
6. Add half a glass of white wine and as much bouillon or water
7. Bring to a boil and reduce by half
8. Return the cutlets and bacon to the casserole
9. Add a liaison of three egg yolks mixed with two spoonfuls of bouillon
10. Thicken over the fire without boiling
11. When serving, add a dash of vinegar

title: Côtelettes de Veau au Vert Pré (Veal Chops with Green Herbs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- veal chops
- butter
- parsley
- ciboule (chives)
- half a clove of garlic
- two cloves
- bay leaf
- pinch of flour
- bouillon
- one glass of white wine
- salt
- coarse pepper
- butter kneaded with flour
- blanched chervil
- lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Place veal chops in a pan with a piece of butter, a bouquet of parsley, ciboule, half a clove of garlic, two cloves, a bay leaf
2. Pass them over the fire and add a pinch of flour, moisten with bouillon and one glass of white wine
3. Season with salt and coarse pepper, cook over a small fire and degrease
4. When cooking is done and the sauce is short, add a piece of butter kneaded with flour the size of a walnut, with a good pinch of blanched and chopped chervil
5. Make the sauce bind, serving a squeeze of lemon juice or a dash of vinegar

title: Côtelettes de Veau en lorgnette (Veal Cutlets Lorgnette)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Medium lard strips seasoned with fine salt, coarse pepper, and four spices
- Scarlet tongue
- Large onions
- Butter

## Directions

1. Have cutlets which you trim
2. Flatten them
3. Lard them with medium lard strips seasoned with fine salt, coarse pepper, and four spices; lard them through and through
4. Stiffen them in a casserole with butter
5. Trim them
6. Have a scarlet tongue; take a pastry cutter the size of a small shield; cut seven rounds of tongue that are thick, three lines deep
7. Have large well-cleaned onions; cut them so they are as thick as your rounds of tongue
8. Remove the interior of your onions or put your round of tongue in it
9. Do as much as you have cutlets and put it on top so that it holds and forms lorgnette
10. When they are prepared, cook them as those called à la Drue
11. At the moment of serving, drain them; glaze around the cutlet and the piece of tongue without touching the onion
12. Arrange them on your plate
13. Take the stock from your cutlets which you reduce with a little espagnole; or lacking sauce, make a small roux which you moisten with your

title: Côtelettes de Veau en Papillotes (Veal Cutlets in Paper)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Fine herbs for papillotes
- Paper
- Oil
- Thin strips of lard
- String
- Clear jus

## Directions

1. Cut and trim the cutlets as in sautéed cutlets.
2. Take a good piece of butter and melt it in a casserole.
3. Place the cutlets on top.
4. Season them with salt and a little coarse pepper.
5. Cook them almost through in the butter.
6. After, place them on a platter and pour the butter in which they have cooked over them.
7. Add fine herbs for papillotes.
8. Let the cutlets cool.
9. Cut a square of paper large enough to easily contain your cutlets.
10. Oil it, that is to say put a little oil on top and rub it.
11. When the paper is prepared, place a thin strip of lard on top.
12. Then place your cutlet with fine herbs on each side.
13. Cover it with a thin strip of lard.
14. Fold the paper over your cutlet.
15. Trim the angles of the paper.
16. Pleat it so that the seasoning cannot escape when on the grill.
17. The pleats of your paper must be as close as possible to the flesh of your cutlet.
18. Tie the end where you finish the pleats of your paper, which must be found at the end of the bone, so that neither the jus nor the butter can escape.
19. A quarter hour before serving, place your cutlets on the grill.
20. Be careful that the fire is gentle so the paper does not burn and your cutlets cook.
21. Arrange them in crowns.
22. Put clear jus underneath.

title: Côtelettes de Veau en Poirée (Veal Cutlets in Pear Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- very thick veal cutlets
- calf's sweetbread
- bacon cut into pieces
- parsley
- shallots
- mushrooms
- pine nuts
- salt
- coarse pepper
- bacon strips for larding
- a little bouillon
- white wine
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut thick veal cutlets and make a pocket in the side with your finger
2. Prepare a stuffing with calf's sweetbread cut into pieces, bacon, parsley, shallots, mushrooms, and pine nuts, all chopped together with salt and coarse pepper
3. Fill the pockets with this stuffing
4. Lard the cutlets and place them in a casserole with bacon strips, a little bouillon, and white wine
5. Cook until done and arrange on the platter
6. Strain the sauce, skim the fat, add a little coulis to bind it, and serve over the cutlets

title: Côtelettes de Veau Grillées (Grilled Veal Chops)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- veal rack
- salt
- coarse pepper
- mushrooms
- parsley
- chives
- garlic
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- verjuice

## Directions

1. Cut a veal rack into chops and pare them properly without being too long
2. Marinate for an hour with salt, coarse pepper, mushrooms, parsley, chives, a point of garlic, and a little warm butter
3. Hold the marinade on the chops by coating with breadcrumbs
4. Grill on a small fire, basting with the remaining marinade
5. When cooked to a nice color, serve under a sauce of clear juice, two spoonfuls of verjuice, salt, coarse pepper
6. You can also serve them without sauce

title: Côtelettes de Veau Marinées (Marinated Veal Cutlets)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one square of veal cut into cutlets
- salt
- coarse pepper
- mushrooms
- parsley
- chives
- a hint of garlic
- warm butter
- breadcrumbs
- clear stock
- two spoonfuls of verjuice

## Directions

1. Trim the cutlets properly without making them too long
2. Marinate them for one hour with salt, coarse pepper, mushrooms, parsley, chives, a hint of garlic, and warm butter
3. Remove them from the marinade and coat with breadcrumbs
4. Cook them over low heat, basting with the remaining marinade
5. When they are cooked to a beautiful color, serve with a sauce made from clear stock, two spoonfuls of verjuice, salt, and coarse pepper, or serve without sauce

title: Côtelettes de Veau piquées glacées (Glazed Larded Veal Cutlets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets from near the kidney
- Fine lard
- Slices of veal
- Carrots
- Onions
- Laurel leaf
- Two cloves
- Consommé or bouillon
- Paper

## Directions

1. Take the cutlets closest to the kidney
2. Trim them as well as possible
3. Do not flatten them too much so they keep their thickness
4. Lard them with fine lard
5. Put lard in the bottom of a casserole, a few slices of veal, some carrots and onions, a laurel leaf, two cloves
6. Put your cutlets on this seasoning
7. Ensure that the larding does not become hidden so that when cooking they can glaze
8. Put a round of buttered paper over the top and fill about one spoon-full with consommé or bouillon
9. Place your casserole on the fire; when the inside boils, put its lid on it and put a little hot fire on top
10. Then place your casserole on gentle fire, but one that always keeps boiling
11. It takes an hour and a half to cook your cutlets
12. At the moment of serving, drain them on a casserole lid, glaze them and serve underneath cucumbers, chicory, tomato sauce, sorrel purée, mushroom purée, spinach, espagnole sauce, sautéed mushrooms, etc.
13. You can also put these cutlets in a buttered casserole moistened with jelly, consommé, or bouillon, and the above seasoning

title: Côtelettes de Veau sautées (Sautéed Veal Cutlets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Seven veal cutlets
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Fine butter, half a pound
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Shallots, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Cut the cutlets rib by rib, ensuring they are all the same thickness
2. Trim them so they are rounded at the thick end of the meat and squared off at the bone side
3. Trim the bone to the size of half an inch
4. Place salt and coarse pepper on your sauté pan or skillet
5. Put your cutlets in the pan with fine salt on top and a little coarse pepper
6. If you wish to sauté them with fine herbs, sprinkle finely chopped parsley and finely chopped shallots on top
7. Melt half a pound of fine butter and pour it over your cutlets
8. At the moment of serving, place them on a hot fire
9. When they are cooked on one side, turn them to the other side
10. Avoid letting your fire brown the cutlets
11. Press your finger on the meat; if you feel the meat is still soft, it is not cooked enough; you would need to turn them and remove them from the hot fire so they cook more gently
12. When they are cooked, arrange them in crowns on the plate
13. Remove the butter that is in your sauté pan or skillet
14. Put in about four spoonfuls of espagnole sauce and a little glaze
15. Detach the glaze produced by your cutlets and put your sauce in the plate
16. If you have no sauce, leave a little butter; put a little flour which you stir with your stock; put a little bouillon in it, a little fine herbs; make it boil; sauce your cutlets and check if they are of good taste

title: Côtelettes de Veau à la Cuisinière (Veal Chops Prepared Cuisinière Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- veal square cut into chops
- a quarter pound of small lard cut in slices
- a bit of butter
- three egg yolks
- bouillon
- blanched parsley
- shallot
- vinegar
- coarse pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut a veal square into chops and trim them
2. Put in the bottom of a pan a quarter pound of small lard cut in slices, a bit of butter, and the chops on top
3. Cook them over a small fire, browning them by turning them often
4. When they are cooked, arrange them on the plate you must serve with the pieces of small lard on top
5. In the pan in which they cooked, put a liaison of three egg yolks with bouillon, blanched and chopped parsley, a chopped shallot
6. Detach everything that can stick to the pan, make it bind over the fire, and then add a dash of vinegar and a bit of coarse pepper
7. Serve over the chops; add a bit of salt if needed, if the small lard is not salty enough

title: Côtelettes de Veau à la Drue (Veal Cutlets à la Drue)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Lard strips, seasoned with salt, pepper, and spices
- Ham strips
- Butter
- Lard
- Slices of veal
- Vegetables cut into lances
- Two cloves
- Laurel leaf
- Parsley and chives bouquet
- Consommé

## Directions

1. Trim your cutlets as with the previous recipe
2. Have lard strips seasoned with salt, pepper, and spices; also make ham strips
3. Lard your cutlets with one strip of lard and one of ham
4. Pay attention that your lard strips are placed symmetrically
5. Stiffen your cutlets in a casserole with butter, then remove them
6. Trim them as neatly as possible so they have a pleasing shape
7. Put lard rinds in the bottom of a casserole, a few slices of veal, vegetables cut into lances, two cloves, a laurel leaf, a bouquet of parsley and chives
8. Put your cutlets in your casserole and cover them with lard and a buttered paper lid, fill one spoon-full with consommé
9. Let them simmer for an hour and a half
10. At the moment of serving, drain them, glaze them with a beautiful glaze, and arrange them on your plate
11. You can serve underneath an espagnole sauce, cucumbers, a purée of white onions, a tomato sauce, etc.
12. Alternatively, you can cook these cutlets by putting in two or three carrots, three or four onions, a laurel leaf
13. Butter the bottom of your casserole; put your cutlets in it with this seasoning
14. Pour in about one spoon-full of jelly, consommé or bouillon
15. Cook on low heat
16. A quarter hour before your cutlets are cooked, cook them on high heat so they fall to glaze, that is so the liquid reduces and they glaze themselves
17. Put them on warm cinders at the moment of serving; remove them to arrange them on the plate
18. If you have no sauce to put with your glaze, use a little flour which you stir and moisten with a little bouillon to detach your glaze where you have put flour; make it boil and sauce your cutlets

title: Côtelettes de Veau à la Lyonnaise (Veal Chops Lyonnaise Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- veal rack
- anchovies
- bacon
- gherkins
- salt
- coarse pepper
- oil
- parsley
- chives
- shallots
- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut a veal rack into chops
2. Lard them with anchovies, bacon, and gherkins
3. Season with salt and coarse pepper
4. Marinate with oil, little salt, coarse pepper, parsley, chives, and shallots
5. Cook slowly in the marinade between two larded bacon strips
6. When cooked, serve with a sauce made as follows: in a pot place chopped parsley, chives, and shallots, salt, coarse pepper, two pieces of amber butter kneaded with a pinch of flour, a spoonful of fine oil, two spoonfuls of good bouillon
7. Thicken the sauce on the fire, and when serving add a squeeze of lemon juice

title: Côtelettes de Veau à la Poêle (Veal Chops Cooked in a Pan)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- veal neck cut into chops
- melted lard
- parsley
- ciboule (chives)
- a bit of truffles
- salt
- pepper
- ham slice

## Directions

1. Cut the neck by chops; remove the bones and leave only the chop
2. Place them in a pan with melted lard, parsley, ciboule, a bit of truffles, salt, pepper, all chopped very fine; a slice of ham
3. Cover with a bard of lard and cook over a small fire with ashes
4. When they are cooked, remove them from the pan and arrange them on the plate you must serve, remove the slice of ham that is in the pan, and put it aside
5. Degrease the sauce, put it on the fire, and serve it over the chops

title: Côtelettes de Veau à la St-Gara (Veal Cutlets Saint-Gara Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Scarlet tongue
- Lard
- Butter
- Nutmeg
- Fine pepper
- Lard strips
- Parings of tongue
- Laurel
- Basil
- Slices of ham
- Carrots
- Onions
- Consommé or bouillon
- Spanish sauce
- Mushrooms, chicory, tomato sauce, or cucumbers (optional garnishes)

## Directions

1. Cut and trim the cutlets as in previous recipes.
2. Cut the scarlet tongue into medium lardons.
3. Grate some lard and warm it, then sauté the lardons in it.
4. Add grated nutmeg and fine pepper to the lardons.
5. Let the lardons cool and lard the cutlets through and through.
6. Put a piece of butter in a large casserole and firm up the cutlets to trim them more correctly.
7. In the casserole, place strips of lard, parings of tongue, a little laurel, a little basil, and a few slices of ham.
8. Place the cutlets on this seasoning, cover them with lard.
9. Add two or three carrots cut into slices, or four onions cut into slices, and fill with two glasses of consommé or bouillon.
10. Cook the cutlets on low heat for two hours.
11. Put fire on top of the lid.
12. At the moment of serving, drain and glaze the cutlets.
13. Pass the cooking liquid through a silk sieve.
14. Add three spoonfuls of large Spanish sauce to a casserole.
15. Add four spoonfuls of the cooking liquid from the cutlets.
16. Reduce the sauce by half.
17. Arrange the cutlets on the platter and add the reduced sauce.
18. If you do not have sauce, make a light roux and moisten with the strained liquid, add two spoonfuls of jus for color, and reduce by one-third.
19. Ensure the sauce is not too salty and add a little coarse pepper.
20. You may serve under these cutlets a sauté of mushrooms, chicory, tomato sauce, or cucumbers.

title: Côtelettes en Papillottes (Veal Cutlets in Paper)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- white papers
- veal cutlets
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- shallots
- oil or butter

## Directions

1. Wrap veal cutlets in white papers with salt, pepper, parsley, chives, mushrooms, and shallots, all chopped
2. Rub the papers with oil or butter and fold the paper around the cutlet, sealing at the end
3. Butter the paper
4. Cook over low heat on the grill over a sheet of buttered paper
5. Serve with the sauce that is inside the paper

title: Côtelettes Grillées Panées (Breaded Grilled Cutlets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs
- Clear jus

## Directions

1. Cut your cutlets and trim them as the previous ones.
2. Season them with a little salt and coarse pepper.
3. Warm a good piece of butter.
4. Dip each cutlet in the butter coming out of the pan.
5. Place it in a vessel containing your breadcrumbs.
6. Stir it inside.
7. Remove it and place it on the table where you have put breadcrumbs.
8. After this, give it shape with your hand.
9. Make it round at the thick end as much as possible.
10. Place it on a board.
11. A half hour before serving, place it on the grill that you position on a gentle fire so your breadcrumbs do not take too much color.
12. When they are cooked, arrange them in crowns.
13. Put clear jus underneath.

title: Côtelettes à l'Écarlate (Cutlets with Scarlet Tongue)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- scarlet tongue lardons
- lard lardons
- salt
- pepper
- spices
- lard strips
- veal slices
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- bay leaf
- thyme
- bouillon
- buttered paper
- scarlet tongue
- reduced espagnole sauce

## Directions

1. Cut your cutlets and trim them as the previous ones
2. Lard them with a lardon of scarlet tongue and a lardon of lard, that is to say several lardons in the same cutlet, half one, half the other
3. Make sure your lardons are seasoned with salt, pepper, spices
4. When your cutlets are well larded, put lard strips in a saucepan with a few veal slices
5. Place them on top
6. Add two carrots, two onions, two cloves, a bay leaf, a little thyme
7. Cover your cutlets with lard strips and a round of buttered paper
8. Fill a small spoon with bouillon
9. Simmer them for two hours with heat above and below
10. At serving time, drain them, glaze them, and arrange them in crowns, putting a piece of glazed scarlet tongue between each cutlet
11. Cut your pieces of tongue like a salmis croûton
12. Your piece of tongue must be hot and cover the meat of your cutlet
13. Use reduced espagnole sauce

title: Côtelettes à la Soubise (Cutlets with Onion Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cutlets
- white onion purée

## Directions

1. Cut your cutlets
2. Trim them
3. Cook them as the cutlets with cucumbers
4. At serving time, drain them, glaze them, arrange them in crowns
5. Put white onion purée in the middle

title: Darioles (Dariole Cakes)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a little more than half a litron of flour
- a quarteron of butter
- a pinch of salt
- a glass of water
- half a setier of cream
- two eggs
- two spoonfuls of flour
- very little salt
- one ounce of sugar

## Directions

1. Put on a table a little more than half a litron of flour with a quarteron of butter, a pinch of salt, and a glass of water
2. Knead everything together to make a firm paste
3. Roll out this paste with a roller to the thickness of a half-finger
4. Cut it into pieces the size of small pie molds
5. Press your fingers on each piece and raise them on the edges by pinching all around to make a border a finger's height and the thickness of a twenty-four sous coin
6. Put them in the oven at a moderate heat for a quarter hour
7. Then add half a setier of cream diluted with two eggs and two spoonfuls of flour, very little salt, and one ounce of sugar
8. When they are cooked, sprinkle fine sugar over them

title: Daube d'Oie (Braised Goose)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one goose
- lardons of seasoned lard
- parsley
- chives
- two shallots
- half a clove of garlic
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- two glasses of water
- as much white wine
- half a glass of eau-de-vie

## Directions

1. Scald the goose and pluck it; make it refirm on the fire, and pluck it again
2. Lard it throughout with lardons of seasoned lard kneaded with parsley, chives, two shallots, half a clove of garlic, all chopped fine, a bay leaf, thyme, basil chopped to a powder, salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
3. After having larded the goose, truss it and place it in a pot just fitting its size with two glasses of water, as much white wine, and half a glass of eau-de-vie, add a little salt and coarse pepper
4. Seal the pot well and cook over a very low fire for three or four hours
5. When cooked and the sauce is very reduced so that it can set in aspic, arrange the daube in its serving dish
6. When it is almost cold, pour the sauce over it, and serve only when it is completely set as a cold entremet

title: De la langue du bœuf (Beef Tongue)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- fat
- salt
- pepper
- bouquet garni of parsley, chives, thyme, bay leaf, basil
- cloves
- onions
- roots
- bouillon
- small lard for larding
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue in fat made with salt, pepper, a bouquet garni of parsley, chives, thyme, bay leaf, basil, cloves, onions, and roots, with just enough bouillon to wet the meat
2. Cook over very low heat
3. When cooked, remove the skin and lard it with small pieces of lard
4. Cook it afterwards on the spit
5. Serve under a sauce with a dash of vinegar

title: Des Asperges (Asparagus)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- water
- salt
- bouillon
- butter
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cut off part of the white and wash well
2. Cook with water and salt, a half quarter hour is sufficient for them to be cooked as needed, they should be somewhat crunchy
3. Arrange them on the plate you will serve
4. If in meat broth, take some coulis, add a little butter, salt, coarse pepper, bind the sauce over the fire and place it on the asparagus

title: Des Concombres (Cucumbers Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- vinegar
- salt
- butter
- bouillon
- bouquet garni
- coulis

## Directions

1. Peel and remove the inside, cut into pieces
2. Soak with a half spoonful of vinegar and a little salt for two hours, turning from time to time, by this means it will release its cold water to the stomach, and press it again before putting it in the saucepan
3. Cook with a piece of butter and bouillon, a bouquet garni
4. When cooked, add a little coulis, remove the fat from the ragout before serving

title: Des Fèves de Marais (Marsh Beans)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Marsh beans
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Savory
- Flour
- Sugar
- Bouillon
- Three egg yolks
- Milk

## Directions

1. If eating with the skin, cook the beans in water for half a quarter hour to remove their acidity
2. Place the beans in a saucepan with butter, a bouquet of parsley, chives, and a little savory
3. Cook over the fire and add a pinch of flour and a little sugar the size of a walnut
4. Moisten with bouillon
5. When cooked, add a liaison made from three egg yolks and a little milk
6. Serve as a side dish

title: Des Gâteaux de Fleurs d'Oranges (Orange Blossom Cakes)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of sugar
- 1 glass of water
- 1 quarterons of orange blossom petals
- Sugar mixed with egg white, consistency of double cream
- Fine sugar

## Directions

1. Make a paper mold with white paper kept double and folded around to make a border two fingers high, like a box, of medium cake size
2. Put 1 pound of sugar in a pan with a glass of water
3. Boil and skim, continuing until, when dipping the skimming spoon in the sugar and blowing through the holes, large sparks fly out that hold together
4. Immediately add a quarterons of orange blossom petals and boil until the sugar returns to the point it was at when you added the flowers
5. Remove from the fire and stir quickly with a spatula, rubbing all around the pan and in the middle, until you see the sugar beginning to rise
6. Quickly throw in a little fine sugar mixed with egg white no thinner than double cream that you have ready
7. Stir quickly and pour your cake into the mold
8. Hold the bottom of the pan on the cake while it is hot, because this prevents it from falling
9. Violet cakes are made the same way, except that for a quarterons of violets only three quarterons of sugar are needed
10. Roasted orange blossom cakes are made the same way, except you roast the orange blossoms with a little fine sugar before putting them in the other sugar

title: Des Haricots au Gras (White Beans in Fat)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- White beans
- Lard, melted
- Good veal jus

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as white beans
2. In place of butter, use melted lard and moisten the beans with good veal jus
3. They are served as a side dish under roasted leg of mutton

title: Des Haricots Blancs (White Beans)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- White beans
- Butter
- Flour
- Onion
- Parsley, chopped
- Chives, chopped
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the beans in water until done
2. In a saucepan, make a roux with butter and a little flour
3. Add chopped onion to the roux and cook it
4. Add the cooked beans to the saucepan with the parsley and chives, chopped, salt, pepper, and a dash of vinegar
5. Simmer everything together for a quarter hour
6. Serve

title: Des Haricots Verds (Green Beans)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Young tender green beans
- Butter
- Parsley
- Ciboule
- Flour
- Good bouillon
- Salt
- Three egg yolks
- Milk
- Verjuice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Select very tender green beans and break off the small ends, then wash them and cook them in water
2. When cooked, drain the beans
3. In a saucepan, melt butter with chopped parsley and ciboule
4. Add the drained beans and toss them two or three times over the fire
5. Add a pinch of flour and a little good bouillon with salt, and allow them to boil until there is no more sauce
6. When ready to serve, add a liaison of three egg yolks mixed with milk, then a dash of verjuice or vinegar
7. Once the liaison has set on the fire, serve as an entremets
8. Alternatively, you may serve them in gravy by using coulis and veal juice instead of the liaison

title: Des Lentilles (Lentils)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Large blonde lentils
- Water

## Directions

1. Choose large lentils of a beautiful blonde color
2. Wash and pick through them
3. Cook in water until done
4. Prepare as you would white beans

title: Des Pieds de Mouton (Sheep's Feet)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's feet
- butter
- bouquet garni
- egg yolks
- milk or cream

## Directions

1. After having the feet well cooked in water, peel off what remains of the hair and remove the large bones
2. Put them in a saucepan with a good piece of butter and a bouquet garni
3. Let them have a few turns on the fire
4. When they are cooked and the sauce reduced, do not degrease it
5. Add three egg yolks diluted with milk or cream if you have it

title: Des Rognons de Mouton (Sheep's Kidneys)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's kidneys
- salt
- pepper
- sauce à l'échalotte

## Directions

1. Cook them on the grill
2. Open them through the middle and pass a small skewer through them
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. When cooked, put a sauce à l'échalotte underneath

title: Différentes Façons d'Accommoder les Vieux Dindons (Different Ways to Prepare Old Turkeys)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- coarse lard
- salt, pepper, parsley, chives, garlic, shallots
- one chopine of white wine
- broth
- roots
- onions
- a bouquet garni

## Directions

1. Pluck, clean, and truss the turkey with legs inside the body
2. Finish singeing it over the coals
3. Lard it with coarse lard seasoned with salt, pepper, parsley, chives, garlic, and shallots, all chopped together
4. Place it to cook in a pot just fitting its size
5. Add one chopine of white wine, broth, roots, onions, a bouquet garni, salt, and pepper
6. Cook over low heat
7. When cooked, strain the broth through a sieve and reduce it to a glaze
8. Let the glaze cool, then spread it over the turkey, or place any remaining glaze inside the turkey
9. Serve the turkey on a platter on a napkin garnished with fresh parsley

title: Dinde aux Truffes (Turkey with Truffles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Three or four pounds of truffles
- Two pounds of lard, scraped
- One pound of best oil
- One pound of fine clarified butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Four spices
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Shallot, finely chopped
- Lard bard
- Oiled paper
- Truffles for sauce
- Espagnole
- Consommé

## Directions

1. Begin several days in advance for best results
2. Singe the turkey lightly and clean it well
3. Gut it through the vent
4. Remove the breastbone
5. Wash and clean three or four pounds of truffles according to the turkey's size
6. Chop the truffle peelings finely or pound them
7. Scrape two pounds of lard and put it in a saucepan with one pound of best oil and one pound of fine clarified butter (clarified because otherwise it would give a sour taste to the truffles; it would be better not to use it then)
8. Add your chopped or pounded truffle peelings and your whole truffles, salt, coarse pepper, four spices, finely chopped parsley and finely chopped shallot
9. Put your saucepan on the fire
10. When your preparation boils, let it cook for ten minutes
11. Stir your truffles from time to time
12. Place them to cool
13. When they are set in the fat, put them in your turkey
14. If time permits, prepare it eight days in advance
15. When putting it on the spit, truss it and cover it with a bard of lard
16. Oil paper and wrap it
17. Secure your turkey on the spit so it does not turn, because your truffles would fall into the dripping pan
18. Half an hour before removing it from the spit, remove the paper and give it a fine color
19. If serving as a relevé, chop two or three truffles and pass them through a little butter, and add five spoonfuls of espagnole and two of consommé, a little four spices and coarse pepper
20. When your sauce is somewhat reduced and degreased, pour it in a sauce boat
21. For roasting it is served without sauce

title: Dindon (Turkey)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Lard
- Atelet
- Clear Robert sauce

## Directions

1. Gut it and singe it somewhat firmly
2. Clean it well
3. Break the breastbone to break the stomach
4. Bard it and spit it
5. Tie the legs onto the spit
6. If it turns, place an atelet on the back that you attach at each end to the spit
7. This meat must be well cooked but not overdone
8. If serving as a relevé, pour clear Robert sauce underneath or in a sauce boat

title: Dindon en Daube (Turkey in Daube)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- old turkey
- salt
- pepper
- four spices
- fine herbs
- lard lardons
- lard slices (bardes)
- string and trussing needle
- veal knuckles
- turkey legs
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- bay leaves
- thyme
- parsley and chives bouquet
- buttered paper
- bouillon
- egg

## Directions

1. Pluck and gut the turkey
2. Cut off its legs and truss the thighs inside
3. Secure them with string and a trussing needle
4. Singe and clean it
5. Season large lardons with salt, pepper, four spices, and fine herbs
6. If you wish, lard the breast and thighs of your turkey as finely as possible
7. Put lard slices in a braising pan, your turkey on top, two veal knuckles, the turkey legs, four carrots, six onions, three cloves, two bay leaves, thyme, a bouquet of parsley and chives
8. Cover your turkey with lard slices and a piece of buttered paper
9. Moisten it with bouillon, four pot spoonfuls, more if the turkey is large, a little salt
10. Simmer for three and a half hours
11. When cooked, remove the braising pan from the fire; do not remove your turkey for half an hour after, so it does not break apart
12. Pass your cooking liquid through a fine cloth
13. Reduce it by a quarter, or more if it is too thin
14. Break an egg into a saucepan; beat it well, and pour the jelly into it; beat it well with your egg
15. Check if it tastes good; if it lacks flavor, add a little aromatic flavoring, some parsley leaves, some chive stems
16. Put it on the fire, stirring vigorously until it is about to boil
17. When it reaches its first boil, place it on the edge of the stove with a lid and fire on top
18. Let it simmer for half an hour
19. Then pass it carefully through a fine cloth and let it cool to serve around the daube

title: Dindon en Pain (Turkey Bread)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one turkey
- foie gras
- mushrooms
- small lard
- salt, fine spices
- parsley, chives
- lard strip

## Directions

1. Singe the turkey over a well-lit stove
2. Debone it carefully
3. Make a raw ragout of foie gras, mushrooms, and small lard cut into small dice, mixed with salt, fine spices, minced parsley and chives
4. Put this ragout inside the body of the turkey
5. Sew up the turkey and shape it like a loaf of bread
6. Place a lard strip over the breast
7. Wrap it in a piece of étamine cloth
8. Place it in a pot to cook

title: Dindon en Pain (Turkey in Bread Form)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One turkey
- Foie gras
- Mushrooms
- Small bacon
- Salt
- Fine spices
- Parsley
- Chives
- Bacon bard for the breast
- Cheesecloth
- Bouillon
- One glass of white wine
- Bouquet of fine herbs
- Coulis

## Directions

1. Flame the turkey over a well-lit stove
2. Bone the turkey completely
3. Mix together raw foie gras, mushrooms, and small bacon cut into small dice, seasoned with salt, fine spices, chopped parsley and chives
4. Place this mixture inside the body of the turkey
5. Sew the turkey closed and shape it like a loaf of bread
6. Place a bacon bard on the breast
7. Wrap the turkey in cheesecloth
8. Place it in a pot that is not too large, with bouillon, one glass of white wine, and a bouquet of fine herbs
9. Cook until done
10. Remove the turkey from the pot and keep it warm
11. Deglaze the bouillon in a saucepan and reduce it to a small sauce
12. Add two spoonfuls of coulis to the sauce
13. Unwrap the turkey from the cheesecloth, remove the string and bacon bards
14. Wipe the turkey clean of fat by pressing with white linen
15. Serve with the sauce poured over it

title: Dindon Roulé (Rolled Turkey)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- meat forcemeat
- lard slices
- one glass of white wine
- good bouillon
- parsley
- chives
- garlic clove
- two cloves
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- two onions
- carrot
- parsnip
- coulis

## Directions

1. Singe a turkey and cut it in half
2. Bone each half completely
3. Spread a good meat forcemeat on each half
4. Roll each half and tie with string
5. Cook covered with lard slices, one glass of white wine, and equal amount of good bouillon
6. Add a bouquet of parsley, chives, garlic, cloves, thyme, bay leaf, basil, salt, pepper, onion slices, carrot, and parsnip
7. When cooked, degrease the sauce and strain through a sieve
8. Add a little coulis to bind the sauce
9. Serve over the meat

title: Dindons à l'Escalope (Turkey Escalope)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey wings and breast
- parsley, chives, shallots, basil, mushrooms
- salt, coarse pepper
- lard strips
- half a glass of white wine
- coulis
- lemon juice or verjuice

## Directions

1. Using the wings and breast from a turkey whose legs have been removed for other preparations, cut them very thinly into filets
2. Arrange them in a saucepan in several layers one on top of the other
3. Season each layer with chopped parsley, chives, shallots, basil, mushrooms, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Cover with a few lard strips
5. Cook over low heat on hot ashes
6. At the midpoint of cooking, add half a glass of white wine
7. Finish cooking and degrease the sauce before serving it over the escalopes
8. If you have coulis, add two spoonfuls to bind the sauce, then add lemon juice or a dash of verjuice

title: Dindons à la Poêle (Turkey Cooked in a Pan)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- butter or melted lard
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- lard slices
- one glass of white wine
- equal amount of bouillon
- coulis

## Directions

1. Singe and clean a turkey, flatten it slightly on the breast
2. Place in a casserole with melted butter or lard, finely chopped parsley, chives, mushrooms, and garlic
3. Brown the turkey and transfer to another casserole with seasoning, salt, and pepper
4. Cover the breast with lard slices
5. Moisten with one glass of white wine and equal amount of bouillon
6. Cook on low heat
7. Degrease and add a little coulis to the sauce to bind it

title: Du Cochon de Lait (Roasted Suckling Pig)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- oil

## Directions

1. Cook the suckling pig on a spit
2. Once well scalded and trussed, cut the skin slightly at the head, shoulders, and thighs so the skin does not tear
3. While cooking, frequently baste it with oil so the skin is crispy
4. Eat it fresh from the spit, otherwise the skin becomes soft and loses its flavor

title: Du Houblon (Hop Salad)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- hops
- water
- salt
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook in water with a little salt
2. When cooked and cooled, drain well and arrange in the plate you will serve
3. Place on top salt, pepper, oil and vinegar

title: Du Potiron et Citrouille (Pumpkin and Squash Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pumpkin or squash
- water
- milk
- butter
- salt
- sugar
- bread

## Directions

1. Cook the pumpkin or squash with water
2. When it is cooked and there is no longer much water, add milk, a piece of butter, salt and sugar if you wish
3. Soak bread in it and do not let it simmer at all

title: Eaux de Cerises, de Groseilles, de Fraises, de Framboises, de Mûres (Refreshing Waters of Cherries, Currants, Strawberries, Raspberries, and Blackberries)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Fruit of choice (cherries, currants, strawberries, raspberries, or blackberries)
- One pint of water per pound of fruit
- A little sugar

## Directions

1. Take such fruit as you judge fitting to make your refreshing water
2. For one pound of fruit put one pint of water
3. Crush your fruit and dilute it with water
4. Pass it through a white cloth and add a little sugar
5. Pass it again through a strainer so your water is clear
6. Keep it cool until the moment you serve it
7. If you wish to make ices from it, add a little more sugar and put your water in tin molds, then freeze it with ice and salt or saltpeter
8. When they begin to freeze, take care to stir them from time to time with a spoon until they are set, because the edges would be too frozen and the middle would not be; when they are set properly, serve them in small cups and drink them immediately

title: Eggs on a Plate, also called Mirror Eggs (Oeufs au Plat, autrement dits au Miroir)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Milk

## Directions

1. Take a pan suitable for fire and spread a little butter on the bottom.
2. Place eggs on top and season with salt and pepper.
3. Add two or three spoonfuls of milk.
4. Cook over low heat on the stove.
5. Pass a hot shovel over them and serve.

title: Eggs with Black Butter (Oeufs au Beurre Noir)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Put a piece of butter in a pan and melt it over the fire.
2. When it stops crackling, add eggs that have been broken into the pan and seasoned with salt and pepper.
3. Cook the eggs.
4. Pass a hot shovel over them to cook the yolk.
5. Serve with a dash of vinegar on top.

title: Eggs with Milk (Oeufs au Lait)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three eggs
- Half a spoonful of flour
- Sugar the size of a walnut
- A little salt
- Three portions of milk

## Directions

1. Mix the three eggs with half a spoonful of flour, sugar the size of a walnut, and a little salt.
2. Add three portions of milk and mix all together.
3. Put into the serving dish.
4. Cook over heat for a quarter-hour.
5. Pass a hot shovel over them and serve as soon as they are cooked.

title: Eggs à la Bagnolet (Oeufs à la Bagnolet)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight fresh eggs
- Cooked ham, chopped
- Cooking juice (coulis)
- Bouillon
- Vinegar
- Pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Poach eight fresh eggs.
2. Put chopped cooked ham in a pan with a little cooking juice and bouillon.
3. Add a dash of vinegar, coarse pepper, and a little salt.
4. Heat the sauce and serve it over the eggs.

title: Eggs à la Duchesse (Oeufs à la Duchesse)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three half-measures of cream
- Sugar
- Orange flowers
- Praline
- Candied citron
- Marzipan
- Eight eggs

## Directions

1. Boil three half-measures of cream with sugar, orange flowers, praline, candied citron, and marzipan, all chopped very finely.
2. Take eight eggs and beat the whites separately, setting the yolks aside.
3. Using a spoon, poach two or three spoonfuls of the beaten whites at a time in the cream, forming poached eggs without yolks.
4. Drain them and stack them one on top of another until you have formed eight poached eggs.
5. Arrange them on the serving dish.
6. Put the cream on the fire to reduce it to the consistency of a sauce.
7. When ready to serve, add the eight yolks and thicken over the fire without boiling, to prevent them from curdling.
8. Arrange the sauce over the egg whites.

title: Emincé de Gigot de Mouton aux oignons (Sliced Leg of Mutton with Onions)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton, sliced
- twelve large onions
- butter (two spoonfuls)
- Spanish sauce (3 spoonfuls)
- consommé (3 spoonfuls)
- coarse pepper
- salt
- croûtons
- flour (if sauce is not available)
- broth or water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice the meat from the leg of mutton as previously described
2. Cut twelve large onions in half, remove the head and tail
3. Cut them into slices forming half-circles, removing the core
4. Put a large piece of butter (about the size of an egg) in a pan
5. Pass the onions over a fairly hot fire
6. When the onions are golden, add three spoonfuls of Spanish sauce and three spoonfuls of consommé, a little coarse pepper and salt
7. Reduce the onion until slightly thick
8. Pour it over the sliced mutton, sauté to mix with the onion, and keep warm without boiling
9. Arrange when ready to serve with croûtons like the preceding ones
10. If sauce is not available, mix one half-spoonful of flour with the butter and onion, moisten with broth or water, add salt and coarse pepper, and when ready to combine with the sliced mutton, add a dash of vinegar

title: Emincé de Gigot à la chicorée (Sliced Leg of Mutton with Chicory)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton, roasted on a spit and cooled
- chicory hearts
- Spanish sauce (3 spoons)
- consommé (3 or 4 spoons)
- coarse pepper
- salt
- butter
- croûtons fried in butter
- flour (if Spanish sauce is not available)

## Directions

1. Once the leg of mutton is roasted and cooled, remove the meat and slice it thinly
2. Deposit the slices in a saucepan
3. Blanch chicory hearts and press well to remove water
4. Mince the chicory and pass it through a sieve with butter
5. Add three spoonfuls of Spanish sauce and three or four spoonfuls of consommé, a little coarse pepper and salt
6. Reduce the chicory
7. When thickened slightly, pour it over the sliced mutton, mix well together, and keep warm without boiling
8. When ready to serve, arrange in a pyramid shape with croûtons cut into mushroom shapes and fried in butter around it
9. Pour a little Spanish sauce over the top, but sparingly
10. If Spanish sauce is not available, pass the chicory in butter with salt and coarse pepper; when well browned, add one spoonful of flour, mix well with the chicory, moisten with broth, reduce if too thin (but it should be more liquid than for an entrée), and pour over the sliced mutton
11. Check seasoning

title: Emincé de Mouton aux concombres (Sliced Mutton with Cucumbers)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton, sliced
- cucumbers, cut in rounds the size of a small coin, eight lines thick
- butter
- Spanish sauce (3 spoonfuls)
- salt
- coarse pepper
- nutmeg, grated
- croûtons
- flour (if sauce is not available)

## Directions

1. Slice the meat from the leg of mutton as previously described
2. Cut cucumbers in rounds the size of a small coin, eight lines thick
3. Place them in cloth and press to remove water
4. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan over a hot fire
5. When the butter is hot, add the cucumbers and sauté constantly so they take on color
6. When they are golden, drain them
7. Add three spoonfuls of Spanish sauce, mix the cucumbers in it
8. Reduce them
9. When slightly thick, place them on the sliced mutton with a little salt, coarse pepper, and grated nutmeg
10. Keep warm
11. When ready to serve, arrange the sliced mutton on the platter with croûtons around it as in the preceding recipes
12. If sauce is not available, leave a little butter with the cucumbers, add almost one spoonful of flour

title: Empotage (Meat Stock)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four pounds of sliced beef
- Two calf necks and one calf knuckle
- Four old hens
- Two spoonfuls of broth
- Carrots
- Turnips
- Onions

## Directions

1. Put in a pot or saucepan (according to the quantity of empotage) three or four pounds of sliced beef, two calf necks and one calf knuckle, four old hens
2. Moisten this quantity of meat with two spoonfuls of broth
3. Put your pot on a stove and boil everything until it is reduced
4. Be careful that your empotage is not too colored; for this you must be careful that your sweating does not stick too much; make it a beautiful blond color
5. Then fill your pot with great broth and garnish it with vegetables such as carrots, turnips, onions
6. Do not add salt since your great broth is seasoned
7. Let your pot remain three and a half hours on the fire
8. When the meats are cooked, pass the broth of your empotage through fine cloth or a sieve
9. Try to make your broth very clear; you will use it to moisten all your soups

title: Entrecôte au Jus (Rib Steak with Jus)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rib of beef from under the shoulder
- Oil or butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Take the rib of beef found under the shoulder
2. Trim it so only the rib bone remains, which you clear at the end
3. Beat it to tenderize it
4. Dip your rib in oil or butter
5. Season it with salt and pepper
6. Grill it over gentle heat so your rib does not burn and cooks slowly
7. It requires half an hour if your rib is not very thick; if it is, it requires three-quarters of an hour or an hour so the heat can penetrate it well
8. When it is cooked, put clear jus underneath

title: Entrecôte Grillée Sauce au Beurre d'Anchois (Grilled Rib Steak with Anchovy Butter Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rib of beef
- Oil or butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Espagnole sauce
- Anchovy butter, the size of an egg
- Roux
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your rib as the one with jus
2. Have a prepared espagnole sauce that is somewhat clear
3. At the moment of saucing, put into your sauce, which will be very hot, anchovy butter the size of an egg, which you will stir well into your sauce until it is well melted, without putting your sauce on the fire, or at least without boiling it
4. Pour it over your rib (see Prepared Espagnole and Anchovy Butter)
5. If you lack sauce, make a light roux which you moisten with seasoned bouillon, and add your anchovy butter to it

title: Entrecôte Grillée Sauce aux Cornichons (Grilled Rib Steak with Gherkin Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rib of beef
- Oil or butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Piquant sauce
- Gherkins
- Water
- Fine pepper
- Shallots
- Breadcrumbs
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare your rib as the one with jus
2. When it is cooked, have a piquant sauce in which you put your gherkins
3. At the moment of saucing, if you do not have gherkins, you can make a sauce with water, salt, pepper, minced shallots, a little breadcrumbs, and a little vinegar
4. Boil everything together
5. At the moment of pouring your sauce, add your gherkins

title: Entrecôte Grillée Sauce Hachée (Grilled Rib Steak with Chopped Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rib of beef
- Oil or butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Chopped sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your rib as the one with jus
2. Sauce it with a chopped sauce (see Chopped Sauce)

title: Entrecôte Grillée Sauce Piquante (Grilled Rib Steak with Piquant Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rib of beef
- Oil or butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare your rib and cook it as the one with jus
2. Pour piquant sauce over it (see Piquant Sauce)

title: Epaule de Veau à la Bourgeoise (Veal Shoulder in the Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal shoulder
- half a setier of water
- two spoonfuls of vinegar
- salt
- coarse pepper
- parsley
- chives
- two cloves of garlic
- one bay leaf
- two onions
- two roots
- three cloves
- one piece of butter

## Directions

1. Put a veal shoulder in a terrine with half a setier of water, two spoonfuls of vinegar, salt, coarse pepper, parsley, chives, two cloves of garlic, one bay leaf, two onions and two roots cut in slices, three cloves, one piece of butter.
2. Cover the terrine with a lid and seal the edges with flour mixed with a little water.
3. Cook in the oven for three hours.
4. Degrease the sauce and pass it through a tamis.
5. Serve over the shoulder.

title: Epaules d'Agneau à la polonaise (Lamb Shoulders à la Polonaise)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- two lamb shoulders
- salt
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)
- aromates piles (ground aromatics)

## Directions

1. Debone two lamb shoulders entirely without leaving any bone
