2. Season them inside with salt, coarse pepper, and a little ground aromatics
3. Gather the flesh together with a needle

title: Epigramme of Lamb
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb forequarters
- Lamb shoulders
- Lamb breast and cutlets
- Atelet sauce or salt and coarse pepper
- Warm butter
- Breadcrumbs
- Tender loin

## Directions

1. Take the forequarters of lamb, or both if you wish, so your entrée is substantial
2. Remove the shoulders
3. Cut the breasts so your cutlets are not damaged
4. Cook your shoulders on the spit and let them cool
5. Cook your breasts as explained for shoulders of lamb with cucumbers
6. When they are cooked, place them between two covers to take on a uniform shape and let them cool
7. Cut them into oval pieces the size of a crouton, pointed on one end
8. Leave a small breast bone showing
9. Coat them with atelet sauce if you have it, or season with a little fine salt and coarse pepper
10. Dip them in warm butter
11. Put them in breadcrumbs and give them a beautiful form
12. Your pieces should be slightly larger than your cutlets
13. Place them on a shallow pan
14. You may also bread your tendons in the English manner and fry them
15. Cut and trim your cutlets
16. Season them with a little salt and a little coarse pepper
17. Place them in your sautoir with warm butter on top

title: Escargots de Vigne en Fricassée de Poulets (Vineyard Snails in Chicken Fricassee Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Vineyard snails
- Wood ashes
- River water
- Butter
- Bouquet of parsley
- Ciboule
- One clove of garlic
- Two cloves
- Thyme
- Laurel
- Basil
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Egg yolks
- Cream
- Parsley
- Mustard
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put a good handful of ashes in a medium pot with river water and bring to a boil
2. Throw in the snails and leave for a quarter hour
3. When they come easily out of their shells, remove them to lukewarm water to clean
4. Place them again in clear water and boil for an instant, then drain
5. In a casserole, put a piece of butter with a bouquet of parsley, ciboule, one clove of garlic, two cloves, thyme, laurel, basil, and salt
6. Heat gently and coat the snails lightly with flour mixed with a little salt and pepper
7. Moisten with bouillon and a little white wine
8. Let cook until the snails are tender and the sauce is reduced
9. When serving, add three yolks of eggs mixed with a little cream and finely chopped parsley
10. Bind without boiling and add a little white mustard with a pinch of nutmeg

title: Espagnole Travaillée (Worked Spanish Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 tablespoons Spanish sauce
- 3 tablespoons consommé
- a handful of mushrooms
- veal stock if needed for color

## Directions

1. If your Spanish sauce is not sufficiently colored, add veal stock
2. Boil the sauce over moderate heat
3. Skim and degrease carefully so the sauce is of good color and not cloudy
4. When the sauce is reduced and thickened like clear porridge, strain through a sieve
5. Place in a saucepan and keep warm in a bain-marie for use as needed

title: Essence de Gibier (Game Essence)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Four rabbits
- Four partridges
- Two calf necks
- Two pounds of sliced beef
- One bottle of dry white wine
- Half great broth and half consommé
- Eight carrots
- Ten onions
- Three cloves
- A little thyme
- A little basil
- A little wild thyme

## Directions

1. Have four rabbits, four partridges, two calf necks, two pounds of sliced beef
2. Put these meats in a pot
3. Throw a bottle of dry white wine into it
4. Boil everything until it has reduced to glaze
5. When you see there is no more jus in your pot, do not wait for the bottom to take color to fill it
6. Put half great broth and half consommé
7. Try not to have too much liquid
8. Garnish your pot with eight carrots, ten onions, three cloves, a little thyme, a little basil, a little wild thyme
9. Let your pot boil gently
10. When your meats are cooked, pass your game essence through fine cloth
11. Be careful to skim your pot well and do not add salt since your great broth is seasoned

title: Essence de Légumes (Vegetable Essence)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of sliced beef
- One calf knuckle
- One hen
- Thirty or forty carrots
- Thirty or forty turnips
- Thirty or forty onions
- Five or six stalks of celery
- Two blanched lettuces

## Directions

1. Put three pounds of sliced beef in your pot, one calf knuckle, one hen, thirty or forty carrots, as many turnips, as many onions, five or six stalks of celery, two blanched lettuces

title: Essence of Vegetables (Vegetable Stock)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of beef slice
- One veal knuckle
- One chicken
- Thirty or forty carrots
- The same amount of turnips
- The same amount of onions
- Five or six celery stalks
- Two blanched lettuces
- One bouquet of chervil
- Four cloves
- Grand bouillon (for moistening)

## Directions

1. Put three pounds of beef slice in your pot with a veal knuckle and a chicken
2. Add thirty or forty carrots, the same amount of turnips and onions, five or six celery stalks, two blanched lettuces, and a bouquet of chervil
3. Add four cloves
4. Moisten your vegetables and meat with grand bouillon
5. Make the pot foam so that your essence becomes very clear
6. When your meats are cooked, pass your essence through a fine cloth
7. Ensure it is very clear and not in great quantity
8. Do not add salt, since your grand bouillon is already seasoned

title: Esturgeon à la Matelote (Sturgeon in Matelote Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of sturgeon
- Bread crumbs
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Flour
- Shallot
- Parsley
- Chives
- Two glasses of red wine
- Capers

## Directions

1. Cut bread crumbs into rounds the size of a small coin.
2. Pass them over the fire with butter until they are a beautiful golden color and let them drain.
3. Cut a piece of sturgeon into small slices, slightly thin.
4. Put them in a plate, arranged so they are not on top of each other, with a piece of butter, salt, and coarse pepper.
5. Cook them over low heat, and as they are cooked on one side, turn them to the other.
6. Cooking takes only a quarter hour.
7. Remove them from the plate and put a little flour in it, which you stir with the butter, and then add finely chopped shallot, parsley, and chives.
8. Moisten with two glasses of red wine.
9. Let the whole boil together for a quarter hour.
10. Put the sturgeon back in the sauce to heat it without boiling.
11. Throw in a little chopped capers.
12. Garnish the edges of the plate with your fried bread croutons, being careful to sprinkle them a little with sauce.

title: Faisan aux Choux (Pheasant with Cabbage)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant, prepared as for stewed pheasant
- Lard strips (bardes)
- One pound of lardons
- One medium-sized cervelas sausage
- Four carrots
- Four onions
- Two cloves
- Two bay leaves
- Several slices of veal
- Cabbages, blanched
- Bouillon
- Coarse pepper
- Game essence sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare your pheasant as for the preceding recipe
2. Place lard strips in a saucepan with your pheasant, one pound of lardons, one medium cervelas sausage, four carrots, four onions, two cloves, two bay leaves, and several slices of veal
3. Blanch your cabbages; tie and cook them with your pheasant
4. Moisten with bouillon; add a little coarse pepper, and no salt because of the bouillon and lard
5. Simmer for two hours
6. At the moment of serving, drain your cabbages
7. Place your pheasant in the middle of your dish, the cabbages, your lardons, and your cervelas sausage around it
8. Pour a game-flavored sauce over and around it

title: Faisan Étouffé (Stewed Pheasant)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant, plucked and cleaned
- Medium lardons, seasoned with salt, coarse pepper, and quatre épices
- Lard strips (bardes)
- White wine
- Bouillon
- Game essence

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean your pheasant, then singe it
2. Tuck the thighs inside; truss it
3. Lard it with medium lardons seasoned with salt, coarse pepper, and a little quatre épices, larder the breast and thighs
4. Cover it with a lard strip and tie it
5. Place lard strips in your casserole with the pheasant on top
6. Cover a cooking vessel which you will moisten with half white wine, half bouillon
7. Simmer it for two hours
8. At the moment of serving, drain it, untruss it, and arrange it on your dish
9. Serve underneath a game essence

title: Faisan à la Purée de Lentilles (Pheasant with Lentil Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant
- Lardons
- Cooking liquid from the pheasant
- Lentil purée

## Directions

1. Empty, truss, and lard your pheasant as you would for the stewed pheasant
2. Cook it the same way
3. At the moment of serving, drain it, untruss it, and arrange it on your dish
4. Mask it with a lentil purée
5. Pass the cooking liquid through a strainer and put it in your purée
6. Reduce it so that it has the taste of pheasant smoke

title: Faisans, Comment les Servir (How to Serve Pheasants)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pheasants or young pheasants
- pheasant livers
- lard
- parsley
- scallions
- salt
- pepper
- lard strips
- paper
- Provençal sauce or other small sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and lard the pheasants
2. Cook them on the spit and serve them with beautiful color
3. For a spit entrée, cook them on the spit with a small farce of their livers made by chopping them with grated lard, chopped parsley, chopped scallions, salt and coarse pepper
4. Wrap them in strips of lard and paper
5. Serve with a Provençal sauce or other small sauce in the modern taste

title: Farce cuite (Cooked Forcemeat)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken breast whites, cut in small dice
- A small piece of butter
- A little salt
- A little pepper
- A little grated nutmeg
- A piece of bread crumb
- Bouillon
- A little finely chopped parsley
- A cooked and cold veal breast, or butter in default
- Five or six egg yolks, according to the quantity of forcemeat

## Directions

1. Cut chicken breast whites into small dice
2. Put a small piece of butter in a saucepan with your raw chicken whites, a little salt, a little pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
3. Cook them on a low fire for ten minutes
4. Drain your whites and let them cool
5. Put a piece of bread crumb in the same saucepan with bouillon and a little finely chopped parsley
6. Stir it with a wooden spoon, mashing and reducing it to a paste
7. When your bouillon is reduced and your bread crumb is well softened, let it cool
8. Have a cooked and cold veal breast; if not available, use butter instead
9. Pound your chicken whites; when well pounded, pass them through a quenelle sieve and set aside
10. Pound your bread crumb the same way, pass it through the sieve and set apart
11. Pound your veal breast, pass it through the sieve and set to the side
12. Make three equal portions of chicken whites, bread crumb, and veal breast
13. Pound it all together
14. When you have pounded it for three quarters of an hour, add five or six egg yolks according to the quantity of forcemeat
15. Pound your yolks with your forcemeat as you add them
16. When your forcemeat is made, remove it from the mortar and put it in a terrine
17. Use it as needed, either with butcher's meat, poultry, or game; use the same procedure and also employ this forcemeat for the gravies

title: Façon de Faire du Vinaigre Rouge, Blanc et Rozat (Method for Making Red, White, and Rose Vinegar)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A barrel
- The strongest vinegar
- Good vinegar
- Clean wine suitable for making vinegar
- Juice of ripe wild blackberries (optional, for color)

## Directions

1. According to the quantity of vinegar you wish to make, take a vessel more or less large; to make twenty pints, take a barrel of that size that is new; if it is old wood, you must have it planed inside.
2. Take one pint of the strongest vinegar that you make boil, and put it boiling into the barrel, which you will plug well with the bung, and roll it while shaking until it is completely cold.
3. Six hours later, remove this vinegar and place the barrel in a warm location.
4. After having bunged it, make a hole in the top of the barrel above the jable, large enough to insert a large funnel.
5. Pour in through the funnel two pints of good vinegar.
6. Eight days later, add one pint of clean wine suitable for making vinegar.
7. Every eight days add one pint of wine until the barrel is half full, then you may add more.
8. You must take care that the wine is always of the same strength as the first that you put in, because if it were weaker, the increase you would make would not have the same strength.
9. Your barrel being full and the vinegar in its state, withdraw two-thirds of it which you put in another vessel.
10. Then you will gradually return wine to the barrel as described above; by this means you always have vinegar.
11. For white vinegar: put ten pints of vinegar on the fire and let it boil until it is reduced to eight; then have it distilled in an alembic, putting in more or less according to the quantity you desire.
12. For rose vinegar: dry an ounce of musk roses in the sun for two days, which you will put in one pint of vinegar; place the roses and vinegar in the sun for fifteen days in a well-stopped bottle.
13. For tarragon, elder, or carnation vinegar: make the same way; for orange flower vinegar, put the flower without being dried.
14. For garlic vinegar: take four pints of white vinegar for one ounce of garlic, twelve cloves, and one nutmeg cut into pieces.

title: Façon de Faire Toutes Sortes de Saucisses (All Kinds of Sausages)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pork meat with more fat than lean
- parsley
- chives
- salt
- fine spices
- veal or pork casings
- truffles (optional)
- shallots (optional)

## Directions

1. Chop the pork meat and add chopped parsley and chives
2. Season with salt and fine spices
3. Stuff everything into veal or pork casings
4. Tie the sausages to the desired length
5. Grill them, giving them the flavor you wish, such as with truffles or shallots
6. For truffles: chop them with the meat according to the quantity you wish
7. For shallots: use very little so the flavor does not dominate
8. For flat sausages: make them the same way, but put the meat in pork caul and grill them the same way

title: Feuillantines (Puff Pastry Tarts with Frangipane)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry dough
- frangipane cream
- egg for gilding

## Directions

1. Make puff pastry dough as described previously
2. Roll out to the size of a pie and thickness of a small coin
3. Place on a pie pan and spread frangipane cream on top
4. Cover with another rolled-out base that is cut out and openwork
5. Glue together by pressing on the edges
6. Gild with beaten egg
7. Bake in the oven for one hour
8. Small ones can be made slightly larger than tartlets using the same method

title: Feuilletage (Puff Pastry)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- two litrons of flour
- half an ounce of salt
- butter the size of an egg
- two egg whites
- two glasses of water
- one pound of butter

## Directions

1. Put two litrons of flour on your pastry board
2. Make a hole in the middle
3. Use half an ounce of salt, butter the size of an egg, two egg whites, and two glasses of water
4. Assemble your dough; it should be as firm as butter
5. Let it rest for half an hour
6. Take one pound of butter; if it is too firm, work it
7. Flatten your puff pastry dough and place the butter on it
8. Fold the dough over the butter so it is well wrapped
9. Let it rest a moment
10. Give it two turns, that is to say with a rolling pin press on the dough, rolling it always until your puff pastry is only half an inch thick everywhere
11. Then fold it in three, that is the long piece is folded one part to two-thirds

title: Filet d'Aloyau au Vin de Malaga (Sirloin Filet with Malaga Wine)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- filet (sirloin)
- gros lardons (large lardons)
- épices (spices)
- thym (thyme)
- deux feuilles de laurier (two bay leaves)
- sel fin (fine salt)
- bardes de lard (bacon strips)
- deux tranches de veau (two veal slices)
- deux tranches de bœuf (two beef slices)
- carottes (carrots)
- oignons (onions)
- bouquet garni (bouquet garni)
- demi-bouteille de vin de Malaga ou trois quarts de bouteille (half bottle or three quarters of a bottle of Malaga wine)
- bouillon (broth)
- grande espagnole (espagnole sauce)

## Directions

1. Trim your filet as in previous recipes
2. Lard it with large lardons containing spices, a little thyme, two minced or crushed bay leaves, and fine salt
3. Tie the filet securely with string
4. Place bacon strips, two veal slices, and two beef slices in the bottom of a casserole with vegetables such as carrots, onions, and a bouquet garni
5. Place your filet in the casserole and cover with bacon strips
6. Add half a bottle or three quarters of a bottle of Malaga wine, depending on the size of your filet, and half a spoonful of broth
7. Bring to a boil; once it releases some broth, simmer gently over low heat for two and a half hours
8. When cooked, strain the cooking liquid through a silk sieve
9. Add three spoonfuls of espagnole sauce and pour the strained liquid into your sauce
10. Reduce to a glaze, meaning you produce only enough juice to sauce your filet
11. Remove and untie the filet, glaze it, and pour your reduction underneath
12. Alternatively, you may cook this filet by placing it between bacon strips with vegetables moistened with Malaga wine and a little broth

title: Filet d'Aloyau au Vin de Malaga (Sirloin Fillet with Malaga Wine)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin fillet
- large lardons
- spices
- thyme
- bay leaves
- salt
- lard strips
- veal slices
- beef slices
- vegetables such as carrots and onions
- bouquet garni
- Malaga wine

## Directions

1. Trim your fillet as in the previous recipes
2. Lard it with large lardons in which you put spices, a little thyme, two bay leaves chopped or crushed, a little fine salt
3. When your fillet is larded, tie it
4. Put in the bottom of a pot strips of lard, two slices of veal, two slices of beef, vegetables such as carrots, onions, a bouquet garni
5. Place your fillet in your pot; garnish it with strips of lard and a half-bottle of Malaga wine, or three-quarters of a bottle, according to how large your fillet is

title: Filet d'Aloyau aux Concombres (Sirloin Fillet with Cucumbers)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin fillet
- cucumbers
- roux
- cooking liquid from the fillet
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Trim your sirloin fillet
2. Cook it as you would cook it braised
3. Drain it and glaze it
4. Place cucumbers underneath
5. If you do not have sauce for your cucumbers, use the cooking liquid from your sirloin
6. Make a light roux which you will moisten with the juice in which your fillet has cooked
7. Pass it through a silk sieve; take care to make your sauce somewhat thin so you can reduce it
8. Skim the fat and pass it through cheesecloth and pour it under your fillet, or place your cucumbers to cook in it; make sure your sauce is well salted and add a little coarse pepper

title: Filet d'Aloyau aux Cornichons (Sirloin Filet with Gherkins)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- filet (sirloin)
- lard fin (fine lard)
- cornichons (gherkins)
- grande espagnole (espagnole sauce)
- vinaigre (vinegar)
- sel (salt)
- poivre fin (fine pepper)
- consommé (consommé)
- roux (roux)
- fond ou bouillon (broth or stock)

## Directions

1. Trim your filet as you would for braising
2. Lard it with fine lard and place it on a spit, then arrange it on your serving platter in such a way that it is not too long so your dish can contain it
3. Prepare your gherkins cut into small sticks
4. Take six spoonfuls of espagnole sauce and add three spoonfuls of vinegar, a little salt, and fine pepper to dominate; add eight spoonfuls of consommé
5. Reduce everything by half, skim and degrease your sauce
6. When reduced, strain it through cheesecloth, add your gherkins, and keep this sauce hot without boiling
7. After your filet has been on the spit for an hour and a half, place it on your platter with your sauce underneath
8. If you lack espagnole, make a light roux, moisten it with stock or broth, season as above, add your gherkins cut into thin slices, salt, and fine pepper

title: Filet d'Aloyau aux Laitues (Sirloin Fillet with Lettuces)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin fillet
- Spanish sauce
- lettuces

## Directions

1. Trim your sirloin fillet as you would trim it braised
2. Drain it and glaze it
3. Add a Spanish sauce to it and arrange a border of lettuces around it
4. If you do not have what you need to make your lettuces, cook your sirloin fillet in advance; use the stock for your lettuces; keep your fillet warm; one hour is enough to cook your lettuces
5. Put a bound sauce on your fillet

title: Filet d'Aloyau aux Oignons Glacés (Sirloin Fillet with Glazed Onions)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin fillet
- glazed onions
- Spanish sauce
- roux
- cooking liquid from the fillet

## Directions

1. Trim your sirloin fillet
2. Cook it as you would cook it braised
3. Drain it and glaze it
4. Place it on your serving dish with glazed onions
5. Put a Spanish sauce worked around it
6. Lacking sauce, make a light roux which you will moisten with the juice in which your sirloin fillet has cooked; add glazed onions to it

title: Filet d'Aloyau Braisé (Braised Sirloin Fillet)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin fillet
- fat to remove
- large lardons
- thyme
- bay leaf
- quatre épices
- salt
- pepper
- lard strips
- veal slices
- beef slices
- four to six onions
- cloves
- parsley
- chives
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Remove the sirloin fillet and remove all the fat from it
2. Lay your fillet on the table on the side where the skin is; make sure the meat lies flat on the table with your hand
3. Take a large sharp knife; place the blade between the skin and the meat so well that by moving your knife back and forth, as if you were lifting a strip of lard, you separate the skin with the fillet and it becomes well unified and trimmed
4. If any skin remains, you must remove it by sliding the blade of your knife as close as possible to the skin to avoid hollows in your fillet
5. When it is well trimmed, take large lardons in which you put a little thyme, a little crushed or finely chopped bay leaf, a little quatre épices, and a little salt and pepper
6. Lard your fillet and tie it, giving it the shape you want
7. Put in the bottom of your pot strips of lard, slices of veal and beef, four or six onions, one of which is studded with three cloves, two bay leaves, a little thyme, a bouquet of parsley and chives
8. Place your fillet in your pot where your braising base is marked; cover it with lard and place a few pieces of meat around it
9. Pour in two large spoonfuls of bouillon and very little salt
10. Bring your braise to a boil, then place it over a gentle fire to simmer for two and a half hours
11. Take the cooking liquid in which your fillet has cooked; pass it through a silk sieve and reduce it
12. When your fillet is drained, untie it and glaze it
13. Dress it on your serving dish and pour the reduced cooking liquid over it
14. If you do not have everything needed to braise your fillet, trim it and lard it with large lard; put your trimmings in the bottom of your pot; tie your fillet; place it on your trimmings; add your carrots, onions, bouquet of aromatics, two spoonfuls of bouillon; if you have none, add water and salt, not too much so that you can use the cooking liquid for what you have to do; when it is cooked, serve it as above: make sure your fillet looks good and is well seasoned

title: Filet d'Aloyau à la Mauglat (Sirloin Fillet Mauglat Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin fillet
- Spanish sauce
- cooking liquid from the fillet
- roux

## Directions

1. This fillet must be cooked the day before
2. Trim and cook your fillet as you would cook it braised
3. When it is cold, make an oval hole in it
4. Cut into dice the meat you have removed from your fillet—neither small nor large pieces
5. Reduce a Spanish sauce with a little cooking liquid from your fillet
6. When your sauce is well reduced, add your small pieces of meat to it and keep it hot in a bain-marie
7. At the moment of serving, reheat your fillet
8. Drain it and glaze it
9. Place it on the serving dish and put your little ragout inside
10. Have a clear Spanish sauce which you will put underneath
11. Lacking sauce, make a light roux which you will moisten with the cooking liquid in which your sirloin fillet has cooked; reduce your sauce well so that it takes on good flavor

title: Filet de Bœuf à la Broche (Beef Filet on the Spit)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef filet from the sirloin piece
- fat covering
- piquant sauce or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Remove your filet from the sirloin piece.
2. Leave it covered with fat, that is to say, pare the fat and leave it three fingers thick all along the filet.
3. Score the surface of the fat so that the heat penetrates your filet better.
4. Pass a large and long skewer along your filet.
5. Fold it somewhat upon itself so it is not too long.
6. Secure your filet on the spit.
7. One and a half hours suffice to cook it, according to the intensity of the fire and the thickness of the filet.
8. When it is cooked, serve a piquant sauce underneath or in a sauceboat; a tomato sauce also suits.
9. This filet is served as a roast.

title: Filet de Bœuf à la Conti (Beef Filet à la Conti)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- filet (beef filet)
- gros lard (large lard)
- beurre (butter)
- quatre carottes (four carrots)
- cinq oignons (five onions)
- deux feuilles de laurier (two bay leaves)
- bouquet de persil et de ciboules (bouquet of parsley and chives)
- trois clous de girofle (three cloves)
- gelée ou bouillon (aspic or broth)
- papier beurré (buttered paper)
- espagnole (espagnole sauce)
- consommé (consommé)
- farine (flour)

## Directions

1. Lard your filet as the previous one and lard it with large lard underneath
2. Tie it in such a way that it forms a spiral
3. Butter the bottom of your casserole and place your filet in it with four carrots, five onions, two bay leaves, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and three cloves
4. Add a spoonful of aspic or broth and a sheet of buttered paper cut in a round shape
5. Bring your filet to a boil; when it releases some broth, place it over low heat and place coals also on the lid of your casserole
6. Check from time to time, ensuring your filet takes on a beautiful color
7. It takes two hours to cook, and the cooking liquid must reduce to a glaze so your filet is glazed when you remove it
8. Remove your vegetables from the casserole along with the fat
9. Add four spoonfuls of espagnole and one of consommé to your casserole and detach the glaze from your filet
10. Strain your sauce through cheesecloth and pour it under your filet
11. If you lack sauce, glaze your filet with the glaze in your casserole; add about a teaspoon of flour that you dissolve with your glaze; add also a glass of broth; bring your sauce to a boil and strain it through cheesecloth; pour it over your filet and ensure your sauce has good seasoning

title: Filet de Bœuf, Sauce Tomate (Beef Filet with Tomato Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- filet (beef filet)
- sauce tomate (tomato sauce)

## Directions

1. Trim and lard your filet as the one called à la Conti
2. Cook it in the same manner
3. Place tomato sauce underneath
4. The Indian variety is the best; you may also braise it according to taste

title: Filet de Lièvre Piqué (Larded Hare Fillet)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- hare, skinned
- larding bacon
- veal slices
- carrot slices
- onion slices
- thyme
- bay leaf

## Directions

1. When your hare is skinned, thrust your knife along the spine of the back from the shoulder to the thigh, detaching the fillet
2. Slide your fingers between the bones and the fillet and detach it so that the thick end of the fillet still holds to the thigh
3. Then place the blade of your knife on the tender side of the fillet and your thumb on the skin side, press your blade on your fillet so that it comes to meet your thumb without cutting the skin
4. Then act as if you are pulling the fillet toward you; the sinewy skin remains and your fillet is found detached and trimmed at the same time
5. If some skin still remained, you would lightly pare your fillet by turning it
6. Lard the fillet with fine bacon and give it the shape you wish, whether long, round, or otherwise
7. Put lard bacon in a casserole, a few slices of veal, a few slices of carrots and onions, a little thyme and bay leaf
8. Place your fillets on this

title: Filet de Lièvre à la Poivrade (Hare Fillets with Poivrade Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one hare or young hare roasted on a spit and served from table
- sauce à la poivrade of strong flavour

## Directions

1. Take a hare or young hare that has been roasted on a spit and served from table
2. Remove the meat to cut it into fillets; if you do not have enough to garnish a dish, leave the bones and cut the pieces large and of equal size
3. Put them in a saucepan with a strong-flavoured poivrade sauce
4. Heat without boiling
5. Serve hot
6. You will find the poivrade sauce in the section on sauces

title: Filet de Mouton en Brézole (Braised Lamb Loin)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole lamb loin
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- a pinch of garlic
- melted lard
- salt
- coarse pepper
- coulis

## Directions

1. Trim the lamb loin of all sinews and cut it thin
2. Place it in a casserole, layer by layer, with chopped parsley, chives, mushrooms, and a pinch of garlic, melted lard, salt, and coarse pepper
3. Cook it braised over very low heat
4. When cooked, remove the fat and detach the fillets, add a little coulis to the sauce and serve

title: Filet de Mouton en Paupiettes (Lamb Loin Roulades)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole lamb loin
- cooked chicken breast
- blanched beef fat
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- four egg yolks
- caul fat

## Directions

1. Cut the whole loin into slices of its full width
2. Flatten the slices and place on each a good forcemeat made with cooked chicken breast, blanched beef fat, parsley, chives, chopped mushrooms, salt, pepper, and four egg yolks
3. Roll the paupiettes and cook them on the spit, wrapped in caul fat
4. Serve au naturel or with a ragout of chicory or lettuce

title: Filet de Sanglier Piqué Glacé (Larded and Glazed Wild Boar Loin)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild boar loin
- Lard
- Wild boar slices
- Thyme
- Sage
- Bay leaves
- Carrots
- Onions
- Buttered paper
- White wine
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Peppercorns
- Roux

## Directions

1. Trim your wild boar loin as you would trim a beef sirloin
2. Marinate for forty-eight hours or longer if desired
3. Shape the loin as desired
4. Place lard in a casserole with a few slices of wild boar, thyme, a little sage, four bay leaves, carrots, and onions
5. Place your loin on this seasoning
6. Cover it with buttered paper
7. Add one glass of white wine, one glass of bouillon, and a little salt
8. Cook over heat above and below for two hours
9. At the moment of serving, drain and glaze it
10. Dress it on the platter
11. Serve a piquant sauce underneath; if you have no sauce, make a light roux, add the cooking liquid from your loin, reduce your sauce by half, check that it is properly salted, and place it under your loin

title: Filet Mignon (Pork Tenderloin)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tenderloins
- fine lard
- lard strips
- veal slices
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- parsley
- chives
- bay leaves
- bouillon
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove your pork tenderloins along their entire length
2. Trim them and lard them with fine lard
3. Leave them lengthwise, or form them into gimblettes, that is to say give them a round shape, and lard them on top
4. Put lard strips in a casserole, a few slices of veal, two carrots, three onions, two cloves, a bouquet of parsley and chives, two bay leaves, and your tenderloins on top of the seasoning
5. Cover them then with a double round of buttered paper
6. Put a small spoonfuls of bouillon
7. Place them on the fire one hour before serving
8. Put fire on the lid to make them glaze
9. At the moment of eating, drain them and glaze them
10. You can put underneath chicory, cucumbers, a mushroom purée, a tomato sauce or a piquant sauce

title: Filets d'Agneau en Blanquette (Lamb Fillets in Blanquette Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- mushrooms
- bouquet garni
- flour
- bouillon
- lamb fillets cooked on the spit
- egg yolks
- milk
- verjuice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Put a piece of butter in a saucepan with mushrooms cut into fillets and a bouquet garni, pass them over the fire, and add a pinch of flour
2. Moisten with bouillon and cook the mushrooms until there is scarcely any sauce remaining
3. Put in the lamb fillets cooked on the spit, cut into small thin pieces, with a liaison of three egg yolks diluted with milk
4. Make the sauce bind over the fire without boiling, season it to good taste
5. Add a dash of verjuice or vinegar when serving

title: Filets d'Agneau à la Béchamel (Lamb Fillets in Béchamel Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cream
- lamb fillets

## Directions

1. Make the béchamel by reducing cream until it is thick enough to make a sauce
2. When it begins to thicken, stir it constantly so it does not become lumpy
3. When ready to serve, put in the fillets cut as described for the blanquette
4. Heat them without boiling, season them to good taste and serve

title: Filets de Bécasses en Canapé (Woodcock Fillets on Toast)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fillets from four woodcocks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Interior of the woodcocks except the gizzard
- Cooked lard the size of an egg
- Parsley
- Shallots
- Aromatic herbs
- Bread for croutons

## Directions

1. Remove and trim the fillets from four woodcocks; arrange them in your sauté pan
2. Season them with salt and pepper; warm a piece of butter and pour it over your fillets
3. Take the interior of your woodcocks, except the gizzard; mix together cooked lard the size of an egg, finely chopped parsley and shallots, a little pounded aromatic herbs, salt, and coarse pepper; chop everything together with your knife
4. When your forcemeat is ready, make croutons slightly larger and in the same shape as your fillets, thick about half an inch
5. Before browning them in butter, make an incision one line from the edge inside all around; when your crouton is browned, hollow it out so it can hold forcemeat
6. Fill each crouton completely so you can place your fillet on top
7. About a quarter hour before serving, put your croutons in the oven or on a grill with gentle heat
8. Sauté your fillets, arrange your croutons flat, and place one fillet on each crouton
9. Cover with the same sauce as before, adding about a walnut-sized piece of glaze

title: Filets de Chevreuil (Roe Deer Loins)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Roe deer loin
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Fine pepper
- Bay leaves
- Cloves
- Thyme
- Onions
- Parsley
- Chives
- Lard strips
- Roe deer slices
- Carrots
- White wine
- Bouillon
- Roux
- Juice
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove the two loins from your roe deer
2. Trim them as explained for beef loin
3. Lard them and place them in a terrine with two glasses of vinegar, salt, fine pepper, four bay leaves, six cloves, six or eight branches of thyme, four onions cut into slices, a small handful of parsley in branches, and a few whole chives
4. Leave to marinate forty-eight hours or longer if you have time
5. When you wish to serve them, remove them from your marinade and prepare them properly
6. Spread strips of lard in a casserole with a few slices of roe deer, three carrots, three onions, two cloves, two bay leaves, and a little thyme
7. Arrange your loins in spirals or otherwise
8. Place them on your seasoning, cover them with buttered paper
9. Pour over them half a bottle of white wine and as much bouillon with a little salt
10. Let them simmer over heat above and below for one hour
11. At the moment of serving, drain them, remove the string or skewers if there are any
12. Glaze them and dress them on your platter
13. Serve a piquant sauce underneath; if you have none, make a light roux that you will moisten with the cooking liquid from your loins, add a little juice so your sauce has good color, three spoonfuls of vinegar, salt, and fine pepper
14. Reduce your sauce by half so it takes on flavor
15. You can also roast these same loins on a spit if you do not wish to braise them

title: Filets de Faisan à la Chevalier (Pheasant Fillets à la Chevalier)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight pheasant fillets
- Pheasant tenderloin fillets (filets mignons)
- Fine lard for larding
- Lard strips (bardes)
- Pheasant trimmings
- Several slices of veal
- Two carrots
- Four onions
- Two bay leaves
- Two cloves
- Buttered paper
- One glass white wine
- Two glasses of consommé
- Salt
- Espagnole sauce
- Butter
- Croûtons

## Directions

1. Remove eight fillets from the pheasant
2. Set aside the tenderloin fillets which you will sauté and arrange in the middle
3. Trim your fillets and lard them with fine lard
4. Place lard strips in a saucepan with the trimmings of your pheasants, several slices of veal, two carrots, four onions, two bay leaves, two cloves
5. Arrange your fillets well so they keep a beautiful form
6. Place over them a round of buttered paper, one glass of white wine and two glasses of consommé, a little salt
7. There should be no liquid over your fillets, but liquid up to the larding
8. Place them on the fire one hour before serving
9. When they boil, place them over gentle heat, and much heat on the lid so they glaze
10. At the moment of serving, drain them, glaze them, pass through silk sieve the liquid in which they cooked
11. Reduce it almost to a glaze
12. Pour over it three spoonfuls of espagnole sauce which you boil with your reduction
13. Pass it through cheesecloth and arrange your fillets flat on croûtons passed in butter and three lines thick
14. Pour your sauce underneath; you can also place lard strips on a baking pan, and your fillets with a glass of white wine, salt, a little coarse pepper, a little aromatic spices, a round of buttered paper, and put them in the oven or under the oven

title: Filets de Lapereau aux Concombres (Young Rabbit Fillets with Cucumbers)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbit fillets
- Cucumbers à la crème
- Chicory, purée of cardoons, purée of mushrooms, sautéed mushrooms, or tomato sauce (optional additions)

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your fillets as the previous ones
2. Drain and glaze them
3. Arrange them in crowns
4. Put your cucumbers à la crème in the middle
5. You may also add chicory, a purée of cardoons, a purée of mushrooms, a sauté of mushrooms, or a tomato sauce

title: Filets de Lapereau en Couronnes (Young Rabbit Fillets in Crowns)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve rabbit fillets
- Fine lard for larding
- Onions
- Bards of lard
- A few slices of veal
- Two carrots cut in slices
- Three onions
- A little thyme and bay leaf
- Bouillon
- Buttered round of paper
- Glaze sauce for serving
- Alternatively: four spoonfuls of gelée

## Directions

1. Raise your fillets in the same manner as explained for hare fillets
2. Lard them with fine lard
3. When larded, fold them over to form a round so they hold their shape
4. Put an onion of the necessary size in the middle of each fillet to fill the void
5. Secure it with small skewers
6. Put bards of lard in a casserole with a few slices of veal, two carrots cut in slices, three onions, a little thyme and bay leaf
7. Place your fillets on this seasoning
8. Add a full small spoonful of bouillon
9. Cover them with a round of buttered paper
10. Simmer with fire above and below for three-quarters of an hour
11. At the moment of serving, drain and glaze them
12. Arrange them in crowns and serve a glaze sauce underneath
13. Alternatively, if you lack lard or veal, put four spoonfuls of gelée and cook your fillets with fairly strong fire above and below; let your liquid reduce to glaze and use it to glaze your fillets

title: Filets de Lapereau à la Polignac (Young Rabbit Fillets à la Polignac)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Six rabbit fillets
- Six additional rabbit fillets
- Fine lard for larding
- Truffles cut in half-crescents
- Butter
- Glaze
- Croûtons
- Sautéed truffles in game stock

## Directions

1. Wash, trim, and lard six fillets
2. Cook them as the previous ones
3. Prepare six other fillets that are well-trimmed and slice them at equal distance with the edge of a knife to create an incision
4. Place a half-crescent of truffle in each incision
5. Garnish the entire length of the fillet this way
6. Prepare your six fillets in the same manner and give them the shape of the others
7. The larded ones should be glazed
8. The ones with truffles, sauté them in butter
9. At the moment of service, drain them
10. Arrange them in crowns with a glazed croûton and a round between them
11. Place in the middle a sauté of truffles in game stock

title: Filets de Lapereaux aux Concombres (Young Rabbit Fillets with Cucumbers)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two large cucumbers
- Two tablespoons of vinegar
- Salt
- A piece of butter
- A bouquet of parsley
- Chives
- One clove of garlic
- Two shallots
- One bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Two pinches of flour
- Two glasses of good broth
- Coarse pepper
- Young rabbit fillets

## Directions

1. Cut the cucumbers into thin slices as fine as possible
2. Place them in a pot with two tablespoons of vinegar and salt, and marinate for two hours, turning them from time to time
3. When they have released their water, press them firmly so that no water remains
4. Put them in a pot with a piece of butter, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a clove of garlic, two shallots, a bay leaf, thyme, and basil
5. Pass them over the fire, turning them often until they are slightly colored
6. Add two pinches of flour
7. Moisten with two glasses of good broth
8. Let cook over low heat for a good half hour until the ragout is slightly thickened
9. Remove the bouquet and add young rabbit fillets that have been sliced and cut like the cucumbers
10. Heat without boiling
11. Season with salt and coarse pepper
12. Serve. The fillets should be the leftovers from roasted rabbits that have been removed from the table.

title: Filets de Lapereaux en Salade (Young Rabbit Fillets Salad)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread crumbs
- Butter
- Leftovers of rabbits cooked on the spit
- Two anchovies
- Capers
- Small white onions cooked in the pot (optional)
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut bread crumbs as neatly as possible like large bacon lardons
2. Place them in a pot and pass them over the fire with butter until they are a beautiful golden color
3. Drain them and set aside
4. Take the leftover cooked rabbit and remove all the meat, cutting it into thick fillets
5. Take the serving plate and arrange on it the bread fillets, the rabbit fillets, and two well-washed anchovies cut into very small fillets
6. Add whole capers
7. If you have small white onions cooked in the pot, add them and mix everything together as neatly as possible in a design
8. Season with salt, coarse pepper, oil and vinegar. Ordinarily they are seasoned only at the table.

title: Filets de Lièvre au Civet (Hare Fillets in Civet Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one roasted hare
- butter, the size of half an egg
- onions
- one garlic clove
- one bay leaf
- two cloves
- a good pinch of flour
- one glass of bouillon
- two glasses of red wine
- salt
- pepper
- a little vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a roasted hare that has been served from the table, remove all the meat and cut it into fillets
2. Break the bones a little and put them with the flanks in a saucepan with butter the size of half an egg, some onions in slices, one garlic clove, one bay leaf, two cloves
3. Pass them over the fire and add a good pinch of flour
4. Moisten with one glass of bouillon and two glasses of red wine, salt, pepper
5. Let boil for half an hour and reduce by half
6. Pass the sauce through a sieve
7. Put the hare fillets in with a little vinegar
8. Heat without boiling

title: Filets de Lièvre au Civet (Hare Fillets in Civet)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted hare
- Hare bones
- Butter
- Onions
- Clove of garlic
- Bay leaf
- Two cloves
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Red wine
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Take a roasted hare that has been removed from the table, remove all the meat and cut it into fillets, lightly crush the bones and place them with the flanks in a saucepan with butter about the size of half an egg, some sliced onions, a clove of garlic, a bay leaf, and two cloves.
2. Pass them over the fire and add a good pinch of flour, moisten with a glass of bouillon and two glasses of red wine, salt, and pepper.
3. Boil for half an hour.

title: Filets de Lièvre Marinés Sautés (Marinated Sautéed Hare Fillets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare fillets
- Salt
- Pepper
- Laurel leaves
- Thyme
- Parsley in sprigs
- Chives whole
- One large glass of vinegar
- Butter
- Flour
- Herbs from the marinade
- Bouillon
- Vinegar from the marinade

## Directions

1. Trim and lard your fillets as in the previous recipe.
2. Place them in a terrine with salt, pepper, two laurel leaves, thyme, parsley sprigs, whole chives, and a large glass of vinegar.
3. Leave them to marinate for up to eight days.
4. When you wish to serve them, remove them from the marinade, trim them, drain them, and shape them as desired.
5. Place them in your sauté pan and coat them with warm butter so they are bathed in it.
6. At the moment of service, place your sauté pan over a high heat so your fillets do not sweat.
7. When they are stiffened on one side, turn them.
8. When you feel your fillets are firm to the touch, remove them, drain them, and arrange them on your plate.
9. Place a poivrade sauce underneath.
10. If you have no sauce, heat your butter until what is in the sauté pan begins to stick.
11. Remove three quarters of the butter.
12. Add a small spoonful of flour, which you stir with your butter.
13. Add some of the herbs from your marinade, a small pot-spoonful of bouillon, half of the vinegar that marinated your fillets, and enough pepper to dominate.
14. Reduce your sauce by half or more if it is too thin.
15. Strain it through a sieve into another pan.
16. Check the seasoning for salt and place it under your fillets.

title: Filets de Perdreaux aux Bigarades (Partridge Fillets with Bitter Orange)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight partridges
- Four spoonfuls of Spanish sauce
- Coarse pepper
- Bitter orange juice and zest
- Bouillon
- Partridge kidneys
- Light roux
- White wine
- Bay leaf

## Directions

1. Put eight partridges on the spit three-quarters of an hour before serving
2. When they are cooked, still slightly undercooked (in their own juice), remove them from the spit
3. Correctly remove the fillets
4. On the platter, arrange a glazed crouton of approximately the same shape as your fillets, but flat
5. In a casserole, put four spoonfuls of Spanish sauce, a little coarse pepper, the juice of a bitter orange with a little of the zest
6. Make your sauce bubble with a little bouillon and pour it under your fillets
7. If you do not have sauce, make a light roux
8. Pound some kidneys from your partridges and put them in your roux
9. Moisten with bouillon, half a glass of white wine, and a bay leaf
10. Reduce by half
11. Skim it and strain through a fine sieve
12. Squeeze your bitter orange into it as explained

title: Filets de Poularde au suprême (Chicken Filets with Supreme Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- five hens
- salt
- pepper
- finely chopped parsley
- four skimming spoons of béchamel
- one spoon of consommé
- very good butter the size of half an egg
- croûton

## Directions

1. Remove the filets from five hens and pare them
2. Remove the nerve skin, place this side on the table, and slip your knife blade between this skin and meat without cutting too much into the flesh
3. Arrange them on your sauté pan
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Dust with finely chopped parsley that has been washed so your whites do not take on harshness or a greenish color
6. At the moment of service, put them over high heat
7. Let them stiffen on one side, then turn them to the other
8. Do not leave them on the heat long
9. Tilt your sauté pan and put your filets on the edge so the butter separates from them
10. Add four skimming spoons of béchamel and one of consommé
11. When it has released three or four boils, add parsley chopped the size of half an egg of very good butter
12. Stir it into your sauce, dip your filets in it and arrange them in crowns with a glazed croûton between on your dish

title: Filets de Soles en Aspic (Sole Fillets in Aspic)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sole fillets
- Fish aspic or gelatin

## Directions

1. Prepare your sole fillets as those in Mayonnaise; if you do not have fish gelatin, use aspic
2. Ensure that your pieces are equal in size
3. Pour melted aspic into your mold and let it congeal
4. Decorate your gelatin before placing your fillets in it
5. Arrange your pieces regularly
6. Fill your mold with melted but cold aspic and place it on ice or in a cold place for it to set
7. At the moment of service, turn your mold over onto the plate, using the same procedure to detach it as for aspic of white poultry meat

title: Filets de Soles en mayonnaise (Sole Fillets in Mayonnaise)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight or ten soles
- Mayonnaise sauce
- Aspic
- Croutons
- Gherkins
- Anchovies

## Directions

1. Fry eight or ten soles
2. When they are cold, put them on the grill over a very low fire without heating them completely, only to make it easy to lift the skin
3. Detach the fillets from your soles
4. As soon as they are lukewarm, remove the skins
5. Then trim and cut your fillets into long squares of two inches
6. Arrange them correctly in a mold or on your plate
7. If in a mold, dip your sole fillets in your mayonnaise sauce and arrange them until your mold is full of fillets
8. Warm your sauce and fill your mold with it
9. Put it on ice or in the cold so your sauce congeals
10. An hour before service, pour it on the plate and decorate with croutons, gherkins, anchovies, etc.
11. If you arrange your pieces on your plate, also pour your sauce over it and decorate it

title: Filets de Soles en salade (Sole Fillets in Salad)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sole fillets
- Four tablespoons of good vinegar
- Two spoonfuls of melted aspic
- Ten spoonfuls of oil
- Chopped ravigote
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Lettuce hearts
- Gherkins
- Capers
- Anchovies
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Use the fillets as those for Mayonnaise
2. Leave them whole or cut them into pieces
3. Put them in a saucepan
4. Make a seasoning composed of four tablespoons of good vinegar, two spoonfuls of melted aspic, ten spoonfuls of oil, chopped ravigote, salt, and coarse pepper
5. Mix everything well and pour it over your fillets that you will toss in it
6. Arrange the pieces correctly and pour your sauce over
7. Put lettuce hearts cut in quarters around the plate and decorate your salad with gherkins, capers, anchovies, croutons, etc.

title: Filets de Soles sautés (Sautéed Sole Fillets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sole fillets
- Chopped and washed parsley
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Butter
- Italian sauce
- Lemon juice
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Lift the four fillets from your soles along with the skin
2. Trim and arrange them on your sauté pan
3. Sprinkle with a little chopped and washed parsley, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Warm some butter and pour it over your fillets
5. At the moment of serving, put them on the fire
6. As soon as they are stiffened, turn them
7. A few minutes are enough for them to be cooked
8. Arrange them on your plate and add Italian sauce and lemon juice
9. You can leave your fillets whole or cut them into two or three pieces
10. Then arrange them in crowns and put croutons around the edge of the plate

title: Filets de Volaille piqués (Studded Poultry Filets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- three hens
- butter
- jellied stock (three skimming spoons)
- buttered paper
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the filets with the end of the wing from three hens
2. Pare and stud them
3. Secure the wing end under your filet to make it bulge and give it grace
4. Butter your casserole
5. Put in jellied stock the size of three skimming spoons
6. Arrange your filets on top with a round of buttered paper
7. One hour before serving, put them over the fire
8. They must boil neither too fast nor too slowly
9. Place good heat on the lid
10. Watch over your filets
11. After three-quarters of an hour, if your jellied stock was not sufficiently reduced, put your casserole over more ardent heat and let it reduce to glaze
12. At the moment of serving, dip the studded side into your glaze
13. Arrange your filets on your dish and put underneath a worked Spanish sauce to which you will add the glaze from your filets

title: Fines Herbes à Papillotes (Fine Herbs in Papillotes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarteron of lard, grated
- six tablespoons of oil
- one quarteron of butter
- four tablespoons of mushrooms, well chopped
- two tablespoons of shallots, well chopped
- two full tablespoons of parsley, well chopped
- salt
- coarse pepper
- a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Grate one quarteron of lard
2. Put six tablespoons of oil and one quarteron of butter in a saucepan
3. Pour in four tablespoons of well-chopped mushrooms and pass them in your saucepan over the fire
4. When your mushrooms are well browned, add two tablespoons of well-chopped shallots
5. Pass them with your mushrooms, then add two full tablespoons of well-chopped parsley
6. Pass everything together, then add salt, coarse pepper, and a little nutmeg
7. Transfer your fine herbs to a bowl and use them as needed

title: Foie de Cochon en Fromage (Pork Liver Cheese)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of liver
- Two pounds of lard
- Half pound of panne (fat)
- Parsley
- Chives
- Salt
- Pepper
- Aromates
- Four spices
- Pork caul or thin lard slices
- Bacon strips
- Lard slices for covering
- Lard for decoration
- Aspic

## Directions

1. For three pounds of liver, put two pounds of lard and half a pound of panne.
2. Chop everything together.
3. Add chopped parsley and chives, salt, pepper, pounded aromates, and four spices.
4. When everything is well chopped, spread a pork caul or thin lard slices in your casserole or mold so that your liver does not stick to it.
5. Put in three finger-widths of your forcemeat and seasoned bacon strips.
6. Put in another three finger-widths of forcemeat, then bacon strips, and so on until your casserole or mold is full.
7. Cover it with lard slices and put it in the oven.
8. Three hours are sufficient to cook it.
9. Let it cool in your mold before removing it.
10. You can heat it; after it has come out, you can decorate it with lard and aspic.

title: Foie de Veau en Crépine (Calf's Liver in Caul Fat)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two large onions
- butter
- one calf's liver
- three quarterons of beef fat
- dried bread crumb
- half a septier of milk
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- salt
- coarse pepper
- four egg yolks
- four egg whites
- lard slices
- pig's caul
- fresh water
- egg
- paper
- sauce
- half a spoonful of verjuice

## Directions

1. Cut two large onions into small dice and put them in a saucepan with butter, cooking over the fire until done
2. Chop one calf's liver finely with three quarterons of beef fat
3. Put the chopped liver into a mortar with the cooked onion, dried bread crumb that has been dried over the fire with half a septier of milk, chopped parsley, chives, and mushrooms, salt, coarse pepper, four egg yolks, and four beaten egg whites
4. Pound everything together
5. Take a medium saucepan and place lard slices on the bottom
6. Place a pig's caul on top that you have soaked for half an hour in fresh water and well pressed with your hands to remove the water
7. Put all the forcemeat into the caul and seal all the edges with beaten egg to hold them together
8. Cover the saucepan with a sheet of paper and a lid
9. Cook over low heat between two beds of hot ashes for half an hour
10. Drain off the fat and gently pull the caul onto a white cloth so as not to break it, and wipe off the remaining fat
11. Arrange on the serving dish and put a good somewhat thin sauce on top where you have put half a spoonful of verjuice

title: Foie de Veau en Crépine (Calf's Liver in Caul)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two large onions
- butter
- one calf's liver
- three quarterons of beef fat
- bread crumb dried over fire

## Directions

1. Cut two large onions into small dice
2. Put in a casserole with a piece of butter and pass over the fire until cooked
3. Chop a calf's liver with three quarterons of beef fat
4. Put in a mortar with the cooked onion and dried bread crumb

title: Foie de Veau piqué à la broche (Calf's Liver Larded and Roasted on a Spit)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One blonde calf's liver
- Large lardons, well seasoned
- Lard
- Skewers
- One large atelet (meat skewer)
- Sauce piquante

## Directions

1. Take a blonde calf's liver and lard it with large, well-seasoned lardons in the same manner as the previous recipe
2. Ensure the liver is not damaged and lard it with thin lard
3. Pass skewers through the width of the liver
4. Spit the liver and place a large atelet beneath it to secure it
5. Tie both ends of the atelet to the spit, ensuring the liver does not move
6. Leave the liver by the fire for one and a half hours, which is sufficient to cook it
7. At the moment of serving, unspit the liver and place it on the platter with piquant sauce beneath it

title: Foie de Veau piqué à la broche (Calf's Liver Larded and Roasted on the Spit)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's liver, well-colored
- Thick lardons, well-seasoned
- Fine lard
- Brochettes
- Atelet

## Directions

1. Have a well-colored liver; lard it with thick lardons, well-seasoned, as for the previous recipe.
2. Be careful that your liver is not damaged.
3. Lard it with fine lard.
4. Pass brochettes through the width of the liver.
5. Spit it, and put a large atelet under your liver to secure it.
6. Tie the two ends of your atelet to the spit.

title: Foie de Veau à l'Italienne (Calf's Liver Italian Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf's liver
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- half a clove of garlic
- two shallots
- half a bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- salt
- coarse pepper
- fine oil
- butter
- flour
- half a spoonful of verjuice or vinegar
- juice

## Directions

1. Cut a calf's liver into very thin fillets
2. Chop finely parsley, chives, mushrooms, half a clove of garlic, two shallots, half a bay leaf, thyme, and basil chopped like powder
3. Take a medium saucepan and put a layer of calf's liver fillets on the bottom
4. Season on top with salt, coarse pepper, fine oil, and a little of all your fine herbs
5. Continue in this manner until you have used all the liver, seasoning each layer as you did the first
6. Cook over low heat for one hour
7. Remove the liver from the saucepan with a skimmer
8. Degrease the sauce and put in a very small piece of beurre manié made with flour and half a spoonful of verjuice or a drop of vinegar
9. Thicken the sauce over the fire turning it with a spoon; if it was too thin, add a little juice
10. Put the liver in the sauce to heat it and arrange on the serving dish

title: Foie de Veau à l'Étouffée (Calf's Liver Stewed)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one blonde calf's liver
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- shallot
- a hint of garlic
- a pinch of flour
- half a septier of wine
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Take a blonde liver, remove the nerves, and cut it into slices the thickness of a finger
2. Melt butter in a pan and cook the liver pieces seasoned with salt and pepper
3. When cooked on one side, turn them to cook on the other
4. Remove from the pan
5. In the same butter, cook parsley, chives, shallot, a hint of garlic, all chopped together
6. Add a pinch of flour and moisten with half a septier of wine
7. Bring the sauce to a boil briefly
8. Serve with a hint of vinegar

title: Foie de Veau à la Bourgeoise (Calf's Liver Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf's liver
- shallots
- butter
- a small pinch of flour
- a glass of water
- a glass of white wine
- salt
- coarse pepper
- three egg yolks
- two spoonfuls of verjuice

## Directions

1. Cut the liver into slices
2. Put in a casserole with shallots and a piece of butter
3. Pass over the fire and add a small pinch of flour
4. Moisten with a glass of water and as much white wine
5. Add salt and coarse pepper
6. Let boil for half an hour
7. Dilute three egg yolks with two spoonfuls of verjuice
8. When the liver is cooked and little sauce remains, add the liaison
9. Thicken without boiling and serve

title: Foie de Veau à la poêle (Calf's Liver in a Skillet)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One calf's liver
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- A good piece of butter
- Four tablespoons of flour
- Half a bottle of white wine
- Broth or water if needed

## Directions

1. Cut and season your liver as in the previous recipe
2. Put a good piece of butter in the skillet; when it is melted, add your liver
3. The fire must be high heat
4. Stir your liver in the skillet
5. When the pieces are firm to the touch, add four tablespoons of flour which you mix with your liver
6. Pour in half a bottle of white wine
7. Stir your liver well so that your sauce binds
8. If it is too thick, add a little broth or water
9. Do not let your liver boil; serve it as soon as it begins to boil
10. Taste to ensure it is properly salted before serving

title: Foie de Veau étouffé (Braised Calf's Liver)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's liver (the blondest are best)
- Lard strips
- Four spices
- Parsley and chives, chopped
- Pepper
- Salt
- Carrots
- Onions
- Cloves
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- White wine
- Poivrade sauce

## Directions

1. Take a calf's liver and lard it with thick lardons.
2. Season it with four spices, chopped parsley and chives, pepper, salt.
3. When your liver is larded, put lard strips in a braising pan or casserole.
4. Put your liver with four carrots, four onions (one of which is studded with three cloves), three bay leaves, a little thyme, a bouquet of parsley and chives, salt.
5. Moisten your liver with a bottle of white wine.
6. Cover it with lard strips and a round of paper.
7. Put it on a stove.
8. When it comes to a boil, place it on another braising pan with fire on the cover.
9. Let it simmer for two hours and you can serve it.
10. Add about four spoonfuls to degrease of poivrade sauce; strain the cooking liquid from your liver and add four spoonfuls of it.
11. Reduce your sauce by half and pour it under your liver.
12. If you do not have poivrade sauce, make a small roux; pass through a silk sieve the cooking liquid in which your liver cooked; moisten your roux with it.
13. Your sauce must be thin so you can reduce it by half.
14. Degrease it and place it under your liver.

title: Fraise de Veau (Calf's Mesentery)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's mesentery
- water
- two carrots
- three onions
- two cloves
- two bay leaves
- thyme
- bouquet of parsley and chives
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Soak the mesentery well in cold water
2. Put it in a pot of boiling water and boil for about a quarter hour
3. Put it in cold water until completely cooled
4. Tie it up and cook it in a blanc, or alternatively cook it with water, two carrots, three onions, two cloves, two bay leaves, a little thyme, a bouquet of parsley and chives, plus a glass of vinegar, and salt for two hours
5. For the sauce, boil vinegar with salt and fine pepper, making sure the seasoning is strong since the mesentery is very bland by itself
6. Serve with the vinegar sauce

title: Fraise de Veau au Gratin de Fromage (Calf Sweetbread with Cheese Gratin)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf sweetbread
- water
- five or six onions
- butter
- flour
- one glass of bouillon
- one spoonful of vinegar
- Gruyère cheese
- two egg yolks
- bread
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cook a calf sweetbread with water
2. When cooked and drained, degrease it slightly
3. Put in a saucepan five or six onions cut into dice with butter and cook them over the fire until done and beginning to color
4. Add a pinch of flour
5. Moisten with a glass of bouillon and one spoonful of vinegar
6. Put the sweetbread in the sauce and let it simmer until the sauce is reduced and of good consistency
7. Add before serving a piece of butter and two egg yolks mixed with grated Gruyère cheese
8. Put this in the bottom of the serving dish and serve the sweetbread on top; fry the fillets of the bread cut in long pieces, layer them around the gratin with butter
9. Make another sauce: take a saucepan and put butter in it as thick as half an egg with a pinch of flour
10. Add a little bouillon and one spoonful of vinegar
11. Thicken this sauce over the fire so it is somewhat thick and put it on the sweetbread
12. Put everything on top, half bread and half grated Gruyère, and brown it underneath a pastry lid cover; it must be of such a color that no sauce remains

title: Fraise de Veau de Différentes Façons - Fried Version (Calf Sweetbread Various Ways - Fried)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf sweetbread
- water
- white flour sauce
- flour
- oil
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cook and prepare the calf sweetbread as previously explained in white flour sauce
2. Degrease it and cut it into small bouquets
3. Dip it in a batter and fry it
4. Serve garnished with fried parsley
5. Make the batter by putting two handfuls of flour in a saucepan with one spoonful of oil and fine salt; thin your batter until it flows from the spoon without being too thin

title: Frangipane (Almond Cream)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Five spoonfuls of flour
- Five eggs
- One pint of milk
- Butter the size of an egg
- A little salt
- Almonds
- One bitter almond per six sweet almonds
- Macarons
- A little candied orange flower
- Sugar, grated in great quantity

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan five spoonfuls of flour, which you will mix with five eggs
2. Pour in one pint of milk, butter the size of an egg, and a little salt
3. Put your mixture over the fire
4. Stir the frangipane constantly without leaving it until it has boiled for ten minutes
5. Be careful it does not brown
6. When it is cooked, let it cool in a bowl
7. Then crush some almonds; for every six, use one bitter almond; crush some macarons until they are a fine powder
8. Add a little candied orange flower and powdered sugar in large enough quantity that it is a good sugar
9. Mix all with your mixture
10. When the frangipane is well worked with a wooden spoon, if it is too thick, add one or two eggs with your puff pastry
11. Make tarts and small cakes of all sorts and in all fashions
12. If you want your cream to be pistacio flavored, when it is cold, put pistachios instead of almonds
13. As this would not give enough color, add a little spinach green; do not use macarons or orange flower, but rather three bitter almonds and sugar

title: Fresh Salmon, How to Prepare It (Fresh Salmon)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh salmon
- oil or good butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut it into slices
2. Marinate it with a little oil or good butter, salt, and pepper
3. Grill it while basting with its marinade
4. Serve with sauces or ragouts as explained for turbot

title: Fresh Sardines or Herrings (Sardine et Hareng Frais)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh sardines or herrings
- Butter
- Flour
- Vinegar
- Fine mustard
- Salt
- Pepper
- Water

## Directions

1. Scale and wash well, dry with a cloth
2. Cook on the grill
3. When cooked, prepare sauce with butter, a little flour, a dash of vinegar, a spoonful of fine mustard, salt, pepper, and a little water
4. Thicken the sauce on the fire and serve over the sardines or herrings

title: Fressure de Veau à la Bourgeoise (Calf's Offal Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's fressure (lung, heart, and spleen)
- good butter
- a bouquet garni
- a pinch of flour
- broth
- three egg yolks
- a little milk
- verjuice

## Directions

1. Cut the fressure into pieces
2. Soak in cold water to disgorge, then blanch briefly in boiling water
3. Put in a casserole with a piece of good butter and a bouquet garni
4. Pass over the fire and add a pinch of flour
5. Moisten with broth
6. When the ragout is cooked and well seasoned, add a liaison of three egg yolks diluted with a little milk
7. Thicken over the fire
8. Before serving, add a hint of verjuice

title: Fricandeau de Veau à la Bourgeoise (Veal Fricandeau in the Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a thick slice of veal rouelle, two fingers thick
- small lard for larding
- bouillon
- bouquet garni
- coulis
- salt

## Directions

1. Take a slice of veal rouelle two fingers thick, which you pierce on top with small lard.
2. Blanch it for a moment in boiling water, and then cook it with bouillon and a bouquet garni.
3. When it is cooked, remove it to deglaze the sauce well, pass this sauce through a tamis into another casserole.
4. Reduce it on the fire until there is almost none left.
5. Put your fricandeau in to glaze it.
6. When it is well glazed on the side of the lard, arrange it on the plate you must serve.
7. Detach what is in the casserole on the fire by putting in a little coulis and very little bouillon, taste if this sauce is of good flavor, and serve it under the fricandeau.

title: Fricassée de Potiron (Pumpkin Fricassee)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pumpkin
- water
- butter
- parsley
- chives
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Cook the pumpkin in water
2. Put it in a saucepan with a piece of butter, parsley, chives, salt and pepper
3. When it has boiled a quarter hour and there is no sauce remaining, add a liaison of egg yolks with cream or milk

title: Fricassée de Poulet (Chicken Fricassee)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One good fat, well-fleshed chicken
- About a quarter pound of butter
- Flour (one tablespoon)
- Bouillon or water (one pot spoon)
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- One bay leaf
- A bouquet of parsley and chives
- Mushrooms
- Small onions
- Three egg yolks (for the liaison)
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Flame the chicken and disjoint it as correctly as possible, lifting the members without hacking or skinning them so they retain a beautiful form
2. Cut the end of the thigh at the side of the leg; break and remove the bone to the joint so your thigh has a beautiful form
3. Warm about a quarter pound of butter in a saucepan
4. Arrange the thighs, then the wings, breast, kidneys and the rest on top
5. Put your fricassee on the fire and stiffen the chicken members at the bottom of your saucepan
6. After they have remained an instant on the fire, sauté them
7. When they are all stiffened, pour a spoon of flour that you will mix with your chicken and butter
8. Add a pot spoon of bouillon; if you have none, moisten it with water
9. Add salt, coarse pepper, a bay leaf, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and mushrooms
10. Bring it to a boil, stirring constantly
11. Skim it and let it go to a high fire
12. One hour is sufficient to cook your fricassee, unless your chicken is tough
13. When it is three-quarters cooked, degrease it
14. Add small onions, all equal and well cleaned, as you judge appropriate
15. Once your fricassee is cooked, remove the bouquet and bay leaf
16. Add a liaison of three egg yolks; take care that it does not boil
17. Add lemon juice if you wish

title: Fricassée de Poulet à la Minute (Quick Chicken Fricassee)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bay leaf
- Mushrooms
- Flour
- Bouillon or water
- Liaison (optional)
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Flame your chicken
2. Put a piece of butter in a saucepan
3. Place your chicken members with salt, pepper, a bay leaf, and your mushrooms
4. Put your saucepan on a high fire
5. Sauté your chicken
6. As soon as your members are well hit by the heat, add a little flour which you mix with your chicken
7. Add a glass of bouillon or water; be careful with the salt
8. If you put in bouillon, stir it on the fire
9. At the first boil, remove your fricassee
10. You may add a liaison if you wish, and lemon juice

title: Fricassée de Poulets (Chicken Fricassee)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two common chickens in good meat
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives (ciboule)
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Two cloves
- Mushrooms
- Ham (optional)
- Flour
- Warm water
- Salt
- Black pepper
- Three egg yolks
- Cream or milk
- Lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Singe and pluck the chickens, then gut them; cut them into pieces and soak them in lukewarm water to disgorge them; also soak the livers after removing the bitter part, the gizzards which you split to remove the insides, the feet which you place on coals to remove the skin, you must cut the spurs, the necks of which you cut half the head
2. Drain the chickens well on a sieve or strainer
3. Put them in a casserole with a piece of good butter, bouquet of parsley, chives, a bay leaf, a little thyme, basil, two cloves, mushrooms, and a slice of ham if you have it; cook over good heat until there is almost no sauce left
4. Add a good pinch of flour and moisten with a little hot water, season with salt and black pepper, cook and reduce to little sauce
5. When ready to serve, add a liaison of three egg yolks diluted with cream or milk, thicken over heat without boiling so the sauce does not break, add lemon juice or a dash of vinegar
6. Arrange the fricassee with the giblets at the bottom, the thighs and wings on top, pour the sauce and mushrooms all over

title: Fricassée de Poulets à la Bourdelais (Chicken Fricassee Bordeaux Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken fricassee as in the previous recipe
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Make the fricassee in the same way as the previous one, with this difference: when it is arranged on its plate, bread it with breadcrumbs; put on the breadcrumbs small pieces of butter the size of a pea, brown it under a pie dish cover or in an oven; serve hot. This method is good for masking a fricassee that has been removed from the table.

title: Fricassée de Poulets à la Chevalière (Chicken Fricassee in the Style of the Chevalier)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two fat, well-fleshed chickens
- Butter
- Flour
- Hot bouillon
- Velouté (four degreasings spoons)
- Bay leaf
- Bouquet of parsley and chives
- Coarse pepper
- Small onions
- Three egg yolks (for the liaison)
- Lard

## Directions

1. Flame the chickens lightly
2. Lift the breasts entirely, lard them finely, and cook them separately in a saucepan to glaze them
3. Lift the other members as correctly as possible
4. Break the thigh bones to the joint and cut the large end at the side of the leg
5. Trim well the kidneys and wings; in a word, cut your pieces neatly
6. Put a piece of softened butter in a saucepan
7. Arrange the members so they take a beautiful form
8. Place your saucepan on the fire
9. Leave your fricassee an instant without sautéing so your thighs take a form they can maintain while cooking
10. Sauté them until they are stiffened
11. Add a little flour which you mix with your chickens and butter
12. Moisten your fricassee with hot bouillon
13. Add four degreasings spoons of velouté, a bay leaf, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and coarse pepper
14. Let it go to a high fire
15. Three-quarters cooked, add your small onions, all equal and well cleaned
16. Degrease your fricassee
17. When it is cooked, at the moment of serving, remove the bouquet and bay leaf
18. Change it to another saucepan
19. Pass the sauce through cheesecloth over it
20. Add a liaison of three egg yolks
21. Dress it on your platter between your four thighs
22. Put the four larded glazed fillets and the garnish in the middle

title: Fried Anchovies (Anchois Frits)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh anchovies
- Flour
- Oil
- Egg
- White wine

## Directions

1. Desalt the anchovies
2. Prepare a paste with flour, a spoonful of oil, and dilute with white wine, making sure the paste is not too liquid
3. Dip the anchovies in the paste and fry
4. Serve with a nice color as an entremets

title: Fried Calf's Brains (Cervelles de Veau frites)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's brains
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Frying batter
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the brains as directed for braised
2. After cooking, cut into six pieces
3. Place in a bowl with fine salt, a little pepper, enough vinegar for the pieces to take the flavor
4. At moment of serving, drain and put in frying batter that is not too hot
5. After frying, drain on white cloth
6. Arrange the pieces on the plate
7. Arrange a bouquet of fried parsley in a pyramid

title: Fried Eggs in Butter (Oeufs Frits en Gras)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Lard or melted butter

## Directions

1. Heat lard or melted butter in a pan until very hot.
2. Add eggs one at a time and fry them.
3. Turn them in the pan to keep them round.
4. Do not allow the yolk to harden.
5. Serve.

title: Fromage de Cochon (Pork Head Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One well-cleaned pig head, boned
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Basil
- Six cloves
- Two pinches of coriander
- Half a nutmeg
- Two garlic cloves
- Four shallots
- Half a handful of parsley
- Vinegar
- Ham slices
- Bouillon
- One pint white wine
- Onion
- Roots

## Directions

1. Take a well-cleaned pig head and bone it completely
2. Remove all the meat and lard without cutting the skin; cut the meat into very thin fillets
3. Do the same with the lard
4. Place lean meat on one plate, fat on another
5. Cut the ears into fillets as well
6. Season all meat on both sides with salt, coarse pepper, thyme, bay leaf, basil, six cloves, two pinches of coriander, and half a nutmeg, all finely chopped
7. Add two minced garlic cloves and four minced shallots
8. Add half a handful of parsley leaves left whole
9. Place the pig head skin in a round pot
10. Arrange all your meat fillets, adding a bit of vinegar and some ham slices if available, with neatly arranged parsley leaves
11. Continue this layering until complete
12. Sew the skin closed, pleating it like a purse
13. Wrap in a white cloth and tie tightly with string
14. Place in a pot sized exactly to fit, and cook for six to seven hours with bouillon, one pint white wine, onion, roots, thyme, bay leaf, basil, one garlic clove, salt, and pepper
15. Once cooked, drain and place in a vessel exactly its size and well-rounded
16. Cover with a lid and place a very heavy weight on top until cold, to give it the shape desired
