17. Serve as a large entremets

title: Fromage Fouetté (Whipped Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a little finely chopped green lemon rind
- three half-setiers of good, thick cream
- the size of a pea of gum tragacanth in powder

## Directions

1. Chop very finely a little green lemon rind and put it in a bowl with three half-setiers of good, thick cream and gum tragacanth the size of a pea in powder
2. Whip the cream, and as the foam becomes thick, remove it with a skimmer and put it in a wicker basket
3. If the basket is not tightly woven, put a piece of muslin or other clear linen in the bottom
4. When all the cream is whipped, let the cheese drain until serving
5. Turn it out into a compotier

title: Fromage Fouetté (Whipped Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A little peel of green citron, finely chopped
- Three half-setiers of good thick cream
- About the size of a pea of gum arabic powder

## Directions

1. Chop the green citron peel very finely and place it in a terrine with three half-setiers of good thick cream and gum arabic powder about the size of a pea
2. Whip your cream, and as the foam becomes thick, remove it with a skimming spoon to place it in a wicker basket
3. If the basket is not tightly woven, place a piece of muslin or other fine cloth in the bottom
4. When all the cream is whipped, let the cheese drain until you are ready to serve
5. Turn it out into a serving dish and sprinkle fine sugar on top

title: Fromage Naturel à la Crème (Natural Cheese with Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one chopine of good milk
- a grain of good rennet the size of a pea

## Directions

1. Heat the milk slightly on the fire
2. While stirring, add a grain of good rennet the size of a pea that you will have diluted with the same milk
3. Set the curd on some ashes by covering it and putting hot ashes on the lid
4. When it is set, put the curd in a small wicker basket made for these small cheeses
5. When well drained, press it in the compotier and serve it with good cream and fine sugar on top

title: Fromage à la Glace à la Bourgeoise (Homestyle Frozen Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One chopine of double cream, or other good cream
- One half-setier of milk
- One egg yolk
- Three quarterons of sugar
- Essences such as orange flowers, bergamot, sweet lime or citron

## Directions

1. Take one chopine of double cream or other good cream, one half-setier of milk, one egg yolk, and three quarterons of sugar
2. Bring to five or six boils and remove from the fire
3. Add several essences such as orange flowers, bergamot, sweet lime or citron
4. Place the mixture in your tin mold and set it to freeze on ice
5. Place your mold in a small bucket proportioned to the mold, after having put well-pounded ice in the bottom of the bucket, a handful of salt or saltpeter
6. Continue to place ice and saltpeter around the mold up to the top
7. When your cheese is frozen and you are ready to serve, have hot water in a cauldron, dip the mold of the cheese in it to make it detach, and dress it in the dish; it must be eaten at once

title: Fromage à la Montmorency (Montmorency Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One chopine of cream
- Two ounces of sugar
- One half coffee spoon of orange flower water

## Directions

1. Place on the fire one chopine of cream with two ounces of sugar
2. When it boils, remove it to cool
3. Then add one half coffee spoon of orange flower water
4. Whip the cream with a wooden whisk, and as the foam becomes somewhat thick, remove it with a skimming spoon and place it in a basket lined with fine cloth
5. Continue whipping until you have no more cream
6. Let the cheese drain and serve in a serving dish or on a plate

title: Fromage à la Princesse (Princess Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One chopine of cream
- One pinte of milk
- Two grains of salt
- Peel of one green citron, grated
- One pinch of coriander
- One small piece of cinnamon
- Three ounces of sugar
- A little more than a pea-sized amount of rennet, diluted with one spoonful of water

## Directions

1. Place on the fire a chopine of cream with one pinte of milk, two grains of salt, grated green citron peel, a pinch of coriander, one small piece of cinnamon, and three ounces of sugar
2. Bring to a boil together and reduce by half
3. Remove from the fire; when it becomes lukewarm, add a little more than a pea-sized amount of rennet diluted with one spoonful of water
4. Strain the cream through a sieve and return it to a warm place near the fire
5. When it has curdled, place it in a small wicker basket to drain and take the shape of a cheese
6. Then turn it out into a serving dish or onto a plate

title: Fruits Secs sans Être Confits (Dried Fruits without Candying)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe cherries
- Ripe plums
- Peaches
- Apricots
- Pears

## Directions

1. For cherries: Choose well-ripened ones that are not turned, arrange them on wicker baskets without piling one on top of another, leave the stems on and put them to dry in an oven of gentle heat like that where bread has just been taken out. Leave them as long as the oven has heat, then remove them to turn them over and put them back in the oven with the same heat until you judge they are dry enough. Let them cool to tie in small bunches and keep in a dry place.
2. For plums: Dry them the same way; pick them very ripe as those that fall by themselves are better because they have more flesh and better flavor.
3. For peaches: Dry as plums, except those picked from the tree are better than those that have fallen. Split them lengthwise and remove the pit. When they are half dry, put them on a clean table and flatten them so they dry evenly, then return them to the oven until dry.
4. For apricots: Make them the same way, except remove the pit without opening them.
5. For pears: Dry them peeled and unpeeled; the first way is better. You take the peels and put them with the pears in a pot full of water, boil them until they begin to soften, being careful when peeling to leave the stems on, and then dry them in the oven the same way as plums.

title: Galantine de Dindon (Turkey Galantine)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- chicken or veal meat
- lard
- salt
- pepper
- four spices
- fine herbs
- aromatic flavoring
- medium lardons
- truffles
- scarlet tongue
- poultry fillets
- foie gras
- lard slices (bardes)
- canvas cloth
- bay leaves
- veal knuckles
- carrots
- onions
- parsley and chives bouquet
- turkey scraps
- thyme
- cloves
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Singe your turkey; make sure it has good meat and is well nourished
2. Begin to bone it through the back; be careful not to damage the breast
3. When it is entirely boned and the sinews of the thighs are removed, lift some of the meat from the breast half an inch from the skin
4. Do the same for the thighs
5. Put with the meat you have cut from the top of your turkey that of two or three chickens, or simply veal or other meat; this is optional
6. If you have two pounds of meat, put two pounds of the fattest lard possible; chop everything together
7. Add salt, pepper, four spices, fine herbs
8. If you wish, when your forcemeat is finely chopped, season medium lardons with pounded aromatics, salt, and pepper, and lard the meat of your turkey
9. Make a layer of forcemeat one inch thick, which you press down well and evenly
10. Put on this layer of forcemeat truffles cut lengthwise, scarlet tongue, lard lardons, the filets of your turkey and poultry, foie gras if you have it
11. Add another layer of forcemeat, and as before, truffles, tongue, lardons, etc., until you have no more forcemeat; perform the same procedure
12. When your forcemeat is used up, roll your turkey so that it contains all the forcemeat without any escaping from any side
13. With a trussing needle and string, sew the meat together as it was in its original form
14. Give your galantine an elongated shape; cover it with lard slices, a little salt
15. Wrap it in canvas cloth into which you put four or five bay leaves
16. Tie your two ends and tie your galantine on top of the canvas so it keeps its shape
17. Put lard slices in a braising pan, your galantine on top
18. Add two veal knuckles, six carrots, six onions, a strong bouquet of parsley and chives, the scraps of your turkey, four bay leaves, a little thyme, three cloves, three pot spoonfuls of bouillon, more if your piece is large
19. Put it on the fire and simmer for three hours

title: Garbure aux Choux (Garbure with Cabbage)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Bread
- Coarse or fine pepper
- Bouillon
- Salt

## Directions

1. Make the garbure the same way as the one with lettuce
2. Some prefer fine pepper instead of coarse pepper, according to taste
3. Be careful with seasoning as cabbage is prone to becoming acrid
4. Do not degrease the cabbage too much
5. Serve a pot of bouillon for people who wish to add it to their plate

title: Garbure aux Laitues (Garbure with Lettuce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Thirty lettuces
- Slices of veal
- Lard
- Bread, thinly sliced
- Two or three carrots
- Three onions
- Two cloves
- Bouillon
- Coarse pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Blanch the lettuces for half an hour, keeping them whole
2. Let them cool, press them, and tie them with string
3. Put slices of veal and lard at the bottom of a saucepan
4. Add the lettuces and cover them with lard along with two or three carrots, three onions, and two cloves
5. Moisten with bouillon and simmer for an hour and a half until cooked
6. Drain and cut lengthwise into slices
7. Make a layer of thinly sliced bread in your dish, then a layer of lettuce, continuing until full
8. Pour the bouillon from the lettuce over it without degreasing, but after passing through a silk sieve
9. Put the dish on the fire to simmer until it has a golden crust
10. Spare the salt due to reduction; add a little coarse pepper to each layer
11. Before serving, degrease your garbure and serve bouillon in a separate vessel

title: Garbure aux Oignons (Onion Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- forty large onions
- half pound of butter or more
- bread cut into very thin slices
- coarse pepper
- broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the onions in half from the top to the other end
2. Cut half of them into four or five parts until they form half a circle
3. Be careful not to include the head or the tail
4. When all your onions are cut this way, take half a pound of butter or more according to the quantity of your onions
5. Fry them in the butter enough so they are well browned
6. Take bread cut into very thin slices
7. Make a layer of bread and a layer of onions
8. Put on each layer a little coarse pepper until your dish is full
9. Drizzle with broth and simmer enough so the gratin forms without letting it burn, which would give it acidity
10. Your soup must be almost dry
11. Put broth in a vessel and serve it so people can add it to their plate
12. Pay attention to salt because of the reduction

title: Garbure à la Polignac (Garbure Polignac Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty chestnuts from Limoges or Auvergne
- Slices of veal
- Lard
- Two bay leaves
- Three cloves
- Six carrots
- Six onions
- A bouquet of fresh celery leaves
- Coarse pepper
- Bouillon
- Bread
- Salt

## Directions

1. Remove the first shell of the chestnuts
2. Put them in water and heat until the water boils; remove and check if the skin lifts
3. Peel them completely so no inner skin remains
4. Put slices of veal and lard at the bottom of a saucepan
5. Add two bay leaves, three cloves, six carrots, six onions, and a bouquet of fresh celery leaves
6. Add the chestnuts seasoned with coarse pepper
7. Cover the chestnuts with lard slices
8. Moisten with bouillon and simmer for three-quarters of an hour to an hour until the chestnuts are cooked
9. Drain and cut in half
10. Make a layer of bread, then a layer of chestnuts in your dish until full
11. Form several lines of chestnuts on your garbure
12. Strain the bouillon in which the chestnuts cooked
13. Pour the bouillon over your garbure and let it boil until it forms a crust
14. Serve a pot of bouillon
15. Alternatively, cook the chestnuts only in bouillon with carrots, onions, cloves, bay leaves, and coarse pepper

title: Garbure à la Vigeroy (Garbure Vigeroy Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Twenty carrots
- Twenty turnips
- Twelve onions
- Six celery hearts
- Twelve leeks
- Six lettuces
- A handful of sorrel
- A pinch of chervil, more or less according to strength desired
- Three-quarters pound of butter
- Bouillon
- A piece of sugar the size of half an egg
- Bread
- Coarse pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut your root vegetables into medium-sized dice and chop the herbs
2. First fry the carrots in three-quarters pound of butter
3. When they are lightly fried, add the turnips and let them fry with the carrots
4. Then add the leeks and onions
5. When everything is golden, add the sorrel and stir with all the vegetables
6. When the sorrel is well melted, moisten with bouillon, not too much as it would be unnecessary
7. Let the vegetables boil for an hour until cooked
8. Add a piece of sugar the size of half an egg
9. Make a layer of bread, then a layer of vegetables, continuing until the dish is full
10. Add a little coarse pepper to each layer
11. Moisten with the bouillon from the vegetables without degreasing
12. Simmer until it forms a golden crust
13. Spare the salt due to reduction
14. This garbure can be made lean by moistening the vegetables with water seasoned with salt and pepper

title: Gaufres (Waffles)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three very fresh eggs
- Flour
- Chopped green citron
- Orange flower water
- Fine sugar
- A little more than one half-setier of cream
- Butter or candle wax for the waffle iron

## Directions

1. Take three of the freshest eggs you can find and dilute them with as much flour as they can absorb, chopped green citron, orange flower water, and fine sugar
2. Dilute all together and then add a little more than one half-setier of cream, until the batter is somewhat liquid
3. When your batter is ready, heat the waffle iron over a fire and rub it inside with candle wax to prevent the waffles from sticking
4. When your waffle iron is hot, put a full spoonful of this batter in it, that is enough
5. Close your waffle iron and return it to the fire
6. When it is cooked on one side, turn it over to the other side and remove it then to place it on a wooden roller
7. Bend them all while hot, and when they are all made, put them in a dry place until you serve them
8. You can keep them for several days if you keep them in a drying room where they will not soften

title: Gelée d'Oranges (Orange Jelly)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 oranges
- 3 lemons
- half pound of sugar
- 3 sticks of isinglass
- water

## Directions

1. Take 10 oranges and 3 lemons
2. Zest 3 oranges very lightly, removing the peel in small portions with a knife
3. Place the zest in a casserole
4. Press the 10 oranges and 3 lemons over the zests
5. Clarify half a pound of sugar
6. Place the orange juice and zests with the clarified sugar brought almost to hard crack stage
7. Heat with the sugar and juice of oranges
8. Pass through fine cloth
9. Add a decoction of 3 sticks of isinglass made by beating well and placing in small pieces in a casserole with 3 spoons of water, simmering for two hours, then passing through cheesecloth without straining or pressing
10. Mix the isinglass with the warm preparation
11. Amalgamate well the isinglass and juice
12. Pour the jelly into small pots and place on ice or in a cool place
13. When the jelly is completely set, remove from ice
14. 7 small pots suffice for an entremets

title: Gelée d'Oranges Renversée (Molded Orange Jelly)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Orange jelly (prepared as for small pots, in larger quantity)
- Fish glue (more than for blancmange)

## Directions

1. Prepare your orange jelly as you would for small pots, but in larger quantity
2. Use more glue than for the blancmange
3. Place it on ice so it freezes well
4. At the moment of service, unmold onto the plate
5. With a well-heated cloth, rub it, and the jelly will detach
6. If there is any liquid, draw it up with a straw

title: Gelées pour les Malades (Jellies for the Sick)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken, flamed, emptied, and plucked
- a veal knuckle of about one and a half pounds
- two pints of water
- a slice of green lemon, skin removed
- or white vinegar
- a quarter pound of sugar
- two or three grains of salt
- two pinches of coriander
- a very small piece of cinnamon
- three eggs with whites and yolks and shells

## Directions

1. Put in a pot a chicken that you have flamed, emptied, and plucked, a veal knuckle of about one and a half pounds, and two pints of water.
2. Bring them to boil and skim well, then simmer on very low heat for three hours.
3. Degrease your broth well and pass it through a fine tamis, put it in a casserole on a good stove with a slice of green lemon, skin removed.
4. If you have no lemon, put in a few drops of white vinegar, a quarter pound of sugar, two or three grains of salt, two pinches of coriander, a very small piece of cinnamon.
5. Boil for a quarter hour, and put in three eggs with whites and yolks and shells, boil gently while stirring often until your jelly is clear and reduced to about three half-setiers.
6. Pass it through a white cloth that is moistened, press it well so that it does not smell of lye and no water remains.
7. Put your jelly in the vessels where it must stay, let it set in a cool place or on ice.

title: Gigot de Mouton aux Légumes Glacés (Leg of Mutton with Glazed Vegetables)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Mortified leg of mutton
- Good bouillon
- Half a head of cabbage
- A dozen root vegetables
- Six large onions
- Three celery stalks
- Six turnips

## Directions

1. Trim the leg of mutton of its fat and the end of the shank; truss it and place in a pot with good bouillon
2. Turn the half head of cabbage and the dozen root vegetables cut in rounds; add six large onions and three celery stalks
3. Blanch everything together for half a quarter hour
4. Remove and refresh in fresh water, press out any water, and truss the cabbage and celery
5. Cook all these vegetables with the leg of mutton
6. When everything is cooked, remove the leg of mutton and vegetables to a platter and wipe away any grease with a white cloth
7. Arrange the leg of mutton on the serving platter with vegetables around it
8. Take the bouillon used to cook the leg of mutton, degrease it and pass through a sieve, reduce it to two spoonfuls to make your glaze
9. Lightly glaze the leg of mutton and vegetables
10. Put clear coulis in the casserole where the glaze was reduced to detach what remains, pass this sauce through a sieve to make it clearer, season it well, and serve over the vegetables without touching the glaze

title: Gigot de Mouton de la Périgord (Leg of Mutton Périgord Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Leg of mutton
- Truffles
- Lard
- Salt
- Fine spices
- Parsley
- Chives
- Garlic
- Veal slices
- Lard strips

## Directions

1. Cut truffles into small lardons and cut lard the same way
2. Mix them together with salt, fine spices, parsley, chives, and a hint of garlic, all minced
3. Lard the leg of mutton all over with the truffles and lard
4. Wrap it in paper for two days so that it does not get air
5. Cook it slowly in a casserole in its own juice, wrapped in slices of veal and lard
6. When cooked, degrease the sauce, add a spoonful of coulis, and serve

title: Gigot de Mouton de sept heures (Seven-Hour Leg of Mutton)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one leg of mutton
- lardons (lard strips)
- salt
- coarse pepper
- thyme
- bay leaf
- lard for barding
- ham slices
- mutton bones, crushed
- mutton slices
- four carrots
- six onions
- three bay leaves
- cloves
- parsley and chives bouquet
- one spoonful of broth

## Directions

1. Debone the leg of mutton halfway up the shank
2. Season with lardons, salt, coarse pepper, thyme and bay leaf, all pounded
3. Lard the interior of the leg, ensuring the lardons do not protrude on top
4. Restore the leg to its original shape and tie it with twine so that the deboning is not apparent
5. Place lard strips in the bottom of a braising pot, followed by ham slices, crushed bones, mutton slices, four carrots, six onions, three bay leaves, a little thyme, three cloves, and a bouquet of parsley and chives
6. Add one spoonful of broth and place all this mixture above the leg of mutton
7. Cover the leg with lard and a buttered paper to cover it
8. Place the leg in the coals to cook for seven hours if it is large, at a gentle fire
9. Also place coals on the lid of the braising pot
10. When ready to serve, drain the cooking liquid, untie the leg, glaze it, and serve with the reduced cooking liquid in which it cooked

title: Gigot de Mouton de sept heures (Seven-Hour Leg of Mutton)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- lardons
- salt
- coarse pepper
- thyme
- bay leaf
- lard strips
- ham slices
- mutton bones
- mutton slices
- four carrots
- six onions
- three bay leaves
- thyme
- three cloves
- parsley and chives bouquet

## Directions

1. Have a leg of mutton which you debone halfway down the handle
2. Season it with lardons, salt, coarse pepper, pounded thyme and bay leaf, and lard the inside of your leg: do not let your lardons stick out on top
3. When it is well larded, make it take its original form; tie it so that it does not appear that it has been deboned
4. Put lard strips in the bottom of your braisière, a few slices of ham, crushed bones, a few slices of mutton, four carrots, six onions, three bay leaves, a little thyme, three cloves, a bouquet of parsley and chives

title: Gigot de Mouton à la Persillade (Leg of Mutton with Parsley)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Mortified leg of mutton
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Bouquet garni
- Clear coulis
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Trim and truss the mortified leg of mutton
2. Cook it with bouillon, very little salt, and a bouquet garni
3. When the leg of mutton is cooked, remove it and degrease the bouillon, then reduce it until it becomes a glaze
4. Put the leg of mutton back in the same casserole so it takes all the substance of the meat, stirring to prevent it from sticking
5. When no more sauce remains in the casserole, arrange the leg of mutton on the serving platter
6. Put clear coulis in the casserole to detach what remains
7. Take a good handful of parsley, blanch it for half a quarter hour in boiling water, refresh it in fresh water, press it, and mince it very fine
8. Put the parsley in your sauce and season it well
9. Serve it under the leg of mutton

title: Gigot de Mouton à Peau (Leg of Mutton in Water)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole leg of mutton
- large lardons seasoned with salt, pepper and quatre épices (four spices)
- lard for barding
- four carrots
- six onions, one studded with four cloves
- four bay leaves
- thyme
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Debone the leg of mutton at the rump down to the thighbone
2. Lard the interior of the leg with large lardons seasoned with salt, pepper and quatre épices, ensuring they do not protrude on one side
3. Tie the leg with twine
4. Place lard strips on top
5. Add four carrots, six onions (one studded with four cloves), four bay leaves, and a little thyme
6. Moisten the leg with water and add salt so that it is completely submerged
7. Boil for three hours
8. When ready to serve, untie the leg and serve with a little of the cooking liquid passed through a sieve
9. Taste to ensure it has good seasoning and flavor
10. Optionally serve with turned potatoes cooked in plain water around the leg, or with tomato sauce

title: Gigot Panaché (Variegated Leg of Mutton)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- gherkins
- ham
- lard
- bouillon
- white wine
- ham slice
- parsley
- chives
- cloves
- garlic
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- hard egg yolks
- capers
- anchovies
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Lard throughout with gherkins, ham, and lard, all cut into lardons
2. Tie and place in a pot just fitting its size with half a setier of bouillon, a glass of white wine, a slice of ham, a bouquet of parsley, chives, three cloves, one clove of garlic, thyme, bay leaf, and basil
3. Cook on low heat for three or four hours
4. Pass part of the sauce through a sieve and skim the fat
5. Add three finely chopped hard egg yolks, capers, anchovies, and blanched parsley
6. Add the slice of ham that cooked with the leg of mutton
7. Chop everything very finely
8. Add a small piece of butter kneaded with flour, thicken the sauce over fire and serve over the leg of mutton

title: Gigot à la bourgeoise (Leg of Mutton in the Bourgeois Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one leg of mutton
- twelve carrots
- twelve onions
- two bay leaves
- two cloves
- twelve potatoes
- parsley and chives bouquet
- one spoonful of broth or water
- salt
- one pound of small lard cut into six pieces

## Directions

1. Prepare the leg of mutton as for the leg in water
2. Place it in a braising pot
3. Add twelve carrots, twelve onions, two bay leaves, two cloves, twelve potatoes, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and one spoonful of broth or water
4. Add salt and one pound of small lard cut into six pieces, removing the top and bottom from each piece
5. Braise gently for three and a half hours, turning it from time to time
6. Ensure the fire continues steadily
7. When ready to serve, untie the leg, arrange it on the platter with the vegetables around it
8. If the cooking liquid is too thin, reduce it and place it under the leg
9. Ensure the leg has a good color, or glaze it

title: Gigot à la Mailly (Leg of Mutton à la Mailly)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- lard
- ham
- mushrooms
- gherkins
- salt
- fine spices
- parsley
- chives
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- bouillon
- white wine
- onion
- carrot
- parsnip
- coulis

## Directions

1. Bone the leg of mutton, reserving the handle
2. Make holes throughout the inside without piercing the skin
3. Prepare a salpicon: cut lard, some ham, mushrooms, and gherkins all into dice, season with salt, mixed fine spices, finely chopped parsley and chives, thyme, bay leaf, and basil powder
4. Mix everything together and stuff throughout the leg of mutton
5. Tie it and place in a saucepan with a glass of bouillon and as much white wine, an onion, a carrot, and a parsnip
6. Cook on low heat, well covered
7. When cooked, skim the fat from the sauce and pass through a sieve, reduce over fire if too thin, add a little coulis to bind if needed
8. Serve over the leg of mutton

title: Gigot à la Royale (Leg of Mutton à la Royale)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- veal sweetbread
- mushrooms
- small eggs
- parsley
- chives
- cloves
- garlic
- flour
- stock
- bouillon
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Braise a leg of mutton in the same manner as mutton with cabbage
2. Prepare a ragout: soak veal sweetbread in tepid water, blanch for half a quarter-hour in boiling water, cool in fresh water, and cut into large dice; remove the cartilage
3. Put the sweetbread in a saucepan with mushrooms cut the same way, sauté over fire with a bouquet of parsley, chives, two cloves, and half a clove of garlic
4. Add a pinch of flour, then moisten with half stock and half bouillon in equal parts
5. Cook on very low heat, season with salt and coarse pepper
6. When cooking is almost complete, skim off fat and add small eggs that have been blanched in boiling water beforehand
7. Remove eggs to fresh water to take off the small skin
8. After they have boiled half a quarter-hour in the ragout, add lemon juice or a small splash of vinegar
9. Serve under the leg of mutton
10. Alternative for small eggs: hard boil two eggs, take the yolks and place in a mortar with half a pinch of salt, pound them, and add one raw yolk; mix well, remove to a table dusted with a little flour, roll into the shape of a small sausage, cut into very small equal pieces, roll each piece in slightly floured hands to round them, and place on a plate as you go; put them in boiling water to cook for an instant, cool in fresh water, and drain on a sieve before putting in the ragout

title: Gigot à la Régence (Leg of Mutton à la Régence)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- lard
- salt
- fine spices
- fine herbs

## Directions

1. Cut a leg of mutton across into three or four pieces
2. Lard each piece with lard seasoned with salt, fine spices, and finely chopped fine herbs
3. Cook them in the same manner as beef à la royale (found on page 57)
4. Serve hot as an entrée, or cold as an entremets

title: Gigot à la Sultane (Leg of Mutton à la Sultane)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- veal scallop
- beef fat
- parsley
- chives
- egg yolk
- eau-de-vie
- salt
- pepper
- white wine
- good bouillon
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- garlic
- cloves
- carrot
- parsnip
- hard egg
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Make a farce with roughly the size of an egg of veal scallop and twice as much beef fat, chopped together
2. Add finely chopped parsley and chives, one raw egg yolk, one spoonful of eau-de-vie, salt, and pepper
3. Make holes over the entire top of the leg of mutton and stuff with this farce
4. Cook on the spit wrapped in paper
5. When cooked, serve with a sauce made as follows: put in a saucepan half a setier of white wine, as much good bouillon, parsley, chives, half a bay leaf, thyme, basil, one clove of garlic, two cloves, a carrot, half a parsnip, salt, and coarse pepper
6. Boil for one hour on low heat until the sauce is reduced by half
7. Pass through a sieve, then add a finely chopped hard egg and a pinch of blanched parsley, finely chopped, as much as a nut of butter kneaded with flour
8. Thicken over fire and serve over the leg of mutton

title: Glace of Cooking (Cooking Glaze)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- The liquid from your cooking (the moistening it produces)
- Fresh butter

## Directions

1. Pass the bottom of your cooking, that is to say the moistening it has produced, through a fine cloth or silk sieve
2. Be careful that it is very clear
3. Then reduce it in a casserole on high heat
4. When your moistening becomes thick like a sauce, that is to say when your glaze clings to the spoon, put it in a small casserole
5. Expose it to a bain-marie or over hot ashes so you can use it at the moment of service
6. Add a small piece of fresh butter to correct the salt

title: Glace of Roots (Root Vegetable Glaze)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Vegetables: carrots, turnips, onions (more of the latter two than carrots)
- Four or five cloves (according to quantity)
- Veal (optional)
- Bouillon or water

## Directions

1. Fill three-quarters of a casserole with vegetables such as carrots, turnips, and onions
2. Use four or five cloves according to quantity, using more of the last two vegetables than carrots
3. Add veal if you wish
4. Moisten everything with bouillon or water
5. Cook your vegetables on a low fire
6. Use the same procedure as for the glaze above

title: Glace of Veal (Veal Glaze)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One veal round, cut into four pieces
- Three chickens
- Many whole vegetables
- Consommé
- Scraps or meat debris (alternative)
- Bouillon or water (alternative)
- Fresh butter

## Directions

1. Cut a veal round into four pieces and add three chickens
2. Add many whole vegetables
3. Fill a casserole with consommé and make everything foam
4. Place the casserole on a gentle fire to simmer for three or four hours until the meat is cooked
5. Pass your glaze through a fine cloth so it becomes clear
6. Alternatively, you can make it with meat scraps or debris that you put in a casserole with many vegetables, bouillon or water
7. Make the meat foam and simmer until the meats are cooked
8. When the liquid is passed through cloth, put your glaze in a casserole on a hot stove and reduce it until it becomes thick like a sauce
9. Avoid dark meats such as game, mutton, or beef, because your glaze would become brown
10. Do not add salt to the glaze, as the reduction alone provides the seasoning

title: Grand Bouillon (Grand Bouillon)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One of the five best cuts of beef: rump, sirloin, breast, nut, or undernut
- Water

## Directions

1. Choose one of the five best cuts desossed, well trussed and well tied
2. Put it in a large pot that you fill almost with water
3. If possible, hang it on a crémaillère, taking care to allow a crust of scum to form
4. When it boils, skim it and add cold water to the pot to cause another scum to rise, which you then remove
5. Refresh your pot three or four times

title: Grand Bouillon (Great Broth)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One of five pieces of beef: the rump, the sirloin piece, the brisket, the knuckle, or the under-knuckle, boned, trussed, and tied
- Water
- Salt
- Carrots
- Turnips
- Leeks
- Onions
- Four or five stalks of celery
- Four or five cloves

## Directions

1. Place one of the five beef pieces, boned, well trussed and well tied, in a large pot that you fill almost with water
2. Put it on the fireplace hook, being careful to let a crust of foam form
3. When it boils, skim it; take cold water and put it in the pot to make another foam rise, which you then remove
4. Refresh your pot three or four times
5. Season with salt, take it down from the fireplace hook, make a bed of ash, and place it on top
6. Garnish the pot, according to its size, with carrots, turnips, leeks, onions, four or five stalks of celery, four or five cloves
7. When your piece of beef is cooked, pass your broth through fine cloth or a silk sieve
8. This broth is good only for moistening cooking, consommé, and game essences

title: Grande Espagnole (Large Spanish Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 knuckles of veal
- 1 pheasant or 4 partridges
- half a ham knuckle
- 4 or 5 large carrots
- 5 onions, one of which studded with 5 cloves
- 1 bottle of dry Madeira white wine
- one pot spoon full of aspic
- a white roux
- 2 or 3 bay leaves
- a little thyme
- mushrooms
- a bouquet of parsley and chives
- a few shallots

## Directions

1. Place all the meats and vegetables into a casserole
2. Moisten your meats with a bottle of dry Madeira wine and one pot spoon full of aspic
3. Place your casserole on a high fire
4. When your moisture is reduced, place it on a gentle fire
5. When your glaze is more than blonde, remove your casserole from the fire and leave it outside for ten minutes so the glaze detaches well
6. Make a roux to dilute with the moistening liquid and pour it over your meat
7. Add 2 or 3 bay leaves, a little thyme, mushrooms, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and a few shallots
8. When your sauce boils, place it on the corner of the stove so it boils gently for 2 or 3 hours until your meats are cooked
9. When skimming and boiling, be careful to skim well what you place on the fire and wipe the interior of your casserole beforehand to avoid cloudy sauces
10. Ensure your sauce is neither too brown nor too pale, neither too clear nor too thick, and that it has a gentle salt

title: Grande Sauce (Great Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- The underside of the veal round (four or one piece according to desired quantity of sauce)
- Two pot-spoonfuls of consommé
- Four or five large turned carrots
- Three large onions
- Four noix (pieces of veal)
- Four or five turned carrots
- Four or five onions, one of which is studded with two cloves
- Two pot-spoonfuls of consommé
- Roux (see Roux recipe)
- Mouillement (moistening liquid)
- Mushrooms
- A bouquet of parsley and chives
- Two bay leaves

## Directions

1. Take the underside of the veal round (four or one piece according to the quantity of sauce you want)
2. Put them in a large casserole with two pot-spoonfuls of consommé
3. Make them sweat over a somewhat hot fire
4. Be careful to remove as much foam as possible
5. Also be careful to wipe with a white cloth what sticks to the inside of your casserole so your sauce is not cloudy
6. When the consommé is reduced, prick your pieces of veal with your knife to draw out the juice
7. Put your casserole on a gentle fire so your meat and glaze attach slowly
8. When the glaze at the bottom of your casserole becomes blonde, remove it from heat and leave it covered
9. After ten minutes, fill it with grand bouillon in which you have put four or five large turned carrots and three large onions
10. Let your pieces of veal simmer for two hours
11. During this time, take the four noix and put them in a casserole with four or five turned carrots and four or five onions, one of which is studded with two cloves
12. Put two pot-spoonfuls of consommé in it
13. Put the casserole with your noix on a somewhat hot fire so the moistening reduces and becomes glaze
14. When you see your glaze is more than blonde, transfer the moistening from your pieces of veal that have boiled for two hours and pour it over your noix
15. Let them detach slowly, then make them start, that is to say boil
16. Make a roux and dilute it with the moistening in your casserole
17. When your roux is well dissolved, pour it over your veal noix
18. Add some mushrooms, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and two bay leaves
19. Be careful to foam well when it begins to boil
20. Skim again when you bind your sauce with your roux
21. Try to make your sauce neither too clear nor too thick; if it is too thick, add some moistening; if it is not thick enough, dilute a little roux and put it in your sauce
22. When your sauce has boiled an hour and a half, degrease it, that is to say remove the grease found on the back of your sauce
23. When your meat is cooked, pass your sauce through a sieve

title: Grande Sauce (Large Brown Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 2 pounds of beef shoulder (according to the size of the sauce)
- 2 or 3 pounds of veal
- poultry trimmings, whatever you have
- a few carrots
- a few onions
- water, one pot spoon full
- a large bouquet of parsley and chives
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 cloves
- mushrooms, if you have them

## Directions

1. Place all the meat and vegetables into a casserole
2. Add one pot spoon full of water
3. Place your meats on a fairly hot fire
4. When there is almost no moisture left in your casserole, move it to a gentle fire so that the glaze at the bottom of your casserole colors without burning
5. When it is a dark blonde and your glaze is brown, moisten it with water if you do not have broth
6. Add a large bouquet of parsley and chives, 2 bay leaves, 2 cloves, and mushrooms if you have them
7. Leave your meat to cook well skimmed and salted for three hours
8. After, pass the juice of your meat through a silk sieve
9. Make a roux and dilute it with your moistening liquid, and let your sauce boil for one hour on low heat
10. Degrease it, then pass it through cheesecloth or a horsehair sieve if you lack cheesecloth
11. Ensure your sauce is neither too pale nor too brown, neither too clear nor too thick, and that it is flavorful and well-salted

title: Gras-Double à la Bourgeoise (Tripe Bourgeoise Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked tripe, cleaned
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Chives
- Garlic
- Thyme
- Grease from the back of the pot or fresh butter
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Take cooked tripe that has been well cleaned and cut it into pieces the size of four fingers
2. Marinate it with salt, pepper, parsley, chives, a pinch of garlic, thyme, a bit of grease from the back of the pot or fresh melted butter
3. Make the seasoning adhere to the tripe by coating it with breadcrumbs
4. Fry it
5. Serve with vinegar sauce

title: Gras-Double à la Sauce Robert (Tripe with Robert Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Onion
- Butter
- Cooked tripe, cut into squares
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Bouillon
- Mustard

## Directions

1. Cut onion into dice and pass it over the fire with a bit of butter
2. When it is half cooked, add the cooked tripe that has been cut into squares and seasoned with salt and pepper
3. Add a little vinegar and a bit of bouillon
4. Let boil for half an hour
5. When serving, add a bit of mustard

title: Green Beans Preserved for Winter
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Green beans
- Water
- Salt
- Brine
- Clarified butter
- Paper or parchment

## Directions

1. Trim the green beans
2. Blanch them in plenty of salted water
3. When they have boiled for ten minutes, cool them with plenty of water
4. When cold, drain them
5. Place them in jars or pots
6. Add brine poured over them
7. Clarify butter and put a one-inch layer on top of the beans; when cold, cover the pots with paper or parchment
8. Alternatively, when trimmed, make layers of beans and salt in pots or a tub, alternating until full, then place a wooden round that fits inside the vessel and put a stone on top
9. To cook, soak for two hours and cook in cold water

title: Grenouilles en Fricassée de Poulets (Frogs in Chicken Fricassee Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Frogs
- Boiling water
- Fresh water
- Mushrooms
- Bouquet of parsley
- Ciboule
- One clove of garlic
- Two cloves
- Butter
- Flour
- One glass of white wine
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Egg yolks
- Cream
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Put the frogs in boiling water and let them boil a little
2. Remove to fresh water and drain
3. Place them in a casserole with mushrooms, a bouquet of parsley, ciboule, one clove of garlic, two cloves, and a piece of butter
4. Pass over the fire two or three times and add a good pinch of flour
5. Moisten with a glass of white wine and a little bouillon, add salt and coarse pepper
6. Cook for a quarter hour and reduce to a short sauce
7. Add a liaison of three egg yolks mixed with a little cream and a small pinch of finely chopped parsley
8. Bind without boiling

title: Grenouilles Frites (Fried Frogs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Frogs
- Water
- Vinegar
- Parsley
- Ciboule
- Sliced onions
- Two cloves of garlic
- Two shallots
- Three cloves
- Laurel leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Flour

## Directions

1. Marinate the raw frogs for one hour in equal parts water and vinegar with whole parsley, ciboule, sliced onions, two cloves of garlic, two shallots, three cloves, a laurel leaf, thyme, and basil
2. Drain and flour them for frying

title: Grenouilles Frites (Fried Frogs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Frogs
- Equal parts water and vinegar
- Parsley
- Ciboule (whole)
- Onion slices
- Two garlic cloves
- Two shallots
- Three cloves
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Flour
- Oil
- White wine

## Directions

1. Place the raw frogs in a marinade of equal parts water and vinegar with parsley, whole ciboule, onion slices, two garlic cloves, two shallots, three cloves, bay leaf, thyme, and basil for one hour
2. Remove the frogs from the marinade and drain them
3. Coat them with flour and fry them, or alternatively dip them in a batter made with flour mixed with a spoonful of oil and a large glass of white wine, ensuring the batter is not too thin and flows thickly when poured with a spoon
4. Serve garnished with fried parsley

title: Grives au Gratin (Thrushes au Gratin)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Thrushes
- Forcemeat
- Velouté
- Butter
- Lard strips
- Buttered paper
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare your thrushes as in the previous recipe; have enough forcemeat to place on the bottom of the dish; pour into your forcemeat about one skimming-spoon of velouté and mix well
2. Place this on your dish set over gentle heat to set your forcemeat; then arrange your thrushes that you have stiffened in butter without adding any paste
3. Once you have removed the string holding the flesh, place them on your forcemeat; cover them with strips of lard and a round of buttered paper; arrange them in the gratin
4. A quarter hour before serving, if you do not have an oven, place them on a gentle stove with a hot camp oven; at the moment of serving, remove all the grease found in your dish with a piece of soft bread; sauce them with an italienne sauce

title: Grives aux Cerises (Thrushes with Cherry Garnish)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Thrushes
- Fine forcemeat
- Aromatic herbs, pounded
- Coarse pepper
- Salt
- Butter
- Parsley
- Shallots
- Flour
- White wine
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Pluck the thrushes whole; singe them lightly; debone them starting from the back; once well deboned, take the livers and pound them with the back of the knife; mix them with fine forcemeat, a little pounded aromatic herbs, and coarse pepper
2. Fill your thrushes with this forcemeat, but first pass a leg through the middle of the body, having cut off a large piece so that it represents the tail of a cherry; once the body is filled with forcemeat, bring the flesh together with a needle and secure it
3. Place your thrushes in a casserole with salt and coarse pepper; warm a piece of butter and pour it over your thrushes
4. Half an hour before serving, put them on the fire to simmer in the butter; at the moment of serving, drain them and remove the strings holding them; arrange them on the plate like cherries in a compote dish
5. Sauce them with an italienne; if you do not have any, reduce the juice your thrushes have rendered; remove three-quarters of the butter; add a little parsley and finely chopped shallots, which you will pass a moment in your butter; add about half a spoonful of flour, the equivalent of half a glass of white wine, and as much bouillon; bring your sauce to a boil a few times; degrease it; check that it is well bound and properly seasoned; use it for your thrushes

title: Grives en Cerises (Thrushes as Cherries)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve thrushes or more
- Fine forcemeat
- Ground aromatic herbs
- Coarse pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Take twelve thrushes or more, very fresh
2. Pluck them carefully to preserve their skin whole
3. Singe them lightly
4. Bone them starting from the back
5. When they are well boned, take the livers and pound them with the back of the knife
6. Mix them with fine forcemeat, a bit of ground aromatic herbs, coarse pepper
7. Mix it all together
8. Fill your thrush with this, but first pass a leg through the middle of the body, of which you have cut off a large piece, so that it represents the tail of a cherry
9. When the body is filled with forcemeat, bring the flesh together with a needle and fasten them
10. Put your thrushes in a saucepan with salt and coarse pepper

title: Grondins à l'Italienne (Gurnard Italian Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Gurnard
- Onion slices
- Parsley
- Two bay leaves
- Two cloves
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- One or two bottles white wine
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the gurnard and tie the head
2. Put them in a casserole with onion slices, parsley, two bay leaves, two cloves, salt, coarse pepper, and one or two bottles of white wine
3. The fish must be covered; simmer for a good quarter hour
4. Drain them and arrange them on the platter; put Italian sauce underneath

title: Gruau (Oatmeal Gruel)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One chopine of water or milk
- One spoonful of oatmeal
- Sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Take one chopine of water or milk and put in one spoonful of oatmeal
2. Let it boil gently for a quarter hour
3. Strain it and drink it clear
4. Add a little sugar if desired

title: Gâteau au Lard (Bacon Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three quarterons of small bacon, cut into very thin slices
- one and a half litrons of flour
- bacon fat rendered from cooking the bacon
- half a pound of butter
- a little salt
- two eggs
- one large glass of water

## Directions

1. Cut three quarterons of small bacon into very thin slices
2. Put them in a saucepan over a low fire to cook for half an hour
3. Spread one and a half litrons of flour on a table and make a hole in the middle
4. Put in the rendered bacon fat from cooking the bacon, half a pound of butter, a little salt, and two eggs
5. Add one large glass of water and knead the dough
6. Let it rest for one hour
7. Distribute all the bacon slices throughout the dough, spacing them somewhat apart from each other
8. Form your cake in the usual way, glaze with beaten egg
9. Bake for a good hour in the oven or under a pie-dish cover

title: Gâteau au Riz (Rice Cake)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint of cream
- Half-pound of sugar
- Three quarterons of rice
- Quarter-pound of butter
- Zest of one lemon
- Four whole eggs
- Four egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil one pint of cream
2. Add half-pound of sugar and three quarterons of rice
3. When the rice bursts, melt a quarter-pound of butter into it
4. Mince the zest of a lemon and add it
5. When the rice is cold, add four whole eggs and four yolks (more if the rice is thick)
6. Butter a mold and line it with breadcrumbs, then invert so only a thin coating remains
7. After half an hour, before serving, put the rice in the mold
8. Place it on red ash and surround it with a cover on top filled with fire
9. The mold must not be full lest the rice overflow as it swells
10. At the moment of service, unmold onto the plate and take care to detach the edges
11. If you wish to make a soufflé, whip six egg whites as for other soufflés and mix them with the rice, pouring into a small silver saucepan

title: Gâteau d'Amandes (Almond Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one litron of flour
- butter, about half an egg's worth
- four eggs, whites and yolks
- a pinch of salt
- one quarteron of fine sugar
- six ounces of sweet almonds, pounded very fine
- sugar and red-hot iron for glazing

## Directions

1. Put one litron of flour on a table and make a hole in the middle
2. Put in butter about the size of half an egg, four eggs, a pinch of salt, and one quarteron of fine sugar
3. Add six ounces of sweet almonds pounded very fine
4. Knead everything together and form into a cake in the usual way
5. Bake it and glaze with sugar using a red-hot iron

title: Gâteau de Brioche (Brioche Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one litron of flour
- warm water
- more than half an ounce of brewer's yeast or a small piece of bread yeast
- two litrons of flour
- one and a half pounds of butter
- ten eggs
- half a glass of water
- nearly one ounce of fine salt

## Directions

1. Put one litron of flour on a table and knead it with a little warm water and more than half an ounce of brewer's yeast, or if you do not have it, a small piece of bread yeast in its place
2. Wrap this paste in a cloth and let it rise in a warm place for a quarter hour in summer and one hour in winter
3. Then put two litrons of flour on a table with the paste you have made as leaven, one and a half pounds of butter, ten eggs, half a glass of water, and nearly one ounce of fine salt
4. Knead everything together with the palm of your hands three times
5. Dust with flour and wrap in a cloth, letting it rise for nine or ten hours
6. Cut this paste according to the size of brioche cakes you want to make
7. Moisten them by rounding them with your hands and flatten the top slightly
8. Glaze with beaten egg
9. Bake in the oven for half an hour for small ones and one and a half hours for large ones

title: Gâteau de Riz (Rice Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a little more than a quarteron of well-washed rice
- water
- good milk
- one litron of flour
- salt
- four eggs
- half a pound of butter

## Directions

1. Put in a small pot a little more than a quarteron of well-washed rice
2. Make it burst over the fire with a glass of water, and then add good milk until it is well cooked and thick
3. Let it cool
4. Make a paste with one litron of flour, salt, four eggs, half a pound of butter, and the rice
5. Knead everything together and form a cake
6. Glaze with beaten egg
7. Bake in the oven for one hour, or under a pie-dish cover, taking care to butter the paper that you put under the cake

title: Gâteau de Savoie (Savoy Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- fourteen eggs
- fine sugar in weight equal to fourteen eggs
- flour in weight equal to seven eggs
- grated green lemon zest
- grilled and chopped orange flower
- refined butter

## Directions

1. Weigh fourteen eggs on a balance
2. On the other side put fine sugar equal in weight
3. Remove the sugar and put flour in its place, weighing the amount of seven eggs
4. Set the flour aside
5. Break the eggs, putting the yolks in one bowl and the whites in another
6. With the yolks put the sugar you have weighed, a little grated green lemon zest, and grilled and chopped orange flower
7. Beat everything together for half an hour
8. Then add the well-beaten egg whites and the flour you have weighed, adding it gently while stirring the batter with a whisk
9. Get a medium-sized and deep saucepan or cake mold that you first rub with refined butter, wipe it well with a cloth and coat with refined butter so it spreads everywhere
10. Put your batter in and bake in the oven with moderate heat for a good hour and a half
11. When cooked, turn it out gently onto a platter
12. If it is a beautiful golden color, serve it natural
13. If it has too much color, glaze it with white icing made with very fine sugar, one egg white, and the juice of half a lemon
14. Beat everything together in a faience dish with a wooden spoon until the icing is very white
15. Use it to cover the whole cake and serve only when the icing is dry

title: Gâteau en Losange (Diamond-Shaped Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- egg for glazing
- sugar and red-hot iron for glazing

## Directions

1. Make a puff pastry as explained previously, page 508
2. Beat it with a rolling pin to the thickness of half a finger
3. Cut it into diamond shapes two fingers wide
4. Glaze the top of the cakes with beaten egg
5. Bake for a good quarter hour in the oven
6. Then glaze with sugar using a red-hot iron

title: Gâteau à la Brie (Brie Cheese Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Brie cheese, well aged
- litre and a half of flour
- three quarters of a pound of butter
- very little salt
- five or six eggs

## Directions

1. Take Brie cheese that is very ripe and knead it with litre and a half of flour
2. Add three quarters of a pound of butter and very little salt
3. Add five or six eggs to thin your dough
4. When well kneaded, moisten it and let it rest for an hour
5. Form your cake in the usual way and bake

title: Gâteau à la Crème (Cream Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one litron of flour
- half a setier of double cream
- a good pinch of salt
- good half a pound of butter
- egg for glazing

## Directions

1. Put one litron of flour on a table and make a hole in the middle
2. Put in half a setier of double cream and a good pinch of salt
3. Knead the dough lightly and let it rest for half an hour
4. Then work a good half pound of butter into the dough
5. Beat it five times like puff pastry dough
6. Form into one large cake or several small ones
7. Glaze with beaten egg and bake in the oven
8. You can use this proportion to make as many cakes as you wish

title: Gâteau à la Royale (Royal Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a pinch of chopped green lemon
- two ounces of sugar
- a little salt
- butter the size of half an egg
- a good glass of water
- four or five spoonfuls of flour
- eggs
- a little candied orange flowers
- two bitter almond biscuits

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan a pinch of chopped green lemon, two ounces of sugar, a little salt, butter the size of half an egg, and a good glass of water
2. Bring to a boil for a moment and add four or five spoonfuls of flour
3. Dry out over the fire, stirring constantly until the paste is very thick and begins to stick to the saucepan
4. Remove from the fire and add one egg at a time, stirring vigorously with the spoon until it is well mixed with the paste
5. Continue adding eggs one by one in this manner until the paste is soft without being liquid
6. Add a little candied orange flowers and two bitter almond biscuits, finely ground, to the paste
7. Form small cakes the size of half an egg on buttered paper
8. Glaze the top with beaten egg
9. Bake for half an hour in the oven at a gentle heat

title: Gâteau à la Royale (Royal Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a pinch of chopped green lemon
- two ounces of sugar
- a little salt
- butter about half an egg's worth
- one good glass of water
- four or five spoonfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan a pinch of chopped green lemon, two ounces of sugar, a little salt, and butter about the size of half an egg
2. Add one good glass of water and bring to a boil for a moment
3. Add four or five spoonfuls of flour
4. Dry it on the fire while stirring constantly

title: Gâteau à la Viande (Meat Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- meat (such as hare, rabbit, beef, or mutton)
- beef fat
- one pound of lard cut into dice
- ten egg yolks
- fine salt
- fine spices
- half a glass of brandy
- mushrooms
- a little shallots
- parsley
- chives
- lard slices for lining

## Directions

1. Choose the meat that will give the name to your cake, such as hare cake, rabbit cake, beef cake, etc. All are made the same way, except that game is mixed with an equal amount of butcher's meat
2. For a mutton cake, take a leg, remove all the meat after removing the skin, and chop it with a little beef fat
3. Mix this meat with one pound of lard cut into dice, ten egg yolks, fine salt, fine spices, half a glass of brandy, mushrooms, a little shallots, parsley, and chives, all chopped
4. Put lard slices at the bottom of a saucepan and your meat farce on top after mixing it well with all its seasoning
5. Cook in the oven for at least three hours
6. When cooked, let it cool in the saucepan, then remove it to turn it out onto a plate
7. Leave the lard slices that are around the cake and rasp them lightly with a knife
8. Serve on a plate garnished with a napkin

title: Gâteaux Fourrés (Filled Cakes)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff pastry
- Jam or marmalade
- Egg for gilding
- Butter
- Nonpareille sprinkles or fine sugar

## Directions

1. Take puff pastry and form two equal cakes the size of your entremets dish, each the thickness of two coins
2. Put jam on the first one, leaving a finger's width of border that you will moisten with a brush dipped in water
3. Place the second cake on the first and glue them well together with your fingers, pressing all around
4. After shaping them a bit, gild them with beaten egg and bake in the oven
5. When they are cooked and coming out of the oven, pass a brush dipped in butter over them and sprinkle nonpareille sprinkles all over
6. Alternatively, instead of nonpareille, you may put fine sugar and pass the hot spatula over it to glaze it

title: Génoises (Fried Pastries with Frangipane)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- short pastry dough
- frangipane cream

## Directions

1. Make short pastry dough
2. Roll out to the thickness of a small coin
3. Cut with a pastry cutter of the same size
4. Place a full teaspoon of frangipane cream on each piece
5. Moisten the edges slightly
6. Cover with another piece of dough
7. Seal together well by pricking all around
8. Fry until golden color
9. Drain on a cloth
10. Glaze the top with sugar and red icing

title: Hachette of All Sorts of Roasted Meats (Minced Roasted Meat Hash)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted meat such as butcher's meat, poultry, or game
- parsley
- chives
- shallots
- mushrooms
- good bouillon
- salt
- coarse pepper
- breadcrumbs
- verjuice

## Directions

1. Cut the roasted meat into very thin slices
2. Put it in a saucepan with a little parsley, chives, shallots, mushrooms (all chopped), a little good bouillon, salt, and coarse pepper
3. Simmer everything on the fire for a quarter hour
4. Take the dish you wish to serve and put a little of the meat's sauce mixed with breadcrumbs on it
5. Arrange your meat on the breadcrumbs and put a little more breadcrumbs on top of the meat
6. Attach it on a gentle fire until a small gratin forms on the bottom of the dish
7. Put the rest of the sauce with a dash of verjuice on top

title: Hachis de Bœuf (Chopped Beef Hash)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four onions
- Butter
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Half a glass of wine
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Chopped beef
- Mustard or vinegar

## Directions

1. Mince three or four onions very fine and put them in a casserole with a little butter
2. Cook over the fire until they are almost cooked
3. Add a good pinch of flour and stir until it is golden in color
4. Moisten with bouillon and half a glass of wine, season with salt and coarse pepper
5. Let boil until the onion is cooked and there is no more sauce
6. Add chopped beef and boil it so it takes on the taste of the onion
7. When serving, add a spoonful of mustard or a dash of vinegar

title: Hachis de Lapereau (Minced Young Rabbit)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Four young rabbits
- Béchamelle, two or three spoonfuls
- Croutons
- Poached eggs
- Small larded fillets, glazed

## Directions

1. Roast four young rabbits on the spit
2. When cooked and cold, remove the fillets and the largest part of the thighs
3. Remove the nerves and skin
4. Mince all the meat, neither too fine nor too coarse
5. Place the minced meat in a casserole with two or three spoonfuls of hot béchamelle
6. Mix the béchamelle with the minced meat; it should not be too thin
7. Keep it warm in a bain-marie
8. Arrange croutons around the dish
9. At the moment of serving, place the minced meat in the center
10. You may place poached eggs around it and small larded, glazed fillets between the eggs
11. If you do not have béchamelle, reduce velouté into which you put a liaison of three egg yolks

title: Hachis de Mouton Couvert (Covered Mutton Hash)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of mutton
- onions
- salt
- flour
- raw juice
- bouillon
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook a shoulder of mutton on the spit.
2. Mince very finely three or four onions.
3. When they begin to take color, add a pinch of salt and a small pinch of flour.
4. Mix the raw juice into this.
5. When it is a beautiful golden color, add two ladles of good bouillon.
6. When the shoulder is cooked on the spit, remove the meat without touching the skin and to the bone.
7. Since it must appear whole, take the meat you have removed, mince it very finely with parsley, chives, and mushrooms.
8. Heat it without boiling and season with nutmeg.
9. Sprinkle it over the top of the shoulder and cover the plat.
10. Then cover it with the shoulder.

title: Hachis de Perdreaux (Partridge Hash)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 partridges
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 clove
- 3 shallots
- sage
- 2 large glasses of bouillon
- eggs or poached eggs
- croutons fried in oil

## Directions

1. Spit-roast 8 partridges; when cooked, let them cool
2. Remove the fillets and chop them; place in a saucepan
3. Make a light roux and add the livers and lungs of your partridges, 1 bay leaf, 1 clove, 3 shallots, and a little sage
4. Brown the whole with your roux; add 2 large glasses of bouillon
5. Reduce the sauce to half; strain it through fine cloth without pressing
6. Add sauce to your hash until it is thick and bound; keep it warm without boiling
7. At service, arrange croutons around your platter and put your hash in the center
8. Add around it soft-boiled eggs or poached eggs, placing a round crouton fried in oil between each egg

title: Hachis de Poularde à la turque (Turkish-Style Minced Chicken)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- two hens
- four skimming spoons of béchamel
- one spoon of cream
- butter the size of an egg
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- poached eggs
- glazed filets mignons or croûtons

## Directions

1. Spit roast two hens
2. When cooled, remove the meat and mince it finely
3. Place it in a casserole
4. Add four skimming spoons of béchamel and one spoon of cream in another casserole
5. When it has boiled a few times, put it in your mince with butter the size of an egg, a little salt, a little pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
6. Keep it hot without letting it boil
7. At the moment of serving, put it in your dish with poached eggs on top; between each egg, a glazed filet mignon or croûton

title: Hachis de Toutes Sortes de Viandes (Hash of All Sorts of Meats)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- various meats from the butcher, poultry or game roasted on a spit
- flour
- bouillon
- meat jus
- salt
- coarse pepper
- coulis or breadcrumb paste

## Directions

1. Chop the meats very fine
2. Pass them through the fire and add a pinch of flour
3. Moisten with half a glass of bouillon and an equal amount of jus
4. Season with salt and coarse pepper
5. Boil for a quarter hour
6. Add the meat to heat through without boiling, to prevent it from shriveling
7. If the meat is tough, boil for at least an hour on very low heat
8. To bind the sauce, add a little coulis, or if you do not have it, add two pinches of fine bread crumbs
9. When serving the hash, surround it with fried bread croutons for good appearance

title: Harengs Grillés (Grilled Herrings)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Herrings
- Salt
- Pepper
- Oil
- Butter
- Mustard
- Haricot purée or tomato sauce (optional)

## Directions

1. Empty and scrape your herrings
2. Wipe them well
3. Add salt and pepper and drizzle with oil
4. A quarter hour before serving, place them on the grill over a hot fire, being careful to turn them
5. When cooked, arrange them on your plate
6. Make a butter sauce in which you place a spoonful of mustard
7. You may use a purée of haricots or a tomato sauce

title: Haricot de Mouton (Lamb Stew)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- butter
- flour
- bouillon or water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- three cloves
- garlic clove
- turnips

## Directions

1. Cut a lamb shoulder into pieces the width of two fingers and a bit longer
2. Make a roux with little butter and a spoonful of flour
3. Brown it on a small fire, stirring always with a spoon, until it is a dark cinnamon color
4. Add the meat and pass it over the fire five or six times, stirring from time to time
5. Add bouillon (or about a pint of warm water if no bouillon), little at a time so the roux can dissolve well, stirring always with the spoon until all is added
6. Season the meat with salt and pepper, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a bay leaf, thyme, basil, three cloves, and a clove of garlic
7. Cook on a small fire
8. Halfway through cooking, tilt the casserole so the fat comes to the top; remove it with a spoon
9. Add turnips cut into pieces and a bouquet
10. Tilt the casserole again to remove the remaining fat
11. When done, the sauce should be neither too clear nor too thick, that is to say of medium consistency
12. Dress your pieces in the serving dish, pour the sauce over them, and sprinkle with chopped parsley

title: Haricot de Mouton (Mutton Ragout)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton (ribs, shoulder or breast)
- butter
- flour
- warm water
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- scallions
- bay leaf
- thyme
- onion
- cloves
- turnips
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut your mutton into pieces, either ribs, shoulder, or breast
2. Make a roux with butter and flour
3. When it is blonde, brown your meat in it for a quarter hour
4. Moisten it with warm water
5. Stir your meat well until your ragout boils
6. Skim it
7. Add salt, pepper, a bouquet of parsley and scallions to which you add a bay leaf, a little thyme, and an onion stuck with two cloves
8. Boil your ragout for two hours
9. Turn your turnips and brown them in butter in a casserole or pan; be careful that your turnips do not blacken
10. When they are blonde, drain them and put them in your ragout
11. When it is three-quarters cooked, add a small piece of sugar
12. Degrease your ragout well before serving
13. Remove the bouquet and the onion
14. Check that it is properly seasoned

title: Haricot de Mouton Distingué (Distinguished Lamb Stew)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb rack
- bouillon
- parsley
- chives
- half a bay leaf
- thyme
- salt
- pepper
- turnips
- water
- jus
- coulis
- glaze

## Directions

1. Take a rack of lamb and cut it by ribs so they are thicker, with each one being thicker than just one rib
2. Cut them and pare them neatly, flatten them a little with the flat of the knife
3. Cook them in a casserole with bouillon, a bouquet of parsley and chives, half a bay leaf, a little thyme, and salt and pepper
4. Have turnips which you will blanch; cut them round, and blanch them about a quarter hour in water and remove them to fresh water
5. Put them to cook with bouillon and jus to color them a little
6. When they are almost cooked, add two or three spoonfuls of coulis
7. When your chops are cooked, degrease the sauce and pass it through a sieve to put it in the ragout of turnips
8. Take care that the ragout is neither too clear nor too thick; reduce it to the point of a glaze
9. Dress the chops in the serving dish that you will serve, and the ragout of turnips over them

title: Haricots au Jus (Beans in Gravy)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Soissons beans
- Roux
- Jus
- Salt
- Black pepper

## Directions

1. The Soissons beans are the best; cook them and drain them.
2. Make a light roux moistened with jus, a little salt, and black pepper.
3. Sauté your beans in the sauce.
4. Serve them very hot. You may fricassee them in the same manner as lentils.

title: Haricots Blancs Nouveaux (Fresh White Beans)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Beans
- Velouté
- Black pepper
- Nutmeg, grated
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Bring water to boil in a pot or saucepan with salt about the size of half an egg of butter.
2. When the water boils, add your beans.
3. As soon as they are cooked, drain them.
4. Throw a good piece of butter in a saucepan with the beans.
5. Sauté them and add a spoonful of velouté, a little salt, black pepper, and a little grated nutmeg.
6. At the moment of serving, add a liaison of two egg yolks.

title: Haricots verts (Green Beans)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Green beans
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- Coarse pepper
- Parsley
- Chives
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Peel and wash the beans
2. Boil water with salt in a large pot and add the beans
3. After a quarter hour of boiling, test if they bend under your finger
4. Drain in a strainer and place in cold water
5. At the moment of serving, melt a good piece of butter in a saucepan
6. Drain the beans and add them with salt, coarse pepper, chopped parsley and chives
7. Place on a hot fire and sauté the beans
8. When well heated, serve on a plate
9. Add lemon juice if desired

title: Haricots verts au beurre noir (Green Beans with Brown Butter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Green beans
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Butter
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Blanch the beans and remove from boiling water
2. Let drain in a strainer
3. Season with salt and coarse pepper and arrange on a plate
4. Pour brown butter over them
5. Alternatively, melt a good piece of butter in a saucepan and brown it
6. Place the green beans in and sauté on a high fire
7. Season before arranging on the plate
8. Add a dash of vinegar

title: Haricots verts liés (Bound Green Beans)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Green beans
- Butter
- Fine herbs
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Blanch the green beans and let cool and drain
2. Add butter the size of two eggs to a saucepan with finely chopped fine herbs and cook slightly
3. When the butter is warm and herbs slightly fried, add two teaspoons of flour mixed with the butter
4. Pour over one glass of bouillon, a little salt, and coarse pepper
5. When the sauce boils, add the beans and sauté them
6. At the moment of serving, add a liaison of two egg yolks
7. Check the beans for good taste
8. You may add lemon juice

title: Haricots à la Purée d'Oignons (Beans with Onion Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beans
- Butter
- Onion purée

## Directions

1. When the beans are cooked, drain them.
2. Put them in a saucepan with butter about the size of two eggs and about eight spoonfuls of onion purée.
3. Sauté them over the fire without letting them boil.
4. Serve them very hot; check that they are well seasoned.

title: Hatelet de Langues de Mouton (Skewer of Sheep's Tongues)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three sheep's tongues cooked in water
- good butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- coulis
- flour
- bouillon
- two egg yolks
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut three cooked sheep's tongues into square pieces of the same size
2. Pass them on the fire in a saucepan with a piece of good butter, salt, pepper, parsley, chives, mushrooms, all chopped
3. Moisten with coulis if you have it, otherwise put a good pinch of flour and moisten with bouillon
4. Let the ragout boil until the sauce is very thick
5. Add two egg yolks, thicken the eggs with the sauce on the fire without letting it boil
6. Let the ragout cool and skewer all the small pieces of tongues on small wooden skewers, making the sauce adhere to them
7. Bread them with breadcrumbs and grill them, basting them from time to time with a little butter
8. When they are grilled to a beautiful color, serve on the skewers

title: Horly de Poulets (Chicken Horly)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two fat chickens
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Parsley leaves
- Scallions
- Two bay leaves
- Juice of two lemons
- A dozen large onions
- Flour
- Oil for frying
- Aspic

## Directions

1. Singe and butcher the two chickens as you would for a fricassée and place the pieces in a vessel.
2. Season the chicken pieces with salt, coarse pepper, a pinch of parsley leaves, some broken scallions, two bay leaves, and the juice of two lemons.
3. Toss the chicken pieces in this seasoning.
4. Peel a dozen large onions of equal size and cut them into slices of equal thickness to form rings, discarding the centers.
5. Half an hour before serving, drain the chicken pieces and dust them with flour.
6. Toss them in a cloth and shake them well.
7. Place them in oil that is not too hot.
8. When they have a beautiful color and you judge them cooked, drain them on white cloth.
9. Heat your oil well.
10. Dust the onion rings with flour, toss them in a cloth, and place them in very hot oil.
11. When they have a beautiful color, drain them on white cloth.
12. Arrange the chicken pieces in a mound on a platter.
13. Place the onions on top and hot aspic underneath.

title: Hure de Cochon (Pork Head Cheese)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork head, boned entire
- Pork meat, fresh, fatty and lean
- Salt
- Peppercorns
- Pounded aromatic herbs
- Four spices
- Parsley and chives, chopped
- Pork bones
- Rinds of pork
- Eight to ten carrots
- Ten onions
- Eight bay leaves
- Seven to eight sprigs of thyme
- Basil
- Parsley and chives
- Six cloves
- Lard cut in long strips
- White cloth
- Water

## Directions

1. Bone the head entirely; take pieces of fresh pork meat, fatty and lean, that you put with your head
2. Season it with salt, peppercorns, pounded aromatic herbs, four spices, chopped parsley and chives; let it sit in a vessel for eight or ten days
3. When it has well absorbed the seasoning, drain it; gather all your pieces lengthwise in your head, such as the tongue, the fillets, the pieces of lard that you cut lengthwise; arrange them so that your head is filled, and regains its original form
4. With a trussing needle and string sew the opening where you have boned it, and tie it with string so that in cooking it does not deform; wrap it in white linen that you tie at both ends
5. Then place it in a braising pan with the bones of your head, some rinds, eight to ten carrots, ten onions, eight bay leaves, seven or eight sprigs of thyme, basil, a large bouquet of parsley and chives, six cloves, a large handful of salt, and some pork scraps if you have any, or other meat
6. Moisten your head cheese with water until it is covered; braise it eight to ten hours over a gentle fire
7. Test it before to see if it is cooked; and if your larding needle has difficulty entering, it is not cooked
8. When you remove it from the fire, leave your head cheese two hours in its cooking liquid; then remove it with another white cloth; press it with your two hands, always keeping its form, but only to extract any liquid that remained in it
9. Let it cool in its cloth: when it is quite cold, unwrap it, prepare it, and place it on a folded napkin on a plate; be careful to remove the strings attached to it
10. You may also chop the meats inside; but the head cheese is more generally esteemed this way: the pieces are more whole, the interior more marbled

title: Huîtres aux Fines Herbes (Oysters with Fine Herbs)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters in their shells
- fine herbs (shallots, parsley, mushrooms)
- lard, grated
- oil and butter
- four spices
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Have your oysters opened and detached, and put them without shells in a saucepan with their water
2. Let them stiffen without allowing them to boil
3. Prepare fine herbs as for cutlets, with shallots, parsley, mushrooms, grated lard, oil and butter, and four spices
4. Mix your oysters with your fine herbs and put them in well-cleaned shells, filling them completely
5. Arrange breadcrumbs on top
6. Place them on the grill over a hot fire with a camp oven on top

title: Huîtres en Coquilles (Oysters in Shells)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- shallots
- parsley
- mushrooms
- bacon
- oil
- butter
- four spices
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Open and detach your oysters and put them without shells in a saucepan with their water
2. Heat them without letting them boil
3. Prepare herbs as for cutlets: shallots, parsley, mushrooms, grated bacon, oil and butter, and four spices
4. Mix your oysters with your herbs and put them in well-cleaned shells; fill them
5. Arrange breadcrumbs on top; then put them on the grill over a hot fire with a camp oven on top

title: Issus d'Agneau à la Bourgeoise (Lamb Offal Bourgeoise)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb head, liver, heart, lungs, and feet
- Water for deglazing
- Bouillon
- Good butter
- Bouquet garni
- Salt
- Pepper
- Three egg yolks
- Milk
- Verjuice

## Directions

1. Remove the jaws and muzzle, and soak all the offal parts cut into pieces in water
2. Blanch briefly
3. Cook over low heat with bouillon, a bit of good butter, a bouquet garni, salt, and pepper
4. When cooked, dilute three egg yolks with a bit of milk and thicken the sauce over the fire
5. Add a dash of verjuice
6. Arrange the head in the serving dish, uncover the brain, place the remaining parts around it, and pour sauce over

title: Italienne (Italian Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- one half tablespoon shallots
- finely chopped mushrooms in the same amount as shallots
- one half-bottle white wine
- butter the size of an egg
- 2 tablespoons velouté
- 1 tablespoon consommé

## Directions

1. Place the parsley, shallots, mushrooms, and white wine in a saucepan with the butter
2. Boil until well reduced
3. When there is almost no liquid remaining in the saucepan, add the velouté and consommé
4. Boil the sauce over moderate heat
