5. Skim and degrease carefully
6. When the sauce is reduced to the proper consistency, meaning it is thick like clear porridge, remove from heat
7. Place in another saucepan and keep warm in a bain-marie

title: Jambon en Gingarat (Ham in Ginger Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- ham
- ham fat or lard
- water
- vinegar
- crushed pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the ham into very thin slices and put them in a saucepan or skillet with a little ham fat or lard
2. Cook on low heat
3. When cooked, arrange the ham on a platter
4. In the same saucepan, add a little water, a dash of vinegar, and crushed pepper
5. Detach what remains in the saucepan by stirring the sauce with a spoon
6. Serve the sauce over the ham

title: Julienne (Vegetable Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- turnips
- leeks
- onions
- celery
- lettuce
- sorrel
- chervil
- butter
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Cut the roots into thin strips the thickness of half a line and eight to ten lines long
2. Cut the onions in two, then into slices to form half-circles
3. Cut leeks and celery into thin strips
4. Slice the lettuce and sorrel thinly
5. Pass the roots through butter until they have softened slightly
6. Add the lettuce, herbs, and chervil, ensuring everything is well softened
7. Moisten with bouillon
8. Boil over low heat for an hour or more until cooked
9. Prepare your bread and pour your Julienne over it

title: Jus (Gravy)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of sliced beef
- Two rabbits, that is the hind legs and saddle only
- One calf knuckle
- Five carrots
- Six onions
- Two cloves
- Two bay leaves
- A bouquet of parsley and chives
- Two spoonfuls of broth
- Great broth or water

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan three pounds of sliced beef, two rabbits (that is to say the hind legs and the saddle, not the breast nor the head), one calf knuckle, five carrots, six onions, two cloves, two bay leaves, a bouquet of parsley and chives
2. Put two spoonfuls of broth in your saucepan which you place on a good fire
3. When the broth is reduced, smother your stove and put your saucepan on it so that your meat releases its jus and it attaches very gently
4. The glaze at the bottom of your saucepan must be almost black
5. When it reaches this point, take your saucepan from the fire and wait ten minutes without moistening it
6. Fill it with great broth or water, but in a lesser quantity
7. Then let your jus simmer for three hours on the fire
8. Let it be well skimmed and seasoned; if you moisten with water the cooked meats, pass your jus through a hair sieve
9. You can also make it with meat scraps: cut slices of onions that you put at the bottom of your saucepan, your meat on top (and the seasoning of the jus above), put two or three glasses of water; reduce it as the jus: when the bottom of your saucepan is almost black, fill it almost with water according to the quantity of meat; add the salt needed and let your jus boil for two hours; after pass it through the hair sieve

title: Jus Maigre (Lean Juice)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- same ingredients as the previous sauce
- same moistening as the other sauces

## Directions

1. Prepare the juice as in the previous sauce
2. Let it attach until it is almost black
3. Use the same ingredients as for the other sauces, and the same moistening
4. When your juice has boiled for an hour and a half, pass it through a silk sieve
5. Use it as needed

title: Kari de Lapereau (Young Rabbit Curry)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two young rabbits, cut into equal pieces
- Three-quarters pound of butter
- One pound of small bacon, cut into small square flat pieces
- Two teaspoons of Indian saffron or turmeric
- Ten small hot peppers, pounded with a little salt
- Two bay leaves
- Two cloves
- Three spoonfuls of flour
- Bouillon or water
- Salt
- Mushrooms
- Small onions
- Artichoke hearts, if available
- Eggplants
- Haricots verts, cauliflower, tomatoes, cucumbers, and other vegetables as in India
- Five or six eggs for liaison

## Directions

1. Cut two young rabbits into equal pieces
2. In a casserole, heat three-quarters pound of butter
3. Add one pound of small bacon cut into small square flat pieces and brown it in the butter
4. Add two teaspoons of Indian saffron or turmeric
5. Add ten small hot peppers pounded with a little salt
6. Add two bay leaves and two cloves
7. Once all the seasoning is well browned in the butter, add the rabbit pieces, which should be well dried to prevent blood
8. Remove the lungs
9. Brown the rabbit well
10. When the pieces are firm, add three spoonfuls of flour and mix well with the ragout
11. Moisten with bouillon or water
12. Add salt; there must be plenty of liquid so it can cook on high heat and reduce
13. Add mushrooms
14. When three-quarters cooked, add small onions, artichoke hearts if available, and eggplants
15. In India, all sorts of vegetables are added: haricots verts, cauliflower, tomatoes, cucumbers, etc.
16. When the curry is cooked, it must be swimming in sauce and fat; consequently, do not degrease it
17. You can serve it in this state, or if you wish, remove the meat and garnish and place them in the dish for serving
18. Make a liaison of five or six eggs in the sauce
19. Stir the sauce over the fire without letting it boil, as it would break
20. When thickened, pass it through a sieve over the ragout
21. Check that it is well spiced and well seasoned
22. Serve with rice bread in water alongside the curry dish, as the person serving the ragout places rice bread on the plate

title: Kari de Tendons de Veau à l'Indienne (Indian-Style Veal Tendons Curry)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal tendons (from one breast)
- Butter (3 quarters pound)
- Saffron or turmeric (half a spoonful of powder)
- Small hot pepper (10 cloves)
- Salt pork, cut in flat squares (1 pound)
- Bay leaves (2)
- Flour (4 spoonfuls)
- Bouillon
- Mushrooms
- Cloves (2) in an onion
- Artichoke bottoms
- Small onions
- Rice (1.5 pounds)
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut and prepare your tendons as in the blanc method
2. Put butter, saffron or turmeric powder, small hot pepper, salt pork cut in flat squares, and bay leaves in a pot
3. Sauté your tendons with butter and pepper until well browned
4. Add four spoonfuls of flour and mix with the tendons and butter
5. Moisten the tendons with bouillon; there should be plenty of sauce
6. Add mushrooms and two cloves stuck in an onion, which you will remove when the curry is cooked
7. When the ragoût is half cooked, add artichoke bottoms that are three-quarters cooked and small onions
8. Do not degrease this ragoût because of its richness
9. For the rice bread: wash one and a half pounds of rice in five or six warm waters
10. Bring water to a boil in a pot; put the rice in to blanch for ten minutes
11. Drain on a sieve
12. Butter a pot of the right size to hold the rice; fill it with rice
13. Put it on a gentle heat with fire on the lid and around it so the rice dries, forms into bread, and takes color
14. At the moment of serving, pour the ragoût into a deep dish such as a tureen or salad bowl; turn over the rice bread and place on a plate beside the curry, as rice is placed on the plate where curry is served
15. If you do not have bouillon to moisten the curry, use water; then add salt, pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
16. The curry must be very yellow
17. Put nothing in the rice

title: Kari à la Française (French-Style Curry)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal tendons
- All ingredients as in Indian-style curry
- Egg yolks (5 or 6)

## Directions

1. Prepare your tendons and make the curry the same as the previous recipe
2. At the moment of serving, remove the meat and garnishes from your curry
3. Make sure it is very rich and the sauce is well bound and abundant
4. Make a liaison of five or six egg yolks according to the quantity of sauce
5. Bind your sauce on the heat, constantly stirring without letting it boil
6. When you see the sauce coat your spoon, pass it through a sieve over your curry
7. The meat and garnishes must bathe in the sauce, and the pepper must dominate
8. Season well with salt
9. If your sauce is too thin, add some velouté

title: Laitues à l'Espagnole (Spanish-Style Lettuce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Lettuce
- Salt
- Black pepper
- Lard
- Veal slices
- Carrots
- Onions
- Cloves
- Bay leaf
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Remove the tough outer leaves from the lettuce.
2. Wash and blanch the lettuce in plenty of salted water.
3. When it has boiled for twenty minutes, refresh it and press it so that no water remains.
4. Season the heart of the lettuce with salt and black pepper and tie it with string.
5. Arrange strips of lard in the bottom of a saucepan with a few slices of veal, two carrots sliced, three onions, two cloves, and a bay leaf.
6. Place the lettuces on top and cover them with lard.
7. Moisten with bouillon and a little meat drippings from the pot.
8. Simmer for one hour.
9. When the lettuce is cooked, drain it on a white cloth and press it in the same cloth to extract the moisture.
10. Trim it, glaze it, and arrange it in crowns with a glazed croûton approximately the size of the lettuce.
11. Serve with Spanish sauce underneath.

title: Lamb Chops à la Pocheé (Poached Lamb Chops)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A rack of aged lamb
- Good butter
- Shallots, chopped
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take a rack of aged lamb, cut it by ribs, and put them in a saucepan with a piece of good butter
2. Cook them over a small fire, turning them from time to time, until they are completely cooked
3. Remove them from the saucepan to drain of their fat
4. Leave about half a spoonful of fat in the same saucepan and add a glass of bouillon, chopped shallots, salt, and coarse pepper
5. Bring to a boil to loosen what sticks to the saucepan
6. Put the chops back in with three egg yolks
7. Bind the sauce over the fire without boiling
8. When serving, add a little nutmeg and a dash of vinegar

title: Lamb Cutlets à la Constance
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Eighteen lamb cutlets
- Coarse pepper
- Ice
- Espagnole sauce
- Consommé
- Lamb crests
- Foie gras
- Lamb kidneys
- Béchamel sauce
- Mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut and trim eighteen lamb cutlets as the previous ones
2. Season them only with coarse pepper
3. Place them in your sautoir with a piece of ice the size of an egg, one deglazing spoonful of espagnole, and two similar spoonfuls of consommé
4. Half an hour before serving, place your cutlets on a moderately high heat
5. Stir them carefully and ensure they do not stick
6. When the cooking liquid is reduced so as to form a glaze, at the moment of serving, arrange your cutlets in a crown
7. Ensure they have the glaze in which they cooked
8. In the center of your crown, place a ragout of crests and foie gras
9. Cook your crests in a blanc and your foie gras and kidneys
10. Make a perfect béchamel
11. Add your garnish to it
12. Add a few turned mushrooms
13. Sauté everything well in your béchamel
14. Place your ragout in the middle of your cutlets
15. Ensure your garnishes are well drained and do not bring any water into your béchamel

title: Lamb Shoulder (Épaule d'Agneau)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- tendons from breast
- cutlets
- bread crumbs
- butter
- salt
- coarse pepper
- sauce with skewers or seasoning
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. From the spitted lamb shoulder, remove the meat and mince it for a blanquette
2. Ensure there is no skin or nerves
3. When the blanquette is prepared, keep it warm in a bain-marie
4. Meanwhile, prepare the tendons from the breast: leave a small bone from the chest
5. Coat with skewer sauce if available, or season with fine salt and coarse pepper
6. Dip in warm butter, place in breadcrumbs, giving them a nice shape
7. They should be slightly larger than the cutlets; place on a baking sheet
8. Alternatively, bread the tendons in English style and fry them
9. Prepare the cutlets: cut and trim; season with salt and coarse pepper
10. Place in a sauté pan with warm butter over them for serving
11. At service time, grill the breast tendons gently over low fire
12. Sauté and glaze the cutlets
13. Arrange in crowns alternating tendons and cutlets
14. When the crown is formed, place the blanquette in the middle

title: Lamb Tails with Cabbage, Bourgeoise Style (Queues de Mouton aux Choux, à la Bourgeoise)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six lamb tails
- bouillon
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- two cloves
- half a clove of garlic
- half a large head of cabbage
- quarter pound of small lard or half a pound
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook five or six lamb tails in a light braise made with a little bouillon, little salt, pepper, a bouquet of parsley, chives, two cloves, and half a clove of garlic.
2. Blanch half a large head of cabbage for a quarter hour in boiling water, then remove to fresh water and press it.
3. Remove the core and chop the cabbage.
4. Cut a quarter pound of small lard or half a pound into small dice.
5. Mix the lard and cabbage together in a roux made with a good pinch of flour and butter.
6. Pass them together and moisten with a little bouillon without salt.
7. Let cook for an hour over low heat until the cabbage and lard are well cooked and the ragout is well bound.
8. Drain the tails and wipe them with a cloth.
9. Arrange them in the dish at a small distance from one another.
10. Cover each tail with ragout and serve hot.

title: Lamb Tails with Rice (Queues de Mouton au Riz)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five lamb tails
- bouillon
- parsley
- chives
- two cloves
- half a clove of garlic
- half a bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- six ounces of well-cleaned rice
- egg yolk, beaten

## Directions

1. Cook the lamb tails with bouillon, a bouquet of parsley, chives, two cloves, half a clove of garlic, half a bay leaf, thyme, basil, salt, and pepper over low heat.
2. Remove from heat and let drain and cool.
3. Wash six ounces of well-cleaned rice several times in lukewarm water, rubbing it with your hands.
4. Put the rice in a small pot with the bouillon used to cook the tails, strained through a sieve without removing the fat; if there is not enough bouillon, add more.
5. Cook the rice over low heat; it should remain quite thick without being overcooked.
6. When half cold, cover the bottom of the serving dish with a little rice and arrange the tails on top so they do not touch.
7. Cover them completely with the remaining rice, preserving the shape of each tail.
8. Glaze the top lightly with beaten egg yolk.
9. Place the dish on a bed of hot ashes with a tourte lid covered with good fire.
10. Let cook until the tails are a golden color and the rice forms a crust.
11. Tilt the dish slightly to drain the fat, wipe the edges, and serve.

title: Lamb Tails à la Prussienne (Queues de Mouton à la Prussienne)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five lamb tails
- half a head of cabbage
- half a pound of small lard
- six large onions
- parsley
- chives
- two cloves
- half a clove of garlic
- very small branch of fennel
- salt
- coarse pepper
- bouillon
- bread crumbs
- butter
- flour
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Blanch the lamb tails, cabbage, and small lard for a quarter hour in boiling water, then remove to fresh water.
2. Press the cabbage and cut it into several pieces, tying each separately.
3. Cut the small lard into several pieces without separating them from the rind, and tie them.
4. Place the tails at the bottom of a small pot, then add the cabbage, lard, and six large onions.
5. Add a bouquet of parsley, chives, two cloves, half a clove of garlic, a very small branch of fennel, salt, and coarse pepper.
6. Cover with bouillon and cook over low heat in a braise.
7. Cut bread crumbs into rounds the size of a small coin and pass them over the fire with butter until they are a beautiful golden color; set aside to toast.
8. Make a roux with a good pinch of flour in the remaining butter from the croutons and brown it.
9. Moisten with bouillon from the cooking of the cabbage and a dash of vinegar; boil for half an hour so the flour cooks and forms a light coulis to your taste.
10. Degrease and strain the sauce through a sieve.
11. When the tails are cooked and no sauce remains, drain them and wipe with a cloth.
12. Arrange the tails interspersed with the cabbage, onions around them, the lard and croutons on top of the cabbage.
13. Serve the sauce on top.

title: Lamproie (Lamprey)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lamprey
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- mustard

## Directions

1. Scald the lamprey as explained in the article on tench, then cut it into sections and flour it before cooking.
2. Cook on the grill like other fish and serve with a sauce of capers or a bourgeoise remoulade sauce.
3. For the sauce, put oil, vinegar, salt, coarse pepper, and mustard in a casserole, all mixed together, and serve in a sauceboat.

title: Langue de Bœuf aux Cornichons (Beef Tongue with Gherkins)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- large lardons seasoned with salt, coarse pepper, four spices, parsley and chives
- lard
- veal and beef slices
- carrots
- onions
- thyme
- bay leaf
- cloves
- bouillon
- piquant sauce
- gherkins

## Directions

1. Have a beef tongue that you will soak to degorge
2. Blanch it for half an hour
3. Let it cool
4. When cold, trim it
5. Prepare large lardons seasoned with salt, coarse pepper, four spices, finely chopped parsley and chives
6. Lard the tongue with these seasoned lardons
7. Cook it in a casserole with some lard, slices of veal and beef, carrots, onions, thyme, bay leaf, and three cloves
8. Moisten the cooking with bouillon
9. Simmer the tongue on low heat for four to five hours or more, depending on how tough the tongue is
10. At serving time, trim it and remove the skin
11. Cut it lengthwise down the middle, but not completely, so the cut tongue forms a heart on the plate
12. Prepare a piquant sauce with gherkins cut into thin slices, or arrange sliced gherkins in a miroton pattern on the edges of the tongue or otherwise
13. Pour the sauce over the tongue and serve
14. Alternatively, prepare the tongue as described above, cook it with lard, carrots, onions, thyme, bay leaf, three cloves, and moisten with water
15. Season the cooking with salt, but not too much so you can use the cooking liquid in which the tongue was cooked
16. To make the sauce, make a small brown roux, moisten it with the jus in which the tongue cooked, add a dash of vinegar

title: Langue de Bœuf en Atelet (Beef Tongue on Skewers)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef tongue
- Italian sauce
- two or three eggs
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. When your tongue is cooked as described for cornichons, allow it to cool
2. Cut it into small thin squares
3. Place the pieces on a plate
4. Reduce Italian sauce until well reduced
5. When well reduced, add a short liaison of two or three eggs
6. Stir carefully so the sauce does not break
7. When the sauce is bound, pour it over the small squares of tongue and allow the sauce to cool
8. Ensure the tongue is well covered with sauce
9. Skewer each small piece with an atelet, ensuring the pieces are thoroughly coated with sauce
10. Make sure all pieces are the same size so the atelet presents a long square with four equal angles
11. Fill any gaps with sauce, smoothing the atelet
12. Dip it in warm butter
13. Roll it in breadcrumbs, then in beaten eggs mixed with a little butter, salt and coarse pepper
14. Bread it while maintaining its square shape
15. Grill it on three sides, browning the fourth side with a camp oven or ice shovel
16. If you lack Italian sauce, use hachée sauce instead

title: Langue de Bœuf en Brésoles (Beef Tongue in Thin Slices)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- salt
- coarse pepper
- fine oil
- white wine
- coulis
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook the tongue slightly more than halfway in water
2. Remove the skin and cut it into thin slices
3. Arrange the slices in a saucepan with finely chopped parsley, chives, and mushrooms
4. Season with salt, coarse pepper, and fine oil
5. Cook over very low heat
6. When it begins to boil, add a glass of white wine
7. When cooked, degrease it and add a little coulis
8. When serving, if it is not sufficiently sharp, add lemon juice

title: Langue de Bœuf en Cartouches (Beef Tongue in Cartouches)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef tongue
- sauce à papillotes
- thin slices of lard
- oiled paper

## Directions

1. Prepare the tongue as you would for cornichons
2. When the tongue is cooked and cold, cut it into small long squares of four inches by eight or ten lines
3. Arrange the pieces on a plate and cover with sauce à papillotes
4. When the sauce is cooled, take a piece of tongue, surround it with sauce and cover it with a very thin slice of lard
5. Take a square of oiled paper and wrap the piece of tongue in it so that it has the shape of a cartouche
6. Grill it over a gentle fire
7. Arrange on the plate in a roof shape or in a pile
8. Do not use sauce; be careful to seal the paper hermetically so the sauce does not escape

title: Langue de bœuf en gratin (Beef Tongue au Gratin)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- parsley
- chives
- five or six tarragon leaves
- three shallots
- capers
- anchovies
- half a handful of breadcrumbs
- butter, size of half an egg

## Directions

1. Cook the beef tongue in a pot after blanching it
2. When cooked, remove the skin and let it cool
3. Cut it into slices
4. Chop parsley, chives, five or six tarragon leaves, three shallots, capers and one anchovy
5. Take half a handful of breadcrumbs, which you mix with butter the size of half an egg, and part of what you have chopped, to put everything together in the bottom of the dish
6. Arrange half of the tongue

title: Langue de Bœuf en Gratin (Beef Tongue au Gratin)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef tongue
- parsley
- chives
- five or six tarragon leaves
- three shallots
- capers
- one anchovy
- half a handful of breadcrumbs
- butter the size of half an egg
- salt
- coarse pepper
- three or four spoonfuls of bouillon
- half a glass of wine

## Directions

1. Blanch the beef tongue and cook it in the pot
2. When cooked, remove the skin and let it cool
3. Slice the tongue
4. Chop together parsley, chives, tarragon, shallots, capers, and anchovy
5. Mix the breadcrumbs with butter and part of the chopped herbs
6. Place this mixture in the bottom of the dish
7. Arrange half of the tongue slices on top
8. Season with salt and coarse pepper, and add the remaining herbs on top
9. Arrange a second layer of the remaining tongue slices
10. Season with salt and coarse pepper
11. Moisten with three or four spoonfuls of bouillon and half a glass of wine
12. Boil until a gratin forms on the bottom of the dish
13. When serving, add a little bouillon so it shows a small sauce

title: Langue de Bœuf en Papillotes (Beef Tongue in Papillotes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef tongue
- sauce à papillotes
- thin slices of lard
- double oiled paper
- clear jus

## Directions

1. When your tongue is cooked as previously described, cut it into pieces in the shape of cutlets
2. Arrange the pieces on a plate and pour sauce à papillotes over them
3. When the sauce is cold, coat the pieces of tongue with it
4. Place a thin slice of lard on top and underneath
5. Take a double square of oiled paper and wrap the piece of tongue in it
6. Pleat all around the edges as tightly as possible so the sauce does not escape during grilling
7. Use a gentle fire to grill these papillotes
8. Arrange them around the edge of the plate and put clear jus underneath

title: Langue de bœuf en paupiette (Beef Tongue in Roulade)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- water
- broth matching the piece of beef
- thin slices of veal or other meat farce
- thickness of a small coin
- egg
- small strips of lard for barding
- breadcrumbs
- piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the core from a beef tongue and blanch it for half a quarter hour in salted water
2. Put it afterwards to cook in the broth matching the beef piece, until the skin can be removed
3. Remove the skin and let it cool
4. Cut it into thin slices across its entire width and length
5. Cover each slice with a farce of veal or other meat farce, the thickness of a small coin
6. Pass a knife dipped in egg over the farce
7. Roll them up and thread them onto a skewer, after placing on each a small strip of lard
8. Cook them on the spit
9. When they are almost cooked, throw breadcrumbs on the lards, give them a golden color over clear fire
10. Serve with a piquant sauce underneath

title: Langue de Bœuf à la Persillade (Beef Tongue with Parsley Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- lard
- bouillon
- salt
- coarse pepper
- vinegar
- parsley

## Directions

1. Blanch the tongue for a quarter hour in boiling water
2. Lard it with fat lard
3. Cook it in the pot with beef
4. When cooked, remove the skin
5. Split it slightly more than halfway down its length to open it in two without separating it
6. Serve with bouillon, salt, coarse pepper, a dash of vinegar if desired, and chopped parsley

title: Langue de Veau (Veal Tongue)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal tongue
- Small skewers

## Directions

1. If it is to make a large relief course or other large piece, cut it at the second cutlet down to the joint of the knuckle
2. Remove the knuckle and the thick muscle so your piece cooks about evenly throughout
3. Secure it with small skewers that go from the flank to the filets
4. Pass the spit from the rib to the thick end near the knuckle

title: Langue à l'Écarlate (Scarlet Tongue)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef tongue
- two ounces of saltpeter
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- peppercorns
- two large handfuls of salt
- two carrots
- two onions
- three cloves
- two pints of water

## Directions

1. Pound two ounces of saltpeter
2. Rub the beef tongue thoroughly all over with the pounded saltpeter
3. Place the tongue in a terrine with thyme, bay leaf, basil, and peppercorns
4. Dissolve two large handfuls of salt in boiling water
5. When the salt is dissolved and the water is cold, pour it over the tongue
6. Leave the tongue in the brine for five or six days or longer
7. If you have time, soak the tongue for two hours before cooking
8. Blanch the tongue
9. Place it in a braise pan with a quarter of the brine, thyme, bay leaf, basil, peppercorns, two carrots, two onions, three cloves, two pints of water, and salt
10. Simmer gently for two hours
11. Remove from heat and allow to cool in its seasoning

title: Langues au Gratin (Sheep's Tongues Gratin)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's tongues
- bouillon
- half a glass of white wine
- bouquet of parsley
- chives
- half a bay leaf
- thyme
- half a clove of garlic
- two cloves
- coulis
- breadcrumbs
- butter or lard
- two raw egg yolks
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the tongues with a little bouillon, half a glass of white wine, a bouquet of parsley, chives, half a bay leaf, a little thyme, half a clove of garlic, and two cloves
2. Bring to boil for half an hour over low heat
3. Prepare the gratin: in a dish put at the bottom breadcrumbs, a piece of butter or lard, two raw egg yolks, parsley, chives, chopped very fine
4. Add a little coulis or one spoonful of bouillon, salt, and pepper; mix everything together
5. Put the dish on some hot ashes until the farce attaches itself to the bottom
6. Then drain off the butter; wipe the edges of the dish
7. Serve the tongues on top with their sauce

title: Langues de Mouton aux navets (Mutton Tongues with Turnips)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fifteen mutton tongues
- Turnips

## Directions

1. Prepare the tongues and cook them as described above
2. At the moment of serving, drain them
3. Arrange them around the platter
4. Cover them with the turnips

title: Langues de Mouton braisées (Braised Mutton Tongues)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fifteen mutton tongues
- Lard bardes
- Four carrots
- Four onions
- One onion stuck with two cloves
- Some veal slices
- Two bay leaves
- Thyme
- A bouquet of parsley and chives
- Medium lardons
- Broth or water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Put mutton tongues to soak to disgorge them
2. Rub and wash them carefully to remove clotted blood
3. Blanch them for one and a half quarter hours
4. Refresh them, drain them, dry them, and cut the cornet
5. You may lard them with medium, seasoned lardons
6. Put lard bardes, four carrots cut into pieces, four onions (one of which is stuck with two cloves), some veal slices, two bay leaves, a little thyme, and a bouquet of parsley and chives in a saucepan
7. Place your tongues on this seasoning
8. Cover them with lard bardes
9. Moisten them with broth
10. Braise them gently for five hours
11. If you have neither veal nor broth, arrange them as described and moisten with water and salt instead
12. Be careful that the liquid is not too abundant so the tongues develop flavor

title: Langues de Mouton en Cannelon (Lamb Tongues as Cannelons)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two lamb tongues, cooked in water
- three egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- forcemeat

## Directions

1. Cut the two cooked lamb tongues into five or six pieces of equal size and in their length
2. Take them and mix them with three egg yolks, let them drain on a cloth, and dry them
3. Add a little of this forcemeat and roll them in a strip of bread
4. Dip them in egg and breadcrumbs, cook them to a beautiful golden color, and serve them with a sauce

title: Langues de Mouton en Papillottes (Lamb Tongues in Paper)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb tongues, cooked and peeled
- salt
- coarse pepper
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- a pinch of garlic
- half a lemon, cut in slices
- fine oil
- lard strips

## Directions

1. After the tongues are cooked in water and cleaned of their skins, marinate them with salt, coarse pepper, parsley, chives, mushrooms, a pinch of garlic (all chopped), half a lemon cut in slices, and fine oil
2. Place each tongue half with all the seasoning in white paper rubbed with oil, with lard strips above and below
3. Fold the paper all around so nothing escapes and cook on the grill over very low heat
4. Serve with the paper

title: Langues de Mouton en Sur-tout (Lamb Tongues in Sur-tout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- white wine
- good broth
- onions
- shallots
- chives
- mushrooms
- parsley
- basil powder
- coarse pepper
- three lamb tongues, cooked in water
- thin bread slices

## Directions

1. Put in a casserole a piece of good soft butter the size of an egg with a good pinch of flour
2. Moisten with a glass of white wine and good broth
3. Add chopped onions, shallots, chives, mushrooms, and parsley, with basil powder and coarse pepper
4. Make this sauce thicken on the fire, boiling until it is thick
5. Have three lamb tongues cooked in water, cut them into thin fillets
6. In the dish you must serve, put a layer of onion at the bottom, then a layer of tongues with sauce, continuing to alternate layers of tongues and sauce, finishing with onion
7. Surround all the sides of your meat with small bread slices cut neatly, panaded with sauce
8. Glaze it in the oven if not too much, wipe the edges, and serve

title: Langues de Mouton à la Bourgeoise (Sheep's Tongues Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three sheep's tongues
- good bouillon
- two spoonfuls of coulis
- breadcrumbs
- one glass of white wine
- parsley
- chives
- one clove of garlic
- mushrooms
- a small piece of butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Blanch three sheep's tongues; after having them cooked in water, split them in half without separating them
2. Put them in a saucepan with good bouillon and two spoonfuls of coulis; if you have no coulis, put about two spoonfuls of breadcrumbs in a little bouillon, bring to boil briefly and pass through a sieve pressing with a spoon
3. Add one glass of white wine, parsley, chives, one clove of garlic, mushrooms, all chopped very fine, a small piece of butter, salt, and pepper
4. Bring to boil for half an hour until the sauce is neither too thick nor too thin
5. Serve

title: Langues de Mouton à la Broche (Lamb Tongues on the Spit)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- four lamb tongues
- salt
- one onion studded with two cloves
- one carrot
- one parsnip
- coarse lard
- small lard
- paper
- three tablespoons of peas
- two tablespoons of verjuice
- a small piece of butter
- flour
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the four tongues in water with salt, an onion studded with two cloves, a carrot, and a parsnip
2. When almost cooked, remove the skin and lard them across with coarse lard, or better, cover all the top with small lard
3. Place them in a lardoir and attach to the spit, wrapped in paper
4. When cooked to a beautiful color, serve them with a sauce made as follows: put in a casserole three tablespoons of peas, two tablespoons of verjuice, a small piece of butter mixed with flour, salt, and coarse pepper, make it thicken on the fire and serve under the tongues

title: Langues de Mouton à la Cuisinière (Sheep's Tongues Cook's Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's tongues grilled
- good butter about the size of a small egg
- two raw egg yolks
- two spoonfuls of verjuice
- bouillon
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. After grilling the tongues, put in a saucepan a piece of good butter about the size of a small egg, two raw egg yolks, two spoonfuls of verjuice, a little bouillon, salt, pepper, and nutmeg
2. Turn it on the fire until it is thickened like a white sauce
3. Serve under the tongues

title: Langues de Mouton à la Flamande (Lamb Tongues Flemish Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two or three onions, cut in slices
- butter
- flour
- one glass of white wine
- half a glass of juice
- mushrooms
- two shallots
- parsley
- chives
- salt
- coarse pepper
- a dash of vinegar
- three lamb tongues, cooked in water

## Directions

1. Cut two or three onions in slices and pass them on the fire with butter until they begin to brown
2. Add a pinch of flour and moisten with a glass of white wine and half a glass of juice
3. Add mushrooms, two shallots, parsley, and chives (all chopped very fine), salt, coarse pepper, and a dash of vinegar
4. Boil everything together for half a quarter hour
5. Have three lamb tongues cooked in water, peel them, and split them in two without separating them
6. Put them in the sauce to boil together until they have taken on flavor and little sauce remains
7. Serve

title: Langues de Veau à la sauce piquante (Calf's Tongues with Piquant Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- eight calf's tongues
- medium lardons, well seasoned
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- thyme
- bay leaf
- one pot-spoon of bouillon
- piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Soak the tongues
2. Blanch them for about a quarter hour
3. Refresh them in cold water
4. Trim them
5. Lard them with well-seasoned medium lardons
6. Cook the tongues in a braise or cooking stock, or with a few carrots, onions, cloves, thyme, bay leaf in moderate amounts, and one pot-spoon of bouillon for three hours
7. Remove the skin from the tongues
8. Glaze them
9. Arrange them around the edge of the platter with an oval crouton in the center
10. Pour piquant sauce over the tongues

title: Langues à la Gasconne (Sheep's Tongues Gascon Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three sheep's tongues cooked in water
- coulis
- parsley
- chives
- half a clove of garlic
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut three cooked sheep's tongues into thin fillets
2. Have a heat-proof dish and put at the bottom a little coulis with parsley, chives, half a clove of garlic, mushrooms, all chopped very fine, salt, and pepper
3. Arrange the tongue fillets on top and season them as well on top as underneath
4. Cover the entire top with breadcrumbs, and scatter small pieces of butter all over the breadcrumbs, about the size of peas, which will nourish the ragout and prevent the breadcrumbs from blackening in the heat
5. Put the dish on a small fire, cover it with a pie-dish lid and put fire on top
6. When it is a beautiful color, serve with light sauce

title: Langues à la Sainte-Menehould (Sheep's Tongues Sainte-Menehould)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's tongues cooked in water
- half a septier of milk
- piece of butter
- parsley
- chives
- one clove of garlic
- two shallots
- two cloves
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- roots and vegetables
- half a bay leaf
- thyme
- basilic
- half a clove of garlic
- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- vinegar

## Directions

1. After cooking the tongues in water, peel and split them in two without separating them
2. Put them to take flavor by boiling for half an hour with half a septier of milk, a piece of butter, parsley, chives, one whole clove of garlic, two whole shallots, two cloves, salt, and pepper
3. Remove the fine herbs and take the fattiest part of the cooking liquid to dip the tongues in it, then bread them with breadcrumbs
4. Grill them to a beautiful color
5. Prepare the piquant sauce: put in a saucepan vegetable scraps, onions, half a bay leaf, thyme, basilic, half a clove of garlic, a piece of butter
6. Pass them on the fire until they begin to take on a little color
7. Add a pinch of flour moistened with a little bouillon, one spoonful of vinegar, salt, and pepper
8. Bring the sauce to boil for a quarter hour, degrease it and pass through a sieve
9. Serve the grilled tongues with this piquant sauce

title: Lapereau (Young Rabbit)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Young rabbit
- Lard

## Directions

1. Skin and gut your young rabbit
2. Break the thigh bones
3. Sear it on the coals
4. Lard it or bard it
5. Place it on the spit as you would a young hare

title: Lapereau au Blanc (Young Rabbit in White Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Skinned and gutted young rabbit
- Butter
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Mushrooms
- Lemon
- Water
- Parsley
- Chives
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- Pepper
- Bacon

## Directions

1. Skin your young rabbit and gut it
2. Cut it into pieces like the previous recipe
3. Remove the liver and lungs
4. Wipe the pieces well so there is no blood, otherwise you would need to blanch it and that causes it to lose flavor
5. Put a quarter pound of butter in a casserole
6. Warm it and put your young rabbit inside
7. Brown it over high heat
8. When you see that all your pieces are stiffened, pour in two tablespoons of flour that you mix with your butter and rabbit
9. Add four tablespoons of bouillon
10. Stir your stew well until it boils
11. Add mushrooms that you have sautéed in water and lemon, a bouquet of parsley and chives, a bay leaf, a little thyme, a little pepper, and about half a pound of bacon that you have cut into small pieces

title: Lapereau en Caisse (Young Rabbit in Paper Cases)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Young rabbits
- Lard, grated, about the size of an egg
- Butter, twice the amount of lard
- Two spoonfuls of oil
- Mushrooms, chopped
- Shallot
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Aromatic spices, ground
- Nutmeg, grated
- Strong double paper cases
- Oil for coating
- Thin lard strips (bardes)
- Breadcrumb (mie de pain)

## Directions

1. Pluck, clean, and cut the young rabbits into equal pieces
2. Put in a saucepan lard about the size of an egg, twice as much butter, and two spoonfuls of oil
3. Wipe your rabbit pieces well to remove the blood
4. Melt the butter and put the rabbit in it
5. Brown it well
6. Add chopped mushrooms, shallot, finely chopped parsley, salt, coarse pepper, a little ground aromatic spices, and grated nutmeg
7. Stir everything together on the fire
8. When the young rabbit has stayed ten minutes on the fire, take a strong double paper case which you oil
9. Put in the bottom a very thin lard strip, and place your rabbit pieces with all their seasoning, another thin lard strip over the top
10. At the moment of serving, place your case on the grill over gentle fire and place a hot camp oven over it
11. When serving, either add a little Italian sauce inside your case, or before putting it on the fire, mask it with breadcrumb and brown it with the oven

title: Lapereau Sauté au Vin de Champagne (Young Rabbit Sautéed in Champagne Wine)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 young rabbit, dressed and cleaned
- Aromatic herbs, pounded
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- 1 small spoon of flour
- 1 glass of Champagne wine

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your young rabbit as in the previous recipe; add the same seasonings.
2. Pour in a small spoon of flour, which you blend with your young rabbit without letting it brown on the fire.
3. Also add about one glass of Champagne wine.
4. Place your casserole on the fire and stir so that your ragout thickens without boiling.
5. When you see your sauce thickened, serve your ragout. This should only be made at the moment of serving. Check that it is properly salted.

title: Lapereau à l'Espagnole (Young Rabbit à l'Espagnole)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 young rabbit, dressed and cleaned, cut into pieces
- 1/4 pound of butter
- 2 large spoonfuls of flour
- 4 pot spoons of bouillon
- Mushrooms, sautéed in water and lemon juice
- Parsley and chives
- 1 bay leaf
- Thyme
- Coarse pepper
- About 1/2 pound of lard, cut into small pieces and blanched
- 24 small, peeled onions of the same size
- 3-4 egg yolks for liaison
- Water (optional)
- Salt

## Directions

1. To avoid blanching the young rabbit and losing some of its flavor, put a 1/4 pound of butter in a casserole.
2. Heat it slightly and put your young rabbit in it.
3. Brown it over high heat.
4. When you see that all your pieces are stiffened, pour in about two large spoonfuls of flour, which you blend with your butter and rabbit.
5. Add about four pot spoons of bouillon.
6. Stir your ragout well until it boils.
7. Add mushrooms that you have sautéed in water and lemon juice, a bouquet of parsley and chives, a bay leaf, a little thyme, and a little coarse pepper.
8. Add about 1/2 pound of lard that you will cut into small pieces and blanch.
9. Cook your ragout over high heat so the liquid reduces.
10. When it is three-quarters cooked, degrease it and add twenty-four small, well-peeled onions of the same size.
11. When your ragout is cooked and reduced, degrease it and add a liaison of three or four egg yolks, depending on the size of your ragout.
12. You can moisten it with water if needed; then add salt.
13. Serve it very hot.

title: Lapereau à la Minute (Young Rabbit à la Minute)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 young rabbit, dressed and cleaned
- 1/4 pound of butter
- Aromatic herbs, pounded
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- A little grated nutmeg
- Parsley and shallots, finely chopped

## Directions

1. After dressing and cleaning the young rabbit, cut it into pieces and remove the lung. Dry the pieces well so no blood remains.
2. Put approximately 1/4 pound of butter in your casserole; when it is a little warm, put your young rabbit in it with a little pounded aromatic herbs, salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg.
3. Cook it over high heat; when your pieces are well stiffened, add a little finely chopped parsley and shallots.
4. Leave it another three or four minutes on the fire.
5. You can serve it coming straight from the casserole. Check that it is properly salted: ten minutes or at most a quarter hour is sufficient to cook your young rabbit.

title: Lapereaux au Gîte (Young Rabbits Braised)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two young rabbits
- Their livers
- A piece of butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- A glass of white wine
- Broth
- A bouquet garni
- Coulis

## Directions

1. Stuff the two young rabbits with their livers, a piece of butter, parsley, chives, and mushrooms (all chopped), salt, and coarse pepper
2. Sew them and truss the hind legs under the belly and the front legs under the nose
3. Insert skewers to hold them in place
4. Cook them with a glass of white wine, broth, a bouquet garni, salt, and coarse pepper
5. When they are cooked, pass the sauce through a sieve and degrease it
6. Add a little coulis and reduce to the consistency of a sauce
7. Dress the sauce over the rabbits as if they were au gîte.

title: Lapereaux aux Fines Herbes (Young Rabbits with Fine Herbs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Young rabbits
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- One clove of garlic
- A piece of butter
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Basil
- A pinch of flour
- A glass of white wine
- A little broth and jus
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbits into pieces and place them in a pot with parsley, chives, mushrooms, a clove of garlic (all chopped), a piece of butter, thyme, bay leaf, and basil (powdered)
2. Pass them over the fire
3. Add a pinch of flour
4. Moisten with a glass of white wine, a little jus and broth
5. Season with salt and coarse pepper
6. Cook and reduce to the consistency of a sauce
7. When ready to serve, take the livers that have cooked with the fricassee, crush them, and add them to the sauce.

title: Lapereaux en Bigarrures (Young Rabbits with Bread and Lard Stripes)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two young rabbits
- their livers
- scraped lard
- butter or beef marrow
- parsley
- chives
- savory
- salt
- coarse pepper
- three egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- small lard strips
- buttered paper
- verjuice

## Directions

1. Skin and empty one or two young rabbits
2. Chop the livers to make a forcemeat with scraped lard, a little butter or beef marrow, chopped parsley, chives, and savory, salt, and coarse pepper
3. Bind this forcemeat with three egg yolks and put it inside the rabbit body, sewing it closed
4. Cook them on the coals
5. Cut strips of breadcrumbs the length of the rabbits with as many strips of small lard, well interlarded
6. Cover the entire body of the rabbits with these strips, alternating bread and lard so only the head of the rabbits shows
7. Wrap them with two sheets of well-buttered paper and cook slowly on the spit
8. When cooked and the bread slightly browned, remove the paper and serve with the bread strips, small lard, and the cooking juices to which you add a dash of verjuice

title: Lapereaux en Hachis (Young Rabbits in Hash)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted young rabbits from the table
- some roasted mutton
- bones from the young rabbits
- butter
- shallots
- one half-garlic clove
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- two good pinches of flour
- one glass of red wine
- one glass of bouillon
- salt
- coarse pepper
- fried croutons

## Directions

1. Take leftover roasted young rabbits that have been served from the table, remove all the meat
2. Mix with a little roasted mutton and hash everything together
3. Take the bones from the young rabbits, cut them into small pieces, put them in a saucepan with a little butter, some shallots, one half-garlic clove, thyme, bay leaf, basil
4. Pass them over the fire and add two good pinches of flour
5. Moisten with one glass of red wine and as much bouillon
6. Let boil for half an hour over low heat
7. Pass the sauce through a sieve and put the hashed meat in it with salt, coarse pepper
8. Heat without boiling
9. Serve hot; if you wish, garnish around the hash with fried croutons like those for spinach

title: Lapereaux en Hatelet (Young Rabbits on Skewers)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Young rabbits
- A half glass of white wine
- Broth
- A bouquet garni
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Beaten egg
- Breadcrumbs
- Fat

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbits into pieces and cook them with a half glass of white wine, broth, a bouquet garni, salt, and coarse pepper
2. When they are cooked, reduce the sauce so that it attaches to the meat
3. Let cool
4. Thread them onto small skewers
5. Dip them in beaten egg
6. Bread them and dip them in fat again to bread them a second time
7. Grill them until they are a beautiful color
8. Serve dry with the skewers.

title: Lapereaux en Poupeton (Young Rabbits in Poupeton)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one young rabbit, cut into pieces
- veal sweetbread
- mushrooms
- beef lard for the poupeton mold

## Directions

1. Make a well-bound stew from a young rabbit cut into pieces with veal sweetbread and mushrooms
2. When finished and cold, prepare a forcemeat similar to that for veal loaf (see page 105)
3. Prepare your rabbit poupeton in the same manner as the veal loaf

title: Lapereaux en Tortue (Young Rabbits Shaped as Turtle)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- white wine
- bouillon
- parsley
- chives
- garlic clove
- cloves
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- coulis

## Directions

1. Empty a rabbit, leave the liver and remove the bitter part
2. Debone the rabbit only in the middle of the belly
3. Make a hole in the skin to pass the front half of the rabbit through it so it becomes inverted and takes the shape of a turtle
4. Tie it and cook with a glass of white wine, a little bouillon, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a garlic clove, two cloves, thyme, bay leaf, basil, salt, and pepper
5. When cooked, pass the sauce through a sieve, degrease it, and add a little coulis to bind it
6. Reduce to sauce consistency and serve over the rabbit

title: Lapereaux Roulés aux Pistaches (Young Rabbits Rolled with Pistachios)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one or two young rabbits
- their livers
- other cooked meat
- breadcrumbs
- milk
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- four egg yolks
- white wine
- bouillon
- bouquet garni
- coulis
- about twenty pistachios, blanched

## Directions

1. Debone one or two young rabbits
2. Make a forcemeat from their livers with some other cooked meat, breadcrumbs passed through milk, parsley, chives, mushrooms, salt, and pepper
3. Bind the forcemeat with four egg yolks
4. Spread this forcemeat on the rabbits, roll them, and tie with string
5. Cook them with a little white wine, bouillon, and bouquet garni
6. When cooked, degrease the sauce and pass through a sieve
7. Add a little coulis to bind it and reduce to sauce consistency
8. When serving on the rabbits, add about twenty blanched pistachios

title: Lapin en Galantine (Rabbit Galantine)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One rabbit, boned except for the head
- Flesh from the thighs of the rabbit
- Fillets of two other rabbits
- Lard, equal amount to the meat
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Aromatic herbs, pounded
- Truffles, chopped
- Medium lardons for larding
- Lardons of scarlet tongue
- Pieces of truffles
- Lardons
- Slices of bacon for larding
- Veal knuckle, cut into pieces
- Debris from the rabbits

## Directions

1. Bone the rabbit, except for the head
2. Remove the largest part of the flesh from the thighs to make room for stuffing
3. Lard the flesh with medium-sized lardons seasoned
4. Mince the flesh from the thighs and fillets of two other rabbits
5. Add an equal amount of lard and mince everything together
6. Add salt, coarse pepper, a little pounded aromatic herbs, and chopped truffles
7. Once the forcemeat is made, lay out the rabbit and season it
8. Put a layer of forcemeat on it, then add lardons of scarlet tongue, pieces of truffles, and lardons
9. Add another layer of forcemeat, continuing in this manner
10. Once the rabbit is well filled, give it its original shape
11. Cover it with slices of bacon
12. Tie it with string and wrap it in white linen, which you will tie again
13. Place slices of bacon in a braising pan
14. Place the rabbit in it along with a veal knuckle cut into pieces and the debris from your rabbits

title: Lapin en Gibelotte (Rabbit Stew)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Skinned and gutted rabbit
- Butter
- Flour
- White wine
- Bouillon
- Mushrooms
- Bacon
- Parsley
- Chives
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Salt
- Pepper
- Eels (optional)
- Small onions (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut your rabbit into pieces of equal size so they cook evenly
2. Put a quarter pound of butter in a casserole with two tablespoons of flour
3. Make a roux; when well browned, add the rabbit pieces to brown
4. Add half a bottle of white wine and about a bottle of bouillon
5. Stir your stew well until it boils
6. Add mushrooms, bacon that you have browned separately in a casserole, a bouquet of parsley and chives with some thyme and a bay leaf
7. Cook your stew over high heat so the braising liquid reduces
8. Add very little salt and some pepper
9. When three-quarters cooked, if desired, add eel sections and thirty small peeled onions that you have sautéed in butter over moderately high heat until well browned
10. Add the onions and eel at the same time
11. Take care to degrease your stew
12. Your sauce should be neither too thick nor too thin
13. Check that the seasoning is good
14. Remove the bouquet and serve your stew

title: Lapin en Matelote (Rabbit Sailor Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit
- one small spoonful of flour
- butter
- rabbit liver
- one glass of red wine
- two glasses of water
- bouillon
- bouquet of parsley
- chives
- one garlic clove
- two cloves
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- salt
- coarse pepper
- a dozen small onions
- one eel cut into sections (optional)
- whole capers
- one anchovy
- croutons fried in butter

## Directions

1. Cut a rabbit into pieces
2. Make a small roux with one small spoonful of flour and a piece of butter
3. Put the rabbit pieces with the liver in it, pass them over the fire and moisten with one glass of red wine, two glasses of water and bouillon
4. Add a bouquet of parsley, chives, one garlic clove, two cloves, thyme, bay leaf, basil, salt, coarse pepper
5. Cook over low heat; half an hour later add a dozen blanched small onions
6. If you wish to add an eel cut into sections, add it only when the rabbit is three-quarters cooked
7. Before serving, remove the bouquet, degrease the sauce and add a good handful of whole capers and one chopped anchovy
8. Serve with croutons fried in butter; pour the sauce over all

title: Lapin Farci Glacé (Stuffed Glazed Rabbit)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbit
- Lard and meat
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Aromatic spices, ground
- Truffles, chopped
- Lardons of scarlet tongue
- Lard strips (bardes)
- Calf knuckle (jarret de veau)
- Two carrots
- Three onions
- Two cloves
- Two bay leaves
- Thyme
- Parsley and chives bouquet
- Half-bottle white wine
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Chop together more lard than meat; add salt, coarse pepper, a little ground aromatic spices, and chopped truffles
2. Extend the rabbit and season it; place a layer of stuffing, and over it lardons of scarlet tongue, pieces of truffles, and lardons
3. Place another layer of stuffing and continue thus
4. Once the rabbit is well filled, give it its original form, cover it with lard strips, tie it, and wrap it in white linen, tying it again
5. Place lard strips in a braising pan; put your rabbit in it with the calf knuckle cut in pieces along with rabbit trimmings, two carrots, three onions (one studded with two cloves), two bay leaves, a little thyme, a bouquet of parsley and chives, half a bottle of white wine, one spoonful of bouillon, and a little salt
6. Simmer your rabbit for two hours over very low heat
7. When cooked, remove the braising pan from the fire; after half an hour, carefully remove your rabbit without breaking it
8. Let it cool in its linen
9. Serve it glazed or with breadcrumb coating
10. If you need aspic for decoration, pass the cooking liquid through a cloth, clarify it like aspic, let the aspic cool, and serve it with your rabbit

title: Lapins au Coulis de Lentilles (Rabbits with Lentil Coulis)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- rabbits
- good bouillon
- small lard
- bouquet garni
- salt
- pepper
- one litron of lentils
- bread

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbits into pieces and cook them with good bouillon, small lard and a bouquet garni, salt, and very little pepper
2. Also cook one litron of lentils à la reine with bouillon, without salt
3. When the lentils are cooked, pass them through a sieve with their bouillon
4. Remove the bread and small lard from its cooking, then reduce it on the fire until you judge it sufficiently bound to serve
5. Heat the rabbit and small lard in it
6. Serve if it is of good flavour in a tureen

title: Lapins en Papillotes (Rabbits in Papillote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One tender young rabbit
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- A pinch of garlic
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Fine oil
- Lard
- White paper
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut the young rabbit into pieces
2. Marinate it with parsley, chives, mushrooms, a pinch of garlic (all chopped), salt, coarse pepper, and fine oil
3. Wrap each piece with its seasoning and a small piece of lard in white paper
4. Butter or brown the paper on the outside
5. Cook over very low heat on the grill, placing another greased sheet of paper underneath
6. Serve with the paper.

title: Lapins à la Bourgeoise (Rabbits in Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one rabbit, cut into pieces
- butter
- mushrooms
- blanched artichoke hearts
- flour
- bouillon
- white wine
- salt
- pepper
- three egg yolks
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the rabbit into pieces and put in a saucepan with a piece of butter, a bouquet garni of mushrooms and blanched artichoke hearts
2. Pass everything over the fire, add a pinch of flour, moisten with bouillon and a glass of white wine, season with salt and pepper
3. When cooked and sauce has reduced, add a liaison of three egg yolks diluted with bouillon
4. Add a little chopped parsley and serve seasoned to taste

title: Leg of Mutton in a Pan (Gigot de mouton à la poêle)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one leg of mutton, aged
- lard
- parsley
- chives
- mushroom
- garlic
- salt
- pepper
- slices of onions
- one glass of white wine
- coulis (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the leg of mutton lengthwise into slices about two fingers thick
2. Make four pieces from the leg
3. Lard all pieces with lard seasoned with parsley, chives, mushroom, a pinch of garlic, all chopped; salt and pepper
4. Line a casserole with a few strips of lard and slices of onions
5. Place the mutton pieces on top
6. Cover the casserole well and cook over very low heat in its own juice
7. Halfway through cooking, add one glass of white wine
8. When cooked, remove the fat from the sauce
9. If you have coulis, add a little to the sauce and serve with reduced sauce

title: Leg of Mutton in Water (Gigot de mouton à l'eau)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one leg of mutton
- lard and anchovy fillets (optional for larding)
- one chopine of water
- one chopine of broth
- parsley
- chives
- half a clove of garlic
- three shallots
- two cloves
- two onions
- one carrot
- one parsnip
- coulis (optional)

## Directions

1. Trim the shank of the leg by cutting the end so it is not so long
2. Lard the meat if desired with lard and some anchovy fillets; if you leave it without, tie it with string
3. Place it in a pot just fitted to its size with one chopine of water and as much broth
4. Bring to a boil and skim
5. Add a bouquet of parsley, chives, half a clove of garlic, three shallots, two cloves, two onions, one carrot, and one parsnip
6. When the leg is cooked, pass the broth through a sieve and remove the fat
7. Place it on the fire to reduce it until it becomes a glaze like a fricandeau
8. Put this glaze on top of the leg
9. Then put a few spoonfuls of broth in the pot to detach what remains
10. If you have some coulis, use it in place of the broth
11. Pass it through a sieve and serve under the leg

title: Leg of Mutton with Cauliflower (Gigot aux choux-fleurs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one leg of mutton
- cauliflowers
- butter
- salt
- coulis
- coarse pepper
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the leg in the same manner as the English style
2. Once dressed on the serving platter, arrange cauliflowers all around that you have blanched for a moment in boiling water, then placed in another boiling water to cook them with a piece of butter and salt
3. When the cauliflowers are cooked and well drained, arrange them neatly around the leg, flower side up
4. Cover with a good sauce made with ordinary coulis, a piece of butter, salt, and coarse pepper
5. Thicken on the fire
6. When serving, add a small splash of vinegar

title: Leg of Mutton with Cauliflower Glazed with Parmesan (Gigot aux choux-fleurs glacés de parmesan)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one leg of mutton
- cauliflowers
- butter
- salt
- coulis
- coarse pepper
- grated parmesan

## Directions

1. Cook the leg and cauliflowers in the same manner as above, with less salt
2. When everything is cooked, take the serving platter and put a little of the same sauce on it
3. Cover the sauce with grated parmesan, sprinkle the entire top with the rest of the same sauce, and over the sauce add parmesan
4. Place your plat on a mild stove, cover it with a pastry lid and cover until it is a beautiful golden color with reduced sauce
5. Before serving, wipe the edges of the platter and drain off the fat found on top of the sauce

title: Leg of Mutton with Cornichons (Gigot aux cornichons)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one leg of mutton
- broth
- water
- parsley
- chives
- one clove of garlic
- three cloves
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- two onions
- two carrots
- one parsnip
- salt
- pepper
- cornichons

## Directions

1. Place a leg of mutton to cook in a pot just fitted to its size with a little broth and water, a bunch of parsley, chives, one clove of garlic, three cloves, thyme, bay leaf, basil, two onions, two carrots, one parsnip, salt, and pepper
2. When cooked, pass the sauce through a sieve and remove the fat
3. Reduce the sauce to a glaze
4. Put it all over the leg
5. Serve underneath a ragout of cornichons made by cutting cornichons into two or three pieces according to their size, scrape them a little and blanch them for a moment in boiling water to remove the strength of the vinegar, and put them in a bound sauce

title: Leg of Mutton à l'Anglaise (English Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one leg of mutton
- coarse lard
- broth
- parsley
- chives
- one good clove of garlic
- three cloves
- one bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- one glass of broth
- nearly as much coulis
- capers
- one anchovy
- parsley
- chives
- one shallot
- one hard egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cut the shank and skin a little on the joint bone so you can bend the shank without disfiguring the leg
2. Lard the whole leg across with coarse lard
3. Tie the leg and place it in a pot just fitted to its size with broth, a bouquet of parsley, chives, one good clove of garlic, three cloves, one bay leaf, thyme, basil, salt, and pepper
4. When cooked, drain it and wipe the fat from it with a cloth
5. For the sauce: put one glass of broth and nearly as much coulis in a casserole, with capers, one anchovy, a little parsley, chives, one shallot, and one hard egg yolk, all chopped very fine
6. Boil for two or three minutes
7. Serve on the leg

title: Leg of Mutton à la Génoise (Genoese Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one good aged leg of mutton
- celery, half-cooked in embers or broth
- cornichons
- tarragon branches, blanched
- lard
- anchovy fillets

## Directions

1. Remove the skin of the leg without detaching it from the shank
2. Lard all the meat with celery that has been half-cooked in embers or broth, cornichons cut into thick strips, some blanched tarragon branches, lard, and anchovy fillets, all lightly seasoned
3. Replace the skin over the top so that nothing shows
4. Secure it with string to prevent it from shrinking during cooking
5. Cook your leg at the hearth as usual
6. Serve with a sauce in which you add a little shallot

title: Leg of Mutton à la Royale (Royal Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one leg of mutton
- veal sweetbread
- mushrooms
- small eggs
- rice

## Directions

1. Braise one leg of mutton in the same manner as the one with cauliflowers
2. Serve on top a ragout mixed with veal sweetbread, mushrooms, and small eggs
3. For the ragout, have veal sweetbread soak in tepid water

title: Leg of Mutton à la Régence (Regency Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one leg of mutton
- coarse lard
- salt
- fine spices
- fine herbs

## Directions

1. Cut the leg of mutton across into three or four pieces
2. Lard each piece with coarse lard seasoned with salt, fine spices, and chopped fine herbs
3. Cook them in the same manner as beef à la royale found on page 54
4. Serve hot as an entrée or cold as a side dish

title: Lentilles Fricassées (Fricasseed Lentils)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Roux
- Herbs or onion, diced
- Bouillon or water
- Lentils
- Salt
- Black pepper

## Directions

1. Make a light roux.
2. Add fine herbs or onion cut into small dice.
3. Pass them through the roux and moisten with a little bouillon or water.
4. When it is diluted, add the lentils with salt and black pepper.
5. Serve them very hot.

title: Lentilles à la Maître-d'Hôtel (Lentils Maître-d'Hôtel)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Lentils
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives, chopped
- Salt
- Black pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the lentils and drain them.
2. Put them in a saucepan with a good piece of butter, parsley, finely chopped chives, salt, and black pepper.
3. Sauté everything together.
4. Serve them very hot.

title: Levraut (Young Hare)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Young hare
- Hare's blood
- Lard
- Hare's liver
- Butter
- Poivrade

## Directions

1. When skinned, break the thigh bones and gut it
2. Flatten the stomach by striking it on the back near the shoulders
3. Sear it on the coals
4. Rub it with its blood, lard or bard it
5. Pass the spit from the tail to the head
6. Chop the raw liver, pass it through butter, add poivrade to it
7. Strain through cheesecloth pressing with a wooden spoon
8. Pour this sauce in a sauce boat or underneath your hare

title: Levraut au Sang (Young Hare in Blood Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one young hare with blood reserved
- coarse lard for larding (optional)
- hare liver
- butter, the size of an egg
- bouquet of parsley
- chives
- one garlic clove
- two shallots
- three cloves
- one bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- a good pinch of flour
- three glasses of bouillon
- one half-setier of red wine
- one spoonful of vinegar
- salt
- coarse pepper
- fine capers

## Directions

1. When plucking and cleaning a young hare, be careful not to lose the blood; set it aside
2. Cut the hare into pieces and lard with coarse lard if desired
3. Put it in a saucepan with the liver and butter the size of an egg, a bouquet of parsley, chives, one garlic clove, two shallots, three cloves, one bay leaf, thyme, basil
4. Pass over the fire and add a good pinch of flour
5. Moisten with three glasses of bouillon, one half-setier of red wine, one spoonful of vinegar, salt, coarse pepper
6. Let boil until the young hare is cooked and a little sauce remains
7. Take the cooked liver, crush it well and mix with the blood you kept
8. When ready to serve, add the blood to make it bind on the fire without boiling, like an egg yolk liaison
9. Then add a half-handful of whole fine capers
10. Serve hot

title: Limandes sur le Plat (Flounder on a Platter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Flounder
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Nutmeg, grated
- Breadcrumbs
- White wine or water

## Directions

1. Empty and clean your flounder
2. Melt a piece of butter on your platter
3. Add a little parsley and chives, salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
4. Arrange your flounder on your platter
5. Add seasoning
6. Drizzle with a glass of white wine or water
7. Cover with breadcrumbs
8. Place on the stove with a camp oven over it

title: Lièvre en Civet (Hare in Wine Stew)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- small pieces of bacon
- hare, cut into pieces
- white or red wine, or vinegar
- bouillon or water
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaves
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- small onions

## Directions

1. Make a roux with more butter than is ordinarily used
2. When it is three-quarters made, add pieces of small bacon and make them turn in your roux over high heat for a moment
3. Then add your hare cut into pieces and when they are well browned, add a bottle of white or red wine, or a glass of vinegar according to taste
4. Finish moistening your civet with bouillon if you have it, or water
5. Add salt, taking care that your small bacon should give salt to your stew
6. Add pepper, two bay leaves, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and mushrooms
7. Your stew must be bathed, that is to say your meat must swim in the sauce
8. Cook it over high heat so that your liquid reduces to three-quarters
9. Have small onions, well peeled and all equal in size, that you will sauté in butter over high heat
10. When they are well browned and your civet is almost cooked, put them in
11. After a quarter hour and a half, remove your civet from the fire
12. Taste to see if it has good seasoning
13. Keep it warm on hot ashes until the moment of serving

title: Lièvre en Daube (Braised Hare)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One hare, plucked and cleaned
- Medium lardons, well seasoned with crushed aromatics, salt and pepper
- Lard strips
- One veal shank, cut into pieces
- Bouquet of parsley and chives
- Three laurel leaves
- Bouquet of thyme
- Three carrots
- Four onions
- Three cloves
- Bouillon or water
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. When your hare is plucked and cleaned, lard it with well-seasoned medium lardons flavored with crushed aromatics, salt, and pepper.
2. When the thighs and fillets are well larded, place some lard strips in a braising pan or daubiére and the hare on top.
3. Place a veal shank cut into pieces around it.
4. Cover it with lard strips.
5. Add a bouquet of parsley and chives, three laurel leaves, a bouquet of thyme, three carrots, four onions, and three cloves.
6. Moisten it with bouillon; if you have none, use water, salt, and pepper.
7. Simmer for two hours according to the quality of the hare.
8. At the moment of serving, drain it, glaze it, and reduce the braising liquid to three quarters.
9. Pass it through a silk sieve and serve it underneath the hare.

title: Lièvre en Haricot (Hare Stew)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare
- Hare liver
- Butter
- Bouquet of parsley
- Chives
- Clove of garlic
- Three cloves
- Two shallots
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Flour
- White wine
- Vinegar
- Water or bouillon
- Turnips
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Skin and empty a hare, keep its liver after removing the bitter part, cut it into pieces and place everything in a saucepan with a piece of butter, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a clove of garlic, three cloves, two shallots, a bay leaf, thyme, and basil.
2. Pass it over the fire and add a full tablespoon of flour, moisten with half a septier of white wine, two spoonfuls of vinegar, two or three glasses of water or bouillon.
3. Cook for one hour, then have turnips cut neatly, blanch them for half a quarter hour in boiling water and put them to cook with the hare.
4. Season with salt and coarse pepper, finish cooking and serve at short sauce.
5. Remove the bouquet and serve hot. If the hare is tender, the turnips should be added at the same time.

title: Lièvre à la Broche (Roasted Hare on the Spit)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Skinned and gutted hare
- The hare's blood
- Hare liver
- Butter
- Shallots
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Flour
- White wine
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. When your hare is skinned and gutted, sear it on a hot stove
2. The meat must be somewhat firm so the larding needle enters more easily
3. Remove from the stove and dip your hand in the hare's blood
4. Pass your hand over the back and thighs
5. Lard the hare from the neck to the end of the thighs
6. Leave a distance of one inch between the loins and the thighs
7. Roast on the spit for one hour to cook the young hare
8. For the sauce: pound the raw liver with the back of your knife
9. Pass it through with a small piece of butter, a little shallots, parsley branches, a little thyme and bay leaf
10. Put three-quarters of a tablespoon of flour that you have heated with your seasoning
11. Add a glass of white wine and two glasses of bouillon
12. Stir your sauce until it boils
13. Add salt and pepper, enough so that pepper dominates
14. Reduce your sauce to more than half
15. When reduced, pass it through a sieve while pressing lightly
16. Serve your sauce in a sauce boat beside your roasted hare dish
17. Alternatively, you can roast a young hare on the spit without larding it; instead bard it from the neck to the thighs

title: Lièvre à la Saint-Denis (Hare Saint-Denis Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One hare, plucked and cleaned
- Medium lardons, well seasoned with crushed aromatics, salt and pepper
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley sprigs
- Thyme
- Laurel
- Whole chives
- Two onions, sliced
- Hare liver, finely chopped
- Lard fat, the same amount as the liver, finely chopped
- Salt
- Crushed aromatics
- Coarse pepper
- Forcemeat for quenelles, twice the amount of the liver forcemeat
- Three egg yolks
- Lard strips
- Veal slices
- Two carrots, sliced
- Three onions
- Bouquet of parsley and chives
- Thyme
- Laurel
- Two cloves
- One bottle of good white wine
- Salt
- Espagnole sauce or light roux
- Butter
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. When you have plucked your hare, clean it and make the smallest possible hole in its belly.
2. Cut off its head.
3. Season medium lardons to lard the fillets and thighs.
4. Place it to marinate for two or three days with salt, fine pepper, parsley sprigs, thyme, laurel, whole chives, and two sliced onions.
5. When you wish to serve it, finely chop the liver and chop an equal amount of fat lard together.
6. Add a little salt, some crushed aromatics, and coarse pepper.
7. Mix this forcemeat with twice as much quenelle forcemeat and blend them together.
8. Add three egg yolks and mix thoroughly.
9. Place this forcemeat in the body of the hare.
10. Sew up the belly skin so the forcemeat does not escape.
11. Place lard strips in a braising pan and the hare on top.
12. Cover it with lard strips.
13. Arrange around it some veal slices, two sliced carrots, three onions, a bouquet of parsley and chives, thyme, laurel, two cloves, a bottle of good white wine, and a little salt.
14. Simmer your hare for two hours or more if it is tough.
15. At the moment of serving, drain it, glaze it, and reduce the braising liquid until almost glazed.
16. Add two spoonfuls of espagnole sauce.
17. Place your hare on a plate with the sauce underneath.
18. If it is too seasoned, add a piece of butter the size of an egg and lemon juice.
19. If you have no espagnole, make a light roux and moisten it with the braising liquid in which your hare cooked.
20. Your sauce should be quite thin so you can reduce it by more than half.
21. At the moment of serving, strain it through a sieve and check the salt seasoning.
22. You can wrap this hare in lard strips, buttered paper, and put it on a spit, serving a poivrade underneath.

title: Longe de Veau Étouffée (Braised Veal Loin)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One veal loin
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Three quarterons of butter

## Directions

1. Debone it in such a manner that you can give it a long square form
2. Season it with salt and coarse pepper inside
3. Tie it up
4. Place it in a braising pan without liquid, only with three quarterons of butter
5. Put it on the fire
6. When it is well heated, cover your braising pan and let it cook on a low fire, turning it from time to time
7. Three and a half hours are sufficient to cook a medium-sized loin
8. You can serve it as a remove for soup, glazing it and putting a glaze sauce underneath for a large piece, or letting it cool and decorating it with aspic

title: Macreuse au Haricot (Scoter Duck with Beans)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one macreuse (scoter duck)
- butter
- lean broth
- one glass red wine
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- one garlic clove
- two cloves
- savory
- turnips
- flour
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Pluck the macreuse and gut it, brown it over coals like poultry you wish to put on the spit
2. Cut it into four pieces and put it in a saucepan to cook over the fire with a little butter for one hour
3. Put it in a small pot with lean broth, a glass of red wine, salt, pepper, a bouquet of parsley, chives, one garlic clove, two cloves, and a little savory
4. Cook over low heat for four or five hours
5. Cut turnips neatly and blanch them for half an hour in boiling water
6. Make a small roux with flour and butter, moisten it with the cooking liquid from the macreuse, and cook the turnips in it
7. When the turnips and macreuse are well cooked, cut bread pieces the size of a small coin, cook them over the fire with a little butter until they are golden colored
8. Arrange the macreuse, turnips, and croutons on the platter, pour the well-degreased and well-seasoned sauce over them

title: Macreuse en Haricot (Scoter in Stew)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- scoter
- butter
- lean bouillon
- red wine
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- garlic
- cloves
- savory
- turnips

## Directions

1. Pluck a scoter and clean it, brown it over coals, as a fowl that you want to put on the spit; after having cut it in four, put it in a pot to pass over the fire with a little butter for an hour, next put it in a small pot with lean bouillon, a glass of red wine, salt, pepper, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a clove of garlic, two cloves, a little savory; cook over low heat for four or five hours, have turnips that you cut neatly.

title: Macédoine à la Béchamel (Vegetable Medley with Béchamel Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- turnips
- small onions
- peas
- asparagus
- white beans
- green beans
- artichoke hearts
- eggplants
- cauliflower
- cucumbers
- small fava beans
- béchamel sauce
- velouté or reduced cooking liquid
- three egg yolks
- butter
- bay leaf
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Prepare many small roots and vegetables in equal and uniform portions, as described for vegetable medleys in entrées: carrots, turnips, small onions, peas, asparagus, white beans, green beans, artichoke hearts, eggplants, cauliflower, cucumbers, small fava beans, and all sorts of roots and vegetables.
2. When the vegetables are cooked in appropriate seasoning, drain them until completely dry.
3. Put everything in a saucepan.
4. Make a somewhat reduced and bound béchamel sauce and pour it over your vegetable medley.
5. Toss to mix the sauce with the vegetables.
6. If you do not have béchamel, reduce velouté and add a liaison of three egg yolks.
7. Pass the velouté through a fine cloth.
8. Put on top an amount of butter the size of an egg.
9. If not using velouté, clarify the cooking liquid from your roots and make a white roux.
10. Moisten with the clarified cooking liquid.
11. Add a bay leaf and mushrooms.
12. Reduce your sauce so it is somewhat bound.
13. Add a liaison of three egg yolks and pass the sauce through a fine cloth over the vegetables, adding an amount of butter the size of an egg.
14. Toss the vegetables in the sauce so the vegetable medley is well coated.
15. Keep it hot without boiling.
