title: Macédoine à la béchamel (Vegetable Medley with Béchamel)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Small root vegetables and vegetables such as carrots
- Turnips
- Small onions
- Peas
- Asparagus
- White beans
- Green beans
- Artichoke hearts
- Eggplants
- Cauliflower
- Cucumbers
- Small broad beans

## Directions

1. Prepare many small roots and vegetables as described for macédoine in entrées
2. Cut carrots, turnips, small onions, peas, asparagus, white beans, green beans, artichoke hearts, eggplants, cauliflower, cucumbers, and small broad beans
3. Prepare in portions of approximately equal and uniform size
4. Cook the vegetables in appropriate seasoning
5. Drain the vegetables thoroughly until well drained

title: Manselle de Perdreaux (Partridge Manselle)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 partridges
- 6 shallots
- handful of parsley leaves
- coarse pepper
- 1 bay leaf
- 4 tablespoons of Spanish sauce
- 1/2 glass of white wine
- grated nutmeg
- 1/2 glass of bouillon
- 1 clove

## Directions

1. Spit-roast 4 partridges and piece them as for partridge salmi
2. Pound the scraps in a mortar with 6 shallots, a handful of parsley leaves, coarse pepper, and 1 bay leaf
3. Pour 4 tablespoons of Spanish sauce into a saucepan along with 1/2 glass of white wine, the pounded scraps, a little grated nutmeg, and 1/2 glass of bouillon
4. Reduce the whole to half; strain your sauce through fine cloth, pressing it through the cloth
5. Place it over your hot partridge limbs, keeping them warm without boiling
6. If you have no Spanish sauce, make a roux; add your pounded scraps, 1 glass of white wine, 2 glasses of bouillon, and 1 clove
7. Reduce the sauce, degrease it, and strain through fine cloth
8. Check the seasoning

title: Maquereau à la Maître d'Hôtel (Mackerel à la Maître d'Hôtel)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- mackerel
- butter
- parsley
- onion
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Grill the mackerel
2. Put inside the body butter mixed with chopped parsley and onion, salt, and coarse pepper

title: Maquereaux à la Maître-d'Hôtel (Mackerel with Maître d'Hôtel Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three mackerel
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Chives
- Parsley in branches
- Oil
- Butter
- Flour
- Water
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Empty the mackerel and remove the intestines by inserting the knife tip in the hole in the middle of the body
2. Wipe them with a damp cloth
3. Split them from the back side from head to tail
4. Place them on an earthenware platter
5. Add salt, coarse pepper, chives, and parsley in branches
6. Drizzle the mackerel with oil
7. Half an hour before serving, place them on the grill at a very gentle fire, being careful to turn them
8. At the moment of serving, arrange them on the plate
9. Make a maître-d'hôtel butter by placing a piece of butter in a saucepan with a spoonful of flour, parsley, finely chopped chives, salt, and pepper
10. Mix the flour with the seasoning
11. Add half a glass of water and lemon juice
12. Place your sauce on the fire and stir constantly
13. At the first boil, if it tastes good, pour it over your mackerel

title: Marinade Cuite (Cooked Marinade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- three carrots
- four onions
- two bay leaves
- thyme
- two cloves
- parsley in branches
- some chives
- two teaspoons of flour
- one glass of vinegar
- two glasses of broth
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Put a piece of butter in a saucepan
2. Cut three carrots into slices and four onions the same way
3. Add two bay leaves, a little thyme, and two cloves
4. Put your saucepan on the fire and pass your vegetables through
5. Add parsley in branches and some chives and pass them with your vegetables
6. Add two teaspoons of flour that you will mix with your butter
7. Add one glass of vinegar and two glasses of broth, salt, and pepper
8. Let your marinade simmer for three-quarters of an hour
9. Pass it through a hair sieve and use it as needed
10. If you have sauce, you can use it in place of flour

title: Marinade cuite (Cooked Marinade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of butter
- Three carrots, cut in slices
- Four onions, cut the same way
- Two bay leaves
- A little thyme
- Two cloves

## Directions

1. Put a piece of butter in a saucepan
2. Cut three carrots in slices and four onions cut the same way, add two bay leaves, a little thyme, and two cloves
3. Put your saucepan on the fire and cook your ingredients

title: Marmelade d'Abricots (Apricot Marmalade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Forty pounds of apricots
- Thirty pounds of sugar

## Directions

1. Clarify thirty pounds of sugar
2. When it reaches the hard ball stage, add the apricots cut into small pieces
3. Cook the marmalade until it falls in a bound state when placed on a skimmer or between the fingers
4. If desired, remove the skin from the apricots for clearer preserves
5. Let the marmalade boil in the sugar for a quarter hour
6. Pass it through a large horsehair sieve
7. Return it to the pot and give it the degree of cooking explained above
8. When the preserves are in pots and have cooled, cut rounds of paper the size of the pot opening
9. Soak them in brandy and apply them over the preserves
10. Cover with paper and tie with string

title: Marmelade d'Abricots sans Façon (Simple Apricot Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- six pounds of apricots
- four and a half pounds of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut as thinly as you can six pounds of not-too-ripe apricots and put them as you go into a clean pot. Crack the pits, remove their skin and cut them very fine to add with the apricots. Pound four and a half pounds of sugar to put with the apricots. Put your pot on a clear fire and stir constantly with a skimming spoon for fear the jam sticks to the bottom. When the apricots are well advanced in cooking, remove the pot from time to time to crush the pieces of apricots that do not turn to jam. Cook until it sticks to your fingers without much resistance, testing by taking some jam in your fingers and pressing them together. Then put it in pots. This method, though simple, is very good.

title: Marmelade d'Amandes Vertes et d'Abricots Verds (Green Almond and Green Apricot Marmalade)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Green almonds or green apricots
- Sugar (equal weight to cooked fruit)
- 1 half-setier water

## Directions

1. Remove the fuzz the same way as explained on page 545
2. Cook them in water until they are quite tender
3. Remove them in fresh water and let them drain
4. Crush them and pass them through a sieve
5. Put this marmalade on the fire to dry it, stirring constantly until it is ready to stick to the pan
6. Weigh this marmalade and put an equal weight of sugar on the fire with a half-setier of water
7. Bring to boil and skim
8. Continue boiling until, dipping two fingers in fresh water, you put them in the sugar and dip them again in water, and the sugar that remains on your fingers breaks clean
9. Add the marmalade at once and dilute it with the sugar without boiling on a gentle fire
10. Put it in pots afterwards

title: Marmelade d'Épine-Vinette (Barberry Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound and a half of sugar
- one pound of barberries
- one glass of water

## Directions

1. Cook one pound and a half of sugar in the same manner as for pear jam. Then add barberry prepared as follows: have one pound of barberries completely stripped of stems, which you put in a pan with one glass of water and bring to a boil until it turns to jam. Pass it through a sieve and press firmly until only the skins remain in the sieve. Put back on the fire what has passed through the sieve.

title: Marmelade d'Épine-Vinette (Barberry Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 pounds of sugar
- 1 pound of barberries, destemmed
- 1 glass of water

## Directions

1. Cook the sugar the same way as for pear marmalade
2. Prepare the barberries by placing them in a saucepan with a glass of water and boil until they become jam-like
3. Pass the mixture through a sieve and press it firmly until only the skins remain in the sieve
4. Return what has passed through the sieve to the fire and boil, stirring constantly until the marmalade is ready to stick to the pan
5. Mix the marmalade with the cooked sugar and stir together until ready to boil
6. Put into jars

title: Marmelade de Cerises (Cherry Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of sugar
- four pounds of cherries

## Directions

1. Cook two pounds of sugar in the same manner as for raspberry jam. Then add four pounds of cherries after removing their pits and stems. Stir them with the sugar and let them boil together until the syrup sticks to your fingers. Remove from the fire to put in pots.

title: Marmelade de Coings (Quince Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar, equal weight to the marmalade

## Directions

1. Cook the quinces in water until they are tender
2. Cool them in fresh water until completely cold
3. Cut them into quarters and remove the cores and skins
4. Crush them and pass through a sieve
5. Put what has passed through on the fire and stir constantly until the marmalade is thick
6. Weigh the marmalade and add an equal weight of sugar, cooking the same way as for pear marmalade
7. Mix the marmalade with the sugar together on the fire and remove when it begins to simmer
8. Put into jars

title: Marmelade de Fleurs d'Orange (Orange Blossom Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- five quarterons of sugar
- half a setier of water
- half a pound of orange blossoms
- alum
- juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Put five quarterons of sugar in a pan with half a setier of water. Bring to a boil and skim well. Continue boiling until dipping the skimming spoon in and shaking it over the sugar, blowing through the holes produces little sparks of sugar. Then add your orange blossom prepared as follows: take half a pound of peeled orange blossoms and boil for half a quarter hour in water. Remove this water from the fire and add a small pinch of alum. Put fresh water on the fire. When it boils, add the juice of one lemon, put your orange blossom in it, and remove with the skimming spoon letting it drain. Boil it in the lemon water until it is tender under the fingers. Then put it for half an hour in fresh water with a little lemon juice. Then press the orange blossom in a cloth to remove the water, and pound in a mortar to make jam. Put this jam in the sugar to blend them together, pouring the sugar several times over a very small fire, without letting it boil or even simmer. Put in pots and sprinkle a little fine sugar on top when cold.

title: Marmelade de Fraises (Strawberry Marmalade)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 half-pound strawberries
- 1 pound sugar
- 1 glass water

## Directions

1. Clean and wash a half-pound of strawberries
2. Let them drain and pass them through a sieve to make marmalade
3. Put on the fire a pound of sugar with a glass of water
4. Bring to boil and skim well
5. Continue boiling until, dipping the skimmer in it and shaking it, long sparks come out
6. Add your strawberry marmalade to dilute it with the sugar, stirring constantly on a medium fire without letting it boil
7. Put it in pots
8. You may regulate the quantity based on how much you wish to make

title: Marmelade de Framboises (Raspberry Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sugar
- two pounds of raspberries

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of sugar in the same manner as for strawberries. When it reaches the proper cooking point, add the raspberries prepared as follows: peel two pounds of raspberries and pass them through a sieve to make jam. Put this jam on the fire to dry it until it is nearly sticking to the pan. Then put it in the sugar and let it boil a few times while stirring constantly, and then put it in your pots.

title: Marmelade de Framboises (Raspberry Marmalade)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 2 pounds raspberries

## Directions

1. Cook a pound of sugar the same way as for strawberries
2. When it is at its cooking point, add the raspberries prepared as follows: clean two pounds of raspberries and pass them through a sieve to make marmalade
3. Put this marmalade on the fire to dry it until it is ready to stick to the pan

title: Marmelade de Poires (Pear Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- rousselet pears
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook in water until tender under the fingers, the quantity of rousselet pears you judge appropriate. Remove the skin and take only the flesh which you pass through a sieve. Put it on the fire and stir constantly until it is nearly sticking to the pan. Then weigh it and put as much sugar in a pan with half a setier of water. Bring to a boil and skim. Continue boiling until dipping the skimming spoon in it and shaking it, long sparks of sugar rise up that hold together. Add the jam to blend it with the sugar on the fire. When it begins to simmer, put it in pots, and when cold, put a little fine sugar on top.

title: Marmelade de Pommes (Apple Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- reinette apples
- sugar, equal weight to the marmalade

## Directions

1. Boil whole apples in water until they begin to soften under the fingers
2. Remove them to fresh water and peel them
3. Take the flesh and pass it through a sieve, pressing firmly
4. Put what has passed through in a pan and dry it on the fire until it is thick
5. Cook an equal weight of sugar to the large plume stage
6. Mix the marmalade and sugar together, stirring with a spatula or wooden spoon
7. Return to the fire only to heat through, stirring constantly
8. When it begins to boil, remove from the fire and put into jars when slightly cooled
9. Cover the jars only when they are completely cold

title: Marmelade de Prunes (Plum Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- plums
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from the plums you wish to use, take the quantity you judge appropriate. Boil them on the fire with a little water until they turn to jam. Pass them through a sieve and put back on the fire what you have passed through, and let it boil until this jam is nearly sticking to the pan. Then weigh it and put as much sugar by weight as you have jam. Put the sugar on the fire with a good half-setier of water. Bring to a boil and skim well. You will know when it is cooked by dipping two fingers in fresh water, then in the sugar, and then in the same water. If the sugar remaining on your fingers breaks cleanly, then add your jam to blend it with the sugar by stirring them together over the fire only until it simmers. Put in pots when cold and sprinkle a little fine sugar on top.

title: Marmelade de Pêches (Peach Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- peaches
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take peaches that are not too ripe. After removing the pits, cut them into small pieces. Then make this jam in the same manner as the simple apricot jam found previously on page 352.

title: Marmelade de Verjus (Verjuice Jam)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds of verjuice, almost ripe
- 1 pound of sugar per pound of marmalade

## Directions

1. Place the verjuice, with stems removed, in water ready to boil
2. When it is ready to boil, remove from the fire and cover to make it return to green color
3. Leave in the same water until it is cold
4. Remove and pass through a sieve, pressing firmly with a spoon to extract as much marmalade as possible
5. Put the marmalade in a pan and dry it on the fire until it is very thick
6. For each pound of marmalade, cook one pound of sugar to the large plume stage
7. Mix the marmalade into the sugar to blend well
8. Return to the fire only to heat through, stirring constantly until it is ready to boil
9. Put into jars

title: Marmelade de Verjus à la Bourgeoise (Verjuice Jam, Homestyle)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- verjuice, not quite ripe
- fine sugar, equal weight to the marmalade

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from the verjuice and place it in water that is on the fire ready to boil
2. When the verjuice begins to pale and has risen to the surface of the water, add a little fresh water and remove from the fire
3. Cover until it has returned to green color, and if it does not become green enough, leave it in the same water and heat it until it does
4. Drain and pass through a sieve, pressing firmly with a wooden spoon
5. Weigh the marmalade and put it in a pan with an equal weight of fine sugar
6. Boil together until, when you dip a finger in the marmalade and press it against another finger, the two fingers stick together without much resistance
7. Put into jars after it has cooled slightly

title: Marrons à l'Espagnole (Chestnuts Spanish Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- fifty chestnuts
- half a quarter pound of butter
- four spoonfuls of espagnole sauce
- two glasses of consommé
- bay leaf
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take fifty chestnuts (they should not be from Lyon, as they are not good for cooking).
2. Blanch them in hot water like almonds.
3. Take great care to remove all the second skin so they have no bitterness.
4. When well blanched, put them in a saucepan with half a quarter pound of butter, four spoonfuls of espagnole sauce, two glasses of consommé, a bay leaf, and a little grated nutmeg.
5. Boil the chestnuts for half an hour.
6. Then remove them from their sauce and put them in another saucepan.
7. Let the sauce reduce and pass it through a fine cloth over the chestnuts.
8. Keep them hot in a bain-marie.
9. At the moment of serving, arrange them on the dish with the sauce underneath.
10. You can also serve them with various sauces.

title: Massepains (Marzipan)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of sweet almonds, blanched and peeled
- Three egg whites for pounding
- Apricot marmalade or other non-liquid preserves
- Candied and pounded orange flowers
- Sugar powder
- Fine sugar
- Six egg whites
- Chopped green citron

## Directions

1. Take one pound of sweet almonds that you have blanched and peeled, pound them
2. While pounding, sprinkle them with three egg whites
3. Mix them then with apricot marmalade or other non-liquid preserves, candied and pounded orange flowers
4. When all is well mixed, place your almonds in a saucepan with sugar powder and dry them over the fire
5. Place them then on a table and work them with fine sugar
6. Add sugar until the paste no longer sticks to your hands
7. Roll it then to form marzipans of whatever shape you wish
8. Take six egg whites that you whip half-way and mix with chopped green citron
9. Dip the marzipans in this mixture and place them after in fine sugar as much as they can take
10. Arrange them on sheets of white paper, which you place on copper sheets, and bake them in the oven with gentle heat
11. To be sure of the oven temperature, put a little paste on a card; if the card takes on color, it is a sign that the oven is too hot

title: Matelote Vierge (Virgin Fish Stew)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish
- White wine
- Small onions
- Butter
- Flour
- Mushrooms
- Egg yolks
- Bread crusts
- Crayfish

## Directions

1. Cook the fish with white wine as in the previous recipe
2. Sauté small onions to white in butter
3. Mix in two spoonfuls of flour with the butter and onions
4. Pour the wine in which the fish cooked, passed through a sieve, over the onions
5. Stir the sauce until it boils
6. Add mushrooms and reduce by a third
7. Decrease the sauce and check the taste
8. Remove the onions and mushrooms with a slotted spoon and place on the fish
9. If the sauce is too thin, reduce it
10. Add a liaison of five egg yolks, or more if the stew is strong
11. Do not boil the sauce
12. When the liaison is in, strain it through cheesecloth over the fish
13. At the moment of serving, arrange on a plat with bread crusts sautéed in butter around it
14. Garnish the stew with crayfish

title: Matelote à la Marinière (Sailor-Style Fish Stew)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp, eel, pike, or barbel
- Small onions
- Butter
- Bay leaves
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- Thyme
- Quatre épices
- Salt
- Pepper
- Red wine
- Flour
- Bread crusts

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the fish
2. Cut into sections
3. Place in a casserole or cooking pot with small onions sautéed in butter, two bay leaves, a bouquet of parsley and chives, mushrooms, a little thyme, a little quatre épices, salt and pepper
4. Add enough red wine so the fish is submerged
5. Place over high heat
6. When the cooking liquid is reduced by a third, take a good piece of butter
7. Mix one or two spoonfuls of flour with the butter and add in small balls to the stew
8. Stir well so the butter and flour bind the sauce
9. Arrange the fish on a plat with bread crusts around and sauce on top

title: Mauviettes aux Fines Herbes (Larks with Fine Herbs)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Larks
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Pounded aromatic herbs
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Shallots, finely chopped
- Mushrooms, chopped
- Espagnole sauce
- Bouillon
- Flour
- White wine

## Directions

1. Pluck, truss and singe your larks; place a good piece of butter in a casserole with fifteen or eighteen larks, salt, coarse pepper, and a little pounded aromatic herbs; place them on a hot fire
2. Once you have sautéed them in your butter for seven or eight minutes, add about one spoonful of finely chopped parsley, the same amount of finely chopped shallots, and chopped mushrooms; sauté them with fine herbs for seven or eight minutes
3. Pour in about two skimming-spoonfuls of espagnole and one spoonful of bouillon; stir them in their sauce over the fire; at the first boil, remove them and serve them
4. If you do not have sauce, add one spoonful of flour, mix it well with your birds; add half a glass of white wine and as much bouillon; bring your sauce to a single boil; check that your ragout is properly seasoned

title: Mauviettes en Croustades (Larks in Pastry Cases)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Larks
- Forcemeat, cooked
- Nutmeg, grated
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Fine herbs
- Lard strips
- Butter
- Four spices
- Bread croutons
- Italienne sauce

## Directions

1. Debone twelve larks; take the interior, except the gizzard; chop it and mix this hash with an equal amount of cooked forcemeat, a little grated nutmeg, a little salt and coarse pepper, and a little fine herbs
2. Place this forcemeat on a silver platter, with a strip of lard on top; place it on a gentle stove with a camp oven over it; once your forcemeat is cooked, put it in a casserole
3. Pour in about two skimming-spoonfuls of velouté and mix well with your forcemeat; fill the interior of your larks with this; give them the shape of a small ball or an oval if you wish; sew them with thread so they do not lose their shape
4. Place a piece of butter in a casserole; warm it gently; add your larks with a little salt, coarse pepper, and a little quatre épices; place them on a gentle fire for seven or eight minutes
5. Once well stiffened, drain them; prepare croutons made with thick-cut bread about an inch and a half thick; make an incision inside at a line from the edge; pass them through butter; when they have a nice color, remove them from the butter and drain them on white linen; hollow out the bread; put some of your forcemeat inside, and your lark from which you have removed the thread; leave a little space above your forcemeat to add some sauce
6. Fifteen minutes before serving, place your croutons garnished with larks on a tourtière and put them in the oven or under a not-too-hot camp oven; at the moment of serving, arrange your croutons on the plate and put a little italienne sauce in each one

title: Maître-d'Hôtel Froide (Cold Maître-d'Hôtel Butter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- A quarter pound of butter
- A little parsley
- A little shallots
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put a quarter pound of butter in a saucepan
2. Add finely chopped parsley and shallots, salt, coarse pepper, and lemon juice
3. Mix everything together with a wooden spoon
4. At the moment of serving, serve your maître-d'hôtel on top, under, or inside meats or fish

title: Maître-d'Hôtel Liée (Bound Maître-d'Hôtel Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- A quarter pound of butter
- One teaspoonful of flour
- Parsley
- Chives
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Two spoonfuls of water
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. In your saucepan, put a quarter pound of butter, one teaspoonful of flour, finely chopped parsley and chives, salt, and coarse pepper
2. Add two spoonfuls of water
3. Put your sauce on the fire at the moment of serving
4. Work it like a white sauce
5. If it is too thick, add lemon juice with a little water
6. This sauce should be as thick as a white sauce

title: Meringues (Meringue Cookies)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- egg whites
- grated lemon zest
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Take egg whites according to the quantity you want to make (five are sufficient to make a small entremet dish)
2. Whip them to stiff peaks in a terrine
3. When well risen, add grated lemon zest and powdered sugar
4. Whip the egg whites a little more, mixing in the sugar and lemon zest
5. Pipe your meringues on a sheet of white paper in small mounds the size of half an egg, without them touching
6. Place a hot cover on top with a little ember on top to cook them
7. When cooked and golden colored, remove from the paper and take out the inside that is not cooked
8. Put a little preserve in its place
9. Glue two together one against the other
10. Serve them as dry as possible

title: Merlans Grillés (Grilled Whiting)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Whiting fish
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Oil
- Butter
- Capers
- Tomato sauce (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the whiting
2. Score them on both sides
3. Season with salt, coarse pepper, and oil
4. Half an hour before serving, place them on the grill over moderately hot fire
5. When grilled, mask them with butter sauce and sprinkle capers over them; you may also use tomato sauce

title: Merlans Grillés (Grilled Whiting)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Whiting
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Oil
- Butter sauce
- Capers
- Tomato sauce (optional)

## Directions

1. Empty and clean your whiting
2. Score them on both sides
3. Add salt, coarse pepper, and oil
4. Half an hour before serving, place them on the grill over a moderately hot fire
5. When grilled, mask them with a butter sauce and sprinkle capers over them
6. You may also use a tomato sauce

title: Merlans à la Bonne Eau (Whiting in Broth)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Whiting
- Parsley root or parsley leaves
- Chives
- Bay leaf
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. Empty your whiting and scrape them
2. Clean them with a damp cloth
3. Cut off the head at the trunk and the tail a little before
4. Place them in a saucepan
5. Add parsley root or parsley leaves, two or three whole chives, a bay leaf, salt, and water
6. Simmer for a good quarter hour
7. Arrange on the plate
8. Place a little of your broth in a saucepan, break several parsley leaves into it, and bring to a boil
9. Pour it over your whiting

title: Merluche à la Gascogne (Dried Cod Gascon Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- white dried cod
- river water
- fine oil
- good butter

## Directions

1. Before soaking, beat the dried cod well all over with a hammer to tenderize it
2. Soak for several days, changing the water
3. Cook briefly with river water
4. Remove and cut into pieces by flakes
5. Put the dried cod in a saucepan with fine oil and an equal amount of good butter
6. Serve with Gascon sauce

title: Merluche à la Gascogne (Stockfish in Gascon Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- merluche (stockfish)
- fine oil
- good butter
- coarse pepper
- garlic
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat the whitest stockfish well all over with a hammer to tenderize it
2. Soak it for several days, changing the water
3. Cook it briefly with river water
4. Remove and break into pieces by layers
5. Put the stockfish in a casserole with fine oil and as much good butter
6. Add coarse pepper, a little garlic and salt if it is too mild
7. Place the casserole on a stove, stirring constantly until the butter is combined with the oil
8. Eat it immediately as this sauce turns as it cools

title: Minced Mutton with Cucumbers
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Minced mutton
- Butter
- Cucumbers
- Spanish sauce (Espagnole)
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Grated nutmeg
- Croutons
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Heat butter in a pan
2. Add cucumbers and sauté constantly until they turn blonde in color
3. Drain the cucumbers
4. Mix in three spoonfuls of Spanish sauce
5. Cook until slightly thick
6. Place on the minced mutton with a little salt, coarse pepper, and grated nutmeg, keeping it warm
7. At the moment of serving, arrange the minced mutton on the plate and put croutons around it
8. If you do not have sauce, leave a little butter with the cucumbers and add nearly one spoonful of flour which you stir with them
9. Moisten with bouillon, salt, coarse pepper, and a dash of vinegar
10. When the cucumbers are sufficiently reduced, place them with the minced mutton

title: Minced Mutton with Fine Herbs
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Minced mutton
- Butter
- Shallots
- Mushrooms
- Parsley
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Coarse pepper
- Grated nutmeg
- Salt
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Prepare the hash as previously described
2. Take a piece of butter twice the size of an egg and one spoonful of finely chopped shallots
3. Pass them in the butter without letting them brown
4. Add four spoonfuls of finely chopped mushrooms
5. Pass them with the shallots
6. Add one spoonful of finely chopped parsley
7. Stir everything on the fire
8. If you do not have sauce, add one spoonful of flour and mix well with the fine herbs
9. Pour in about two glasses of bouillon
10. If the sauce is too thin, reduce it until slightly thick
11. Pour the sauce over the minced mutton
12. Add a little coarse pepper and grated nutmeg
13. Mix the sauce well with the minced meat
14. At the moment of serving, check the seasoning and put croutons around it

title: Minced Mutton with Gherkins in Anchovy Butter
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Minced mutton
- Spanish sauce (Espagnole)
- Anchovy butter
- Gherkins
- Flour
- Fond or bouillon
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Mince the meat as in previous minced preparations
2. Reduce six spoonfuls of Spanish sauce until two-thirds remains
3. Add a piece of anchovy butter about half the size of an egg
4. Stir the sauce so the butter melts
5. Pour it over the minced mutton, keeping it warm without boiling
6. Add eight or ten gherkins cut into quarters and mix with the minced mutton
7. At the moment of serving, arrange on the plate with croutons around it
8. If you do not have sauce, make a light roux and moisten it with cooking stock or bouillon
9. When the sauce is well reduced, add salt and coarse pepper
10. When ready, add the anchovy butter and gherkins and pour the sauce over the minced mutton
11. Keep warm without boiling
12. At the moment of serving, toss well in the pan so the butter does not turn to oil, then arrange on the plate with croutons around it

title: Minced Mutton with Mushrooms
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Minced mutton
- Mushrooms
- Butter
- Spanish sauce (Espagnole) reduced
- Consommé
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Laurel leaf
- Croutons
- Poached eggs

## Directions

1. When your meat is minced as described above, mince finely about twenty mushrooms
2. Press them in a cloth
3. Put a quarter-pound of butter in a pan with the mushrooms
4. Pass them on the fire until the butter turns to oil
5. Then add four spoonfuls of reduced Spanish sauce and three spoonfuls of consommé
6. Reduce your sauce to half
7. Pour it over your meat and mix
8. Keep your hash warm without boiling
9. If you do not have sauce, add one spoonful of flour which you stir briefly on the fire with the mushrooms
10. Add three glasses of bouillon, a little salt, a little coarse pepper, and a laurel leaf
11. Reduce everything by half
12. Pour the sauce over the meat
13. Check if your hash has good seasoning
14. Serve with croutons around it
15. You can also serve poached eggs on top

title: Minced Mutton with Poached Eggs
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Leg of mutton
- Spanish sauce (Espagnole)
- Croutons
- Poached eggs

## Directions

1. Put a leg of mutton on the spit
2. When cold, remove enough meat for your hash
3. Remove nerves and skins
4. Mince the meat very fine
5. Place in a pan
6. Take four spoonfuls of reduced Spanish sauce and reduce it further by a quarter
7. Pour it over the minced mutton
8. Mix the sauce together
9. Keep warm only
10. At the moment of serving, pour onto the plate
11. Place croutons around it
12. Serve poached eggs on top

title: Minced Mutton à la Bourgeoise
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted leg of mutton
- Butter
- Onions
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Coarse pepper
- Muscade
- Salt
- Water

## Directions

1. If you have leftover roasted leg of mutton, remove the meat
2. Remove nerves and skins
3. Mince the meat
4. Put it in a pan
5. Cut six onions into dice
6. Put a quarter-pound of butter in the pan
7. Add the onions
8. Fry them in the butter until they are blonde
9. Add one spoonful of flour
10. Stir it briefly on the fire with the onions
11. Pour in about two glasses of bouillon
12. Add a little coarse pepper
13. If you do not have bouillon, moisten the onions with water and salt
14. Reduce the sauce until slightly thick
15. Put it on the minced mutton
16. Grate a little nutmeg over it
17. Mix the sauce with the meat
18. Keep warm without boiling
19. Before serving, check that it has good seasoning

title: Miroton de Pommes (Apple Miroton)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Several apples
- Powdered sugar
- Half a setier of brandy (eau-de-vie)
- Lemon juice
- Cinnamon powder
- Apricot marmalade

## Directions

1. Remove the peel and core from several apples with an apple corer
2. Cut them into slices of the same size and thickness
3. Put them in a terrine with powdered sugar, half a setier of brandy, lemon juice, and a little cinnamon powder
4. When the apples have infused for three or four hours, let them drain on a sieve
5. Arrange the apple slices in a miroton pattern around the plate with the scraps, and apricot marmalade in the middle
6. Add several rows of miroton so they form a dome
7. Half an hour before serving, put your plate in the oven or on red embers with the field oven on top
8. Make sure it has a nice color
9. You can do the same with pears

title: Moitié de Bif (Half Roast Beef)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Half lamb cut at two cutlets near the saddle
- Small atelets
- Lard

## Directions

1. Cut the lamb at two cutlets near the saddle as squarely as possible
2. Secure the flanks with small atelets
3. Ensure your piece has a fine shape
4. Lard it to the end of both thighs
5. Leave a small distance from the thighs to the loins
6. Place your piece on the spit
7. Make more fire on the side of the thighs
8. If not larding your roast, bard it
9. If serving as a relevé, place clear stock underneath

title: Morue au Beurre Noir (Cod with Black Butter)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- water
- vinegar
- bouillon
- coarse pepper
- butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cook the cod in water and drain
2. Place it on the serving dish with half a glass of vinegar and as much bouillon
3. Add coarse pepper
4. Boil for half a quarter hour
5. Put hot butter on top with fried parsley

title: Morue en Beignets (Cod Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- flour
- wine
- oil
- salt
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take cooked and well-drained cod and select the largest flakes
2. Dip them in a batter made with flour, wine, a little oil and very little salt
3. Fry them
4. Serve garnished with fried parsley

title: Morue en Dauphin (Fresh Cod Shaped as a Dolphin)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cod
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives, whole
- garlic clove
- bay leaf
- butter
- carp roe (laitances)
- asparagus tips
- mushrooms
- bouquet of parsley and chives
- flour
- white wine
- good lean broth
- egg yolks
- cream

## Directions

1. Season the cod with salt, pepper, parsley, chives, garlic, and bay leaf
2. Take an iron skewer called a hatelet and pass it through the fresh cod starting at the eyes, through the middle of the body, and finishing at the tail, shaping it to look like a dolphin
3. Place it on a baking dish and baste it with its marinade
4. Cook it in the oven
5. When cooked, remove the skewer and arrange it on the serving platter
6. For the ragout: boil three carp roe briefly in water; also boil asparagus tips briefly in water
7. Put everything in a saucepan with a good piece of butter, mushrooms, and a bouquet of parsley and chives
8. Pass over the fire and add a pinch of flour, then moisten with a glass of white wine and good lean broth
9. When the ragout is cooked and the sauce is reduced and seasoned to taste, add a liaison of three egg yolks and cream
10. Bind the sauce over the fire and serve over the cod
11. For best results, add the asparagus only when the ragout is nearly finished

title: Morue en Stinquerque (Cod à la Stinquerque)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- butter
- parsley
- onion
- anchovy
- garlic
- coarse pepper
- capers
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. In the serving dish, place a little butter at the bottom with chopped parsley, onion, anchovy, garlic, coarse pepper, and some whole capers
2. Cover with cod and make several layers this way until the dish is full
3. Top with breadcrumbs and simmer over a small fire with a pie cover on top to give it color

title: Morue Fraîche en Dauphin au Ragoût de Laitances de Carpes et Pointes d'Asperges (Fresh Cod in Dolphin Shape with Carp Roe and Asparagus Tips)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One very fresh cod
- Fine oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Whole chives
- One clove of garlic
- One bay leaf
- An iron skewer called a hatelet
- Carp roes

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the fresh cod.
2. Cisele and dry it.
3. Marinate it for two hours with fine oil, salt, pepper, parsley and whole chives, one clove of garlic, and one bay leaf.
4. Take an iron skewer called a hatelet and pass it through the cod starting at the eyes, through the middle of the body, and finishing at the tail, making it take the shape of a dolphin.
5. Put it on a pie dish and baste it with its marinade.
6. Cook it in the oven.
7. When it is cooked, remove the hatelet and arrange it on the plate you will serve it on.
8. Serve with a ragoût made as follows: boil three carp roes for a moment in water.

title: Morue Marinée Frite (Fried Marinated Cod)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook cod in water and remove in flakes
2. Marinate and fry like skate, with the difference that only a little salt should be in the marinade

title: Morue Salée (Salted Cod)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- salted cod
- flour
- butter
- pepper
- milk
- verjus or parsley and chives

## Directions

1. Choose cod with white flesh, black skin, and large flakes
2. Wash it after scaling
3. Cook it briefly in a pot with river water
4. Drain and remove the flesh by flakes or leave it whole
5. In a casserole, put a little flour and a piece of butter with a little pepper
6. Dilute with a little milk
7. Add verjus in grains and make the sauce thicken on the fire
8. Add the cod to impart flavor
9. Serve
10. In another season, use chopped parsley and chives instead of verjus

title: Morue à l'Œuvre-d'hôtel (Cod Hotel-style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one desalted cod
- a good piece of butter
- coarse pepper
- a little grated nutmeg
- parsley
- chives
- a pinch of flour (optional)
- water from cooking the cod
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Once the cod is properly desalted, scrape and clean it
2. Cook it in pure water and drain it
3. Put a good piece of butter cut into small pieces in a saucepan
4. Add coarse pepper, a little grated nutmeg, and finely chopped parsley and chives; if desired, mix in a pinch of flour
5. Stir the seasoning together
6. Place the cod on top with about a spoonful of cod cooking water if it is not too salty
7. At serving time, place the saucepan on the fire without leaving it; stir constantly so the butter does not turn to oil
8. When the cod is well mixed with the butter and hot, if it is too thick, add a spoonful of water
9. Arrange it on your platter and add lemon juice when serving

title: Morue à la Béchamelle (Cod with Béchamel Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked cod
- Butter, a good piece
- Flour, one tablespoon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg, grated, a little
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Chives, chopped and rinsed
- Cream, one glass

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the cod as in the preceding recipes; drain it and remove the bones.
2. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan with one tablespoon of flour, a little salt, pepper, a little grated nutmeg, finely chopped parsley, and chopped and rinsed chives; mix this seasoning with the butter.
3. Add one glass of cream to the mixture.
4. Place the sauce on the fire, stirring constantly until it comes to a boil; if it is too thick, thin it with cream; it should be thick as porridge.
5. Pour the sauce over the cod and keep it warm without boiling.
6. At the moment of serving, arrange it in a mound shape, that is to say forming a dome on your plate.

title: Morue à la béchamelle (Cod with Béchamel Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one desalted cod
- butter
- flour
- salt
- coarse pepper
- grated nutmeg
- parsley
- chives
- cream

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the cod as in the previous recipes
2. Drain it and remove the bones
3. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan with one spoonful of flour, a little salt, coarse pepper, a little grated nutmeg, finely chopped parsley, and finely chopped chives
4. Mix this seasoning with the butter
5. Add one glass of cream
6. Put the sauce on the fire and stir constantly until it boils; if it is too thick, thin it with cream

title: Morue à la Crème (Cod in Cream Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- butter
- flour
- garlic
- coarse pepper
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Cook the cod in water
2. Once cooked, drain it and remove the flesh in flakes
3. In a saucepan, melt a good piece of butter with half a spoonful of flour and a minced point of garlic
4. Add coarse pepper and moisten with cream or milk
5. Make the sauce bind over the fire and add the cod fillets
6. Heat and serve

title: Morue à la Crème (Cod in Cream Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- water
- butter
- flour
- garlic
- coarse pepper
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Cook the cod in water
2. Drain and remove the flesh by flakes
3. In a casserole, put a good piece of butter and half a spoonful of flour
4. Add a touch of chopped garlic and coarse pepper
5. Moisten with cream or milk
6. Thicken the sauce on the fire
7. Add the cod fillets
8. Heat and serve
9. If you wish to coat it, add a little more butter and three egg yolks, arrange in the serving dish, coat the top, and brown it

title: Morue à la Maître-d'Hôtel (Cod with Herb Butter)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- fresh water
- parsley
- chives
- coarse pepper
- nutmeg
- butter
- verjus

## Directions

1. Scale and wash the cod
2. Put it in fresh water in a pan or pot
3. Place on the fire; when it begins to boil, skim it
4. Remove from the fire as soon as it boils
5. Cover with a cloth for half a quarter hour
6. Drain the water
7. Place on a dish with chopped parsley and chives, coarse pepper, grated nutmeg, a good piece of butter, and a spoonful of verjus
8. Heat while turning it over
9. Serve immediately

title: Morue à la Provençale (Cod Provençal Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cod
- shallot
- garlic
- parsley
- chives
- lemon
- coarse pepper
- oil
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Take cooked and well-drained cod
2. In the serving dish, place at the bottom a shallot, a little garlic, parsley, chives, lemon slices with skin removed, coarse pepper, two spoonfuls of oil, and butter the size of half an egg
3. Arrange the cod on top
4. Put the same seasoning on top as below
5. Coat with breadcrumbs
6. Place the dish on a small fire so it simmers gently
7. Brown the top with a hot shovel or a country stove cover

title: Morue à la provençale (Cod Provençal)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one desalted cod
- a good piece of butter
- coarse pepper
- grated nutmeg
- parsley
- chives
- one spoonful of good oil
- zest of half a lemon
- one garlic clove (optional)
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the cod as in the previous recipe
2. Put a good piece of butter cut into small pieces in your saucepan
3. Add coarse pepper, grated nutmeg, finely chopped parsley and chives, one spoonful of good oil, the zest of half a lemon, and one garlic clove if desired
4. Stir everything together to mix the seasoning
5. Place the cod on top
6. At serving time, put it on the fire and stir constantly so the butter does not turn to oil
7. Arrange it on your platter and add lemon juice when serving

title: Morue à la Sauce aux Câpres et Anchois (Cod with Caper and Anchovy Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cod
- water
- caper and anchovy sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the cod in water
2. Drain it
3. Deglaize it hot in the serving dish
4. Pour caper and anchovy sauce over the top

title: Mou de Veau au roux (Calf's Sweetbread with Roux)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One calf's sweetbread
- Butter
- Broth or water
- Onions
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut and prepare the sweetbread as in the blanc recipe
2. Make a roux with your butter and brown your sweetbread in it
3. Moisten with broth or water
4. Add the same seasoning as in the previous recipe
5. When it is three-quarters cooked, add your onions and a little grated nutmeg
6. When nearly cooked, degrease it
7. At the moment of serving, check that your ragout is well seasoned

title: Mou de Veau à la poulette (Calf's Sweetbread with Poulette Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One calf's sweetbread
- Half a pound of butter
- Two tablespoons of flour
- Broth
- Two bay leaves
- A bouquet of parsley and chives
- A handful of mushrooms
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Small onions
- A little nutmeg
- Three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the sweetbread into pieces the size of a large onion
2. Soak it to disgorge
3. Blanch it for a quarter hour, pressing it well into the water so it does not blacken
4. Put it in cold water and drain
5. Melt half a pound of butter in a saucepan and add your sweetbread
6. Let it turn for a quarter hour
7. Pour in two tablespoons of flour and stir well with the sweetbread to mix
8. Moisten it with plenty of broth so it will reduce
9. Add two bay leaves, a bouquet of parsley and chives, a handful of mushrooms, salt, and coarse pepper
10. When the sweetbread is three-quarters cooked and reduced, add small, well-peeled onions
11. When the ragout is entirely cooked, keep it warm
12. At the moment of serving, prepare a liaison of three egg yolks with a little grated nutmeg
13. Add this liaison to your sweetbread, stirring constantly over the fire
14. Ensure it does not boil so the liaison does not break
15. Check that the seasoning is good
16. Be sure to degrease your ragout before binding it

title: Moules à la Poulette (Mussels Poulette Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- butter
- chives
- parsley
- flour
- pepper
- nutmeg
- eggs
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. After scraping your mussels well and washing them thoroughly, put them dry in a saucepan over a hot fire; sauté them
2. As they open, remove the shell and put them in another saucepan
3. Pass the water from your mussels through a silk sieve
4. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan, chopped chives that you will brown in the butter; add your parsley, then brown it a little
5. Mix in a spoonful of flour that you will blend with your butter
6. Moisten your herbs with the mussel water; add a little pepper and grated nutmeg
7. Let your sauce come to a boil a few times; add a liaison of two or three eggs, depending on the quantity of mussels, and put them in your sauce
8. Sauté them and keep them hot without letting them boil
9. At the moment of service, add lemon juice and arrange them on your plate

title: Mouton entier à la broche (Whole Lamb on the Spit)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Young, tender lamb
- Lamb leg meat
- Salt and coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Take a young, tender lamb, except for the backbone
2. Remove the bones from the shoulders and ribs
3. Fill it with tender leg meat that you season with salt and coarse pepper
4. Return it to its original shape
5. Pass the spit from one end to the other with skewers and string to secure the meat
6. Make sure it has an even color
7. You can prepare this large piece in an oven

title: Mussels (Moules)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- mussels
- butter
- parsley
- chives
- flour
- bouillon
- egg yolks
- cream
- verjus

## Directions

1. After washing well, scrape their shells and drain them and put them dry in a pot on good heat from the stove; the heat will make them open, you will shell them afterwards one by one; take care to remove the crabs if you find any.
2. Put your mussels, after having removed them from their shells, in a pot with a piece of good butter, parsley, chopped chives, pass them over the fire; add a small pinch of flour, moisten with a little bouillon; when there is no more sauce, add a liaison of three egg yolks with cream, bind your sauce and add after a dash of verjus.

title: Mutton Tails with Sorrel Purée
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton tails
- Sorrel purée

## Directions

1. Prepare your tails and cook them as the braised ones
2. At the moment of serving, drain them
3. Glaze them
4. Put them on a sorrel purée

title: Navets Glacés (Glazed Turnips)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 or 18 large turnips
- butter
- velouté
- bouillon
- coarse pepper
- sugar, the size of half an egg
- meat juice
- flour

## Directions

1. Peel and turn the turnips into the shape of a pear or other form
2. Put a little butter in a casserole and heat
3. Add the turnips and let them take color
4. Drain them and transfer to a clean casserole
5. Add 4 spoonfuls of velouté, as much bouillon, coarse pepper, sugar the size of half an egg, and a spoonful of meat juice
6. Simmer the turnips until cooked, then reduce the sauce
7. Arrange the turnips and pour the sauce over them
8. Alternatively, once the turnips are browned in butter, add a spoonful of flour, sugar, and bouillon, cook the same way, and serve with the sauce

title: Noix de Veau aux Truffes à la Bonne Femme (Veal Noix with Truffles in the Homestyle)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three veal noix (round cuts)
- lard for larding
- truffles
- salt
- parsley
- chives
- good bouillon
- coulis

## Directions

1. Take three veal noix which you trim by lightly removing the meat that prevents a good appearance.
2. You must lard them throughout with pieces of lard and truffles, the two combined together with fine salt, parsley, chives, and chopped truffles.
3. Cook them with good bouillon.
4. When they are cooked and the sauce is well degreased, put in two spoonfuls of coulis.
5. Reduce the sauce so that it is neither too short nor too long, and serve it over the veal noix.

title: Noix de Veau en aspic (Veal Loin in Aspic)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal loin, larded and glazed
- Aspic
- Cold ragout of rooster crests and kidneys
- Béchamel

## Directions

1. Prepare a larded and glazed veal loin the day before your dinner.
2. Pour melted aspic into your mold to the thickness of one inch.
3. When the aspic in your mold is well congealed, cut your veal into slices all of the same thickness and size.
4. Arrange your pieces in crowns, one on top of the other until your round is complete; there must be half an inch of distance between the meat and your mold.
5. Have a small cold ragout of rooster crests and kidneys in a béchamel, which you will place in the middle of the meat.
6. Put the mold in ice or in a cold place so that the gelatin sets firm.
7. Fill the mold with melted but cold aspic and let it congeal.
8. Put the mold in a cold place, and at the moment of serving, soak it in more than lukewarm water.
9. Remove it quickly into slightly warm water.
10. Dry your mold by putting your plate on top and turning it over.
11. If your aspic does not detach, heat a cloth and rub your mold with it.
12. Remove it gently.
13. If there is any melted gelatin on your plate, suck it out with a straw.
14. If your aspic was turned over some time before serving, it must be kept in a cold place.

title: Noix de Veau en Ballotine (Veal Cushion Ballottine)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One veal cushion, with or without teat
- Thick lard for larding
- Half a pound of butter
- Salt and coarse pepper
- One quarter-pound of grated lard
- One quarter-pound of oil
- Shallots, finely chopped, one spoonful
- A dozen mushrooms, finely chopped
- Parsley, one spoonful
- Nutmeg, grated
- Six oiled sheets of paper
- Thin lard bards
- String

## Directions

1. Lard the veal cushion with thick lard in the same manner as veal in the bourgeoise style, with the same seasoning in the larding pieces
2. Place half a pound of butter in a casserole and put the cushion in it
3. Sauté over fire for three-quarters of an hour without allowing it to take color
4. Dust with salt and coarse pepper
5. Remove from the casserole and place on a plate
6. If there is juice in the casserole with the butter, reduce it until the butter becomes oil
7. Add the grated lard and oil
8. Add finely chopped shallots (one spoonful) and let them fry a little in the butter
9. Add a dozen finely chopped mushrooms, pass them with the butter, and add parsley (one spoonful)
10. When all is well combined, add grated nutmeg and coarse pepper
11. Pour the fine herbs over the veal cushion
12. When cooled, oil six sheets of paper
13. Wrap the cushion with a thin lard bard on top and bottom, then wrap it with a sheet of paper so the seasoning does not escape, then cover with another sheet, continuing until all six sheets are used
14. Ensure the cushion is hermetically sealed, the bundle has a fine shape, and the folds are well made
15. Tie the bundle with string as one ties a piece of beef, so the paper does not come undone on the grill
16. One hour before serving, place the cushion on the grill with very gentle heat
17. Watch that the paper does not burn and the seasoning does not escape
18. When the cushion is grilled, remove only the string and serve the cushion in the paper with the sauce inside
19. If the first sheet is too dark, it should be removed

title: Noix de Veau en Caisse (Veal Round in Paper Case)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal round, one inch thick, same size as for fricandelles
- White paper
- Butter
- Oil or butter
- Parsley, chives, shallots, mushrooms, all chopped very finely
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- White vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut veal round one inch thick, the same size as for fricandelles
2. Make a white paper case the size of the meat and butter it all over on the outside
3. Put the meat inside with oil or butter, parsley, chives, shallots, and mushrooms all chopped very finely, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Place the case on the grill with a buttered paper sheet underneath
5. Cook over very low heat or on hot ashes, being careful not to let the paper burn
6. If the fire is too strong, reduce it a little with the shovel
7. When the meat is cooked on one side, turn it over to cook the other side
8. Serve in the paper case after lightly drizzling white vinegar on top

title: Noix de Veau en Surprise (Veal Cushion en Surprise)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One beautiful veal cushion
- Thick and thin larding fat
- Lard bards
- Veal slices
- Two carrots
- Four onions, one studded with two cloves
- One bay leaf
- A bouquet of parsley and chives
- Salt
- Butter
- Buttered paper
- Mushrooms
- Béchamel sauce
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the cushion as for veal à la Conty: lard it with thick and thin larding fat
2. Place lard bards in the bottom of a casserole or braising pan
3. Add veal slices, two carrots, four onions (one studded with two cloves), one bay leaf, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and a little salt
4. Place the veal cushion in the casserole
5. Cover only the teat with lard bards
6. Butter a round double sheet of paper and cook the cushion with fire on top and below
7. When the cushion is cooked, let it cool
8. Make an oval hole and remove the flesh, leaving enough at the bottom so the cushion can hold a ragout without leaking
9. Cut the meat removed from the cushion into small dice, except for the top, which you preserve to mask the ragout so one cannot see the cushion has been opened
10. Also cut mushrooms into small dice and add them with the meat to a béchamel sauce
11. Heat the veal cushion
12. At serving time, drain it well and glaze it
13. Pour the small ragout into the hollow of the cushion
14. Cover it with the top you have preserved so one cannot see the cushion has been opened
15. It must be glazed, and you will put Spanish sauce underneath

title: Noix de Veau Piquée Glacée (Larded and Glazed Veal Cushion)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One whole veal cushion
- Thin larding fat
- Lard bards
- Veal slices
- Two or three carrots
- Four onions, one studded with two cloves
- One bay leaf
- A bouquet of parsley and chives
- Consommé
- Chicory
- Cream
- Cucumbers
- Glazing sauce or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Beat the veal cushion well
2. Remove the skin and teat covering the cushion
3. Place the meat skin-side down on the table
4. With a sharp knife, slip it between the top nervous layer and the flesh, as if lifting a bard, being careful not to cut too deeply into the meat to minimize waste and keep the cushion well-formed
5. Remove any remaining skin as gently as possible so no knife marks are visible
6. Lard the prepared cushion with thin larding fat
7. Place lard bards in a casserole with a few veal slices, two or three carrots, four onions (one studded with two cloves), one bay leaf, a bouquet of parsley and chives
8. Position the cushion in the casserole so it rounds in the middle
9. Cover with a double sheet of buttered paper
10. Add one spoonful of consommé
11. Bring to a boil; when it has thrown off a few bubbles, place on gentle heat and put fire on the lid
12. Cook for two hours
13. At serving time, drain it well, glaze it, and serve underneath with chicory, cream, cucumbers, and either glazing sauce or tomato sauce as you see fit

title: Noix de Veau Piquée à la Conty (Veal Knuckle Larded in the Conty Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One veal knuckle, whole and well covered
- Fine lard for larding
- Coarse lard for larding
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Three carrots, turned into large batons
- Four large onions
- Two cloves
- Two laurel leaves
- Parsley and chive bouquet
- One spoonful of jelly or consommé or bouillon
- Grande Espagnole sauce
- Flour and bouillon

## Directions

1. Remove your knuckle whole and well covered
2. Beat it like the previous one
3. Pare the flesh that is under the nipple, without touching the nipple
4. Lard with fine lard the flesh that appears from your knuckle
5. When your knuckle is larded with fine lard, lard it underneath with coarse lard
6. Season the lardoons with salt and coarse pepper
7. When your knuckle is larded, truss it like the previous one
8. Put it in a casserole
9. Cover it with buttered paper
10. Put in three carrots turned into large batons, four large onions, one of which is studded with two cloves, two laurel leaves, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and one spoonful of jelly; if you have no jelly, put consommé or bouillon in it
11. Cook your knuckle on a gentle fire for two hours, fire above, fire below
12. When your knuckle is three-quarters cooked, remove your vegetables with your knuckle, and make it fall to glaze, that is to say put it on a hot fire, and reduce the liquid of your knuckle until it is glazed
13. At the moment of serving, remove your knuckle from your casserole
14. Untie it
15. Glaze it with what is in your casserole
16. Put three spoonfuls of grande Espagnole sauce in your casserole, where you will have removed the fat, and loosened the glaze
17. Put this sauce under your knuckle; in case you have no sauce, you would put a little flour in it that you would loosen with a little bouillon

title: Noix de Veau à la Bourgeoise (Veal Knuckle with Vegetables)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One veal knuckle, well trimmed
- Lard for larding
- Fine salt
- Pepper
- Four spices
- Parsley
- Chive, finely minced
- Thyme
- Laurel
- Butter
- Four large carrots, turned into large batons
- Four large onions
- Two laurel leaves
- Two glasses of bouillon

## Directions

1. Remove your knuckle of veal well trimmed
2. Place it in a white cloth
3. Beat it with a cleaver
4. Lard it with large lard
5. Season your lardoons with fine salt, pepper, four spices, parsley and very finely minced chive, a little thyme and laurel also minced, which you mix well with your lardoons
6. Your knuckle must be well larded
7. Keep your knuckle covered with its skins, taking care that the lardoons do not pierce the skin on top
8. Tie it with a trussing needle and string that you put around, so that the skins covering your knuckle do not curl back, and when cooked they are well covered
9. Butter the bottom of your casserole
10. Put your veal knuckle in it
11. Also put around four carrots which you turn into large batons, four large onions, two laurel leaves, two full glasses of bouillon
12. Cover your knuckle with a round of buttered paper
13. When your knuckle boils, put it on a gentle fire for two hours
14. Put a little fire on the cover of your casserole
15. At the moment of serving, drain your veal knuckle
16. Untie it
17. Put underneath the stock which you reduce by half, and the vegetables that cooked with your knuckle
18. Glaze it if you have glaze; you can serve with this knuckle, while glazing it, a glaze sauce, a tomato sauce, chicory, an oseille purée, lettuces, a mushroom purée, etc.

title: Noix de Veau à la Chantilly (Veal Round with Chantilly)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal round, cut the same way as for paupiettes
- Parsley, chives, shallots, mushrooms, all chopped
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Oil or butter

## Directions

1. Cut veal round the same way as for paupiettes, but without forcemeat
2. Season with oil or butter, salt, coarse pepper, parsley, chives, shallots, and mushrooms, all chopped
3. Roll and thread onto a skewer
4. Cook on a spit
5. Serve with a clear sauce, well seasoned

title: Oeufs au Fromage (Eggs with Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter pound of grated Gruyère cheese
- butter the size of half an egg
- parsley
- chives
- a little nutmeg
- half a glass of white wine
- six eggs

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan a quarter pound of grated Gruyère cheese, butter the size of half an egg, chopped parsley, chopped chives, a little nutmeg, and half a glass of white wine
2. Boil over a small fire, stirring until the cheese is melted
3. Then add six eggs to scramble and cook over a small fire
4. Serve garnished with breadcrumbs on the edges of the plate

title: Oeufs au Gratin (Eggs au Gratin)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread crumbs
- Butter, a good piece
- Anchovy, chopped
- Parsley
- Chives
- Shallot
- Egg yolks, three
- Eggs, seven or eight
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Take a heat-safe dish and place on it a small gratin made with bread crumbs, a good piece of butter, chopped anchovy, parsley, chives, and shallot, all chopped together
2. Mix in three egg yolks and combine everything
3. Place this mixture in the bottom of the dish to the thickness of a coin and let it set on a low fire
4. Then break seven or eight eggs on top and season with salt and coarse pepper
5. Cook gently and pass a red-hot iron or shovel over the top
6. When cooked with soft yolks, serve

title: Oeufs au Gratin (Gratinéed Eggs)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs prepared as for Eggs in Cream
- croutons
- 4 egg yolks
- flour

## Directions

1. Prepare eggs as you would for Eggs in Cream
2. Glue croutons around the edge of the dish and pour your eggs into it
3. Bread the top
4. Pass four yolks through a sieve
5. Mask them with it
6. Place them on red coals with a well-heated field oven on top
7. When they have a nice color, serve them

title: Oeufs au Gratin au Parmesan (Gratineed Eggs with Parmesan)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- breadcrumbs the size of half an egg
- grated Parmesan cheese
- butter
- two raw egg yolks
- a little nutmeg
- coarse pepper
- ten eggs

## Directions

1. Put in the bottom of the serving plate breadcrumbs the size of half an egg with a little grated Parmesan cheese, butter, two raw egg yolks, a little nutmeg and coarse pepper
2. Mix everything together and spread it on the bottom of the plate
3. Brown it over a small fire, then break ten eggs over it
4. Dust the top of the eggs with grated Parmesan
5. Cook and pass a hot shovel over it
6. Serve with the yolks runny

title: Oeufs au Pain (Eggs with Bread)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a handful of breadcrumbs
- a fish-measure of cream
- salt
- pepper
- a little nutmeg
- ten eggs

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan half a handful of breadcrumbs with a fish-measure of cream, salt, pepper, and a little nutmeg
2. When the bread has absorbed all the cream, break ten eggs into it
3. Beat them together to make an omelet

title: Oeufs au Petit Lard (Eggs with Bacon)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a good quarter pound of bacon with good marbling
- seven or eight eggs
- coarse pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into small thin slices
2. Put it in a saucepan over a small fire until cooked, turning it carefully
3. Pour the melted bacon into the serving plate with two spoonfuls of jus
4. Break seven or eight eggs over it
5. Add the bacon slices, coarse pepper, and a little salt
6. Cook over a small fire
7. Pass a hot shovel over it
8. Serve with runny yolks

title: Oeufs Brouillés à la Coque (Scrambled Eggs in the Shell)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread crumbs
- Eggs
- Butter, a small piece
- Parsley
- Chives, chopped
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cream, two spoonfuls

## Directions

1. Cut as many rounds of bread crumbs as you wish to serve eggs, shaped like a small snuff box
2. Make a small hole in the middle to hold an egg lengthwise
3. Take the eggs you wish to scramble and break them quickly at one end to empty them
4. Scramble them by putting them in a saucepan with a small piece of butter, a little chopped parsley and chives, salt, coarse pepper, and two spoonfuls of cream
5. Cook on the fire while stirring constantly until they are cooked
6. Return them to their cleaned and drained shells and place them on the bread crumb bases

title: Oeufs en Croquettes (Egg Croquettes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 hard eggs
- cream sauce
- parsley and chives, finely chopped and washed
- bread crumb
- beaten egg
- oil for frying

## Directions

1. Hard boil eighteen eggs
2. Cut the whites and yolks into small dice
3. Put them in a saucepan
4. Make a cream sauce in which you put a little finely chopped and washed parsley and chives
5. Pour it over your eggs; they should be somewhat bound together
6. Stir them in their sauce
7. Let the mixture cool
8. Take a spoonful and pour it onto a board
9. When the eggs are well cold, shape them into croquettes
10. Roll them in bread crumb
11. Dip in beaten egg and bread them a second time
12. At the moment of service, put them in very hot oil
13. When they have a nice color, drain them on white cloth or in a strainer
14. Arrange them on the dish

title: Oeufs en Filets (Eggs with Fillets)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- onion
- mushrooms
- a small point of garlic
- flour
- bouillon
- a glass of white wine
- salt
- coarse pepper
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Pass over the fire with butter, onion, and mushrooms cut into fillets, with a small point of garlic
2. When the onion begins to color, add a good pinch of flour
3. Moisten with bouillon and a glass of white wine, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Boil for half an hour and reduce to sauce consistency
5. Add hard-boiled eggs with the whites cut into fillets and the yolks whole
6. Boil for a moment and serve

title: Oeufs en Peau d'Espagne (Eggs in Spanish Skin)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three spoonfuls of coulis
- the same amount of jus
- six eggs
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Dilute three spoonfuls of coulis and the same amount of jus with six eggs, whites and yolks
2. Season with salt and coarse pepper
3. Pass through a sieve and put on the serving plate
4. Cook in a bain-marie
5. When set, cut with several knife strokes while serving
6. Put clear jus on top

title: Oeufs en Salade (Egg Salad)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- hard-boiled eggs
- salad greens
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop a little lettuce and place it in the bottom of a plate
2. Arrange hard-boiled eggs cut in half symmetrically on top
3. Surround with salad greens
4. Season with oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper

title: Oeufs en Surtout (Eggs in Disguise)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- Shallots
- A pinch of flour
- A half-setier of milk
- Salt
- Black pepper
- Seven hard-boiled eggs
- Bread crumbs
- Butter, hot

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan a piece of butter with parsley, chives, mushrooms, and shallots, all chopped
2. Pass it over the fire and add a pinch of flour
3. Moisten with a half-setier of milk
4. Season with salt and black pepper
5. Boil until the sauce is bound
6. Add seven hard-boiled eggs cut in four
7. Let them boil and arrange them on the dish that you will put on top so that one does not see the ragout of eggs underneath
8. Rub the top of the omelet with hot butter
9. Bread it with bread crumbs and pour butter over it again
10. Brown it underneath a pie-dish lid

title: Oeufs en Timbale (Eggs in Timbale)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Eggs, six
- Coulis, three or four spoonfuls
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Melt a little butter to coat the inside of six copper goblets or small timbales
2. Take six eggs, whites and yolks, and thin them with three or four spoonfuls of coulis
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Pass through a sieve and put into the goblets, being careful not to fill them completely
5. Cook in a bain-marie with gently boiling water
6. When they are firm, run a knife gently around the edge to detach them from the goblet and flip them into the serving dish
7. Serve with clear jus

title: Oeufs en Timbales (Eggs in Ramekins)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- six eggs
- three or four spoonfuls of coulis
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Melt a little butter to butter the inside of six copper goblets or small ramekins
2. Take six eggs, whites and yolks, and dilute them with three or four spoonfuls of coulis
3. Season with salt and pepper
4. Pass through a sieve and put into the goblets (do not fill them completely)
5. Cook in a bain-marie with the water boiling gently
6. When they are firm, gently pass a knife around the edges to detach them from the goblet and turn them out onto the plate
7. Serve with clear jus

title: Oeufs Farcis (Stuffed Eggs)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 hard eggs
- an equal portion of butter and egg yolks
- bread crumb soaked in milk
- chives and parsley, finely chopped and washed
- salt
- coarse pepper
- a little grated nutmeg
- 2 or 3 whole egg yolks

## Directions

1. Hard boil ten eggs
2. Cut them in half lengthwise
3. Remove the yolks and pound them in a mortar
4. Pass them through a quenelle sieve
5. Soak a bread crumb in milk and press well to extract the milk; pound it and pass it through a sieve along with the eggs
6. Pound as much butter in the mortar as you have pounded yolks
7. Mix equal portions of butter and egg yolks together
8. When your stuffing is well pounded, add a little finely chopped and washed chives and parsley, salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
9. Pound the stuffing again
10. Add two or three whole egg yolks; keep the stuffing workable by adding egg as needed
11. When finished, place it in a vessel
12. Arrange it a finger's thickness in the bottom of the dish
13. Stuff your egg halves
14. Dip a knife blade in egg white to smooth the top
15. Arrange the eggs in order on the stuffing in the dish
16. Place it on red coals with a field oven on top
17. When they have a nice color, serve them

title: Oeufs Frits (Fried Eggs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- parsley
- chives
- salt
- coarse pepper
- one beaten egg
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Make three very thin omelets of three eggs each
2. Season with parsley, chives, salt, and coarse pepper
3. As you make them, spread them on a saucepan lid and roll them tightly
4. Cut each omelet in half to make six pieces from three
5. Dip them in beaten egg and coat with breadcrumbs
6. Fry them to a beautiful color
7. Serve garnished with parsley

title: Oeufs Grillés (Grilled Eggs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- white paper
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- parsley
- chives
- a point of garlic
- salt
- coarse pepper
- eight eggs

## Directions

1. Take a large sheet of white paper and cut it into eight small equal squares
2. Fold each square in half to make small cases and butter them inside and out
3. Mix a good piece of butter with half a handful of breadcrumbs, parsley, chives, a point of garlic, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Put this mixture in the bottom of your cases
5. Break an egg into each case
6. Season the top with a little fine salt and coarse pepper
7. Cook them over a small fire on the grill
8. Pass a hot shovel over them
9. Serve with runny yolks in the paper cases

title: Oeufs Pochés au Jus (Poached Eggs in Juice)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Put water three-quarters full in a medium saucepan with salt and a little vinegar
2. When it boils, place it at the edge of the stove and break the egg
3. Be careful not to damage the yolk when opening the shells
4. Pour the egg gently into the water
5. Put in four of them and let them set
6. Keep the water boiling
7. Remove them from the water with a perforated spoon and put your finger on top; if it has a little consistency, put it in cold water

title: Oeufs à l'Ail (Eggs with Garlic)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- ten cloves of garlic
- two anchovies
- a good pinch of capers
- oil
- a thread of vinegar
- salt
- coarse pepper
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Cook ten cloves of garlic for half a quarter hour in water
2. Pound them with two anchovies and a good pinch of capers
3. Dilute them with oil, a thread of vinegar, a little salt, and coarse pepper
4. Put this sauce in the bottom of the serving plate
5. Arrange hard-boiled eggs neatly on top

title: Oeufs à la Bourgeoise (Eggs Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- breadcrumbs
- Gruyère cheese
- eight or ten eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Spread butter as thick as a knife blade in the bottom of the serving plate
2. Scatter very thin slices of breadcrumbs and small slices of Gruyère cheese all over it
3. Then add eight or ten eggs
4. Season with a little salt, nutmeg, and coarse pepper
5. Cook over a small fire on a stove

title: Oeufs à la Crème (Creamed Eggs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a setier of cream
- eight eggs
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Put half a setier of cream in the serving plate
2. Boil and reduce by half
3. Add eight eggs, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Cook them
5. Pass a hot shovel over the top
6. Serve with runny yolks

title: Oeufs à la Crème (Eggs in Cream)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 hard eggs
- a good piece of butter
- a spoonful of flour
- parsley and chives, finely chopped
- salt
- coarse pepper
- a little grated nutmeg
- a glass of cream

## Directions

1. Hard boil twelve eggs
2. Cut them into slices
3. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan with a spoonful of flour, a little finely chopped parsley and chives, salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
4. Mix everything together
5. Add a glass of cream
6. Turn the sauce over the fire
7. At the first boil, pour it over your eggs
8. Toss them
9. When they are well heated, serve them

title: Oeufs à la Huguenote (Eggs à la Huguenote)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Jus
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Take the serving dish and place it on a moderate fire with a little jus
2. Gently break eggs so that the yolks remain whole
3. Season with salt and coarse pepper
4. Cook the tops with a red-hot iron or shovel
5. Serve semi-soft

title: Oeufs à la Jardinière (Eggs Gardener Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- four or five large onions
- butter
- flour
- a chopine of milk
- salt
- coarse pepper
- ten eggs

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan four or five large onions cut into fillets with a piece of butter
2. Pass them over the fire until almost cooked
3. Then add a good pinch of flour
4. Moisten with a chopine of milk
5. Season with salt and coarse pepper
6. Boil until the sauce is thick
7. Remove from the fire to add ten eggs which you beat together
8. Put everything in the serving plate to cook over a small fire covered with a pie-dish lid

title: Oeufs à la Jardinière (Eggs with Vegetables)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Four or five large onions, cut into strips
- A piece of butter
- A good pinch of flour
- One chopine of milk
- Salt
- Black pepper

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan four or five large onions cut into strips with a piece of butter
2. Pass them over the fire until they are almost cooked
3. Then add a good pinch of flour
4. Moisten with one chopine of milk
5. Season with salt and black pepper
6. Boil until the sauce is thick
7. Remove from the fire to add ten eggs that you beat together
8. Put everything in the dish you must serve
9. Cook it over a small fire covered with a pie-dish lid

title: Oeufs à la Neige (Eggs in Snow)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 eggs
- 2 spoonfuls of powdered sugar
- a little orange flower powder
- 1 pint of milk
- 6 ounces of sugar
- a little orange flower powder or other flavoring

## Directions

1. Break ten eggs and separate the whites and yolks
2. Beat the whites as you would for a biscuit; when they are well set, add two spoonfuls of powdered sugar and a little orange flower powder
3. Pour a pint of milk into a saucepan with six ounces of sugar and a little orange flower powder or other flavoring
4. When the milk boils, take a spoonful of the beaten whites and put it in the milk; poach the whites
5. Let them drain on a sieve; when all are poached, remove half the milk or only a quarter of it
6. Blend the yolks and put them in the milk; stir with a wooden spoon
7. When you see them binding together, remove from heat and strain through cheesecloth into another saucepan
8. Arrange the eggs on the dish and mask them with your sauce
