title: Oeufs à la Pauvre Femme (Poor Woman's Eggs)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- 12 eggs
- bread crumb
- sauce

## Directions

1. Warm a little butter on your dish
2. Break twelve eggs and put them on the heated butter
3. Cut bread crumb into small dice and let it cook in the butter
4. When it is well golden, scrape and scatter it over your eggs
5. Put a hot field oven on top
6. When the eggs are cooked, pour a sauce made of reduced Spanish sauce over them

title: Oeufs à la Provençale (Eggs Provençal)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 glass of oil
- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- lemon zest
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Put a glass of oil in a small pan and heat it well
2. Break an egg into a small vessel, add salt and pepper, and pour it into the oil
3. Spread the boiling white with a spoon
4. Turn it and when it has a nice color on both sides, drain it on a hair sieve
5. Twelve are sufficient for an entremets
6. The eggs must be fresh
7. Arrange them in crowns; after trimming them, put a glazed crouton between each egg
8. Use a reduced Spanish sauce to which you add the zest of half a lemon and pour it underneath

title: Oeufs à la Tripe (Eggs à la Tripe)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 hard eggs
- a good piece of butter
- 12 onions
- a spoonful of flour
- 2 glasses of cream
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Hard boil twelve eggs
2. Cut them into slices and put them in a saucepan
3. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan
4. Cut twelve onions into slices and cook them in white in the butter
5. When they are softened, put in a spoonful of flour that you mix with the onions
6. Add two glasses of cream, salt, and pepper
7. Let the onions simmer and reduce
8. Pour them over your eggs
9. Toss them and serve well hot

title: Oeufs à la Tripe au Roux (Eggs in Brown Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Flour, one spoonful
- Onions, a handful, cut into small squares
- Bouillon
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Vinegar, a dash
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Take a little butter and one spoonful of flour and brown them on the fire
2. Add a handful of onions cut into small squares and cook them in this roux, adding a bit more butter
3. Moisten with bouillon
4. When the onions are cooked, add sliced hard-boiled eggs
5. Bring to a gentle boil
6. Add a dash of vinegar, salt, and pepper
7. Serve with reduced sauce

title: Oeufs à la Tripe aux Concombres (Eggs with Cucumbers)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumbers
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives, chopped
- Flour, a pinch
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Milk

## Directions

1. Cut cucumbers into small pieces the size of a finger
2. Pass them through the fire with butter, parsley, and chopped chives
3. Add a pinch of flour
4. Moisten with a little water seasoned with salt and pepper
5. When they are cooked and there is no more sauce, add sliced hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters
6. Add milk and bring to a gentle boil
7. Taste to ensure good flavor and serve

title: Oie en Daube (Braised Goose)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose, plucked and cleaned
- Lard strips, seasoned with salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Your goose plucked, cleaned, singed and trimmed; truss the legs inside; lard it with strips of lard seasoned with salt and pepper

title: Oie en Daube (Braised Goose)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one goose, plucked, cleaned, singed and plucked
- lardons seasoned with salt, pepper, and quatre épices
- lard for barding
- four carrots
- four onions
- three cloves
- a large bouquet of parsley and chives
- one veal shank, cut into several pieces
- salt
- three spoonfuls of bouillon

## Directions

1. Truss the goose's legs inside
2. Lard the goose with seasoned lardons
3. Place lard rinds in the bottom of a braising pan and put the goose on top
4. Add four carrots, four onions, three cloves, a large bouquet of parsley and chives, and one veal shank cut into several pieces
5. Cover the goose with lard rinds
6. Add salt and three spoonfuls of bouillon
7. Braise for two hours, longer if the goose is old
8. At service time, drain and unbind the goose
9. Strain the cooking liquid through a silk sieve
10. Reduce by half
11. Degrease and glaze the goose
12. Pour the reduction underneath
13. You may serve around it glazed onions, tomato sauce, glazed cabbage or glazed turnips

title: Oie Farcie à la Broche (Roasted Stuffed Goose)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Chestnuts or large chestnuts
- Four or five sausages
- Goose liver
- Lard fat or butter
- One shallot
- A hint of garlic
- Parsley
- Chives
- One young, tender goose

## Directions

1. Peel chestnuts, removing both the first and second skins by roasting in a perforated pan and stirring until the second skin can be removed
2. Reserve some chestnuts for a ragout
3. Chop the remaining chestnuts and place in a casserole with the meat of four or five sausages, the chopped goose liver, two spoonfuls of lard fat or a good piece of butter, one shallot, a hint of garlic, and chopped parsley and chives
4. Pass everything together over the fire for a quarter hour
5. Let cool
6. Empty, singe, and pluck a young tender goose
7. Fill the goose with this stuffing
8. Sew it closed so nothing escapes
9. Roast on a spit
10. Serve with a ragout of chestnuts as found in the ragout section

title: Oie à la Daube (Braised Goose)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One goose not tender enough for the spit
- Bacon lardons
- Parsley
- Chives
- Two shallots
- Half clove of garlic
- Laurel leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Nutmeg
- Two glasses of water
- Two glasses of white wine
- Half glass of brandy

## Directions

1. Empty the goose and truss its legs into the body
2. Singe and pluck it
3. Lard it all over with bacon lardons seasoned and mixed with chopped parsley, chives, two shallots, half a clove of garlic, a laurel leaf, thyme and basil (chopped like powder), salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
4. Tie the goose with string and place it in a pot just fitting its size
5. Add two glasses of water, two glasses of white wine, and half a glass of brandy, plus a little more salt and coarse pepper
6. Seal the pot well and cook over very low heat for three or four hours until the sauce is very short

title: Oie à la Moutarde (Goose with Mustard)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One young, tender goose
- Goose liver
- Two shallots
- Parsley
- Chives
- Laurel leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Mustard
- Breadcrumbs
- Flour
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Singe, pluck, and eviscerate a young tender goose
2. Chop the liver after removing the bitter part and mix it with two chopped shallots, parsley, chives, a laurel leaf, thyme and basil (chopped like powder), a good piece of butter, salt, and coarse pepper
3. Stuff the goose and sew it closed
4. Roast it on a spit, basting from time to time with a little butter
5. As you baste, hold a plate underneath to catch what falls
6. When the goose is almost cooked, mix one spoonful of mustard into the butter used for basting
7. Spread it back on the goose and bread it gradually until the entire top is well covered with breadcrumbs
8. Finish cooking until it has a beautiful golden color
9. For the sauce: in a casserole, place butter the size of half an egg mixed with two pinches of flour, one good spoonful of mustard, one teaspoonful of vinegar, and a small glass of bouillon, salt, and coarse pepper; bind over the fire
10. Serve the sauce under the goose

title: Oignons Glacés (Glazed Onions)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 or 18 onions, all of the same size
- butter
- salt
- coarse pepper
- 1 glass of water
- sugar, the size of half an egg
- butter
- buttered paper
- Spanish sauce or flour
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Peel the onions carefully, taking care not to cut the head and tail too much
2. Butter the bottom of a casserole and add the onions placed on their head side
3. Add salt, a little coarse pepper, a glass of water, sugar the size of half an egg, and as much butter
4. Place a round of buttered paper over the onions
5. Cook on a fairly hot fire
6. When the liquid is reduced by half, place on a gentle fire
7. At the moment of serving, glaze the onions
8. Arrange them on the plate and add Spanish sauce, or alternatively: take a teaspoon of flour mixed with the glaze of the onions, add half a glass of bouillon, turn the sauce, and when it is bound, pour it under the onions
9. Check that it is properly seasoned

title: Oignons glacés (Glazed Onions)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- water
- sugar
- paper

## Directions

1. Take fifteen or eighteen onions, all of the same size
2. Peel them carefully, being careful not to cut the head and tail too much
3. Butter the bottom of a casserole
4. Add the onions head-side down with salt, a little coarse pepper, one glass of water, butter the size of half an egg, and a piece of buttered paper over the onions

title: Omelets of All Kinds (Des Omelettes de Toutes Façons)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Fine salt
- Butter (preparation incomplete)

## Directions

1. Take the desired number of eggs and put them in a pan with fine salt.
2. Beat the eggs well.
3. Melt butter (recipe incomplete at page break)

title: Omelette aux Truffes (Truffle Omelet)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter
- truffles sautéed in espagnole sauce
- espagnole sauce
- flour
- bouillon
- meat jus
- salt
- coarse pepper
- quatre épices

## Directions

1. Break eggs, season and beat them.
2. Put a piece of butter in a pan over a clear fire.
3. As soon as it is melted, add the eggs.
4. Stir the omelet by shaking so it does not stick, or with a fork or spoon, lift them until your omelet is set.
5. Remove the bulk of the inside with a spoon.
6. Take truffles sautéed in a reduced espagnole sauce and put them in the void you have made in the omelet.
7. Fold it so it has the shape of a turnover.
8. Place it on the dish.
9. Chop two truffes very finely.
10. Pass them in a small piece of butter.
11. Add four spoonfuls of espagnole sauce and pour your sauce hot over the omelet.
12. If you do not want to use sauce, take a teaspoonful of flour that you mix with your truffles when they are cooked, and moisten them with bouillon, jus, a little salt, coarse pepper, and very little quatre épices.
13. By this same procedure, you can make omelets with mushrooms, with poultry or game purée, etc.

title: Omelette Soufflée (Soufflé Omelet)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- powdered sugar
- zest of half a lemon
- butter
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Break six eggs and separate the whites and yolks.
2. Add four spoonfuls of powdered sugar.
3. Chop very finely half the zest of a lemon skin and put it with the yolks.
4. Mix them with the sugar and lemon.
5. At the moment of serving the dish, whip your egg whites as for a biscuit.
6. Mix the yolks well with the whites.
7. Then put a quarter pound of butter in the pan over a fire not too hot.
8. As soon as the butter is melted, add the eggs.
9. Stir the omelet so the bottom comes to the top.
10. When you see that the omelet has absorbed the butter, pour it as a turnover onto a buttered dish and place it on a bed of red coals.
11. Throw powdered sugar over the omelet.
12. Place a very hot camp oven over it.
13. Take care lest your omelet take too much color.

title: Omelettes (Basic Omelette)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs, quantity as desired
- Fine salt
- Butter
- Parsley and chives, optional, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Take the quantity of eggs you wish to use and place them in a saucepan with fine salt
2. Beat the eggs well
3. Melt butter in a frying pan
4. Put the eggs into the pan and cook the omelette until it has a nice color on the bottom
5. Flip it into the serving dish
6. Those who like parsley and chives may add them finely chopped

title: Omelettes aux Harengs Sevrés (Smoked Herring Omelettes)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Smoked herrings
- Eggs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Open the herrings along the back and grill them
2. Chop them and put them in the omelette as if you were adding ham
3. Do not add salt to the eggs
4. Finish this omelette as you would the others

title: Omelettes aux Herbes (Herb Omelettes)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Salt
- Butter
- Choice of: bacon, veal kidney, asparagus tips, truffles, mushrooms, morels, or other mushrooms

## Directions

1. Your filling must always be cooked and seasoned as if you were serving it on its own
2. When it is cold, chop it so it mixes well with the eggs
3. Beat everything together
4. Make this omelette in a frying pan as you would the others
5. Adjust the seasoning based on what is in the filling so as not to oversalt the omelette

title: Oreilles d'Agneau farcies (Stuffed Lamb's Ears)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve lamb ears
- blanc (white cooking liquid)
- farce cuite (cooked forcemeat)
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- four eggs
- salt
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)
- parsley

## Directions

1. Let the twelve lamb ears disgorge and blanch
2. Refresh them
3. Dry them
4. Singe them
5. Cook them in a small blanc for an hour and a half
6. Drain them
7. Fill them with cooked forcemeat
8. Dip them in tepid butter, then in breadcrumbs
9. Break four eggs into the rest of your butter
10. Add salt and coarse pepper
11. Beat it all together
12. Dip your ears in it
13. Coat them with egg all over
14. Put them in breadcrumbs so they are completely masked by breadcrumbs
15. Put them on a board
16. At the moment of serving, put your frying fat on the fire
17. When it is hot, put your ears in it
18. Be careful that your frying fat is not too hot
19. When they have a nice colour, remove them and drain them on white linen
20. Fry some parsley
21. Dress your ears and put it on top

title: Oreilles de Cochon (Pig's Ears)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's ears
- Butter
- Twelve large onions
- Flour (one tablespoon)
- Vinegar (half a glass)
- Bouillon (one glass)
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Cook the pig's ears in the same seasoning as for hure (head cheese).
2. When they are cold, cut them into small strips and place them in a casserole.
3. Cut twelve large onions in half, remove the head and tail, and cut them in semi-circles.
4. Pass the onions in a good piece of butter until they are well browned.
5. If you do not have sauce, add one tablespoon of flour which you stir with your onions.
6. Add half a glass of vinegar, one glass of bouillon, salt, and coarse pepper.
7. Let the onions come to a boil a few times.
8. Place them over your sliced pig's ears.
9. Sauté everything together and keep it warm without boiling.
10. At the moment of serving, arrange your ragout on the plate and surround it with croutons.

title: Oreilles de Cochon à la Purée de Lentilles (Pig's Ears with Lentil Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's ears, well flamed and cleaned
- One liter of lentils
- Two carrots
- Three onions, one studded with two cloves
- Two bay leaves
- Salt
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Place one liter of lentils in your casserole and put your pig's ears in it.
2. Add two carrots, three onions (one studded with two cloves), and two bay leaves with salt.
3. If your ears have not come out of the brine, cook everything together.
4. When the ears are cooked, remove them and place them in a casserole with a little bouillon to keep them warm.
5. Put your lentils in a cloth strainer, remove the bouillon, and press them through with a wooden spoon.
6. Pass your purée through the cloth strainer.
7. Add a little bouillon if it is too dry.
8. Put it on the fire and reduce it if it is too thin.
9. Check that it is properly seasoned.
10. At the moment of serving, drain your ears, arrange them on the plate, and mask them with your purée.
11. You can also serve them with purée of peas, beans, onions, tomato sauce, etc., and serve them with the lentils whole if you wish.

title: Oreilles de Veau au Fromage (Calf's Ears with Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- six calf's ears
- a glass of white wine
- twice as much broth
- salt
- pepper
- a bouquet of parsley and chives
- a clove of garlic
- two cloves
- a half bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- a little butter
- a handful of crumb of bread
- a half-setier of milk
- a bit of grated Gruyère cheese
- a little butter
- four raw egg yolks
- hot butter for breading
- a mixture of bread crumb and grated Gruyère cheese

## Directions

1. Scald six calf's ears well
2. Blanch them for a quarter hour in boiling water
3. Remove to cold water and clean off any remaining hairs
4. Put them to cook with a glass of white wine and twice as much broth, salt, pepper, a bouquet of parsley and chives, a clove of garlic, two cloves, a half bay leaf, thyme, basil, and a little butter
5. When cooked, drain them
6. Make a stuffing: heat a handful of bread crumb over the fire with a half-setier of milk and a bit of grated Gruyère cheese
7. Stir until the bread crumb is well thickened
8. Let cool, then add a little butter and four raw egg yolks
9. Mix together and stuff the inside of the ears
10. Dip the ears in warm butter to bread them, using a mixture of half bread crumb and half grated Gruyère cheese
11. Arrange in the serving dish
12. Brown them nicely under a pastry cover
13. Wipe the edges of the dish and serve without sauce

title: Oreilles de Veau aux Pois (Calf's Ears with Peas)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- four calf's ears
- clear broth
- lemon or verjuice in grain
- salt
- pepper
- a bouquet of parsley
- cloves
- a hint of garlic
- a bay leaf
- one and a half liters of small peas
- a piece of butter
- a bouquet of parsley and chives
- a pinch of flour
- half juice and half broth
- sugar the size of a walnut
- fine salt
- coulis (optional)

## Directions

1. Boil the four calf's ears for a moment in hot water, then remove to cold water
2. When well cleaned, cook them with clear broth, a little lemon or verjuice in grain, salt, pepper, a bouquet of parsley, cloves, a hint of garlic, and a bay leaf
3. When cooked and white, remove and set aside
4. For the peas: pass them over the fire with a piece of butter, a bouquet of parsley and chives
5. Add a pinch of flour and moisten with half juice and half broth
6. Cook over low heat
7. When cooked, add sugar the size of a walnut and a little fine salt
8. If you have coulis, add a spoonful
9. The ragout should not be thin
10. Serve over the calf's ears

title: Oreilles de Veau aux Pois (Calf's Ears with Peas)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Four calf's ears
- water
- clear bouillon
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take four ears and boil them a moment in hot water, then remove to fresh water
2. When well cleaned, cook them with clear bouillon and a little lemon

title: Oreilles de Veau de Plusieurs Façons (Calf's Ears Multiple Ways)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf's ears
- good bouillon
- half a septier of white wine
- half a lemon
- or verjus in grain
- bouquet garni
- salt
- carrots
- lard
- sauce

## Directions

1. Take well-blanched ears and blanch them, then pluck them so no hair remains
2. Make a braise this way: put good bouillon in a small pot, half a septier of white wine, half a lemon cut in slices with skin removed, or verjus in grain if in season
3. Add a bouquet garni, salt and some carrots
4. Cook the ears in this, cover them with slices of lard
5. When cooked, serve with piquant sauce
6. You can also make them into smaller dishes; you can stuff them, dip them in beaten eggs to bread them and serve them fried
7. Always cook them in the braise first before preparing them any other way

title: Oreilles de Veau en marinade (Calf's Ears in Marinade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's ears
- blanc
- marinade
- batter for frying

## Directions

1. Prepare the ears as for the Italian style version
2. Cook them in a blanc
3. When cooked, drain them
4. Cut them lengthwise into two, three or four pieces as desired
5. Pour marinade over them a quarter hour before serving
6. Drain them
7. Put them in frying batter

title: Oreilles de Veau farcies (Stuffed Calf's Ears)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's ears
- cooked forcemeat
- egg
- oil or lard for frying
- clear jus

## Directions

1. Prepare the ears and cook them as in the previous recipe
2. Stuff them with cooked forcemeat
3. Mix the forcemeat in well
4. Bread the ears in egg
5. Fry them
6. Put clear jus underneath

title: Oreilles de Veau à l'Italienne (Calf's Ears Italian Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- eight calf's ears, blanched
- lard bards
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Soak eight calf's ears that have been blanched
2. Blanch them
3. Refresh them in cold water
4. Singe them
5. Wipe them dry
6. Slit the tip of the ear, cutting to the middle
7. Put lard bards on the bottom of a casserole
8. Place the ears on them
9. Cover them with lard bards
10. Pour a poêle over them and cover the inside of the casserole with buttered paper
11. Simmer for two and a half hours
12. Drain them
13. Arrange them on the platter and sauce them with an Italian sauce. If you do not have a poêle, place them between two lard bards and prepare a short blanc, pour it over them, and cook them.

title: Oreilles de Veau à la Tartare (Calf's Ears Tartare Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- four calf's ears
- a skewer for each ear
- butter
- bread crumb
- a little broth
- verjuice
- shallots
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Blanch four calf's ears in boiling water
2. Split them at the thick end without separating them completely
3. Pass a skewer through each one to keep them open
4. Put them in a small pot to cook the same way as the previous recipe
5. When cooked and well drained, dip them in warm butter and bread them with bread crumb
6. Grill them, lightly basting with the remaining butter
7. When they have a nice color, serve them with a clear sauce made of a little broth, verjuice, chopped shallots, salt, and coarse pepper

title: Ortolans (Ortolans)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- ortolans
- small silver skewers

## Directions

1. Lard them while putting them on small silver skewers
2. Put them to a high fire
3. Seven or eight minutes are sufficient to cook them

title: Oysters in Hash (Huîtres en Hachis)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- butter
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- flour
- white wine
- lean bouillon
- egg yolks
- cream

## Directions

1. Take fifty oysters in a basket that you put in hot water; when it is ready to boil, you remove it to put them in fresh water, drain them, and then take only the tender parts.
2. If you take all of it, let the hard part be chopped very fine.
3. Put the rest with it to chop as well.
4. If you wish to mix with carp meat, that will increase your hash and give it good taste.
5. Put in a pot a good piece of butter, with parsley, chive, chopped mushrooms, pass them over the fire and add a pinch of flour; moisten them afterwards with half a setier of white wine and as much lean bouillon.
6. Put in to cook your hash, until there is no more sauce, and season it to good taste.
7. When you are ready to serve, add a liaison of three egg yolks and cream; serve for an entrée.

title: Oysters in Ragout (Huîtres en Ragoût)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- bouillon
- anchovies
- salt

## Directions

1. Blanch them in their water over very low heat; be careful they do not boil, as that would shrivel them.
2. Put them afterwards in fresh water, remove them next to drain well on a sieve, you then have a good fatty bouillon without salt; put two chopped anchovies and the oysters; heat them without letting them boil, and serve as you judge appropriate.

title: Oysters, Grilled (Huîtres Grillées)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- oysters
- butter
- pepper
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Open them and put in melted butter, a little pepper, breadcrumbs, cook them on the grill with a red hot pan on top.

title: Pain de Lapin à la Saint-Ursain (Rabbit Loaf Saint-Ursain Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbit quenelle forcemeat
- Butter
- Rabbit brains
- Rabbit fillets
- Rabbit kidneys
- Espagnole sauce
- Game fumé
- Champagne wine
- Optional: veal sweetbreads, cockscombs

## Directions

1. Fill a hollowed out mold (well buttered) with quenelle forcemeat
2. Place it in a bain-marie and let it simmer
3. When the forcemeat in your mold is cooked, unmold it onto your dish at the moment of serving, ensuring there is no water
4. Place in the hollow of your loaf sautéed rabbit brains, fillets and kidneys
5. Prepare an espagnole sauce worked with game fumé and half a glass of Champagne wine
6. When your sauce is well reduced, pour it over the garnishings inside your loaf and glaze the outside
7. Alternative garnishings may include small veal sweetbreads, cockscombs, etc.

title: Pain de Lièvre (Hare Terrine)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Forcemeat made with hare meat
- Hare liver
- Thin lard bacon slices
- Boiling water

## Directions

1. Prepare a forcemeat as described previously
2. Pound the hare liver well and pass it through a quenelle sieve
3. Collect all of the sieved liver without losing any
4. Mix the liver into the forcemeat
5. Fill a mold almost completely with the forcemeat
6. Line the bottom and sides of the mold with thin slices of bacon
7. Place the forcemeat inside the mold
8. Put boiling water in a casserole
9. Place the mold in the casserole such that the water comes up to within a finger's breadth of the top
10. Simmer the water gently
11. Place bacon slices on top of the terrine
12. Cover the casserole with a lid and place fire on top
13. Test the terrine after one hour to see if it is cooked
14. Remove from the mold at the moment of serving
15. Glaze it and serve with a game sauce
16. If desired, make a hole in the middle of the terrine to place sautéed hare kidneys with Champagne wine, or tenderloin fillets inside (this can only be done for a large game preparation)

title: Pain de Purée de Perdreaux (Partridge Purée Bread)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge purée
- ten to twelve egg yolks
- butter
- blonde sauce made with game essence

## Directions

1. Make a purée as above
2. Add ten to twelve raw egg yolks to the mortar
3. Pass your purée through a sieve
4. When it is passed, have a mold that is hollow in the middle
5. If you want to put a small ragout in it, butter it and put your purée inside
6. Place your mold in a water bath
7. Let it simmer for three-quarters of an hour
8. At the moment of serving, turn your bread out onto your plate
9. Moisten it with a blonde sauce made with game essence, well flavored

title: Palais au Gratin (Beef Palate Gratin)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate, cooked in blanc
- cooked farce
- veal teat, well cooked and thin
- lard
- italienne sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the cooked beef palate into long bands fifteen lines wide
2. Spread cooked farce on the underside of the palate (the side without a raised edge)
3. Place a thin band of well-cooked veal teat on the farce, add a little more farce on top
4. Roll the band and arrange it around the inside of the dish where you have placed farce
5. Form a cordon of farce in the bottom that makes a turban shape
6. Cover the palate with lard so that the heat from the oven or field oven does not roast the palate
7. After approximately fifteen minutes to a half hour in the oven, remove the fat
8. Pour italienne sauce into the middle
9. Optionally, place scarlet tongue between each piece of beef palate to form a crest; these would be called palais à la Saint-Garat

title: Palais de Bœuf au beurre d'anchois (Beef Palate with Anchovy Butter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef palate
- Spanish sauce
- anchovy butter

## Directions

1. Your palates cooked, drain and cut them into not very large rounds
2. Reduce a Spanish sauce
3. When your sauce is ready, pass it through a sieve
4. Let your sauce be boiling at the moment of serving
5. Put in an amount of anchovy butter the size of half an egg
6. Be careful to stir it well in your sauce
7. Put your palates in and toss them
8. Do not put your ragout on the fire so that your sauce does not boil
9. However serve very hot

title: Palais de Bœuf en Menus Droits (Beef Palate in Fine Strips)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate
- onion
- butter
- good bouillon
- coulis
- bouquet garni
- mustard

## Directions

1. Clean the palate well and cook it in water
2. Peel off the skins and cut into slices
3. Sauté onion in butter over the fire
4. When half cooked, add the palate slices
5. Moisten the ragout with good bouillon, a little coulis, and a bouquet garni
6. Season to taste
7. When well degreased and the sauce sufficiently reduced, add a little mustard when serving

title: Palais de Bœuf Mariné (Marinated Beef Palate)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate cooked in water
- salt
- pepper
- garlic clove
- vinegar
- bouillon
- butter
- flour
- bay leaf
- three cloves

## Directions

1. Peel the cooked palate and cut it the length and width of a finger
2. Make a marinade with salt, pepper, a garlic clove, vinegar, a little bouillon, a small piece of butter mixed with flour, a bay leaf, and three cloves
3. Warm the marinade and place the beef palate in it for two or three hours
4. Remove and drain the palate, dry it, flour it
5. Fry and serve with fried parsley

title: Palais de Bœuf à la Lyonnaise (Beef Palate Lyonnaise)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef palate
- brown onion purée

## Directions

1. When your beef palates are cooked, cut them into round or square pieces
2. Put them in a brown onion purée
3. Make sure your ragout does not boil, but is well hot

title: Palais à l'Allemande (Beef Palate in the German Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate cooked in blanc
- velouté
- egg yolks
- parsley

## Directions

1. When your palates are cooked in blanc, drain them
2. Cut them as for béchamel
3. Have well reduced velouté
4. Bind it with two egg yolks
5. Pass your bound sauce through a sieve and put it over your palates which are in a saucepan
6. Add finely chopped parsley that you have blanched
7. Toss your palates in your sauce
8. Serve them well hot

title: Palais à la Béchamelle (Beef Palate with Béchamel Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate cooked in blanc
- béchamel sauce
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Have beef palates cooked in blanc that you cut into small squares, that is to say the size of a small coin
2. Have a béchamel sauce
3. Toss them in your sauce
4. Make sure it is neither too thin nor too thick
5. Add a little coarse pepper
6. Serve it well hot

title: Panade (Bread Panade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Soft bread crumb (soft-boiled is best)
- Water
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Butter, about the size of half an egg (more or less, depending on how strong the panade should be)
- Two or three eggs for binding (depending on the quantity of panade)

## Directions

1. Put the bread crumb in a small earthenware pot or other vessel with water, salt, and a bit of coarse pepper
2. Add the butter
3. Simmer everything together for one hour
4. At the moment of serving, add a binding of two or three eggs
5. Take care that your panade does not boil when the binding is in it

title: Partridges with Cabbage (Perdrix aux Choux)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- barding lard
- salt pork (petit lard)
- cervelat sausage or ham
- veal slices
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- bay leaves
- cabbage
- buttered paper
- bouillon
- espagnole sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare partridges as for stewed partridges
2. Place bards of lard in a casserole with the partridges, a pound of blanched and cleaned salt pork, a large cervelat or a piece of ham, and a few slices of veal
3. Cover the partridges with bards of lard
4. Add four carrots, four onions, two cloves, and two bay leaves
5. Blanch the cabbage, tie with twine, and press them
6. Place the cabbage on top of the partridges
7. Cover with bards of lard and a round of buttered paper
8. Add two spoonfuls of bouillon
9. Simmer for two hours
10. At service, drain and untie them
11. Dress on a platter
12. Drain and press the cabbage to dry, and dress around the partridges
13. Cut the lard into pieces and place at intervals on the cabbage with the cervelat
14. Top with espagnole sauce

title: Paupiette de Palais de Bœuf (Beef Palate Paupiette)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef palate
- sauce à atelet
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the palate as in the previous recipe
2. Cut bands wider so that your roll will be somewhat long, about like a croquette
3. Coat with sauce à atelet around it (to coat your paupiette)
4. Bread it with egg and fry it like croquettes
5. Place parsley around and on top; serve without sauce underneath

title: Paupiettes (Veal Rolls)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal slices, two fingers wide and at least three fingers long
- Forcemeat of any meat, or godiveau made with a little veal round, as much beef fat, a little parsley, chives, one shallot
- Two egg yolks
- Half a tablespoon of brandy
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lard barding

## Directions

1. Cut veal slices and flatten them with a cleaver so they are no thicker than a small coin
2. Spread forcemeat or godiveau on each slice
3. To make godiveau: chop the veal round, beef fat, parsley, chives, and shallot very finely; mix in two egg yolks, half a tablespoon of brandy, salt, and pepper
4. Roll the slices and tie them
5. Place a lard barding on each paupiette and tie with string
6. Cook on a spit wrapped in paper
7. When cooked, bread the top of the barding and give it a golden color over clear heat
8. Serve with a sauce of clear jus, well seasoned

title: Paupiettes de Filets de Merlans (Whiting Fillet Paupiettes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Whiting fillets
- Fish forcemeat
- Lard strips or buttered paper
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the fillets as for sautéing
2. Trim them evenly
3. On the inside, spread a little fish forcemeat equally
4. Roll the fillet over it so it forms a barrel shape
5. Put some of the same forcemeat on your platter and arrange your fillets around and in the middle
6. Cover them with lard strips or double buttered paper
7. Put them in the oven or on a stove with a camp oven on top; half an hour is sufficient to cook them
8. Mask them with Italian sauce

title: Paupiettes à la Braise (Braised Veal Rolls)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal slices
- Forcemeat or godiveau
- Egg yolks
- Brandy
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lard barding
- Half a glass of white wine
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Make paupiettes the same way as the previous recipe, but without lard barding on top
2. Put lard barding in the bottom of a casserole
3. Arrange paupiettes on the barding
4. Cook over very low heat with half a glass of white wine and as much bouillon, a little salt, and coarse pepper
5. When cooked, arrange them in the serving dish
6. Degrease the cooking sauce, pass it through a sieve, and serve it over the paupiettes

title: Peas Preserved for Winter
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Small peas
- Good corks
- String
- Water
- Pitch or tar

## Directions

1. Fill bottles with small peas
2. Cork them well with very good corks and tie them with string
3. Place the bottles in a large pot with cold water
4. Put it on the fire; when the water has boiled for half an hour, remove the pot from the fire
5. Let the water cool and remove the bottles
6. Pitch the corks and the bottle opening
7. To use, cook in cold water and use the same way as other fresh peas

title: Perche (Perch)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- perch
- white wine
- capers or other sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the gills and empty the perch, removing only half of its roe.
2. Cook in a court-bouillon with white wine.
3. When cooked, remove the scales, arrange on the serving plate, and cover with a sauce of capers or another sauce as you judge appropriate, or some lean ragout.
4. If serving in fat, the sauce or ragout will make the difference.

title: Perdreau en Papillotes (Partridge in Paper)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge
- butter
- fine herbs for papillotes
- paper
- oil
- thin bacon strips
- clear jus

## Directions

1. When your partridge is cleaned and singed, cut it in half from the neck to the rump and separate the pieces
2. Put butter in a saucepan and let the partridge pieces finish cooking for seven or eight minutes until almost cooked
3. Let them cool on a plate
4. Add fine herbs for papillotes on top
5. When the partridges are cold, cut a square of paper large enough to contain half of your partridge
6. Spread oil on your paper
7. Place a thin strip of bacon on it, your partridge half on top with your fine herbs, and another thin strip of bacon to cover it
8. Fold your paper over it and pleat it into papillote form
9. Half an hour before serving, place your papillotes on the grill over a very gentle fire
10. Arrange them in crowns on your plate with clear jus underneath

title: Perdreau Grillé (Grilled Partridge)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge
- butter
- salt
- coarse pepper
- breadcrumbs
- remoulade or tartar sauce
- clear jus or piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare your partridge as in the previous recipe; melt butter in a saucepan and dip your partridge in it so that it is covered with butter everywhere
2. Season it with salt and coarse pepper
3. Roll it in breadcrumbs so it is well breaded all over
4. Half an hour before serving, place your partridge on the grill over a gentle fire
5. When serving, place remoulade or tartar sauce on your plate and your partridge on top of it
6. If desired, you can serve clear jus underneath or a piquant sauce

title: Perdreau Sauté (Sautéed Partridge)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- partridge
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- espagnole sauce
- lemon
- flour
- white wine
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Prepare your partridge as in the tartar recipe
2. Place a good piece of butter in a saucepan
3. Place your partridge in it stomach-side down
4. Twenty minutes before serving, cook it over a high heat
5. Add salt and pepper
6. Turn it when you feel it is firm under your finger
7. Remove it from heat and place it on the plate with espagnole sauce reduced with the juice of half a lemon
8. If you have neither sauce nor lemon, remove three-quarters of the butter from your saucepan
9. Add a little flour, a half-glass of white wine, as much bouillon, salt, and pepper
10. Let your sauce boil a few times and place it under your partridge

title: Perdreau Sauté (Sautéed Partridge)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 partridge
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare your partridge as you would for the tartare partridge
2. Place a good piece of butter in a saucepan (recipe continues beyond the page)

title: Perdreau à la Tartare (Partridge Tartare)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 partridge
- butter
- salt
- coarse pepper
- bread crumbs
- rémoulade or tartare sauce

## Directions

1. Clean, singe, truss, and split your partridge as in the previous recipe
2. Warm butter in a saucepan and dip your partridge in it so it is well coated
3. Dust with salt and coarse pepper
4. Roll it in bread crumbs so it is well breaded all over
5. Half an hour before serving, place your partridge on a grill over gentle heat
6. When serving, place rémoulade or tartare sauce on your platter with the partridge on top
7. If desired, you can serve clear jus underneath or a sharp sauce

title: Perdreaux en Entrée (Young Partridges as an Entrée Dish)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Young partridges
- Their livers
- Lard, grated
- Salt
- Parsley, chopped
- Chives, chopped
- Butter
- Lard for wrapping
- Paper for wrapping

## Directions

1. Pluck and singe the partridges, then gut them
2. Make a small forcemeat from their livers mixed with grated lard, a little salt, chopped parsley and chives
3. Place this forcemeat inside the body and sew it closed so nothing escapes
4. Truss the legs over the breast
5. Reheat them in a casserole over the fire with a little butter
6. Cook them on the spit wrapped in lard and paper
7. When cooked, serve them with the sauce or ragout of your choice

title: Perdreaux Poêlés (Braised Partridges)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three fresh partridges of the same size
- Three-quarters of a pound of butter
- Salt
- Juice of one lemon
- Coarse pepper
- Aromatic herbs, pounded
- Lard strips
- Lemon slices
- Three spoonfuls of Spanish sauce
- Three spoonfuls of game essence

## Directions

1. Select three fresh partridges of equal size, not skinned on the breast or bruised
2. Empty them through the vent, removing the intestines, gizzard, and liver to avoid making incisions elsewhere
3. Singe them lightly when well plucked
4. Mix three-quarters of a pound of butter with a little salt, the juice of one lemon, some coarse pepper, and pounded aromatic herbs, all well combined
5. Fill your partridges with this butter
6. Truss and tie them as you would a braised hen
7. Place strips of lard in a casserole, put the partridges on top
8. Cover them with lemon slices and strips of lard
9. Pour a poêle over them
10. Simmer for a good half hour
11. Drain them
12. At the moment of serving, remove the trussing strings and arrange on the platter
13. Place a fine crayfish between each partridge
14. Add three spoonfuls of Spanish sauce and three spoonfuls of game essence reduced by half, and pour under the partridges
15. If you do not have sauce or game essence, make a light roux and strain through silk sieve the cooking liquid from the partridges
16. Moisten it and add half a glass of white wine
17. If your sauce is too thin, reduce it until it is thick enough to sauce the partridges
18. Be sure to skim it and strain it through a fine sieve
19. Check that it is properly salted

title: Perdreaux à l'Espagnole (Partridges in Spanish Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridges
- Six spoonfuls of Spanish sauce
- Half a bottle of white wine
- Bay leaf
- Bouquet of parsley and chives
- Clove

## Directions

1. Place partridges on a moderately hot fire
2. Brown them slowly so they do not take on color
3. When well browned, add six spoonfuls of Spanish sauce, half a bottle of white wine, a bay leaf, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and a clove
4. Simmer the partridges for three quarters of an hour
5. Remove them when cooked and place in a casserole
6. Skim the sauce and reduce it by half
7. Strain it through a sieve over the partridges
8. At the moment of serving, remove the trussing strings and arrange on the platter
9. Serve the sauce underneath
10. If you do not have Spanish sauce, leave the partridges in their butter, make a light roux which you will moisten with white wine and half a bottle of bouillon, pour this over the partridges with the seasoning
11. When you reduce your sauce, skim it as much as possible and reduce it until it is thick enough to serve as sauce
12. Check that it is properly salted

title: Perdreaux à la Monglas (Partridges Monglas Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridges
- Two or three truffles
- About twenty mushrooms
- Six spoonfuls of Spanish sauce
- Six spoonfuls of game essence

## Directions

1. Empty and singe your partridges
2. Truss them as you would a braised hen
3. Put them on the spit
4. When cooked, let them cool
5. Remove the breast meat in such a way that the rest of the body forms an oval well
6. Cut the meat into small dice and put in a casserole
7. Cut two or three truffles also into small dice
8. Have about twenty mushrooms, cut them small
9. Mix them together with your truffles and your partridge meat
10. Add six spoonfuls of Spanish sauce and six spoonfuls of game essence
11. Reduce everything by a third
12. Strain through a fine sieve over your small ragout
13. Keep your partridges warm in a casserole
14. At the moment of serving, remove the trussing strings
15. Put your ragout inside your partridges with Spanish sauce underneath

title: Perdreaux à la Saint-Laurent (Partridges Saint-Laurent)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- salt
- coarse pepper
- oil
- 2 tablespoons of Spanish sauce
- juice of 1.5 lemons
- lemon zest
- or: fine bread crumbs (brown crusts), 7 or 8 shallots, pepper, salt, 1/2 glass of water, 6 tablespoons of vinegar, 4 tablespoons of oil

## Directions

1. Clean and singe your partridges; cut off the legs and truss the thighs inside without making a large hole near the croupion
2. Split the partridge down the back from neck to croupion
3. Spread open the partridge and flatten it on the breast so it has a greater surface area
4. Dust with salt and coarse pepper
5. Place the breast side in a saucepan with oil and brown it over heat
6. Turn to the other side to brown equally
7. Half an hour before serving, place on a grill over moderate heat
8. Take 2 tablespoons of Spanish sauce, juice of 1.5 lemons, and zest it slightly to put in your sauce with salt and coarse pepper; bring the sauce to a boil once only
9. Place the partridge on the platter and sauce over it
10. If you have neither sauce nor lemon, finely bread some brown bread crusts; chop 7 or 8 shallots with a little pepper and salt, 1/2 glass of water, 6 tablespoons of vinegar, and 4 tablespoons of oil
11. Bring this sauce to a boil 2 or 3 times and place it over your partridge

title: Petit Salé de Cochon (Cured Pork)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork (various cuts, loin preferred)
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut pork into pieces of desired size
2. Crush salt; for every 15 pounds of meat, use 1 pound of salt
3. Rub the meat thoroughly with salt
4. Place in a vessel as it is salted
5. When full, seal the vessel well to prevent air exposure
6. Can be used after 5 or 6 days
7. For longer storage, use slightly more salt
8. Note that fresher cured meat is better
9. Serve with pea purée, cabbage ragout, vegetable ragout, lentil purée, or turnip purée
10. Do not add salt to the accompanying ragout

title: Petite Bécassine (Small Snipe)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Snipes
- A good piece of butter
- Chopped shallots
- A little grated nutmeg
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Juice of two lemons
- Half a glass of white wine
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Truss your snipes, singe them and pluck them
2. Put a good piece of butter in a casserole with your snipes, chopped shallots, a little grated nutmeg, salt, and coarse pepper
3. Cook them on a hot fire; when you have sautéed them for seven or eight minutes, add the juice of two lemons, half a glass of white wine, and a little bread crumbs
4. Leave them on the fire until your sauce has come to a boil; then remove them from the fire and serve

title: Petite Béchamel (Small Béchamel Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately one pound veal cut into dice
- one half-pound ham cut into dice
- a few carrots
- a few small onions
- three cloves
- two bay leaves
- a little basil
- one half-pound butter
- 5 tablespoons wheat flour
- 2 pints milk

## Directions

1. Place the veal, ham, carrots, onions, cloves, bay leaves, basil, and butter in a saucepan
2. Brown the mixture over heat, stirring with a spoon and being careful that the meat does not take on color
3. When the meat is well browned, add the wheat flour and stir
4. When the flour is well mixed with the butter and meat, add the milk
5. Stir constantly so the sauce does not stick
6. Simmer over moderate heat for one hour and a half
7. If the sauce reduces too much, add more milk or broth
8. The sauce should have the consistency of porridge when served
9. If it is too thick, add a little milk, cream, or broth
10. Do not oversalt this sauce because of the ham and because it must be reduced
11. Strain through a sieve

title: Petits Oignons Glacés (Glazed Small Onions)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- small onions
- sugar
- broth or water

## Directions

1. Peel your small onions carefully, taking care not to cut the head or tail too much and not to damage the skin
2. Butter the bottom of your saucepan and place the head of your small onions upside down
3. Add sugar the size of a walnut and broth or water up to the tail of the small onions
4. Put them over a hot fire
5. When the liquid is reduced three-quarters, reduce the heat to low and let them glaze
6. Use them when necessary

title: Petits Pois Verts au Petit Beurre (Green Peas with Butter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Peas
- Butter
- Water
- Salt
- Black pepper
- Sugar
- Parsley
- Chives

## Directions

1. Take two liters of peas.
2. Put a quarter pound of butter in them.
3. Sprinkle with water and knead them together with your hands.
4. Let them drain in a strainer.
5. Put them in a saucepan over a hot fire.
6. Sauté the peas when they have felt the heat well.
7. Moisten them with boiling water.
8. Add salt, black pepper, the equivalent of half a walnut of sugar, a bouquet of parsley and chives.
9. Reduce the liquid until there is almost none left.
10. At the moment of serving, when the peas are boiling, add three small pieces of butter, or about the size of two eggs of butter.
11. Sauté them without putting them on the fire until they are well bound.
12. Arrange them in a bush shape; check that they have good salt.

title: Petits Pois à l'Anglaise (English-Style Peas)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Salt
- Peas
- Butter

## Directions

1. Bring water to a boil in a large saucepan three-quarters of an hour before serving.
2. When the water boils, add salt and your peas.
3. Let them boil until the moment of serving.
4. Drain them in a strainer.
5. Put them on the plate with six small pieces of butter on top, or only a quarter pound and a half of butter, without mixing it with the peas.

title: Petits Pois à la Bourgeoise (Peas Bourgeoise)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Light white roux
- Peas
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onions
- Parsley
- Chives
- Romaine lettuce
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make a light white roux.
2. Add the peas; when they are well browned, moisten with boiling water.
3. Add salt, pepper, four onions, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and sliced romaine lettuce.
4. Let them reduce while cooking.
5. When the peas are cooked and at the moment of serving, add a liaison of three egg yolks.
6. Do not let them boil with the liaison for fear it will break; make sure they are well seasoned.

title: Petits Pois à la Demi-Bourgeoise (Peas in the Half-Bourgeoise Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One and a half litron of small peas
- Butter
- Parsley
- Ciboule
- One head lettuce, cut in four
- Two egg yolks
- Cream

## Directions

1. Wash the small peas and place them in a saucepan with a piece of butter, a bouquet of parsley and ciboule, and the head lettuce cut in four
2. Cook them in their own juices over very low heat
3. When the peas are cooked and there is almost no sauce remaining, add a liaison of two egg yolks mixed with cream
4. Allow the liaison to thicken on the fire and serve

title: Petits Pots au Café Noir (Little Pots with Black Coffee)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 small pots of coffee with water
- 3 ounces of sugar
- 6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Measure 8 small pots of coffee made with water
2. Add 3 ounces of sugar, or more if the pots are large
3. Bring to a boil and allow to cool
4. Break 6 egg yolks into a casserole and mix them with the coffee
5. Pass the mixture four times through cheesecloth
6. When the water boils, fill your small pots and place them in a bain-marie

title: Petits Pots au Café Vierge (Little Pots with Virgin Coffee)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 small pots of cream
- 1 quarteron of sugar
- 2 ounces of coffee
- 6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Measure out 10 small pots of cream and bring to a boil
2. Add 1 quarteron of sugar and keep the cream hot
3. Toast 2 ounces of coffee until blonde
4. Place the coffee directly from the pan into the cream and immediately cover with a casserole lid
5. Allow the cream to cool
6. Pass the cream through a sieve to remove the coffee grounds
7. In a casserole, beat 6 egg yolks and mix them with your cream
8. Pass the entire mixture four times through cheesecloth
9. Bring water to a boil and pour the cream into 7 small pots
10. Place them in a bain-marie to set

title: Petits Pots au Café Vierge (Small Pots with Virgin Coffee)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Ten measures of cream
- One quarter-pound of sugar
- Two ounces of coffee
- Six egg yolks

## Directions

1. Measure ten small pots of cream and bring it to boil
2. Put in one quarter-pound of sugar and keep your cream hot
3. Roast two ounces of coffee; when it is blonde, put it fresh from the pan into the cream
4. Immediately place a saucepan lid on top
5. Let the cream cool and strain it through a sieve to remove the coffee grain
6. Then put six egg yolks in a saucepan

title: Petits Pots au Caramel (Little Pots with Caramel)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar the size of half an egg
- water
- citron zest
- 8 small pots of water
- 1 quarteron of sugar
- 6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put sugar the size of half an egg in a casserole with a little water and citron zest
2. Boil until the caramel is made and has a slightly brown color
3. Measure 8 small pots of water and add 1 quarteron of sugar and a little citron zest
4. Bring to a boil and allow to cool
5. Break 6 egg yolks and mix them with the liquid
6. Pass four times through cheesecloth
7. When the water boils, fill your small pots and place them in a bain-marie

title: Petits Pots aux Pistaches (Little Pots with Pistachios)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 small pots of cream
- 1 quarteron of sugar
- 1 quarteron of pistachios
- 1 whole egg
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 teaspoon of spinach juice

## Directions

1. Fill 10 small pots with cream and pour into a casserole
2. Bring the cream to a boil
3. Add 1 quarteron of sugar
4. Blanch 1 quarteron of pistachios and pound them finely
5. Mix the pistachios with the boiling cream and bring to a boil
6. Allow to cool
7. In a casserole, beat 1 whole egg and 4 egg yolks and mix with your preparation
8. Add 1 teaspoon of spinach juice
9. Pass five or six times through cheesecloth
10. When the water boils, fill the small pots and place them in a bain-marie

title: Petits Pots à la Fleur d'Orange (Small Pots with Orange Flower Water)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Seven measures of cream or good milk
- Three ounces of sugar
- One spoonful of candied orange flower water

## Directions

1. Measure seven times one small pot of cream or good milk and put in three ounces of sugar and one spoonful of candied orange flower water
2. Bring your milk to a boil and let it cool
3. If your pots are large, put seven egg yolks; if small, five are sufficient
4. Mix them with your milk and strain the composition four or five times through cloth
5. Check that the cream tastes good and pour it into your small pots
6. Put water in a large saucepan and bring it to boil and place the small pots in it
7. They must be placed in the boiling water only up to three-quarters height so water does not enter; place the lid of the saucepan and put fire on top
8. Let your water bath go very gently
9. After ten minutes, check if your small pots are set; remove them, dry them, and serve them cold
10. You can give these small pots any flavor you wish: orange flower, lemon, tea, vanilla, violet, etc.

title: Petits-Pâtés Friands (Small Savory Pastries)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- small piece of veal round
- beef marrow equal in amount to the veal
- parsley, chives, and mushrooms, all chopped
- two whole eggs
- salt and pepper
- half a setier of cream
- puff pastry
- egg for glazing
- cooked chicken breast from the spit
- one chopine of good bouillon
- small bouquet of mixed herbs
- butter
- three egg yolks
- cream for liaison
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Make small ordinary pastries with puff pastry
2. Chop together a piece of veal round and beef marrow
3. Add chopped parsley, chives, and mushrooms
4. Mix in two whole eggs, salt, and pepper
5. Dilute this mixture with half a setier of cream and taste to correct seasoning
6. Use small pastry molds and line with thin layers of puff pastry about the thickness of a small coin
7. Spread some of this forcemeat on the pastry and cover with another layer of pastry
8. Glaze with egg and bake in the oven
9. While they bake, chop cooked chicken breast very finely
10. Put about one chopine of good bouillon in a saucepan with a small bouquet of mixed herbs and a little butter
11. Reduce the bouillon to a quarter of its volume and remove the bouquet
12. Add the chopped chicken breast and a little salt
13. Heat without boiling and add a liaison of three egg yolks mixed with cream
14. Thicken on the fire and add lemon juice
15. When the pastries are removed from the oven, remove the top of each to take out the meat
16. Replace the meat with a spoonful of the chicken ragout
17. Replace the cover on each small pastry and serve as hot as possible

title: Pheasant Fillets with Truffles (Filets de Faisan aux truffes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant fillets
- Truffles
- Lard
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Butter
- Spanish sauce
- Game stock

## Directions

1. Remove, trim, and lard your fillets; cook them as previously described
2. Remove the nerve from the tenderloin; cut a thin round of truffle and divide it in half
3. Make six incisions in your small fillet at equal distance, and place your half-circle of truffles in them
4. Once your small fillets are all garnished, give them a half-round shape
5. Place them on a tart pan between two lards of bacon, with a little salt and coarse pepper
6. At serving time, place them on a stove, and a hot country oven on top, or sauté them in butter
7. Drain your large fillets; glaze them; put a sauté of truffles on your plate and your fillets on top; in the middle, arrange your small fillets garnished with truffles

title: Pheasant Périgord Style (Faisan à la Périgordux)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One pheasant
- One and a half pounds of truffles
- Half pound of lard
- Quarter pound of butter
- Quarter pound of oil
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Four spices
- Lard bacon

## Directions

1. Empty and singe your pheasant; break the two breast bones; it must be emptied by the pocket so as not to damage the croup of your pheasant
2. Have a pound and a half of truffles which you clean and peel, that is to say remove the top layer of your truffles which you set aside
3. Chop them very finely; place then a half pound of grated lard in a casserole, a quarter pound of butter, a quarter pound of oil which you will heat
4. Put your truffles cut into pieces about the size of a large nut; sauté them; add a little salt, a little coarse pepper, a little of the four spices
5. When the truffles have boiled in your lard for five minutes, put your chopped trimmed pieces in; let them cool, and put everything by the pocket in the body of the pheasant
6. Arrange a very thin slice of lard bacon on the place where your truffles entered, and put the skin back on top
7. Truss the legs as for a roasted hen; bind them well; fasten the skin of the pocket so the truffles do not come out while cooking
8. Put lard bacon in a casserole, your pheasant on top; cover it well with bacon; mark a braising pan without lemon which you will put on top; cover it

title: Pheasant Thighs with Lentil Purée (Cuisses de Faisan à la parée de lentilles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant thighs
- Small lard
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Aromatic herbs
- Lard bacon
- Carrots
- Onions
- Bay leaves
- Cloves
- Veal
- Bouillon
- Lentil purée
- Glazed croutons

## Directions

1. After removing the fillets from a pheasant, employ the thighs; remove them from the loins; take as much skin as possible; debone them to the joint
2. Put inside a piece of pounded small lard about the size of half an egg, seasoned with salt, coarse pepper, a little pounded aromatic herbs
3. Gather the meat together with a needle and thread; sew them so your thighs form a round at the thick end
4. Put lard bacon in a casserole; place your thighs in it; put lard bacon on top; add two carrots, four onions cut in slices, two bay leaves, two cloves, some slices of veal, one pot spoon full of bouillon
5. Simmer your thighs for an hour and a half
6. At serving time, drain them; unbind them, and arrange them in crowns, one thigh, one glazed crouton approximately the size of the thigh; put your purée in the middle
7. If you wish to arrange them otherwise, mask them with your purée

title: Pheasant with Truffles (Faisan aux Truffes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- truffles
- lard (bacon)
- coarse pepper
- quatre épices (four spices)
- pheasant trimmings
- barding lard
- espagnole sauce
- game stock or cooking liquid
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil the truffles in lard for five minutes
2. Add finely chopped pheasant trimmings and let cool
3. Using a piping bag, fill the pheasant body with the mixture
4. Arrange a thin bard of lard over the opening where the truffles were inserted and cover with skin
5. Truss the legs as for a braised hen and tie securely
6. Secure the pocket skin so truffles do not escape during cooking
7. Place bards of lard in a casserole with the pheasant on top
8. Cover well with bards and place a braising pan (poêle) without lemon on top
9. Cover with a round of buttered paper and simmer for one hour
10. At service, drain the pheasant and untie carefully without tearing
11. Dress on a platter
12. Chop two truffles and pass through butter
13. Add three tablespoons of espagnole and three tablespoons of game stock or cooking liquid
14. Reduce sauce by half, degrease, and pour under the pheasant

title: Pickled Gherkins
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Small gherkins
- Long pepper
- Samphire
- Tarragon
- Cloves
- Small onions
- Vinegar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Brush small gherkins without scraping them
2. Place them in earthenware pots with long pepper, samphire, tarragon, a few cloves, and small onions
3. Have vinegar with salt added to it
4. Boil the vinegar and pour it into the pot with the gherkins and seasonings
5. The next day, boil it again, repeating up to four times total; then your gherkins will be green and very crisp
6. When cold, cover with parchment or paper

title: Pieds d'Agneau en cartouche (Lamb's Feet in Cartouche)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb feet
- blanc (white cooking liquid)
- fines herbes à papillotes (fine herbs in papillotes)
- lemon
- paper
- oil
- lard
- salt

## Directions

1. When your feet are cooked in your blanc, trim them
2. Arrange fine herbs in papillotes
3. Put your lamb feet in your hot fine herbs
4. Let them take two or three turns over the fire
5. Squeeze a whole lemon over them
6. Put them to cool on a platter
7. After that, cut long squares of paper large enough to well envelope your foot
8. Oil your paper
9. When your feet are cold, introduce some of your fine herbs into the empty space and around the foot
10. Wrap it in a very thin bard of lard
11. Roll it in your paper so it forms a cartouche
12. Be careful to close the ends so that being on the grill the seasoning does not escape
13. Half an hour before serving, put them on the grill over a gentle fire
14. The paper should have a nice color
15. Serve them dry or with a clear juice beneath

title: Pieds d'Agneau en marinade (Lamb's Feet in Marinade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb feet
- blanc (white cooking liquid)
- marinade
- salt
- poivre (pepper)
- vinaigre (vinegar)
- pâte à frire (frying batter)
- parsley

## Directions

1. When your feet are cooked in a blanc, put a marinade over them
2. If you do not have one, put a little fine salt, pepper, and half a glass of vinegar
3. A moment before serving, drain them
4. Put them in a frying batter
5. When your feet have taken on batter all over, put them in frying fat that is not too hot
6. When they have a nice color, remove them from your frying fat
7. Let them drain on white linen
8. Dress them on your platter and put a handful of fried parsley on top

title: Pieds d'Agneau farcis (Stuffed Lamb's Feet)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb feet
- farce de quenelle de volaille (poultry quenelle forcemeat)
- nutmeg
- fines herbes (fine herbs)
- blanc (white cooking liquid)
- sauce hollandaise verte (green hollandaise sauce)

## Directions

1. Debone your lamb feet as the preceding ones
2. Before blanching them, fill them with a forcemeat of poultry quenelle in which you put a little grated nutmeg and a little fine herbs
3. When your feet are well filled, sew the end with a needle and heavy thread so your forcemeat does not come out
4. Blanch them for five minutes in boiling water
5. Refresh them and singe them, removing the hair in the cleft of the foot
6. Make a short blanc, that is with little liquid
7. Pour it over your lamb feet which are in your saucepan and simmer them for two hours
8. At the moment of serving, drain them
9. Trim them and dress them on the platter
10. Mask them with a green hollandaise sauce

title: Pieds d'Agneau à la poulette (Lamb's Feet à la Poulette)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb feet
- blanc (white cooking liquid)
- velouté travaillé (worked velouté)
- parsley
- egg
- lemon juice
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)
- salt

## Directions

1. When your feet are scalded, debone them, that is remove the large bone up to the joint
2. Be careful not to cut or tear the skin
3. Put them to disgorge
4. Blanch them
5. Refresh them
6. Drain them
7. Dry them
8. Singe them
9. Remove the hair found in the cleft of the foot
10. Put them to cook in a blanc
11. Let them simmer for two hours
12. Feel them; if they are not cooked enough, then boil them for another half hour
13. Drain them
14. Trim them
15. Put them in a saucepan
16. Pour in four spoonfuls of worked velouté
17. Add some finely chopped parsley
18. Add to your sauce a liaison of one egg
19. When your sauce is bound, pour it over your lamb feet, stirring them well in your sauce
20. At the moment of serving, add lemon juice, a little coarse pepper, and dress your feet on your platter
21. Check that they are properly seasoned

title: Pieds d'Agneaux Gratinés (Gratinéed Lamb's Feet)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- dozen lamb feet
- eighteen or twenty small white onions
- bread crumbs
- grated cheese
- butter
- egg yolks
- veal stock

## Directions

1. Cook a dozen lamb feet and eighteen or twenty small white onions in a good braise
2. Make a small gratin with bread crumbs, a little grated cheese, a piece of butter, and three egg yolks, mix all together
3. Spread it on the bottom of the serving dish
4. Put this dish on hot ashes to cook and slightly brown the gratin
5. Arrange the lamb feet on top, interspersed with small onions
6. Let it simmer a little more over the fire
7. Skim off the fat and serve with a good veal stock sauce on top

title: Pieds de Cochon Farcis aux Truffes (Pig's Feet Stuffed with Truffles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pig's feet
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Carrots
- Onions
- Cloves
- Bouquet of parsley and chives
- Saltpeter
- Half bottle of white wine
- Bouillon
- Cooked chicken breasts
- Bread crumbs
- Veal udder (dried in bouillon)
- Egg yolks (three or four)
- Truffles
- Four spices
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Cream
- Pork or veal caul
- Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare your half pig's feet and cook them in the same seasoning as those prepared à la Sainte-Menehould.
2. Simmer them for eight hours.
3. Remove them from their cooking liquid when they are half cold.
4. Unwrap your feet and remove the bones.
5. Make a forcemeat with cooked chicken breasts; chop and pound bread crumbs; dry in bouillon over the fire the veal udder, using as much bread as chicken, as much udder as bread and chicken combined, all well pounded.
6. Mix in three or four egg yolks, chopped truffles, a little four spices, salt, coarse pepper, and a little cream.
7. When your forcemeat is finished, add truffles cut in slices.
8. Fill the interior of the foot in place of the bones with your forcemeat.
9. Cover this forcemeat with pork or veal caul.
10. Take care to preserve the shape of your feet.
11. Dip them in warm butter and coat them with breadcrumbs.
12. Fifteen minutes and a half before serving, place them on a grill over a gentle fire.
13. Pay attention that they are grilled on both sides.
14. Arrange them on the plate without sauce.
15. If you do not have chicken, use other meat such as veal, young rabbit, pheasant, etc.

title: Pieds de Cochon à la Sainte-Menehould (Pig's Feet Sainte-Menehould)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pig's feet
- Ribbon or wide thread
- Thyme
- Bay leaf
- Carrots
- Onions
- Cloves
- Bouquet of parsley and chives
- Saltpeter
- Half bottle of white wine
- Bouillon or water (two tablespoons per pot)
- Meat scraps
- Butter
- Coarse pepper
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Wrap your half pig's feet with wide ribbon or thread so that they do not come apart while cooking.
2. Place them in a casserole with thyme, bay leaf, carrots, onions, cloves, a bouquet of parsley and chives, a little saltpeter, half a bottle of white wine, and two tablespoons per pot of bouillon or water.
3. Add a few meat scraps if you have them.
4. Simmer for twenty-four hours without stopping.
5. Let them cool in their cooking liquid.
6. Carefully unwrap them and leave them until the next day.
7. When you want to prepare them, dip them in warm butter.
8. Season them with coarse pepper and roll them in breadcrumbs, taking as much as possible.
9. Place them on a grill over a gentle fire and serve them without sauce.

title: Pieds de Mouton au Gratin (Sheep's Feet au Gratin)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's feet
- white wine, one glass
- bouillon, three spoonfuls
- coulis, three spoonfuls
- bouquet of parsley
- chives
- garlic, half a clove
- cloves, two
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the feet in water
2. Put them in a saucepan with a glass of white wine, three spoonfuls of bouillon and as much coulis, a bouquet of parsley, chives, half a clove of garlic, two cloves, salt, and coarse pepper
3. Boil them on a small fire and reduce to a short sauce
4. Remove the bouquet
5. Serve them on a gratin, as that of sheep's tongues, previously on page 69

title: Pieds de Mouton au Gratin (Sheep's Feet au Gratin)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet
- Water
- White wine
- Bouillon
- Coulis
- Parsley
- Chives
- Garlic
- Cloves
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook them in water.
2. Place them in a saucepan with a glass of white wine, three spoonfuls of bouillon and as much coulis, a bouquet of parsley and chives, half a garlic clove, two cloves, salt, and coarse pepper.
3. Boil gently and reduce to a short sauce.
4. Remove the bouquet and serve on a gratin like that of sheep's tongues.

title: Pieds de Mouton aux Concombres en Fricassée de Poulets (Sheep's Feet with Cucumbers in Chicken Fricassée)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's feet
- cucumbers
- vinegar, one spoonful
- salt
- butter, one piece
- bouquet of parsley
- chives
- garlic, one clove
- bay leaf, half a leaf
- cloves, two
- flour, one pinch
- bouillon
- egg yolks
- cream

## Directions

1. Cut each foot into three pieces after cooking in water and thoroughly cleaning
2. Cut cucumbers into large pieces and marinate for one hour with one spoonful of vinegar and a little salt
3. Press them well in your hands to extract all the water
4. Put them in the saucepan with a piece of butter, a bouquet of parsley, chives, one clove of garlic, half a bay leaf, two cloves
5. Pass everything together on the fire
6. Add a pinch of flour, moistened with bouillon
7. Let boil on a small fire until the cucumbers are cooked and there is almost no sauce remaining
8. Add a liaison of egg yolks diluted with cream
9. Thicken the sauce on the fire without letting it boil, for fear it turns
10. Before serving, taste if there is enough salt and vinegar, add a little coarse pepper

title: Pieds de Mouton de Différentes Façons (Sheep's Feet in Various Ways)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet
- Water
- Butter
- Bouillon
- Bouquet of herbs
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Always cook sheep's feet in water for whatever ragout you wish to make.
2. When well cooked, remove the leg bone and leave the foot whole.
3. Peel off any remaining hair and place in a saucepan with butter, bouillon, a bouquet of all sorts of fine herbs, salt, and pepper.
4. Boil gently for half an hour.
5. When they have taken on enough flavor, remove to a clean cloth to dry them of their fat.
6. Arrange on the serving platter and cover with whatever sauce you judge appropriate, such as à la Hollande, à l'Espagnole, or others found in the sauce section.

title: Pieds de Mouton en Marinade (Sheep's Feet in Marinade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar
- Flour
- Oil or fat for frying
- Fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your sheep's feet as those prepared in white sauce; when they are cooked, drain them, trim them, and then place them in a marinade
2. If you do not have marinade, season them with a little salt and pepper; drench them with vinegar so that they are thoroughly covered
3. At the moment of serving, drain them and place them in a frying batter; place them in hot oil: pay attention that they have a nice color; drain them; arrange them on the plate; sprinkle fried parsley on top

title: Pieds de Mouton en Surtout (Sheep's Feet en Surtout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet
- Water
- Meat forcemeat
- Egg (beaten)
- Bread crumbs
- Clear piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the feet as above.
2. Take a flameproof serving platter and spread a layer of forcemeat in the bottom.
3. Arrange the feet on top of the sauce and cover them with the same forcemeat.
4. Smooth the top with a knife dipped in beaten egg and coat with bread crumbs.
5. Place the platter on a small fire, cover with a pie cover, and place coals on top until golden brown.
6. Drain off the fat and serve with a clear piquant sauce underneath.

title: Pieds de Mouton Farcis (Stuffed Sheep's Feet)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet
- Water
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Laurel leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Garlic
- Cooked meat
- Beef fat
- Bread crumbs
- Milk
- Parsley
- Chives
- Egg yolks
- Egg (beaten)

## Directions

1. Cook the feet in water with salt, pepper, laurel leaf, thyme, basil, and garlic for half an hour.
2. Remove and debone as much as possible.
3. Prepare the stuffing by chopping a small piece of cooked meat and mixing with beef fat, bread crumbs softened with milk, seasoned with salt, pepper, and chopped parsley and chives.
4. Bind with three egg yolks and stuff the feet.
5. To fry: dip in beaten egg, coat with bread crumbs, and fry until golden.
6. To grill: dip in hot butter, coat with bread crumbs, and either grill or bake with a pie cover and coals on top.
7. Drain off any fat and serve with clean platter edges. Optionally serve with a clear sauce.

title: Pieds de Mouton à l'Anglaise (Sheep's Feet English Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet
- Water
- Bouillon
- Verjuice
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onions
- Garlic
- Carrot
- Butter
- Bread crumbs
- Piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Cook a dozen sheep's feet in water.
2. Place them in a saucepan with bouillon, a spoonful of verjuice, salt, pepper, some onion slices, a garlic clove, and a carrot cut into zest.
3. Boil for half an hour to develop flavor.
4. When well cooked, drain and remove the bones.
5. Replace each bone with a piece of bread crumb of the same length and thickness.
6. Fry by passing them on the fire with butter until they achieve a beautiful golden color.
7. Place a piece in each foot to imitate the removed bone.
8. Arrange on the serving platter and cover with piquant sauce.

title: Pieds de Mouton à la Ravigote (Sheep's Feet with Herb Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet
- Water
- Butter
- Bouquet garni
- Bouillon
- Coulis
- Salt
- Pepper
- Chervil
- Burnet
- Purslane
- Staghorn
- Balm
- Tarragon
- Chives

## Directions

1. After cooking them in water, remove the large bones.
2. Place them in a saucepan with butter, a bouquet garni, bouillon, coulis, salt, and pepper.
3. Boil until the sauce is almost reduced.
4. Prepare the ravigote by blanching chervil, burnet, purslane, staghorn, a little balm, a little tarragon, and chives for about fifteen minutes at most.
5. Remove from water, press, and chop very fine.
6. Serve the herbs in the ragout, ensuring the sauce is neither too clear nor too thick, and season to good taste.

title: Pieds de Mouton à la Sainte-Menehould (Sheep's Feet Saint-Menehould Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet
- Water
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Garlic
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bread crumbs
- Piquant sauce

## Directions

1. After cooking them in water, remove the large bones and leave them whole.
2. Place them in a saucepan with a good piece of butter, chopped parsley, chives, a hint of garlic, salt, and pepper.
3. Cook until almost all sauce is reduced, stirring near the end to prevent sticking.
4. When cooled, dip them in the remaining sauce and coat with bread crumbs.
5. Grill them and serve dry with a piquant clear sauce.

title: Pieds de Mouton à la Sauce Robert (Sheep's Feet with Robert Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- butter, one piece
- sheep's feet
- bouillon
- coulis, a little
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- vinegar, a thread of

## Directions

1. Cut the onion into threads
2. Put it in a saucepan with a piece of butter
3. Cook it halfway
4. Put the sheep's feet cut into three and thoroughly cleaned, moistened with bouillon and a little coulis seasoned with salt and pepper
5. When your ragout is cooked, add mustard, a thread of vinegar, and serve at a short sauce

title: Pieds de Veau à la Camarguot (Calf's Feet Camargue Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Four calf's feet
- verjuice
- butter mixed with flour
- salt
- pepper
- shallot
- bouillon
- anchovy
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cook four calf's feet in water, then when cooked and well drained, place them in a casserole
2. Add two spoonfuls of verjuice, a piece of butter mixed with a pinch of flour, salt, coarse pepper, chopped shallot, and a glass of bouillon
3. Let them simmer for half an hour over low heat
4. Before serving, add a chopped anchovy which you mix well into the sauce and a pinch of blanched parsley, finely chopped
5. If the sauce lacks acidity, add a bit more verjuice and serve with a short sauce

title: Pieds de Veau à la Sainte-Menehould (Calf's Feet Sainte-Menehould Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Four calf's feet
- bouillon
- lard
- eau-de-vie
- parsley
- chives
- garlic cloves
- cloves
- bay leaves
- thyme
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- coriander
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Split four well-blanched calf's feet down the middle with a cleaver and tie them
2. Place them in a pot with rich bouillon, a spoonful of lard, a glass of eau-de-vie, a bouquet of parsley and chives, two cloves of garlic, three cloves, two bay leaves, thyme, basil, salt, pepper, and a pinch of coriander
3. Cook over low heat until done and only a small amount of sauce remains
4. Let them cool slightly, then remove and coat with bread crumbs
