5. Lightly sprinkle the top with bread crumbs using the same fat, grill until a nice color, and serve as an hors-d'œuvre or side dish

title: Pigeons au Basilic (Pigeons with Basil)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- small pigeons
- basilic
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- parsley

## Directions

1. After gutting and trussing the legs inside, cook the pigeons in a braise as you would beef tongue, page 29, using a little more basil.
2. When cooked, remove them from the braise to cool.
3. Dip them in two beaten eggs as for an omelet, coat them as you go with bread crumbs, fry them, and serve garnished with fried parsley.

title: Pigeons au Court Bouillon (Pigeons in Court Bouillon)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four large pigeons
- Coarse lard for larding
- A bouquet of parsley and chives
- One clove of garlic
- Two shallots
- Two cloves
- One bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- One parsnip
- One carrot
- Two onions
- Butter the size of half an egg
- Salt
- Pepper
- One glass of white wine
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Singe and empty the pigeons
2. Truss the legs into the body
3. Lard them with coarse lard and put them in a pot just to their size
4. Add a bouquet of parsley, chives, one clove of garlic, two shallots, two cloves, one bay leaf, thyme, basil, one parsnip, one carrot, two onions, and butter the size of half an egg
5. Season with salt and pepper
6. Moisten with one glass of white wine and an equal amount of bouillon
7. Cook over low heat
8. When the pigeons yield to finger pressure, pass the sauce through a sieve and reduce it
9. If it is too thin, add half a teaspoon of verjuice or a splash of vinegar
10. Serve the sauce over the pigeons

title: Pigeons au Soleil (Pigeons in the Sun)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- small young pigeons
- white wine
- one glass
- parsley
- chives
- one clove of garlic
- two cloves
- salt
- coarse pepper
- a small piece of butter
- flour
- two handfuls
- fine salt
- a little oil
- white wine for thinning the batter
- fried parsley

## Directions

1. Have small young pigeons well scalded and emptied; they must retain their wings, head, and feet
2. Pass a skewer through the thighs of each to prevent them from spreading too much
3. Blanch them briefly in boiling water
4. After thoroughly cleaning them, place them in a saucepan with one glass of white wine, a bouquet of parsley, chives, one clove of garlic, two cloves, salt, coarse pepper, and a small piece of butter
5. When cooked, drain them and let cool, then dip them in a batter and fry until golden
6. Serve hot with fried parsley around them
7. For the batter: put two handfuls of flour and fine salt in a saucepan with a little oil, and gradually add white wine to thin the batter until it is neither too thin nor too thick, meaning it should run from the spoon when poured

title: Pigeons aux Asperges en Petits Pois (Pigeons with Asparagus cut as Small Peas)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Small asparagus
- Pigeons

## Directions

1. Cut small asparagus into small peas, taking only the tender part and stopping cutting as soon as the knife meets resistance
2. When you have the equivalent of one and a half litrons, put them in fresh water and wash them several times without fear they will break
3. Blanch them for half a quarter hour in boiling water
4. Remove them to fresh water and drain them
5. Prepare them in the same manner as pigeons with small peas, with the difference that in the bouquet you will add a little savory and two cloves

title: Pigeons aux Fines Herbes (Pigeons with Fine Herbs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- four somewhat large pigeons
- egg yolks
- butter
- flour
- salt
- coarse pepper
- mushrooms
- shallots
- parsley
- chives
- garlic
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- white wine
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Blanch the pigeons briefly in boiling water, pluck them, and split them halfway down the back to flatten them a little.
2. Place them in a casserole with the chopped livers, a piece of butter mixed with a good pinch of flour, salt, coarse pepper, mushrooms, shallots, parsley, chives, half a garlic clove, all chopped very fine, half a bay leaf, thyme, and basil chopped like powder.
3. Let simmer for half an hour over a low fire.
4. Then add half a glass of white wine and an equal amount of bouillon and finish cooking over a low fire.

title: Pigeons aux Petites Racines (Pigeons with Small Root Vegetables)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons
- Braises or bacon
- Lard for barding
- Veal slices
- Carrots
- Onions
- Bay leaves
- Clove
- Parsley and chives
- Thyme
- Bouillon
- Glazed lettuce
- Small root vegetables

## Directions

1. Empty your pigeons and singe them.
2. Truss and bard them.
3. Place braises in a casserole with your pigeons.
4. Cover with bards and arrange some slices of veal around them with two carrots, three onions, two bay leaves, one clove, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and a little thyme.
5. Add one spoonful of bouillon.
6. Let simmer for an hour.
7. At the moment of service, drain, unbard, and arrange on your platter.
8. Surround with a border of glazed lettuce and place small root vegetables in the middle.

title: Pigeons aux Pois (Pigeons with Peas)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four pigeons
- A good piece of butter
- One litron of small peas
- A bouquet of parsley and chives
- A pinch of flour
- One glass of water
- Salt
- Two eggs
- Cream

## Directions

1. Scald and blanch the pigeons
2. If they are large, cut them in two after tucking the legs inside
3. Put them in a casserole with a good piece of butter, one litron of small peas, and a bouquet of parsley and chives
4. Pass them over the fire and add a pinch of flour
5. Moisten with one glass of water
6. Cook over low heat
7. When cooked and there is no more sauce, add a little fine salt and a liaison of two eggs with cream
8. Bind on the fire without boiling
9. Serve with a light sauce

title: Pigeons aux Tortues (Pigeons with Turtles)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- turtles
- white wine
- bouillon
- bouquet garni
- pigeons

## Directions

1. Cut the head and feet from turtles and cook them with white wine, bouillon, and a bouquet garni
2. Remove them carefully from their shells, being careful to remove the bitter parts
3. Put the turtles in a finished pigeon ragoût of good flavor

title: Pigeons Cauchoix aux Pois (Cauchois Pigeons with Peas)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- four large Cauchois pigeons
- one litron of fine peas
- good butter
- a bouquet
- flour
- bouillon
- jus
- coulis
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Scald and clean the pigeons and cut them in four
2. Place them in a casserole with one litron of fine peas, one litron for four pigeons
3. Add a piece of good butter and a bouquet
4. Pass them over the fire, sprinkle with a pinch of flour, and moisten with bouillon and a little jus
5. When they are cooked, add a spoonful of coulis, a little sugar the size of a hazelnut, and fine salt
6. If you have neither jus nor coulis, add a liaison of three egg yolks thinned with a little bouillon

title: Pigeons en Beignets (Pigeons as Fritters)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover pigeons
- flour
- white wine
- oil
- salt
- seasoning
- fried parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Use leftover pigeons from the table and cut them in half
2. Have them take flavor in a seasoning
3. Let them cool
4. Dip them in a batter made with flour, white wine, a spoonful of oil and salt
5. Fry them
6. Serve garnished with fried parsley

title: Pigeons en Compote (Stewed Pigeons)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pigeons
- Butter
- Flour
- Bacon or salt pork
- Bouillon or water
- Salt
- Parsley and chives
- Bay leaf
- Coarse pepper
- Mushrooms
- Small onions

## Directions

1. Empty, singe, and truss the legs inside your three pigeons.
2. Truss them to maintain a more pleasing shape.
3. Throw a piece of butter in a casserole and warm it.
4. Add one spoonful of flour and make a roux.
5. Cut slices of bacon and brown them in the roux.
6. Place your pigeons in the casserole and sear them well.
7. Pour one and a half spoonfuls of bouillon or water over the pigeons.
8. Stir well until they boil.
9. If using water, add salt, a bouquet of parsley and chives, a bay leaf, coarse pepper, and mushrooms.
10. Skim the ragout.
11. When the pigeons are three quarters cooked, add small onions, well peeled and all of the same size, first passed in butter until golden.
12. Drain them and place in the ragout, which you will degrease.
13. Before serving, arrange the pigeons on your platter with the garnish on top.

title: Pigeons en Crépine (Pigeons in Crepinette)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- five small pigeons
- bouillon
- half glass of white wine
- parsley
- chives
- half clove of garlic
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- two cloves
- salt
- pepper
- veal round
- beef fat
- dried breadcrumbs
- milk or cream
- mushrooms
- three egg yolks
- egg whites
- caul fat
- egg
- coulis

## Directions

1. Have five small pigeons scalded and blanched briefly in boiling water
2. After tucking their feet into the body, cook them for half an hour with a little bouillon, half a glass of white wine, a bouquet of parsley, chives, half a clove of garlic, thyme, bay leaf, basil, two cloves, salt, and pepper
3. Let them cool
4. Make a forcemeat with veal round, beef fat, breadcrumbs dried with milk or cream, parsley, chives, and chopped mushrooms, salt, and pepper
5. Bind with three egg yolks and beaten egg whites
6. Wrap each pigeon with forcemeat and a piece of caul fat
7. Hold the caul in place with beaten egg, bread them, and cook them under a pastry lid cover for half an hour over low heat so the forcemeat has time to cook
8. Wipe them of their fat and serve with the sauce from the pigeon cooking that you degrease, pass through a tamis, add a little coulis to bind it

title: Pigeons en Fricandeau (Pigeons Fricandeau-Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- thin lard

## Directions

1. Lard the entire top of your pigeons with thin lard
2. Cook and glaze them exactly like veal fricandeau à la bourgeoise

title: Pigeons en Fricassée de Poulets (Pigeon Fricassee)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- large pigeons
- medium pigeons

## Directions

1. Cut large pigeons into four pieces, and medium ones in half
2. Cook them in the same manner as chicken fricassees

title: Pigeons en Hâtelet (Pigeons on Skewers)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeon ragoût
- butter
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Use a pigeon ragoût that has been removed from the table
2. Put a piece of butter in the ragoût and heat it, and for better results add two or three egg yolks
3. Then skewer everything on small hâtelets
4. Bind the sauce afterward, bread them, and grill until golden
5. Serve without sauce

title: Pigeons en Matelote (Pigeons in Red Wine Stew)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized pigeons
- butter
- small white onions
- bacon
- bouquet garni
- flour
- bouillon
- white wine
- egg yolks
- verjuice

## Directions

1. Blanch the pigeons with legs trussed inside and pass them in a casserole with a little butter and a dozen small white onions that you have cooked for half a quarter-hour in water to peel them.
2. Add a quarter pound of well-larded bacon cut into slices and a bouquet garni.
3. Add a pinch of flour and moisten with half bouillon and half white wine.
4. When the pigeons are cooked and reduced to little sauce, add a liaison of three egg yolks with verjuice or vinegar when serving.

title: Pigeons en papillotes (Pigeons in Paper)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- salt
- coarse pepper
- aromatic spices
- butter
- one quarter pound of minced fat pork
- four tablespoons of oil
- three tablespoons of finely chopped mushrooms
- one tablespoon of shallots
- parsley
- salt and spices
- paper
- oil

## Directions

1. After emptying and singeing your pigeons, cut off their legs and tuck the thighs inside
2. Cut them down the back like a chicken à la tartare
3. Flatten them
4. Season with salt, coarse pepper, and a little ground aromatic spices
5. Put a good piece of butter in a casserole with one quarter pound of minced fat pork and four tablespoons of oil
6. Place the casserole on the fire with your pigeons inside
7. Sauté them for a good quarter hour
8. Place them on a plate
9. In your butter, pass three tablespoons of finely chopped mushrooms, one tablespoon of shallots, an equal amount of parsley, all finely chopped, a little salt and spices
10. When they have returned in the butter, place them on your pigeons
11. Let them cool
12. Prepare squares of paper which you oil
13. Wrap your pigeons as instructed in papillotes articles
14. Half an hour before serving, place your pigeons on the grill at a gentle fire
15. At the moment of service, arrange them on the plate with clear broth underneath

title: Pigeons en Ragoût d'Écrevisses (Pigeons with Crayfish Stew)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three or four medium pigeons
- good bouillon
- white wine
- bouquet of parsley and chives
- garlic clove
- cloves
- salt
- pepper
- mushrooms
- good butter
- crayfish
- flour
- cream
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Blanch the pigeons after gutting them, split them slightly on the back to open the breast a bit, and cook them with good bouillon, a glass of white wine, a bouquet of parsley and chives, a garlic clove, two cloves, salt, and pepper.
2. When cooked, put mushrooms (the size of half an egg), good butter, and a dozen peeled crayfish in a casserole.
3. Pass them over the fire and add a pinch of flour, moisten with the cooking liquid from the pigeons that you pass through a sieve.
4. Let the stew boil for half an hour until little sauce remains.
5. Add a liaison of three egg yolks with cream, a little nutmeg, and a small pinch of very finely chopped parsley; thicken without boiling over a moderate fire while stirring constantly.
6. Drain the pigeons to dress them in the serving dish and put the crayfish stew on top.

title: Pigeons en Surprise (Pigeons in Surprise)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Five small pigeons
- Five fine head lettuces
- Pigeon livers
- Parsley
- Chives
- Five or six tarragon leaves
- A little chervil
- Two shallots
- A piece of butter or grated lard
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Two egg yolks
- Bouillon, slightly fatty
- A bouquet of parsley
- Chives
- Two cloves
- Two onions
- One carrot
- One parsnip
- Veal coulis
- Butter mixed with flour
- Two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Scald the pigeons, empty them, and truss the legs into the body; set the livers aside
2. Blanch the pigeons briefly in boiling water
3. In the same water, blanch five fine head lettuces and boil them for a good quarter hour
4. Remove the lettuces to fresh water and press them firmly so no water remains
5. Split them in two without detaching the leaves
6. Make a farce with the pigeon livers, parsley, chives, five or six tarragon leaves, a little chervil, two shallots, all chopped very finely and mixed with a piece of butter or grated lard, salt, coarse pepper, bound with two egg yolks
7. Place the farce on top of each lettuce half
8. Wrap each pigeon with a lettuce so they are not visible
9. Tie them with string and put them to cook in slightly fatty bouillon with a bouquet of parsley, chives, two cloves, two onions, one carrot, one parsnip, salt, and pepper
10. Cook for one hour over low heat
11. When cooked, drain the pigeons and untie them
12. Wipe them of their fat with a cloth
13. Serve a good veal coulis over them; if you have no coulis, use slightly less salt in the cooking, pass the sauce through a sieve and degrease it
14. Reduce the sauce to the consistency of a sauce and add a knob of butter mixed with flour and two egg yolks
15. Finish on the fire without boiling
16. Serve the pigeons wrapped in lettuce

title: Pigeons en Surtout (Pigeons Wrapped)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- ragoût
- meat forcemeat
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- clear coulis

## Directions

1. Make a ragoût as for pigeons à la bourgeoise, reduced to a short sauce
2. Let it cool
3. Take the serving dish that should go to the fire and put a good meat forcemeat in the bottom
4. Arrange the pigeon ragoût on top
5. Cover it with the same forcemeat as below so the ragoût is not visible
6. Smooth it with a knife dipped in egg, bread it with breadcrumbs
7. Cook under a pastry lid cover until golden brown
8. Drain off the fat
9. Serve with a good sauce of clear coulis on top

title: Pigeons Gautier à l'aurore (Gautier Pigeons Aurora)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- six pigeons
- butter
- salt
- coarse pepper
- grated nutmeg
- juice of one lemon
- bay leaf
- flour
- consommé
- mushrooms
- one onion
- clove
- two eggs
- breadcrumbs
- beaten eggs
- parsley

## Directions

1. Lightly singe six spoon-style pigeons
2. Put a piece of butter in a casserole with salt, coarse pepper, a little grated nutmeg, the juice of one lemon, a bay leaf
3. Put your pigeons in it and place over the fire
4. When they are well browned, take half a tablespoon of flour which you mix with your butter
5. Add a full tablespoon of consommé and let them boil on high heat
6. Add mushrooms and an onion studded with a clove
7. After a quarter hour remove your pigeons from the sauce
8. If the sauce is not sufficiently reduced, leave it on the fire until only about four tablespoons remain
9. Add a liaison of two eggs
10. Remove the bay leaf and onion
11. Pour your sauce over your pigeons and let them cool
12. Chop the mushrooms and mix them with the sauce
13. Use this to stuff your small pigeons
14. Coat them well with sauce and put them in breadcrumbs, then in beaten seasoned eggs
15. Coat a second time
16. At the moment of serving, fry them
17. When they have a nice color, arrange them on your plate with fried parsley in the middle
18. Once your pigeons are cooked and cold, you can cut them in half, stuff them and bread them the same way, then fry them

title: Pigeons Gautier à la financière (Gautier Pigeons Financière)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- six pigeons
- cock crests and kidneys
- foie gras
- truffles

## Directions

1. Take six pigeons which you prepare and cook like the preceding ones
2. At the moment of serving, drain them and arrange around the edge of the plate
3. Put in the middle of your pigeons a ragout of cock crests and kidneys, foie gras and truffles (see Mixed Ragout)

title: Pigeons à la Bourgeoise (Pigeons Cooked in a Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- bouillon
- bouquet garni of all sorts of mushrooms
- artichoke hearts, cut in quarters and cooked halfway
- salt
- pepper
- coulis or liaison of three egg yolks diluted with bouillon and chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Blanch the pigeons briefly and remove them to fresh water, pluck them, and place them in a casserole with bouillon, a bouquet garni of all sorts of mushrooms, two artichoke hearts cut in quarters and cooked halfway, salt, and pepper.
2. When cooked, add a little coulis and serve with short sauce.
3. If you have no coulis, add a liaison of three egg yolks diluted with bouillon and a little chopped parsley.

title: Pigeons à la Bourgeoise (Pigeons in Home-style Manner)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- bouillon
- a bouquet garni
- mushrooms of all sorts
- artichoke hearts
- salt

## Directions

1. Scald, clean, and truss the pigeons by tucking the feet inside
2. Blanch them for a moment and remove them to fresh water, then clean them
3. Place them in a casserole with bouillon, a bouquet garni with all sorts of mushrooms, two artichoke hearts cut in four and cooked halfway through, and salt

title: Pigeons à la Broche (Spit-Roasted Pigeons)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons
- Vine leaves
- Lard for barding

## Directions

1. Empty and singe your pigeons fairly well.
2. Pluck and truss them.
3. Place a vine leaf under the bard if you have one.
4. Half an hour is sufficient for cooking.

title: Pigeons à la Casserole (Casserole of Pigeons)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Aromatics
- Flour
- Lemon juice or vinegar
- Bouillon or water

## Directions

1. Prepare your pigeons as previously described.
2. Put a good piece of butter in your casserole and warm it.
3. Season with salt, coarse pepper, and a little crushed aromatics.
4. Place them breast-side down in the casserole over fairly brisk heat.
5. Turn them after ten minutes.
6. Half an hour is sufficient for cooking.
7. Arrange on your platter.
8. Remove three quarters of the butter remaining in the casserole.
9. Sprinkle in a pinch of flour and mix with your cooking liquid, the juice of a lemon or two spoonfuls of vinegar, and half a glass of bouillon or water.
10. Add salt as needed.
11. Bring your sauce to boil twice and coat your pigeons with it.
12. Check that it is well seasoned.

title: Pigeons à la Crapaudine, Sauce au Verjus (Spatchcocked Pigeons with Verjuice Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- good pigeons
- fine oil
- salt
- coarse pepper
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- bread crumbs
- onion
- verjuice
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Truss the legs inside; if large, cut them in half, otherwise only split them down the back and flatten them without breaking the bones too much.
2. Marinate them with fine oil, salt, coarse pepper, parsley, chives, and chopped mushrooms; let them absorb the seasoning as much as possible and coat with bread crumbs.
3. Place them on the grill and baste with the rest of their marinade; grill them over a low fire until a beautiful golden color.
4. For the sauce: put a chopped onion in a mortar with verjuice, pound everything together well, and extract as much juice as you can; mix with bouillon, salt, and coarse pepper, heat, and serve under the pigeons.
5. These same pigeons can be served without verjuice using another clear sauce that is slightly piquant; you can use butter, lard, or good pot fat instead of oil.

title: Pigeons à la cuillère (Pigeons with Spoon Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- six pigeons
- butter
- juice of one lemon
- salt
- coarse pepper
- bacon rashers
- poêle (braising liquid)
- crayfish
- green hollandaise sauce

## Directions

1. Lightly singe six Gautier-style pigeons
2. When they are plucked and trimmed, put a good piece of butter in a casserole with the juice of one lemon, a little salt and coarse pepper
3. Stiffen your pigeons in this seasoning
4. Put them in a casserole between bacon rashers and the butter in which you seared them
5. Add poêle to cook them (see Poêle)
6. A good quarter hour before serving, put them on the fire
7. At the moment of service, drain them and arrange on your plate
8. Place a crayfish between each pigeon
9. Pour green hollandaise sauce underneath

title: Pigeons à la Dauphine (Pigeons Dauphiné Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- scalded pigeons
- lard strips
- bouillon
- lemon slice
- bouquet garni
- glazed calf sweetbreads

## Directions

1. Cook the scalded pigeons between strips of lard with a little bouillon and a slice of lemon and a bouquet garni
2. Serve them with glazed calf sweetbreads prepared like fricandeau

title: Pigeons à la Marianne (Pigeons in the Marianne Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pigeons
- Two spoonfuls of lard
- One glass of bouillon
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Two bay leaves

## Directions

1. Prepare three pigeons as for the previous recipes
2. Flatten them slightly with the meat cleaver
3. Put them in a casserole with two spoonfuls of lard, one glass of bouillon, salt, coarse pepper, and two bay leaves
4. Cook them over hot cinders so they boil very gently
5. When they yield to finger pressure, dress them in the platter you will serve

title: Pigeons à la Marianne (Pigeons Mariana)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three pigeons
- two spoonfuls of butter
- one glass of bouillon
- salt
- coarse pepper
- two bay leaves
- one anchovy
- three shallots
- a pinch of capers
- nutmeg, the size of a walnut
- butter mixed with a good pinch of flour

## Directions

1. Prepare three pigeons as previously described, flatten them slightly with the cleaver, and place them in a saucepan with two spoonfuls of butter, one glass of bouillon, salt, coarse pepper, and two bay leaves
2. Cook them over hot coals so they boil very gently
3. When they yield to pressure from your finger, arrange them on the serving dish after draining and wiping away their fat
4. Remove the bay leaves from the sauce and degrease it
5. Add the chopped anchovy, three shallots, and a pinch of capers, all chopped, nutmeg the size of a walnut, and butter mixed with a good pinch of flour
6. Thicken over the fire and serve over the pigeons

title: Pigeons à la Poêle (Pan-Cooked Pigeons)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- small pigeons
- good butter
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- a hint of garlic
- salt
- coarse pepper
- sliced veal
- half glass of white wine
- lard
- white paper
- coulis

## Directions

1. Pluck and empty small pigeons, leaving them their feet
2. Lightly brown them on the fire
3. Pass them into a saucepan with a little good butter, parsley, chives, mushrooms, a hint of garlic, all chopped, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Then put them with all their seasoning in another saucepan with sliced veal that you have blanched briefly in boiling water
5. Add half a glass of white wine, cover with lard and a sheet of white paper
6. Put a lid on the saucepan and cook over low heat so they only simmer
7. Then degrease the cooking liquid, add a little coulis to bind it
8. Serve over the pigeons

title: Pigeons à la Saint-Laurent (Pigeons Saint-Laurent)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Four pigeons
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Breadcrumbs
- Clear broth or water
- Shallots
- Vinegar
- Breadcrumb coating

## Directions

1. Take four pigeons and singe them lightly.
2. Truss the legs inside the body.
3. Split them down the back from neck to tail.
4. Empty and beat them on the breast so the pigeon is flat.
5. Season with coarse pepper and salt.
6. Heat a piece of butter in a saucepan and dip them in it, then roll in breadcrumbs.
7. When well breaded, half an hour before serving, place them breast-side down on the grill over gentle heat.
8. Turn them at the appropriate time.
9. Arrange on the platter.
10. Place clear broth underneath or a shallot sauce made with: half a glass of bouillon or water, salt, fine pepper, two tablespoons of finely chopped shallots, three spoonfuls of good vinegar, and one spoonful of breadcrumb coating.
11. Bring the sauce to boil two or three times and pour under your pigeons.
12. Check that it is well seasoned.

title: Pigeons à la Sainte-Menehould (Pigeons in the Saint-Menehould Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three large pigeons
- Butter the size of an egg mixed with two pinches of flour
- Parsley in branches
- Whole chives
- Two onions in slices
- Zest of carrots and parsnips
- One whole clove of garlic
- Three cloves
- Salt
- Pepper
- One bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Three portions of milk
- Breadcrumbs for breading
- Fat from the sainte-Menèhoult
- Sauce rémoulade

## Directions

1. Empty the pigeons, keeping the livers
2. Truss the legs into the body
3. Singe and pluck them
4. Put in a casserole butter the size of an egg mixed with two pinches of flour, parsley in branches, whole chives, two onions in slices, zest of carrots and parsnips, one whole clove of garlic, three cloves, salt, pepper, one bay leaf, thyme, and basil
5. Moisten with three portions of milk
6. Bring to a boil and then put in the pigeons to cook over very low heat for one hour
7. When cooked, remove them to drain
8. Remove the fat from the sainte-Menèhoult and put it on a plate
9. Dip the pigeons in it and bread them as you go
10. Grill to a beautiful color, basting them with the remaining fat in which you dipped them
11. Serve dry with sauce rémoulade in the sauce boat

title: Pièce d'Aloyau (Sirloin Piece)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- whole sirloin piece from the thick end of the tenderloin to the first rib
- string
- skewers
- piquant sauce with many cornichons

## Directions

1. Take a whole sirloin piece, that is to say from the thick end of the tenderloin to the first rib.
2. Have it removed as squarely as possible, without removing the fat that is on the tenderloin.
3. Take care to remove the surface and leave three fingers thick of fat so that your tenderloin is well covered.
4. Spit your piece so that it does not turn; for this you must pass the spit along the bones attached to the filet.
5. Secure your meat with small skewers and make sure there is not more meat on one side than the other.
6. If your piece were not spitted straight, you would put a large skewer on the hard filet and attach it at each end with string, tightening it as much as possible; it is the tenderloin that is in view.
7. This sirloin is served to follow large pieces.
8. Serve also a piquant sauce with many cornichons underneath.
9. This piece is excellent stewed in a braising pan, cooked without liquid, with fire above and below.

title: Pièce d'Aloyau (Sirloin Roast)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Meat
- Skewers

## Directions

1. Put skewers to hold your meat from the flank near the bones of the filet
2. Pass your spit from the thick to the thin part of the filet
3. In case it turns, put a strong skewer on top of the sirloin to hold it
4. Attach both ends firmly to the spit
5. Serve with a piquant sauce underneath as a relief course after the soup

title: Plies Grillées Sauce aux Câpres (Grilled Flounder with Caper Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Flounder
- Salt
- Pepper
- Oil
- Butter
- Capers
- Anchovy butter or Spanish sauce (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the flounder
2. Score them
3. Season with salt, pepper, and oil
4. Half an hour before serving, place them on the grill over moderately hot fire
5. When cooked, arrange them on the platter and mask them with butter sauce; sprinkle capers over them
6. You may also use Spanish sauce with the amount of anchovy butter the size of half an egg, or use Italian sauce

title: Plies à l'Italienne (Flounder Italian Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Flounder
- Italian sauce
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. Clean and gut the flounder
2. Cook them in good water or court-bouillon
3. Arrange them on your platter and put Italian sauce on top; that is, add a liaison of an egg yolk

title: Poires de Martin sec (Martin Sec Pears Compote)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fifteen martin sec pears
- One half-pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Hollow out the head of each pear slightly
2. Shorten the stem by detaching it a little from the pear
3. Clarify one half-pound of sugar and extend the syrup with water
4. Place the pears in the syrup
5. Simmer them for a good hour
6. When they are almost cooked, let them reach a full boil
7. Remove them from the heat and arrange on a serving dish
8. When the syrup is sufficiently reduced, pour it over the pears

title: Poires Séchées à la Façon de Reims (Dried Pears in the Reims Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Rousselet or Doyenné pears
- Sugar
- Water

## Directions

1. Peel the pears from top to bottom, gather the stem slightly and cut off the small end.
2. Throw them into fresh water as you peel them, then bring them to boil until they yield under the fingers.
3. Remove them with a skimmer as they are done and throw them into fresh water.
4. Once drained, for every fifty pears, put one pound of sugar with a pint of water.
5. When the sugar is melted, put the pears in to let them steep for two hours.
6. Remove them and dress them on wicker baskets, round end up, to pass the night in an oven of gentle heat.
7. The next day, re-dip the pears in the sugar and return them in the same way to the oven, continuing this for four days, and the last time do not remove them until they are completely dry.
8. Keep them in a dry place as long as desired.

title: Poitrine au Coulis de Lentilles ou Coulis de Pois (Veal Breast with Lentil or Pea Purée)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- bouillon
- bacon
- herbs
- garlic
- salt
- lentils or dried peas
- spinach

## Directions

1. Cut a veal breast into pieces the length of a finger
2. Blanch and cook with good bouillon, a half-pound of bacon cut into slices, a bouquet of fine herbs, a clove of garlic, little salt
3. While this cooks, also cook a half-litron of lentils or dried peas with water or bouillon
4. When well cooked, pass them through a sieve as a purée
5. If making pea purée, before passing cook a handful of spinach in water, press and pound it, add it to the peas so the purée is green, then pass it, moistening with the cooking liquid from the tendons to give body to the purée
6. Place the tendons and bacon in the purée
7. Reduce on the fire if the purée is too thin
8. Serve in a terrine

title: Poitrine d'Agneau à la Sainte-Menehould (Lamb Breast à la Sainte-Menehould)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- two lamb breasts
- barbs
- a few slices of veal
- two calves' heads
- two onions
- two cloves
- two bay leaves
- thyme
- bouillon
- fine salt
- coarse pepper
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Place the lamb breasts with barbs, veal slices, calves' heads, onions, cloves, and bay leaves
2. Add thyme and a spoonful of bouillon
3. Simmer for two hours
4. Remove, untie, and drain them
5. Sprinkle with fine salt and coarse pepper
6. Place the breasts between two lids to cool
7. Remove the bones and trim them
8. Dip in warm butter, then in breadcrumbs, giving them a nice shape
9. Place on a tile or lid and brush with a feather dipped in butter
10. Drain the butter over the breasts and sprinkle breadcrumbs on top, blowing away excess
11. A quarter hour before serving, place them on the grill over gentle fire
12. Cover with a heated country oven to give color to the breasts
13. Dress on the platter with clear juice

title: Poitrine de Bœuf (Beef Breast)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- beef breast
- salt
- coarse pepper
- butter
- bread crumbs
- parsley

## Directions

1. Cut a beef breast of the desired size
2. Bone it almost entirely up to the tendon
3. Give your piece a rectangular shape
4. Tie it with string and make it well rounded
5. Cook it
6. For serving à la Sainte-Menehould: cook it somewhat less than if serving plain
7. Prepare it the day before so you can better trim and finish it
8. Season with fine salt and coarse pepper
9. Dip it in butter or with a brush
10. Butter it, making sure your butter is not too hot
11. Apply bread crumbs on top as much as possible with your brush dipped in butter and let it drip over your piece of beef
12. Sprinkle bread crumbs so that it forms a crust
13. Before serving, let your piece of beef take color over the fire and heat it sufficiently
14. Place a worked and clear Spanish sauce underneath your piece with just a little coarse pepper

title: Poitrine de Mouton aux Petites Racines (Lamb Breast with Root Vegetables)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb breast
- lard strips
- ham slices
- carrots
- onions
- bay leaves
- thyme
- bouillon
- buttered paper
- small root vegetables

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your breast as the one said en carbonnade
2. When your pieces are cooked

title: Poitrine de Mouton en Carbonnade (Lamb Breast Carbonnade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb breast
- lard strips
- ham slices
- carrots
- onions
- bay leaves
- thyme
- bouillon
- buttered paper
- chicory or spinach, sorrel, tomato sauce, or white onion purée

## Directions

1. Cut off the red bone that is attached to the tendons
2. Divide your breast into pieces forming the matelote crouton, that is to say a pointed oval
3. Put lard strips in a casserole
4. Add a few slices of ham and your carbonnade pieces on top
5. Cover them with lard
6. Add two carrots cut into slices, four onions also cut, two bay leaves, a little thyme
7. Pour in one full spoon of bouillon
8. Add a round of buttered paper
9. Simmer them with heat above and below for three hours
10. At serving time, drain them, glaze them, and put chicory or spinach, sorrel, tomato sauce, white onion purée, etc. on top

title: Poitrine de Mouton en Haricot (Lamb Breast Ragout)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb breast
- onions
- carrots
- bay leaves
- thyme
- bouillon
- water
- salt
- turnips
- butter
- flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut your breast into pieces of whatever shape you judge appropriate
2. Put round slices of onions in the bottom of your casserole
3. Arrange your pieces of breast on top
4. Add two carrots, two bay leaves, a little thyme
5. Add a good glass of bouillon
6. Bring your meat to a boil until your braising liquid is reduced to a light brown glaze
7. Add two full spoons of bouillon or water
8. Then add salt
9. Simmer your breast for two hours
10. When it is cooked, pass your braising liquid through a silk sieve
11. Remove your pieces of breast and take out the rib bones
12. Turn turnips into small sticks, put them in a casserole, and sauté them in butter until they turn blonde
13. Then add a spoonful of flour which you mix with your turnips
14. Pour the braising liquid in which your breast cooked into your turnips
15. Add a small piece of sugar
16. When your turnips are cooked, if the sauce is too thin, remove them and put them on your meat
17. Then reduce your sauce to the proper consistency
18. Take care to degrease it and pass it through cheesecloth over your breast and turnips
19. Then simmer your ragout for half an hour so it takes on flavor

title: Poitrine de Veau (Veal Breast)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast
- Skewers
- Lard (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the ends of the breast bones
2. Remove the red bones that hold the tendons
3. Put your breast on a strong skewer to avoid a large spit hole
4. Secure your breast on the spit by attaching both ends of your skewer to the spit
5. Put a layer of lard on your breast if you wish

title: Poitrine de Veau au Basilic (Veal Breast with Basil)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- bouillon
- parsley
- chives
- garlic
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- cloves
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- parsley for frying

## Directions

1. Cut the veal breast into pieces one inch wide
2. Blanch briefly in boiling water
3. Cook with bouillon, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a clove of garlic, thyme, bay leaf, basil, two cloves, salt, and pepper
4. When cooked, reduce the sauce until it adheres to the meat
5. Remove the meat to a plate to cool
6. Dip each piece in beaten egg like an omelet
7. Coat with breadcrumbs as you proceed
8. Fry to a nice color and serve garnished with fried parsley

title: Poitrine de Veau au Roux (Veal Breast with Roux)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal breast
- a small piece of butter
- one spoonful of flour
- one chopine of water or bouillon
- salt
- pepper
- one bouquet garni
- half a spoonful of vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the veal breast into pieces or leave it whole.
2. Make a roux with a small piece of butter and one spoonful of flour; when it is roux and of a good color, add one chopine of water or bouillon.
3. Then add the veal and cook it on low heat.
4. Season with salt, pepper, one bouquet garni, and half a spoonful of vinegar.
5. When the meat is cooked, degrease the sauce and serve at short sauce.

title: Poitrine de Veau aux Laitues (Veal Breast with Lettuces)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- needle for trussing
- lard bardes
- carrots, sliced
- onions
- two bay leaves
- parsley and chives bouquet
- veal breast trimmings
- lard for covering
- bouillon
- salt
- lettuces
- espagnole sauce or light roux

## Directions

1. Prepare your breast as the previous one
2. Truss it the same way
3. Put lard bardes in the bottom of your pan, sliced carrots, onions, two bay leaves, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and the trimmings from your breast
4. Cover it with lard
5. Moisten it with bouillon and a little salt
6. Cook it over a gentle fire for two and a half hours
7. At the moment of serving, drain it, untie it, glaze it, and place it on your plat
8. Also arrange a cordon of lettuces around it, which you will glaze
9. Have a prepared espagnole sauce which you will place under it
10. If you do not have sauce, make a light roux which you will moisten with the cooking liquid from your breast
11. Make the sauce loose so you can make it proper for serving

title: Poitrine de Veau aux Oignons Glacés (Veal Breast with Glazed Onions)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- lard
- onions
- two bay leaves
- thyme
- consommé
- coarse pepper
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Pare and truss your veal breast as for the glazed version
2. Place lard in the bottom of your casserole
3. Cut onions into slices and put them in the bottom of your casserole
4. Place your veal breast there
5. Cover it with lard
6. Add more sliced onions on top, two bay leaves, a little thyme, half a pot spoon of consommé, a pinch of coarse pepper
7. Cook your veal breast with fire underneath and on top for two and a half hours
8. When it is cooked, drain it and glaze it with the glaze from your onions
9. Arrange it on the platter with glazed onions around it
10. Pour two spoonfuls of prepared Spanish sauce into your onion glaze, with a spoonful of consommé
11. Detach your glaze with your sauce and place it under your veal breast
12. Ensure everything is hot when you serve

title: Poitrine de Veau Farcie (Stuffed Veal Breast)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast with rib bones
- three quarterons of veal round
- one pound of veal udder
- parsley
- shallots
- salt
- coarse pepper
- nutmeg
- three raw egg yolks
- lard
- bouillon
- Spanish sauce
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the end of the rib bones in your veal breast
2. Make an incision between the top meat and that of the ribs
3. Introduce the following stuffing: mince three quarterons of veal round
4. Add one pound of veal udder, minced also with your veal that is already minced
5. Mix everything together
6. Add finely minced parsley, finely minced shallots, salt, coarse pepper
7. Grate a little nutmeg on top
8. Add three raw egg yolks
9. Mince the mixture again to amalgamate everything together
10. Take this stuffing and place it inside your veal breast as much as it can hold
11. Sew with a larding needle and string the meats that contain your stuffing so that it does not escape while cooking
12. When your veal breast is trussed, place lard in your braising pan
13. Put your veal breast there, having taken care to tie it
14. Cover it with lard
15. Place a cooking utensil to cook it; if there is not enough liquid, add a little bouillon
16. When your veal breast has cooked for three hours, at the moment of serving, drain it
17. Remove the strings that contain it and glaze it
18. Place it on the platter
19. Use for sauce a reduced Spanish sauce in which you put a piece the size of a walnut of glaze, and the value of half an egg of fine butter that you will melt in your boiling sauce
20. When you put it in with the spoon, stir your sauce well and pour it underneath
21. If you do not have veal udder for your stuffing, use lard, fat, veal kidneys, or beef kidneys
22. If you do not have a cooking utensil, put carrots, onions, a bunch of parsley and chives, two bay leaves, thyme, three cloves
23. Cover your veal breast with lard
24. Moisten with bouillon; if you do not have any, put water and salt to replace the sauce
25. Make a light roux that you will sprinkle with the liquid in which your veal breast has cooked
26. Make your sauce long so that you can reduce it and so that it takes on flavor
27. Add a little coarse pepper

title: Poitrine de Veau glacée (Glazed Veal Breast)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- needle for trussing
- gelée or bouillon
- lard bardes
- buttered paper
- three or four carrots
- four onions
- two bay leaves
- two cloves
- bouillon or water
- salt
- parsley and chives bouquet
- grande espagnole sauce
- flour
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cut your breast as squarely as possible
2. Bone it to within two finger-widths of the tendons
3. Secure the meat with a trussing needle so that your breast is well rounded on top
4. Take care to cut the tender bones that extend beyond the tendons
5. Place your breast with the tendons facing down in your pan with a pot spoon of gelée
6. Cover your breast with lard bardes and buttered paper
7. Bring to a boil, then place over a gentle fire
8. Also place fire on the braising pan's lid
9. Two and a half hours of cooking are sufficient
10. If you do not have gelée to moisten your breast, add three or four carrots, four onions, two bay leaves, two cloves, a pot spoon of bouillon; if you have none, use water with salt, and a bouquet of parsley and chives
11. When your breast is three-quarters cooked, remove your vegetables and reduce your cooking liquid to a glaze
12. When your breast is cooked and the glaze is at the bottom of your pan, turn your breast so that the top side glazes
13. At the moment of serving, untie your breast and place it on your plat
14. Add two large spoonfuls of defatted grande espagnole sauce to your glaze with a spoonful of bouillon
15. Detach your glaze over the fire and place this sauce under your breast
16. If you do not have sauce, remove the fat from your glaze, add a little flour and a little bouillon, or water if you have none
17. Detach your glaze over the fire, stirring with a wooden spoon
18. Add a little coarse pepper and salt if your sauce is too mild
19. Pour this sauce under your breast

title: Poitrine de Veau Marinée Frite (Fried Marinated Veal Breast)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one veal breast
- a small piece of butter
- one spoonful of flour
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- parsley
- chives
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- three cloves
- onions
- roots
- water
- flour for frying

## Directions

1. Cut the veal breast into pieces the width of a finger.
2. In a casserole, make a roux with a small piece of butter and one spoonful of flour.
3. Add salt, pepper, vinegar, parsley, chives, thyme, bay leaf, basil, three cloves, onions, and roots, with water.
4. Heat the marinade on the fire, stirring constantly.
5. Place the meat in it to soak for two or three hours.
6. Remove the meat, dry it, and flour it.
7. Fry it; when cooked, serve garnished with parsley.

title: Poitrine de Veau à la Glace (Glazed Veal Breast)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- sliced carrots
- onions
- two bay leaves
- bunch of parsley and chives
- lard
- bouillon
- salt
- pepper
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare and truss your veal breast
2. Cover it with lard
3. Moisten it with bouillon and a little salt
4. Cook over low heat for two and a half hours
5. At the moment of serving, drain it, remove the trussing strings, glaze it, and arrange it on your platter
6. Place a border of glazed lettuces around it
7. Make a Spanish sauce that you will place underneath; if you do not have sauce, make a light roux that you will moisten with the cooking liquid in which your breast has cooked
8. Make the sauce long so that you can reduce it
9. Add a little coarse pepper

title: Poitrine de Veau à la Purée de Champignons (Veal Breast with Mushroom Puree)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- mushroom puree
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Prepare, truss, and cook your veal breast as for the glazed version
2. Place a mushroom puree underneath
3. At the moment of serving, mix an egg yolk into the puree, stirring it together with your puree

title: Poitrine Farcie (Stuffed Veal Breast)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- forcemeat

## Directions

1. The breast must be cut specially so that all the skin remains attached
2. Place your chosen forcemeat between the skin and the tendons
3. Sew the skin so the forcemeat does not escape
4. Cook it on the spit or braised
5. Serve with whatever sauce or vegetable ragout you wish, such as with the forcemeat, leeks, peas, gherkins, or roots

title: Poitrine à l'Allemande (Veal Breast German Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- bouillon
- white wine
- herbs
- pepper
- coulis
- capers
- anchovies
- poultry livers
- parsley
- shallot
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Blanch the breast
2. Cook it whole with a little bouillon, a half-glass of white wine, a bouquet of fine herbs, and pepper
3. When cooked, dress it on a platter and fold the skin to the sides to expose the tendons
4. Pour over a German sauce made with a little coulis, capers, anchovies, two cooked poultry livers, blanched parsley, and a shallot, all chopped very fine
5. Boil an instant and add a little coarse pepper
6. Alternatively, degrease and sieve the cooking liquid from the breast, add a walnut-sized piece of good butter kneaded with flour and a pinch of blanched chopped parsley, and thicken on the fire

title: Poitrine à la Sainte-Menehould (Lamb Breast Sainte-Menehould)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb breast
- salt
- pepper
- warm butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cook your whole breast in the same manner as your carbonnade, except you will not add ham
2. When the breast is cooked, remove the rib bones
3. Add a little salt and a little pepper on top
4. Dip it in warm butter
5. Bread it as well as possible
6. Then put it on the grill over a gentle fire
7. With a brush, drain butter over the entire breast
8. Sprinkle breadcrumbs on it
9. Have a well-heated camp oven to give your breast color
10. When it is well blonde, put it on the platter with a clear jus underneath

title: Pommes au Riz (Apples with Rice)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Several apples
- Sugar syrup
- Lemon juice
- Apricot marmalade
- Rice
- Cream
- Powdered sugar
- Lemon peel
- Basil, citron peel, candied cherries, verjuice (for decoration)

## Directions

1. Remove the peel and core from several apples with an apple corer
2. Make a thin syrup in which you press lemon juice
3. Put the apples in it
4. When they have boiled, test them with a fork: if it enters easily, remove them and let them drain on a silk sieve
5. With some other apples, make a little marmalade or use apricot marmalade
6. Arrange some in the bottom of the plate
7. Put the apples on top
8. Pour apricot marmalade inside
9. Mask them with rice prepared as for rice cake, made creamy with cream, sugar, and a little minced lemon peel
10. Mask the apples giving them the shape you wish
11. Decorate them with basil, citron peel, candied cherries, verjuice, etc.

title: Pommes de terre sautées au beurre (Potatoes Sautéed in Butter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from raw potatoes and turn them to equal size
2. Cut them into round slices the width of a small coin, one and a half lines thick
3. Put a good piece of butter in a casserole and place on a hot fire
4. Add the potatoes and sauté constantly until they are golden
5. Drain them in a strainer
6. Sprinkle with fine salt and arrange on the platter with no other seasoning

title: Pommes de terre à l'anglaise (Potatoes English Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- salt
- butter
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes well and cook them in salted water
2. Peel them when cooked
3. Warm a good piece of butter in a casserole
4. Cut the potatoes into slices and place them in the butter
5. Add salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
6. Sauté the potato slices in the butter, being careful not to let it turn to oil
7. Serve on a platter

title: Pommes de terre à la crème (Potatoes with Cream)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- parsley
- chives
- cream
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Put a good piece of butter in a casserole with one tablespoon of flour, salt, coarse pepper, a little grated nutmeg, parsley, and well-chopped chives
2. Mix everything together
3. Add one glass of cream
4. Place the sauce on the fire and stir until it boils
5. Cut the potatoes into slices and put them in the sauce
6. Serve hot

title: Pommes de terre à la lyonnaise (Potatoes Lyonnaise)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- onions
- butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- bouillon or water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes in water and cut them into slices
2. Place them in a casserole
3. Make a clear onion purée and pour it over the potatoes; keep warm without boiling
4. Alternatively, put a good piece of butter in a casserole and cut eight onions into slices
5. Place on the fire and when well browned, add one teaspoon of flour, mixing well with the onions
6. Add salt, coarse pepper, one small pot spoon of bouillon or water, and a dash of vinegar
7. Simmer the onions for a quarter hour
8. Place them on the potatoes and keep warm

title: Pommes de terre à la maître-d'hôtel (Potatoes with Parsley and Chives)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- water
- salt
- butter
- parsley
- chives
- pepper
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes in salted water
2. Cut them into slices
3. Place them in a casserole with a good piece of butter, chopped parsley, chopped chives, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Place on the fire and sauté with the butter and herbs
5. If the butter turns to oil, pour in a spoonful of water
6. At the moment of serving, add lemon juice

title: Pommes de terre à la provençale (Potatoes Provençal Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- oil
- lemon zest
- parsley
- chives
- nutmeg
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- potatoes
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Put a good piece of butter in a casserole and cut it into several pieces
2. Pour three tablespoons of oil over it with the zest of half a lemon, parsley, well-chopped chives, a little grated nutmeg, a small pinch of flour, salt, and coarse pepper
3. Peel potatoes fresh from boiling water and cut them into quarters, or sixths if too large
4. Stir them on the fire in the seasoning without boiling
5. At the moment of serving, add lemon juice

title: Pork Chops (Côtelettes de Porc Frais en Ragoût)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh pork loin
- Bouillon
- Bouquet garni
- Salt
- Pepper
- Veal sweetbread (ris de veau)
- Mushrooms
- Poultry (sometimes)
- Butter
- Flour
- White wine
- Parsley
- Chives
- Garlic
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Cut a fresh pork loin into chops
2. Cook with a little bouillon, bouquet garni, little salt and pepper
3. Blanch a veal sweetbread, cut it into large dice, and place in a saucepan with mushrooms, sometimes poultry, and a little butter
4. Heat on the fire and sprinkle with a good pinch of flour
5. Moisten with half bouillon and a glass of white wine, adding enough juice to color the ragout
6. Season with salt, coarse pepper, bouquet of parsley, chives, half a clove of garlic, and two cloves
7. Let cook and reduce to a short sauce
8. Serve over the chops

title: Pork Head (Tête de Cochon)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork head
- Lard
- Salt
- Mixed spices
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- Garlic
- Bouillon
- Red wine
- Bouquet garni
- Onions
- Roots
- Pepper
- White cloth

## Directions

1. Sear the head over a clear fire on a well-heated stove
2. Scrub it vigorously with a brick, then with a knife
3. Once clean, debone it halfway without removing the skin
4. Lard it on the inside with thick lard
5. Season with salt, mixed spices, parsley, chives, mushrooms, and garlic, all chopped
6. Wrap in white cloth, tie it, and cook in a good braising liquid made with bouillon, red wine, a large bouquet garni, onions, roots, salt and pepper
7. Once cooked, let it cool in the braising liquid
8. Drain it on a napkin to remove excess liquid
9. Serve cold as a main course

title: Potage au Lait (Milk Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of milk
- salt, two or three grains
- sugar (optional)
- sliced bread
- five egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil one pint of milk with two or three grains of salt and sugar if desired
2. Slice bread and arrange it in the serving dish
3. Pour some of the milk over the bread to soak it
4. Keep it warm on hot ashes without allowing it to boil
5. Cover the dish
6. When ready to serve, mix five egg yolks with the remaining milk
7. Place on the fire, stirring constantly
8. When the milk thickens, remove promptly from the heat as this indicates the eggs are cooked; if you delay, the eggs will curdle

title: Potage au Lait (Milk Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk
- Sugar or salt

## Directions

1. Bring your milk to a boil
2. After it has boiled, season your milk with sugar or salt, as you judge best
3. At the moment of serving, pour your milk over your bread

title: Potage au Lait Distingué (Distinguished Milk Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three chopines of milk
- small strip of green lemon peel
- pinch of coriander
- small piece of cinnamon
- salt, two or three grains
- about three ounces of sugar

## Directions

1. Boil three chopines of milk with a small strip of green lemon peel, a pinch of coriander, a small piece of cinnamon, two or three grains of salt, and about three ounces of sugar
2. Allow it to boil and reduce to half
3. Pass it through a sieve
4. Finish as for the previous milk soup

title: Potage au Lait Lié (Bound Milk Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk
- Sugar
- Salt
- Four eggs (for one pint of milk)
- Bread

## Directions

1. When your milk has boiled, add sugar and a small pinch of salt
2. At the moment of serving, add a binding of four eggs to your hot milk
3. Put it on the fire and stir well with a wooden spoon
4. When you see your milk thicken and stick to your spoon, remove it from the fire (take care that it does not boil, as it would curdle)
5. Pour it over your bread
6. Choose well-sliced crust from the top of the loaf; soft bread is best for this soup

title: Potage au Pain et à la Purée Maigre (Soup with Bread and Lean Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Lean purée
- Lean bouillon
- Bread

## Directions

1. Put the purée in lean bouillon
2. Boil them together, but not too long, for fear your soup will become acrid
3. Skim your soup and pour it over your bread a quarter hour before serving

title: Potage au Riz (Rice Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of rice (more or less according to the size of your soup, one quarter pound for four bowls)
- good broth
- veal juice

## Directions

1. Wash the rice in lukewarm water three or four times, rubbing it with your hands.
2. Cook it on low heat for three hours with good broth and veal juice.
3. When cooked, degrease it.
4. Taste to check if it has good salt.
5. Serve neither too thick nor too liquid.

title: Potage au Riz (Rice Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound of well-cleaned rice
- Warm water
- Bouillon at a rolling boil
- Two spoonfuls of veal blonde sauce, or one spoonful of jus
- Salt

## Directions

1. Take half a pound of well-cleaned rice
2. Wash it four or five times in warm water while rubbing well
3. Then wash in cold water
4. Moisten it with bouillon at a rolling boil so the rice does not turn to mush
5. Boil for two hours on low heat
6. Ensure your bouillon is not too salty due to reduction so it acquires a beautiful color
7. Add two spoonfuls of veal blonde sauce or one spoonful of jus
8. Carolina rice is the best for cooking

title: Potage au Riz aux Oignons (Rice and Onion Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- a good piece of butter
- water
- salt
- fine pepper
- a quarter pound of rice

## Directions

1. Cut onions into dice, without putting in either the head or tail
2. Pass your onion in a good piece of butter
3. Let it fry until it is more than blond
4. Then moisten it with water, as much as needed for your soup
5. Season it with salt, fine pepper
6. Put a quarter pound of rice (or more according to the size of your soup) in your broth where your onion is, and let it boil for an hour and a half
7. If one wanted to avoid finding the onion, you let it cook; after half an hour you pass your broth through a sieve, and you put your rice in it to finish cooking

title: Potage au Riz et Coulis de Lentilles en Maigre (Rice Soup with Lentil Coulis, Meatless)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- all sorts of roots
- cabbage
- turnips
- onions
- celery
- leek
- half-liter of peas
- half-liter of lentils à la reine
- one quarter pound of rice
- a piece of butter

## Directions

1. Make first a good meatless broth with all sorts of roots, cabbage, turnips, onions, celery, leek, in proportion to the strength, and a half-liter of peas.
2. Put separately in a small pot a half-liter of lentils à la reine, which you cook with this broth.
3. When cooked, pass them as a purée.
4. Take one quarter pound of rice after washing it well.
5. Cook it in a small pot with a piece of butter and your clarified meatless broth.
6. When it is cooked and seasoned as it should be, add the lentil coulis to it.
7. Take care that your soup is not too thick.

title: Potage au Riz Faubonne à l'eau (Faubonne Rice Soup with Water)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 carrots
- 4 turnips
- 6 leeks
- 6 onions
- a little parsley root
- a good piece of butter
- water
- a quarter pound of rice
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cut these roots into small dice
2. Pass everything with a good piece of butter
3. When your vegetables are well passed, moisten them with water, as much as needed for your soup
4. Put a quarter pound of rice, or more if your soup is large
5. Make your soup boil for an hour and a half
6. When your rice and vegetables are cooked, add salt and coarse pepper for seasoning
7. Be careful that your soup does not have too much butter and never any scum

title: Potage au Riz à la Purée (Rice Soup with Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Bouillon
- Purée of choice
- Salt

## Directions

1. Use less liquid in your rice so that purée can be added
2. When your rice is well cooked, pour your purée at the moment of serving
3. Be careful to degrease your soup well
4. Season with good salt

title: Potage au Vermicelle aux Oignons (Vermicelli and Onion Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- a good piece of butter
- water
- salt
- fine pepper
- vermicelli

## Directions

1. Cut your onion into very fine strips
2. Make your soup as you would the rice soup, except that you must let your vermicelli boil only for half an hour

title: Potage aux Choux (Cabbage Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cabbage
- small piece of bacon cut into slices with the rind attached
- bouillon
- bread crusts

## Directions

1. Blanch the cabbage with a piece of small bacon cut into slices attached to the rind, tie each separately with string
2. Cook them separately in a small pot with the bouillon described above
3. When the cabbage and bacon are cooked, simmer the soup with the same bouillon and bread crusts
4. Serve the cabbage around the soup with the bacon, or in the simple bourgeoise manner on top
5. Take care to salt the bouillon very little because of the bacon
6. Root vegetables and turnips are made the same way; celery must be blanched longer

title: Potage aux Choux (Soup with Cabbage)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- two cabbages
- veal slices
- lard
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Blanch two cabbages cut in four pieces, more or less depending on their size, for half an hour in boiling water
2. Refresh them, then drain them
3. Tie them with string
4. Place slices of veal at the bottom of your pot, cover with lard
5. Place your cabbages on top, cover with lard
6. Place two carrots, two onions, and two cloves on top
7. When your cabbages are cooked, dip your bread soup as that with bread, that is with good bouillon
8. Drain your cabbages and place them on your soup
9. Pass the cooking liquid from your cabbages, remove the fat, and pour it into your soup
10. Alternatively, after blanching the cabbages, pressing and tying them, place them in your pot with carrots and onions, moisten with the fat of bouillon, and braise for two hours or more according to the quality of the cabbage, until it is cooked

title: Potage aux Choux maigres (Lean Cabbage Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cabbage
- a good piece of butter
- water
- salt
- coarse pepper
- bread

## Directions

1. Slice half a cabbage; avoid putting in the large white cores
2. Pass it with a good piece of butter according to the quantity of cabbage
3. When it is well passed and beginning to turn blond, moisten your cabbage with water
4. Add salt, coarse pepper, and let your soup boil for three quarters of an hour or an hour, until your cabbage is cooked
5. At the moment of serving, pour your soup over your bread

title: Potage aux Concombres (Cucumber Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- lard strips
- carrots
- onions
- coarse pepper
- two cloves
- broth
- bread

## Directions

1. Cut cucumbers into small ovals
2. Blanch them for ten minutes
3. Let them cool and drain
4. Put strips of lard in the bottom of the saucepan
5. Add your cucumbers and cover them with lard
6. Add carrots, onions, coarse pepper, and two cloves
7. Cook for three quarters of an hour
8. Prepare your broth as for bread soup
9. Place cucumbers on top
10. Pass the broth through a silk sieve, degrease it, and pour over your soup
11. Alternatively, after blanching your cucumbers, cook them in broth for three quarters of an hour and pour them over the bread of your soup

title: Potage aux Croûtons à la Purée (Soup with Croutons and Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Soft bread crumb
- A quarter pound of butter
- Purée (of turnips, carrots, lentils, or peas)
- A small piece of sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut slices of bread crumb three or four lines thick
2. Form them into squares, ovals, or rounds slightly larger than a small coin
3. Make twenty or thirty croutons depending on the size of your soup, ensuring they all have the same thickness and size
4. Put a quarter pound of butter in your pot with your bread pieces and set it on a high heat
5. Take care to always toss your croutons until they are well browned
6. Remove them from the pot and drain them on white linen
7. Put them in your soup tureen
8. Five minutes before serving, pour a clear, boiling purée (of turnips, carrots, lentils, or peas, whichever you prefer) over your croutons
9. Take care to put a small piece of sugar in your vegetable purée to destroy its acridness
10. This soup can be made lean or with meat

title: Potage aux Herbes (Herb Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- all sorts of cleaned and well-washed herbs
- parsnip
- carrot
- sorrel
- lettuce
- chervil
- purslane
- a little celery
- good broth
- a little veal juice

## Directions

1. Put all sorts of cleaned and well-washed herbs in a small pot with a parsnip and a carrot cut into small strips.
2. The herbs are sorrel, lettuce, chervil, purslane, and a little celery cut into strips.
3. Cook everything with good broth and a little veal juice.
4. When cooked and of good salt, simmer the soup.
5. Serve your herbs in the soup naturally without making a garnish.

title: Potage aux Herbes maigres (Lean Herb Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful of sorrel
- 2 lettuces
- a strong pinch of chervil
- a piece of butter
- water
- salt
- coarse pepper
- 3 eggs for the liaison

## Directions

1. Take a handful of sorrel that you will cut into strips, 2 lettuces cut the same way, a strong pinch of chervil also cut
2. Pass everything with a piece of butter
3. When everything is well melted, moisten it with water, salt, coarse pepper
4. Make everything boil for half an hour
5. Add a liaison of 3 eggs at the moment of serving

title: Potage aux Laitues Entières (Soup with Whole Lettuce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- veal slices
- lard
- onions
- carrots
- bouillon
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel the lettuce carefully, ensuring the leaves hold well with the heart
2. Wash them thoroughly
3. Blanch them by throwing into boiling water with a handful of salt according to the quantity of water
4. When they have boiled for half an hour, refresh them in a bucket of fresh water
5. Press them well
6. Tie them with string so they do not separate
7. Place slices of veal at the bottom of your pot, then lard strips
8. Place your lettuces on top, cover with lard
9. Cut slices of onions and carrots and garnish your lettuces
10. Moisten with bouillon
11. Cook over low heat for an hour until well cooked
12. Alternatively, after blanching, place them in your pot and cook with bouillon and fat from the pot
13. When cooked, dip your bread with good bouillon, then drain your lettuces and arrange them on your soup

title: Potage aux Laitues Émincées (Soup with Sliced Lettuce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce
- butter
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Peel your lettuces down to the yellow part
2. Wash them
3. Cut them very thin, forming thin strips
4. Pass your lettuce through butter until well melted
5. Moisten with your bouillon
6. Cook for one hour
7. Dip your bread soup as customary

title: Potage aux Menues Herbes (Soup with Fine Herbs)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- two lettuces
- handful of sorrel
- chervil
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Peel and wash everything
2. Crush your lettuce leaves, rejecting the cotton parts
3. Also crush the sorrel and chervil
4. Take bouillon from the back of the pot and pass through a sieve
5. Make the herbs melt
6. Moisten them with bouillon so they can cook
7. When cooked, dip your bread soup with good bouillon and arrange your herbs on your soup

title: Potage aux Poireaux (Leek Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- leeks
- a piece of butter
- water
- a little cinnamon
- salt
- coarse pepper
- bread

## Directions

1. Cut your leeks to one inch long, then cut them into strips
2. Pass them with a piece of butter
3. When your leeks are fried so that they are somewhat blond, moisten your soup with water
4. Add a little cinnamon, salt, coarse pepper, and let everything boil for half an hour
5. At the moment of serving, pour your broth over your bread

title: Potage de Croûtes (Bread Crust Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- bread crusts
- bouillon with fat not removed
- lentil coulis

## Directions

1. Take a silver platter with bread crusts
2. Moisten them with bouillon that has not been defatted
3. Simmer your crusts until a small gratin forms at the bottom of the platter
4. Drain off the remaining fat from the platter
5. Serve the lentil coulis on top

title: Potage de Lait aux Oignons (Milk Soup with Onions)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- butter
- milk
- salt
- bread

## Directions

1. Use fewer onions than in the preceding recipe
2. Cook them gently over a low fire with butter until they are cooked without being colored
3. Bring milk to a boil and add it with the onion seasoned with a little salt
4. Put sliced bread thinly in the serving dish with a part of your broth
5. Cover it and place it on a little hot ashes
6. When your bread is well soaked, add the rest of the broth
7. Serve

title: Potage de Riz Maigre (Lean Rice Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Parsnip
- Carrot
- Onions
- Parsley root
- Cabbage
- Celery
- Turnips
- Pea water
- Butter
- Onion juice
- Salt

## Directions

1. Wash the rice three or four times in warm water, rubbing it well in your hands
2. Cook it in a good lean bouillon made with parsnip, carrot, onions, parsley root, cabbage, celery, and turnips in moderate quantities, with no one vegetable dominating the others, especially restraining the celery and parsley root
3. Add a piece of butter and onion juice to the bouillon
4. Cook over low heat for three hours
5. Season to taste
6. Serve neither too clear nor too thick

title: Potage du Hameau de Chantilly (Chantilly Hamlet Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of beef brisket
- One whole veal knuckle
- Two partridges
- Two domestic pigeons
- Good bouillon or consommé
- Carrots
- Turnips
- Onions
- Leeks
- Two stalks of celery
- Two cloves
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Put the beef brisket, veal knuckle, partridges, and pigeons in a medium pot, ensuring the meats are well tied to remain whole
2. Fill your pot with good bouillon or consommé
3. Skim your pot
4. Garnish with carrots, turnips, onions, leeks, celery, and cloves
5. When your meats are well cooked, at the moment of serving, arrange them on a large shallow platter
6. Place around your meats carrots, turnips, onions, leeks by compartment, so the vegetables are not mixed together
7. Turn forty or fifty carrots into rounds two inches long, somewhat thick, all of the same length and thickness; prepare the same amount of turnips, onions, and medium leeks of the same thickness and well cleaned
8. Cook them in a separate bouillon, adding a small piece of sugar to each cooking to remove bitterness
9. When your vegetables are cooked, arrange them around your meats
10. Serve on the side a pot of bouillon from your pot, passed through a fine cloth or silk sieve so your bouillon is very clear
11. Season with good salt; do not serve the beef with this soup

title: Potage Faubonne (Vegetable Soup Faubonne)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- turnips
- leeks
- onions
- celery
- lettuce
- sorrel
- butter
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Cut the vegetables into dice
2. Crush the sorrel and lettuce
3. Pass the roots through butter the same way
4. Then add the leeks and herbs, all together
5. Moisten with enough bouillon for your soup
6. Always dip as a bread soup

title: Potage Printanier en Maigre (Spring Soup, Meatless)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of fresh peas
- chervil
- purslane
- lettuce
- sorrel
- three or four onions
- a pinch of parsley
- a piece of butter
- six egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put in a pot one liter of fresh peas, chervil, purslane, lettuce, sorrel, three or four onions, a pinch of parsley, and a piece of butter.
2. Boil everything together.
3. Pass it afterward as a light purée.
4. Simmer the soup with three quarters of this broth.
5. With the remaining broth, mix six egg yolks that you bind over the fire.
6. Put them in your soup when you are ready to serve, after tasting to check if it has good salt.

title: Potage Vermicelle Faubonne (Faubonne Vermicelli Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- turnips
- leeks
- onions
- parsley root
- a good piece of butter
- water
- vermicelli
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the roots into small strips instead of dice
2. Cook your vegetables before putting in your vermicelli
3. Then, your vegetables cooked, put your vermicelli in your broth, which you take care to skim from time to time so that your vermicelli does not clump together
4. Salt as needed and coarse pepper, that is its seasoning

title: Potage à l'Artois (Artois Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- croutons
- butter
- pea purée
- salt

## Directions

1. Pass croutons in butter, that is to say fry them until they are blonde
2. They are nice if they are round or oval shaped, or large dice
3. They must be made with bread crumb
4. You can give them any shape you wish
5. Make a green purée (see Purée de Pois Nouveaux)
6. Bring it to the consistency that is clear enough for your soup
7. Melt a piece of good butter at the moment of serving
8. Pour it over your croutons at the same time
9. Make sure your purée is properly salted

title: Potage à l'Impératrice (Empress Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four chickens
- Two large spoonfuls of rice cooked for a quarter hour in boiling water
- Good consommé

## Directions

1. Roast three or four chickens on a spit
2. When cooked, let them cool
3. Remove the meat and pound it with rice that has cooked for a quarter hour in boiling water
4. When the chicken breast and rice are well pounded together, thin your purée with good consommé and pass through a sieve
5. When your purée is passed, moisten it with consommé so it is neither too thin nor too thick
6. Prepare crusts as for bread soup and moisten with boiling consommé a quarter hour before serving
7. In your consommé put your poultry scraps and let them simmer on a low fire for two hours
8. Pass your bouillon through a fine cloth or silk sieve
9. Ensure your bouillon is not too amber-colored, that is, not too dark in color
10. Pour your purée at the moment of serving; ensure your soup is very hot and well salted

title: Potage à l'Oignon Gratinée (Gratinéed Onion Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Onions
- Half a pound of butter or more according to quantity of onions
- Bread cut in very thin slices
- Coarse pepper
- Bouillon
- Salt

## Directions

1. Fry the onions in butter enough so they are well golden
2. Take bread cut in very thin slices
3. Make a layer of bread and a layer of onions
4. Put a little coarse pepper on each layer until the dish is full
5. Moisten with bouillon and simmer so the crust forms without letting it burn
6. This would give it a harsh taste
7. Your soup must be almost dry
8. Put bouillon in a vessel and serve it so people can add to their plate
9. Be careful with salt due to reduction

title: Potage à la Chantilly (Chantilly Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- croutons
- lentil purée
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Have croutons (as in the preceding article) square
2. Pour a lentil purée at the top (see Purée de Potage)
3. Clarify it and keep it clear enough
4. Add a piece of butter the size of an egg
5. Take care to melt it without putting it on the fire
6. Do not neglect good salt
7. Pour your purée only at the moment of serving

title: Potage à la Chicorée à l'eau (Chicory Soup with Water)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 curly chicories
- a piece of butter
- water
- salt
- coarse pepper
- a little nutmeg
- 3 eggs for the liaison
- bread

## Directions

1. Slice 4 or 5 curly chicories; be careful to put in as little of the white cores as possible
2. Pass your sliced chicory in a piece of butter
3. When your chicory is well passed, do not let it brown, moisten your chicory with water
4. When your chicory has boiled for three quarters of an hour, at the moment of serving your soup (to which you will have added salt, coarse pepper and a little nutmeg), bind it with a liaison of 3 eggs, and pour it over your bread at the moment of serving

title: Potage à la Citrouille (Pumpkin Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter of a medium pumpkin or squash
- water
- butter, the size of an egg
- salt
- one pint of milk
- sugar to taste
- sliced bread

## Directions

1. Remove the skin and seeds from the pumpkin quarter
2. Cut the pumpkin into small pieces and place in a pot with water
3. Cook until reduced to a marmalade and no water remains
4. Add a piece of butter the size of an egg and a little salt
5. Allow it to boil a few more times
6. Boil one pint of milk and add sugar to taste
7. Pour the milk into the pumpkin
8. Arrange sliced bread in the serving dish
9. Moisten the bread with some of the pumpkin broth
10. Cover the dish and place it on some hot ashes for a quarter hour to allow the bread to soak, taking care that it does not boil
11. When serving, add the remaining pumpkin broth, well heated

title: Potage à la Condé (Condé Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of red beans
- broth
- two carrots
- two onions
- pot fat
- two cloves
- crusts passed in butter

## Directions

1. Cook the beans with broth
