2. Add two carrots, two onions, a little pot fat, and two cloves
3. When cooked, pass through a sieve
4. Make a clear purée
5. Pour over crusts that have been passed in butter, that is to say fried

title: Potage à la Crécy (Soup Crécy)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- six large carrots
- four large turnips
- six large onions
- three celery stalks
- four leeks
- butter
- sugar
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Slice six large carrots, four large turnips, six large onions, three celery stalks, four leeks (more if your soup is strong)
2. Put a piece of butter and a piece of sugar about the size of half an egg in your pot with your vegetables
3. Pass them white, that is do not let them take color by softening them on the fire
4. When you see they want to take color, moisten them with bouillon and braise for two hours on the fire
5. Pass them through cheesecloth
6. Then clarify your purée
7. Try to ensure it is not too thick
8. Afterward dip your bread with good bouillon

title: Potage à la Distillère (Distillery-Style Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread
- Butter
- Milk

## Directions

1. Cut your bread as for the Monaco soup
2. Pass your bread pieces through butter as explained in the crouton soup
3. Put the number of croutons needed so that your soup is not too thick
4. At the moment of serving, pour your bound milk over your croutons
5. Take care that your milk is neither too much nor too little sweetened; add five or six grains of salt

title: Potage à la Grimod de la Reynière (Grimod de la Reynière Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One well-trussed capon
- Two pigeons
- A piece of beef brisket weighing three pounds
- Good bouillon
- Carrots
- Turnips
- Onions
- Celery
- Leeks
- Two cloves
- Lettuce

## Directions

1. Put a well-trussed capon, two pigeons, and a three-pound piece of beef brisket, all well tied for good appearance, in a medium pot
2. Fill this pot with good bouillon
3. Skim it
4. Garnish with carrots, turnips, onions, celery, leeks, and two cloves
5. At the moment of serving, put your capon and two pigeons in a shallow platter with whole lettuces around the platter, small onions, carrots cut into large dice, and turnips the same way
6. Arrange these three kinds of vegetables in large quantity, cooked as for the Hameau soup
7. When your vegetables are cooked, arrange them around your capon so they form a mound
8. Pass the bouillon from your pot through a fine cloth or silk sieve
9. Serve beside your platter a pot full of hot bouillon, well seasoned with salt

title: Potage à la Monaco (Monaco-Style Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Soft bread crumb
- Fine sugar
- Milk

## Directions

1. Cut bread crumb into small rectangular squares, three lines thick, two and a half inches long, and one and a half inches wide
2. Dust your bread pieces with very fine sugar and toast them over a gentle fire so they do not take too much color
3. Put enough of them in your soup tureen so that your soup is not too thick
4. At the moment of serving, pour your bound milk over your bread
5. This soup can also be made without being bound

title: Potage à la Purée de Gibier (Game Purée Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three pounds of beef brisket
- Four old partridges
- Two pounds of veal knuckle
- One pheasant
- Carrots
- Onions
- Four stalks of celery
- Three cloves
- A small bouquet of fennel
- Three young partridges for roasting
- Breadcrumbs, three times the size of an egg
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Put the beef brisket, old partridges, veal knuckle, pheasant, carrots, onions, celery, cloves, and fennel in a medium-sized pot
2. Take the bouillon from this for your soup
3. Roast three young partridges on a spit
4. Pound them cold in a mortar with breadcrumbs that have been soaked in bouillon
5. Moisten your pounded partridges with bouillon
6. When well pounded, pass them through a sieve
7. When your purée is passed, add bouillon so it is neither too thick nor too thin
8. Place on a gentle fire; it must not boil
9. Soak croutons with bouillon as for bread soup

title: Potage à la Purée de Haricots Blancs (White Bean Purée Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- white beans
- broth
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare your soup the same way as the lentil purée
2. Keep it clear and do not boil too much
3. At the moment of pouring over your crusts, add a piece of good butter which you will melt by stirring your purée as much as necessary
4. Before serving your soup, check if it is seasoned enough

title: Potage à la Purée de Lentilles (Lentil Purée Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one liter of lentils
- broth
- two carrots
- two onions
- two cloves
- crusts

## Directions

1. Cook the lentils with broth, two carrots, two onions, and two cloves
2. When the lentils are cooked, pass them through a sieve
3. Make a clear purée
4. Boil it to degrease it
5. Pour over your crusts a moment before serving so the bread has time to soak

title: Potage à la Purée de Navets (Soup with Turnip Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- butter
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Make it the same way as carrot purée except that the purée should have a blonde color
2. Do not reduce too much when clarifying it, because this makes the purée acrid

title: Potage à la Purée de Pois Nouveaux (New Pea Purée Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- two and a half liters of peas
- quarter pound of butter
- small handful of parsley
- a few chives
- broth
- salt
- vert if needed
- crusts

## Directions

1. Put peas in cold water
2. Add a quarter pound of butter and mix with the peas by handling them
3. Drain them in a colander
4. Put them in a saucepan and add a small handful of parsley and some chive stems
5. Put on moderate fire and stir from time to time
6. After half an hour on the fire, put them in a mortar
7. When well crushed, pass through a sieve
8. Moisten with cold broth so the purée passes easily
9. Make it clear as for a soup
10. Boil it
11. If it is not green enough, add vert and pour over your crusts ten minutes before serving so they are soaked

title: Potage à la Purée de Racines (Soup with Root Vegetable Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty or forty carrots
- quarter pound of butter
- bouillon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Slice your carrots
2. Take a quarter pound of butter and pass them through, turning them from time to time so they do not stick
3. When they have been on the fire for three quarters of an hour or an hour, moisten with bouillon
4. Add a small piece of sugar about the size of half an egg
5. Let them cook for two hours over low heat
6. Pass them through a sieve
7. If there is not enough of their bouillon and your purée is too thick, moisten it with bouillon from the pot
8. Your bread crusts should be as for bread soup
9. Dip them with clear bouillon so they absorb more easily
10. Pour your purée over them
11. Ensure it is lightly bound so your soup is not too thick
12. Before putting it in, take care to add bouillon to thin it
13. Boil it to be able to skim and remove the fat

title: Potage à la Russe (Russian-Style Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty carrots
- thirty turnips
- thirty small onions
- twenty leeks
- ten celery heads
- six whole lettuces
- broth

## Directions

1. Take vegetables all of the same size, an inch high
2. Blanch them
3. Cook them in broth
4. Cook the lettuces separately
5. Pour everything into a tureen without adding bread

title: Potage à la Vierge (Virgin Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- ordinary broth, about one chopine of the fattiest part
- bread crumb the size of an egg
- white meat of roasted poultry
- sweet almonds
- six hard-boiled egg yolks
- half-setier of cream or a spoonful of milk
- salt to taste
- bread crusts, finely breadcrumbed

## Directions

1. Boil the fattiest part of ordinary broth (about one chopine) for a few boils on a stovetop
2. Pound together the roasted poultry white meat, sweet almonds, and six hard-boiled egg yolks very finely in a mortar
3. Mix this pounded mixture with the broth where you have placed the bread crumb
4. Pass everything through a fine sieve, adding half-setier of cream or a spoonful of milk
5. Season to taste
6. Keep it warm in a bain-marie
7. Simmer your soup gently with well-breadcrumbed bread crusts and a little broth
8. When ready to serve, add your hot coulis to the soup without boiling it, as it would curdle

title: Potage à Veau pour Collation (Veal Soup for Collation)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter of cabbage
- four roots
- two parsnips
- six onions
- a foot of celery
- a small parsley root
- three or four turnips
- sorrel
- beet greens
- chervil
- half-liter of peas

## Directions

1. Take a pot of about three pints.
2. Put in it a quarter of cabbage, four roots, two parsnips, six onions, a foot of celery, a small parsley root, and three or four turnips.
3. Make a bundle with sorrel, beet greens, and chervil, which you tie well together.
4. Tie a half-liter of peas in white linen.
5. Boil all these vegetables with water for three hours.
6. Pass this broth through a sieve.
7. Simmer your soup after having added to the broth the salt it needs.
8. You will garnish the soup with the vegetables that are in the pot.
9. If you wish, you can regulate putting more or less vegetables according to the quantity of broth you want to make.

title: Potages au Fromage en Gras et en Maigre (Cheese Soup, Rich and Lean)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- broth
- cabbage
- other vegetables
- salt
- half a pound or three quarters of a pound of Gruyère cheese
- bread
- butter
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Make a broth as explained for lean soups
2. Use more cabbage than other vegetables
3. When finished and passed through a sieve, add very little salt
4. Take the serving dish that should go on the fire
5. Grate half of the Gruyère cheese and cut the other half into thin slices
6. Put a little grated cheese in the bottom of the dish with some small pieces of butter
7. Cover with very thinly sliced bread
8. Then put a layer of sliced cheese, then a layer of bread covered with grated cheese, put another layer of bread, and finish with sliced cheese and small pieces of butter
9. Moisten with part of your broth
10. Simmer until a small crust forms at the bottom of the dish and no broth remains
11. Before serving, add more broth and a little coarse pepper
12. This soup should be served somewhat thick
13. For the rich version, make it the same way using good rich cabbage broth, do not degrease the broth too much, and add no butter

title: Potages au Lait d'Amandes (Almond Milk Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sweet almonds
- water
- a pint of water
- a little sugar
- very little salt
- cinnamon
- coriander
- a zest of lemon
- sliced and dried bread

## Directions

1. Put a half-pound of sweet almonds on the fire with water.
2. When it is about to boil, remove your almonds to remove their skin.
3. Throw them as they go into fresh water.
4. Then drain them to pound them well in a mortar, watering them from time to time with a spoonful of water, for fear they turn to oil.
5. Put in a pot a pint of water, a little sugar, very little salt, cinnamon, coriander, and a zest of lemon.
6. Boil this water with what you have put in it for about a quarter hour on a stove.
7. Use this composition afterward to pass your almonds through a cloth, pressing them several times with a wooden spoon.
8. Take the dish you must serve and put sliced and dried bread on it.
9. Pour the almond milk over it as hot as you can without boiling.
10. If you want your almond milk simpler, pass it simply with hot water, then add to it the sugar you judge appropriate, and very little salt.

title: Potages aux Petits Oignons Blancs (Small White Onion Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- small white onions
- bread soaked in egg white

## Directions

1. Blanch the onions, remove their first skin
2. Cook them separately in a small pot
3. When they are cooked, arrange them in a border around the edge of the platter where you will serve your soup
4. To make them hold, place on the edges of the platter a strip of bread dipped in egg white
5. Heat the platter for a moment over a stove so the bread attaches
6. Use these strips to help hold all sorts of soup garnishes in place

title: Potages d'Asperges à la Purée Verte (Asparagus Soup with Green Purée)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- roots
- water or broth
- one liter of green peas
- asparagus of medium size
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Make a broth of roots like the preceding one
2. When passed through a sieve, take a part to cook one liter of green peas
3. Take asparagus of medium size, as much as you need to garnish the soup
4. Cut them to the length of three fingers
5. Blanch them briefly in boiling water and remove them to fresh water
6. Drain them and tie them in several small bundles, cut off a little the end of the tip, and put them to cook with the peas
7. When the peas are cooked, pass them into a purée
8. Simmer the soup with the root broth
9. Make a garnish on the edges of the dish with the asparagus
10. When serving, add the pea coulis
11. The rich version is made the same way using good rich broth in place of the lean

title: Potages de Concombres (Cucumber Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- good broth
- veal juice

## Directions

1. Cut the cucumbers neatly.
2. Cook them in a small pot with good broth and veal juice for coloring.
3. When cooked, simmer the soup with their broth and the broth from the pot.
4. Season the soup with good salt.
5. Serve garnished with cucumbers.

title: Potages de Concombres (Cucumber Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- good bouillon
- veal juice for coloring

## Directions

1. Cut the cucumbers neatly
2. Put them to cook in a small pot with good bouillon and veal juice for coloring
3. When they are cooked, simmer the soup with their bouillon

title: Potages de Marrons en Gras et en Maigre (Chestnut Soup, Rich and Lean)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of butter
- three onions
- two roots
- one parsnip
- one celery root
- three leeks
- half a clove of garlic
- two cloves
- water
- salt
- one hundred or one hundred fifty large chestnuts

## Directions

1. Put butter in a saucepan with three onions cut into slices, two roots, a parsnip, a celery root, three leeks, all cut into small pieces, half a clove of garlic, and two cloves
2. Cook all together until they are slightly colored
3. Moisten with water and boil for one hour
4. Pass your broth through a sieve and season with salt
5. Take one hundred or one hundred fifty large chestnuts, remove the first skin, and put them over the fire in a perforated pan, stirring always until you can remove the second skin
6. When well peeled, cook them with part of the broth
7. Set aside those that are whole to garnish the soup, and crush the others and pass them through a sieve to make a coulis that you will moisten with the broth from their cooking
8. Simmer the soup with the root broth, and when serving add the chestnut coulis
9. The rich version is made the same way, using good rich broth in place of the lean

title: Potages Maigres aux Oignons (Lean Onion Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately a dozen medium onions
- a piece of butter
- water or lean broth
- salt
- coarse pepper
- bread

## Directions

1. Cut the onions into strips
2. Put them in a saucepan with a piece of butter
3. Cook them over the fire, turning them from time to time, until they are cooked and lightly colored
4. Moisten them with water or lean broth, if you have it
5. Add salt and coarse pepper
6. Bring to a boil a few times
7. Then add bread to make your soup simmer in the ordinary way

title: Potages Maigres aux Oignons (Lean Onion Soup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- about a dozen medium onions
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut about a dozen medium onions into filets
2. Place them in a pot with a piece of butter
3. Toss them over the fire, turning them from time to time until cooked

title: Potages Maigres de Plusieurs Façons (Lean Soups of Several Kinds)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- small onions, roots, turnips, cabbage, or celery (choice of one for garnish)
- butter
- water
- salt
- onion
- carrots
- parsnips
- celery root
- garlic clove
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- three cloves
- parsley
- chives
- bread crumb filets
- egg white

## Directions

1. Choose the vegetable you wish to use for the soup garnish
2. Cut it neatly and blanch it for a quarter hour in boiling water
3. Remove it to fresh water and cook it with a piece of butter, water, and salt
4. In a separate pot, place a piece of butter with onion, carrots, parsnips, celery root, garlic clove, bay leaf, thyme, basil, three cloves, parsley, and chives, all cut into small pieces
5. Ensure you do not put in this pot the same vegetable you are cooking separately for garnish
6. Toss these vegetables over the fire, turning them from time to time until they are cooked and colored
7. Add water and boil for half an hour with the vegetables
8. This should make a flavorful broth of beautiful color as if it contained meat juices
9. Pass it through a sieve and add it to the pot where the vegetable for garnish is cooking
10. When it is cooked and the broth seasoned to taste, use it to simmer the soup gently in a pot
11. Place the serving dish on the fire and arrange small filets of bread crumb on the edges, dipped in a little egg white, to make them stick
12. Arrange your vegetable garnish neatly on top
13. Pour the soup and serve, or if serving in a bowl, simply place the vegetable on top of the poured soup without elaborate arrangement

title: Poularde Accompagnée (Hen with Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One hen (poularde)
- Ragout
- Lard
- Good sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the wishbone from the breast after gutting the hen
2. Fill it with a mixed ragout like the one found in the ragout section
3. Cook the hen on the spit, wrapped in lard and paper
4. Serve with a good sauce such as sauce à l'Espagnole or à la Sultane

title: Poularde au Blanc-manger (Hen with Blanc-manger)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint of good milk
- Thyme
- Laurel
- Basil
- Coriander
- One handful of bread crumbs
- One quarter pound of panne (lard), cut into small pieces
- One dozen sweet almonds, ground very fine
- Salt
- Nutmeg, grated
- Five raw egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil the milk in a saucepan with thyme, laurel, basil, and coriander until reduced to half
2. Pass it through a sieve and add a handful of bread crumbs
3. Return to the fire until the bread has absorbed the milk
4. Remove from fire and add the panne cut into small pieces, the ground almonds, salt, grated nutmeg, and five raw egg yolks
5. Put all this into the body of the hen, which should be singed, gutted and well cleaned
6. Sew it up so nothing escapes
7. Cook it between lard strips
8. Moisten with milk, season with salt and a little coriander
9. When cooked and well wiped of its fat, serve with a sauce à la reine

title: Poularde au Court Bouillon (Hen in Court Bouillon)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one good hen
- butter
- two onions
- one root
- one parsnip
- bouquet of parsley
- chives
- one clove of garlic
- three cloves
- two shallots
- salt
- coarse pepper
- two glasses of bouillon
- one glass of white wine
- one spoonful of verjus

## Directions

1. Flame the hen, heat the legs to remove the skin, cut the claws in half, clean it and truss the legs by making them enter into the body
2. Tie the hen and put it in a pot just its size with a piece of butter, two onions in slices, a root, a parsnip, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a clove of garlic, three cloves, two shallots, salt, coarse pepper
3. Moisten with two glasses of bouillon, one glass of white wine, one spoonful of verjus
4. Cook on low heat
5. When your hen bends under the finger, pass all the court-bouillon through a sieve, reduce it over the fire to the consistency of a sauce
6. Serve it over the hen

title: Poularde au riz (Hen with Rice)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde (hen)
- riz (rice)
- bouillon (broth)
- consommé (consommé)
- beurre (butter)
- muscade (nutmeg)
- sel (salt)
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)
- jaunes d'oeufs (egg yolks)
- jus de citron (lemon juice)
- bardes de lard (lard strips)
- espagnole (espagnole sauce)

## Directions

1. After flaming your hen, bone it entirely from the back, taking care not to damage the breast
2. Boil half a pound of well-washed and cleaned rice in broth for ten minutes; drain it on a horsehair sieve
3. Place a quarter pound of butter in a casserole and warm it; add your rice; grate a little nutmeg, add a little salt and coarse pepper, and mix everything together
4. Add four egg yolks and mix them well with your rice; let it cool; fill your hen with this mixture as much as it can hold
5. Sew up the back and give it its original shape; rub the breast with lemon juice; cover it with lard strips and tie it
6. Place lard strips in a casserole with your hen and a cooking vessel containing no lemon; place it on the fire one hour before serving
7. Take half a pound of clean rice and place it in a casserole with three times as much consommé or good broth (if the half pound of rice fills one glass, use three glasses of consommé); simmer your rice for three-quarters of an hour and drain it on a horsehair sieve
8. Mix in four spoonfuls of well-reduced espagnole sauce; add butter the size of an egg
9. At the moment of serving, drain your hen, remove the string, place it on the plate, and make a ring of your hot rice; pour a clear espagnole sauce with a piece of glace the size of half a walnut underneath your hen
10. If you do not have sauce, make a light roux; pass through a silk sieve the liquid in which your hen cooked and pour it over your roux; reduce it and use it in place of espagnole sauce; check that it has good seasoning

title: Poularde au Sang (Hen with Blood Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- Butter
- One good half-setier of pork blood
- Four egg yolks
- One quarter pound of panne (lard), chopped
- A small pinch of ground coriander
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan chopped parsley, chives, mushrooms, and a small piece of butter
2. Cook on the fire, then add a good half-setier of pork blood with four egg yolks, chopped panne, a small pinch of ground coriander, salt, and coarse pepper
3. Thicken all together on the fire without letting it boil, stirring well
4. When cold, put it in the body of a hen
5. Cook the hen on the spit wrapped in lard and paper
6. Serve with a piquant sauce

title: Poularde aux Moules (Hen with Mussels)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde (hen)
- moules (mussels)
- persil (parsley)
- ciboules (scallions)
- beurre (butter)
- béchamelle (béchamel sauce)
- velouté (velouté sauce)
- consommé (consommé)
- muscade râpée (grated nutmeg)
- jaunes d'oeufs (egg yolks)
- farine (flour)
- bouillon (broth)

## Directions

1. Truss and cook your hen as you would a poêlée hen; rinse the mussels thoroughly in several waters and place them in a casserole over a hot fire to detach them; do not let them boil so they do not shrivel
2. Drain them in a casserole; pass some parsley and scallions through a small piece of butter; add four spoonfuls of béchamel (or if you have none, velouté), one spoonful of consommé, and a little grated nutmeg
3. Bring your sauce to a boil a few times and pour it over your mussels
4. At the moment of serving, drain your hen, untruss it, and place it on your plate; add to your mussels a liaison of three egg yolks
5. If using velouté, place them on the fire to bind; do not let them boil
6. If you do not have velouté, add a little flour to your herbs and butter; moisten with broth and proceed as described above

title: Poularde aux Oignons (Hen with Onions)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one good tender hen
- its liver
- lard
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- small white onions
- bouillon
- coulis

## Directions

1. Flame, pluck and clean the hen; take its liver, chop it and mix with lard scraped with a knife, parsley, chives and mushrooms; chop everything together, season with salt and pepper, mix well this stuffing and stuff your hen with it
2. Sew it closed so the stuffing does not escape
3. Cook it on a spit, wrapped in lard and paper
4. When it is cooked, serve it with a ragout of small white onions made as follows: take small white onions and cut a little off the tip of the head and tail
5. Cook them for a quarter hour in boiling water, remove them in fresh water to remove the first skin
6. Cook them afterwards in bouillon; when they are cooked and drained, put them to take flavor in good well-seasoned coulis, making them boil a few times over a stove
7. Serve around the hen

title: Poularde aux Oignons d'une Autre Façon (Hen with Onions, Another Way)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one hen
- onion ragout
- coulis from the cooking

## Directions

1. Cook it on a spit or braised like hen between two dishes
2. Mix the sauce from its cooking into the onion ragout to give it body

title: Poularde aux Truffes (Hen with Truffles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde (hen)
- truffes (truffles)
- beurre (butter)
- sel (salt)
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)
- quatre épices (four spices)
- bardes de lard (lard lardons)
- rouelle de veau (veal wheel)
- oignons (onions)
- carottes (carrots)
- feuilles de laurier (bay leaves)
- clous de girofle (cloves)
- bouillon (broth)
- jus de citron (lemon juice)
- espagnole (Spanish sauce)
- consommé (consommé)

## Directions

1. Flame and gut the hen through the stomach
2. Remove the breastbone
3. Fill the hen with truffles that you have passed through a good piece of butter seasoned with salt, coarse pepper, and four spices
4. Arrange everything in the body of the hen
5. Truss the hen as for an entrée
6. Rub the stomach with lemon juice
7. Cover with lard lardons
8. Place two or three lardons in a saucepan with the hen on top
9. Cut veal wheel into large dice about a pound and a half, add four onions, three carrots cut into pieces, two bay leaves, two cloves, half a pound of butter, and all the truffle trimmings
10. Put everything in a saucepan over a hot fire and pass for ten minutes
11. Pour in a pot spoon of broth
12. Place this seasoning on your hen
13. One hour before serving, put on the fire with fire on the lid
14. At the moment of serving, drain and unbridle the hen and dress it on your platter
15. For sauce, chop three truffes and pass them through butter
16. Add six skimming spoons of espagnole and three spoons of consommé
17. Put twelve truffles in your sauce
18. Reduce by a third, degrease, and place under your hen
19. Check that it is properly salted
20. If you do not have espagnole, make a light roux that you will moisten with the broth in which your hen has cooked, reduce it and degrease it, and use it as sauce

title: Poularde aux truffes (Hen with Truffles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde (hen)
- truffes (truffles)

## Directions

1. After flaming and cleaning the hen from the breast, remove the breastbone; fill it with truffles

title: Poularde en Bigarrure (Hen in Variegated Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- deux belles poulardes (two fine hens)
- bardes de lard (lard lardons)
- lard fin (fine lard)
- tranches de veau (veal slices)
- oignons (onions)
- carottes (carrots)
- clous de girofle (cloves)
- feuille de laurier (bay leaf)
- thym (thyme)
- persil (parsley)
- ciboules (chives)
- papier beurrée (buttered paper)
- gelée (jelly)
- truffes (truffles)
- beurre (butter)
- sel (salt)
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)
- quatre épices (four spices)
- jus de citron (lemon juice)
- langues à l'écarlate (scarlet tongues)
- poêle (poele)

## Directions

1. Take two fine hens with well-fleshed stomachs
2. Flame them lightly
3. Remove the four fillets with the wings as correctly as possible
4. Remove the four thighs while preserving almost all the skin
5. Lard your four fillets with fine lard
6. Pare them and cover your wing tips so they cannot be seen, so they have appearance and your fillets are more elevated
7. Put them in a saucepan with lard lardons, veal slices, two onions cut in slices, two carrots the same, two cloves, a bay leaf, a little thyme, a bouquet of parsley and chives, and your fillets over this seasoning
8. Cover with a buttered paper sheet
9. Fill with a small pot spoon of jelly
10. Completely bone your four thighs
11. Chop four truffles that you will pass through butter, a little salt, a little coarse pepper, a little four spices
12. When your truffles are cold, fill the four thighs with them
13. Gather the meats with a needle and thread so that your thighs form a flat round at the thick end
14. Cut off a foot three-quarters and put it in the thigh so the claws show
15. Pinch thereafter a piece of butter, lemon juice, salt, and pepper in a saucepan
16. Put it on the fire with your thighs inside
17. Make them stiffen afterwards
18. Cover them with lardons and place a poêle on top
19. Let them cook and cool
20. Lard two of them all the way through with truffles in small equal sticks and at the same distance
21. Lard the two others with scarlet tongues the same way as those with truffles
22. Put them in a saucepan with the broth passed through silk sieve in which they cooked
23. Remove the nerves from the tenderloin fillets and put truffles there as it is said for rabbit fillets
24. Put them in a silver platter with warm butter and a hint of salt
25. Three-quarters of an hour before serving, put your fowl fillets on the fire
26. Place some on the lid
27. Keep your thighs warm
28. At the moment of serving, drain your fillets and glaze them

title: Poularde en campine (Hen Cooked in Paper)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde (hen)
- lard râpé (grated lard)
- beurre (butter)
- huile (oil)
- échalotes (shallots)
- champignons (mushrooms)
- persil (parsley)
- quatre épices (four spices)
- sel (salt)
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)
- papier huilé (oiled paper)

## Directions

1. Have a beautiful hen, flame and clean it from the breast; remove the breastbone; cut off the legs and tuck the thighs inside; cut off the wings
2. Place in a casserole half a pound of grated lard, as much butter, and four spoonfuls of oil; place your hen on a low fire for three-quarters of an hour; it need not take color
3. Remove it; then place in your casserole two spoonfuls of finely chopped shallots, which you will let brown briefly with six spoonfuls of finely chopped mushrooms, two spoonfuls of chopped parsley, a little of the four spices, salt, and coarse pepper
4. When everything has browned for ten minutes, pour it over your hen and let it cool
5. Oil six large sheets of paper; on the first one place your hen with a very thin lard strip and half of the herbs that you will arrange in its body, with the rest underneath and on top with a very thin lard strip
6. Wrap your piece as hermetically as possible with the remaining oiled sheets; ensure your campine has a beautiful shape; tie it with string over the paper
7. Place it in a hot oven for one hour, or on a grill over a low fire and in a hot country oven for three-quarters of an hour
8. At the moment of serving, untie it; remove the first and second sheets if they are too browned, and serve it in the paper

title: Poularde en Canelon (Hen Roulade)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One hen (poularde)
- Good poultry forcemeat
- Lard strip
- One half-glass of white wine
- Good broth
- Bouquet garni
- Salt
- Pepper
- Two spoonfuls of coulis

## Directions

1. Debone it completely after cutting it in half
2. Place a good poultry forcemeat on each half
3. Roll them and cover the top with a lard strip
4. Tie with string and cook for one hour with a half-glass of white wine, good broth, a bouquet garni, salt, and pepper
5. When cooked, pass the sauce through a sieve
6. Degrease it and add two spoonfuls of coulis
7. Reduce on the fire to the consistency of a sauce
8. Remove the lard strips and strings
9. Serve the sauce over the hen roulades

title: Poularde en Croustade (Hen in Crust)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One hen (poularde)
- Large lard strips, well interlarded
- Good broth
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bouquet garni
- Butter kneaded with flour
- Milk
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Singe it, gut it, truss the legs into the body and lard it crosswise with large lard strips well interlarded
2. Cook it with a little good broth, salt, pepper, and a bouquet
3. When cooked, let all the sauce attach around it and let it cool
4. In a saucepan, put a good piece of butter kneaded with half a spoonful of flour, moisten with a little milk, salt, and pepper
5. Thicken this sauce so it is well thick
6. Spread it all over the hen and add bread crumbs gradually until it forms a crust
7. Brown it under a covered baking dish lid
8. Serve with a piquant sauce

title: Poularde en Filets (Hen Fillets)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover cooked hen (poularde)
- Sauce, thickened with roux or white sauce like béchamel, or sauce à la reine

## Directions

1. Take hens that have been removed from the table and cut them into fillets
2. Heat them in a good thickened sauce made with roux or white sauce like béchamel, or sauce à la reine

title: Poularde en petit deuil (Chicken in Half Mourning)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde (hen)
- citron (lemon)
- bardes de lard (lard strips)
- truffes (truffles)
- beurre (butter)
- velouté (velouté sauce)
- consommé (consommé)
- sel (salt)
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)

## Directions

1. Flame, pluck, and clean your hen; cut off its legs and tuck the thighs inside; truss it so that the breast protrudes well; cover it with lemon slices and lard strips
2. Place lard strips in a casserole, your hen on top; add a poêle (cooking vessel); cook it three-quarters of the way and remove from the fire
3. For best results, let it cool; with a small piece of wood, make holes on the breast at equal intervals, and in each hole place a small truffle lardon; arrange them neatly so they form an oval covering the full width of the breast
4. Cover your studded piece with lard; place it in a casserole and pass the cooking liquid through a silk sieve
5. One hour before serving, let it simmer; at the moment of serving, drain and untruss it; place it on your plate
6. Chop a truffle and pass it with a small piece of butter; add three or four spoonfuls of velouté, one of consommé, very little salt, and a bit of coarse pepper
7. Bring your sauce to a boil a few times; place it under your hen

title: Poularde Entre Deux Plats (Hen Cooked Between Two Dishes)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One poularde
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- Garlic
- Slices of veal
- Bacon bards
- Coulis
- Verjus

## Directions

1. Flame and clean the poularde, truss the legs into the body
2. Prepare it in a casserole over the fire with a piece of butter, salt, pepper, chopped parsley, chives, mushrooms, and a hint of garlic
3. Place slices of veal in the bottom of a casserole
4. Place the poularde on top with its seasoning
5. Cover it with bacon bards
6. Cook this way over hot ashes
7. When cooked, deglaze the sauce and pass it through a sieve
8. Add one spoonful of coulis and a dash of verjus
9. Taste the sauce to ensure it is well flavored
10. Serve the sauce over the poularde

title: Poularde Masquée (Masked Hen)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one good hen
- its liver
- lard
- parsley
- chives
- shallots
- two egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- fat from the pot
- new ham fillets
- soft bread fillets

## Directions

1. Flame, pluck and clean the hen
2. Make a stuffing from its liver with scraped lard, parsley, chives, shallots, two egg yolks, salt, pepper
3. Stuff the inside of the hen, sew it closed at both ends
4. Brown it in a pan with fat from the pot, then put it on the spit
5. Garnish all around with fillets of new ham and fillets of soft bread, the length of the hen
6. Wrap everything in several leaves of white paper so that the jus and fat cannot escape
7. Cook it at very low heat without basting
8. When taking it off the spit, be careful to put something underneath to catch the jus
9. Dress the hen in the serving dish with the ham and bread slices around it, with the jus it rendered

title: Poularde à la Bourgeoise (Hen Braised Country-Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One poularde
- Good butter
- Two onions
- Two roots
- Bouquet garni of fine herbs
- Salt
- Half a glass of white wine
- Coulis

## Directions

1. Flame and clean the poularde, truss the legs into the body
2. Put a little good butter in the bottom of a casserole
3. Cut two onions into slices and place them in the butter
4. Place the poularde on top, with the breast down
5. Cover the poularde with two onions in slices
6. Add two roots cut into fillets and a bouquet garni of fine herbs
7. Season with a little salt
8. Cook the poularde this way over hot ashes
9. At half the cooking time, add half a glass of white wine
10. When cooked, deglaze the sauce and pass it through a sieve
11. Add a little coulis to the sauce
12. Serve the sauce over the poularde

title: Poularde à la Béchamel (Hen with Béchamel Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one hen cooked on a spit
- a chopine of cream or half a setier of milk
- butter the size of half an egg
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- two shallots
- half a clove of garlic
- parsley
- chives
- one egg yolk
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Ordinarily one takes a hen cooked on a spit that has been removed from the table; cut it into pieces, or better, when the hen is almost whole and large, lift all the meat and cut it into fillets
2. Put in a pan a chopine of cream or half a setier of milk; when it boils, put into it butter the size of half an egg worked with a pinch of flour, with salt, pepper, two shallots, half a clove of garlic, parsley, chives
3. Boil on low heat for half an hour
4. When it is reduced to the consistency of a sauce, pass it through a fine sieve, put the hen in it to warm without boiling
5. If the sauce is not quite bound, put an egg yolk in it, make it thicken without boiling, serving two or three drops of vinegar

title: Poularde à la Chevalière (Hen à la Chevalière)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde (hen)
- beurre (butter)
- sel (salt)
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)
- épices (spices)
- bardes de lard (lard lardons)
- lard fin (fine lard)
- tranches de veau (veal slices)
- carottes (carrots)
- oignons (onions)
- clous de girofle (cloves)
- feuille de laurier (bay leaf)
- thym (thyme)
- persil (parsley)
- ciboules (chives)
- bouillon (broth)
- papier beurrée (buttered paper)
- ragoût de crêtes et de rognons de coqs (ragout of cock combs and kidneys)

## Directions

1. Flame, gut, and remove the breastbone of your hen
2. Mix butter, salt, coarse pepper, and a little spices
3. Fill your hen with this mixture
4. Bridle it as for an entrée
5. Make the stomach swell as much as possible
6. Lard it with fine lard a second thick, or end to end well garnished
7. Arrange lard lardons in the bottom of an oval saucepan
8. Put your hen there and around it a few veal slices, three carrots, three onions, two cloves, a bay leaf, a little thyme, a bouquet of parsley and chives
9. Fill with a pot spoon of grand broth
10. Cover with a buttered paper sheet
11. Put it on the fire an hour before serving
12. Place a hot fire on the lid
13. Take care that your fire does not glaze your piece too much
14. At the moment of serving, drain your hen, unbridle it, and dress it on your platter
15. Put underneath a ragout of cock combs and kidneys

title: Poularde à la Chia (Hen with Chia)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One hen (poularde), roasted on the spit
- Chia (a type of gherkin from the Indies)
- Sauce with coulis

## Directions

1. Cut the chia into slices
2. Soak them for a quarter hour in nearly boiling water
3. Drain them and put them in a sauce with coulis
4. Serve over a hen cooked on the spit

title: Poularde à la Crème (Hen with Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One hen (poularde), roasted on the spit
- Meat from the breast
- One handful of bread crumbs
- One pint of milk
- Nearly one half-pound of beef fat
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- Salt
- Pepper
- Five egg yolks

## Directions

1. Chop very fine the meat from the breast of a hen that has been roasted on the spit and removed from the table
2. Mix it with a good handful of bread crumbs boiled on the fire with one pint of milk reduced until it is thick and cooled
3. Add nearly one half-pound of beef fat, parsley, chives, mushrooms, all chopped, salt, pepper, and five egg yolks
4. Put this forcemeat in the body of the hen and in place of the breast
5. Smooth the top with a knife dipped in beaten egg
6. Coat with bread crumbs
7. Put the hen in a baking dish on lard strips
8. Cover with paper and cook under a covered baking dish lid
9. Serve with a piquant sauce

title: Poularde à la Cuisinière Bourgeoise (Hen Prepared as the Cook Makes It)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one hen
- its liver
- a little butter
- parsley
- chives
- a pinch of chopped garlic
- salt
- coarse pepper
- two egg yolks
- hot butter
- one egg yolk
- bread crumbs
- half a glass of bouillon
- a little vinegar
- butter the size of half an egg
- flour
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Flame, pluck and clean a hen, stuff it with its liver mixed with a little butter, parsley, chives, a pinch of chopped garlic, salt, coarse pepper, two egg yolks
2. Cook it on a spit
3. When it is cooked, sprinkle the top with a little hot butter in which you have dissolved an egg yolk, bread it with bread crumbs
4. Make it take on a beautiful golden color over the fire
5. Serve it with sauce made as follows: put in a pan half a glass of bouillon, a little vinegar, butter the size of half an egg worked with a good pinch of flour, salt, coarse pepper, grated nutmeg
6. Make it thicken over the fire

title: Poularde à la Matelotte (Hen in Matelote Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one hen
- white wine
- bouillon
- six large onions
- chard
- parsnips
- bouquet of parsley
- cloves
- chives
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- two slices of lemon
- salt
- pepper
- coulis

## Directions

1. Flame and clean the hen, leave the wings and legs, and truss as for cooking in a pot
2. Lard it with lard
3. Cook it with white wine, a little bouillon, six large onions, chard and parsnips properly cut; a bouquet of parsley, cloves, chives, thyme, bay leaf, basil, two slices of lemon, salt, pepper
4. Cook on low heat
5. When it is cooked, dress the hen in the serving dish with the onions and roots around it
6. Serve with its sauce well degreased; if you have coulis to put in the sauce, it will have more consistency

title: Poularde à la Montmorency (Hen Montmorency Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one hen
- livers cut in dice
- small lard
- small eggs

## Directions

1. Prick the top of the hen; after flaming and cleaning it, fill it with livers cut in dice, small lard, small eggs
2. Sew the hen so nothing escapes
3. Cook it like a fricandeau and glaze it the same way

title: Poularde à la Montmorency (Hen with Montmorency Garnish)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One hen (poularde)
- Poultry livers, cut into dice
- Small lard
- Small eggs

## Directions

1. Lard the top of the hen
2. After singeing and gutting it, fill it with diced poultry livers, small lard, and small eggs
3. Sew up the hen so nothing escapes
4. Cook it like a fricandeau and glaze it in the same manner

title: Poularde à la Persillade (Hen with Parsley)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One raw or roasted poularde
- Good bouillon
- Coulis
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Parsley
- Water
- Verjus

## Directions

1. Take a raw poularde or one that has already been roasted and served; even if it is partially eaten, this does not matter
2. Cut it into pieces and cook it in a casserole with good bouillon and coulis, salt, and a little coarse pepper
3. When cooked and the sauce is sufficiently reduced, add a good pinch of very finely chopped parsley that has been briefly cooked in water beforehand
4. Press the parsley well
5. When serving, add a dash of verjus

title: Poularde à la Reine (Hen Royale)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- poularde (hen)
- bardes de lard (lard lardons)
- chevilles de bois (wooden pegs)
- velouté (velvet sauce)
- jaune d'œuf (egg yolk)
- beurre (butter)
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)
- sel (salt)

## Directions

1. When your hen is cooked in a pan, let it cool
2. Remove the meat from the stomach in the shape of an oval hole the length of your piece
3. With the meat make a cooked forcemeat
4. Fill your hen and give it its original form
5. Envelope the around of your fowl with lard lardons which you will secure with small wooden pegs
6. Put it on a tourtière in the oven, or place your tourtière on a low fire with the country oven a little warm above for a good hour
7. At the moment of serving, remove the lardons that surround your hen
8. Dress it on your platter
9. Add underneath a reduced velouté in which you will put a liaison of an egg yolk and butter the size of half an egg, a little coarse pepper
10. Check that your sauce is properly salted

title: Poularde à la Saint-Gara (Hen at the Saint-Gara)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Hen
- Lard strips
- Scarlet tongue
- Salt
- Pepper
- Four spices
- Veal slices
- Carrots
- Onions
- Cloves
- Parsley
- Chives
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- Lemon juice
- Bouillon
- Red tongue
- Butter
- Nutmeg
- Velouté
- Consommé
- Meat glaze

## Directions

1. When the hen is singed and emptied, prepare lardons of fat lard with scarlet tongue
2. Season them with salt, pepper, and four spices, mixing well
3. Remove the bones from inside the breast of your hen
4. Cut off the legs and truss the thighs inside
5. Lard your hen correctly, alternating lard and tongue lardons from the breast to the kidneys, spacing them equally to form an oval pattern
6. Truss your hen making the breast dome nicely and give it a pleasant shape
7. Place lard strips in a saucepan with several veal slices, some carrots cut into slices, four onions (one stuck with two cloves), a bouquet of parsley and chives, and a bay leaf with a little thyme
8. Rub with lemon juice several lard strips with which you will cover your hen
9. Place it on your seasoning with a round of buttered paper to cover it
10. Pour in about a small spoonful of bouillon and a little salt
11. Put it on the fire an hour before serving and let it simmer
12. Take a piece about the size of an egg from the reddest of the tongue you have used
13. Pound it until pulverized
14. Add a piece of butter the size of half an egg, a little coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg; pound all this again
15. Put it in a saucepan and dilute with a spoonfuls of velouté
16. Heat your purée without boiling so it dilutes; pour in about two spoonfuls of consommé in which you will have melted a piece of meat glaze the size of half a walnut
17. When your purée is well diluted, strain it through a sieve, working it through; this purée should represent a sauce, not a purée, so it must be clear
18. Keep it warm in a bain-marie
19. At the moment of serving, drain your hen, untruss it, arrange it on your plate, and put your sauce underneath; ensure it is a good red color and well seasoned

title: Poulet au Riz (Chicken with Rice)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken
- Rooster crests and kidneys
- Mushrooms
- Lard slices
- Veal rouelle slices
- Two carrots
- Three onions
- Two cloves
- One bay leaf
- Thyme
- Consommé
- One pound of rice
- Bouillon
- Pâte round
- Butter
- Velouté
- Three eggs
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. When your chicken is singed and gutted, cut off the legs and truss the thighs inside; debone the interior of the breast of your chicken without damaging the exterior; put inside a ragout of rooster crests and kidneys, mushrooms, and add a strong liaison to your ragout; when it is cold, put it in your chicken; take care to wrap the ends well with lard slices so your ragout does not escape; when your chicken cooks, pour lemon juice over its breast; cover it with lard slices; put some also in the bottom of your saucepan with some slices of veal rouelle, two carrots cut in slices, three onions, two cloves, a bay leaf, a little thyme, two tablespoons of consommé; place your chicken on this seasoning; put it on the fire: three-quarters of an hour is enough to cook it if it is young; pass through a silk sieve the cooking liquid in which your chicken cooked; add to it one pound of well-cleaned and well-washed rice; put good bouillon in case there is not enough; boil your rice for a quarter of an hour; leave it to drain on a hair sieve; butter a saucepan large enough to contain it; put a round of pâte in the middle, your rice over it; place your chicken so it cannot be seen; fill your saucepan with rice; one hour before serving, make it take color like a rice cake; put fire on the lid: at the moment of serving, turn over your rice loaf; remove the round of pâte, and clear out the interior of your loaf without damaging the edges or bottom; pour in a full tablespoon of reduced velouté, mushrooms, the rest of your chicken ragout, coarse pepper, a liaison of three eggs, a piece of butter the size of an egg; put them in the interior of your rice loaf; make sure your sauce is properly salted.

title: Poulet Froid à la Sauce d'Estragon (Cold Chicken with Tarragon Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cold roasted chickens
- Velouté
- Eight spoonfuls of degreased stock
- Four spoonfuls of aspic
- Two tablespoons of tarragon vinegar
- Coarse pepper
- One egg yolk (if needed)
- Fresh parsley
- Fresh tarragon
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Aspic for decoration
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Carve two cold roasted chickens that have been cooked in a pan as correctly as possible.
2. Place the pieces in a saucepan with velouté, eight spoonfuls of degreased stock, four spoonfuls of aspic, and two tablespoons of tarragon vinegar, and a little coarse pepper.
3. Reduce the entire mixture by one third.
4. If your sauce is a little brown, add a liaison of one egg yolk.
5. When reduced to the proper consistency, add some finely chopped parsley and tarragon.
6. Add two boils of bouillon to your sauce.
7. Check the seasoning for salt and pour it over your cold chicken pieces.
8. Allow to cool.
9. Arrange on your platter and pour the sauce, which will be almost congealed, over your chickens.
10. Decorate this dish with aspic and croutons.

title: Poulet Gras aux Truffes (Fat Chicken with Truffles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken
- Truffles
- Lard for barding
- Oiled paper

## Directions

1. Lightly singe your chicken.
2. Pluck and clean it by the stomach.
3. Arrange your chicken as you would a fattened hen with truffles.
4. Place a small slice of lard between the skin of the pocket and the truffles, to prevent the skin from bursting.
5. Attach the legs to the spit and wrap it in oiled paper.

title: Poulet à la Tartare (Breaded Chicken)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Lightly singe your chicken.
2. Cut off the legs and truss the thighs inside.
3. Remove the giblet pocket and neck.
4. Split the chicken down the back from neck to tail.
5. Empty it and flatten it without bruising it.
6. Warm a good piece of butter.
7. Season your chicken with salt and coarse pepper and dip it in the warm butter, coating it thoroughly.
8. Roll it in breadcrumbs, pressing them on as much as possible.
9. Three quarters of an hour before serving, place it on the grill over a gentle fire.
10. At the moment of serving, arrange it on the platter and serve with tarragon sauce.

title: Poulet à la Tartare (Chicken Tartare Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One chicken
- Good fresh butter
- Parsley
- Chives (ciboule)
- Mushrooms
- A hint of garlic
- Salt
- Black pepper
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Singe and gut the chicken, sear it on the fire and cut it in half, break its bones slightly, and marinate it with good fresh butter that you melt, mixed with parsley, chives, mushrooms, a hint of garlic, all chopped, salt, and pepper
2. Dip it in the butter and coat with breadcrumbs, grill it over low heat, and serve dry or with a good light sauce

title: Poulets au Cerfeuil (Chicken with Chervil)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Two carrots
- One parsnip
- Two or three onions
- One large clove of garlic
- Two cloves
- One bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- One glass of white wine
- Bouillon
- Good butter
- Flour
- Two pinches of chervil

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan a little butter with two carrots, one parsnip cut in strips, two or three onions in slices, one large clove of garlic, two cloves, one bay leaf, thyme, and basil
2. Cook over medium heat until slightly colored
3. Moisten with one glass of white wine and as much bouillon
4. Cook over low heat and reduce by half
5. Strain through a sieve
6. Add a piece of good butter the size of half an egg worked with a good pinch of flour and two pinches of chervil chopped very finely
7. Thicken this sauce over the fire
8. Serve over chickens cooked on the spit

title: Poulets au Fromage (Chicken with Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two chickens
- Butter

## Directions

1. Singe and pluck the chickens; after gutting them and tucking the legs in the body, split them slightly on the back and flatten them with the cleaver, sear them in butter

title: Poulets au Fromage (Chicken with Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two chickens
- Butter
- Half a glass of white wine
- Bouillon
- Bunch of parsley
- Chives
- Half a clove of garlic
- Two cloves
- Half a bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Good butter
- Flour
- Gruyère cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Flame and pluck two chickens; after having emptied them and tucked the legs into the body, split them slightly on the back and flatten them with the cleaver
2. Brown them in a saucepan with a little butter
3. Moisten with half a glass of white wine and as much bouillon
4. Add a bunch of parsley, chives, half a clove of garlic, two cloves, half a bay leaf, thyme, basil, salt, and pepper
5. Cook for one hour over low heat
6. Remove the chickens and put into the sauce a piece of good butter the size of a walnut, worked with a pinch of flour
7. Thicken the sauce over the fire
8. Prepare the serving dish with a layer of this sauce on the bottom, and on the sauce a small handful of grated Gruyère cheese
9. Place the chickens on top, and on the chickens put the remaining sauce, then as much grated Gruyère cheese as you put beneath
10. Place the dish over low heat with a pie-dish cover with fire on top
11. When they are a beautiful golden color, serve hot
12. If your cheese is very salty, do not put salt in the cooking of the chickens

title: Poulets au Réveil (Chicken Awakening)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two chickens
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Two tarragon leaves
- Two or three branches of chervil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fat from the pot
- Lard
- Two carrots
- Two onions
- One clove of garlic
- Two cloves
- One bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil

## Directions

1. Flame and pluck two chickens and empty them
2. Chop the livers which you mix with a piece of butter, parsley, chives, two tarragon leaves, two or three branches of chervil, all chopped, salt, and pepper
3. Stuff the chickens with this forcemeat and truss with the legs
4. Brown on the fire with a little butter or fat from the pot
5. Cook on the spit wrapped in lard and paper
6. Put in a saucepan the butter used to brown the chickens, with two carrots in strips, two onions in slices, one clove of garlic, two cloves, one bay leaf, thyme, and basil

title: Poulets au Verjus de Grains (Chickens with Green Grape Verjuice)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickens
- Chicken liver
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Two onions
- One clove of garlic
- One carrot
- One parsnip
- Two cloves
- Flour
- One glass of bouillon
- A good handful of green grape verjuice
- Two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Singe, pluck and empty the chickens
2. Stuff the inside with the liver mixed with butter, parsley, chives, salt, coarse pepper
3. Cook on the spit
4. Put in a saucepan a little butter with two onions, one clove of garlic, parsley, chives, one carrot, one parsnip, two cloves
5. Pass all together over the fire until colored
6. Add a good pinch of flour
7. Moisten with one glass of bouillon
8. Let cook and reduce to half
9. Strain through a sieve
10. Take a good handful of green grape verjuice, remove the seeds and blanch them an instant in boiling water
11. Remove them to drain
12. Put them in the sauce with two egg yolks, bind over the fire without boiling, stirring always
13. As soon as it reaches a boil, remove from the fire and serve over the chickens

title: Poulets aux Choux-Fleurs (Chicken with Cauliflower)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two medium chickens
- Chicken livers
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives (ciboule)
- Salt
- Black pepper
- Lard
- Buttered paper
- Cauliflower
- Water
- Coulis
- Egg-sized piece of butter

## Directions

1. Singe, pluck, and gut the chickens; remove the bitter part from the livers, chop them and mix with a piece of butter, chopped parsley, chopped chives, salt, and black pepper
2. Put this small forcemeat in the body, leave the legs and truss them like a young turkey
3. Sear them on the fire in a casserole with a little butter or lard, then wrap them with lard and buttered paper to roast on the spit
4. When cooked, arrange them on the serving plate, arrange cauliflower well drained around them, which you will have cooked with water, a little butter and salt
5. Pour over a sauce made with a little coulis, butter the size of half an egg, salt, and black pepper, thicken it over heat without boiling

title: Poulets aux Croûtons (Chickens with Croutons)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one large chicken or two, depending on the size of your dish
- chicken liver
- lard
- paper
- fine oil
- bread croutons
- two onions
- parsley
- chives
- shallot
- poultry liver
- one glass of white wine
- one spoonful of coulis
- salt
- crushed pepper

## Directions

1. Make a stuffing from the chicken liver and cook the chickens on the spit wrapped in lard and paper
2. When cooked, prepare the sauce by heating fine oil and frying two bread croutons quickly
3. Drain the croutons and in the same oil fry two onions cut into strips
4. When the onions are three-quarters cooked, add chopped parsley, chives, shallot, and poultry liver
5. Moisten with one glass of white wine and one spoonful of coulis
6. Degrease the sauce and season with salt and crushed pepper
7. Cook on low heat for a quarter hour
8. Serve the chickens and croutons on the side

title: Poulets aux Petits Pois (Chickens with Peas)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickens
- One liter of peas
- Butter
- Bunch of parsley
- Chives
- Flour
- Juice
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the chickens into pieces and put them in a saucepan with one liter of peas, a piece of butter, a bunch of parsley, chives
2. Pass them over the fire and add a good pinch of flour
3. Moisten with half juice and half bouillon
4. Cook and reduce to a short sauce
5. Add salt only a moment before serving, and a little sugar if desired

title: Poulets Braisés aux Fines Herbes (Braised Chickens with Fine Herbs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two chickens
- Chicken livers
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Two leaves of tarragon
- Two or three branches of chervil
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Lard
- Paper
- Two roots or zests
- Two onions
- One clove of garlic
- Two cloves
- One bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- One glass of white wine
- Bouillon
- Herbs for salad garnish: tarragon, burnet, chervil, chives, watercress with walnuts

## Directions

1. Singe and pluck two chickens, empty them and chop the livers which you mix with a piece of butter, parsley, chives, two leaves of tarragon, two or three branches of chervil, all chopped, salt, coarse pepper
2. Stuff the chickens with this mixture and truss with the legs
3. Brown over the fire with a little butter or pot fat
4. Cook on the spit wrapped in lard and paper
5. Put in a saucepan the butter used to brown the chickens, with two roots or zests, two onions in slices, one clove of garlic, two cloves, one bay leaf, thyme, basil
6. Pass over the fire without browning
7. Moisten with one glass of white wine and as much bouillon
8. Boil gently for half an hour and strain through a sieve
9. Take herbs for salad garnish such as tarragon, burnet, chervil, chives, watercress with walnuts, each according to taste, to make about half a handful which you chop very fine
10. Put in the sauce to infuse for half an hour on hot ashes without boiling
11. Strain through a sieve and press the herbs to extract their essence
12. Add to the sauce the size of two walnuts of good butter mixed with a good pinch of flour, salt, coarse pepper
13. Bind over the fire without boiling
14. Serve over the chickens

title: Poulets en Caisse (Chicken in Paper Case)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two chickens
- Parsley
- Chives (ciboule)
- Shallots
- Garlic
- Fine oil
- Salt
- Black pepper
- Lard bacon slices
- Paper
- Verjuice (optional)

## Directions

1. Singe and gut the chickens, truss the legs in the body, leave the wings and flatten the chickens slightly
2. Marinate them with whole parsley, chives, shallots, garlic, fine oil, salt, and black pepper
3. Make a paper case, put the chickens in it with all their seasoning, and cover them with lard slices and paper
4. Cook over low heat on the grill or under a pie dish cover; when cooked, remove the fine herbs and lard slices; serve in the case, putting a few drops of verjuice on the chickens; you can also remove them from the case and serve with the sauce of your choice

title: Poulets en Entrée de Broche (Chicken Spit Entrée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two fat white chickens of equal size
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. You take two fat white chickens of equal size; singe and gut them through the cavity; remove the breastbone; put a good piece of butter in a saucepan with salt, coarse pepper

title: Poulets en Entrée de Broche (Chickens as Spit Course)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickens
- Lard, grated
- Chicken liver
- Parsley
- Chives
- Salt
- Fat from the cooking pot
- Paper

## Directions

1. Singe at the flame of a furnace, empty the chickens and put in the body a little grated lard and the chopped chicken liver, a little chopped parsley and chives, very little salt
2. Sew them so nothing comes out
3. Brown them in a saucepan with fat from the pot
4. Cook on the spit, wrapped in lard and paper
5. Do not place over too ardent a fire, for fear they color, because chickens as a spit course should be served white
6. When cooked, arrange them on the serving plat and add whatever sauce or stew you judge appropriate

title: Poulets en Entrée de Broche (Roasted Chickens as an Entrée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two fat, well-whitened chickens of equal size
- A good piece of butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Juice of a lemon
- Thin slices of lemons
- Lard slices (for barding)
- Buttered paper

## Directions

1. Flame the chickens and empty them through the vent
2. Remove the breastbone
3. In a saucepan, put a good piece of butter, salt, coarse pepper, and lemon juice; mix the seasoning with your butter
4. Fill the chickens with this mixture
5. Truss and bridle them as for an entrée
6. Arrange thin slices of lemon on the breast of your chickens
7. Cover them with slices of lard
8. Spit them and wrap them in buttered paper
9. Three quarters of an hour before serving, put them on the fire; take care that they do not take on color
10. At the moment of serving, remove them from the spit and unbridle them
11. Remove the lard and lemon that may remain
12. Circle the opening near the rump
13. Dress them on your platter and put underneath a hot aspic, a bound velouté, a tomato sauce, sautéed mushrooms in suprême, or a crayfish butter

title: Poulets en Hatelet (Chickens on Skewers)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted chickens
- Egg
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Chives
- Breadcrumbs
- Butter or oil

## Directions

1. Take roasted chickens that have been served from the table
2. Cut them into pieces and skewer each piece on silver skewers or small wooden skewers
3. Dip them in beaten egg, season with salt, pepper, chopped parsley, chives
4. Bread them and dip in butter or oil, bread them again
5. Grill over gentle fire, lightly basting with a little oil
6. Serve dry or with a clear sauce

title: Poulets en Mayonnaise (Chickens in Mayonnaise)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two cold chickens cooked in a poêle
- Velouté
- Jelly
- Tarragon vinegar
- Coarse pepper
- One egg yolk
- Parsley
- Tarragon

## Directions

1. Cut up two cold chickens that have been cooked in a poêle as correctly as possible.
2. Put the pieces in a casserole with velouté, eight degrease-spoonfuls, four spoonfuls of jelly, and two spoonfuls of tarragon vinegar and coarse pepper.
3. Reduce everything by one-third.
4. If your sauce is somewhat brown, add a liaison of one egg.
5. When reduced to the proper point, add some finely chopped parsley and tarragon.
6. Bring two boils to your sauce.
7. Check the seasoning and put the sauce on your cold chicken pieces.
8. Let it cool.
9. Dress it on your platter.

title: Poulets Poêlés (Braised Chicken)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two chickens
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Juice of one lemon
- Lard
- Citron slices

## Directions

1. When your two chickens are plucked, singe them lightly, pluck them clean, and gut them through the cavity to avoid making a hole at the croup; remove the breast bones; put a good piece of butter in a saucepan with salt, coarse pepper and the juice of a lemon; mix this seasoning with your butter; fill your chickens with it; cut off the neck near the kidneys; truss them with a trussing needle and string that you pass from one thigh to the other, putting one leg between your string and securing it on the thigh; make the breast swell and give your chicken as much grace as possible; arrange the skin of the cavity so the butter does not escape; put lard slices in a saucepan; cut very thin slices of citron; place them on the breast of your chickens; place them in your saucepan; cover them with lard and put a lid on top to cook them; three-quarters of an hour before serving, put them on the fire; take care that they always simmer; at the moment of serving, drain them and unbind them; trim the hole near the croup; dress them on the platter; you will serve with hot aspic, thickened velouté, tomato sauce, sautéed mushrooms, a ragout of rooster crests and kidneys, etc.

title: Poulets à l'Estragon (Chicken with Tarragon)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A good pinch of tarragon leaves
- Two chickens
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Two pieces of good butter the size of a walnut
- Flour
- Two egg yolks
- Half a glass of juice
- Two spoonfuls of bouillon
- A dash of vinegar

## Directions

1. Blanch a good pinch of tarragon leaves for half a quarter hour
2. Drain in fresh water, chop finely after pressing, flame and pluck two chickens
3. Empty them and take the livers which you chop, and mix with a piece of butter, a quarter of the chopped tarragon, salt, and pepper
4. Put this small forcemeat into the body of the chickens
5. Place them in a saucepan, truss with their legs, and brown them in fat or butter
6. Put a strip of lard on the breast and cook them on the spit wrapped in paper
7. When cooked, put the remaining chopped tarragon in a saucepan with two pieces of good butter the size of a walnut, worked with a pinch of flour, two egg yolks, half a glass of juice, two spoonfuls of bouillon, and a dash of vinegar
8. Thicken the sauce without boiling, for fear the eggs turn
9. Serve over the chickens

title: Poulets à la Giblote (Chickens Giblote Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Chickens
- Giblets
- Mushrooms
- Bunch of parsley
- Chives
- One clove of garlic
- Half a bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Flour
- One glass of white wine
- Bouillon
- Juice
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the chickens into pieces and put them in a saucepan with the giblets, mushrooms, a bunch of parsley, chives, one clove of garlic, half a bay leaf, thyme, basil
2. Add a good pinch of flour
3. Moisten with one glass of white wine, bouillon, and enough juice to color the stew
4. Season with salt and coarse pepper
5. Cook and reduce to a short sauce

title: Poulets à la Jardinière (Chicken with Vegetables)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two medium chickens
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- A pinch of garlic
- Mushrooms
- Salt
- Pepper
- Breadcrumbs
- Juice
- Three spoonfuls of verjuice
- Two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Empty two medium chickens, heat the legs to pluck them
2. Cut off the spurs and push the legs into the body of the chickens and brown them on the fire, pluck them, and cut each chicken in two
3. Flatten them a little with the cleaver
4. Marinate for one hour with hot butter, parsley, chives, a pinch of garlic, mushrooms, all chopped very finely, salt, and pepper
5. Keep as much marinade on the chickens as possible and coat them with breadcrumbs
6. Grill over low heat, basting them with the remaining marinade
7. When they are cooked a beautiful color, serve with a sauce made with a little juice, three spoonfuls of verjuice, salt, pepper, a little chopped parsley, and two egg yolks
8. Thicken without boiling

title: Poulets à la Matelote (Chicken Sailor Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A dozen small white onions
- Two medium carrots
- One parsnip
- Butter
- Flour
- One glass of white wine
- Bouillon
- Bunch of parsley
- Chives
- Half a clove of garlic
- Two cloves
- One bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Salt
- Pepper
- One large chicken or two small ones
- Anchovy
- A pinch of capers

## Directions

1. Cut the head and tail from a dozen small white onions and blanch them half a quarter hour in boiling water
2. Drain in fresh water to remove the first skin
3. Cut two medium carrots and one parsnip the length of two fingers and cut them around like batons
4. Put in a saucepan a small piece of butter with two pinches of flour and brown to a cinnamon color while stirring over the fire
5. Moisten with one glass of white wine and as much bouillon
6. Add the carrots, small onions, a bunch of parsley, chives, half a clove of garlic, two cloves, one bay leaf, thyme, basil, salt, and pepper
7. Boil over low heat for half an hour
8. Flame, pluck, and empty one large chicken (or two small ones), brown it on the fire and cut it in four, put it in the ragout
9. Add, if you wish, the liver, neck, wings, and legs
10. Boil over low heat for one hour
11. When cooked, there should be little sauce remaining; degrease it and add a chopped anchovy and a pinch of capers
12. Serve hot

title: Poulets à la Monglat (Chicken Monglat)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two chickens
- Mushrooms
- Veal teat
- Béchamel sauce
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. You will cook two chickens as those made à la poêle; let them cool; remove the meat from the breast forming an oval hole the length of the chicken; cut the meat into small dice; add to it mushrooms cut the same way, the size of a cooked veal teat prepared the same way; put everything in a saucepan; pour in about four tablespoons of béchamel, a little coarse pepper that you will let melt; pour it over your small ragout that you will keep warm in a bain-marie; then heat your chickens in a saucepan with a little of their stock: at the moment of serving, drain them; dress them on your platter, and put this ragout inside your chickens: take care that it is not too liquid; put a somewhat light béchamel under your chickens; if you do not have any, use velouté.

title: Poulets à la Montmorency (Chicken Montmorency)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two fat, white chickens of equal form and without blemishes
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Grated nutmeg
- Lemon juice
- Fine lard for larding
- Lard slices
- Veal slices
- Two carrots
- Three onions
- Two cloves
- One bay leaf
- Parsley and chives bouquet
- Buttered paper round
- Grand bouillon
- Spanish sauce
- Glace

## Directions

1. Take two fat white chickens of equal form without blemishes; singe and gut them; remove the breastbone; put a good piece of butter in a saucepan with salt, coarse pepper, a little grated nutmeg, lemon juice; mix the seasoning with your butter; fill your two chickens with it; truss them as for an entrée; lard them with fine lard on the breast; put lard slices in the bottom of a saucepan; place your two chickens there, lard slices around them, veal slices to fill the gaps, two carrots, three onions, two cloves, a bay leaf, a bouquet of parsley and chives, a round of buttered paper, about one tablespoon of grand bouillon; put them on the fire three-quarters of an hour before serving, and fire also on the lid: at the moment of serving, drain them, unbind them, glaze them, and dress them on the platter; you will serve a clear Spanish sauce in which you will add about the size of half a walnut of glace; one can serve underneath an aspic, tomato sauce, sautéed truffles, a small mixed ragout, a sautéed mushrooms.

title: Poulets à la Poêle (Chicken in the Pan)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two medium chickens
- Butter
- A hint of garlic
- Two shallots
- Mushrooms
- Parsley
- Chives (ciboule)
- Flour
- One glass of white wine
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Black pepper

## Directions

1. Singe and pluck two medium chickens, split them in half down the middle of the breast, gut them and pass them in a casserole with a piece of butter, a hint of garlic, two shallots, mushrooms, parsley, chives, all chopped
2. Add a pinch of flour, moisten with a glass of white wine and as much bouillon, season with salt and black pepper, cook and reduce to short sauce; degrease before serving

title: Poulets à la Reine (Chickens in the Style of the Queen)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three small chickens à la reine
- Butter

## Directions

1. Flame the chickens
2. Empty them through the vent, truss them, and garnish them with butter
3. Cook them as you would cook braised chickens
4. At the moment of serving, drain them and unbridle them
5. Dress them on your platter with a hollandaise sauce and a large crayfish in each chicken
6. You may serve underneath a hot aspic, a tomato sauce, a crayfish butter, or a mixed ragout

title: Poulets à la Sainte-Menehould (Chickens Sainte-Menehould Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two common chickens
- Butter
- One glass of white wine
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Bunch of parsley
- Chives
- One clove of garlic
- Thyme
- Laurel
- Basil
- Two cloves
- Beaten egg
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Singe, empty and truss the legs in the body of two common chickens
2. Put them in a saucepan with a piece of butter, one glass of white wine, salt, coarse pepper, a bunch of parsley, chives, one clove of garlic, thyme, laurel, basil, two cloves
3. Cook gently and attach all the sauce around the chickens
4. Dip the chickens in beaten egg, bread with breadcrumbs, dip again in butter and bread again
5. Grill to a beautiful golden color
6. Serve dry or with a clear sauce that is somewhat piquant

title: Poupelain (Choux Pastry Cake)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three glasses of water
- a good pinch of salt
- butter the size of half an egg
- half a litre of flour
- eggs

## Directions

1. Put water, salt, and butter in a saucepan and bring to boil
2. Remove from fire and add half a litre of flour that has been thinned
3. Put back on fire, stirring constantly until the dough is thick and begins to stick to the pan
4. Transfer the dough to another pan
5. Add one egg at a time and mix in as you go
6. Continue adding eggs one by one until the dough is soft but not liquid
7. Butter a pan of the size you want to make the poupelain
8. Put your dough in to bake in the oven for an hour and a half
9. Remove from the pan and cut in half by turning the knife all around to remove the top
10. Remove all the dough inside that is not cooked
11. Fill with hot butter, rubbing with feathers or a brush
12. Sprinkle fine sugar everywhere and very finely chopped lemon zest
13. Replace the top of the poupelain on the other half
14. Rub everywhere with butter and fine sugar on top
15. Glaze with red icing

title: Purée d'Oignons Blanche (White Onion Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- onions (prepared as for the previous purée)
- four tablespoons of velouté
- one pint of cream
- a piece of sugar the size of a walnut
- flour (if no velouté available)
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare your onion as for the previous purée
2. Cook your onion over a gentle fire so it does not take color
3. When it is well melted, add four tablespoons of velouté and one pint of cream, and a piece of sugar the size of a walnut
4. Reduce your purée over high heat while stirring constantly
5. When it is thickened, pass it through cheesecloth
6. Ensure it has good seasoning
7. If you have no velouté, put one spoonful of flour, cream, salt, and pepper
8. Finish your purée as above
9. Put it in a bain-marie or over a gentle fire so it does not boil

title: Purée d'Oignons Brune (Brown Onion Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty or forty onions, depending on size
- one and a half quarter pounds of butter
- two tablespoons of espagnole sauce
- one large spoonful of broth
- flour (optional, if no sauce is available)
- a piece of sugar the size of a walnut

## Directions

1. Peel thirty or forty onions according to their size
2. Cut them in two from top to bottom
3. Remove the two ends to prevent your purée from being acrid
4. Put one and a half quarter pounds of butter in your saucepan
5. Cut your onions into slices forming half-circles
6. Put them in your butter
7. Cook your onions until they are blonde
8. Add two tablespoons of espagnole sauce and one large spoonful of broth
9. Let your purée reduce
10. When it is sufficiently thickened, pass it through cheesecloth
11. Do not let it boil anymore to prevent it from becoming acrid
12. Keep it hot in a bain-marie
13. If you have no sauce, put one spoonful of flour in when the onion is blonde, then add broth and a piece of sugar the size of a walnut
14. When your purée is reduced, pass it through cheesecloth

title: Purée d'Oseille grasse (Rich Sorrel Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sorrel, according to the quantity of purée desired
- Three or four lettuce hearts
- A handful of chervil
- A good piece of butter
- Chopped mushrooms
- Shallots
- Parsley
- Four spoonfuls of velouté, or more according to the quantity of purée
- Or one spoonful of flour if velouté is not available
- Bouillon
- A little salt
- Pepper
- Five or six egg yolks

## Directions

1. Prepare the sorrel, lettuce hearts, and chervil, cleaning them well
2. Chop them well and press them to extract the juice
3. Put a good piece of butter in your saucepan with chopped mushrooms, shallots, and parsley, and cook them in the butter
4. Put the sorrel over your fine herbs and cook
