5. When they are at their point, add four spoonfuls of velouté, or more according to the quantity of purée (see Velouté); if you do not have it, put one spoonful of flour and moisten your purée with bouillon, a little salt, and pepper
6. Make it reduce; when it is reduced enough, add five or six egg yolks
7. Pass it through a sieve and place it in a saucepan to use as needed

title: Purée d'Oseille maigre (Lean Sorrel Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Seasoned sorrel
- Six egg yolks
- Two spoonfuls of flour
- Cream, if available
- Or three glasses of milk

## Directions

1. Prepare your sorrel and cook it like the previous recipe
2. When it is well cooked in butter, have six egg yolks in which you put two spoonfuls of flour that you mix with cream, if you have it, or three glasses of milk
3. Put this mixture in your sorrel
4. Make it reduce on a fairly hot stove, stirring continually with a wooden spoon
5. When your purée is reduced enough, pass it through a sieve and keep it hot for use

title: Purée d'Écrevisses (Crayfish Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Nutmeg
- Tender breadcrumbs, three times the size of an egg

## Directions

1. Place your crayfish on a fairly hot fire so they can cook for half an hour
2. Pass your cooked crayfish through a sieve (étamine) to make a purée
3. When your purée is passed, put it in a saucepan on a gentle fire
4. Ensure it does not boil and is not too thick
5. Prepare crusts as for the previous soup and serve with the purée

title: Purée de Cardons (Purée of Cardoons)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cardoons cooked in blanc
- three degreasing spoonfuls of velouté
- six spoonfuls of consommé
- cream reduced
- a piece of glace the size of a walnut

## Directions

1. Cook your cardoons in a blanc
2. Cut them into small pieces
3. Have three degreasing spoonfuls of velouté and six spoonfuls of consommé
4. Place your cardoons with this sauce
5. Reduce it with your cardoons
6. When they are reduced to a paste, pass them through a sieve
7. Keep your purée as thick as possible
8. If it is too thick, thin it with reduced cream
9. Add a piece of glace the size of a walnut
10. Do not boil your purée; keep it hot in a bain-marie

title: Purée de Cardons en Croustade (Cardoon Purée in Croustade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- crumb from a large loaf of bread
- butter
- cardoon purée
- butter, the size of half an egg

## Directions

1. Make croûtons from the crumb of a large loaf of bread, leaving them one and a half inches thick
2. Make an incision on the edge before passing them in butter
3. Pass them in butter until well browned
4. Drain them and remove the interior crumb
5. Fill them with purée to which you add butter the size of half an egg
6. Mix the butter into the purée when it is very hot

title: Purée de Champignons (Purée of Mushrooms)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- very white mushrooms
- water
- juice of a lemon
- a piece of butter
- six spoonfuls of large velouté
- as much consommé
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Have very white mushrooms
2. Cut off the earthy part of the stem
3. Wash them
4. Put a little water in a pot and squeeze in the juice of a lemon
5. Sauté them inside, drain them, mince them as finely as possible
6. Place them in a white cloth and press them well
7. Take a piece of butter and place it in a pot
8. Pour in lemon juice
9. Add your minced mushrooms
10. Cook them until your butter turns to oil
11. Add six spoonfuls of large velouté and as much consommé
12. Reduce until your purée is thick enough
13. Add a little coarse pepper
14. Transfer your purée to another pot
15. In the absence of velouté, use a full spoon of flour and pour in bouillon

title: Purée de Haricots pour entrée (Bean Purée for an Entrée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One liter of beans
- Water
- Salt
- Butter
- A dozen onions
- Four spoonfuls of espagnole sauce
- One glass of bouillon
- Bouillon or cooking stock

## Directions

1. Cook one liter of beans in water, salt, and butter
2. Put three pounds of butter in a saucepan; clean a dozen onions, cut off the head and tail, and thin them
3. Put them with your butter and cook on a fairly hot fire
4. When your onions are nicely browned, add four spoonfuls of espagnole sauce and one glass of bouillon
5. Let your onions simmer for half an hour or three quarters of an hour
6. Add your cooked beans when your sauce is well reduced
7. Stir your beans well with your sauce
8. When the mixture is done, pass it through a sieve; if your purée is too thick, moisten it with a little consommé or bouillon
9. Once passed, do not boil it again to avoid bitterness; keep it hot in a bain-marie or on a gentle stove
10. If you do not have espagnole, put one spoonful of flour with your onions, stir it, and moisten your purée with bouillon or some cooking stock if you have it, and make your purée as explained

title: Purée de Lentilles (Purée of Lentils)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half litron of queen lentils or other varieties
- vegetables for cooking
- lard
- beef
- three or four degreasing spoonfuls of espagnole
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Have one and a half litron of queen lentils or other varieties
2. Prepare them as the peas described above
3. When they are cooked, remove the vegetables, lard and beef
4. Pass them through a sieve
5. Once you have expressed the bouillon, place your purée in a pot with three or four degreasing spoonfuls of espagnole
6. Pour more liquid into this purée than into pea purée, as it must boil for a long time so it becomes red
7. Be careful to skim and degrease it well
8. Be careful it is not too salty, because in reducing it would become acrid
9. After it is reduced, place it in another pot for serving at the moment and for the indicated uses

title: Purée de Lentilles au Maigre (Lean Lentil Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Lentils
- Lean bouillon or water
- Vegetables (as for pea purée)

## Directions

1. Make your lentil purée the same way as you would dried pea purée
2. You can add it to your soup after your rice or vermicelli is cooked

title: Purée de Légumes (Vegetable Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- six large carrots
- four large turnips
- six large onions
- three stalks of celery
- four leeks
- a piece of butter
- a piece of sugar the size of half an egg

## Directions

1. Mince the carrots, turnips, onions, celery, and leeks
2. Put a piece of butter and a piece of sugar the size of half an egg in your saucepan with your vegetables
3. Pass them blanched, that is to say do not let them take color by tossing them over the fire
4. When you see they will start to take color, moisten them with broth and let them simmer for two hours over the fire
5. Pass them through a sieve
6. Clarify your purée
7. Make sure it is not too thick
8. After, soak your bread with good broth
9. Serve the purée poured over the bread

title: Purée de Marrons (Chestnut Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Chestnuts
- Bouillon
- A quarter pound of tender bread crumb
- Coarse salt
- Butter
- Croûtes (bread slices)

## Directions

1. Cook the chestnuts as explained for the garbure
2. After cooking, reserve twenty-four whole
3. Pound the rest
4. Soak in bouillon a piece of tender bread crumb weighing a quarter pound
5. Pound this with the chestnuts
6. When everything is well crushed, dilute with hot bouillon
7. Pass through a cloth sieve
8. Put your purée on the fire, keeping it clear enough so the soup is not too thick
9. Pour it over croûtes passed in butter at the moment of serving
10. Add your twenty-four chestnuts on top
11. Ensure your soup is well seasoned with salt
12. This soup can also be made lean by using lean bouillon instead of meat bouillon

title: Purée de Perdreaux (Partridge Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- eight or ten partridges
- velouté sauce made with game essence
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Roast eight or ten partridges on a spit according to the amount of purée you need
2. Let them cool
3. When cooked, remove the meat from the breast
4. Pound them in a mortar
5. Have a velouté sauce made with game essence; put six to eight spoonfuls of defatted sauce in your mortar to make your purée liquid
6. Pass it through a sieve without putting it on the fire
7. Press it well with a wooden spoon
8. When your purée is well passed, keep it warm without boiling it
9. Add salt, pepper, and a piece of butter the size of an egg
10. Use it as you wish, either in soup, in croutons, on bread, etc.

title: Purée de Pois Secs (Dried Pea Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried peas
- Lean bouillon or water
- Three carrots
- Three onions (one studded with two cloves)
- Two celery stalks
- A quarter pound of butter
- Salt
- Parsley leaves
- Chive tops
- Sorrel (optional, for color)

## Directions

1. Wash your peas in several waters
2. Put them in a pot and moisten with lean bouillon or water
3. If using water, add three carrots, three onions (one studded with two cloves), two celery stalks, a quarter pound of butter, and salt to taste
4. Cook everything together
5. Once the peas are cooked, remove the vegetables and pass the peas through a sieve
6. For fresh peas: sauté them with a piece of butter, a handful of parsley leaves, and a bit of chive tops with salt
7. Cover the pan and let them sweat for half an hour, stirring from time to time
8. When the peas yield under your finger, pound them in a mortar and pass the purée through a sieve
9. If the purée is not green enough when added to your soup, add a spoonful of sorrel juice

title: Purée de Pois secs (Purée of Dried Peas)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one litron of dried peas
- one pound of small lard blanched
- one and a half pounds of beef slices
- two carrots
- three onions, one studded with two cloves
- bouillon
- three or four degreasing spoonfuls of velouté
- spinach green

## Directions

1. Take one litron of dried peas that you will wash
2. Place them in a small pot, adding one pound of blanched small lard, one and a half pounds of beef slices, two carrots, three onions of which one is studded with two cloves
3. Fill your pot almost entirely with bouillon
4. When your peas are cooked, strain them through a sieve
5. Dry them and remove the lard, vegetables and beef
6. Pass your peas through a sieve
7. Moisten them little by little with the bouillon in which they cooked
8. Ensure your purée is thick, as it is easier to thin than to thicken
9. Pour it into a pot
10. Add three or four degreasing spoonfuls of velouté
11. If it is too thick, add a little of the cooking liquid
12. Boil it, skim and degrease it
13. When your purée is sufficiently thickened, transfer it to another pot
14. Check that it is well seasoned and salted
15. At the moment of serving, green it with spinach green

title: Purée de Pois verts (Purée of Green Peas)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half litron of green peas
- a quarter pound of butter
- a handful of parsley leaves
- a little green from scallion tops
- salt
- half a soup spoon of consommé or bouillon
- spinach green if needed

## Directions

1. Have one and a half litron of green peas; soak them in water
2. Add a quarter pound of butter with which you will work your peas
3. Discard the water and drain your peas in a strainer
4. Place them in a pot on a fire that is not too hot
5. Add a handful of parsley leaves and a little green from scallion tops
6. Sauté your peas for a quarter hour
7. Add a little salt and half a soup spoon of consommé or bouillon
8. Boil them on a less hot fire, covering the pot with its lid
9. After your peas have been three-quarters of an hour on the fire, place them in a mortar to pound them
10. Pass them through a strainer using cold consommé or bouillon
11. Once the purée is passed, deposit it in a pot
12. If it is not green enough, add spinach green, though the purée should be green enough by itself
13. Heat it at the moment of serving so it does not turn yellow

title: Purée de racines pour entrée (Purée of Roots for an Entrée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty carrots
- seven or eight onions
- half a pound of butter
- a piece of sugar the size of a walnut
- good bouillon
- four degreasing spoonfuls of velouté

## Directions

1. When your carrots are clean, cut them into slices
2. Place half a pound of butter in a pot
3. Add seven or eight onions from which you have removed the top and bottom, cut them in four forming a circle
4. When your butter is melted, add your vegetables
5. Stir them so they do not stick
6. When you see your vegetables are slightly softened, moisten them with good bouillon
7. Add a piece of sugar the size of a walnut
8. Let your purée simmer for three hours
9. Test with two fingers if your vegetables crush easily
10. Remove your purée from the fire to place it in your sieve
11. Drain the liquid
12. Crush your vegetables and pass them through a sieve
13. Moisten it from time to time to help it pass
14. Your purée must not be too thin and must not boil for a long time if you want it not to become acrid
15. When your purée is in the pot, add four degreasing spoonfuls of velouté
16. If you have any remaining liquid from your vegetables, pour it into your purée
17. Reduce it while skimming and degreasing until it is thick enough to mask your entrées

title: Purée de Volaille (Poultry Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- three hens
- veal teat the size of an egg
- salt
- pepper
- grated nutmeg
- two skimming spoons of white jellied stock or consommé
- three spoonfuls of béchamel or velouté
- croûtons

## Directions

1. Spit roast three hens
2. When cooked and cooled, remove the whites
3. Pound them well in a mortar
4. Add veal teat the size of an egg, a little salt, pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
5. Pound again so everything mixes well together
6. Melt two skimming spoons of white jellied stock or consommé and three spoonfuls of béchamel or velouté
7. Put these five spoonfuls in a casserole over the fire and add your pounded whites that you will thin out
8. Do not let them boil
9. Pass everything through a sieve
10. When your purée is in a casserole, keep it hot in a bain-marie
11. At the moment of serving, put croûtons in it

title: Pâte Allemande (German Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- flour, divided into fourths
- leavening made from one quarter
- 1/3 butter
- 1/6 sugar
- sliced almonds
- 1/6 currants
- salt
- 1 glass of cream
- eggs

## Directions

1. Take a quarter of your flour for the leavening of the three remaining quarters
2. Put them in a terrine with 1/3 butter, 1/6 sugar, almonds sliced lengthwise, and 1/6 currants (for 3 pounds of flour: 1 pound butter, 1/2 pound sugar, etc.)
3. Salt your dough as you would brioche dough
4. Warm the butter over a quarter of flour
5. Pour a glass of cream and the rest of the liquid (eggs) over it
6. Dissolve everything together
7. When your dough is soft, though somewhat thick, add the leavening
8. Mix it well with the dough
9. Butter a casserole or mold and let the dough rise in a warm place like baba, for 5 or 6 hours
10. When well risen, put it in the oven at the same heat level as for baba

title: Pâte Appelée Feuilletage (Puff Pastry)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- un litron de farine
- sel
- eau
- beurre (presque autant que de pâte)

## Directions

1. Mettez le litron de farine sur la table avec un peu de sel et d'eau
2. Pétrissez un moment la farine avec l'eau, que cette pâte ne soit ni trop molle ni trop épaisse
3. Laissez-la reposer deux heures avant de vous en servir
4. Prenez ensuite presque autant de beurre que de pâte
5. Abattez la pâte avec le rouleau, mettez le beurre dans le milieu, et donnez cinq tours en été et six en hiver
6. Pour chaque tour, abattez la pâte avec le rouleau, jusqu'à ce qu'elle soit de l'épaisseur d'un demi-doigt, et jetez de tems en tems et légèrement peu de farine
7. Quand chaque tour est fini, repliez la pâte en trois, et recommencez chaque tour jusqu'à définition

title: Pâte Brisée (Broken Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of flour
- one ounce of fine salt
- two pounds of butter
- six whole eggs
- three glasses of water

## Directions

1. Prepare a quarter of flour on your pastry board
2. Make a hollow in the middle
3. Put one ounce of fine salt, two pounds of butter, six whole eggs, and three glasses of water
4. Work your butter with the eggs, salt and water
5. Mix your flour with the butter
6. Assemble your dough without kneading and give it four turns as explained in the puff pastry
7. This dough is used to make plumb cakes and other bases

title: Pâte Brisée pour les Pâtés Froids (Shortcrust Pastry for Cold Patés)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- un demi-boisseau de farine
- deux livres de beurre
- un demi-quarteron de sel
- eau presque bouillante

## Directions

1. Mettez la farine sur la table et faites un trou dans le milieu pour y mettre le sel fin et le beurre
2. Prenez de l'eau presque bouillante que vous mettez sur le beurre, et le maniez avec les mains dans cette eau jusqu'à ce qu'il soit tout-à-fait fondu
3. Mêlez ensuite la farine et pétrissez à tour de bras le plus promptement que vous pouvez, jusqu'à ce qu'elle soit bien liée
4. Laissez reposer cette pâte pendant trois heures avant de vous en servir
5. Dressez avec tel pâté de viande que vous jugerez à propos

title: Pâte Brisée pour les Tourtes (Shortcrust Pastry for Pies)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of flour
- five quarterons of good butter
- approximately one ounce of salt
- water

## Directions

1. Put your flour on a very clean table
2. Make a hole in the middle to put the salt
3. Add the butter in small pieces
4. Add water carefully, as too much will make the dough too loose and without structure
5. Mix the butter well with the water, and gradually with the flour
6. When the flour has absorbed all the water, knead with force of arm; your dough cannot be too thick, provided it is well-bound and has no lumps in it
7. Make this dough at least two hours before using it, so it has time to rest

title: Pâte d'Amandes pour faire de l'Orgeat (Almond Paste for Orgeat)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of sweet almonds
- ½ pound of sugar, pounded
- Water

## Directions

1. Blanch the almonds by soaking them briefly in hot water, then remove their skins
2. Pound them well, adding a little water from time to time so they do not turn to oil
3. When well pounded, add ½ pound of pounded sugar
4. Make a paste of all of it for use when you see fit
5. This paste keeps for six months and nearly a year
6. When you wish to use it, take a piece as large as an egg and dissolve it in three half-septiers of water, then pass it through a cloth

title: Pâte à Baba (Baba Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- leavening made as for brioche dough
- 1 pound of raisins in a box
- 1/4 pound of currants
- 1 teaspoon of saffron powder

## Directions

1. Make the leavening as for brioche dough
2. In the dough mixture, add 1 pound of raisins in a box, 1/4 pound of currants, and 1 teaspoon of saffron powder for a quarter batch
3. Follow the same process as for brioche dough, but keep the dough much softer
4. When the dough is finished, butter a large casserole and put your dough in it
5. Place it in a warm location and let it rise for 6 hours
6. Your dough will be very swollen
7. Put it in a hot oven as for brioche

title: Pâte à Biscuit (Biscuit Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fifteen eggs
- One pound of fine powdered sugar
- A little orange flower water or a little finely chopped lemon zest, or such seasoning as you judge fit
- One pound of flour for large biscuits, or three-quarters pound for small biscuits
- Powdered sugar for dusting

## Directions

1. Break the fifteen eggs; put the whites in one bowl and the yolks in another
2. With the yolks employ one pound of fine powdered sugar
3. Add a little orange flower water or a little finely chopped lemon zest, or such seasoning as you judge convenient
4. Beat your yolks and sugar well with a spatula or wooden spoon
5. When the yolks are somewhat lightened, beat the whites with a willow whisk
6. As soon as they are firm and hold upright, join your yolks to them
7. If for a large biscuit, put one pound of flour; if for small biscuits, use only three-quarters pound
8. Mix it lightly with the yolks and whites
9. Your dough well mixed, arrange it in a buttered mold or in your pans
10. Dust the exterior with powdered sugar
11. Place your biscuit in a gentle oven
12. You can also spoon it into small molds

title: Pâte à Biscuit (Biscuit Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 eggs
- 1 pound of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Break 15 eggs, putting the whites in one bowl and the yolks in another
2. With the yolks use 1 pound of powdered sugar

title: Pâte à Brioche (Brioche Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 liter of flour, divided into 4 parts
- 1 ounce of leavening
- warm water
- 1 ounce of salt
- 2 pounds of butter
- 12 eggs

## Directions

1. Take a quarter of your flour and make a well large enough to contain your water
2. Put 1 ounce of leavening in the well and dilute it with warm water
3. Assemble your dough and place it in a clean vessel
4. Place it in a warm location
5. As soon as you notice your leaven has swollen by half, make a hole in the remaining three liters of flour
6. Put 1 ounce of salt dissolved in a little water, 2 pounds of butter, and 12 eggs into the hole
7. Work the butter with the eggs and assemble your dough
8. If it is too firm, add more eggs until the dough is somewhat soft
9. When it reaches the right consistency, fold it twice and add your leaven
10. Separate your dough with your fingers by working it in your hands, changing its place by cutting it two or three times
11. Place flour in a white cloth and put your dough in it, covering it well
12. Let it rise for 10 to 12 hours
13. Shape it by flattening, folding and refolding it on a floured surface, giving it a round form
14. Press in the middle and place another small round piece of brioche dough on top to form the entire brioche
15. Place on buttered paper or in a buttered mold or casserole
16. Give various shapes to make different small cakes
17. Bake in a warm oven

title: Pâte à Choux (Choux Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint of water
- More than one quarter-pound of butter
- One lemon zest
- Two ounces of sugar
- A little salt
- Flour
- Icing, or almonds and sugar, or pistachios
- Preserves

## Directions

1. For one pint of water in a saucepan, put more than one quarter-pound of butter, one lemon zest, two ounces of sugar, and a little salt
2. When the water is ready to boil, add the flour and work the dough as with the pouplin dough
3. Keep it somewhat firmer so it is more manageable
4. Give this dough whatever shape you wish
5. Glaze it, or put almonds and sugar on top, or pistachios; if there is nothing on top, put preserves inside

title: Pâte à Dresser (Dresser's Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- two litrons of flour
- half an ounce of salt
- three quarterons of butter
- six egg yolks
- one glass of water

## Directions

1. Put two litrons of flour on a table
2. Make a hollow in the middle
3. Add half an ounce of salt, three quarterons of butter, six egg yolks, and one glass of water
4. Work the butter with the water, eggs and salt, ensuring the butter is well softened
5. Mix your flour little by little and combine everything together
6. When your dough is assembled, knead it with your fists until well kneaded
7. If there is not enough moisture, add more
8. Once you have kneaded twice, that is enough, as more kneading risks burning it, especially in summer, and the dough would no longer hold together and would break when working it
9. For larger quantities, for every quarter use one and a half pounds of butter, one ounce of salt, and ten egg yolks for half a bushel, three pounds of butter for a bushel, and so on
10. Your dough should be quite firm so as not to be caught off guard
11. Add one glass of water in several additions
12. If you use this dough for meat pies, make it softer

title: Pâte à Frire (Frying Batter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one litron of flour
- six egg yolks
- two tablespoons of oil
- one piece of butter the size of an egg
- salt
- pepper
- one glass of beer
- two egg whites

## Directions

1. Put one litron of flour in a bowl
2. Add six egg yolks, two tablespoons of oil, and one piece of butter the size of an egg that you will warm so it mixes with the batter
3. Add salt, pepper, and one glass of beer
4. Dilute your batter so there are no lumps
5. Add more butter if your batter is too thick, but take care that it is not too thin
6. It should run from the spoon when you let it fall
7. Beat two egg whites as for biscuit and mix them with your batter
8. Use it as needed
9. You can moisten your batter with white wine or milk

title: Pâte à la Duchesse (Duchess Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint of cream
- One spoonful of orange flower water
- Two ounces of sugar
- One quarter-pound of butter
- A little salt
- Flour
- Eggs
- Icing

## Directions

1. Pour one pint of cream into a saucepan with one spoonful of orange flower water, two ounces of sugar, one quarter-pound of butter, and a little salt
2. When the cream begins to boil, add flour as described for the pouptin dough
3. Work it the same way, that is to say add eggs little by little while always kneading the dough with a wooden spoon
4. Keep it as firm as the choux dough
5. Give it whatever shape you wish by putting flour on the pastry board and rolling it to make small duchess breads
6. You can spoon them onto a baking sheet
7. Cook them after the puff pastry and glaze them

title: Pâte à la Madeleine (Madeleine Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of flour
- 1 pound of sugar
- 1/2 pound of butter
- orange blossom water or lemon zest, finely chopped
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Put 1 pound of flour, 1 pound of sugar, and 1/2 pound of butter (warmed) in a casserole
2. Add a little orange blossom water or a little finely chopped lemon zest
3. Add 6 eggs
4. Mix everything together
5. Add more eggs if your dough is too thick
6. Butter a mold or several small ones and arrange the dough in it
7. Bake in a gentle oven

title: Pâte à la Turque (Turkish Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound of blanched almonds
- 1 pound of flour
- 1/2 pound of butter
- 3/4 pound of powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon of saffron powder
- eggs

## Directions

1. Pound 1/2 pound of blanched almonds well until very fine
2. Use 1 pound of flour, 1/2 pound of butter, 3/4 pound of powdered sugar, and 1 teaspoon of saffron powder
3. Pound everything together
4. Add eggs gradually until your mixture is soft
5. Butter a surface and arrange it on top
6. Give it an even thickness and let the dough bake in a gentle oven
7. Cut it with a knife or pastry cutter
8. When you remove it from the oven, give it whatever shape you wish
9. You can substitute pistachios for almonds if you prefer

title: Pâte à Pouptin (Pouptin Dough)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint of water
- One half-quarter pound of butter
- One lemon zest
- A little salt
- One liter of flour, sifted through silk
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan one pint of water, one half-quarter pound of butter, one lemon zest, and a little salt
2. Place the saucepan over the fire
3. When the mixture is ready to boil, sift one liter of flour through silk and put into your saucepan as much as the water can absorb
4. When the dough is very thick, cook it by stirring constantly with a wooden spoon
5. Let it cool
6. Then break an egg into it and mix it with the dough
7. Keep adding eggs until your dough is soft
8. Butter a large saucepan to contain the dough
9. Put it to cook in an oven hotter than for biscuit
10. Your pouptin must be somewhat dry
11. When it reaches one-quarter of the saucepan height, it will be full when your dough is cooked
12. Remove it from the vessel and spread some preserve inside, coating the interior

title: Pâté de Lièvre à la Bourgeoise (Hare Pâté Bourgeoise Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare
- Hare blood
- Large lardons
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley
- Chives
- Garlic
- Half a glass of brandy
- Piece of butter

## Directions

1. Skin the hare and keep its blood; after emptying it, cut it into pieces and lard throughout with large lardons rolled in salt, pepper, chopped parsley, chives, and garlic.
2. Place it in a small pot with half a glass of brandy and a piece of butter; cook over a low fire.
3. When it is cooked and there is almost no sauce, add the blood, heat it without letting it boil.
4. Arrange the hare in the serving dish; serve everything together so that it appears to be a single piece.
5. Serve this pâté cold as an entremets.

title: Pâtés de Viande (Meat Patés)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- rouelle de veau, gigot de mouton, perdrix, bécasses, filets de lièvres, poulardes, chapons, ou dindons (au choix)
- gros lard
- sel fin
- fines épices
- persil et ciboule hachés
- bardes de lard
- beurre
- œuf battu (blanc et jaune)
- eau-de-vie
- pâte brisée

## Directions

1. Après avoir vidé les perdrix, bécasses, chapons, poulardes, troussez-leur les pattes dans le corps, et cassez-leur un peu les os avec le dos du couperet
2. Faites-les revenir sur de la braise
3. Après les avoir essuyés et épluchés, lardez-les partout avec du gros lard manié dans le sel fin, fines épices mêlées, persil et ciboule hachés
4. Pour le veau et le mouton, faites la même chose à la réserve que vous ne les faites point revenir sur de la braise
5. Quand votre viande est bien préparée, coupez des bardes de lard suffisamment pour couvrir toute votre viande
6. Prenez la moitié de la pâte, que vous arrondissez avec les mains en la roulant sur la table
7. Battez ensuite la pâte avec le rouleau, jusqu'à ce qu'elle soit de l'épaisseur d'un demi-doigt
8. Mettez cette pâte sur une feuille de papier beurré, et dessus la pâte votre viande bien serrée l'une contre l'autre, et l'assaisonnez de sel fin et fines épices
9. Couvrez de bardes de lard et beaucoup de beurre par-dessus
10. Mettez ensuite une abaisse de pâte aussi épaisse que celle de dessous
11. Mouillez avec un doroir les deux endroits qui doivent se toucher, pour qu'ils se collent tous ensemble
12. Appuyez partout avec les doigts pour les unir
13. Reprenez après le doroir, que vous trempez dans l'eau pour mouiller dessus du pâté
14. Relevez ensuite la pâte qui déborde pour la faire monter le long du pâté
15. Unissez-la promptement sans trop appuyer, crainte de percer la peau
16. Faites un trou sur le milieu au-dessus, de la largeur du pouce
17. Faites une cheminée de pâte, où vous mettrez une carte roulée, de crainte que le trou ne se referme en cuisant
18. Dorez partout le pâté avec un œuf battu, blanc et jaune
19. Mettez des bonnets de la liberté faits avec de la même pâte, et redorez une seconde fois, un moment avant que de le mettre au four
20. Mettez par la cheminée du pâté deux cuillerées d'eau-de-vie
21. Faites-le cuire au four au moins quatre heures
22. Quand il sera cuit, mettez-le dans un endroit frais pour le faire refroidir, et bouchez sa cheminée avec un morceau de pâte crue

title: Quartier d'Agneau de Derrière (Hind Quarter of Lamb)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Hind quarter of lamb
- Small atelets
- Lard
- Trussing needle
- Twine

## Directions

1. Cut your quarter at two cutlets near the saddle as squarely as possible
2. Put small atelets to hold the flanks
3. Bard them and pass the spit from the end of the handle to the end of the fillet without piercing it
4. Secure it with a trussing needle and twine on the spit
5. Allow one hour for your quarter to cook

title: Quartier d'Agneau de Devant (Front Quarter of Lamb)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Front quarter of lamb
- Lard
- Large atelet (skewer)
- Oiled paper

## Directions

1. Bard your front quarter of lamb
2. Spit it with a large atelet and place on the spit
3. Cover with oiled paper
4. Half an hour before removing from the spit, remove the paper
5. Give it a fine color

title: Quartier de Chevreuil (Haunch of Venison)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- venison loin and leg
- lard fin (fine lard)
- marinade

## Directions

1. Bone your venison loin and leg
2. Lard it with fine lard
3. Place it in marinade like venison loins; it can remain eight days in the marinade
4. When you wish to serve it, remove it from the marinade and put it on the spit
5. Two hours suffice to cook it
6. At the moment of serving, remove the skewers and strings
7. Prepare the handle by wrapping it in a piece of paper
8. Place it on a roasting platter
9. Serve a sauceboat with a piquant sauce

title: Quartier de Chevreuil (Venison Quarter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison quarter
- Lard
- Vinegar
- Salt
- Fine pepper
- Seven or eight bay leaves
- Thyme
- Onion slices
- Parsley in branches
- Whole chives
- Piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Lard the venison
2. Place it in a terrine
3. Pour in a bottle of vinegar, salt, fine pepper, seven or eight bay leaves, thyme, onion slices, parsley in branches, and whole chives
4. Leave it five or six days if you can
5. Spit it as you would a leg of mutton
6. Pour a piquant sauce underneath or in a sauce boat
7. It can be served as a relevé

title: Quartier de Mouton (Lamb Quarter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb
- Skewers
- String or needle for trussing
- Clear jus

## Directions

1. Cut at the second cutlet near the saddle, as square as possible
2. Secure the flanks with skewers
3. Remove the thick muscle bone
4. Beat your leg
5. Pass the spit near the handle down to the ribs with a trussing needle
6. Fix your piece of rack on the spit
7. If you serve it as a relief course, put clear jus underneath

title: Quartier de Mouton (Leg of Mutton)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Leg of mutton with bones
- Atelets (skewers)
- Twine
- Clear stock

## Directions

1. Cut at the second cutlet near the saddle, as squarely as possible
2. Secure the flanks with atelets
3. Remove the chine bone
4. Beat your leg of mutton
5. Pass the spit near the handle through to the ribs, using a trussing needle
6. Fix your piece of square cut onto the spit
7. If serving as a relevé, place clear stock underneath

title: Quartier de Sanglier (Wild Boar Quarter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild boar quarter
- Piquant sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the wild boar
2. Marinate it for five or six days as you would venison
3. Place it on the spit
4. Serve a piquant sauce underneath or in a sauce boat
5. It can be served as a relevé

title: Quasi de Veau à la Crème (Veal Cushion in Cream Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one pint of milk
- a good piece of butter mixed with flour
- two cloves of garlic
- four shallots
- parsley
- chives
- endive
- one bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- four cloves
- two onions in slices
- salt
- pepper
- coulis
- flour for the sauce

## Directions

1. Place in a vessel just the size of the quasi, a pint of milk with a good piece of butter mixed with flour, two cloves of garlic, four shallots, parsley, chives, endive, one bay leaf, thyme, basil, four cloves, two onions in slices, salt, and pepper.
2. Heat this marinade and simmer it on the fire until the butter is melted.
3. Remove from the fire and place the quasi in it, letting it take on flavor for twelve hours.
4. Then drain it and dry it well.
5. Cover it with well-oiled paper and roast it on the spit.
6. Serve it with a piquant sauce made as follows: pass on the fire two onions in slices with a piece of butter; when well colored, add a pinch of flour, moisten with coulis, salt, and pepper.
7. Boil for a quarter hour, degrease the sauce and pass it through a sieve.
8. Serve the sauce under the quasi.

title: Quenelles de Carpe (Carp Quenelles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Carp meat
- Anchovy

## Directions

1. Remove the flesh from the carp
2. Prepare it and use the same procedure as for poultry quenelles, except add one or two anchovies to the fish meat

title: Quenelles de Filets de Merlans (Whiting Fillet Quenelles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Whiting fillets
- Two or three anchovies

## Directions

1. Use the same procedure as for making chicken quenelles, except add two or three anchovies to your whiting flesh
2. Use the same proportions

title: Quenelles de Lapin à l'Essence de Gibier (Rabbit Quenelles with Game Essence)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbit forcemeat
- Butter
- Bread
- Meat
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Nutmeg
- Aromatics
- Eggs
- Game forcemeat or rabbit scraps
- Espagnole sauce
- White wine

## Directions

1. Fill a tablespoon with forcemeat and dip your knife in boiling water; smooth the forcemeat with the knife blade giving it a rounded shape
2. With another spoon also dipped in boiling water, remove the forcemeat from the first spoon and place it successively into a large buttered casserole
3. Prepare as many quenelles as needed
4. When the bottom of your casserole is full, place a round of buttered paper over your quenelles
5. Three quarters of an hour before serving, gently pour boiling salted water into your casserole
6. Let it boil very gently so your quenelles poach but do not burst
7. At the moment of serving, drain them on white linen
8. Prepare the sauce: put four tablespoons of degreased espagnole in a casserole, six tablespoons of game forcemeat, and half a glass of white wine
9. Reduce everything to one third, skim well, and pass through a sieve
10. Sauce and coat your quenelles with this preparation

title: Quenelles de pommes de terre (Potato Quenelles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- nutmeg
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- egg yolks
- egg whites
- bouillon
- espagnole sauce

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes in red ashes
2. Remove everything hard from the potato and take only the floury part
3. Mix this part in a mortar and pound it well
4. Pass it through a quenelle strainer
5. Make a pile and add half butter, pounding it with the potatoes
6. When well pounded, grate in a little nutmeg, add salt, coarse pepper, parsley, and well-chopped chives
7. Add five or six egg yolks, or more according to quantity
8. Beat two or three egg whites and fold them with the mixture
9. Poach them in bouillon as with poultry quenelles
10. Drain the quenelles, arrange on the platter, and pour espagnole sauce over them; alternatively use tomato sauce, portugaise sauce, béchamel or reduced velouté

title: Queue de Bœuf aux Champignons (Braised Beef Tail with Mushrooms)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tail
- Mushrooms
- Butter, the size of an egg
- Flour, one spoonful
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. When your beef tail is cooked in a braise, arrange it on a platter
2. Mask it with a mushroom ragout (see Mushrooms for an Entrée)
3. If you lack sauce, sauté your mushrooms in a piece of butter the size of an egg
4. When you see your butter turning to oil, add a spoonful of flour
5. Mix it with your butter and mushrooms
6. Moisten them with the cooking liquid in which your beef tail cooked
7. Make sure your ragout is well seasoned with salt
8. Add a little pepper

title: Queue de Bœuf aux Choux (Oxtail with Cabbage)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- oxtail
- braised cabbage
- lard
- large carrots
- espagnole sauce
- salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the oxtail as in the previous recipe
2. Arrange braised cabbage around it as an entrée with a piece of lard that has cooked with the cabbage and large carrots
3. Surround your sauce with espagnole sauce
4. Alternatively, you may cook the cabbage and lard with the oxtail and some large carrots
5. Salt the cabbage well, blanch it, and tie it before adding it to the cooking pot so you can remove it whole
6. Pour a bound sauce over the oxtail or serve it with some of the cooking liquid

title: Queue de Bœuf aux Navets (Braised Beef Tail with Turnips)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tail
- Turnips
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook your braised beef tail
2. Have turnips cut into small sticks (see Small Turnips)
3. Pour the turnip sticks over your beef tail so it takes on the taste of turnip
4. Put the surface of your small turnip sticks into the cooking liquid
5. If you do not have sauce for your turnips, use the cooking liquid from your beef tail to cook your turnips

title: Queue de Bœuf aux Navets (Oxtail with Turnips)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- braised oxtail
- turnips
- cooking liquid

## Directions

1. Cook the oxtail braised
2. Have turnips cut into small batons
3. Pour the turnips over the oxtail so it acquires the taste of turnip
4. Put the trimmings of the turnip batons in the cooking liquid
5. If you have no sauce for the turnips, use the cooking liquid to cook them

title: Queue de Bœuf aux Oignons Glacés (Braised Beef Tail with Glazed Onions)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tail
- Glazed onions
- Espagnole sauce
- Roux
- Cooking liquid from beef tail
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cook your beef tail in a braise
2. Drain it, arrange it on your platter in a pyramid shape, and arrange glazed onions around it (see Glazed Onions)
3. Add a prepared espagnole sauce
4. If you lack espagnole, make a light roux and moisten it with the cooking liquid, passed through a silk sieve, in which your beef tail cooked
5. Make your sauce clear so you can reduce it
6. Season it with good flavor and salt
7. Add a little pepper, and sauce your beef tail with it
8. Take care to degrease it

title: Queue de Bœuf Braisée en Hochepot (Braised Oxtail Hochepot)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- oxtail
- lard
- veal or beef pieces
- three or four carrots
- four or five onions
- three cloves
- thyme
- two bay leaves
- bouillon
- lettuces
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the oxtail into pieces, joint by joint
2. Soak in water for two hours
3. Blanch for half an hour
4. Place in cold water and drain
5. Trim the pieces
6. Line the bottom of a casserole with lard and a few pieces of veal or beef
7. Cover similarly with lard
8. Add three or four carrots, four or five onions (one studded with three cloves), a little thyme, and two bay leaves
9. Moisten with bouillon and bring to a boil
10. Move to a gentle fire and cook slowly for two or three hours, depending on how tough the oxtail is
11. When cooked, place the pieces in the middle of the plate
12. Arrange a ring of braised lettuces around the edge
13. Arrange small carrot batons over the oxtail to cover it and glaze the lettuces
14. Alternatively, cook the oxtail in a casserole with water and seasoning as above, with salt and carrot trimmings that have been scraped before turning; you can also moisten your small vegetables with the liquid in which the oxtail has cooked

title: Queue de Bœuf en Hochepot (Oxtail Stew)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Oxtail, cut into pieces
- Good bouillon
- Bouquet garni
- Salt
- Onions
- Carrots
- Parsnips
- Turnips
- Cabbage
- Coulis

## Directions

1. Cut the oxtail into pieces and blanch it
2. Cook with good bouillon, a bouquet garni, and little salt for five hours
3. At the midway point of cooking, add onions, carrots, parsnips, turnips, and a bit of cabbage, all blanched and cut neatly
4. When everything is cooked, remove it onto a cloth and wipe it so no grease remains
5. Arrange the vegetables with the meat in a proper terrine for serving at table
6. Decrease the sauce in which the meat cooked, add a bit of coulis, and reduce over the fire if the sauce is too thin
7. Take care that there is not too much salt, pass it through a sieve, and pour it over the meat and vegetables
8. You may also serve the oxtail the same way using only one type of vegetable at a time, or without vegetables and with different sauces instead

title: Queue de Bœuf en Matelotte (Oxtail in Red Wine Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Oxtail, cut into pieces
- Butter
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Onions, whole
- White wine
- Parsley
- Chives
- Garlic
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Cloves
- Salt
- Pepper
- Anchovy
- Capers
- Bread
- Butter for frying

## Directions

1. Cut the oxtail into pieces and blanch it in boiling water
2. Remove it to fresh water, then cook it halfway in bouillon with no seasoning
3. When half cooked, make a roux with a bit of butter and a spoonful of flour
4. Moisten this roux with the bouillon in which you cooked the oxtail
5. Add the pieces of oxtail with a dozen large whole onions that you have blanched beforehand to remove their outer skin
6. Add a half-setier of white wine, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a clove of garlic, a bay leaf, a bit of thyme, basil, two cloves, salt, and pepper
7. Cook over a low fire until the oxtail and onions are cooked
8. Take care to decrease well
9. Add a minced anchovy and two pinches of whole capers to the sauce
10. Arrange the pieces of oxtail in the middle of the platter, the onions around, and on top place seven or eight pieces of bread cut to the size of a small coin, which you will pass through butter
11. When ready to serve, pour over everything the sauce, which should be thick

title: Queue de Bœuf à la Purée d'Oignons (Braised Beef Tail with Onion Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tail
- Onion purée

## Directions

1. Braise your beef tail
2. Drain it, arrange it on the platter, and mask it with an onion purée (see Onion Purée for an Entrée)

title: Queue de Bœuf à la Purée de Lentilles (Braised Beef Tail with Lentil Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tail
- Lentil purée

## Directions

1. Braise your beef tail
2. When it is cooked, drain it and arrange it on your platter
3. Mask it with a lentil purée (see Lentil Purée for an Entrée)

title: Queue de Bœuf à la Purée de Pois Verts (Braised Beef Tail with Green Pea Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tail
- Green pea purée

## Directions

1. Braise your beef tail
2. When it is cooked, drain it, arrange it on the platter, and mask it with a green pea purée (see Green Peas; see Green Pea Purée for an Entrée)

title: Queue de Bœuf à la Purée de Racines (Braised Beef Tail with Root Vegetable Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tail
- Root vegetable purée

## Directions

1. When your beef tail is cooked in a braise, drain it and arrange it on your platter, and mask it with a root vegetable purée (see Root Vegetable Purée for an Entrée)

title: Queue de Bœuf à la Sainte-Menehould (Oxtail Sainte-Menehould)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked oxtail
- salt
- coarse pepper
- warm butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. When the oxtail is cooked as in the hochepot recipe, season it with a little salt and coarse pepper
2. Dip it in warm butter and roll it in breadcrumbs
3. Bread it twice
4. Brown it in the oven or under the grill

title: Queue de Bœuf à la Sainte-Ménehould (Oxtail Sainte-Ménehould Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Oxtail
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley, minced
- Chives, minced
- Shallots, minced
- Garlic, minced
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the oxtail into three pieces: cut it first in the middle, and split the thick end in two with the cleaver
2. Cook it in the pot like a piece of beef
3. When cooked, let it cool
4. Marinate it for one hour with a bit of oil, salt, coarse pepper, minced parsley, chives, two minced shallots, and a pinch of minced garlic
5. Make the marinade adhere to the oxtail by coating it with breadcrumbs
6. Grill it to a nice color, basting it with the rest of its marinade while it is on the fire
7. Serve without sauce

title: Queue de Bœuf à la Sauce Tomate (Braised Beef Tail with Tomato Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef tail
- Tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Braise your beef tail
2. Arrange it on a platter
3. Mask it with tomato sauce (see Indian Tomato Sauce)

title: Queue de Morue Farcie (Stuffed Cod Tail)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two large handfuls of breadcrumbs
- chopine of milk
- butter
- parsley
- chives
- salt
- pepper
- six egg yolks
- cod tail
- mushrooms
- shallots
- garlic
- flour
- milk
- three egg yolks
- buttered paper
- beaten egg
- breadcrumbs
- bouillon
- beurre manié
- verjus

## Directions

1. In a casserole, put breadcrumbs with a chopine of milk and bring to a boil
2. Dry on the fire until the breadcrumbs are very thick
3. Let cool
4. Add butter the size of an egg, chopped parsley, chives, salt, pepper, and six egg yolks
5. Cook the cod tail in water as previously described
6. Drain it and remove all the flesh, leaving only the bone
7. Separate into fillets
8. In a casserole, put butter the size of an egg with mushrooms cut into fillets, parsley, chives, two shallots, and half a clove of garlic, all chopped
9. Pass on the fire and add half a spoonful of flour
10. Moisten with half a setier of milk, add coarse pepper
11. Boil until the sauce is thick
12. Add the cod with three egg yolks and thicken on the fire without boiling
13. Let cool
14. On the serving dish, place the bone of the tail and wrap the small end of the tail in buttered paper
15. Make a border around all sides of the bone with some of the stuffing
16. Put the cod ragout in the middle
17. Cover over with the remaining stuffing so that the ragout is not visible and forms a cod tail
18. Smooth everywhere with a knife
19. Dip in beaten egg and coat with breadcrumbs
20. Cook and brown in the oven or under a country stove cover
21. When cooked to a nice color, serve with clean dish edges
22. You can put at the bottom a sauce made with a glass of good bouillon, butter the size of a walnut worked with flour, a spoonful of verjus, little salt, and coarse pepper, thickened on the fire

title: Queue de Mouton au Riz (Sheep's Tail with Rice)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's tails, five beautiful ones
- bouillon
- bouquet of parsley
- chives
- cloves, two
- garlic, half a clove
- bay leaf, half a leaf
- thyme
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- rice, about six ounces

## Directions

1. Put the tails to cook with bouillon, a bouquet of parsley, chives, two cloves, half a clove of garlic, half a bay leaf, thyme, basil, salt, pepper
2. Cook them on a small fire
3. Remove them from their braise to let them drain and cool
4. Take about six ounces of well-cleaned rice, wash it several times in warm water rubbing with your hands
5. Put it in a small pot with the bouillon that served to cook the tails, passed through a sieve without degreasing it, and if there was not enough, you add more

title: Queue de Mouton de Plusieurs Façons (Sheep's Tail in Several Ways)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- sheep's tails, five or six
- bouillon
- onions, two
- roots, two
- bouquet of fine herbs
- salt
- pepper
- eggs, two
- bread crumbs
- fine oil or fat from the back of the pot
- sauce à l'échalotte

## Directions

1. Cook the tails in a small braise composed of bouillon, two onions and two roots, a bouquet of fine herbs, salt, pepper for three or four hours
2. When cooked this way, they can serve you for different preparations
3. If you want to put them on the grill when they are cold, dip them in two eggs beaten as for an omelette, bread them with bread crumbs
4. When all are breaded, dip them after in fine oil or fat from the back of the pot that is warm
5. Bread them a second time and put them to grill on a small fire
6. Be careful to baste them on the grill with the rest of the oil or fat
7. When grilled to a beautiful color, serve them dry or with a little sauce à l'échalotte
8. If it is to serve them fried, when they are cooked and cooled, bread them simply in beaten eggs, dip them in bread crumbs, and fry them to a beautiful color; serve them with fried parsley
9. When cooked à la braise, they are served with a coulis of lentils and small bacon, or a ragout of cabbage and small bacon

title: Queues de Cochon à la Purée (Pork Tails with Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- six pork tails
- lentils
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- bay leaf
- bouillon or water
- salt

## Directions

1. You will have six pork tails to which you leave their rind
2. Cut them eight inches long from the thickest end
3. Clean them and singe them
4. Cook them with your lentils in which you put two carrots, two onions, two cloves, one bay leaf
5. Moisten your lentils with bouillon or water
6. Then add salt
7. When your tails are cooked, put them in a casserole with a little bouillon
8. Pass your lentils through a sieve
9. Put your purée in a casserole
10. Reduce it if it is too thin
11. At the moment of serving, arrange your pork tails on the plate
12. Cover them with your purée

title: Queues de Veau aux Choux et au Petit Lard (Veal Tails with Cabbage and Bacon)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two veal tails
- half a pound of bacon cut in slices
- half a large cabbage cut in four pieces
- a bouquet of parsley
- chives
- half a clove of garlic
- three cloves
- a small piece of nutmeg
- bouillon
- salt
- coarse pepper
- coulis or cooking liquid from the cabbage
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut the two veal tails in two and blanch them briefly with the bacon slices
2. Blanch the cabbage quarters separately for a quarter hour, then refresh in cold water and press well
3. Remove the cores and tie the cabbage pieces
4. Place the veal tails in a small pot with the tied bacon and cabbage, add the bouquet of parsley and chives, half a clove of garlic, three cloves, and a small piece of nutmeg
5. Moisten with bouillon, add a little salt and coarse pepper
6. Boil on a low fire until the veal tails are cooked
7. Remove everything from the pot to drain and wipe off the fat
8. Arrange the veal tails interspersed with cabbage, with the bacon on top
9. Make a sauce by heating coulis (or the strained, defatted cooking liquid from the cabbage) in a pan with a little butter and coarse pepper, thickened with flour if needed
10. Pour the sauce over the cabbage and meat and serve

title: Queues de Veau à la Sainte-Menehould (Veal Tails Prepared Sainte-Menehould Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- three veal tails
- rich bouillon
- parsley
- ciboule (chives)
- one clove of garlic
- three cloves
- two shallots
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- one onion
- one carrot
- one parsnip
- three egg yolks
- a good pinch of flour

## Directions

1. Take three veal tails that you cut in two
2. Blanch them briefly in boiling water
3. Place them in a small pot with rich bouillon, a bouquet of parsley, ciboule, one clove of garlic, three cloves, two shallots, a bay leaf, thyme, basil, salt, pepper, one onion, one carrot, one parsnip
4. Boil until they are cooked and very little sauce remains
5. Remove them to cool
6. Pass the sauce through a fine sieve so that the fat passes through; there should remain about half a glass
7. Put it in a pan with three egg yolks diluted with a good pinch of flour
8. Make it bind over the fire so that it is a bit thick
9. Then dip the veal tails in it

title: Racines en Menus Droits (Roots in Fine Strips)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- onion
- butter
- flour
- bouillon
- carrots
- parsnips
- celery
- turnips
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- mustard

## Directions

1. Take onion that you cut into strips
2. Cook them in a small roux made with butter and flour
3. When the onion is almost cooked, moisten it with bouillon and finish cooking it
4. Take carrots, parsnips, celery, turnips that have been cooked in the pot, cut them quickly into strips, and put them in the onion ragout
5. Season with salt, pepper, and a dash of vinegar
6. When serving, add mustard

title: Racines à la Crème (Roots with Cream)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- large tender roots
- butter
- bouquet of parsley
- chives
- garlic clove
- shallots
- cloves
- basil
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- bouillon
- egg yolks
- cream
- white vinegar

## Directions

1. Take large tender roots, scrape and wash them, blanch them for half an hour in boiling water
2. Cut them into thick strips and put them in a casserole with a piece of good butter, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a garlic clove, two shallots, two cloves, basil
3. Pass them over the fire, add a pinch of flour, salt, pepper, and good bouillon
4. Cook and reduce to a short sauce
5. Remove the bouquet and add a liaison of three egg yolks with cream, thicken without boiling
6. When serving add a large dash of white vinegar

title: Ragout of Sheep Tails
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep tails
- Veal sweetbreads
- Artichoke bottoms
- Chestnuts
- Veal quenelles
- Mushrooms
- Butter
- Espagnole sauce
- Consommé
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Jus

## Directions

1. When your tails are cooked as described for braised tails, keep them somewhat firm so they do not break in your ragout
2. Cut them in two
3. Put them in a saucepan with veal sweetbreads and four artichoke bottoms cut in quarters, or else chestnuts and a few veal quenelles
4. Turn a handful of mushrooms that you sauté in butter
5. Drain them
6. Put with your mushrooms a spoonful of espagnole, and half of consommé
7. Reduce your sauce by one third and pour it over your ragout
8. If you do not have sauce, put with your mushrooms two spoonfuls of flour, moisten it with bouillon, and add a little jus to give color
9. Reduce it until it is bound enough to put with your ragout
10. You may serve it in a terrine, in a silver saucepan, or in a hot pâté

title: Ragoût au Salpicon (Salpicon Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One sweetbread of veal, blanched
- Two artichoke bottoms, blanched
- Mushrooms
- A bouquet of parsley, chives
- Half a clove of garlic
- One clove
- Half a bay leaf
- A little basil
- A piece of butter
- A good pinch of flour
- Meat juice
- White wine
- A little bouillon
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan one blanched veal sweetbread, two blanched artichoke bottoms, and mushrooms, all cut in dice, with a bouquet of parsley and chives, half a clove of garlic, one clove, half a bay leaf, a little basil, and a piece of butter
2. Pass it over the fire and add a good pinch of flour
3. Moisten with meat juice, white wine, a little bouillon, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Cook and reduce to a short sauce
5. Degrease before serving

title: Ragoût d'Écrevisses (Crayfish Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Water
- Half a glass of good white wine
- Good bouillon
- One glass of good coulis

## Directions

1. Boil the crayfish for a moment in water
2. Peel the tails and put them in a saucepan with a half-glass of good white wine, as much good bouillon, and a glass of good coulis
3. Boil them for a quarter hour and serve as you wish
4. Alternatively, if you wish to serve them with a crayfish coulis, cook them with a little bouillon and white wine, and when there is almost no sauce remaining, put them in a crayfish coulis

title: Ragoût de Choux (Cabbage Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a medium cabbage
- Good butter
- Flour
- Bouillon
- Jus
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil half a medium cabbage in water for half an hour
2. Remove it to fresh water, press it well, and remove the heart
3. Chop the cabbage slightly and place it in a casserole with a piece of good butter
4. Pass it over the fire, add a good pinch of flour, moisten with bouillon and jus until there is enough to give a golden color to your ragout
5. Let it boil on low heat until the cabbage is cooked and reduced to a short sauce
6. Season with salt, pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
7. Serve beneath the meat you judge fit

title: Ragoût de Farce (Herb Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sorrel
- Lettuce
- Chervil
- Parsley
- Chives
- Purslane
- Good butter
- Flour
- Jus
- Coulis
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg yolks
- Cream or milk

## Directions

1. Put in a casserole sorrel, lettuce, chervil, parsley, chives, and purslane, all well washed, chopped and well pressed, with a good piece of butter
2. Pass them over a good fire until there is no more water
3. Add a good pinch of flour, moisten with jus and coulis, salt, and pepper
4. Cook and serve at short sauce
5. If it is meatless, after adding the flour, moisten with lean bouillon, let it boil until the herbs are cooked and no sauce remains
6. Add a liaison of three egg yolks diluted with cream or milk, let it bind over the fire without boiling

title: Ragoût de Foie Gras (Foie Gras Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Foie gras (livers)
- Two spoonfuls of coulis
- Half a glass of white wine
- Good bouillon in equal measure
- Bouquet of parsley
- Chives
- Half a clove of garlic
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Remove the bitter part from the livers and leave them whole
2. Blanch them for an instant in boiling water
3. Place them in a casserole with two spoonfuls of coulis, half a glass of white wine, as much good bouillon, a bouquet of parsley, chives, half a clove of garlic, salt, and pepper
4. Let them boil for half an hour, taking care to degrease well
5. Serve with whatever meat you wish, or alone as an entremets

title: Ragoût de Foie Gras (Foie Gras Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Foie gras livers
- Water
- Coulis
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Remove the bitter part from the livers and leave them whole
2. Blanch them briefly in boiling water
3. Put them in a saucepan with two spoonfuls of coulis

title: Ragoût de Laitances (Fish Roe Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two carp roe
- Two spoonfuls of coulis
- Half a glass of white wine
- Bouillon in equal measure
- Bouquet of parsley
- Chives
- Half a clove of garlic
- Onions in slices
- Parsnip root cut in thin slices
- Parsley
- Chives
- Clove of garlic
- Two cloves
- Half a bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Butter
- Flour
- White wine
- Lean bouillon
- Egg yolks
- Cream or milk
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Boil two carp roe for a moment in boiling water and place them in a casserole with two spoonfuls of coulis, half a glass of white wine, as much bouillon, a bouquet of parsley, chives, half a clove of garlic
2. Let them boil for a quarter hour, season with salt and pepper
3. For meatless: put in a casserole onions in slices, a parsnip root cut in thin slices, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a hint of garlic, two cloves, half a bay leaf, thyme, basil, and a piece of butter
4. Pass them over the fire, add a pinch of flour, moisten with a glass of white wine and as much good lean bouillon
5. Let it boil and reduce by half, pass the sauce through a sieve, add the roe to let it boil for a quarter hour
6. Before serving, add a liaison of three egg yolks diluted with cream or milk, salt, and pepper, let it bind over the fire without boiling

title: Ragoût de Marrons (Chestnut Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a hundred chestnuts
- Half a glass of white wine
- Two spoonfuls of coulis
- A little bouillon

## Directions

1. Remove the first skin from half a hundred chestnuts and put them in a perforated pan to stir over the fire until you can remove the second skin
2. Put them in a saucepan with half a glass of white wine, two spoonfuls of coulis, and a little bouillon
3. Cook and reduce to a short sauce
4. Pay attention that they are well cooked and whole

title: Ragoût de Moules (Mussel Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Mussels
- Mushrooms
- Bouquet of parsley
- Chives
- Clove of garlic
- Two cloves
- Butter
- Onion in slices
- Root vegetable
- Flour
- White wine
- Mussel water
- Jus
- Coulis
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. For the meatless preparation, refer to page 224
2. For the meat version: put in a casserole a few mushrooms, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a clove of garlic, two cloves, a small piece of butter, an onion in slices with a root
3. Pass them over the fire until they are colored, add a pinch of flour, moisten with a glass of white wine, mussel water, and jus
4. Let boil for a good half hour, degrease, add a little coulis; if you have none, use a little flour and jus
5. Reduce to the consistency of a sauce, pass it through a sieve, add the mussels without shells after you have had them open over the fire, a little pepper and salt, if the mussel water has not salted the sauce enough

title: Ragoût de Mousserons, Champignons, et Morilles (Ragout of Mousserons, Mushrooms, and Morels)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Mousserons or mushrooms or morels
- Butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Flour
- One glass of bouillon
- Half a glass of white wine
- As much jus
- Coulis
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Put mousserons in a saucepan with a piece of butter, a bouquet of parsley and chives
2. Pass them over the fire and add a pinch of flour
3. Moisten with a glass of bouillon, a half-glass of white wine, and as much jus
4. Cook for a good hour
5. Skim the fat and add a little coulis, or if you do not have any, add a little more flour
6. Season with salt and coarse pepper
7. For mushrooms and morels, proceed the same way, except that morels must be well washed and beaten in several waters to remove the sand

title: Ragoût de Passe-Pierre (Samphire Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Pickled samphire leaves
- Good coulis
- Water for blanching

## Directions

1. Take the pickled samphire, using only the leaves
2. Blanch them for a moment in boiling water to remove the strength of the vinegar
3. Press them to remove the water
4. Place them in a good sauce made with bound coulis

title: Ragoût de Passe-Pierre (Samphire Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Preserved samphire
- Water
- Good sauce with thickened coulis

## Directions

1. Take preserved samphire, using only the leaves
2. Blanch them briefly in boiling water to remove the strength of the vinegar
3. After they are pressed to remove the water, put them in a good sauce made with thickened coulis

title: Ragoût de Pistaches (Pistachio Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a handful of pistachios
- Water
- Good coulis

## Directions

1. Put pistachios in boiling water for a moment to remove the skin and throw them into fresh water as you go
2. Let them drain, then put them in a sauce made with good coulis

title: Ragoût de Truffes (Truffle Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium truffles
- Good butter
- Parsley
- Chives
- Half a clove of garlic
- Two cloves
- Flour
- One glass of bouillon
- One glass of white wine
- Coulis
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Peel medium truffles and cut them into slices
2. Put them in a saucepan with a small piece of good butter, a bouquet of parsley, chives, half a clove of garlic, and two cloves
3. Pass them over the fire and add a pinch of flour
4. Moisten with a glass of bouillon and as much white wine
5. Cook on a small fire for half an hour
6. Skim the fat and add a little coulis, salt, and coarse pepper

title: Ragoût Mêlé (Mixed Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms
- Foie gras
- Two or three artichoke hearts
- Bouquet of parsley
- Chives
- Half a clove of garlic
- Butter
- Flour
- Half a glass of white wine
- Coulis
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Small eggs
- Egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put in a casserole mushrooms cut into four, foie gras, two or three artichoke hearts cooked halfway in water and cut into pieces, a bouquet of parsley, chives, half a clove of garlic, a little butter
2. Pass everything over the fire, add a pinch of flour, moisten with half a glass of white wine, a little coulis and bouillon
3. Cook for half an hour, degrease, season with salt and pepper
4. If you have small eggs, boil them for an instant in water, remove the small skin and put them in the ragout
5. To make artificial eggs: take two hard egg yolks that you pound and mix with a raw egg yolk and a pinch of fine salt, place them on a floured table, roll them like a small sausage and cut into small pieces of equal size, roll each piece in your hands with a little flour to round them, and place them as you go on a floured plate
6. When they are all made, put them for a moment in boiling water, after they have boiled twice, remove them to fresh water, let them drain before putting them in the ragout
7. If you wish to put this ragout in white sauce, do not put coulis in it; before serving, add a liaison of three egg yolks with cream

title: Raie au beurre noir (Skate with Black Butter)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one skate
- court-bouillon
- parsley leaves
- black butter sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the skate as in the previous recipe
2. Clean and trim it in the same way
3. Fry parsley leaves and place them around the skate
4. Coat it with black butter sauce

title: Raie au Fromage (Skate with Cheese)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half of a fine bouclée skate
- milk (demi-setier)
- butter (size of half an egg)
- flour (two pinches)
- garlic clove
- two cloves
- two shallots
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- Gruyère cheese, grated
- twelve small white onions
- two small pieces of fried bread, cut in rounds
- parmesan cheese (optional)

## Directions

1. Skin a fine half of the bouclée skate and cut it into four equal pieces
2. Wash it and cook with a demi-setier of milk and butter kneaded with two pinches of flour, a garlic clove, two cloves, two shallots, a bay leaf, thyme, basil, little salt, and pepper
3. Boil before adding the skate (cooking requires little time)
4. Remove the skate from the sauce to drain it
5. Strain the sauce through a tamis and reduce it to the consistency of a bound sauce
6. Put half the sauce in the bottom of the serving dish
7. Sprinkle a small handful of grated Gruyère cheese over it
8. Arrange the skate pieces on top
9. Between the pieces, arrange a dozen small white onions cooked in broth and well drained, and two small pieces of fried bread cut in rounds, interspersing them neatly
10. Pour the remaining sauce over everything
11. Cover with grated Gruyère or Parmesan cheese
12. For best results, place the dish over a low fire that boils gently until there is almost no sauce left
13. Glaze the top with a hot shovel or a pie-dish cover with a good fire underneath
14. When the top is a beautiful golden color, serve

title: Raie Marinée Frite (Marinated Fried Skate)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- skate
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- garlic clove
- onions
- vegetable root zests
- cloves
- flour
- parsley for frying

## Directions

1. Skin the skate and cut it into pieces as for the preceding recipe
2. Marinate for two or three hours with a little water, vinegar, salt, pepper, parsley, chives, garlic, onions in slices, vegetable root zests, and cloves
3. Drain and dry thoroughly
4. Flour and fry
5. Serve with fried parsley

title: Raie à la Sainte-Menehould (Skate à la Sainte-Menehould)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- skate
- butter
- flour
- milk (chopine)
- salt
- pepper
- bouquet of parsley, chives, garlic clove, two shallots
- three cloves
- thyme
- bay leaf
- basil
- pine nuts in slices
- vegetable roots in zests
- rémoulade sauce

## Directions

1. Skin the skate and cut it into pieces two fingers wide
2. Cook over very low heat for half an hour
3. In a saucepan, make a roux with butter and a spoonful of flour
4. Gradually moisten with a chopine of milk, seasoning with salt, pepper, a bouquet of parsley, chives, garlic, two shallots, three cloves, thyme, bay leaf, basil, sliced pine nuts, and vegetable root zests
5. Boil for a good quarter hour, then add the skate to cook
6. Once cooked, dip the skate in the richest part of the sauce to bread it, then grill it basting with a little butter
7. Serve dry with rémoulade sauce in a sauceboat

title: Raie à la sauce blanche (Skate with White Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one skate
- court-bouillon
- one glass of vinegar
- white sauce
- capers or cornichons cut into dice

## Directions

1. Cook the skate in court-bouillon with a glass of vinegar
2. When cooked, remove the slime or skin from both sides
3. Trim it and place it on the platter
4. Coat it with white sauce and top with capers or cornichons cut into dice

title: Raie à la Sauce de son Foie (Skate with Liver Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- skate
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- garlic
- butter
- flour
- capers
- anchovy
- skate liver
- salt
- pepper
- water or broth

## Directions

1. Cook the skate as described on page 211
2. For the sauce, put parsley, chives, mushrooms, and a hint of garlic, all finely chopped, with a little butter in a saucepan
3. Pass several times over the fire
4. Add a good pinch of flour and a piece of butter
5. Add finely chopped capers and anchovy, the cooked and crushed liver of the skate, salt, and coarse pepper
6. Moisten with water or broth and bind over the fire
7. Serve over the skate

title: Raisinet (Grape Preserve)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Grapes
- Peeled and quartered fruit

## Directions

1. Remove the seeds from the grapes and put the seeds in a cauldron
2. Let it boil a few times, stirring well with a spatula
3. When the seeds are well burst, pass the juice through a horsehair sieve
4. Pour it back into the cauldron; when it boils, add the peeled fruit cut into quarters
5. Cook each fruit separately; as soon as they have boiled a few times, remove them and let them drain
6. Make sure the fruits are cooked firm; skim your reduction well
7. Reduce the juice to a quarter, meaning only a quarter should remain of the total
8. Put your fruits into it; stir well with a spatula without stopping
9. When you see the grape preserve will thicken, put a little on a plate; if you see it congeals, remove the cauldron from the fire
10. Let the grape preserve rest a moment, and arrange it in your pots
11. Place them in a warm oven until the next day
12. Dip a paper disk in brandy and cover them with another paper; put string around it
13. Place the grape preserve in a dry place

title: Ramequins (Cheese Puffs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A good piece of well-aged Brie cheese
- A piece of butter about one quarter pound
- A half setier of cold or hot water
- Very little salt
- One anchovy
- Flour
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Crush a good piece of Brie cheese in a saucepan with a piece of butter of about one quarter pound, a half setier of cold or hot water, very little salt, and one chopped anchovy.
2. Make everything boil together, and add as much flour as the sauce can absorb.
3. Let it dry on the fire until your paste is very thick.
4. Transfer it to another saucepan and blend in as many eggs as the paste can absorb without becoming liquid; the paste must hold together without running.
5. Shape this paste into small pieces the size of a pigeon egg on a baking sheet and bake them in the oven.
6. The ramequins should be light and a beautiful golden color when done.

title: Ratafia d'Abricots (Apricot Ratafia)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One quarter pound of apricots
- Apricot kernels (from broken pits)
- Two pints of eau-de-vie
- Half a pound of sugar
- A little cinnamon
- Eight cloves
- Very little mace

## Directions

1. Cut the apricots into small pieces
2. Break the pits to extract the kernels, peel and crush them
3. Place them in a jug with the apricots, two pints of eau-de-vie, half a pound of sugar, a little cinnamon, eight cloves, and very little mace
4. Cork the jug well and let it infuse for fifteen days or three weeks
5. Take care to frequently remove the jug from storage
6. After infusing, strain it through a strainer to put it in bottles to be stored in the cellar

title: Ratafia d'anis (Anise Liqueur)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 1/2 setier water
- 1/2 setier water
- 3 ounces anise
- 3 chopines brandy

## Directions

1. Put 1 pound of sugar in a pan with 1/2 setier of water, boil them together until the sugar is well skimmed and clear
2. Then boil 1/2 setier of water separately
3. Put 3 ounces of anise in it, remove from fire without letting it boil, let it infuse for a quarter hour and put it in the sugar with 3 chopines of brandy
4. Stir everything together before putting it in a jug, seal the jug well and put it in the sun
5. Let the liqueur infuse for three weeks
6. Before putting it in bottles, strain it through a cloth or filter
7. This makes two pints

title: Ratafia de bigarades et de citrons (Bitter Orange and Lemon Liqueur)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 bitter oranges or 8 lemons
- 3 chopines brandy
- 1 pound sugar
- 1/2 setier water

## Directions

1. Take 8 bitter oranges or 8 lemons, peel them lightly without cutting into the white pith, cut the peel into small zests and put them in a jug with 3 chopines of brandy
2. Let them infuse together for three weeks
3. Then put 1 pound of sugar in a pan with 1/2 setier of water, boil them together and skim well
4. Put this in the jug with the brandy and let it infuse another twelve to fifteen days
5. Strain it through a cloth to put in bottles
6. This liqueur will keep for several years

title: Ratafia de Cerises (Cherry Ratafia)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Good ripe cherries
- A little raspberries
- Cherry pits (kernels from the pits)
- Three pints of cherry juice (resulting from pressing)
- Two pints of eau-de-vie
- One quarter pound of sugar per pint
- Three handfuls of crushed cherry pits
- A handful of coriander
- A little cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take good ripe cherries, remove the stems and pits
2. Mix with a little raspberries and crush everything together
3. Place them in a clean jug and let them sit for four or five days
4. Take care to stir the marc two or three times daily to give it flavor and a beautiful color
5. Press the marc well to extract all the juice
6. Measure the juice and for three pints of juice add two pints of eau-de-vie
7. Crush three handfuls of pits from the same cherries, with one quarter pound of sugar per pint
8. Put everything to infuse in the same jug with a handful of coriander and a little cinnamon
9. Stir it daily for seven or eight days
10. After which, pass it through a strainer until clear and put it in well-corked bottles, then store in the cellar

title: Ratafia de Coings (Quince Liqueur)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- good quinces, pitted and peeled
- 2 pints of eau-de-vie per 3 pints of quince juice
- 1 quarteron of sugar per pint of juice
- cinnamon
- coriander
- ginger
- mace

## Directions

1. Crush the quinces after removing the pits and peel
2. Press them well in a clean cloth
3. Measure the juice obtained
4. Mix 2 pints of eau-de-vie with 3 pints of juice and add 1 quarteron of sugar per pint, cinnamon, coriander, ginger, and mace in moderate amounts
5. Infuse everything together for ten or twelve days in a well-corked jug so it does not become exposed to air
6. Pass it through a fine strainer
7. Put it in clean bottles
8. Cork it well and put it in the cellar; the older it is, the better it will be

title: Ratafia de Fleurs d'Oranges (Orange Blossom Liqueur with Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of sugar
- 1 glass of water
- half a pound of orange blossom leaves
- 1 pint of eau-de-vie

## Directions

1. Put the sugar in a pan with a glass of water and bring to a boil, skimming
2. Continue boiling until, dipping a skimming spoon in it and blowing through the holes, large sparks of sugar come out
3. Remove from the fire and add half a pound of orange blossom leaves
4. Let them boil two or three times with the sugar
5. Remove from the fire and cover well
6. Leave them in the sugar for five or six hours
7. Return them to a low fire with a pint of eau-de-vie
