8. Keep on low heat just long enough for the sugar to mix well with the eau-de-vie
9. Pass your liqueur through a cloth and put it in bottles
10. Adjust the quantities according to how much you wish to make

title: Ratafia de Fleurs d'Oranges (Orange Blossom Liqueur)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of orange blossoms, cleaned
- 2 pints of eau-de-vie
- 1 pint of water
- 1 pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Infuse the orange blossoms in the eau-de-vie, water, and sugar for twenty-four hours
2. Pass them through the strainer as with the others

title: Ratafia de Fruits Rouges (Red Fruit Ratafia)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two pounds of cherries
- One pound of currants
- One pound of black guignes (wild cherries)
- One pound of raspberries
- One pound of blackberries
- Pits from half the cherries
- One quarter pound of sugar per pint of ratafia
- Eau-de-vie in equal quantity to the fruit juice
- One stick of candy

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of cherries from which you remove the stems and pits, one pound of currants, one pound of black guignes, one pound of raspberries, one pound of blackberries that you add later if you do not have them at the same time
2. Crush all these fruits together to put them in a jug with their juice and the pits of half the cherries that you have crushed
3. Let everything ferment together for three days
4. Then pass the juice through a sieve to return it to the jug with an equal amount of eau-de-vie to the fruit juice, one quarter pound of sugar per pint of ratafia, one stick of candy
5. Let it infuse for two months, then draw off the ratafia clear to put it in bottles

title: Ratafia de genièvre (Juniper Liqueur)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pints brandy
- 1 good handful juniper berries
- 1.5 pounds sugar
- 1 chopine water

## Directions

1. Put 2 pints of brandy in a jug with 1 good handful of juniper berries
2. Boil 1.5 pounds of sugar with 1 chopine of water until it is well skimmed and clear
3. Put the sugar mixture in the jug with the brandy and juniper
4. Seal the jug well and keep it in a warm place for about five weeks
5. Before straining it through a filter or cloth, wait until it is clear
6. When clear, put it in bottles and seal them well
7. This liqueur is stomachic and good when kept for a long time
8. This makes three pints

title: Ratafia de noix (Walnut Liqueur)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 whole walnuts
- 3 chopines brandy
- 1 pound sugar
- 1/2 setier water
- 1 small piece rock candy
- 1 pinch coriander

## Directions

1. Take a dozen whole walnuts that are just formed, split them in half and put them in a jug with 3 chopines of brandy
2. Seal the jug well and keep it in a cool place for six weeks
3. Be careful to shake the jug from time to time
4. Then put 1 pound of sugar in a pan with 1/2 setier of water, boil and skim
5. After straining the brandy through a cloth, add the sugar to it with a small piece of rock candy and a pinch of coriander
6. Let it infuse for about another month
7. Strain it clear and put it in bottles

title: Ratafia de Noyaux et de Graines (Liqueur of Kernels and Seeds)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of apricot kernels, choosing the best
- 2 pints of eau-de-vie
- 1 pint of water
- 1 pound of sugar
- 1 handful of coriander
- a little cinnamon

## Directions

1. Infuse the apricot kernels in the eau-de-vie and water with sugar, coriander, and cinnamon for eight days
2. Pass it through the strainer until it is very clear
3. Put it in bottles

title: Ratafia de Raisin Muscat (Muscat Grape Ratafia)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe muscat grapes
- Brandy, equal measure to the juice
- Sugar, one quarter pound per pint
- Mace
- Cinnamon
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Take ripe muscat grapes and put them in a terrine to crush them and extract as much juice as you can
2. Pass it through a sieve
3. Measure this juice to put with as much brandy, one quarter pound of sugar per pint, according to the quantity you have
4. Add to it in proportion a little mace, cinnamon and cloves
5. Put everything together in a well-stoppered jug to infuse five or six days, stirring it every day
6. Then pass it through a filter
7. When it is clear, put it in well-stoppered bottles

title: Ravigote à l'Huile (Ravigote with Oil)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- A ravigote (mixed herbs)
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- One spoonful of cold velouté
- Two spoonfuls of oil
- Four or five spoonfuls of vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop a ravigote and put it in a saucepan with salt, coarse pepper, and one spoonful of cold velouté
2. Stir your sauce with the ravigote
3. Add two spoonfuls of oil and mix well with your sauce so that it is bound
4. Add four or five spoonfuls of vinegar
5. Your sauce must be well stirred at the moment of putting it on your salad of meat or fish

title: Red Mullet (Rouget)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Red mullet
- White wine
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Roots and onions (bouquet)

## Directions

1. Do not scale the red mullet; gut, wash and keep the livers
2. Cook on the grill like the weever and serve with the same sauces
3. Make sure to put the livers in the sauce served over it

title: Red Mullet à la Parisienne (Greliot)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Red mullet (greliot)
- White wine
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Roots
- Onions

## Directions

1. Gut and wash without scaling
2. Cook with white wine, a little butter, salt, pepper, and a bouquet of roots and onions
3. Boil the court-bouillon for half an hour so it has flavor when you add them
4. When cooked, remove from court-bouillon and gently remove the scales everywhere except the head
5. Serve with the same sauces as above

title: Ris de Veau aux Fines Herbes (Calf Sweetbreads with Fine Herbs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Fennel
- parsley
- chives
- garlic
- shallots
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- Three or four calf sweetbreads

## Directions

1. Chop very finely a little fennel, parsley, chives, a small amount of garlic, and two shallots
2. Mix all these fine herbs with a piece of good butter the size of half an egg, fine salt, and coarse pepper
3. Blanch three or four calf sweetbreads and prick them in several places on top to allow the butter and fine herbs to penetrate

title: Ris de Veau aux Fines Herbes (Sweetbreads with Fine Herbs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Fennel
- Parsley
- Chives
- A small amount of garlic
- Two shallots
- Butter (about half an egg's worth)
- Fine salt
- Coarse pepper
- Three or four sweetbreads (ris de veau)
- Bacon slices
- Half a glass of white wine
- As much good broth

## Directions

1. Chop very finely some fennel, parsley, chives, a small amount of garlic, and two shallots
2. Mix all these fine herbs with butter (about half an egg's worth), fine salt, and coarse pepper
3. Blanch three or four sweetbreads
4. Prick them in several places to allow the butter and herbs to enter
5. Place the sweetbreads in a casserole with bacon slices on top
6. Add half a glass of white wine and as much good broth
7. Cook them on low heat, letting them only simmer
8. When cooked, remove the grease from the sauce, which should be short
9. Serve the sauce over the sweetbreads; if you have a spoonful of coulis, add it to the sauce for improvement

title: Ris de Veau en Caisses (Sweetbreads in Paper Cases)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two large sweetbreads or three small ones
- Oil or melted bacon
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- One shallot
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Paper for making cases

## Directions

1. Soak the sweetbreads in warm water to disgorge them
2. Blanch them by boiling in water for half a quarter-hour
3. Remove to fresh water and remove the cornet
4. Cut the sweetbreads and throat into small slices
5. Marinate them with oil or melted bacon, chopped parsley, chives, mushrooms, shallot, salt, and coarse pepper
6. Make seven or eight small paper cases about three fingers long
7. Rub the bottom with oil
8. Put the sweetbreads with all seasonings into the cases
9. Place the cases on a grid with an oiled paper beneath
10. Cook on very low heat from hot ashes for half an hour, watching that the fire does not catch the paper
11. When cooked, add a squeeze of lemon juice or a dash of white vinegar

title: Ris de Veau en Hatelet (Sweetbreads on Skewers)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A quarter pound of small bacon in thin square slices
- Two sweetbreads, disgorged and blanched
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- One shallot
- A small amount of garlic
- Flour
- Broth
- Four raw egg yolks
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into small thin squares the width of a finger
2. Cook them in a casserole over low heat until half cooked
3. Cut the sweetbreads into dice
4. Add them to the casserole with the bacon, chopped parsley, chives, mushrooms, shallot, and garlic
5. Pass everything together on the fire and add a good pinch of flour
6. Moisten with broth and boil for half an hour until there is no more sauce
7. Season with salt and coarse pepper if needed
8. When almost cooked, add four raw egg yolks
9. Thicken on the fire without boiling, making the sauce thick enough to cling to the meat
10. Remove from heat when half cooled
11. Thread everything onto a small silver skewer or wooden skewers
12. Coat with breadcrumbs as you go
13. Grill over low heat to a beautiful golden color
14. Serve dry for an entremets or side dish

title: Ris de Veau en Ragoût (Sweetbreads in Ragout)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One large sweetbread
- Mushrooms
- Butter
- Bouquet of parsley
- Chives
- Half a bay leaf
- Two cloves
- Half a clove of garlic
- Flour
- One glass of good broth
- Half a glass of white wine
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Two spoonfuls of coulis (or three egg yolks mixed with cream for entremets)
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare the sweetbread by disgorging and blanching
2. Cut it into five or six pieces
3. Put in a casserole with mushrooms, butter, bouquet of parsley, chives, half a bay leaf, two cloves, and half a clove of garlic
4. Pass on the fire and add a pinch of flour
5. Moisten with one glass of good broth and half a glass of white wine
6. Season with salt and coarse pepper
7. Boil on low heat for half an hour
8. Degrease and add two spoonfuls of coulis (or for entremets, use a liaison of three egg yolks mixed with cream instead of coulis)
9. Thicken on the fire without boiling to avoid curdling
10. Serve with short sauce
11. Add a small dash of vinegar if lacking acidity

title: Ris de Veau Frits (Fried Sweetbreads)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two fairly large sweetbreads
- Butter mixed with flour
- Half a glass of vinegar
- One large glass of water
- Three cloves
- One clove of garlic
- Two shallots
- Three or four chives
- A pinch of parsley
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Pepper
- Parsley for frying

## Directions

1. Soak the sweetbreads in warm water for one hour to disgorge
2. Blanch them for a quarter-hour at a rolling boil
3. Remove to fresh water and cut each piece into three parts
4. Put in a casserole butter mixed with flour, half a glass of vinegar, one large glass of water, three cloves, garlic, two shallots, three or four chives, a pinch of parsley, bay leaf, thyme, basil, and pepper
5. Heat the marinade, stirring the butter until melted
6. Add the sweetbreads and remove from heat to marinate for one and a half to two hours
7. Drain and dry them with a cloth
8. Flour them and fry to a beautiful color
9. When removed, throw parsley into the frying oil to fry it green and crispy
10. Serve the parsley around the sweetbreads

title: Ris de Veau à la Lyonnaise (Calf Sweetbreads Lyonnaise Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four calf sweetbreads
- lard
- bouillon
- parsley
- chives
- garlic
- cloves
- tarragon
- salt
- verjuice
- butter mixed with flour
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Soak and blanch three or four calf sweetbreads, then take half a pound of well-cooked lard, cut it into lardons and place in a casserole to render over low heat until almost cooked
2. Lard the sweetbreads across and place them in a casserole with good bouillon, a bouquet of parsley and chives, half a clove of garlic, two cloves, five or six tarragon leaves, and a pinch of salt
3. Cook the sweetbreads for half an hour, then pass the cooking liquid through a sieve and skim off the fat
4. Return it to the fire to reduce it into a glaze to coat the top of the sweetbreads
5. Put half a glass of bouillon in a casserole with two spoonfuls of verjuice, scrape up what remains in the pan, then add a piece of butter mixed with a pinch of flour the size of a walnut and two egg yolks
6. Make the sauce bind over the fire without boiling and serve under the sweetbreads as an hors-d'œuvre or side dish

title: Ris de Veau à la Pluche Verte (Calf Sweetbreads with Green Herbs)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four calf sweetbreads
- bouillon
- white wine
- parsley
- chives
- garlic
- clove
- bay leaf
- basil
- salt
- pepper
- verjuice
- butter mixed with flour

## Directions

1. Soak three or four calf sweetbreads in lukewarm water to disgorge them and blanch for a quarter hour in boiling water
2. Plunge them in fresh water, remove the cornet, keep the throat lobes, and place the sweetbreads and throat lobes in a pot with a little bouillon, a glass of white wine, a bouquet of parsley and chives, a clove of garlic, a clove, half a bay leaf and a few basil leaves
3. Add salt and pepper and cook until done, then pass the sauce through a sieve
4. Reduce the sauce if it is too thin, then add half a spoonful of verjuice with a piece of butter mixed with a pinch of flour the size of a walnut
5. Make the sauce bind over the fire until it has the consistency of double cream, add a good pinch of blanched parsley finely chopped
6. Dress the sweetbreads in the plate and pour the sauce over, serving as an hors-d'œuvre or side dish

title: Rissoles (Meat Pastries)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted meat
- butter
- parsley
- chives
- flour
- bouillon
- salt
- coarse pepper
- pastry dough made with flour, butter, water, and salt

## Directions

1. Cut leftover roasted meat into small pieces the size of peas
2. Pass the pieces in a pan with good butter, chopped parsley, and chopped chives
3. Add a pinch of flour and moisten with a little bouillon
4. Season with salt and coarse pepper
5. Reduce to a short sauce so it sticks to the meat
6. Let cool
7. Make a pastry dough with flour, a little butter, water and fine salt, knead and roll with a roller as thin as a coin
8. Cut circles from the dough, fill each with a small amount of meat
9. Fold and seal well together
10. Cut with a small wheel or knife
11. Fry until they are a beautiful golden color
12. Serve as an appetizer

title: Riz au Lait (Rice Milk Pudding)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Water
- Milk
- Salt
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the rice well
2. Cook it for half an hour over low heat with a little water to make it burst
3. Gradually add hot milk little by little until it is cooked
4. Season with salt and sugar

title: Riz au Lait (Rice Milk Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- rice, one quarter pound or more
- milk, boiling
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. After having washed a quarter pound of rice, or more, depending on how large your soup is, you have your milk boiling; you put your rice in it; you let it boil on low heat for an hour and a half, and you try that there is enough milk so that your rice bursts at ease, and that it is not in paste: your rice burst ready to serve, you will put the sugar needed so that your soup is good; you will put a little salt in it, that is to say five or six grains: be careful not to cover the vessel in which your rice cooks entirely, because the milk would turn.

title: Riz au Lait (Rice with Milk)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- A quarter pound of rice (or more, depending on the size of your soup)
- Boiling milk

## Directions

1. Wash a quarter pound of rice (or more, according to the size of your soup)
2. Have your milk boiling; put your rice in it
3. Boil it on a gentle fire for one hour and a half
4. Take care that there is enough milk so that your rice...

title: Riz au Lait d'Amandes (Rice with Almond Milk)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- almonds, half-pound sweet almonds
- bitter almonds, a few
- milk, two pints for soup
- fresh water
- sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. You will blanch your almonds, that is to say you will put them in a saucepan and water that you will make almost boil; you will see if the skin of the almond comes off; when your almonds are blanched, when the skin is removed, you will mix them in fresh water; a half-pound of sweet almonds, a few bitter almonds suffice for a soup of two pints of milk; you pound your almonds in a mortar; when they are well ground, you put them in a saucepan; you wet them with a half-setier of milk; then you put them in a fine cloth, and press them until all the milk has come out; you pour this milk into your soup at the moment of serving it; serve it very hot, and with good sugar and a little salt.

title: Riz Maigre à la Purée de Pois Verts (Lean Rice with Green Pea Purée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Lean bouillon
- Green pea purée

## Directions

1. Wash your rice in four or five lukewarm waters
2. Moisten your rice with lean bouillon
3. Boil it for one hour and a half
4. When your rice is cooked, one hour and a half before serving your soup, add your purée
5. Watch that your purée binds your soup, and that it is neither too thick nor too thin

title: Rognon de Bœuf à la Bourgeoise (Beef Kidney Bourgeoise Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef kidney
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Chives, minced
- Garlic, minced
- Vinegar
- Coulis

## Directions

1. Cut the kidney into thin slices
2. Pass it over the fire with a piece of butter, salt, pepper, minced chives and a pinch of minced garlic
3. When cooked, add a dash of vinegar and a bit of coulis
4. Do not let it boil anymore, for fear it will shrivel

title: Rognons de Cochon au Vin de Champagne (Pork Kidneys in Champagne Wine)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- pork kidneys
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- parsley
- shallots
- flour
- Champagne wine

## Directions

1. Slice your kidneys
2. Put a piece of butter in a casserole that you will place on a brisk fire
3. Put your sliced kidney in it, salt, pepper, a little grated nutmeg, parsley and shallot, all chopped very fine
4. Sauté your sliced meat constantly, so that it does not stick
5. When your kidney is stiffened, add a spoonful of flour that you will stir with your sliced meat
6. Pour in a glass of Champagne wine
7. Turn your ragout without letting it boil
8. This ragout can be moistened with water, bouillon, or other white wine; see if it is well seasoned

title: Rouelle de Veau Entre Deux Plats (Veal Rouelle Braised Between Two Plates)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a thick piece of veal rouelle
- thick lard for larding
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- a pinch of garlic
- salt
- pepper
- one onion
- two roots

## Directions

1. Take a piece of veal rouelle, as thick as you can to make a good dish, and lard it with thick lard, with parsley, chives, mushrooms, a pinch of garlic, all chopped, salt, and pepper.
2. Put the veal in a well-covered casserole and cook it in its own juice with an onion and two roots.
3. When it is cooked on very low heat, skim the fat from the sauce it has rendered, and serve the sauce over your piece of veal.
4. If you have coulis, you can put some in your sauce, it will only be better.

title: Roux Blanc (White Roux)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Prepare butter and flour as for the blonde roux
2. Place on a stovetop that is not too strong
3. Stir constantly without leaving it until it is very hot
4. Do not let it take on any color; the whiter it is, the better
5. Use only wheat flour, as rye flour is not good for thickening

title: Roux Blond (Blonde Roux)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of butter
- 1 liter of flour, more if your butter can absorb more
- wheat flour (old flour is best; rye flour does not thicken well)

## Directions

1. Let 1 pound of butter melt in a casserole
2. Add 1 liter of flour, more if your butter can absorb it; that is to say, your flour mixed with the butter should be thicker than if it were a thin porridge; if it is too thin, add more flour
3. Place your butter and flour on a fairly hot stove
4. Stir it until your roux is somewhat blonde
5. You will have a gentle fire on which you place some ash; place your roux on it and let it go on low heat until it is a beautiful blonde
6. Be careful to use wheat flour; old flour is best; rye flour does not thicken well
7. You use this roux to thicken all the sauces mentioned

title: Roux Blond (Blonde Roux)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- wheat flour

## Directions

1. Prepare butter and flour
2. Place on a fairly hot stovetop
3. Stir until the roux becomes a light blonde color
4. Be careful to use only wheat flour, as rye flour does not thicken well

title: Rémoulade (Standard Rémoulade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One glass of mustard
- A little shallots
- A little ravigote
- Six or seven spoonfuls of oil
- Three or four spoonfuls of vinegar
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Two raw egg yolks

## Directions

1. Put one glass of mustard in a bowl so you can thin it
2. Chop a little shallots and ravigote and add them to your mustard
3. Add six or seven spoonfuls of oil and three or four of vinegar, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Mix everything together
5. Add two raw egg yolks and stir with your rémoulade
6. Mix well so that your sauce is well bound
7. It should be somewhat thick

title: Rémoulade Indienne (Indian Rémoulade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Ten hard-boiled egg yolks
- Eight spoonfuls of oil
- Ten cloves of small chili pepper
- One teaspoonful of powder of Indian saffron or turmeric
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Four or five spoonfuls of vinegar

## Directions

1. Pound ten hard-boiled egg yolks
2. When well pounded, moisten your yolks with eight spoonfuls of oil, adding them one after another while always pounding your egg yolks
3. Add ten cloves of small chili pepper, one teaspoonful of Indian saffron or turmeric powder, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Take four or five spoonfuls of vinegar
5. Mix everything together as well as possible
6. Strain this sauce through a sieve like a purée
7. It should be somewhat thick
8. Put it in a sauce boat

title: Rémoulade Verte (Green Rémoulade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- A small handful of chervil
- Half as much salad burnet
- Tarragon
- Small chives
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- One glass of mustard
- Half a glass of oil
- Two or three raw egg yolks
- Four or five spoonfuls of vinegar
- A little spinach green (optional, if not green enough)

## Directions

1. Blanch the chervil, salad burnet, tarragon, and chives (called ravigote)
2. When well pressed, pound them
3. Add salt, coarse pepper, and one glass of mustard and pound all together
4. Add half a glass of oil and mix well with the ravigote and mustard
5. When well blended, add two or three raw egg yolks and four or five spoonfuls of vinegar
6. Mix everything and strain it through a sieve as if it were a purée
7. Your rémoulade should be somewhat thick
8. If it is not green enough, add a little spinach green

title: Rôt de Bif (Beef Roast)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb
- Skewers
- String
- Clear jus

## Directions

1. Cut the lamb at two cutlets near the saddle; try to make this end cut very square
2. Secure the flanks with skewers
3. Give a beautiful form to the saddle
4. Remove the thick muscle bones from both legs; beat them well
5. Put your beef roast on the spit
6. Hold it with skewers and string that you pass through the ribs and tie to the spit
7. Put clear jus underneath as a relief course

title: Rôt de Bif (Roast Lamb)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb cut to the second rib on the flank
- larding bacon
- thread for trussing

## Directions

1. Cut your lamb up to the second rib of the flank, which makes half the lamb with the two thighs held together
2. Lard the saddle and part of the thighs with fine bacon
3. Give your saddle a well-rounded shape and place it on the spit, securing it with skewers or small metal pins
4. Also take care to secure your last ribs to the spit with a trussing needle so that your roast does not take on a bad shape and does not turn
5. Two hours are sufficient to cook it
6. You can serve the roast without larding it if you cover it with lard bacon, or you can do without that as well

title: Rôties au Jambon (Ham on Toast)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- six or seven slices of bread
- butter
- ham slices
- water
- flour
- bouillon without salt
- vinegar
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cut six or seven slices of bread the width of two good fingers
2. Pass them in butter until they are a beautiful golden color
3. Cut as many slices of ham of the same size, which you desalt for one hour in water if it is not fresh
4. Put them in a saucepan over a small fire for one hour
5. When it is cooked, remove it from the saucepan, and put in the same saucepan a pinch of flour to make a small roux, and moisten it with bouillon without salt and a good splash of vinegar
6. Boil for a good quarter hour
7. After having defatted it, pass this sauce through a sieve
8. Dress the ham on the toast with the sauce over it, with a few grains of coarse pepper

title: Rôties au Lard (Bacon on Toast)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of bread
- salt pork, cut into small dice
- one raw egg
- parsley
- chives
- one shallot
- coarse pepper
- vinegar sauce

## Directions

1. Cut slices of bread the width of two fingers and of equal size
2. Put on them enough salt pork cut into small dice and worked with a raw egg, parsley, chives, one shallot, all chopped, coarse pepper
3. Fry them over a small fire
4. Serve with a clear sauce and a splash of vinegar

title: Rôties au Lard (Bacon Toast)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread slices, two fingers wide and of equal size
- Small bacon, cut into small dice
- One raw egg
- Parsley
- Chives
- One shallot
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Cut bread slices two fingers wide and of equal size
2. Mix small bacon cut into small dice with one raw egg, parsley, chives, one minced shallot, and coarse pepper
3. Place sufficient bacon mixture on each bread slice
4. Fry at low heat
5. Serve with clear sauce and a dash of vinegar

title: Rôties aux Anchois (Anchovy Toast)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread crumbs
- Butter
- Half a dozen anchovies, well-washed
- Oil
- Vinegar
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Pass bread crumbs in butter
2. Arrange on top half a dozen anchovies that have been well-washed and cut into thin fillets lengthwise
3. Season the slices with oil, vinegar, and coarse pepper

title: Rôties aux Concombres (Cucumber Toast)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Cucumber ragout
- Three egg yolks
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare a cucumber ragout as marked on page 259
2. When finished and well-bound, add three egg yolks
3. Dress on bread crumbs
4. Finish as spinach toast

title: Rôties aux Épinards (Spinach Toast)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach ragout, finished and thick
- Two raw egg yolks
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare a finished spinach ragout that is well-flavored and thick
2. Add two raw egg yolks to the ragout
3. Arrange the spinach on bread crumbs cut as previously
4. Smooth with a knife dipped in egg
5. Bread the top of the bread crumbs
6. Fry
7. Serve without sauce

title: Rôties de Rognons de Veau (Veal Kidney Toast)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread crumbs, same size as previous slices
- One veal kidney cooked on a spit
- Its fat
- Parsley
- Chives
- One shallot
- Salt
- Pepper
- Four egg yolks
- Egg whites
- Bread crumbs for breading

## Directions

1. Cut bread crumbs same size as previously made slices
2. Prepare a farce by mincing a veal kidney cooked on a spit with an equal amount of its fat, parsley, chives, one minced shallot, salt, and pepper
3. Bind with four egg yolks and beaten egg whites
4. Place this farce on the slices
5. Smooth the top with a knife dipped in beaten egg
6. Bread with bread crumbs
7. Cook on a baking sheet with a little heat above and below
8. Serve with a small clear sauce, somewhat elevated in flavor

title: Rôties de Toutes Sortes de Viandes (Toast of All Kinds of Meats)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover meat from the table
- Bread crumbs
- Two raw egg yolks
- Bread crumbs for breading

## Directions

1. Take any leftover meat from the table
2. Cut it into small dice to make a well-bound ragout
3. When cold, add two raw egg yolks
4. Dress the meat on bread crumbs
5. Smooth the top with a knife dipped in beaten egg
6. Bread with bread crumbs
7. Fry until golden
8. Serve with clear sauce

title: Rôties à la Minime (Minimist Toast)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread pieces, two fingers wide and slightly longer, two coins thick
- Oil
- Anchovy fillets
- Shallot
- Parsley
- Chives
- A touch of garlic
- Half a bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil powder
- Coarse pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut bread pieces two fingers wide, slightly longer, and two coins thick
2. Put them in a pan with oil and pass over low heat, turning from time to time until golden
3. Dress them on a plate
4. Place anchovy fillets on top
5. In the oil used to cook the slices, add minced shallot, parsley, chives, a touch of garlic, half a bay leaf, thyme, basil powder, coarse pepper, and a little vinegar
6. Boil for a moment
7. Dress over the slices and serve

title: Salmi at the Table with Spirits (Game Stew with Alcohol)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- One spoonful of chopped shallots
- Three-quarters of a glass of white wine
- Juice of three lemons
- Butter the size of half an egg
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Place the roasted birds on the table, cut them up and leave them on the plate that you place on a spirit-alcohol burner
2. Add salt, coarse pepper, one spoonful of chopped shallots, three-quarters of a glass of white wine, the juice of three lemons, and butter the size of half an egg
3. Sprinkle your salmi with bread crumbs
4. Let it simmer for ten minutes, turning it from time to time
5. Check that your sauce is well-seasoned and distribute your pieces

title: Salmi de Bécasse (Woodcock Salmi)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock
- Parsley
- Shallots
- Bay leaf
- Garlic
- Pepper
- Butter
- White wine
- Spanish sauce
- Consommé
- Clove
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Put three woodcocks on the spit; when they are cold, remove the members as correctly as possible, pare them, and put them in a saucepan.
2. Crush your scraps in a mortar and add a pinch of parsley leaves, six shallots, a little bay leaf, a clove of garlic if you like, and coarse pepper.
3. Give a few blows with the pestle on these scraps.
4. Put a piece of butter in a saucepan and place it on the fire.
5. Put your pounded scraps in it and let them brown for ten minutes.
6. Pour in a glass of white wine, six spoonfuls of Spanish sauce, and three spoonfuls of consommé.
7. Reduce everything to almost half.
8. Make sure your sauce is well thickened and pass it through a strainer, pressing it.
9. Put it over your woodcock members which you keep warm without letting them boil.
10. At the moment of serving, arrange your members and put croutons between, underneath, or on top as you wish.
11. If you have no sauce, make a light roux and put your pounded scraps in it, then pass them through; pour in a glass of white wine, two glasses of bouillon, and a clove; reduce your sauce to half and pass it as described above.

title: Salmi de Chasseur (Hunter's Game Stew)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock
- Liver and interior of the woodcock
- Chopped chives or shallots
- Two glasses of white wine
- Salt
- Fine pepper
- A few bread crusts

## Directions

1. Put your woodcock on the spit and cut it up
2. Place the pieces in a casserole
3. Chop the liver and inside of the woodcock and put it with your pieces, along with chopped chives or shallots, two glasses of white wine, salt, fine pepper, and a few bread crusts
4. Bring your salmi to a boil two or three times and serve at table

title: Salmi de Perdreaux (Partridge Salmi)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 partridges
- 6 shallots
- 1 handful of parsley leaves
- 1 bay leaf
- coarse pepper
- salt
- 1 glass of white wine
- 2 glasses of bouillon
- 6 tablespoons of Spanish sauce (or roux and partridge scraps if Spanish sauce unavailable)
- 1 whole lemon

## Directions

1. Spit-roast four partridges and let them cool if you have time
2. Carefully remove the limbs; crush the scraps with a knife
3. Place in a saucepan 6 tablespoons of Spanish sauce, the partridge scraps, 6 shallots, 1 glass of white wine, a pinch of parsley leaves, 1 bay leaf, a good pinch of coarse pepper, very little salt, and 1 glass of bouillon
4. Bring the sauce to a high heat; when reduced to less than half, strain through fine cloth over the partridge limbs
5. Keep the limbs hot in their sauce without boiling
6. At service, dress the partridge limbs with croutons dipped in the sauce
7. If you have no Spanish sauce, make a light roux, strain the scraps through it, add shallots and parsley, pour in 1 glass of white wine and 2 glasses of bouillon with salt, coarse pepper, and 1 bay leaf; reduce the sauce to half and strain through fine cloth over the limbs
8. At service, add the juice of one whole lemon and check the seasoning

title: Salmi de Table à l'Esprit-de-Vin (Partridge Salmi with Spirits, Prepared at Table)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- salt
- coarse pepper
- 1 glass of white wine
- juice of 2 lemons
- zest from lemon peel
- shallots, minced
- garlic, pounded
- bread crust, grated

## Directions

1. When ready to eat a salmi at table, piece the partridges and place them on a silver platter set over a spirit lamp
2. Add salt, coarse pepper, 1 glass of white wine, juice of 2 lemons with a little zest of the peel
3. Add minced shallots brought from the kitchen and a little pounded garlic if desired
4. Sprinkle grated bread crust over the salmi
5. Let it simmer for 10 minutes and serve
6. The salt and coarse pepper should dominate the flavors

title: Salmi de Table à l'Esprit-de-vin (Woodcock Salmi Made at Table with Spirits)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Woodcock
- Salt
- Pepper
- Shallots
- White wine
- Lemon juice
- Butter

## Directions

1. When your woodcocks are on the table, piece them and leave them on the plat which you put on a spirit lamp.
2. Add salt, coarse pepper, a spoonful of chopped shallots, three-quarters of a glass of white wine, the juice of three lemons, and butter about the size of half an egg.
3. Sprinkle your salmi with chervil.

title: Salsifis (Salsify)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify root
- water
- white vinegar
- salt
- butter, the size of an egg
- four spoonfuls of white vinegar

## Directions

1. Scrape the salsify root to remove the black surface, leaving no spots
2. As they become white, place them in a vessel with water and white vinegar
3. Pour a large amount of water into a pot and add salt
4. Add four spoonfuls of white vinegar
5. Once the water boils, add the salsify
6. Let them boil for one hour, testing if they are cooked (they should be neither too much nor too little)
7. Drain them and arrange them in white or brown sauce

title: Salsifis Frits (Fried Salsify)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify, cooked and drained
- white sauce, somewhat enhanced
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- frying batter

## Directions

1. Once the salsify is cooked, drain it
2. Make a white sauce that is somewhat enhanced
3. Sauté the salsify in it and let it cool on a plate
4. At the moment of serving, dip them in frying batter and fry them
5. Alternatively, when drained, place them in a terrine with a little salt, pepper, and vinegar and let them marinate briefly before putting them in the batter

title: Sarcelles en Entrée de Broche (Teals on the Spit as an Entrée)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Teals
- Lemon zest
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Juice of a lemon
- Lemon slices
- Lard strips
- Buttered paper
- Three spoonfuls of worked Spanish sauce
- One spoonful of consommé
- Glaze the size of a walnut
- Zest of a quarter lemon

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean your teals
2. Singe them lightly
3. Clean them carefully so no down or quills remain
4. Truss them and fix them with a larding needle and string
5. Chop a little lemon zest and mix it with a piece of butter, salt, coarse pepper, and the juice of a lemon
6. Put this seasoned butter inside the body of your two teals
7. Give them a pleasing shape
8. Put them on the spit
9. Cover them with lemon slices
10. Put lard strips on them and wrap them in buttered paper, on which you sprinkle salt
11. Tie your paper well so it can contain your seasoning
12. When on the spit, three-quarters of an hour before serving, put them on the fire
13. At the moment of serving, unbridle them and drain them (that is, let the butter they have in their body come out), and remove the lemon slices on top
14. Arrange them on your plate
15. Pour in three spoonfuls of worked Spanish sauce, one spoonful of consommé, glaze the size of a walnut, the zest of a quarter lemon, a bit of coarse pepper
16. Make your sauce come to a boil and put it under your teals

title: Sarcelles à la Batellière (Teals Bargeman Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Teals
- Butter
- Shallots
- Parsley
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Nutmeg
- Flour
- White wine
- Lemon juice
- Lemon zest
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Remove the thighs, fillets, and rump from your teals
2. Cut your fillets into three pieces lengthwise of the same thickness
3. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan with your pieces of teal, chopped shallots, parsley, salt, coarse pepper, a little grated nutmeg
4. Put your saucepan on a hot fire
5. Sauté what is in your saucepan for ten to twelve minutes
6. Taste if your pieces are well browned
7. Then take a full spoonful of flour and mix it well with your ragout
8. Add a glass of white wine
9. Stir your ragout an instant until it comes to a boil
10. If your sauce was too thick, add a half glass of white wine
11. As soon as your ragout comes to a boil, remove it from the fire to eat right away
12. If you are in the city, when your teals are sautéed enough, add half a spoonful of flour, the juice of two lemons, a bit of its zest, and three or four spoonfuls of bouillon
13. Turn your ragout on the fire when it is thickened
14. Check that it is well seasoned and serve it

title: Sauce ail Mouton (Garlic and Shallot Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Shallot
- A little garlic
- One spoonful of bouillon
- Two spoonfuls of coulis
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Put minced shallot and a little garlic in a saucepan
2. Crush the garlic and shallot together
3. Add one spoonful of bouillon to dilute them
4. Add two spoonfuls of coulis, salt, and pepper
5. Make a good bouillon with this sauce
6. Pass it through a sieve into another saucepan
7. Before serving, heat it for a moment

title: Sauce Allemande (German Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- velouté
- two or three egg yolks
- butter the size of half an egg
- coarse pepper
- salt
- veal cut into dice
- a little butter
- carrot pieces
- three or four small onions
- one bay leaf
- two cloves
- one spoonful of flour
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Take worked velouté and add two or three egg yolks according to the quantity of sauce you need
2. After binding it, add butter the size of half an egg after your butter has melted, stirring your sauce
3. Strain through a sieve to avoid finding any particles
4. Keep your sauce in a bain-marie
5. Add a little coarse pepper and ensure it is well salted
6. Alternatively, pass a little veal cut into dice in a saucepan with a little butter, a few pieces of carrot, three or four small onions, one bay leaf, and two cloves
7. When everything is browned, add one spoonful of flour, which you stir with what is in your saucepan
8. Then moisten with bouillon and stir your sauce until it boils
9. Once your meat is cooked, strain your sauce through a sieve and bind it as above

title: Sauce Appétissante (Appetizing Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- All sorts of small salad herbs
- Three shallots
- One spoonful of bouillon
- Two spoonfuls of coulis
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- A little butter
- One spoonful of mustard

## Directions

1. Take all sorts of small salad herbs, each according to its strength, and after peeling and washing them, put them with three shallots
2. Boil everything a little in a small pan and refresh it in fresh water
3. After pressing it, pound it well fine in a mortar
4. Put it in a saucepan with one spoonful of bouillon and two spoonfuls of coulis
5. Dilute everything and pass it through a sieve into another saucepan
6. Add salt, coarse pepper, a little butter, and one spoonful of mustard
7. Thicken the sauce without letting it boil

title: Sauce au Beurre d'Anchois (Anchovy Butter Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Well-reduced Spanish sauce
- Anchovy butter, amount of half an egg
- Lemon juice
- Or alternatively: small brown sauce and anchovy butter

## Directions

1. Have the well-reduced Spanish sauce ready
2. At the moment of serving, add anchovy butter the size of half an egg and lemon juice to destroy the salt that the butter might produce
3. When adding the anchovy butter to your hot sauce, stir well with a spoon so that the butter binds well with the sauce
4. If you lack Spanish sauce, make a small brown sauce and add your anchovy butter
5. This sauce can be made as a meatless version

title: Sauce au Beurre d'Écrevisse (Crayfish Butter Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Velvet sauce, worked as needed for saucing
- Crayfish butter, amount of an egg

## Directions

1. Have the velvet sauce worked as needed for saucing ready
2. At the moment of serving, add crayfish butter the size of an egg to your very hot velouté
3. Stir your sauce well so that the butter binds with it
4. If your sauce does not have enough color, add a little more crayfish butter
5. This sauce can also be made in a meatless version using meatless velouté

title: Sauce au Beurre à l'Ail (Garlic Butter Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Velvet sauce, worked as needed for saucing
- Garlic butter, amount of half a walnut
- Fine butter, amount of half an egg

## Directions

1. Take velvet sauce worked as needed for saucing
2. At the moment of serving, add garlic butter the size of half a walnut and stir it into your sauce
3. Add fine butter the size of half an egg to give it smoothness
4. If you lack velvet sauce, you can make a little sauce with veal scraps or with a cooking stock, adding garlic butter at the moment of serving according to the quantity of sauce, but the garlic must dominate much
5. You can also make it with brown sauce or in a meatless version

title: Sauce au Blanc (White Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Good bouillon (fat or lean depending on preparation)
- A good crumb of bread
- A bouquet of parsley, chives, garlic, shallot, thyme, bay leaf, basil, cloves, a little nutmeg
- Some whole mushrooms
- One glass of white wine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Three egg yolks
- Cream

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan fat or lean bouillon, a good crumb of bread, a bouquet garni of parsley, chives, garlic, shallot, thyme, bay leaf, basil, cloves, a little nutmeg, some whole mushrooms, one glass of white wine, salt, and pepper
2. Boil everything together and reduce to half
3. Pass this sauce through muslin to make a small coulis
4. When ready to use it, add a liaison of three egg yolks with cream
5. Thicken it on the fire and be careful not to let it turn

title: Sauce au Fumé de Gibier (Game-Smoked Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four partridges or wild rabbits
- Two carrots
- Three or four onions
- Two cloves
- Two bay leaves
- A little thyme
- Half a bottle of white wine
- Game essence or consommé
- Six to eight tablespoons of Spanish sauce or other suitable sauce

## Directions

1. Put the partridges or rabbits, carrots, onions, cloves, bay leaves, and thyme in a saucepan
2. Moisten everything with half a bottle of white wine
3. Reduce the wine until it is a glaze
4. Moisten with game essence if you have it; otherwise use consommé
5. When your game is cooked, pass this essence through a cloth
6. Work in six to eight tablespoons of Spanish sauce or another suitable sauce
7. When your sauce is reduced and degreased to the right point, pass it through muslin and put it in a saucepan in a bain-marie
8. You can also use game scraps for this sauce; if you lack Spanish sauce or essence, moisten the game scraps with white wine that you reduce to a glaze, or with bouillon; incorporate this into a brown sauce that you reduce; your sauce should be of good salt and flavor

title: Sauce au Jus d'Orange (Orange Juice Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a glass of good broth
- As much juice
- A few zests of bitter orange peel
- Good butter about half the size of an egg, mixed with a small pinch of flour
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Juice of one bitter orange

## Directions

1. Put half a glass of good broth in a saucepan with as much juice, a few zests of bitter orange peel, good butter about half the size of an egg mixed with a small pinch of flour, salt and coarse pepper
2. Bind on the fire and then squeeze in the juice of one bitter orange

title: Sauce au Petit-Maître (Petit-Maître Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One glass of white wine
- Half a lemon, sliced
- A little very fine bread crumbs
- Two spoonfuls of good oil
- A bouquet of parsley, chives
- Two cloves of garlic
- A little tarragon
- Two cloves
- A little good bouillon
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan one glass of white wine, half a lemon cut in slices, a little very fine bread crumbs, two spoonfuls of good oil, a bouquet of parsley and chives, two cloves of garlic, a little tarragon, two cloves, a little good bouillon, salt, and coarse pepper
2. Boil everything together over very low heat for a quarter hour
3. Degrease and pass your sauce through a sieve
4. Serve this sauce for poultry or game

title: Sauce au Verjus (Verjuice Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two spoonfuls of verjuice
- As much coulis
- Coarse salt
- Pepper
- Shallot, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Put two spoonfuls of verjuice in a saucepan with as much coulis, coarse salt and pepper, and finely chopped shallot
2. Make this sauce quite clear
3. Heat it and serve for grilled meats

title: Sauce aux Câpres en Anchois (Capers and Anchovy Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Good butter
- A good pinch of flour
- One anchovy, chopped
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Fine whole capers
- Two whole chives
- Jus

## Directions

1. Put good butter in a saucepan and dilute with a good pinch of flour, one chopped anchovy, salt, coarse pepper, fine whole capers, two whole chives
2. Moisten little by little with jus until there is enough to give your sauce a brown color
3. Bind the sauce on the fire
4. If the sauce is too thick, add a little broth
5. Remove the chives before serving

title: Sauce aux Petits Œufs (Small Egg Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One glass of good broth
- A dash of vinegar
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Three hard-boiled egg yolks, chopped
- Good butter, about half the size of an egg
- A small pinch of flour

## Directions

1. Put one glass of good broth in a saucepan with a dash of vinegar, salt, coarse pepper, three chopped hard-boiled egg yolks, and good butter about half the size of an egg mixed with a small pinch of flour
2. Bind on the fire and serve as desired

title: Sauce aux Truffes (Truffle Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three truffes, finely chopped
- Oil or butter
- Four or five tablespoons of velouté
- One spoonful of consommé

## Directions

1. Chop two or three truffles very fine
2. Have them lightly brown in oil or butter, according to taste
3. Add four or five tablespoons of velouté and one spoonful of consommé to your truffles
4. Boil your sauce a quarter hour over low heat
5. Degrease it and put it in a small saucepan
6. Keep it in a bain-marie

title: Sauce aux Truffes (Truffle Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two or three truffles
- Oil or butter
- Four or five spoonfuls of velouté
- One spoonful of consommé
- Or alternatively: a little flour
- Five or six spoonfuls of bouillon
- A little coarse pepper
- A little quatre épices (four spices)

## Directions

1. Chop the truffles very finely
2. Brown them lightly in oil or butter
3. Add four or five spoonfuls of velouté and one spoonful of consommé
4. Boil your sauce for a quarter hour over low heat
5. Degrease it, then place it in a small saucepan and keep it in a bain-marie
6. If you do not have velouté, use a little flour with your truffles
7. Add five or six spoonfuls of bouillon, a little coarse pepper, and a little quatre épices
8. Simmer your sauce and serve it for the indicated dishes

title: Sauce Bachique (Bachic Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One spoonful of fine oil
- Half-setier of good bouillon
- One chopine of white wine
- Shallot
- Watercress
- Tarragon
- Chervil
- Parsley
- Chives
- A little garlic
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- A little coulis (optional)

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan one spoonful of fine oil, half-setier of good bouillon, and one chopine of white wine
2. Boil everything together and reduce to more than half
3. Add minced shallot, watercress, tarragon, chervil, parsley, chives, a little garlic, salt, and coarse pepper, all chopped very fine
4. Boil everything together for a moment and serve
5. If you have a little coulis to add to the sauce, it will only be better

title: Sauce Blanche (White Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter pound of butter
- half a spoonful of flour
- salt
- coarse pepper
- one spoonful of vinegar
- a little water

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan a quarter pound of butter, half a spoonful of flour, salt, and coarse pepper
2. Work everything together with a wooden spoon
3. Pour in one spoonful of vinegar and a little water
4. Place your sauce on the fire and stir until it is bound
5. Do not let it boil, so it does not smell like glue

title: Sauce Blanche aux Câpres et Anchois (White Sauce with Capers and Anchovies)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- butter the size of a walnut
- a pinch of flour
- one glass of bouillon
- one chopped anchovy
- fine capers
- salt
- coarse pepper
- two whole chives

## Directions

1. Melt butter in a saucepan and mix with a pinch of flour
2. Dilute with one glass of bouillon
3. Add chopped anchovy, fine capers, salt, coarse pepper, and two whole chives
4. Thicken the sauce over the fire
5. Remove the chives before serving

title: Sauce Bourgeoise (Bourgeois Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one glass of white wine
- as much meat juice
- two good pinches of very fine breadcrumbs
- butter the size of a walnut
- two shallots
- parsley
- chives
- salt
- coarse pepper
- a dash of verjuice

## Directions

1. Boil over low heat for half an hour with wine, meat juice, breadcrumbs, butter, shallots, parsley, and chives
2. Season with salt and coarse pepper
3. Add a dash of verjuice when serving

title: Sauce Brune Maigre (Lean Brown Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of butter
- four or five large carrots, cut into slices
- five or six large onions, sliced
- two or three parsley roots
- three bay leaves
- a pinch of thyme
- three cloves
- three carp, cut into pieces
- two pike of medium size, also cut into pieces
- salt and pepper
- half a bottle of white wine
- a little lean broth
- a large bouquet of parsley and chives
- two or three handfuls of mushrooms
- a blonde roux
- silk sieve (for straining)

## Directions

1. Put butter in the bottom of your saucepan
2. Add the sliced carrots, onions, parsley roots, bay leaves, thyme, and cloves
3. Add the carp and pike pieces, salt and pepper
4. Moisten with half a bottle of white wine and a little lean broth
5. Let the reduction attach to the bottom of the pan until it has enough color, meaning the bottom of your pan is brown
6. If you have lean broth, moisten with it; otherwise, add half a bottle of white wine to deglaze, and fill with water
7. Add a large bouquet of parsley and chives, and two or three handfuls of mushrooms
8. Let boil for an hour and a half
9. Pass the liquid through a silk sieve
10. Make a blonde roux
11. When it reaches the right point, pour the fish juice over it
12. Be careful to properly thin the roux when moistening to avoid lumps
13. Let the sauce boil for an hour, skimming and defatting it
14. Pass it through cheesecloth

title: Sauce de Cane (Cane Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quarter pound of butter
- one teaspoon of saffron or turmeric powder
- five small pods of hot chili pepper, minced or crushed
- five tablespoons of velouté
- a little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put the butter in a saucepan
2. Heat the butter until it simmers
3. When it is very hot, add five tablespoons of velouté
4. Stir the sauce well
5. Serve it without removing the fat and without straining through cheesecloth
6. Add a little grated nutmeg
7. Your sauce should be very hot

title: Sauce de Cane (Cane Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a quarter pound of butter
- One teaspoonful of Indian saffron powder, turmeric, or terra-mérita
- Five small cloves of enraged chili pepper
- Five spoonfuls of velouté
- A little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Put half a quarter pound of butter in a saucepan with one teaspoonful of Indian saffron powder, turmeric, or terra-mérita, and five small cloves of chili pepper, chopped or crushed
2. Heat your butter until it trembles
3. When well hot, add five spoonfuls of velouté
4. Stir your sauce well and serve it without removing the fat and without straining it through a sieve
5. Add a little grated nutmeg
6. Your sauce should be very hot

title: Sauce Hachée (Chopped Herb Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- a pinch of chopped parsley
- a pinch of shallots
- one spoonful of mushrooms, all chopped
- half a glass of vinegar
- a little coarse pepper
- four spoonfuls of Spanish sauce
- as much bouillon
- one spoonful of chopped capers
- two or three chopped cornichons
- one or two anchovies
- butter

## Directions

1. Throw the chopped parsley, shallots, and mushrooms into a saucepan along with half a glass of vinegar and a little coarse pepper
2. Place the saucepan on the fire and reduce the vinegar until almost none remains
3. Add four spoonfuls of Spanish sauce and as much bouillon
4. Reduce and degrease the sauce
5. When it reaches the proper point, add one spoonful of chopped capers and two or three chopped cornichons
6. Transfer the sauce to another saucepan and place it in a bain-marie
7. At the moment of serving, add one or two anchovies pounded and worked with butter, stirring well into the sauce

title: Sauce Hachée (Chopped Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- a pinch of chopped parsley
- a pinch of shallots
- 1 tablespoon mushrooms, all chopped
- one half-glass vinegar
- a little coarse pepper
- 4 tablespoons Spanish sauce
- as much broth

## Directions

1. Place the parsley, shallots, mushrooms, vinegar, and pepper in a saucepan
2. Place on heat and reduce the vinegar until almost none remains
3. Add the Spanish sauce and broth
4. Reduce and degrease the sauce
5. When it reaches the proper consistency, it is ready

title: Sauce Hollandaise (Hollandaise Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- reduced velouté
- coarse pepper
- a dash of tarragon vinegar
- butter the size of half an egg
- a little spinach juice

## Directions

1. Take reduced velouté in which you will put coarse pepper and a dash of tarragon vinegar
2. Keep your sauce warm
3. At the moment of serving, add butter the size of half an egg, which you will melt in your hot sauce
4. Then take a little spinach juice, which you will dilute in your sauce at the moment of serving

title: Sauce Indienne (Indian Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter, amount of half an egg
- Three cloves of hot small pepper, well crushed
- Powder of saffron from India or turmeric, amount of a thimble
- Four tablespoons of the preceding velvet sauce, without binding
- Two tablespoons of bouillon
- Butter, amount of an egg

## Directions

1. Heat the butter in a saucepan until it is slightly fried
2. Add the crushed hot pepper and turmeric powder
3. Add four tablespoons of the preceding velvet sauce without binding and two tablespoons of bouillon
4. Reduce and degrease your sauce
5. Transfer it to another saucepan and keep it warm in a bain-marie
6. At the moment of serving, add butter the size of an egg and stir well with your sauce
7. You can also bind it; it will be good with many things

title: Sauce Indienne (Indian Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- butter the size of half an egg
- three cloves of hot pepper, well crushed
- one thimble of Indian saffron powder or turmeric
- four spoonfuls of velvet sauce without being bound
- two spoonfuls of bouillon
- butter the size of an egg

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan butter the size of half an egg, three cloves of well-crushed hot pepper, and one thimble of Indian saffron powder or turmeric
2. Heat your butter until it is a little fried
3. Then add four spoonfuls of velvet sauce unbounded and two spoonfuls of bouillon
4. Reduce and degrease your sauce
5. Transfer it to another saucepan and keep it warm in a bain-marie
6. At the moment of serving, add butter the size of an egg, which you will stir well with your sauce

title: Sauce Italienne en Gras et en Maigre (Italian Sauce in Meat and Meatless Versions)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two good spoonfuls of fine oil
- chopped mushrooms
- a bouquet of parsley, chives, half a bay leaf, one clove of garlic, two cloves
- a pinch of flour
- white wine
- good bouillon
- a little coulis
- salt
- coarse pepper
- For meatless version: lean bouillon, more flour, two or three spoonfuls of onion juice

## Directions

1. Put oil and chopped mushrooms in a saucepan with the herbs and spices
2. Heat over the fire and add a pinch of flour
3. Moisten with white wine, good bouillon, and a little coulis
4. Season with salt and coarse pepper
5. Boil for half an hour
6. Skim off fat and remove the bouquet
7. For meatless version, use lean bouillon and more flour with onion juice instead of coulis

title: Sauce Mêlée (Mixed Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- A little point of garlic
- A little butter
- A pinch of flour
- Good bouillon
- Two gherkins
- Three egg yolks

## Directions

1. Take parsley, chives, mushrooms, and a little point of garlic, all well chopped, and pass them over the fire with a little butter
2. Add a pinch of flour and moisten with good bouillon
3. When your sauce is cooked and reduced to half, add two minced gherkins and a liaison of three egg yolks diluted with bouillon
4. Thicken your sauce and season it to good taste
5. Serve for whatever you judge appropriate

title: Sauce Piquante (Piquant Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Two large onions, sliced
- One carrot
- One parsnip
- Thyme, bay leaf, basil
- Two cloves
- Two shallots
- One clove of garlic
- Parsley, chives
- A good pinch of flour
- Broth
- One spoonful of vinegar
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Put butter in a saucepan with two large onions in slices, one carrot, one parsnip, a little thyme, bay leaf, basil, two cloves, two shallots, one clove of garlic, parsley, chives
2. Pass everything together over the fire until it is well colored
3. Add a good pinch of flour and moisten with broth and one spoonful of vinegar
4. Boil over very low heat, skim and pass through a sieve
5. Season with salt and coarse pepper
6. Use this sauce for everything that needs to be enhanced

title: Sauce Piquante (Piquant Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- a dash of vinegar
- two cloves of hot pepper
- a pinch of fine pepper
- one bay leaf
- a little thyme
- three spoonfuls of Spanish sauce
- two spoonfuls of bouillon
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Put into your saucepan a dash of vinegar, two cloves of hot pepper, a pinch of fine pepper, one bay leaf, and a little thyme
2. Reduce what is in your saucepan to half
3. Pour in three spoonfuls of Spanish sauce and two spoonfuls of bouillon
4. Reduce your sauce enough so it is like a clear pap
5. Add the necessary salt for it to be of good taste

title: Sauce Piquante Froide (Cold Piquant Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A good pinch of salad greens
- Half a clove of garlic
- Two shallots
- A little mustard
- Oil
- A dash of vinegar
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Pound very finely a good pinch of salad greens with half a clove of garlic and two shallots
2. Dilute everything with a little mustard, oil, a dash of vinegar, salt and coarse pepper

title: Sauce Pluche (Parsley Leaf Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Four or five spoonfuls of velouté
- Half a glass of white wine
- Coarse pepper
- A little parsley root
- Parsley leaves

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, put four or five spoonfuls of velouté and half a glass of white wine, coarse pepper, and a little parsley root cut into small strips
2. Cook the parsley root in your sauce
3. When it is sufficiently reduced, have parsley leaves broken into four or five pieces each
4. Blanch these parsley pieces in salted water
5. When the parsley is blanched, refresh it in cold water
6. At the moment of serving, add the blanched parsley to your sauce

title: Sauce Poivrade (Peppery Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- a large pinch of parsley leaves
- a few chives
- two bay leaves
- a little thyme
- a strong pinch of fine pepper
- one glass of vinegar
- a little butter
- two large spoonfuls of Spanish sauce
- one spoonful of bouillon

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan a large pinch of parsley leaves, a few chives, two bay leaves, a little thyme, a strong pinch of fine pepper, one glass of vinegar, and a little butter
2. Reduce the vinegar and seasoning until little remains in the saucepan
3. Pour in two large spoonfuls of Spanish sauce and one spoonful of bouillon
4. Reduce the sauce to its proper point and strain through a sieve without pressing
5. The vinegar and fine pepper should dominate the flavor

title: Sauce Provençale (Provençal Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two spoonfuls of fine oil
- chopped shallot and mushroom
- two whole cloves of garlic
- a pinch of flour
- bouillon
- one glass of white wine
- salt
- coarse pepper
- a bouquet of parsley and chives

## Directions

1. Put oil, chopped shallot and mushroom, and whole garlic cloves in a saucepan
2. Heat over the fire and add a pinch of flour
3. Moisten with bouillon and one glass of white wine
4. Season with salt and coarse pepper
5. Add a bouquet of parsley and chives
6. Boil over low heat for half an hour
7. Skim off fat leaving only enough oil so it appears oily and light
8. Remove the bouquet and garlic cloves
9. Serve as you judge appropriate

title: Sauce Ravigote Hachée (Chopped Ravigote Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Chervil
- Chives
- Salad burnet
- Tarragon
- Velouté
- Two spoonfuls of vinegar
- A little coarse pepper
- Butter

## Directions

1. Chop the chervil, chives, salad burnet, and tarragon finely, with the tarragon dominating
2. In velouté, add two spoonfuls of vinegar and a little coarse pepper
3. Keep your sauce hot
4. At the moment of serving, add your chopped ravigote with a small piece of fine butter
5. Stir your sauce to melt the butter and mix the herbs together
6. Ensure the sauce is well seasoned

title: Sauce Robert
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Twelve onions
- A good piece of butter
- One spoonful of flour, if no sauce is available
- One pot spoonful of bouillon
- One spoonful of vinegar
- Salt
- Pepper
- Two spoonfuls of mustard

## Directions

1. Cut twelve onions in half, removing the head and tail, and cut them into small dice
2. Put a good piece of butter in a saucepan with your onions and place the saucepan on a hot fire
3. When your onions are browned, if you do not have sauce, take one spoonful of flour and put it with your onions
4. Pour one pot spoonful of bouillon and one spoonful of vinegar, add salt and pepper
5. Make your sauce reduce until it is thick enough to coat
6. At the moment of serving, add two spoonfuls of mustard and use as needed

title: Sauce Robert Bourgeoise (Bourgeois Robert Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- one spoonful of flour
- three large finely chopped onions
- butter for cooking the onion
- bouillon
- salt
- coarse pepper
- a dash of vinegar and mustard

## Directions

1. Melt butter with one spoonful of flour in a saucepan and brown the flour over low heat
2. When it is a good color, add three large finely chopped onions and enough butter to cook the onion
3. Moisten with bouillon
4. Skim off fat and let boil for half an hour
5. When ready to serve, add salt, coarse pepper, a dash of vinegar and mustard
6. Use this sauce for fresh pork and turkey

title: Sauce Romaine (Roman Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound veal cut into dice
- one half-pound ham cut into dice
- 2 chicken thighs
- 3 or 4 carrots
- 4 onions
- 2 bay leaves
- 3 cloves
- a little basil
- one half-pound butter
- a little salt
- 12 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 1 pint cream

## Directions

1. Place the veal, ham, chicken thighs, carrots, onions, bay leaves, cloves, basil, butter, and salt in a saucepan over moderate heat
2. When the meat is slightly browned, crush the hard-boiled egg yolks finely
3. Add the crushed egg yolks to the saucepan with the meat
4. Stir the yolks with the meat when the butter is hot
5. Only put the saucepan on the heat after adding liquid
6. Stir the yolks well, then pour in the cream little by little so the yolks dissolve properly
7. Boil the entire mixture over moderate heat
8. Stir constantly so the sauce does not become lumpy
9. If the sauce is too thick, add cream or milk
10. When it has boiled for one hour and a half, strain through a sieve and use as indicated

title: Sauce Tomate Française (French Tomato Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fifteen to twenty tomatoes
- A little bouillon
- Salt
- Ground pepper
- Velouté
- Butter, amount of an egg

## Directions

1. Put fifteen to twenty tomatoes in a saucepan with a little bouillon and salt and ground pepper
2. Cook and reduce them
3. When your tomatoes are thick, pass them like a purée through muslin
4. If your sauce was too thin, put it in a saucepan and reduce it
5. Pour four or five tablespoons into a little velouté
6. At the moment of serving, add butter the size of an egg and let it melt in your sauce
7. Before serving, check if it is well seasoned and of good flavor

title: Sauce Tomate à l'Indienne (Indian-Style Tomato Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Five or six onions
- A little thyme
- A little bay leaf
- Twelve to fifteen tomatoes or love apples
- Bouillon or butter
- Salt
- Five or six cloves of hot small pepper
- A little powder of saffron from India or turmeric
- A glass of bouillon

## Directions

1. Cut five or six onions and put them in a saucepan
2. Add a little thyme and bay leaf
3. Add twelve to fifteen tomatoes
4. Add bouillon from the back of the pot, or a good piece of butter
5. Add salt, hot pepper cloves, turmeric powder, and a glass of bouillon
6. Put your tomatoes on the fire
7. Take care to stir from time to time, because this sauce is susceptible to burning
8. When you see that what is in your saucepan becomes a little thick, pass this sauce through muslin like a purée
9. Do not make it too thin

title: Sauce à l'Allemande (German Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a little coulis
- as much bouillon
- a pinch of blanched and chopped parsley
- two cooked poultry livers
- one anchovy and capers, all finely chopped
- butter the size of half an egg
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Put all ingredients in a saucepan
2. Thicken the sauce over the fire
3. Serve as you judge appropriate

title: Sauce à l'Anglaise (English Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two hard egg yolks
- one anchovy
- capers
- one glass of good bouillon
- salt
- coarse pepper
- butter the size of half an egg mixed with a pinch of flour

## Directions

1. Chop two hard egg yolks and put half in a saucepan with chopped anchovy and capers
2. Add one glass of good bouillon, salt, and coarse pepper
3. Add butter kneaded with a pinch of flour
4. Thicken the sauce over the fire
5. Pour over what you wish to serve and sprinkle the remaining chopped egg yolk on the meat

title: Sauce à l'Aurore (Aurora Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Velouté
- Two spoonfuls of lemon juice
- Coarse pepper
- A little grated nutmeg
- Four hard-boiled egg yolks

## Directions

1. In a saucepan, have prepared velouté with two spoonfuls of lemon juice, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
2. Pass four hard-boiled egg yolks through a sieve to form a vermicelli-like consistency
3. At the moment of serving, add the yolks to your hot sauce
4. Be careful not to let the sauce boil when the yolks are in it
5. Ensure the sauce is well seasoned

title: Sauce à l'Espagnole (Spanish Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- coulis
- one good glass of white wine
- as much good bouillon
- a bouquet of parsley and chives
- two cloves of garlic
- two cloves
- half a bay leaf
- a pinch of coriander
- two spoonfuls of oil
- one onion in slices
- one carrot root and half a parsnip
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Heat the sauce over very low heat for nearly two hours
2. Skim off the fat
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Season with a little salt and coarse pepper
5. Use as desired

title: Sauce à l'Orléans (Orléans Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three or four small spoonfuls of vinegar
- A little fine pepper
- A little shallots
- Butter, amount of half an egg
- Brown sauce, worked
- Four or five gherkins
- Three hard-boiled egg whites
- Four or five anchovies
- One cooked calf's head or sweetbread
- One spoonful of whole capers

## Directions

1. Put vinegar, fine pepper, shallots, and butter in a saucepan
2. Reduce everything together
3. Pour in four or five tablespoons of worked brown sauce
4. At the moment of serving, cut four or five gherkins into dice
5. Cut three hard-boiled egg whites in the same manner
6. Divide four or five anchovies in half to remove the bone, then cut into small squares
7. Cut a cooked calf's head or sweetbread into dice the same size as the gherkins
8. Add one spoonful of whole capers
9. At the moment of serving, put all this in your sauce and place it on the fire for an instant; it must not boil
10. You can also make this sauce with a small roux, which you moisten with a cooking stock or bouillon; season your sauce as before and add the same ingredients

title: Sauce à la Carpe (Carp Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a little lard
- a few slices of veal
- three or four pieces of carp
- one onion
- two shallots
- one carrot root
- one glass of white wine
- two good spoonfuls of coulis
- as much bouillon
- salt
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Place lard, veal slices, carp pieces, onion, shallots, and carrot in the bottom of a saucepan
2. Sweat over low heat for half an hour
3. When it begins to stick to the pan, moisten with one glass of white wine and two good spoonfuls of coulis
4. Add as much bouillon
5. Boil and reduce over low heat to the consistency of a sauce
6. Skim off fat and pass through a sieve
7. Season with salt and coarse pepper

title: Sauce à la Crème (Cream Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- a little butter
- parsley, chives, shallot, all chopped
- one whole clove of garlic
- a good pinch of flour
- cream or milk
- good butter
- a pinch of blanched and finely chopped parsley
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Put butter, chopped herbs, and whole garlic clove in a saucepan
2. Heat over the fire and add a good pinch of flour
3. Moisten with cream or milk
4. Boil for a quarter hour
5. Pass the sauce through a sieve into another saucepan
6. When ready to serve, add a little good butter, a pinch of finely chopped blanched parsley, and coarse pepper
7. Thicken the sauce over the fire
8. Use for all kinds of white entries

title: Sauce à la Marquise et Piquante (Marquise and Piquant Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two good pinches of fine breadcrumbs
- butter the size of a small coin
- one full spoonful of fine oil
- chopped shallot
- salt
- coarse pepper
- verjuice

## Directions

1. Put breadcrumbs, butter, oil, and chopped shallot in a saucepan
2. Season with salt and coarse pepper
3. Add enough verjuice to clarify

title: Sauce à la Marquise et Piquante (Marquise and Piquant Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two good pinches of fine bread crumbs
- Good butter the size of a small coin
- One spoonful of fine oil
- Minced shallot
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Verjuice, sufficient to thin the sauce

## Directions

1. Mix in a saucepan two good pinches of fine bread crumbs, butter the size of a small coin, one spoonful of fine oil, minced shallot, salt, coarse pepper, and verjuice sufficient to thin the sauce
2. Heat the sauce and thicken it by stirring with a spoon

title: Sauce à la Poivrade (Pepper Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter about half the size of an egg
- Two or three onions, sliced
- Carrots and parsnips cut into zests
- One clove of garlic
- Two shallots
- Two cloves
- One bay leaf
- Thyme, basil
- A good pinch of flour
- One glass of red wine
- One glass of water
- One spoonful of vinegar
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Put butter about half the size of an egg in a saucepan with two or three onions in slices, carrots and parsnips cut into zests, one clove of garlic, two shallots, two cloves, one bay leaf, thyme, basil
2. Pass everything over the fire until it begins to color
3. Add a good pinch of flour and moisten with one glass of red wine, one glass of water, one spoonful of vinegar
4. Boil for half an hour, skim and pass through a sieve
5. Add salt and coarse pepper
6. Use for everything that needs to be enhanced

title: Sauce à la Portugaise (Portuguese Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter pound of butter
- two raw egg yolks
- one spoonful of lemon juice
- coarse pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Put into your saucepan a quarter pound of butter and two raw egg yolks
2. Add one spoonful of lemon juice, coarse pepper, and salt to taste
3. Place it on a fire that is not too ardent
4. Be careful to stir it and not leave it, because your sauce would curdle
5. When it becomes a little warm, whisk it by taking sauce in your spoon and letting it fall back into the saucepan
6. Stir with force so that your butter binds with the yolks
7. Make this sauce only at the moment of serving
8. If it is too thick, add a little water

title: Sauce à la Provençale (Provençal Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Parsley
- Chives
- One clove of garlic
- Mushrooms
- Oil
- Half a setier of white wine
- A little broth
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Chop parsley, chives, one clove of garlic and mushrooms
2. Put everything in a saucepan with a little oil and pass over the fire
3. Moisten with half a setier of white wine and a little broth, salt and coarse pepper
4. Reduce to the consistency of a sauce
5. Skim before serving

title: Sauce à la Ravigote (Ravigote Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One glass of excellent bouillon
- Half a coffee spoon of vinegar
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Good butter the size of a walnut, mixed with flour
- Two pinches of salad garnish: a little tarragon, chives, chervil, pimpinella, watercress

## Directions

1. Put in a saucepan one glass of excellent bouillon, half a coffee spoon of vinegar, salt, coarse pepper, and good butter the size of a walnut mixed with flour
2. With two pinches of salad garnish ingredients like tarragon, chives, chervil, pimpinella, and watercress, boil these herbs for a moment in the sauce
3. Press them well and chop them very fine
4. Put them in the sauce and thicken it on the fire to serve on whatever you wish

title: Sauce à la Reine (Queen's Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Several mushrooms
- One onion
- One carrot
- One parsnip
- Half a clove of garlic
- Parsley, chives
- A pinch of flour
- One large glass of broth
- As much white wine
- Half a setier of milk
- Bread crumb, about half the size of an egg
- Salt
- Coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Put butter in a saucepan with several mushrooms, one onion, one carrot, one parsnip, half a clove of garlic, parsley, chives
2. Pass everything over the fire and add a pinch of flour
3. Moisten with one large glass of broth and as much white wine
4. Boil for one hour, skim and pass through a sieve
5. Boil half a setier of milk with bread crumb about half the size of an egg
6. When the bread has absorbed all the milk, pass it through a sieve by pressing hard with a spoon
7. Put it into your sauce with salt and coarse pepper

title: Sauce à la Rocambole (Rocambole Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a glass of white wine
- As much broth
- Two or three spoonfuls of coulis
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Five or six rocamboles, crushed

## Directions

1. Put half a glass of white wine in a saucepan with as much broth and two or three spoonfuls of coulis, salt and coarse pepper
2. Boil for a quarter hour and add five or six crushed rocamboles before serving

title: Sauce à la Rémoulade (Remoulade Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- One shallot
- Parsley
- Chives
- A point of garlic
- One anchovy
- Capers
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- A little mustard
- Oil
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Put one shallot, parsley, chives, a point of garlic, one anchovy and capers in a saucepan, all finely chopped, with salt and coarse pepper
2. Dilute with a little mustard, oil and vinegar

title: Sauce à la Sultane (Sultane Sauce)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one chopine of bouillon
- one glass of white wine
- two slices of lemon with skin removed
- two cloves
- one clove of garlic
- half a bay leaf
- parsley
- chives
- one onion and one carrot root
- salt
- coarse pepper
- one hard egg yolk
- a pinch of blanched and finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Put all ingredients in a saucepan
2. Boil for one and a half hours over low heat and reduce to the consistency of a sauce
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Add a little salt, coarse pepper, one hard egg yolk chopped, and a pinch of finely chopped blanched parsley

title: Saucisses (Sausages)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork meat, very tender
- One pound of lard for every pound of meat
- Parsley
- Chives
- Aromatic herbs, pounded
- Spices
- Salt
- Pepper
- Intestines
- Wine such as Champagne, Rhine wine, Madeira, Malvoisie, or Constance (optional)

## Directions
