1. Take the tender meat of pork; put one pound of lard for every pound of meat that you will chop fairly fine
2. Add parsley, chives, a little pounded aromatic herbs and a little spices, salt, and pepper; mix everything well together, and put your meat in the intestines
3. One can add wines inside, such as a glass of Champagne, Rhine wine, Madeira, Malvoisie, Constance; this is a matter of taste

title: Saucisson d'une Épaule de Mouton (Sausage of a Shoulder of Mutton)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of mutton
- godiveau farce
- lardons
- ham
- parsley
- chives
- garlic clove
- cloves
- bay leaf
- lardons
- parsnip
- salt
- pepper
- bouillon
- coulis

## Directions

1. Bone a shoulder of mutton and fill it with a godiveau farce the thickness of a small finger.
2. Cover the farce with slices of lardons and ham cut into fillets.
3. Put a little farce on top, just to hold them in place.
4. Roll the shoulder and wrap it tightly in linen.
5. Cook it in a little bouillon with a bouquet of parsley, chives, a garlic clove, three cloves, a bay leaf, lardons, parsnip, salt, and pepper.
6. When cooking is done, degrease the sauce and pass it through a sieve.
7. Reduce it if it is too thin.
8. Put it on ice with coulis to bind it.
9. Serve the shoulder glazed.

title: Saucisson de Tranches de Bœuf (Beef Sausage Roll)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef cut the width of two hands and thick as two fingers
- Beef fat
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- Two shallots
- Basil leaves
- Salt
- Pepper
- Four egg yolks
- Bouillon
- A glass of white wine
- An onion
- Two cloves
- A carrot
- A parsnip

## Directions

1. Cut the beef in half lengthwise while keeping the same width
2. Flatten it by beating
3. Trim the edges to make them even
4. Mince the trimmings with beef fat, parsley, chives, mushrooms, two shallots, and basil leaves, all chopped very finely
5. Season with salt and pepper
6. Bind the filling with four egg yolks and spread it on the beef slices
7. Roll them in the shape of a sausage and tie with string
8. Cook in a casserole with a little bouillon, a glass of white wine, salt, pepper, an onion, two cloves, a carrot, and a parsnip
9. Cook on a low fire
10. Pass the cooking liquid through a sieve and remove the fat
11. Reduce it to the consistency of a sauce without strong flavor
12. Serve the sauce over the sausages
13. You can also serve them with vegetable ragout as you see fit; if you wish to serve them cold as a side dish, reduce the sauce by letting it boil with the sausages until almost no fat remains, let cool with what remains, and serve on a napkin

title: Saucisson de Tranches de Bœuf (Beef Slice Sausage)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one piece of beef the width of two hands and thickness of two fingers
- beef fat
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- two shallots
- basil leaves

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef cut the width of two hands and thick as two fingers
2. Cut it in two while keeping it the same width
3. Pound it to flatten it further
4. Trim the edges by cutting them slightly to make them even
5. Chop the trimmed edges with beef fat, parsley, chives, mushrooms, two shallots, and a few basil leaves, all chopped very fine

title: Saumon au Bleu (Salmon au Bleu)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- Wine, eight or ten bottles
- Carrots, seven or eight
- Onions, sliced
- Cloves, four
- Bay leaves, six
- Thyme, a little
- Salt
- Parsley, a handful in sprigs
- Butter, a good piece
- Flour, three tablespoons
- Fish or veal stock, one tablespoon each of court-bouillon and blond
- Pepper
- Gherkins, diced
- Anchovies, eight or ten
- Capers
- Pickled nasturtiums

## Directions

1. Empty your salmon without cutting the belly; wash and dry it well.
2. Place it in a fish kettle; add eight or ten bottles of wine, seven or eight carrots, onions cut into slices, four cloves, six bay leaves, a little thyme, salt, and a handful of parsley in sprigs; your fish must be immersed in the court-bouillon.
3. Simmer for two hours.
4. When you want to serve it, let it drain; place a napkin on your plate and the salmon on top, with parsley around it.
5. If you serve it as a relevé, put a good piece of butter in a saucepan; mix three tablespoons of flour and one tablespoon each of the fish court-bouillon and fish or veal stock into it.
6. Place your sauce on the fire, stirring until it boils, then add pepper and reduce it by half.
7. Strain it through fine silk into a saucepan.
8. Cut gherkins into dice, along with eight or ten anchovies, capers, and pickled nasturtiums, and put them in your sauce; keep it warm without boiling.
9. Mask your salmon with this sauce; you can also use anchovy butter instead of gherkins if you prefer.

title: Saumon en Caisses (Salmon in Paper Cases)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Two slices of fresh salmon, about half a finger thick
- Fine oil
- Parsley
- Chives
- Mushrooms
- Half a clove of garlic
- One shallot
- Half a bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- White paper
- Breadcrumbs
- Lemon juice
- Spanish sauce (optional)

## Directions

1. Marinate the salmon slices for one hour with fine oil, parsley, chives, finely chopped mushrooms, half a clove of garlic, one shallot, half a bay leaf, thyme, and basil chopped like powder, salt, and coarse pepper.
2. Make a paper case of white paper the size of the two salmon slices.
3. Rub the undersides of the paper with oil and place them on a plate.
4. Put the salmon in the case with all its seasoning.
5. Coat the top with breadcrumbs.
6. Cook in the oven, or alternatively place the plate on a small stove with a pie dish cover and fire on top.
7. When the salmon is cooked and the top is a beautiful golden color, add lemon juice when serving.
8. If you wish to add Spanish sauce, degrease the salmon cooking liquid before putting it on.

title: Saumon en salade (Salmon Salad)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked salmon
- Four tablespoons of good vinegar
- Two spoonfuls of melted aspic
- Ten spoonfuls of oil
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Chopped ravigote
- Lettuce hearts
- Croutons
- Gherkins
- Capers
- Anchovies

## Directions

1. When your salmon is cooked, separate it into pieces
2. Put it in your salad sauce
3. Pour into a saucepan four tablespoons of good vinegar, two spoonfuls of melted aspic, ten spoonfuls of oil, salt, coarse pepper, and chopped ravigote
4. Toss your salmon in this sauce and arrange it on your plate
5. Pour your sauce over it
6. Put lettuce hearts cut in quarters around it
7. Decorate your salad with croutons, gherkins, capers, and anchovies

title: Saumon Sauce aux Câpres (Salmon with Caper Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon steak
- Oil
- Parsley
- Chives
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter sauce
- Capers

## Directions

1. Have a salmon steak; marinate it with oil, parsley, chives, salt, and pepper.
2. If the steak is thick, it will take an hour to cook.
3. Arrange it on your plate; add a butter sauce on top with capers that you sprinkle over the salmon.

title: Saumon à la Génoise (Salmon Genoese Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- Dark red wine
- Carrots
- Onions
- Cloves
- Bay leaves
- Thyme
- Salt
- Butter, a good piece
- Flour, two tablespoons

## Directions

1. Cook your salmon as in the preceding recipe; moisten it with dark red wine and add the seasoning of the preceding recipe without salting too much.
2. Put a good piece of butter in your saucepan with two tablespoons of flour, which you will mix with the butter.
3. Pass the court-bouillon through a silk sieve and combine it with the butter.
4. Place it on the fire and stir until your sauce boils; reduce it by half.
5. Skim and degrease it; then strain it through fine silk; keep it warm without boiling.
6. At the moment of serving your fish, drain it and arrange it plainly on the plate; then mask it with your sauce.

title: Saumon à la Mayonnaise (Salmon with Mayonnaise)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon steak
- Cold mayonnaise
- Aspic for glazing
- Croutons
- Capers
- Gherkins

## Directions

1. When your salmon steak is cooked, you can serve it whole or in pieces
2. Arrange the pieces on your plate
3. Mask them with cold mayonnaise
4. Take care to add aspic so it will glaze
5. Decorate the edge of the plate with croutons, capers, and gherkins

title: Sauté de Filet de Bœuf (Sautéed Beef Filet)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- filet de bœuf (beef filet)
- sel (salt)
- gros poivre (coarse pepper)
- beurre (butter)
- sauce espagnole (espagnole sauce)
- sauce piquante (piquant sauce)
- sauce tomate (tomato sauce)
- beurre d'anchois (anchovy butter)
- roux (roux)
- parures de sauté (trimmings from sauté)
- carotte (carrot)
- deux oignons (two onions)
- clou de girofle (clove)
- feuille de laurier (bay leaf)
- fond ou bouillon (broth or stock)
- cornichons (gherkins)
- vinaigre (vinegar)

## Directions

1. Cut your beef filet in four lengthwise
2. Cut your pieces into short meat of the thickness of three lines
3. Flatten them and make them round, about the size of a six-franc coin or slightly larger
4. Be careful when rounding them not to leave tough skin
5. Your pieces should be roughly the same size
6. Arrange them flat in a sauté pan or something that can replace it
7. When all your pieces are flattened, trimmed, and arranged in your pan, season them with salt and coarse pepper
8. Melt a good piece of butter and pour it over them
9. At the moment of serving, place your pan over a hot fire; when they are stiffened on one side, stiffen them on the other
10. Test with your finger if your piece is cooked; if too soft, leave it longer
11. When your pieces are cooked, arrange them in a miroton around the platter, putting the smallest in the middle
12. You may pour your sauté with a well-worked and well-corseted espagnole sauce
13. You may mix with a piquant sauce a tomato sauce or anchovy butter sauce
14. If you lack sauce, make a light roux in which you put the trimmings of your sauté, a carrot cut in small dice, two onions, a clove, a bay leaf
15. Pass everything through your roux, which you moisten with a little stock or a little broth
16. Cook and reduce this sauce for three-quarters of an hour
17. Pass it through cheesecloth
18. Add a little coarse pepper, gherkins, a dash of vinegar
19. In short, give your sauce whatever taste you want
20. You may also add anchovy butter or serve it quite simply, ensuring it has good seasoning and good taste

title: Sauté de Filets de Brochets (Sautéed Pike Fillets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fillets from three medium pike
- Parsley
- Scallion
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Butter
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the fillets from three medium pike
2. Cut them into somewhat long squares and trim them; be sure they are all the same size
3. Arrange them in your sauté pan; add finely chopped parsley and scallion, a little grated nutmeg, salt, and coarse pepper
4. Warm a good piece of butter and pour it over your fillets
5. At the moment of serving, put them on the fire; as soon as the butter boils, turn them over
6. It takes only an instant to cook them; lean your sauté pan to the side and your fillets to the height so that your butter drains off
7. Arrange them on your platter and put Italian sauce mixed with liaison on top

title: Sauté de Filets de Bécasses (Sautéed Woodcock Fillets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fillets from four woodcocks
- A good piece of butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- A little powdered rosemary
- Half a glass of white wine
- Bay leaf
- Clove
- Six spoonfuls of espagnole sauce
- A glass of bouillon
- Juice of one lemon

## Directions

1. Remove the fillets from four woodcocks, trim them, and place them in your sauté pan
2. Warm a good piece of butter and pour it over them; add salt, coarse pepper, and a little powdered rosemary
3. When ready to serve, place your fillets on a hot fire; when they are stiffened on one side, turn them; leave them on the fire just a moment so they are not overcooked
4. Drain them and arrange them in crowns with a crouton between each
5. Sweat the trimmings of your woodcocks with half a glass of white wine, a bay leaf, and a clove, and let your sauce reduce to a glaze
6. When reduced, add half a glass of white wine, a glass of bouillon, and six spoonfuls of espagnole sauce; reduce everything by half
7. Strain your sauce through cloth and place it under your fillets
8. If you do not have espagnole sauce, add a glass of white wine and three glasses of bouillon to your trimmings; let it simmer and make a light roux that you will moisten with your reduction; add salt and coarse pepper, reduce your sauce, skim it, and strain it through cloth
9. When serving, add the juice of one lemon and check that it is well-seasoned

title: Sauté de Filets de Bécasses à la Provençale (Sautéed Woodcock Fillets Provençal Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fillets from woodcocks
- Salt
- Pepper
- Four spices
- Oil
- Garlic
- Woodcock trimmings
- Parsley
- Cloves
- Shallots
- Bay leaf
- Flour
- White wine
- Bouillon
- Lemon juice
- Lemon zest

## Directions

1. Prepare your fillets as the previous ones; season them with salt, pepper, and four spices; cover them with oil and add a clove of pounded garlic
2. Pour oil in a casserole; add your woodcock trimmings and let them brown with a pinch of parsley leaves, a clove of garlic, two cloves, six shallots, and a bay leaf
3. When your trimmings are well browned, add about a spoonful of flour; moisten them with a glass of white wine and three glasses of bouillon
4. Reduce your sauce by half, skim it, and strain it through cloth
5. When ready to serve, sauté your fillets and arrange them in crowns with a glazed crouton between each
6. Add the juice of one lemon and a little zest to your sauce and pour it under your fillets

title: Sauté de Filets de Cailles (Sauté of Quail Fillets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Quail fillets
- Butter
- Croutons
- Spanish sauce
- Meat glaze

## Directions

1. Take the fillets of twelve quail, pare them, and arrange them in a sauté pan.
2. Make a piece of butter lukewarm and pour it over your fillets.
3. At the moment of serving, put them over a hot fire; four or five minutes are sufficient to cook them.
4. Drain them and arrange them in crowns on your plat with a glazed crouton between each fillet.
5. Make a circle around the plat, then in the interior.
6. If you serve them as an appetizer, six quail are sufficient.
7. Sauce them with a clear Spanish sauce in which you put a glace about the size of half a walnut.

title: Sauté de Filets de Lapereau aux Truffes (Sauté of Young Rabbit Fillets with Truffles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Ten or twelve young rabbit fillets
- Truffles
- Three quarternons of butter
- Velouté sauce
- Croûtons

## Directions

1. Raise ten or twelve fillets of young rabbit
2. Remove the sinew skin
3. Cut them into round slices, all approximately equal
4. With the blade of the knife, flatten these slices
5. Cut the angles to give them a round or oval shape
6. Place each piece in your sautoir or tourtière, continuing for all your fillets
7. When all are trimmed and arranged, peel and trim truffles
8. Cut them into equal slices
9. Put them on the fillets until they are covered
10. Warm three quarternons of butter and pour it over
11. At the moment of serving, put your sauté on a strong fire
12. When your pieces are stiffened on one side, turn them lightly with a spoon
13. Position your sautoir so your butter separates from the sauté
14. When drained, put it in a velouté reduced at the moment of serving and arrange it on the plate
15. Put also a border of croûton

title: Sauté de filets de Lapereau à la Périgord (Sautéed Young Rabbit Fillets Périgord Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- ten fillets of young rabbit
- truffles
- salt
- coarse pepper
- three quarterons of butter
- nutmeg
- game stock
- two egg yolks
- butter the size of half an egg
- croutons

## Directions

1. Trim and prepare ten fillets, removing the nerve-like skin
2. Cut the pieces one and a half inches long
3. Make an incision at equal distance in which you will place a half-circle of truffles, putting six pieces of truffle in each piece of fillet, ensuring they penetrate slightly into the flesh
4. When the ten fillets are thus prepared, put the prepared pieces in your sauté pan
5. Season with salt and coarse pepper
6. Warm three quarterons of butter in which you will grate a little nutmeg, and pour it over your fillets
7. At the moment of serving, put your sauté pan on an ardent stove; when your fillets are stiffened on one side, turn them to the other; leave them only an instant
8. Test by pressing your finger on top; if your piece resists, it is cooked
9. Tilt your sauté pan so the butter goes to one side with a spoon
10. Put your fillets on top; when they are drained, place them in a casserole
11. Pour in a velouté worked with rabbit stock
12. Bind your sauce with two egg yolks and butter the size of half an egg that you will melt while binding your sauce
13. Pass it through a sieve over your fillets
14. Toss them and dress them immediately on your dish so your sauce does not separate
15. Check that it is properly salted and put croutons around the dish

title: Sauté de Filets de Lapereau à la Périgueux (Sauté of Young Rabbit Fillets à la Périgueux)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Ten rabbit fillets
- Truffles

## Directions

1. Raise and trim ten fillets
2. Remove the sinew skin
3. Cut your pieces one and a half inches long
4. Make an incision at equal distance

title: Sauté de Filets de Lièvre ou Levreau (Sautéed Hare or Young Hare Fillets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- ten fillets of hare or young hare
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- butter
- white wine
- espagnole sauce
- flour

## Directions

1. Ten fillets are sufficient to make a sauté
2. Cut them into flat slices and flatten them with the blade of your knife
3. Trim them by cutting the angles and rounding them as well as possible
4. When your piece is trimmed, place it in the sauté pan or on a hollow cooking vessel successively until the end of your fillets
5. Season them with fine salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
6. Put warm butter on top
7. At the moment of serving, place your sauté pan over a hot fire
8. Take care to move it to detach your pieces of fillets
9. When they are stiffened on one side, turn them to the other side
10. It takes only a moment for your sauté to be cooked
11. Then put it in a saucepan
12. Remove the butter that is in your sauté pan without removing the juice from the fillets
13. Add a glass of white wine to detach what is stuck to the sauté pan
14. Add four spoons of espagnole sauce
15. Reduce everything by half and pass your sauce through a cloth onto your sauté
16. Take care to drain the juice from the fillet so that your sauce is not too thin, for the sauce must cling to your sauté
17. If you do not have sauce, heat the butter that is in the sauté pan, and if there is too much, remove half of it
18. When you see that what is on the bottom of the sauté pan begins to stick, add a spoonful of flour that you will mix with your butter
19. Add a glass of white wine, a glass of bouillon, a bay leaf, and a little thyme
20. Reduce everything by half and pass your sauce through a cloth onto your sauté
21. Before passing it, see if it has good seasoning

title: Sauté de Filets de Maquereaux (Sautéed Mackerel Fillets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Mackerel fillets
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Parsley
- Chives, finely chopped
- Butter
- Velouté
- Egg yolks
- Lemon juice
- Ravigote, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Lift the fillets from your mackerel in their entirety
2. Slide your knife between the skin and flesh to remove the skin
3. Trim your fillets and place them in a sauté pan with salt, coarse pepper, parsley, and finely chopped chives
4. Warm a good piece of butter and pour it over them
5. At the moment of service, place them on the fire and stir them to prevent sticking
6. When they are slightly heated on one side by a few bubbles of the butter, carefully turn them to avoid breaking them
7. Ensure they are cooked and arrange on the plate
8. In a saucepan, place a good piece of butter, a spoonful of velouté, three egg yolks, the juice of two lemons, salt, coarse pepper, and finely chopped ravigote
9. Turn your sauce constantly until it is bound; do not let it boil as it would curdle
10. Check the seasoning and pour it over your fillets

title: Sauté de Filets de Merlans (Sautéed Whiting Fillets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Whiting fillets
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare the fillets as you would mackerel fillets
2. Sauté them with the same seasoning
3. Arrange them on a platter and mask them with Italian sauce

title: Sauté de Filets de Merlans (Sautéed Whiting Fillets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Whiting fillets
- Seasoning
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare your fillets as those of mackerel; sauté them with the same seasoning
2. Arrange them on a plate and mask with an Italian sauce (see Italian Sauce)

title: Sauté de Filets de Perdreaux (Sautéed Partridge Fillets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Six partridges of the same size
- Three-quarters of a pound of butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Spanish sauce with game essence
- Bouillon
- White wine
- Flour
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Take six partridges of the same size
2. Remove the fillets and trim them
3. Remove the tough skin
4. Place them skin-side down on the table
5. Slide your knife blade between the skin and the flesh from one end to the other, always keeping the blade inclined toward the skin so as not to remove too much flesh
6. Put your fillets in your sauté pan
7. Season with salt and coarse pepper
8. Heat three-quarters of a pound of butter and pour it over them
9. Ten minutes before serving, put them over a hot fire
10. When they are stiffened on one side, turn them over to the other side
11. Leave them only an instant
12. Tilt your pan so that the butter separates from your fillets
13. Arrange them in a crown on your platter with a glazed crouton between each fillet
14. For sauce, use Spanish sauce worked with game essence
15. If you have no sauce or game essence, attach the juice that your fillets have released into your pan
16. When the base is blonde, remove half the butter
17. Add one spoonful of flour and mix it in
18. Add a glass of bouillon or a little juice and half a glass of white wine
19. Make your sauce bubble a few times
20. Strain through a fine sieve
21. Add a little coarse pepper and lemon juice
22. Check that it is properly salted
23. Put it under your fillets

title: Sauté de Filets de Perdreaux aux Truffes (Sautéed Partridge Fillets with Truffles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridge fillets
- Truffles
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Spanish sauce with game essence

## Directions

1. Prepare your fillets as in the previous recipe
2. When arranged in the sauté pan, clean your truffles
3. Cut them in thick round slices of one and a half lines thick
4. Arrange them on your fillets with a little salt and coarse pepper
5. At the moment of service, sauté them over a hot fire
6. Drain them and arrange in a crown with a glazed crouton between each fillet
7. Put your truffles in the same sauce as the previous recipe
8. Sauté them in it and place them in the middle of your fillets

title: Sauté de Filets de Poulets au Suprême (Sautéed Chicken Fillets in Suprême)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fillets of five fat chickens
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Finely chopped and washed parsley
- A good piece of butter
- Velouté, reduced
- Glazed croutons
- Fresh butter

## Directions

1. Take the fillets of five fat chickens and trim them as described for chicken fillets
2. Arrange them in the sauté pan or on a tourte
3. Season them with salt, coarse pepper, and finely chopped washed parsley
4. Warm a good piece of butter and pour it over them
5. At the moment of serving, put them on a hot fire
6. When they are stiffened on one side, turn them on the other
7. Remove them an instant after
8. Separate them from the butter
9. Dress them in crowns on a platter with a glazed crouton between each fillet
10. Have velouté reduced into which you will dip your fillet
11. Before dressing it, put the bottom of your sauté into it
12. After having removed the butter, put in a piece about the size of half an egg of fresh butter which you will let melt in your velouté

title: Sauté de filets de Truites (Sautéed Trout Fillets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Trout fillets
- Finely chopped and washed parsley
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Grated nutmeg
- Good butter
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Lift the fillets from the trouts and trim them
2. Remove the skin from the scale side
3. Cut your fillets into small slices at least the size of a six-franc coin
4. Trim your pieces so they are of equal size and thickness
5. Arrange them in your sauté pan
6. Sprinkle with finely chopped and washed parsley, salt, coarse pepper, and grated nutmeg
7. Warm a good piece of butter and pour it over the pieces
8. At the moment of service, put your sauté pan on a hot fire
9. When the sauté is stiffened on one side, turn it
10. Leave it only an instant on the fire
11. Arrange them in a miroton around the plate and place the rest in the middle
12. Add a lean or rich Italian sauce

title: Sauté de Filets à la Viard (Sautéed Teal Fillets Viard Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Fillets of five teals
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Pinch of ground aromatic herbs
- Butter
- White wine
- One spoonful of consommé
- Four spoonfuls of Spanish sauce
- Zest of bigarade
- Juice of bigarade
- Glaze the size of a walnut
- Flour
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Take the fillets of five teals
2. Remove the nerves found on the side of the skin
3. Cut them into aiguillettes (cut the fillet into four or five lengthwise, all the same thickness)
4. Place them in a sauté pan
5. Season with salt, coarse pepper, a small pinch of ground aromatic herbs
6. Warm a piece of butter and pour it over
7. Sweat your scraps with a glass of white wine
8. When they have reduced to a glaze, put in a full spoonful of consommé
9. Simmer for one hour and pass your gravy through a silk sieve
10. Put into a saucepan a full four spoonfuls of Spanish sauce
11. Add your gravy and let it reduce by half
12. Pass it through a cloth
13. Add a bit of bigarade zest and the juice of the bigarade, and glaze the size of a walnut
14. Heat your sauce well without boiling it
15. At the moment of service, put your sauté pan on a hot stove
16. When your fillets are stiffened on all sides and you judge them cooked, tilt your sauté pan and put your fillets on top so you do not take butter with the spoon
17. Put them on the plate in the shape of a bush and sprinkle them with your sauce

title: Sauté de Foie de Veau (Sautéed Calf's Liver)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One calf's liver
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- A little spice
- Fine herbs if desired
- Half a pound of butter
- One glass of Champagne wine
- Four tablespoons of prepared Spanish sauce
- Or one tablespoon of flour, two glasses of white wine, and a little broth

## Directions

1. Cut the liver in half lengthwise
2. Cut pieces across the width, two or three lines thick
3. Cut off the angles and shape the liver pieces like a matelote crouton, making them oval and pointed on one side
4. Season the prepared pieces with salt, coarse pepper, a little spice, and fine herbs if desired
5. Put half a pound of butter in your sauté pan or cooking vessel and warm it gently
6. Arrange your pieces in the sauté pan
7. At the moment of serving, place it on high heat
8. When the pieces are stiffened on one side, turn them to the other
9. Press your finger on the piece; when it is somewhat firm, remove the liver from the fire
10. Deposit your sauté into a saucepan
11. Remove the butter from your sauté pan and put in a full glass of Champagne wine and four tablespoons of prepared Spanish sauce
12. Reduce this sauce by half and strain it through a fine sieve over your sauté
13. Keep it warm without letting it boil
14. When serving, arrange your pieces in coronets and pour your sauce over them
15. If you do not have Spanish sauce, put one tablespoon of flour in your sauté pan where the butter from your sauté remains; when the flour is thinned out, add two glasses of white wine and a little broth; if the sauce is too thick, season with salt and pepper; when it is somewhat reduced, pour it over your liver

title: Sauté de Noix de Veau (Sautéed Veal Cushion)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One veal cushion
- Three quarter-pounds of butter
- Fine salt and coarse pepper
- Parsley and chives, finely chopped
- Velouté sauce
- Two eggs

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the veal cushion
2. Cut the cushion into long pieces, then into thin slices slightly larger than a small coin and one and a half lines thick
3. Beat each small piece with the back of your knife and arrange them in your sauté pan
4. When they are well rounded and the cushion is cut, trimmed, and arranged, warm three quarter-pounds of butter and pour it over the veal pieces seasoned with fine salt, coarse pepper, parsley and chives finely chopped
5. Just before serving, place the sauté pan on high heat
6. When the pieces are slightly heated on one side, turn them; they should barely see the fire
7. When the sauté is cooked, remove it from the butter and put it in a casserole
8. Make a reduction of slightly more velouté than usual
9. Add the juice thrown off by the sauté
10. Mix in a liaison of two eggs
11. When the sauce is bound, strain it through a cloth over the sauté
12. Toss it so it takes the sauce and dress it on the plate

title: Sautéed Lamb Cutlets
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb cutlets
- Butter
- Ice
- Espagnole sauce, worked
- Consommé

## Directions

1. Cut and trim your lamb cutlets as you would mutton cutlets
2. Season them after trimming well
3. Place them in a sautoir or shallow pan
4. Add enough melted butter for them to bathe in it
5. At the moment of serving, place them on high heat
6. When the butter has well heated them on one side, turn them to the other
7. When you feel they are firm to the touch, remove the butter and add a good piece of ice
8. Toss them in your melted ice
9. Arrange them in a crown
10. Pour two deglazing spoonfuls of worked espagnole sauce into your sautoir, and one spoonful of consommé
11. Stir your sauce to detach the ice
12. Strain it through tamis cloth and pour it over your cutlets

title: Sautéed Pheasant Fillets (Sauté de filets de Faisan)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Eight pheasant fillets
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Butter
- Spanish sauce
- Game stock
- Glazed croutons
- Truffles (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove and trim eight pheasant fillets; if the skin side still has a nervous skin, place that side on the table and slide your knife between the skin and meat so none remains
2. Trim the edges of your fillets so they are correct; place them in your sauté pan with a little salt and coarse pepper
3. Remove the nerves from the tenderloins and place them the same way
4. Warm about three-quarters of a pound of butter and pour it over them
5. At serving time, place your fillets on a hot fire; when they are stiffened on one side, turn them to the other; leave them only a moment; touch them with your finger; if the meat resists, remove them
6. Arrange them in crowns, one fillet, one glazed crouton, and so on; place your tenderloins in the middle
7. For sauce, use a Spanish sauce worked with game stock; if you do not have any, remove the butter in your sauté pan; leave the juice your fillets have given and put your sauce in it
8. Make it boil five or six times; then pour it under your fillets; you can put a sauté of truffles in the middle over your tenderloins

title: Sautéed Pheasant Fillets with Truffles (Sauté de filets de Faisan aux truffes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant fillets
- Truffles
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Nutmeg
- Butter
- Game stock
- Egg yolks
- Velouté sauce
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Remove your fillets and take off the nervous skin
2. Cut your fillet in two lengthwise; slice your pieces two lines thick; give them an agreeable shape
3. Put them in your sauté pan or tart pan with salt, coarse pepper, a little grated nutmeg; warm some butter and pour it over them
4. Remove the nerve from the tenderloins and put them with your sauté
5. Peel your truffles; cut them into rounds one and a half lines thick; arrange your truffle rounds on your fillets
6. At serving time, put your sauté on the fire; turn your fillets; when they are stiffened on one side turn them to the other; leave them another moment on the fire
7. Tilt your sauté pan and put your sauté high so the butter separates from your sauté
8. Take velouté reduced with game stock; make a liaison with two egg yolks
9. If you wish, sauté your sauté in your sauce and dress it right away so your sauce does not break; put croutons around your plate

title: Scrambled Eggs, Lean Style (Oeufs Brouillés en Maigre)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Cream
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Put eggs in a pan with a little cream instead of cooking juice.
2. Season to taste.
3. Cook over heat, stirring constantly with a small stick with two or three branches.
4. Serve immediately when cooked.

title: Scrambled Eggs, Natural Style (Oeufs Brouillés au Naturel)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter
- Cooking juice (coulis)
- Salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Put eggs in a pan with a little butter and two spoonfuls of cooking juice.
2. Season to taste.
3. Cook over heat, stirring constantly with a small stick with two or three branches.
4. Serve immediately when cooked.

title: Selle de Mouton braisée (Braised Saddle of Mutton)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- half a saddle of mutton from the first rib to the leg
- salt
- pepper
- lard strips for barding
- three carrots
- four onions
- two cloves
- bay leaf
- thyme
- parsley and chives bouquet
- one tablespoon of bouillon
- buttered paper

## Directions

1. Debone the saddle
2. Season the interior with salt and pepper
3. Roll it to present a long square shape
4. Tie it with string
5. Place lard strips in a casserole
6. Put the saddle in the casserole with three carrots, four onions, two cloves, a bay leaf, a little thyme, and a bouquet of parsley and chives
7. Cover the saddle with lard strips
8. Add the trimmings from the saddle
9. Add one tablespoon of bouillon and a round of buttered paper
10. Cook the saddle for two and a half hours over and under fire
11. At the moment of serving, drain it, remove the string, remove the skin from above, glaze it
12. Serve underneath what you wish, such as chicory, spinach, a puree of chard, a puree of mushrooms; you can put lettuce around it, sauce underneath, glazed onions, or serve it English style, that is to say, when you have removed the skin, instead of glazing it, sprinkle very fine parsley and clear juice underneath

title: Selle de Mouton panée à l'anglaise (Saddle of Mutton Breaded English Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- saddle of mutton prepared and cooked as in the braised recipe
- salt
- coarse pepper
- butter
- breadcrumbs
- three eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook the saddle as for the braised version
2. When cooked, season with salt and coarse pepper
3. Dip it in warm butter
4. Coat it with breadcrumbs
5. Warm butter the size of half an egg
6. Break three eggs and mix them with the butter
7. Add a pinch of salt
8. Dip the saddle in the eggs and coat it all over
9. Place it in the breadcrumbs and give the saddle a fine shape
10. Three quarters of an hour before serving, put it on the grill with a very hot campfire oven above it to give it color
11. At the moment of serving, dress it on your platter
12. Put clear juice underneath; if your saddle was cold, you would have more ease in breading it

title: Selle de Mouton pour relevé (Saddle of Mutton for a Second Course)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- whole mutton from the shoulder defect to the leg
- salt
- coarse pepper
- aromatic spices
- meat from a leg of mutton
- lard strips
- mutton bones and leg bones
- six carrots
- six onions
- three cloves
- four bay leaves
- thyme
- parsley and chives bouquet
- bouillon or water
- buttered paper

## Directions

1. Cut the mutton in its entirety from the shoulder defect to the leg
2. Debone the ribs down to the spine
3. Ensure the saddle is perfectly well covered with fat and skin
4. Season the interior with salt, coarse pepper, and pounded aromatics
5. Put the meat from a leg of mutton inside, without leaving the skins, to fill it
6. Give the saddle its original form by rolling each side of the flanks and closing the meat inside so that the saddle is evenly rounded
7. Tie it with string so it does not lose its fine form
8. Put lard strips in the bottom of your braisière
9. Place your saddle there; if you wish, cook it in a pan so it is very white, or put the bones from your leg and ribs, six carrots, six onions, three cloves, four bay leaves, a little thyme, a strong bouquet of parsley and chives
10. Moisten it with bouillon or water if you do not have any, then add salt
11. Cover the saddle with lard strips and buttered paper
12. Braise it for five hours over and under fire
13. At the moment of serving, drain it, untie it, remove the skin from above; take care that the fat is bare
14. Sprinkle very finely chopped parsley on it, clear juice underneath, or glaze it
15. Put around it, if you wish, glazed onions or turnips, large carrots, potatoes, or tomato sauce

title: Selle de Mouton à l'Anglaise (Saddle of Mutton English Style)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- saddle of mutton
- salt
- coarse pepper
- butter
- ten eggs
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Have a saddle like the previous one, which you debone, fill, season, tie, and cook the same way as the previous recipe
2. It must be cooked the day before
3. To be able to bread it more easily, remove the skin from above; pare it; season it with salt and coarse pepper
4. Butter it with a brush of feathers; bread it; let it cool
5. Warm a quarter pound of butter in a vessel
6. Break ten eggs into it and season them with salt and coarse pepper; beat them with your butter
7. Daub your saddle all over with this and bread it entirely
8. When it is breaded, warm some butter; dip your brush in it and let it drip onto your saddle so that only drops fall on top
9. Sprinkle breadcrumbs so that the top is well covered
10. One hour before serving, put your saddle on a plafond in a large oven; let it not be too hot so your saddle has a fine color
11. At the moment of serving, when it is well hot, slide a casserole lid under your saddle and place it on the platter
12. Put clear juice underneath

title: Sheep Cutlets Grilled Breaded
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep cutlets
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Bread crumbs
- Clear jus

## Directions

1. Trim your cutlets as described for sautéed cutlets
2. Make a piece of butter lukewarm
3. Put them in it after seasoning them with salt and coarse pepper
4. When they are covered with butter, put them in bread crumbs, ensuring they take it exactly
5. Put them on the table and give them an agreeable shape
6. Place them on a saucepan cover, sprinkling bread crumbs over and under them
7. A quarter hour before serving, put them on the grill over a moderately hot fire, taking care that your cutlets do not cook too much and your bread crumbs do not burn
8. Arrange them in a crown and put a clear jus underneath

title: Sheep Cutlets Sautéed
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep cutlets
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Glaze
- Bound sauce
- Clear jus
- Small turned roots

## Directions

1. Cut your cutlets from the third rib near the neck to the last, about one inch thick
2. Trim them, that is to say remove the skins and bones, except the rib bone
3. Give it a round shape on the side of the fillet, from which you have removed the bones to the end of the fillet rib
4. Trim the end of the bone on the side of the breast so that one can take the cutlet with the fingers without touching the meat
5. Beat the fillet of your cutlet with a cleaver
6. Trim it once more to remove the flesh that exceeds the others
7. Put them in your sauté pan, season them, pour warm butter over them
8. At the time of serving, put your sauté pan over a hot fire
9. Let your cutlets stiffen on one side, turn them over on the other
10. Five minutes are enough to cook them
11. Put your finger on them: if your cutlets are firm, remove them from the fire
12. Arrange them in a crown
13. You may, an instant before they are cooked, put some glaze with your cutlets
14. Put a bound sauce over them in which you put a piece of glaze
15. You may serve a clear jus underneath, and in the middle of your cutlets some small turned roots

title: Sheep Necks with Small Root Vegetables (Cous de Mouton aux petites racines)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two sheep necks
- Lard strips
- Meat trimmings
- Four carrots
- Five onions, one of which studded with three cloves
- Two bay leaves
- Thyme
- Bouquet of parsley and chives
- Bouillon or water
- Salt
- Small root vegetables

## Directions

1. You will have two sheep necks
2. Cut off the bloody end
3. Tie them up
4. Put lard strips in a casserole, meat trimmings, four carrots, five onions, one of which studded with three cloves, two bay leaves, a little thyme, a bouquet of parsley and chives
5. Put your necks with this seasoning
6. Cover them with lard strips
7. Moisten them with bouillon or water
8. Then add salt
9. When your necks boil, put them over a gentle fire for four hours
10. At the moment of serving, drain them
11. Arrange them on your plate
12. Pour your small root vegetables over them (See Small Root Vegetables)
13. You can also instead of root vegetables put turnips cut into small sticks (See Turnips for entrée)
14. If you do not have sauce for your root vegetables, make a light roux that you moisten with the cooking liquid in which your necks have cooked
15. Prepare your small root vegetables as those described

title: Sheep Necks à la Sainte-Menehould (Cous de Mouton à la Ste-Menehould)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two sheep necks
- Lard strips
- Three carrots
- Four onions
- Bouquet of parsley and chives
- Two bay leaves
- Thyme
- Two cloves
- Mutton or veal trimmings
- Bouillon or water
- Salt
- Solfia
- Coarse pepper
- Butter
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Two necks suffice to make an entrée
2. Cut off the bleeding end
3. Tie them up
4. Cook them between lard strips, adding three carrots, four onions, a bouquet of parsley and chives, two bay leaves, a little thyme, two cloves, some mutton or veal trimmings
5. Moisten them with bouillon or water
6. Add salt
7. Let your necks simmer for four hours
8. When they are cooked, season them with a little solfia and coarse pepper
9. Dip them in warm butter
10. Mix them in the bread crumbs
11. Take care that there is some everywhere
12. An hour before serving, put them on the grill over gentle fire
13. Turn them on three sides
14. Make them take color on top with the country oven
15. At the moment of serving, arrange them on the plate
16. Put clear jus underneath

title: Sheep Tails Breaded English Style
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep tails
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Butter
- Eggs (four)
- Bread crumbs
- Clear jus

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your sheep tails as described for braised tails
2. When they are cooked, drain them and season them with salt and coarse pepper
3. Make a piece of butter lukewarm
4. Dip your tails in it
5. Put them in bread crumbs
6. Break four eggs which you put with your butter
7. Beat everything together
8. Dip your tails in your eggs, making sure the tail takes it everywhere
9. Put it in bread crumbs so that your tails are exactly breaded, well united, and retain their shape well
10. Half an hour before serving, put them on the grill over a very gentle fire
11. Cover them with a very hot camp oven to make them take color
12. At the time of serving, arrange them on the plate and put a clear jus underneath

title: Sheep Tails with Purée (Chicoree)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep tails
- Chicory
- Butter
- Flour
- Stock from cooking the tails
- Coarse pepper
- Salt

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your tails as described for braised tails
2. At the time of serving, drain them and glaze them
3. Put them on chicory
4. If you do not have sauce to cook your chicory, put a piece of butter in a saucepan
5. Chop your chicory and put it with your butter
6. Pass it over the fire for ten minutes
7. Put a good pinch of flour in it, which you blend with your chicory
8. Pass the stock in which your tails cooked, removing the fat, and moisten your chicory with it
9. Add a little coarse pepper
10. Reduce your chicory until it is thick
11. Ensure it is well salted
12. Put it on the plate with your glazed tails on top

title: Sheep Tails with Purées
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep tails
- Purée of lentils, peas, green beans, or tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your tails as described for braised tails
2. At the time of serving, drain them and arrange them on the plate
3. Cover them with a purée of lentils, green peas, beans, or a tomato sauce
4. If you have neither meat nor lard to cook your tails, cook them with the seasoning explained for braised tails
5. Season them well with salt
6. These tails may be served in a terrine; instead of seven, you will need twelve

title: Sheep Tongues au Gratin (Langues de Mouton au gratin)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep tongues
- Forcemeat of poultry or godiveau quenelles
- Velouté
- Lard
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your tongues as those described as braised
2. Prepare a forcemeat à quenelle of poultry or godiveau in which you will put a little velouté in the bottom of your plate
3. Place your tongues so that they rest on the gratin
4. You can arrange them in different ways
5. Cover them with lard strips
6. Put your plate over a fire that is not too hot, so that your gratin does not burn
7. Place the country oven on top
8. Take care that it is not too hot
9. When you see that your gratin is cooked, at the moment of serving, put an Italian sauce on it
10. Take care to remove the fat that is in your plate and the lard strips that cover your tongues

title: Sheep Tongues en Atelet (Langues de Mouton en atelet)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep tongues
- Hashed sauce
- Raw egg yolk
- Veal teat or small lard
- Butter
- Bread crumbs
- Beaten egg
- Italian sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your tongues as those described as braised
2. Cut them into square pieces, two lines thick
3. Make a hashed sauce to thicken it
4. When reduced, put a raw egg yolk that you will mix with your sauce which will be almost boiling
5. Put your pieces of tongue with your sauce
6. Stir everything well so that your pieces take up the sauce
7. Place them on a plate to cool
8. Have a cooked veal teat or small lard that you will cut into squares of the same size as those of your tongues
9. Put a piece of tongue, a piece of teat, and so on to the end of the skewer
10. When it is full, pour sauce over it
11. With the knife, smooth it on the four sides
12. Dip your skewer in butter
13. Put it in the bread crumb
14. After dip it in beaten egg
15. Bread it once more
16. Take care that your four sides are well united, and your four corners well formed
17. Your skewers finished, a quarter hour and a half before serving, put them on the grill over gentle fire
18. Turn them on three sides, and on the fourth make them take color in the country oven or with a hot red palette for glazing
19. At the moment of serving, put them on the plate
20. Pour Italian sauce underneath

title: Sheep Tongues en Papillotes (Langues de Mouton en papillotes)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep tongues
- Fine herbs for papillotes
- Oiled paper squares
- Lard strips
- Clear jus

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your tongues as those described as braised
2. When they are cooked, cut them in half lengthwise
3. Put them on a plate
4. Pour fine herbs for papillotes over them (See Fine Herbs for papillotes)
5. Have squares of oiled paper
6. When your tongues, seasoned with fine herbs, are cold, put on your paper a small piece of lard strip, your half tongue on top
7. Put also a little fine herbs with a small strip of lard
8. Fold your paper so that your tongue is enclosed
9. Cut the corners of your paper, and pleat it so that your fine herbs cannot escape
10. Tie the end of your papillote
11. A quarter hour before serving, put them on the grill over gentle fire, so that your paper does not burn
12. At the moment of serving, arrange them in crowns, and put clear jus underneath

title: Sheep Tongues with Small Root Vegetables (Langues de Mouton aux petites racines)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep tongues
- Small root vegetables
- Suet

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your tongues as those described as braised
2. At the moment of serving, drain them
3. Arrange them around the edge of the plate
4. Pour your small root vegetables in butter in the middle
5. If you do not have sauce for your small root vegetables, make a light roux that you will moisten with the cooking liquid in which your tongues have cooked
6. Put your root vegetables to cook in it
7. Throw in a small piece of suet

title: Sheep Tongues with Tomato Sauce (Langues de Mouton, sauce tomate)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep tongues
- Tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your tongues as those described as braised
2. At the moment of serving, drain them
3. Arrange them around the edge of the plate
4. Mask them with a tomato sauce (See Tomato Sauce)

title: Sheep Tongues with Turnips (Langues de Mouton aux navets)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep tongues
- Turnips

## Directions

1. Prepare your tongues and cook them as described in the braised method above
2. At the moment of serving, drain them
3. Arrange them around the edge of the plate
4. Mask them with your turnips (See Turnips for entrée)

title: Sheep's Feet (Pieds de mouton)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet
- Water
- Butter
- Bouquet garni
- Egg yolks
- Milk or cream
- Verjuice or vinegar

## Directions

1. After boiling them well in water, peel off any remaining hair and remove the large bones.
2. Place them in a saucepan with a good piece of butter and a bouquet garni, and let them cook a few turns on the fire.
3. When cooked and the sauce reduced, do not degrease them.
4. Mix three egg yolks with milk or cream, and add to the sauce.
5. Let it bind on the fire, and when serving, add a dash of verjuice or vinegar.

title: Sheep's Feet in Marinade (Pieds de Mouton en marinade)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet, prepared and cooked as in the white sauce recipe
- Salt
- Pepper
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your sheep's feet as those made in the white sauce manner
2. When they are cooked, drain and trim them
3. Put them in a marinade
4. If you have no marinade, season them with a little salt and pepper
5. Coat them with vinegar so that they receive it all
6. At the moment of serving, drain them

title: Sheep's Feet in Tomato Sauce (Pieds de Mouton à la sauce tomate)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet, prepared and cooked as in the white sauce recipe
- Tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your sheep's feet as those made in the white sauce manner
2. At the moment of serving, drain them and arrange them hot on the plate
3. Coat them with a tomato sauce

title: Sheep's Feet in White Sauce (Pieds de Mouton au blanc)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet, scalded
- Blanc (white cooking liquid)
- Six spoonfuls of velouté
- Four spoonfuls of consommé
- Two egg yolks
- Nutmeg
- Coarse pepper
- Butter, the size of two eggs
- Chives or shallots, finely chopped
- Parsley, finely chopped
- One spoonful of flour
- One small spoonful of bouillon
- Lemon juice
- Salt

## Directions

1. Have sheep's feet that have been scalded
2. Clean them up to the joint and blanch them; refresh them
3. Remove the tuft of wool found in the cleft of the feet
4. Dry them well and put them to cook in a blanc
5. Let them simmer on the fire for four hours
6. Test them to see if they are cooked; if the flesh yields under the fingers, remove them to drain
7. Trim the extremities so that your feet are whole and clean
8. Put them in a casserole and pour in six spoonfuls of defatted velouté and four of consommé
9. Reduce it almost by half
10. Just before serving, add a liaison of two egg yolks to your sauce with a little nutmeg
11. When thickened, pass it through fine cloth over your feet
12. Keep them warm without letting them boil
13. Add a little coarse pepper
14. If you have no velouté, take butter the size of two eggs in a casserole with finely chopped chives or shallots and let them warm in the butter
15. Add a good pinch of finely chopped parsley and stir with the hot butter
16. Add one spoonful of flour and stir everything together
17. Add one small spoonful of bouillon, a little coarse pepper, and a little nutmeg
18. Add a liaison of two eggs to your sauce
19. When it has boiled a little, pour it over your feet
20. At the moment of serving, add lemon juice and check that your sauce is properly salted

title: Sheep's Feet Provençale Style (Pieds de Mouton à la Provençale)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet, prepared and cooked as in the white sauce recipe
- Twelve large onions
- Half a pound of oil
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg, grated
- Juice of three or four lemons
- Two spoonfuls of bouillon

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your sheep's feet as those made in the white sauce manner
2. Drain and trim them, and place them in a casserole
3. Cut twelve large onions in half and slice them to the thickness of a line, so that your onion forms a half-circle
4. Remove the head, tail, and heart so that only half-circles remain
5. Put half a pound of oil in a casserole and place it on a hot fire
6. When your oil is very hot, put in your onions and stir them with a wooden spoon handle
7. When your onions are golden, remove them from the fire
8. Remove a little of the oil and add salt, pepper, grated nutmeg, the juice of three or four lemons, and two spoonfuls of bouillon
9. Just let it come to a boil
10. Put this sauce over your sheep's feet and keep them warm without letting them boil
11. At the moment of serving, arrange your sheep's feet on your plate and mask them with your onions
12. If you have no lemon juice, use vinegar instead

title: Sheep's Feet with Onion Purée (Pieds de Mouton à la purée d'oignons)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sheep's feet, prepared and cooked as in the white sauce recipe
- Warm onion purée, somewhat thin

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook your sheep's feet as those made in the white sauce manner
2. Trim twelve of them and put them in a casserole
3. Have a warm onion purée that is somewhat thin and pour it over your feet
4. Keep them warm without letting them boil
5. At the moment of service, arrange them on the plate and coat them with your purée

title: Shoulder of Lamb with Cucumbers
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb shoulders
- Medium lardons, seasoned
- Four spices
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Lard strips
- Cucumbers
- Cream

## Directions

1. Debone your lamb shoulders down to the handle
2. Lard them on the inside with seasoned medium lardons, four spices, salt and coarse pepper
3. Arrange them so they have an elongated form
4. Tie them and cover with lard strips
5. Cook them as the previous shoulders
6. At the moment of serving, drain them, remove the ties, and glaze
7. Place creamed cucumbers on your plate
8. Place your glazed shoulders on top
9. You may serve them with chicory, tomato sauce, or mushroom purée

title: Shoulder of Lamb à la Polonaise
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Two lamb shoulders
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Aromatic herbs, ground
- Lard for larding
- Lard strips for laying in pan
- Veal slices
- Four carrots
- Four onions
- Two bay leaves
- Thyme
- Bouillon
- Truffles
- Butter

## Directions

1. Completely debone two lamb shoulders without leaving any bone
2. Season the insides with salt, coarse pepper, and a little ground aromatic herbs
3. Gather the meat together with a trussing needle and string, forming a small balloon shape
4. Lard the top of the shoulders with fine lard, leaving about two finger widths around the edge unlarded
5. Place lard strips and veal slices in a casserole
6. Place the two shoulders on top
7. Add four carrots, four onions, two bay leaves, and a little thyme
8. Add a full pot-spoon of bouillon and place a round of paper on top
9. Braise for two hours with heat above and below so the lard takes on a beautiful color
10. At the moment of serving, drain the shoulders, remove the trussing, and glaze them
11. Cut the truffles into small lardons with a fork
12. Make small holes where the larding was and insert the truffle lardons around
13. Chop truffles and pass them through a small piece of butter
14. Add four deglazing spoonfuls of worked velouté
15. Arrange the shoulders on your plate and pour this sauce underneath

title: Sirop d'Abricots (Apricot Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of very ripe apricots
- two pounds of sugar
- one glass of water

## Directions

1. Remove the pits from the apricots after having peeled the almond
2. Cut them into small pieces
3. Put two pounds of sugar in a pan with one glass of water and cook as the previous cherry syrup
4. Add the apricots with the pits
5. Cook together over medium heat until taking syrup with one finger and touching it to another, it forms a thread when opened that holds somewhat without breaking
6. Pass them through a sieve

title: Sirop d'Orgeat (Orgeat Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sweet almonds
- two ounces of seeds of the four cold seeds
- half an ounce of bitter almonds
- half a setier of warm water
- one pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Put the almonds in boiling water and remove from the fire
2. Remove them when the skin comes off easily
3. As you remove the skins, throw them in fresh water
4. Let them drain and put them in a mortar with the cold seeds
5. Pound everything together until very fine
6. To prevent them from turning to oil, add from time to time a half-spoonful of water
7. Then dilute them in a good half-setier of warm water
8. Put them on hot ashes to infuse for three hours
9. Pass them through a cloth, pressing with a wooden spoon to express all the almond juice
10. Take one pound of sugar and cook it as for maidenhair fern syrup
11. Finish it in the same way on hot ashes

title: Sirop de Capillaire (Maidenhair Fern Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one ounce of maidenhair fern leaves
- one chopine of boiling water
- one pound of sugar
- one good glass of water

## Directions

1. Put the maidenhair fern leaves in a chopine of boiling water and remove immediately
2. Put them to infuse for at least twelve hours on hot ashes
3. Pass them through a sieve
4. Put one pound of sugar in a pan with a good glass of water
5. Make it boil and skim
6. Continue boiling until, dipping two fingers in fresh water and then in the sugar and quickly dipping them back in fresh water, the sugar that remains on your fingers breaks cleanly
7. Add your maidenhair fern water without boiling
8. Remove as soon as they are mixed together and put in a terrine
9. Cover and place on hot ashes, maintaining an even heat without burning for three days
10. You will know the syrup is done when, taking some syrup on a finger and pressing it against the other, opening both, a thread forms that does not break easily
11. Put it in bottles and do not cork them until they are completely cold

title: Sirop de Cerises (Cherry Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of beautiful ripe and healthy cherries
- one large glass of water
- two pounds of sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the stems and pits from the cherries
2. Put them on the fire with one large glass of water
3. Let them boil eight or ten times and pass through a sieve
4. Put two pounds of sugar on the fire with one glass of water
5. Let it boil and skim well
6. Continue boiling until when dipping the skimming spoon into it and shaking it over the sugar, and blowing through the holes, sparks of sugar come out
7. Immediately add all the cherry juice
8. Let them boil together until they have taken on the consistency of a strong syrup

title: Sirop de cerises (Cherry Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds ripe healthy cherries
- 1 large glass water
- 2 pounds sugar
- 1 glass water

## Directions

1. Take 2 pounds of beautiful ripe healthy cherries, remove the stems and pits, and put them on the fire with 1 large glass of water
2. Boil them eight or ten times and strain through a sieve
3. Put 2 pounds of sugar on the fire with 1 glass of water, boil it and skim well
4. Continue boiling it until the sugar reaches the proper consistency

title: Sirop de Choux Rouges pour Fortifier la Poitrine (Red Cabbage Syrup to Strengthen the Chest)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one large red cabbage
- water
- one pound of Narbonne honey
- one glass of water

## Directions

1. Cut and wash one large red cabbage
2. Put it in a pot with water and cook for three or four hours until only about a chopine of water remains
3. Pass the cabbage through a sieve, pressing strongly to extract all the juice
4. Let it rest and draw it off clear
5. Put one pound of Narbonne honey in a pan with a glass of water
6. Make it boil, skimming often
7. When the honey is completely clear, add your cabbage water and boil together until it reaches the consistency of the previous syrup

title: Sirop de Citron (Lemon Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sugar
- a small glass of water
- juice of one small lemon

## Directions

1. Put half a pound of sugar in a pan with a small glass of water
2. Make it boil and skim
3. Continue boiling until, taking sugar with a finger and pressing it against the other, you open both fingers and a thread forms that breaks and makes a drop on the finger
4. Then add the juice of one small lemon
5. Make it boil a few more times and serve

title: Sirop de Coings (Quince Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one dozen very ripe quinces
- one pound of brown sugar
- one half-setier of liquid

## Directions

1. Take a dozen very ripe quinces, remove their cores and skins
2. Pound the flesh and put it in a large cloth to wring it with force of arms, by this process you will draw out all the juice
3. Let this juice rest and draw it off clear
4. On one half-setier you will take one pound of brown sugar which you will cook in the same way as that of verjuice syrup
5. When your brown sugar has its degree of cooking add the quince juice and let them boil together until the syrup has the same consistency as the previous one

title: Sirop de Coquelicot (Poppy Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of poppies
- one chopine of boiling water
- one pound of sugar
- one glass of water

## Directions

1. Put half a pound of poppies in a terrine and pour a chopine of boiling water over them
2. Let infuse for twenty-four hours on hot ashes
3. Boil for two boils and pass through a sieve, pressing strongly to extract all the juice
4. Put one pound of sugar in a pan with a glass of water
5. Make it boil and skim well
6. Add your poppy water and boil together until the consistency of syrup
7. You will know it is done when, taking it on the finger and pressing it against the other, a thread forms that does not break easily

title: Sirop de Guimauve (Marshmallow Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of brown sugar
- three quarterons of marshmallow root, chopped
- one chopine of water

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of brown sugar in the same way as that for verjuice syrup
2. Prepare a marshmallow water as follows: cook three quarterons of chopped marshmallow root in one chopine of water after having scraped and washed it
3. Let it boil until the water sticks to the fingers
4. Then put it in a cloth and wring it with force of arms
5. Let it rest and draw it off clear
6. Take the clearest part and put it in the brown sugar
7. Let them boil together until they have the consistency of a strong syrup like the previous ones

title: Sirop de Mûres (Mulberry Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two hundred of beautiful black mulberries
- one large glass of water
- two pounds of sugar
- one half-setier of water

## Directions

1. Put the mulberries on the fire with one large glass of water
2. Let them boil five or six times until they have rendered all their juice and pass through a sieve
3. Let it rest and pass it a second time through a finer sieve
4. Put two pounds of sugar on the fire with a half-setier of water
5. Let it boil and skim
6. Continue boiling until dipping two fingers in water and putting them in the sugar, you redip them in fresh water, the sugar remaining on your fingers breaks cleanly
7. Add your mulberry water and heat until it is incorporated with the sugar, taking care that it does not boil
8. Put it afterwards in a well-covered terrine to place on warm ashes for three days
9. Maintain it with equal heat as much as you can without it being burning
10. You will know it is at its point when taking syrup with one finger and pressing it against the other, and opening both, a thread forms that does not break easily
11. Put it in bottles and do not cork it until it is completely cold

title: Sirop de Pomme (Apple Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarteron of healthy reinette apples
- one half-setier of water
- one pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Take one quarteron of healthy reinette apples, cut them into slices as thinly as you can
2. Cook them with one half-setier of water
3. When they are in marmalade, put them in a cloth to wring them strongly and express all the juice
4. Let this juice rest and draw it off clear
5. On one half-setier, cook one pound of sugar in the same way as for the cherry syrup
6. When it is at its cooking point, add your juice

title: Sirop de Pomme (Apple Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of reinette apples, healthy
- half a setier of water
- one pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the apples into the thinnest slices you can
2. Cook them with half a setier of water
3. When they are reduced to marmalade, put them in a cloth and wring them strongly to express all the juice
4. Let the juice rest and draw it off clear
5. Cook one pound of sugar in the same manner as for cherry syrup
6. When the sugar reaches the proper cooking point, add your apple juice and boil them together until, taking syrup on a finger and pressing it against the other finger and opening both, a thread forms that does not break easily

title: Sirop de Verjus (Verjuice Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of brown sugar
- one half-setier of water
- two pounds of green and large verjuice

## Directions

1. Put two pounds of brown sugar on the fire with a half-setier of water
2. Let boil and skim
3. Continue boiling until dipping the skimming spoon in the brown sugar, shaking it over, you blow through the holes, sugar that flies off in sparks should come out
4. Prepare the verjuice as follows: take two pounds of green and large verjuice, remove the stems and pound them, express the juice by passing it through a fine sieve, let it rest and draw it off clear
5. Add it to the brown sugar and let them boil together until it is reduced to a strong syrup, which you will know when a thread forms in your fingers like the previous one

title: Sirop violet (Violet Syrup)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound violets
- 1/2 setier boiling water
- 2.5 pounds sugar
- 1/2 setier water

## Directions

1. Put 1/4 pound of cleaned violets in a terrine and pour 1/2 setier of boiling water over them
2. Put something clean on the violets to keep them submerged in the water, cover them and place on hot ashes for two hours
3. Strain the violets through a cloth, pressing hard to extract the water
4. This quantity of violets should yield about 1 pint of liquid
5. If you have 1 pint, put 2.5 pounds of sugar in a pan with 1/2 setier of water, boil and skim
6. Continue boiling until when you dip your fingers in water and then in the sugar and redip in water, the sugar breaks clean
7. Then pour in your violet water, being very careful that the syrup does not boil
8. When well combined, put the syrup in a terrine, cover it and place on hot ashes for three days, maintaining the most even heat possible without being too hot
9. You will know the syrup is done by putting two fingers in it and drawing them together; if a thread forms that does not break, put it in bottles

title: Smoked Herrings à la Sainte-Ménéhould (Harengs Sorés à la Sainte-Ménéhould)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- A dozen smoked herrings
- Water
- Milk
- Hot butter
- Bay leaf
- Thyme
- Basil
- Egg yolks
- Pepper
- Verjuice

## Directions

1. Cut off the end of the head or tail of each herring
2. Soak for four hours in water, then two hours in half a setier of milk
3. Drain and dry
4. Dip in hot butter mixed with half a bay leaf, thyme, basil chopped like powder, two egg yolks, and coarse pepper
5. Coat as you dip them in the butter and grill lightly
6. Put two spoonfuls of verjuice in the bottom of the serving dish and arrange the herrings on top

title: Soft-Boiled Eggs (Oeufs Mollets)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Water

## Directions

1. Put water in a pan and bring to a boil.
2. Add as many eggs as desired and boil for exactly five minutes.
3. Quickly remove the eggs and place them in fresh water.
4. Peel them gently so as not to break them.
5. The whites will be cooked and the yolks will remain soft and flexible under your fingers.
6. Serve whole.

title: Soles Frites (Fried Sole)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- sole
- flour
- oil for frying

## Directions

1. Make an incision on the back of the sole, dust them with flour like all other fish, and put them in fairly hot oil for frying

title: Soles Sautées (Sautéed Sole)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Sole fish
- Parsley, chopped and washed
- Chives, chopped
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Nutmeg, grated
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut off the head and tail of the sole; after emptying and cleaning them, place them in your sauté pan
2. Sprinkle with a little chopped and washed parsley, a little chopped chives, salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
3. Warm a good piece of butter and pour it over them
4. At the moment of service, place them over a hot fire; stir them so they do not stick
5. Once one side is cooked, turn them over to the other side
6. Arrange them on your plate and drizzle with an Italian sauce (see Italian Sauce)

title: Soles sautées (Sautéed Soles)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Soles
- Chopped and washed parsley
- Chopped chives
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut the head and tail from your soles
2. After cleaning them, put them in your sauté pan
3. Sprinkle them with a little chopped and washed parsley, a little chopped chives, salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg

title: Soles sur le plat (Soles on the Plate)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Soles
- Butter
- Parsley
- Shallots
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Nutmeg
- Fine herbs
- White wine
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the soles and dry them
2. Make an incision with the knife blade on the back ridge on the dark side
3. Melt butter on a plate
4. Add chopped parsley, finely chopped shallots, salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg
5. Put your soles on the plate and sprinkle fine herbs, salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg on top
6. Add a glass of good white wine
7. Mask them with bread crumbs, sprinkled with drops of butter
8. A quarter hour before serving, put your soles in the oven or on a gentle heat with a very hot country tile to cover them
9. If you do not use an oven, use bread crumbs instead of bread

title: Soles, Limandes, Carlets et Plies Entre Deux Plats à la Bourgeoise (Sole, Dab, Plaice and Flounder Between Two Plates, Bourgeois Style)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- sole, dab, plaice or flounder
- good butter
- parsley
- onion
- mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- breadcrumbs
- verjus

## Directions

1. Scale the fish
2. Melt good butter and put it in the serving dish with chopped parsley, onion, and mushrooms, salt and pepper
3. Arrange the fish on top
4. Make the same seasoning on top of the fish as underneath
5. Cover the dish well and cook over a small fire on a stove
6. When cooked, serve with a short sauce and add a dash of verjus on top
7. Alternatively, after preparing as above, you can cover the top with breadcrumbs and cook it in the oven or under a pie cover

title: Soufflé de Frangipane (Frangipane Soufflé)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Six tablespoonfuls of flour
- One whole egg
- Four egg yolks
- One pint of cream
- Butter (size of one egg)
- Six tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar
- Two bitter macarons
- Three sweet macarons
- One dried biscuit
- One tablespoonfull of candied orange flower in powder

## Directions

1. Put six tablespoonfuls of flour in a saucepan and mix with one whole egg and four yolks
2. Add one pint of cream and butter (size of one egg)
3. Place over fire and stir constantly
4. When cooked, let it cool
5. Add six tablespoonfuls of powdered sugar, two bitter macarons and three sweet ones, and one dried biscuit, all finely crushed
6. Add one tablespoonfull of candied orange flower powder
7. Mix everything together
8. Add four egg yolks, more if the mixture is thick
9. Whip five egg whites as for biscuit
10. Add them to the mixture
11. Place the soufflé in a silver saucepan and cook as the previous ones

title: Soufflé de Marrons (Chestnut Soufflé)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- chestnuts
- water
- butter
- powdered sugar
- six egg yolks
- six egg whites

## Directions

1. Blanch your chestnuts only in water.
2. Then cook them in water, adding whatever flavoring you wish.
3. When cooked, drain them on a white cloth.
4. Dry them well and put them in a mortar.
5. Pound them thoroughly and pass them through a quenelle sieve.
6. Put the chestnuts back in the mortar.
7. Add half as much butter as chestnuts.
8. Add powdered sugar.
9. After pounding everything together well, add six egg yolks, or more according to the quantity of your soufflé.
10. Pound them also with your chestnuts.
11. If your paste is sufficiently liquid, remove it from the mortar to put it in a saucepan.
12. A good half hour before serving, whip the whites of the six eggs.
13. When they are stiff like for a biscuit, mix them lightly with the mixture.
14. Pour it into a silver saucepan and cook the soufflé as described for other soufflés.
15. It must only be served at the moment of eating.

title: Soufflé de Pain au Café Vierge (Bread Soufflé with Virgin Coffee)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Three half-setiers of cream
- Six ounces of sugar
- Four ounces of coffee, lightly roasted

## Directions

1. Boil three half-setiers of cream
2. Add six ounces of sugar
3. Lightly roast four ounces of coffee
4. Remove from heat and throw it into the cream
5. Cover the saucepan
6. Pass it through a strainer onto the bread crumb
7. Let it cool and make the soufflé as the previous one

title: Soufflé de Pain à la Vanille (Vanilla Bread Soufflé)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One pint of cream (more if the soufflé is large)
- One stick of vanilla
- Six ounces of sugar
- One pound soft bread
- Butter (size of two eggs)
- Two whole eggs
- Four egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil the pint of cream; add the vanilla stick and six ounces of sugar, bringing it to a boil three or four times
2. Take a one-pound soft bread; remove the crumb and place it in the hot cream
3. Let it soak until the mixture is cold
4. Put the crumb in a white cloth and press it to extract the cream
5. Place it in a mortar with the vanilla and pound well
6. Add butter (size of two eggs), two whole eggs, and four egg yolks
7. When well combined, pass it through a sieve or quenelle tamis, pressing with a wooden spoon
8. Place the bread purée in a saucepan
9. Whip four egg whites as for biscuit and mix them with the bread crumb
10. Place the soufflé in a silver saucepan and put it in a gentle oven
11. You may also place it on red ash with a camp oven over it
12. Once cooked, serve immediately

title: Soufflé de Pommes de Terre (Potato Souffle)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- One chopine of cream
- Six ounces of sugar
- Six tablespoons of potato starch
- Four egg yolks

## Directions

1. Boil one chopine of cream with six ounces of sugar
2. Mix six tablespoons of potato starch with the eggs and cream

title: Soufflé de pommes de terre (Potato Soufflé)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- one chopine of cream
- six ounces of sugar
- six tablespoons of potato starch
- four egg yolks
- butter the size of an egg
- a little grated lemon zest
- six egg yolks
- one or two whole eggs
- four egg whites

## Directions

1. Boil one chopine of cream with six ounces of sugar and six tablespoons of potato starch; dilute the starch with the egg yolks, cream, butter the size of an egg, and a little chopped lemon zest
2. Place the mixture on the fire and stir until it comes to a boil
3. Let it cool
4. Add six egg yolks mixed together; if the soufflé is too thick, add one or two whole eggs
5. Beat four egg whites as for biscuit and fold them gently into the soufflé
6. Arrange in a small silver casserole and cook as other soufflés

title: Soup aux Œufs pochés (Poached Egg Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- poached eggs
- very good broth
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. Poach eggs; when they are poached, refreshed and trimmed so that they are clean to put in your soup tureen
2. Ten minutes before serving, pour your very good broth over the eggs that will be in your soup tureen
3. Add a little coarse pepper to your broth

title: Soup with Lazagnes (Lazagne Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- good bouillon
- half pound of lazagnes (more or less depending on the size of your soup)
- coarse pepper

## Directions

1. When your bouillon is in your saucepan and boiling, add half pound of lazagnes
2. Boil for three quarters of an hour, or a bit more, until your lazagnes are sufficiently swollen but not too much, as your soup would not be distinguished and would turn to paste
3. At the moment of pouring them into your tureen, add a little coarse pepper
4. Alternatively, you can blanch them in salted water without letting them cook too long; then put them in the bouillon

title: Soup with Noodles (Noodle Soup)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- half a liter of flour
- four egg yolks
- a little salt
- a little water
- good bouillon
- coarse pepper
- optional: nutmeg and egg whites

## Directions

1. Have half a liter of flour; add four egg yolks, a little salt and a little water
