author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- salt
- pepper
- two bay leaves
- onion slices
- parsley sprigs
- chives, cut
- white wine or water and vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash your crayfish well
2. Place them in a saucepan with salt, pepper, two bay leaves, onion slices, parsley sprigs, and cut chives
3. Add a bottle of white wine, or water and vinegar
4. Place over a hot fire and sauté them from time to time
5. When they have boiled for a quarter hour, remove from the fire and leave them in their seasoning
6. Reheat them when you wish to serve

title: Écrevisses en Coulis (Crayfish Coulis)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Crayfish
- Boiling water
- Fresh water
- Good bouillon (meat or fish depending on use)
- Vegetables for bouillon

## Directions

1. Boil the crayfish briefly in boiling water
2. Remove them and place in fresh water
3. Shell out the tails and set aside along with the shells
4. Pound the shells for three hours until fine
5. Dilute the pounded shells in good bouillon and pass through a strainer
6. If making a ragout, cook the crayfish tails in a little bouillon and reduce almost dry, then add to the coulis
7. Season to taste
8. Heat without boiling
9. For a soup, keep the coulis thinner and add to the soup when half-cooked, heating without boiling

title: Écrevisses à la Crème (Crayfish in Cream)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- cream sauce
- nutmeg, grated

## Directions

1. Remove the small legs from your crayfish and the shell from the tail
2. Cut the tip of the head and that of the large legs
3. Make a cream sauce in which you will grate a little nutmeg
4. Pour it over your crayfish and let them simmer a little

title: Épaule de Mouton au Four (Shoulder of Mutton Baked in the Oven)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of mutton
- small lard for larding (optional)
- onions
- parsnip
- carrot
- garlic clove
- cloves
- bay leaf
- basil leaves
- water or bouillon
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. If desired, lard a shoulder of mutton with small lard.
2. In the bottom of a casserole proportioned to the size of the shoulder, place some sliced onions, parsnip, carrot cut into strips, a garlic clove, two cloves, a half bay leaf, and a few basil leaves.
3. Add approximately a good half-setier of water or bouillon, and pepper.
4. If the shoulder is larded with small lard, use less salt.
5. Place the shoulder on top and cook it in the oven.
6. When it is cooked, pass the sauce through a sieve and press the vegetables firmly so they make a light purée to bind the sauce.
7. Degrease this sauce and serve it over the shoulder.

title: Épaule de Mouton aux Oignons Glacés (Lamb Shoulder with Glazed Onions)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder, well covered with meat
- lardons seasoned with salt and pepper
- lard strips
- carrots
- onions
- cloves
- bay leaves
- thyme
- bouillon or water
- salt
- espagnole sauce
- flour for roux

## Directions

1. Have a well-covered lamb shoulder
2. Bone it halfway down the handle
3. Lard the interior with seasoned lardons
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Once larded, truss it into a ball or lengthwise shape
6. Bridge it so it retains the form you wish to give it
7. Then tie it with string
8. Place lard strips in a braising pan
9. Place your shoulder in it
10. Add three or four carrots, five onions (one stuck with two cloves), two bay leaves, a little thyme, the shoulder bones, and a few other pieces if you have them
11. Moisten with bouillon or water
12. Add salt and simmer your shoulder for three and a half hours
13. At serving time, drain your shoulder, remove the bridge, and untie it
14. Glaze it and arrange it on the platter
15. Place glazed onions around it
16. Use a reduced espagnole sauce
17. If you do not have sauce, make a light roux with the braising liquid in which you cooked your shoulder
18. Ensure your sauce is thin enough to be reduced
19. Check that it is properly seasoned
20. You may serve around this shoulder lettuce, small turnips, small root vegetables, lentil purée, or peas

title: Épaule de Mouton en Croustade (Lamb Shoulder in Crust)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- bouillon
- bouquet garni
- salt
- glaze
- coulis
- veal round
- beef fat
- eggs
- pepper
- parsley
- chives
- mushrooms
- cream
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Break the bones underneath with the back of the cleaver
2. Cook it with bouillon, a bouquet garni, and very little salt
3. When cooked, remove the shoulder from the cooking liquid to degrease the sauce
4. Reduce the sauce to a glaze and glaze the entire top of the shoulder and let it cool
5. Put bouillon in the same casserole to detach everything remaining in it
6. Add a little coulis and pass this sauce through a sieve into another casserole which you will heat when serving
7. Take the lamb shoulder and place it on a baking dish and cover it all over with a forcemeat as described for lamb chops in dressing gown
8. Coat with breadcrumbs

title: Épaule de Mouton à la Roussie (Shoulder of Mutton with Parsley)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of mutton
- parsley branches
- lard
- hot lard or fat
- shallots
- juice
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take two handfuls of parsley branches without removing the stems and well green.
2. Lard the top of the shoulder with parsley so it is well covered.
3. Put it on the spit.
4. When the parsley is heated, pour hot lard gently over the parsley with a spoon and baste with it from time to time until the shoulder is cooked.
5. To serve, mince two shallots and put them in a little juice with salt and coarse pepper.
6. Heat the sauce and pour it under the shoulder.

title: Épaule de Mouton à la Sainte-Ménehould (Shoulder of Mutton à la Sainte-Ménehould)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- shoulder of mutton
- bouillon
- parsley
- chives
- garlic clove
- cloves
- bay leaf
- thyme
- basil
- onions
- lardons
- salt
- pepper
- coulis
- butter
- flour
- egg yolks
- bread crumbs
- shallot sauce

## Directions

1. Cook a shoulder of mutton with a little bouillon, a bouquet of parsley, chives, a garlic clove, three cloves, a bay leaf, thyme, basil, onions, lardons, salt, and pepper.
2. When it is cooked, remove it from the cooking liquid and drain it.
3. Dress it on the serving plate.
4. Make a well-bound sauce by taking two spoonfuls of coulis, putting it in a casserole with a piece of butter mixed with flour and three egg yolks.
5. Bind it over the fire and pour it over the shoulder.
6. Coat it with bread crumbs and gently moisten the bread crumbs with the degreased cooking liquid from the shoulder.
7. Brown it under a covered tourte dish with a little fire on top.
8. Drain the fat from the plate, wipe the edges, and serve under a clear shallot sauce or simply a little juice with salt and coarse pepper.

title: Épaule de Veau (Veal Shoulder)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal shoulder
- Spanish sauce

## Directions

1. Pass the spit near the handle so it goes through a hole in the shoulder blade without damaging the top
2. Serve as a relief course after the soup with Spanish sauce worked underneath

title: Épaule de Veau aux petites Racines (Veal Shoulder with Small Root Vegetables)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- veal shoulder
- lard for larding, cut into strips
- fine salt
- coarse pepper
- parsley, finely chopped
- two bay leaves
- thyme, finely chopped
- four spices
- lard for bardes
- veal slices
- shoulder bones
- six carrots
- seven or eight onions
- two bay leaves
- three cloves
- parsley and chives bouquet
- bouillon or water and salt
- small root vegetables (carrots and turnips), turned into olives or small sticks
- brown sauce or light roux
- lettuce or mushroom purée (optional)

## Directions

1. Bone the veal shoulder as you would for a galantine
2. Prick the shoulder internally with lard strips seasoned with fine salt, coarse pepper, finely chopped parsley, two bay leaves, finely chopped thyme, and a little four spices
3. When the shoulder is well larded, roll it lengthwise
4. Tie it the same way as the galantine
5. Put lard bardes, several slices of veal, and the shoulder bones in the bottom of a braising pan
6. Place the tied shoulder on top, cover it with lard
7. Add six carrots, seven or eight onions, two bay leaves, three cloves, a bouquet of parsley and chives
8. Moisten the shoulder with bouillon; if you have none, use water and salt
9. Cover with buttered paper
10. Bring to a boil and place over a gentle fire
11. Also place fire on the braising pan's lid
12. Three hours are sufficient for it to be cooked
13. At the moment of serving, drain it, untie it, glaze it, and place it on a long plat
14. Arrange many small root vegetables, turned into olives or small sticks, around the shoulder; they should be cooked in brown sauce
15. If you do not have lard or veal to cook it, simply cook it with the bones, vegetables and aromatics, moistening with water in place of bouillon and adding salt
16. If you do not have sauce for your small root vegetables, make a light roux which you will moisten with the cooking liquid from your shoulder, and add your small root vegetables (after blanching them) with a small piece of sugar to remove any bitterness
17. You can also place lettuces around this shoulder, a mushroom purée, etc.

title: Éperians Frits (Fried Smelts)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- smelts
- flour

## Directions

1. Do not clean them, wash and dry well between two cloths
2. Flour them and fry over a high fire
3. Serve as a roast course

title: Éperlans à la Bonne Eau (Smelts in Broth)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Smelts
- Water
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Clean your smelts
2. Using a small skewer, thread them through the eyes, eight or ten at a time
3. Place them in a flat but somewhat deep vessel
4. Pour good water over them
5. Break parsley leaves into four or five pieces
6. Take a little of your broth and bring to a boil
7. A quarter hour before serving, place your smelts on the fire
8. Remove them from the broth and arrange on the plate
9. Pour the broth with parsley over them

title: Épinards (Spinach)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Butter
- Salt
- Flour
- Milk or cream
- Veal coulis and jus (for the rich version)

## Directions

1. After cleaning and washing the spinach, cook it in water.
2. Remove the spinach and place it in fresh water to press it well.
3. Put it in a casserole with a piece of good butter and let it boil on a low fire for a quarter of an hour.
4. Add a pinch of salt, a pinch of flour, and moisten with milk or cream.
5. For the rich version, replace the cream with good veal coulis and jus.
6. When prepared this way, you can serve it with meat roasted on a spit.

title: Épinards au Velouté (Spinach in Velouté Sauce)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach, blanched
- Butter
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Velouté sauce
- Consommé
- Croutons
- Flour
- Bouillon or cream

## Directions

1. After the spinach is blanched (see English-Style Spinach), chop it and put it in a saucepan with a good piece of butter; add salt and coarse pepper.
2. Place the spinach on the fire; when well cooked, pour over it about five tablespoons of velouté and as much consommé.
3. If the spinach is thin, reduce it and dress it on the plate with croutons around it.
4. If you prefer it with bouillon or cream, when well cooked, add about two teaspoons of flour; mix it with the spinach and moisten it with bouillon or cream; if too wet, reduce it.

title: Épinards à l'Anglaise (English-Style Spinach)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Young spinach
- Water
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- Nutmeg, grated
- Butter
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Blanch young spinach, as old spinach does not suit this preparation.
2. Boil water in a large pot; add a handful of salt.
3. After washing the spinach well, put it in the pot; push it down well so it does not take on a smoky taste.
4. When it blends with the water, feel with your fingers to see if it yields; then refresh it as you would chicory.
5. Chop it and put it in a saucepan with salt, coarse pepper, and a little grated nutmeg.
6. With a wooden spoon, stir it over the fire; when the spinach is well heated, add a good piece of butter.
7. Mix it with the spinach without placing it on the fire, to prevent the butter from turning to oil.
8. Dress it on the plate with croutons around it.

title: Étuvée de Goujons (Braised Gudgeons)
author: Menon
body:
## Ingredients

- Gudgeons
- Good butter
- Parsley
- Ciboule (scallions)
- Mushrooms
- Two shallots
- Thyme
- Laurel
- Basil
- Salt
- Coarse pepper
- One glass of red wine

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the gudgeons, then dry without washing
2. In the serving dish, place good butter at the bottom with finely chopped parsley, ciboule, mushrooms, two shallots, thyme, laurel, and basil
3. Add salt and coarse pepper
4. Arrange the gudgeons on top and season them above and below
5. Moisten with a glass of red wine
6. Cover the dish and boil on good heat until only a little sauce remains
7. Cook for about fifteen minutes

title: Œufs Brouillés au Jambon (Scrambled Eggs with Ham)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggs
- Butter
- Quarter pound of ham
- Croutons

## Directions

1. Prepare scrambled eggs as usual
2. Cut a quarter pound of ham into small dice
3. Finish preparing the eggs as customary
4. Arrange croutons around them

title: Œufs Brouillés à la Pointe d'Asperges (Scrambled Eggs with Asparagus Tips)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus tips
- Eggs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Cut asparagus tips and blanch them
2. Add them to the eggs
3. Let them set
4. Arrange on a plate and arrange asparagus tips around it
5. You may also add peas, small cucumbers, chopped cauliflower, or other vegetables

title: Œufs Pochés au Jus (Poached Eggs in Jus)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Salt
- Vinegar
- Eggs
- Black pepper
- Jus

## Directions

1. Fill three-quarters of a medium saucepan with water, salt, and a little vinegar
2. When the water boils, place it on the edge of the stove
3. Break the eggs carefully without damaging the yolk
4. Pour the egg gently into the water
5. Poach four eggs and let them set
6. Keep the water boiling throughout
7. Remove the eggs from the water with a slotted spoon and test with your finger; if they have some consistency, place them in cold water
8. Poach them again in fresh water and change the water
9. Just before serving, reheat them
10. Drain them on white linen and arrange on a plate
11. Put a little black pepper on each egg and jus underneath

title: Œufs Pochés à l'Aspic (Poached Eggs in Aspic)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Aspic
- Poached eggs
- Truffles

## Directions

1. Warm the aspic slightly
2. Pour some into the bottom of a mold or several small molds
3. Let it congeal
4. Decorate the poached eggs with truffles
5. Place them on the jelly
6. Fill the molds with melted aspic
7. Place on ice or in the cold until serving
8. At the moment of serving, detach the aspic and place it on a plate

title: Œufs Pochés à la Chicorée (Poached Eggs with Chicory)
author: A. Viard
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicory with cream
- Poached eggs
- Cream sauce

## Directions

1. Arrange chicory with cream on a plate
2. Place poached eggs on top
3. You may put under the poached eggs: cream-dressed cabbage, sorrel purée, mushroom purée, cardoon purée, cucumbers with cream, chopped celery with cream, tomato sauce, or asparagus tips
